title: In situ vibrational spectroscopy was used to reveal the microstructure evolution mechanism of iron garnet single crystals growing from multi-component oxide melts
abstract: The microstructure geometry and self-assembly mechanism of the (TbBi) 3 FeO 5 O 12 -based melts were revealed at the ion cluster level. This work is conducive to regulating the performance of ReIGs using the melt structure.

title: Enhanced nitrate electrosynthesis by in situ growth of CoS 2 nanoparticles on poly(zwitterionic liquids) functionalized polypyrrole nanotubes based on accurate nitrate detection
abstract: The effective nitrate electrosynthesis can be achieved over CoS 2 /PVIPS/PPyNTs with a yield of 41.14 μg h −1 mg act. −1 at 1.75 V ( vs. RHE). In situ SO 3 2− -to-SO 4 2− conversion on CoS 2 reduces the energy barrier of the PDS, accelerating the NOR process.

title: Enhancing NO 2 sensing performance and stability: low-crystallinity conjugated polymers with localized aggregates via ethylene glycol pendants
abstract: Conjugated polymers are vital for detecting harmful gases, but balancing gas diffusion and crystallinity is challenging. We demonstrate an ethylene glycol-based low crystallinity design that enhances NO 2 sensing and stability.

title: Anion-rich solvation structures in high entropy aqueous electrolytes for supercapacitors with enlarged potential windows and superior rate capabilities
abstract: The use of aqueous electrolytes as suitable electrolytes for supercapacitors (SCs) strongly depends on the operating cell voltage while preserving good rate capabilities so as to obtain high energy and power densities over numerous cycles.

title: Maximizing thermoelectric performance in SnTe through strategic co-doping, nanostructuring, and topological insights
abstract: Illustrate the optimizations of the thermoelectric figure of merit for doped SnTe through various effects, including nanoprecipitates, surface states, and phonon scattering.

title: A degradable and tough supramolecular epoxy network with shape memory and clustering-triggered emission functions
abstract: The abundant non-covalent interactions and unique molecular structure design of EP–Zn endow it with multifunctionality, including shape memory, fluorescence, biodegradability, and recyclability.

title: Unlocking recent progress in niobium and vanadium carbide-based MXenes for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: This review examines the potential capability of Nb–C and VC MXenes as advanced anode materials for enhancing the performance of Na-ion batteries (SIBs). The crucial challenges and future prospects of these SIB electrodes have elaborated in detail.

title: Correction: Synergistically enhanced energy storage performance of Bi 0.47 Na 0.47 Ba 0.06 TiO 3 -based relaxor ferroelectrics via dual engineering of dynamic nanodomains and defect regulation
abstract: Correction for ‘Synergistically enhanced energy storage performance of Bi 0.47 Na 0.47 Ba 0.06 TiO 3 -based relaxor ferroelectrics via dual engineering of dynamic nanodomains and defect regulation’ by Jiangping Huang et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, 13 , 30053–30064, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA04…

title: SnSe encapsulated in N-doped graphitic carbon prepared by exchange methods for a high-performance anode in potassium-ion batteries
abstract: SnSe@NGC was synthesized in N-doped carbon via an exchange method. The SnSe@NGC electrode with optimized NGC thickness shows improved electrochemical performance by enhancing conductivity and structural stability in PIB cycling.

title: Sodiation-driven amorphous Co-based species in slope-dominated hard carbon with ultralong cycling life for sodium-ion hybrid capacitors
abstract: Incorporating Co 3 O 4 into slope-dominant hard carbon compensates for plateau region capacity loss, maintaining both rapid kinetics and high capacity.

title: In situ doped cobalt sub-nanometer clusters in hierarchically porous carbon for enhanced oxygen reduction reaction performance
abstract: Hierarchically porous carbons embedded with Co sub-nanometer clusters provide efficient mass transfer and a high density of finely dispersed active sites for enhanced ORR electrocatalysis.

title: Iridium-based electrocatalysts for the hydrogen oxidation reaction toward alkaline exchange membrane fuel cells
abstract: This review highlights recent advances in Ir-based electrocatalysts based on different design strategies. This review will guide future research in the development of high-performance Ir-based HOR electrocatalysts for AEMFCs.

title: Blade-coated organic photovoltaics with a dichlorophthalic acid self-assembled monolayer
abstract: A self-assembled monolayer material, 2C2BA, has been introduced as an alternative to PEDOT:PSS within OPVs. This strategic replacement has resulted in enhancements in photovoltaic performance for devices spanning from small to large area.

title: Achieving ultra-high energy storage performance in (Bi 0.5 Na 0.5 ) 0.7 Sr 0.3 TiO 3 -based relaxor ferroelectrics
abstract: Study on the dielectric energy storage properties of (Bi 0.5 Na 0.5 ) 0.7 Sr 0.3 TiO 3 -Sm(Mg 2/3 Nb 1/3 )O 3 ceramics.

title: Improving Li dendrite suppression capability of garnet solid-state electrolytes by in situ LiF grain boundary modification
abstract: A facile wet synthesis method to in situ homogeneously distribute LiF at the grain boundaries of LLZTO is developed to enhance its Li dendrite suppression capability. The Li|LLZTO-LiF|Li cell exhibits stable cycling over 8500 h.

title: Safeguarding CoMnMo-oxide nanosheets in acidic oxygen evolution reaction via an atomic layer deposited TiO 2 conformal coating
abstract: Conformal TiO 2 coating via ALD prevents the dissolution and deactivation of the acidic OER properties of the underlying CoMnMoO 4 catalyst by electronically stabilizing the structure through Mo-mediated coupling.

title: Exploring the potential of a potassium 4-piperidinolate/4-pyridinolate pair for reversible hydrogen storage
abstract: This reversible hydrogen storage system is based on a molecular engineering strategy that combines the effects of ring nitrogen and alkali metal in a single molecule to modulate dehydrogenation thermodynamics.

title: Hot-injected ligand-free SnTe nanoparticles: a cost-effective route to flexible symmetric supercapacitors
abstract: In this study, we report a novel approach for synthesizing tin telluride (SnTe) nanostructures using a hot injection method with water as the solvent, a significant departure from traditional organic solvents.

title: High-pressure-triggered homodisperse reconstruction of graphene nanosheets for Na ion storage
abstract: Microfluidization enables precise interlayer spacing adjustment and pore structure control in restructured graphene nanosheets, optimizing electron transport efficiency during sodium ion intercalation/extraction.

title: Reversible CO 2 adsorption in preformed solid semiclathrates within porous silica gels
abstract: Reversible hydrate-based gas separation (HBGS) was proposed as a promising technology for CO 2 capture.

title: Dual-ion synergy in boosting reaction kinetics and conductivity of a VO 2 · x H 2 O cathode for stable zinc-ion batteries
abstract: An anion–cation co-doped and modulated VO 2 · x H 2 O nanobelt structure boosts conductivity, expedites Zn 2+ transfer kinetics, and synergistically improves structural stability.

title: Ultrafast photocatalytic degradation enabled by atomically dispersed Ag on BiOIO 3 nanosheets: unraveling synergistic photo-thermal-ferroelectric effects
abstract: This work pioneers single-atom Ag-modified BiOIO 3 for ultrafast photodegradation through multi-physical-field synergy, establishing a novel photo-thermal-ferroelectric mechanism enabled by atomic-scale engineering for revolutionary photocatalysis.

title: Exploring the superconductivity of CaSrB 5 : high-pressure stability and electron–phonon interactions
abstract: CaSrB 5 is thermodynamically stable at 40 GPa and exhibits excellent superconducting properties, providing valuable theoretical insights for the exploration of new superconductors.

title: Tribocatalysis: a successful marriage of triboelectricity and heterogeneous catalysis
abstract: We discuss how tribocatalysis utilizes friction-generated charges to drive chemical transformations. The proposed mechanistic understanding, catalyst design, and future research directions are examined.

title: Bridging lab-to-fab gaps: recent advances in the reproducibility of organic solar cells
abstract: This review comprehensively summarizes the recent advances in OSC reproducibility of organic solar cells from material synthesis, film preparation, and interfacial & electrode, which would provide significant insights into large-scale fabrication.

title: Microscopic simulation of stacking fault energy and mechanical properties in CrCoNiAl x Ti y high-entropy alloys
abstract: DFT, MD, and ML reveal how Al/Ti doping tunes SFE and mechanical properties in CrCoNi-based HEAs.

title: Unveiling the synergistic mechanism of transition metal–boron coordination in B 4 C 12 nanosheets for electro- and photocatalytic nitrogen fixation
abstract: First principles calculations suggest that TM@B 4 C 12 (TM = V, Mo, Re) materials are promising candidates for high-performance electrocatalytic and photocatalytic NRR.

title: Immobilising molecular redox mediators for the oxygen evolution reaction using self-assembled monolayers
abstract: Chemical grafting of tetrathiafulvalene redox mediators on the electrode surface enhances oxygen evolution reaction and avoids shuttle effects. This route provides a novel platform of molecularly engineered electrocatalysts.

title: Topological quantum materials in catalysis
abstract: Let's quantum: topological quantum materials offer high electron mobility, stable surface states, and resistance to contamination, making them ideal candidates for next-generation heterogeneous catalysts.

title: Enhanced anodic charge storage in asymmetric hybrid supercapacitor featuring dione–diimide-based electron deficient conjugated polymers
abstract: Anodic pseudocapacitive electrodes fabricated from dione–diimide-based conjugated polymers deliver high energy and power densities.

title: The influence of the buried interface on the orientational crystallization and thermal stability of halide perovskite thin films
abstract: The charge transport layers directly affect the shape, size, and preferred orientation of grains in polycrystalline perovskite films and can be associated directly with the performance and stability of the perovskite structures and the devices.

title: PEIE-mediated strategies for highly efficient and stable perovskite solar cells overcoming BCP thermal aggregation
abstract: Perovskite solar cells (PSCs) have attracted considerable research interest in recent decades due to their remarkable power conversion efficiencies.

title: Recent progress in metal–organic framework-based materials for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction
abstract: The utilization of renewable electricity to turn CO 2 into treasure through electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction (eCO 2 RR), as well as the conversion of excess electricity into chemical energy storage, has become one of the research hotspots.

title: In silico navigation of Na + ion solvation in deep eutectic solvents to propel the next wave of sodium-ion battery electrolytes
abstract: Beyond volatility: DES electrolytes emerge as the molecular architects of next generation sodium-ion performance, stability, and sustainability.

title: Stable coal tar pitch-derived hard carbon anodes with modulated oxygen functional groups for enhanced Li-ion storage
abstract: As a class of important anode materials for lithium-ion batteries (LIBs), carbon materials are widely researched and applied due to their abundant resources, low cost, and easy preparation.

title: Enhanced degradation of organic contaminants using a PVDF/AC-NaCl piezocatalyst: effect of geometric design in fluid flow environments
abstract: A geometry-optimized PVDF/AC-NaCl piezocatalyst was rotated in water, demonstrating an energy-efficient advanced oxidation process.

title: Bacterial cellulose/reduced graphene oxide bilayer films for moist-electric power generation
abstract: The moist-electric working mechanism and high-power output of a bacterial cellulose/reduced graphene oxide bilayer film.

title: Direct utilization of light energy to promote the power density of zinc–air batteries using Co 3 O 4 @Cu x O air photocathodes with porous octahedral superstructures
abstract: Bifunctional Co 3 O 4 @Cu x O air photocathodes with octahedral structures were prepared to promote the power density of zinc–air batteries (ZABs), enabling practical applications for lighting up LED bulbs.

title: Efficient ethane production via SnCl 4 Lewis acid-enhanced CO 2 electroreduction in a flow cell electrolyser
abstract: The proposed mechanistic pathway for CO 2 reduction on CTC catalysts in a KOH electrolyte.

title: Electrochemical anilinium reduction: identifying the metastable surface intermediate on Pt and its voltage-driven decomposition to hydrogen evolution
abstract: Anilinium undergoes direct electroreduction on Pt, forming a multilayered metastable surface intermediate when adsorbed. Microelectrode voltammetry reveals voltage-driven decomposition to H 2 , and DFT shows reduced anilinium stacking on H-bound Pt.

title: Lowering the sulfur density at the margins to trigger molybdenum disulfide nanoarray growth into branches to boost their hydrogen evolution reaction activity
abstract: The planting of molybdenum disulfide (MoS 2 ) nanoarrays on a specific substrate can be realized in 1 minute via dynamic magnetic fields.

title: Nanostructure-dependent lattice oxygen reactivity and degradation of CoNi oxyhydroxide OER electrocatalysts: a mechanistic study
abstract: Oxyhydroxide catalysts show structure-dependent lattice oxygen reactivity and strongly correlated degree of structural degradation during the OER.

title: Enhancing the thermoelectric figure of merit of BiN via polymorphism, pressure, and nanostructuring
abstract: BiN is a promising candidate as thermoelectric material, but many variables need to be optimized. In this work, we maximize its zT based on pressure, temperature, carrier concentration, grain size and polymorphism.

title: Pore structure-dependent pyroelectric properties of porous PZT ceramics fabricated by template method
abstract: Compared with random, radial, and unidirectional, bidirectionally freeze-cast porous PZT builds aligned pore pathways that tune d T /d t , lower permittivity and density, and preserve the pyroelectric coefficient, yielding superior F i , F p , and J p .

title: A Pt–Mn–Al trimetallic nanoporous catalyst for reversible hydrogenation/oxidative dehydrogenation of N-heterocycles
abstract: A PtMnAlNPore trimetallic catalyst exhibited good yields with high selectivity for hydrogenation/oxidative dehydrogenation of N-heterocycles under mild reaction conditions.

title: A customized fullerene based metal oxide electron transport layer surface activity inhibitor to enhance the operational stability of perovskite solar cells
abstract: One of the most notorious problems with classic perovskite (MAPbI 3 ) is the rapid degradation induced by superoxide radicals (O 2 ˙ − ) generated on the surface under light and O 2 conditions (light/O 2 ).

title: Sb 4 O 5 Cl 2 embedded in carbon polyhedra for fast charge kinetics towards high-capacity Li-ion capacitors
abstract: Sb 4 O 5 Cl 2 embedded in carbon polyhedra is designed for Li-ion capacitors (LIC) using a confine strategy. Owing to the enhanced charge transport and reduced volume expansion, the assembled LIC device exhibits high energy/power density and lifetime.

title: Operando XPS and NEXAFS to link the OER mechanism with the fast electro-oxidation of organic pollutants on a porous NiMnO 3 –rGO anode
abstract: Electro-oxidation is one of the most promising and eco-friendly technologies for water decontamination.

title: Multi-element collaboration in Cr 2 TiAl 1− x Si x C 2 MAX for the oxide barrier formation in a 550 °C LBE environment
abstract: Corrosion of Cr 2 TiAl 1− x Si x C 2 MAX in LBE leads to decomposition, whereas multi-elements promote the formation of protective Al 2 O 3 and Cr 2 O 3 .

title: Drive-throughs to driveways: high-strength composites from multi-material post-consumer waste collected from fast food restaurants
abstract: This study evaluates the use of post-consumer fast-food restaurant waste and elemental sulfur to create high-strength composite materials. Compressive strengths exceed those of C62 building brick and flexural strengths are competitive with OPC.

title: An active and robust La 0.75 Sr 0.25 Cr 0.5 Mn 0.5 O 3 -based fuel electrode coated with in situ grown nanoparticles via electron conduction and oxygen exchange enhancements for solid oxide electrolysis cells
abstract: An SOEC with the LCN infiltrated LSCCM-GDC fuel electrode for CO 2 electrolysis.

title: Conformation-driven exciton transfer in pyrene-sulfone covalent organic frameworks for bifunctional photocatalysis
abstract: We report a new COF consisting of pyrene and sulfone moieties, which reversibly transforms from twisted to planar in polar media and shows conformation-driven exciton transfer kinetics and bifunctional photocatalytic activities of HER and OER.

title: MnTiO 3 as a carbon-free cathode for rechargeable Li–O 2 batteries
abstract: Advancing carbon-free cathodes is vital for enhancing Li–O 2 battery performance. Here, we report for the first time the use of MnTiO 3 as a carbon-free cathode, exhibiting low overpotential and exceptional electrochemical properties.

title: Co-MOF-derived core–shell CoP@Co 3 O 4 nanoparticle loaded N-doped graphene: an efficient catalyst for the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: An N-doped graphene-supported CoP@Co 3 O 4 core–shell structure was prepared by a simple one-step pyrolysis of a novel Co-MOF. The active nanoparticles embedded in the graphene layer ensure high conductivity and stability of the catalyst.

title: Enabling mesopore engineering inside Al-rich MOR zeolites via a sequential fluorination-acid leaching-alkaline treatment strategy
abstract: Hierarchical Al-rich MOR zeolites with an intracrystalline mesopore centered at 10–20 nm were synthesized by a tandem fluorination-acid leaching-alkaline treatment protocol.

title: Metal–organic framework-derived nano-CoS-enhanced photoelectrochemical water splitting performance of the BiVO 4 photoanode
abstract: This study demonstrated a novel metal–organic framework (MOF)-derived strategy for fabricating CoS/BiVO 4 heterostructure photoanodes with exceptional photoelectrocatalytic water splitting performance.

title: Direct electrochemical co-polymerization of EDOT and hydroquinone
abstract: Co-PEDOT–QH 2 films electrochemically polymerized from a solution containing hydroquinone and 3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene are promising electrode materials for energy storage, owing to quinone redox capacity combined with PEDOT conductivity.

title: Zirconium fluoride-supported high-entropy fluoride: a catalyst for enhanced oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: ZrF 4 supported high entropy fluoride catalyst outperforms benchmark catalyst with potential to advance water splitting technologies.

title: Identifying the nanostructure of residual Li in high-Ni cathodes for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Identifying the nanostructure of residual Li in high-Ni cathodes is important for their long-term survival. This paper highlights the in-depth understanding of residual Li in nanoscale and suggests appropriate approaches to control the residual Li.

title: A non-fluorinated, weakly solvating electrolyte for efficient sodium–sulfurized polyacrylonitrile batteries
abstract: A non-fluorinated weakly solvating electrolyte is explored to promote uniform sodium deposition and reversible sulfur redox reactions for high-performance sodium-sulfurized polyacrylonitrile batteries.

title: A superhydrophobic wood aerogel for radiative cooling and sound absorption
abstract: A multifunctional superhydrophobic wood aerogel featuring sound absorption, thermal insulation and radiative cooling.

title: Conjugated polymer nanoparticle-enhanced molecularly imprinted poly( o -phenylenediamine) electrochemical sensors for selective, sensitive and rapid detection of trace perfluorooctanesulfonate
abstract: Perfluorooctanesulfonate (PFOS), a representative persistent organic pollutant, demands rapid and sensitive detection methods to support environmental protection and health risk assessment.

title: Vacuum preparation of charge transport layers for perovskite solar cells and modules
abstract: This review examines various vacuum deposition techniques utilized for the fabrication of charge transport layers (CTLs) in perovskite solar cells and modules, providing an analysis of the advantages, limitations, and thin film characteristics.

title: Scalable solid solution strengthened Li–Ag alloy anodes for high-energy-density lithium batteries
abstract: Schematic illustration of the fabrication process of ultrathin Li–Ag alloy foil.

title: Regulation of transition metal atoms supported on defective h-BN by adjacent monovacancies for electrochemical CO 2 reduction: mechanism and d-band spin-polarization effect
abstract: Introducing adjacent monovacancies can regulate the magnetic moments of transition metal atoms anchored on hexagonal boron nitride, thereby changing their adsorption strength for CO to achieve the regulation of electrochemical CO 2 reduction activity.

title: Lattice variations in CaTiO 3 cubes and cuboids and their use in photocatalytic benzimidazole formation
abstract: Synchrotron X-ray diffraction analysis reveals bulk and surface layer lattices in CaTiO 3 cubes and cuboids. Their lattice variations yield different photocatalytic and piezoelectric responses. The crystals can photocatalyze benzimidazole formation.

title: Recent advances in zwitterionic hydrogels: structure, applications and challenges
abstract: The current structure and diverse applications of zwitterionic hydrogels are summarized, and the properties of hydrogels can be effectively improved through structural optimization.

title: Transformative chelation pathways unveiling NiMOF-LDH hybrids on MgO for high-efficiency photocatalysis
abstract: Tailoring NiMOF growth via EDTA sequencing for enhanced photocatalysis; controlled EDTA addition directed NiMOF or NiMg LDH formation on MgO, where NiMOF exhibited superior crystallinity, stability, and photocatalytic efficiency.

title: Robust cross-linked Na 3 V 2 O 1.6 (PO 4 ) 2 F 1.4 @rGO&MWCNTs as a high-performance cathode for aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: A novel cathode material integrates Na 3 V 2 O 1.6 (PO 4 ) 2 F 1.4 nanoparticles into a 3D dual-carbon conductive matrix, enhancing surface electronic conductivity to improve electrochemical performance.

title: Unveiling high-yield dinitrogen-to-green ammonia production via highly defective Cu 4 Bi 5 S 10 /Mn 4 V 2 O 9 electrocatalysts in alkaline H-cell and stack-cell reactors
abstract: The first report of a water-based PEM stack cell with an eNRR rate of 1711 > 1500 μg h −1 cm −2 . Electrocatalytic dinitrogen reduction with a NH 3 yield rate of 4356 μg h −1 cm −2 and 46.1% faradaic efficiency achieved in an H cell.

title: Recent status, key strategies and challenging perspectives of smart batteries for next-generation batteries
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive overview of the current development of smart batteries, which can be divided into three parts: smart materials, smart manufacturing and smart sensing.

title: Hypercrosslinked polymers (HCPs) for CO 2 capture and catalytic conversion: synthesis, functionalization, and applications
abstract: The escalating concentration of atmospheric carbon dioxide (CO 2 ), primarily driven by fossil fuel consumption, presents critical challenges to environmental sustainability and climate change mitigation.

title: Interfacial engineering of Z-scheme CsPbBr 3 /BiOCl heterojunction via solvothermal ion exchange for enhanced CO 2 photoreduction
abstract: An intimate CsPbBr 3 /BiOCl Z-scheme heterojunction is developed via solvothermal ion exchange method with strong interfacial interactions and modulation of optoelectronic properties enhance charge transfer and CO 2 photoreduction efficiency.

title: Support-facet-mediated metal–support interaction regulation of Pt-based catalysts for efficient dehydrogenation
abstract: A methylcyclohexane (MCH)/toluene (TOL) system is considered a promising candidate for large-scale green hydrogen storage, and catalytic MCH dehydrogenation is the key reaction for effective system recycling.

title: Modifying Li 2 B 12 H 12 to become a highly electrochemically stable solid electrolyte
abstract: A highly stable solid electrolyte from the partial thermal decomposition of Li 2 B 12 H 12 at 380 °C.

title: Immobilizing t BP via tailor-made π-conjugated hole transport materials for efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: X87 is a novel π-conjugated hole transport material (HTM) featuring an SFX core and anthracene substituents. Its planar structure enhances π–π interactions with t BP, enabling PSCs to achieve 24.07% PCE and excellent long-term stability.

title: Two-dimensional Janus BeSeCl as a potential anode material for sodium- and potassium-ion batteries
abstract: Highly effective anode materials play an essential role in constructing and commercializing metal-ion batteries with greater energy and power density.

title: Construction of a graphene/cellulose aerogel embedded with UiO-66-CN for highly efficient uranium capture via electro-adsorption
abstract: UiO-66-CN/GCA was demonstrated to be a promising material for highly efficient electro-adsorption of uranium in seawater, achieving an impressive 110.1 mg g −1 of adsorption capacity in natural seawater in only 24 h.

title: Competition of single-atom Cu and Ni catalysts anchored on boron-doped graphdiyne for highly efficient oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Single-atom catalysts (TM/B x -GDY) are rationally designed for highly efficient OER, especially for the competition of Ni/B4-GDY via AEM and Cu/B6-GDY via OCM.

title: Enhancing zinc-ion energy storage: impact of MOF-5 derived carbonization temperature on performance
abstract: We report the carbonization of metal–organic framework-5 (MOF-5) at various temperatures to investigate the correlation between physicochemical properties and electrochemical performance in zinc-ion energy storage.

title: Boosting the HER/OER bifunctional electrocatalytic activity of Ni–Cu alloy via water-containing deep eutectic solvent system
abstract: A bifunctional Ni–Cu/CF catalyst electrode were developed via one-step electrodeposition within a water-containing ChCl/EG DES system. The electrode showed efficient water-splitting performance, requiring a low cell voltage of 1.86 V at 50 mA cm −2 .

title: Interfacial heterophase BaTa 2 O 6 :N with spatial CoO x /Pt cocatalysts for stable water oxidation in pH-unadjusted Fe 3+ redox media toward solar energy storage
abstract: Interfacial engineering of N-doped BaTa 2 O 6 heterophase junctions with spatially separated CoO x /Pt cocatalysts enables acid-stable solar water oxidation and Fe 3+ /Fe 2+ -mediated energy storage for the hydrogen farm strategy.

title: A review of machine learning applications in polymer composites: advancements, challenges, and future prospects
abstract: Machine learning can facilitate the development of next-generation high-performance polymer composites with superior mechanical strength, durability, and environmental sustainability.

title: Engineering heterointerface and composition for ultrathin broadband microwave absorbers based on high entropy oxide/alloy heterostructured composites
abstract: High-entropy spinel/ferroalloy heterostructured composites were synthesized, which exhibited superior MW absorption via rational composition and heterointerface engineering enabling synergistic multiple-loss mechanisms.

title: A cobalt single-atom catalyst with ultralow metal loading for activation of peroxymonosulfate to generate singlet oxygen with almost 100% selectivity
abstract: Compared with the metal-free catalyst (N–C), the Co single-atom catalyst (Co–N–C) with 0.34 wt% of metal loading exhibits significantly enhanced PMS activation to generate 1 O 2 with almost 100% selectivity.

title: Functional carbon-based covalent bridging bonds unlocking superior sodium-ion storage
abstract: This review focuses on the transformative role of the carbon-based covalent bridging bonds in the field of sodium-ion batteries, providing valuable insights for advancing the next-generation high-performance sodium-ion batteries.

title: Solar driven conversion of agricultural biomass to H 2 over few-layer MoS 2 modified ultra-small TiO 2 nanoparticle photocatalysts
abstract: Few-layer MoS 2 modified ultra-small TiO 2 nanoparticles were developed as highly efficient photocatalysts for photocatalytic H 2 production from agricultural biomass.

title: SiO x enhances prelithiation kinetics and homogeneity in graphite-based anodes
abstract: In the SiO x /Gr anode, lithium is preferentially absorbed by the SiO x domains during prelithiation with the participation of the electrolyte, and the subsequent diffusion of Li + into graphite establishes a uniform lithium gradient.

title: Electrochemically activated copper-based catalyst from a coordination polymer for stable hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: A novel strategy for developing highly stable hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) catalysts with aligned Cu 2+ /Cu + /Cu 0 active sites by electrochemically activating a copper-based coordination polymer (CP).

title: Acid-etched serpentine separator with hierarchical porosity and dual-functional polysulfide regulation for high-performance lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: HCl-treated serpentine enhances porosity and active sites for effective polysulfide adsorption, improving reaction kinetics and Li + transport for superior Li–S battery performance.

title: In situ conversion of delithiated residues into Na 4 Fe 3 (PO 4 ) 2 P 2 O 7 towards upcycling of spent lithium-ion batteries
abstract: An upcycling process was developed based on the in situ conversion of de-lithiation residues into single-crystal sodium-ion cathode materials through crystallographic phase reconstruction.

title: Design of novel SnIX (X = Br/Cl) Janus layers: electronic, optical, and photocatalytic properties, as well as defect and strain engineering
abstract: Novel SnIX (X = Br/Cl) Janus layers (JLs) for the photocatalytic water splitting hydrogen production.

title: The synergistic effect of Ni and WO 2 effectively boosts the electrochemical reduction of nitrite to ammonia
abstract: Ni nanoparticles deposited on the WO 2 polyhedron self-supported Ni foam (Ni@WO 2 /NF) acts as an efficient electrocatalyst for selective NO 2 − reduction to NH 3 , offering an NH 3 yield rate of ∼17 959.3 μg h −1 cm −2 and a faradaic efficiency of ∼94.6%.

title: Efficient photocatalytic degradation of an insoluble indigo dye by a CdS nanorod-embedded porous polymer film
abstract: Porous polymer films with CdS nanorods, fabricated via a modified breath figure method, showed enhanced visible-light photocatalytic degradation of insoluble indigo dye due to a microreactor effect that confines dye molecules near catalytic surfaces.

title: Plasmonic copper nanoparticle aggregates for near-infrared light-driven hydrogen evolution
abstract: The aggregation of copper nanoparticles synergistically broadens light absorption and enhances charge separation, enabling efficient hydrogen production under red/NIR light.

title: Visualizing SO 2 gas via selective adsorption–desorption by an α,β-unsaturated iminium salt
abstract: A strategy has been developed to separate SO 2 gas from other toxic gases. The cheletropic reaction between silica-supported α,β-unsaturated iminium salt LI@MOS with SO 2 gas showed reversible adsorption–desorption as high as 20 mmolg −1 .

title: Tandem catalysis for nitrate reduction to ammonia boosted by CoFe-layered double hydroxide on Cu 2+1 O nanowires in neutral media
abstract: Electrochemical conversion of nitrate (NO 3 − ) in wastewater to valuable ammonia (NH 3 ) under ambient conditions is a facile and competitive route for nitrate remediation.

title: BiVO 4 /Bi:NiO/Co–Ci photoanode and BiVO 4 –Cu 3 BiS 3 tandem cell for unbiased solar hydrogen evolution and simultaneous urea oxidation
abstract: Integration with a Cu 3 BiS 3 photocathode enables a BiVO 4 –Cu 3 BiS 3 tandem cell for unbiased, solar-driven, coupled urea oxidation and hydrogen evolution, delivering a photocurrent density of 2.0 mA cm −2 and stable operation exceeding 20 hours.

title: Vermiculite reshaped nickel-based hydrotalcite derived composites efficiently drive methane dry reforming
abstract: Vermiculite reshaped nickel-based hydrotalcite derived composites efficiently drive methane dry reforming.

title: Constructing multiple microstructures in Sb 2 Te 3 -based thermoelectrics enables a high-performance segmented power generator
abstract: High In doping suppresses antisite defects, while Zn co-doping induces microstructures for efficient electron–phonon scattering. Notably, a 17 × 17 segmented generator achieves 9.25% conversion effciency under a 350 K temperature gradient.

title: Electrocatalytic hydrogenation of unsaturated organics using Mo and W porous carbon-encapsulated nanostructures: impact of metal type on properties and performances
abstract: N-doped carbon-encapsulated W and Mo carbide/nitride nanocomposites were synthesized. Their performances in the electrocatalytic hydrogenation (ECH) of benzaldehyde suggest that W@C:N architectures are competitive materials for ECH applications.

title: Suppressing the penetration of 2D perovskites for enhanced stability of perovskite solar cells
abstract: As conventional surface passivation may damage perovskite films, fluorinated PEAI with different substitution positions was studied. The m-FPEAI shows the strongest steric hindrance, thus suppressing penetration and improving device performance.

title: Revealing the synergistic role of the CoCu alloy and CN derived from a bimetallic MOF in promoting photoelectrocatalytic water oxidation
abstract: The Fe 2 O 3 @CoCu/CN composite photoanode by calcination of bimetallic CoCu-MOFs loaded on Fe 2 O 3 nanoarrays was constructed for highly efficient photoelectrocatalytic water decomposition.

title: Recent advances in MBenes for Li/Na-ion batteries: from synthesis to application
abstract: This review examines MBenes for their structural versatility and electrochemical performance in Li/Na-ion batteries, while addressing synthesis challenges and outlining future research directions for next-generation energy storage.

title: Tough silicone-based elastomers enhanced by synergistic Zn( ii )–carboxylate interactions and weak hydrogen bonds between incompatible soft segments
abstract: A silicone-based elastomer with superior mechanical properties is developed by optimizing Zn( ii )–carboxylate interactions and weak hydrogen bonds between PDMS and PPG.

title: Computational design of polaronic conductive Li-NASICON mixed ionic–electronic conductors
abstract: The polaron-conductive Li-NASICONs featuring three-dimensional corner-sharing frameworks are potential candidates for mixed ionic electronic conductors in electrochemical energy storage devices.

title: Unveiling the photocatalytic potential of two-dimensional ferroelastic LuSX monolayers for efficient water splitting: a first-principles discovery
abstract: Two-dimensional ferroelastic materials can be utilized to enhance photocatalytic applications.

title: Stabilizing *CO intermediate on nitrogen-doped carbon-coated Cu x O y derived from metal–organic framework for enhanced electrochemical CO 2 -to-ethylene
abstract: A nitrogen-doped carbon-coated Cu x O y catalyst derived from metal–organic framework exhibits excellent performance toward electrochemical CO 2 to ethylene through stabilizing the *CO intermediate.

title: Boosting catalytic activity by using the interfacial electric field of VN–V 2 O 3 heterogeneous nanoparticles for efficient lithium polysulfide conversion
abstract: The VN–V 2 O 3 nanoparticles with interfacial electric feld accelerate the conversion of LiPSs.

title: Zeolitic imidazolate framework-enhanced conductive nanocomposite hydrogels with high stretchability and low hysteresis for self-powered multifunctional sensors
abstract: A conductive nanocomposite hydrogel, enhanced by zeolitic imidazolate frameworks, exhibits high stretchability and low hysteresis, providing potential for applications in self-powered sensors.

title: Light stimulation enhanced detection of NO at ppb-level at room temperature using MoS 2 /WSe 2 /GaN heterostructure sensor
abstract: With rapid technological advancement, there is a strong demand for developing efficient and precise gas sensing systems for monitoring toxic gases such as nitrogen oxides and ubiquitous pollutants from industrial and vehicular emissions.

title: A highly moisture-stable ferroelectric ammonium phosphate salt showing piezoelectric energy harvesting and rotation sensing applications
abstract: A water-stable ferroelectric compound DIPA·BNPP was shown to exhibit high piezoelectric energy conversion efficiency for its polymer composite. It shows LED lighting and RPM sensing applications due to the high device sensitivity and responsivity.

title: One-pot synthesis of amorphous aluminosilicates with tunable aluminum coordination for enhanced cracking performance
abstract: Amorphous aluminosilicates represent a class of important solid acid catalysts. Herein, a series of amorphous aluminosilicate catalysts with tunable Si/Al molar ratios were synthesized for efficient catalytic cracking of cumene.

title: Samarium ion-induced interfacial regulation to construct high-performance anodes for aqueous zinc metal batteries
abstract: The introduction of the SmCl 3 additive suppresses dendrite formation and side reactions by adjusting the electric double layer and occupying the protruding active sites on the anodes. The symmetrical and full batteries show good performance.

title: Machine learning-assisted exploration of the interfacial valence electron fitting rule for MBene-based single-atom catalysts
abstract: Valence electron fitting rules for OER intermediate adsorption, serving as a correction to the d-band center theory. Machine learning-assisted exploration of activity origins and adsorption energy descriptor construction for MBene-type SACs.

title: Hybrid SnO 2 /g-C 3 N 4 layers with plasma-induced modifications for enhanced charge transport in perovskite solar cells
abstract: Plasma was used to transform the randomly sized & oriented graphitic carbon nitride (GCN) nanosheet into size uniform, functionalized GCN nanoparticles which was used as an effective defect passivating agent for ETL in perovskite solar cells.

title: Anode|electrolyte|cathode interface engineering to develop a robust zinc metal hydrogel battery
abstract: A dual-interface engineered cathode integrated on a glass fibre separator serves as the cathode, electrolyte, separator, and artificial SEI on the Zn anode, enabling a stable rechargeable Zn metal hydrogel battery.

title: Over-discharge-induced capacity degradation mechanisms in large-capacity semi-solid-state lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Over-discharge aging of large-format semi-solid-state Li-ion batteries is dominated by cathode cracking and CEI growth, which can be quantitatively tracked by ultrasonic attenuation.

title: (NH 4 ) 2 HPO 4 -mediated closed-pore/pseudographite synergy in thin-walled hard carbon for enhanced Na + storage and kinetics
abstract: N/P-Doped microlayered hard carbon with expanded interlayer spacings and rich closed-pores was fabricated, affording abundant sodium storage sites and multi-level Na + diffusion highways, achieving superior capacity and rate performance.

title: Macrocycle-containing metal–organic frameworks in adsorption and related applications
abstract: A periodic summary is given for the current development stage of the adsorbents constructed using macrocycle-containing metal–organic frameworks.

title: Sulfur-based hybrid multilayers on Li metal anodes with excellent air stability for ultralong-life and high-performance batteries
abstract: The Al-DMP hybrid multilayers improve electrolyte wettability and lithiophilicity, resulting in superior cycling performance. The integration of a superlattice structure significantly enhances air stability, maintaining stability for 60 hours.

title: Controllable dispersion of nickel phthalocyanine molecules on graphene oxide for efficient electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: Adjusting the NiPc/GO ratio during the hydrolysis of protonated NiPc in the GO-assisted reaction enables the dispersion of Ni–N 4 –C active sites, forming a Ni( ii )/Ni( iii )/GO heterojunction with a FE CO of 98.6% for the electrocatalytic CO 2 RR.

title: A Keggin-type polyoxometalate/COF thin separator for improving the performance of lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: POMs/DTQ-COF composites were prepared by post-synthesis strategy and applied to the improvement of lithium–sulfur battery separator. The electrochemical performance of the battery was effectively improved.

title: Charge separation engineering via CoAl 2 O 4 /ZnCdS heterojunction and d-band center modulation for synergistically enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: Nanoflower-shaped CoAl 2 O 4 /ZnCdS photocatalyst achieves efficient solar hydrogen production.

title: Facile synthesis of Ru atom clusters on MXene nanosheets through gamma-ray radiation for plasma boosting hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Ru/Ti 3 C 2 T x synthesized through gamma-ray radiation exhibited enhanced hydrogen evolution reaction activity under the illumination of near infrared light across all pH ranges.

title: MoSe 2 embedded in (NiCo)Se 2 nanosheets to form heterostructure materials for high stability supercapacitors and efficient hydrogen evolution
abstract: A 3D heterojunction of (NiCo)Se 2 /MoSe 2 /NF with staggered nanosheet coverage is constructed. It exhibits expected energy density (55.99 Wh kg −1 ) and HER performance with an overpotential of 71.2 mV at 10 mA cm −2 and a Tafel slope of 67.3 mV dec −1 .

title: A self-assembled nanozyme featuring precise active centers and topography exhibits controlled catalytic interplay with mitochondrial protein while regulating electron flow during bioinspired oxygen reduction
abstract: This work demonstrates a highly controlled electron transfer process at the interface of a self-assembled nanozyme featuring a well-defined active site and a redox protein cytochrome c, enabling bioinspired oxygen reduction.

title: Boosting surface reconstruction in engineered nickel sites through selenium vacancies to enhance urea oxidation reaction
abstract: Selenium vacancies in the Se vac @NiSe-NS catalyst promote surface reconstruction for enhanced urea oxidation reaction.

title: Accelerated screening of water-stable MOF structures using the digital reticular chemistry method
abstract: Using machine learning, an optimal model was developed based on a novel set of descriptors and a relatively small dataset, demonstrating excellent performance, generalization ability, and reliability.

title: A data-driven machine learning approach for interpretable prediction of desalination stability of carbon materials for capacitive deionization
abstract: Eight features were employed to predict the desalination stability of carbon materials using different ML models. Experimental validation confirmed the reliability of the models, demonstrating the effectiveness of ML in the CDI field.

title: X BP index as a predictive tool for fast-charging performance of artificial graphite anodes in lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A structural descriptor X BP was proposed to establish the relationship between the molecular structure of the precursor and the kinetics of lithium storage, providing a new strategy for the design of fast-charging anodes.

title: A bifunctional Na-deficient strategy induced pure phase Na 4− x Fe 3 (PO 4 ) 2 P 2 O 7 cathode with high capacity for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: This study proposes a Na-deficient strategy for the structural modulation of NFPP, successfully synthesizing pure-phase Na 4− x Fe 3 (PO 4 ) 2 (P 2 O 7 ) with a near-theoretical specific capacity of 127.2 mA h g −1 at 0.1C.

title: Light-induced degradation of methylammonium tin iodide absorber layers
abstract: An Sn-based perovskite was synthesized with no detectable Sn(+4). Upon illumination in vacuum for 61 h, it was shown that SnI 2 is formed at the surface of the absorber with traces of Sn(0) and no Sn(+4).

title: Continuous and reversible tuning of inter-layer spacings in two-dimensional conductive metal organic frameworks
abstract: The removal and exchange of solvents in two-dimensional metal organic frameworks enabled reversible tuning of their stacking distances. Surprisingly, no correlation was noted between stacking distances and their charge transport properties.

title: Solar-driven dehydrogenation and dehydration of formate to syngas with near-zero CO 2 emission
abstract: The heterojunction of Co 3 O 4 –CdS shows dual functions of photocatalytic dehydrogenation and dehydration to simultaneously produce syngas (H 2 and CO) from formate, with near zero CO 2 emission under alkaline conditions.

title: Customized structural reconstruction for an IrO x catalyst using Ni–Co dual coordination towards enhanced water electrolysis in PEM electrolyzers
abstract: A customized structural reconstruction strategy is developed to tackle the activity-stability paradox of the IrO x electrocatalyst for acid water electrolysis.

title: Prediction of high photoconversion efficiency and photocatalytic water splitting in vertically stacked TMD heterojunctions MX 2 /WS 2 and MX 2 /MoSe 2 (M = Cr, Mo, W; X = S, Se, Te)
abstract: By constructing MX 2 /WS 2 and MX 2 /MoSe 2 (M = Cr, Mo, W; X = S, Se, Te) heterojunctions, promising candidates with high photoconversion efficiency and excellent photocatalytic water-splitting properties have been identified.

title: Enhanced air stability by calcium doping in Na 2/3 [Fe 1/2 Mn 1/2 ]O 2 cathode material for Na-ion batteries
abstract: Ca doping enhances air stability by forming a Ca-rich surface region, which suppresses Na + /H + exchange and helps retain electrochemical performance after air exposure.

title: Structural and electronic tunability of Ruddlesden–Popper oxyfluorides through nickel–copper substitution in La 2 Ni 1− x Cu x O 2.5 F 3 (0 ≤ x ≤ 1)
abstract: Tunability of the band gap energy is achieved for the highly fluorinated system La 2 Ni 1− x Cu x O 2.5 F 3 while retaining the overall structural distortion.

title: Constructing highly selective multidirectional hydrogel strain sensors with a pre-stretching strategy
abstract: A hydrogel strain sensor with a wrinkle-crack bilayer conductive structure achieves ultrahigh anisotropic selectivity (7.93) and sensitivity (GF = 575.51), enabling multidirectional human motion detection and sign language recognition.

title: Emerging multimetal LMFP-based cathodes for lithium-ion batteries: a review
abstract: Multimetal LMFP cathodes offer enhanced conductivity, stability, and energy density for lithium-ion batteries. This review outlines doping, structural integration, and entropy strategies to overcome key limitations and guide future developments.

title: Unlocking the potential of semi-transparent Ta 3 N 5 photoelectrodes for high-performance, reproducible solar redox flow cells
abstract: Semi-transparent Ta 3 N 5 photoelectrodes are fabricated by electrophoretic deposition over an optimized Ti-doped TiO 2 underlayer, and display a high photocurrent density of ca. 4.0 mA cm −2 and a maximum power density of ca. 1.1 mW cm −2 at 1.0 V RHE .

title: Efficient bifunctional water splitting catalysts enabled by crystalline–amorphous Ni x S y @NiFe LDH heterojunctions
abstract: This work developed a crystalline–amorphous Ni x S y @NiFe LDH heterojunction with outstanding activity for both the OER and the HER. As a result, the water splitting cell achieved 10 mA cm −2 at 1.48 V, outperforming noble metal Pt/C/NF‖RuO 2 /NF.

title: Achieving high-performance OER catalysis with dual-site modulated Fe-based perovskites
abstract: Improving OER activity of Fe-based double perovskite through dual-site modulation.

title: Single-step electrodeposition of CNT/GO–Zn composites for enhanced stability in AFLBs
abstract: Lithium nucleation on 1D and 2D carbon nanostructure composite zinc layers.

title: Highly active and selective dual-atom modified MXene catalysts for carbon dioxide reduction to ethanol
abstract: The CO 2 reduction to ethanol properties of dual-atoms anchored on Ti 2 CO 2 are explored. Co–Fe/Ti 2 CO 2 emerges as optimal catalyst, exhibiting superior ethanol selectivity/activity via Co electronic delocalization for enhanced intermediate adsorption.

title: Advancements in thermoelectric hydrogels: structural design and material innovation for biomedical and wearable applications
abstract: Thermoelectrics (TEs), enabling the direct conversion between heat and electrical energy, have demonstrated extensive application potential in wearable and biomedical fields.

title: Dual-reaction-centre enabled concurrent N–C–N coupling for effective urea electrosynthesis
abstract: Dual-reaction-centre catalysts PcTM 1 –O 8 –TM 2 facilitate urea electrosynthesis from NO and CO 2 via concurrent N–C–N coupling. The independent reaction centres, TMN 4 and TMO 4 , can be individually optimized for enhanced performance.

title: A novel biopolymer-based omniphobic coating system functionalized with polysilazane for anti-smudge, chemical shielding, and bacterial anti-adhesion applications
abstract: A biopolymer-based omniphobic coating system was developed using polysilazane as an effective omniphobic agent, and the obtained omniphobic coatings demonstrated anti-smudging, chemical shielding, and bacterial anti-adhesion properties.

title: A deep eutectic solvent-based semi-interpenetrating polymer electrolyte for high-voltage stable lithium-metal batteries
abstract: This study presents a facile approach to fabricating ethyl cyanoacrylate based semi-interpenetrating polymer network (SIPN) structured SPEs. The SIPN based SPEs matched with high voltage cathodes still exhibit excellent cycling performance.

title: A nanoconfinement-driven interface boosts zinc deposition kinetics toward dendrite-free zinc anodes
abstract: Dendrite growth and side reactions are two major obstacles for aqueous zinc-ion batteries (AZIBs) in the field of stationary energy storage.

title: Highly conductive PANI/ATMP/AgNO 3 composite hydrogel electrodes for all-hydrogel-state supercapacitors
abstract: Highly conductive PANI/ATMP/AgNO 3 composite hydrogel electrodes for flexible high energy density supercapacitors.

title: Solid–liquid phase change materials meet hydrogels: syntheses and multifunctional applications
abstract: Phase change hydrogels (PCHs), used in multiple engineering scenarios, have been developed to overcome leakage, structural instability, and low thermal conductivity of solid–liquid phase-change materials.

title: Nanoporous biocarbon functionalised with crystalline FeS nanostructures as a high-performance anode for lithium-ion batteries and insights into its Li storage mechanism
abstract: This paper highlights a novel concept for incorporating FeS into the high surface area carbon matrix by utilizing dithiooxamide and iron acetate, which leads to improved performance in lithium-ion storage.

title: Dual-functional surface of MXene anodes boosts long-term cyclability of lithium-metal batteries
abstract: The dual-functional Au@F-rich Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene anode synergistically enhances lithium deposition and cycle stability in lithium metal batteries through lithiophilic seed effects and LiF-rich SEI formation.

title: Benzothiophene-based covalent organic frameworks for H 2 O 2 electrosynthesis: the critical role of conjugated structure
abstract: This study uncovered the underlying impact mechanism of conjugation size in COFs on H 2 O 2 selectivity in H 2 O 2 electrosynthesis.

title: Liquid–liquid interfacial approach for the facile synthesis of Tp–NBD COFs for efficient photocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: A β-ketoenamine-linked 2D COF with a non-planar amine monomer is reported for photocatalytic H 2 evolution. It exhibits a high surface area (677.5 m 2 g −1 ) and HER activity of 21 mmol g −1 h −1 , demonstrating excellent efficiency and stability.

title: Free-standing graphene films decorated with lithiophilic particles as host electrodes for lithium plating/stripping in anode-less lithium metal batteries
abstract: Graphene serves as a lithium host, lithiophilic particles lead to the formation a LiF- and Li 3 N-rich SEI, and the Mg–Li alloy (LA103Z) foil compensates for lithium loss, guaranteeing highly reversible lithium plating/stripping behaviors.

title: Boosting oxygen evolution via lattice oxygen activation in high-entropy perovskite oxides
abstract: A novel high-entropy perovskite oxide (LaPrSr)(FeCoNi)O 3 exhibited excellent oxygen evolution reaction (OER) performance via a lattice oxygen-mediated mechanism (LOM), which underscoring its potential for advanced energy conversion applications.

title: Coordination engineering of Pd single-atom catalysts for non-radical organohalide degradation
abstract: Synthesize high-valent Pd single-atom catalysts through coordination environmental modulation to boost the degradation of organohalide contaminants via nonradical catalyzation.

title: Polythieno[3,4- b ]pyrazine: pathways to metallic charge transport
abstract: For the first time, an alkyl-chain free variant of polythieno[3,4- b ]pyrazine (PTP) was created in a conductive state via oxidative chemical vapour deposition (oCVD).

title: Surface-modified cellulose for CO 2 hydrate control: molecular insights and sustainable inhibitor design
abstract: Novel cellulose-based materials were synthesized and evaluated as environmentally friendly CO 2 hydrate inhibitors through combined experimental and molecular dynamics approaches.

title: Enhancing ionic conductivity in Li 6+ x Ge x P 1− x S 5 Br: impact of Li + substructure on ionic transport and solid-state battery performance
abstract: Substitution of Ge( iv ) for P( v ) in Li 6 PS 5 Br presents additional Li + sites and expanding cage-like Li + substructure. As catholyte it presents enhanced transport properties in solid-state battery cathodes.

title: Quinone-annulated imidazolium salts as dual electrolyte-sorbents for electrochemical capture of carbon dioxide
abstract: Molecular design of ionic materials that can act as both reactive sorbents and electrolytes in redox-swing CO 2 capture systems.

title: Conductive TiN network-assisted fast-charging of lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The partial conversion of TiN nanoparticles allows the formation of conductive Li 3 N–TiN clusters, enhancing the fast-charging capability of graphite.

title: TiO 2 nanotube-coated hierarchical SiC nanowires as novel electrode materials with enhanced electrochemical performances for supercapacitors
abstract: Original porous nanostructure with coral-like TiO 2 NTs grown on hierarchical SiC NWs for good corrosion resistance, conductivity and large specific surface was constructed by hydrothermal method. It is a promising electrode material for supercapacitor.

title: Nanorod-assembled urchin-like molybdenum–manganese oxide heterostructure with enhanced oxygen vacancies as a cathode for quasi solid state zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Nanorod-assembled urchin-like MoO 3 –MnO 2 cathode materials are fabricated using MoO 3 nanobelts as the starting material through a self-assembly strategy.

title: Anti-freezing polyacrylamide hydrogel electrolyte for rapid response self-powered electrochromic devices
abstract: A frost-resistant hydrogel electrolyte with high ionic conductivity at −40 °C has been developed for wearable Zn//WO 3− x electrochromic devices.

title: A novel strategy for the reduction of coordinated water in Prussian blue analogues for their application as cathode materials for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: In this study, we report a new strategy to obtain excellent sodium storage performance utilizing ethylene glycol for ligand pre-exchange substitution of H 2 O molecules in [Fe(H 2 O)] 2+ to reduce the H 2 O molecules introduced during the synthesis of PBAs.

title: Targeted tuning of water adsorption at P / P 0 = 0.2 in multivariate metal–organic frameworks for boosting water-sorption-driven refrigeration
abstract: We report a strategy of optimizing the content and distribution of nitrogen sites in MOFs by a multivariate strategy to precisely improve water uptake at P / P 0 = 0.2, thus boosting the cooling performance for refrig-2 applications.

title: Optimizing membrane structure through interfacial instability of a two-component system to increase mechanical and separation performance
abstract: A bimodal fiber was successfully prepared by adding PVP to reduce the interaction between the two components, which had good emulsion separation ability and antifouling effect.

title: Anion exchange polymer membrane (AEPM)-based separators: an unexplored frontier for ZAB and AAB systems
abstract: We explored and summarized AEPMs as suitable separators for ZABs and AABs. The specific rationales behind the shortened lifespan of the anode have been addressed. The role of the AEPM in mitigating the issues is also discussed.

title: Mapping the configuration space of half-Heusler compounds via subspace identification for thermoelectric materials discovery
abstract: New high-throughput search strategies based on statistical identification of promising chemical subspaces show potential for accelerating half-Heusler thermoelectric materials discovery.

title: Inducing n-type photoanodic behavior in p-type bismuth ferrite via ferroelectric polarization
abstract: This research highlights the significant potential ferroelectric polarization possesses to readily, rapidly and potentially reversibly alter the p/n behavior of a BFO photoelectrode under mild treatment conditions.

title: Si anodes for Li + batteries: what is the ideal structure?
abstract: Quantitative governing equations are proposed to correlate structural features with performance as guiding principles to design porous p-Si structures for Li ion battery anodes.

title: Highly ordered and high-aspect-ratio Au nanopatterns directly fabricated on Cu for efficient anode-less Li metal batteries
abstract: Vertical aligned high-aspect-ratio Au hole-cylinder nanopatterns on Cu. The 3D topology guides Li nucleation, suppresses dendrites, and with Ag alloying boosts full-cell coulombic efficiency in lean-electrolyte anode-less Li-metal batteries.

title: Enhancing performance of lithium metal batteries through acoustic field application
abstract: The graphic illustrates how an external acoustic field stabilizes the SEI layer by enhancing lithium-ion mass transfer at slip lines and kinks, reducing pit formation and promoting a more uniform SEI, ultimately improving battery performance.

title: Light-induced degradation of mixed-cation, mixed-halide perovskite: observed rates and influence of oxygen
abstract: The kinetics of light-induced Pb 0 formation in mixed-cation, mixed-halide perovskite films are studied using sub-bandgap absorption measurements. Oxygen is shown to prevent Pb 0 formation and modulate the optoelectronic performance decline.

title: Sustainable and recyclable ionogels for ionic thermoelectric supercapacitor application
abstract: This work develops a recyclable ionogel with a dynamic cross-linked network. BMIM:Cl and CaCl 2 enhance thermoelectric properties, achieving −30.4 mV K −1 and 142.8 mV under 5 °C, advancing sustainable energy.

title: Efficient solar-assisted seawater splitting in alkaline solution using perovskite–graphene–Si tandem integration
abstract: Schematic illustration for the PEC seawater splitting of the Ni–Fe LDH/Gr/Si HJT device.

title: Enhancing photocatalysis in conjugated microporous polymers via π-bridge engineering between pyrene and thiazolothiazole
abstract: With a donor–π–acceptor architecture, the Py-π-TzTz-CMP photocatalyst exhibits superior performance in aerobic hydroxylation of phenylboronic acid to phenol under green light irradiation.

title: A polymer binder with amine-facilitated lithium-ion transfer, enabling high-loading silicon-based anodes
abstract: The unique amine-containing polymer binder significantly accelerates Li + transport and mechanical strength through nitrogen–lithium interactions. It provides a reliable guarantee for long-term battery use, outperforming traditional binders.

title: Rapid fabrication of selective barriers through layer-by-layer self-assembly of non-stoichiometric polyelectrolyte complexes for salt and dye fractionation
abstract: Layer-by-layer self-assembly of oppositely charged non-stoichiometric polyelectrolyte complexes enables rapid formation of molecular selective membranes for high-efficiency fractionation of salts and dyes.

title: Forged by charge: polaron-induced matrix formation in silicon nitride conversion-type anodes for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Charge-trapping in silicon nitride anodes govern structural transformations during the initial lithiation.

title: Dual protective layer on lithium metal anodes for improved electrochemical performance – in-depth morphological characterization
abstract: Dual protective layers on the surface of lithium metal electrodes can largely enhance stability and cycle life.

title: Correction: Band gap and photo charge carrier tailoring in zirconium doped carbon nitride using ZrCl 4 –DMF–melamine for photocatalytic degradation of rhodamine B
abstract: Correction for ‘Band gap and photo charge carrier tailoring in zirconium doped carbon nitride using ZrCl 4 –DMF–melamine for photocatalytic degradation of rhodamine B’ by Chinathun Pinming et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA09247C.

title: Expression of concern: Preparation of reduced graphene oxide–Ni(OH) 2 composites by electrophoretic deposition: application for non-enzymatic glucose sensing
abstract: Expression of concern for ‘Preparation of reduced graphene oxide–Ni(OH) 2 composites by electrophoretic deposition: application for non-enzymatic glucose sensing’ by Palaniappan Subramanian et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2014, 2 , 5525–5533, https://doi.org/10.1039/C4TA00123K.

title: Phase separation dynamics in sodium solid-state batteries with Na–K liquid anodes
abstract: Alkali metal anodes hold great promise for high-energy-density batteries for grid-scale applications.

title: Regulating the local electronic structure to design a reliable dual-active site organic anode compatible with high-performance lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Three organic electrode active materials (MnPA, SnPA, and Fe 2 PA 3 ) with reasonable structural designs for high-performance LIBs were reported in this study.

title: Degradation of yttria-stabilized zirconia electrolytes: impact of NiO dissolution with and without phase transformation
abstract: NiO dissolution into 8YSZ during fabrication of solid oxide cells accelerates degradation. Distinct pathways of oxide ion diffusivity degradation of 8YSZ are revealed, with temperature-dependent effects on phase transformation and Ni precipitation.

title: Stepwise optimization strategy of ferroelectric–paraelectric laminated ceramics
abstract: A stepwise optimization strategy, integrating layer ratio adjustment, linearity modulation, and interface strengthening, is proposed, providing guidance for designing high-performance ferroelectric–paraelectric laminated energy-storage ceramics.

title: A layered and highly porous poly-ionic liquid-based membrane as a gel polymer electrolyte for rechargeable lithium-ion batteries
abstract: An optimized gel polymer electrolyte membrane with a unique layered structure enables high ionic conductivity, wide electrochemical stability, and long-cycling LiFePO 4 battery performance.

title: A nontoxic, high-voltage zinc–bromine battery utilizing multi-oxidation-state bromine (Br − /BrO − /BrO 3 − ) redox chemistry
abstract: Due to safety issues in Li-ion batteries, Zn–Br batteries are a promising alternative. Our Br − /BrO − /BrO 3 − triple redox system achieves 2.2 mAh cm −2 , 83% retention after 700 cycles, and a >2.0 V discharge voltage.

title: Structured droplets dominated by interfacial self-assembly of topology-tunable Janus particles towards macroscopic materials
abstract: Structured macro-droplets, stabilized by self-assembled and jammed hemispherical Janus particles at water–oil interfaces, enable scalable fabrication of multi-functional granular materials, e.g. , magnetic/fluorescent capsules.

title: Accurately tuning the pore size and acidity of mesoporous zeolites for enhancing the catalytic hydrocracking of polypropylene
abstract: Al-MCM-41, characterized by its moderately acidic mesoporous nanosphere structure, serves as an effective catalyst for the hydrocracking of polypropylene, achieving a high liquid fuel yield of 70.23% at a relatively low temperature (220 °C).

title: Constructing high-performance zinc metal anodes based on an amino-functionalized highly three-dimensional porous structure interface layer
abstract: Aqueous zinc ion batteries (AZIBs) are one of the most well-liked power source systems at present because of their low price and elevated security.

title: Ultrasonically induced liquid-phase exfoliation boosts excellent performance of Bi 0.5 Sb 1.5 Te 3 /PVDF thermoelectric films
abstract: High thermoelectric performance and excellent cooling performance of flexible Bi 2 Te 3 based films have been achieved via flaky Bi 2 Te 3 powders with weak surface oxidation, prepared by ultrasonically induced liquid-phase exfoliation.

title: An insight into the electrochemical performance of lithium-ion battery anodes via an O/N bifunctional group strategy in Janus MoB
abstract: By correlating atomic-scale charge redistribution with macroscopic electrochemical performance, this contribution provides foundational insights for synthesizing high-performance LIB anodes.

title: Poly(ether-imide-ester)s incorporating sulfur-containing amino acids: a first step toward more sustainable high-dielectric polymer materials
abstract: The development of high-dielectric polymer materials is critical for advancing energy storage and conversion technologies.

title: Bionic inspired electrostatic-complexed reduced graphene oxide/covalent organic framework nanosheets for high-performance nanofiltration membranes
abstract: Inspired by sandcastle worms, electrostatically complexed laminar membranes by integrating positive covalent organic framework nanosheets (CONs) with negative rGO were fabricate, achieving high nanofiltration (NF) performance.

title: Scalable, thermally stabilized MOF-graphene fibers with hierarchical porosity for high-performance energy storage devices
abstract: Liquid crystal-facilitated on-demand printing of structurally stabilized copper benzene tricarboxylate frameworks using Keggin-type polyoxometalates enables the realization of efficient, high-performance fiber supercapacitors.

title: A 3D activated microporous protective layer for high-energy lithium metal batteries
abstract: A 3D microporous polymer interlayer (PCHP) stabilizes Li metal anodes by enhancing mechanical strength and ionic conduction. It achieves 76% capacity retention over 400 cycles with NCM and 93% over 500 cycles with high-loading LFP at 2 mA cm −2 .

title: Highly efficient and stable CsPbBr 3 perovskite photoanodes based on inverse opal TiO 2 layers in photoelectrochemical water splitting
abstract: Sunlight in the CsPbBr 3 perovskite generates e − /h + pairs; the inverse opal scaffold enhances light absorption and charge separation, enabling electrons and holes to drive water splitting, producing H 2 and O 2 , with arrows showing charge paths.

title: Cation exchange membranes based on the Donnan exclusion effect for aqueous organic redox flow batteries
abstract: Ion exchange membranes are a key parameter determining the energy efficiency of redox flow battery (RFB) systems as they facilitate the rapid transport of charge carrier ions while preventing the cross-diffusion of redox-active species.

title: Atomic-level environment engineering in carbon-based single-atom catalysts: a review of theoretical insights for hydrogen evolution and triiodide reduction
abstract: Five microenvironment engineering strategies are proposed to modulate single-atom active sites, integrating computational catalysis, functional materials design, and sustainable energy applications to drive interdisciplinary advances.

title: Introducing phenol-yne chemistry for the design of lignin-based vitrimers: towards sustainable and recyclable materials
abstract: Designing of lignin-based vitrimers using phenol-yne chemistry to develop sustainable and recyclable aromatic polymer materials.

title: Microstructural effects on intermediate temperature proton conduction in an oxalate-based coordination polymer
abstract: Microstructure control of the CP proton conductor {[((CH 3 ) 2 NH 2 ) 3 SO 4 ] 2 [Zn 2 (C 2 O 4 ) 3 ]} n (DSZO) offers improved powder processing capabilities and formation of dense electrolyte pellets.

title: Impact of engineered dual-scale porosity for application-tailored battery electrodes
abstract: The fabrication of free-standing binderless electrodes with tunable micron-sized channels and engineered sub-micron pores is reported, as well as the quantification of the architecture–performance relationships of 18 such low-tortuosity electrodes.

title: Tough and recyclable hydrogel electrolytes with continuous ion migration pathways for dendrite-free zinc-ion batteries under harsh conditions
abstract: Mechanically robust, environmentally adaptive, and recyclable PVA composite hydrogels are utilized in zinc-ion batteries to effectively suppress dendrite growth and inhibit side reactions.

title: High-entropy Cr(NiFeCoV) 2 O 4 catalysts via CO 2 laser thermal shock: advancing electrochemical water oxidation with multi-metal synergy
abstract: This work reports the synthesis of a high-entropy Cr(NiFeCoV) 2 O 4 catalyst via a rapid continuous-wave CO 2 laser thermal-shock method. The Cr(NiFeCoV) 2 O 4 catalyst shows excellent OER performance, requiring a low overpotential of 284 mV at 10 mA cm −2 .

title: A robust grape-like superhydrophobic surface for efficient oil–water separation and anti/de-icing
abstract: A robust grape-like photo/electro-thermal dual-synergistic superhydrophobic cotton fabric exhibits highly efficient oil–water separation and anti/de-icing performance.

title: Enhanced borohydride oxidation on a Pd–Ni(OH) 2 /C catalyst via hydroxyl enrichment on nearby active sites
abstract: The enriched OH species on the Ni(OH) 2 surface enhance the oxidative removal of H ad on Pd, boosting DBFC performance, even with air as the oxidant. The DBFC achieves a peak power density of 625 mW cm −2 and stability for over 120 h at 200 mA cm −2 .

title: Multiple artificial mechanoreceptor-embedded waterproof ciliated E-skin via direct-ink-writing vertically 3D printing toward health management of seafarers
abstract: A sensitive and waterproof ciliated E-skin, inspired by epidermal hair and mechanoreceptors, is fabricated via vertically 3D printing. It enables real-time pulse monitoring under high humidity, applicable to health management in maritime personnel.

title: Rational design of a two-dimensional metal–organic framework for high-efficiency bifunctional oxygen electrocatalysis
abstract: A two-dimensional MOF with excellent bifunctional OER/ORR activity, named Rh-HITT is screened by first-principles.

title: Double built-in electric fields and surface reconstruction endow Ag/CoNiV-LDH/CoO with superior water splitting activity
abstract: An Ag/CoNiV-LDH/CoO catalyst with enhanced conductivity, optimized double built-in electric fields and a regulated electronic structure for sustainable and efficient water splitting.

title: Recent progress in minimizing the loss channel via triplet excitons in nonfullerene-based organic solar cells
abstract: Recent advances in minimizing the loss channel via triplet excitons in nonfullerene organic solar cells are summarized. Potential directions that might address the existing and emerging challenges are proposed to further boost the PCE.

title: Structural and thermodynamic analysis of triple conducting ceramic materials BaCo 0.4 Fe 0.4 Zr 0.2− X Y X O 3− δ
abstract: Neutron diffraction, thermogravimetry, and high temperature solution calorimetry measurements reveal insights into proton and oxygen-ion kinetics in BaCo 0.4 Fe 0.4 Zr 0.2− X Y X O 3− δ triple ionic–electronic conducting ceramics.

title: Passivating defects and optimizing interfaces to boost the comprehensive performance of carbon-cathode hole-transport-layer-free CsPbI 2 Br solar cells via an ionic liquid
abstract: A carbon-cathode hole-transport-layer-free CsPbI 2 Br perovskite solar cell exhibits reduced defect density in the CsPbI 2 Br layer due to treatment with an ionic liquid ([BMIM][OTF]), resulting in an enhanced power conversion efficiency of 15.39%.

title: Pt-nanoparticles on ZnO/carbon quantum dots: a trifunctional nanocomposite with superior electrocatalytic activity boosting direct methanol fuel cells and zinc–air batteries
abstract: A ternary PtNP-ZnO@CQDs nano-catalyst, made via a one-pot process, outdoes commercial catalysts in MOR, OER, and ORR. As a Zn–air battery cathode, it delivers high energy density and durability, showing great promise for future energy applications.

title: Enhanced thermoelectric properties of bilayer graphdiyne through twist angle and pressure regulation
abstract: In this work, the thermoelectric properties of two-dimensional twisted graphdiyne were studied by using first-principles calculations and the Boltzmann transport equation.

title: {Fe 2+ –imidazole} catalyst grafted on magnetic {Fe@Graphitized C} nanoparticles: a robust hybrid–catalyst for H 2 production from HCOOH
abstract: Covalent grafting of Fe-imidazole on FSP-made graphitized carbon nanoparticles with a magnetic Fe-core yields a highly durable hybrid catalyst for H 2 production.

title: Atomic layer deposition of nickel sulfide thin films and their thermal and electrochemical stability
abstract: An atomic layer deposition process for NiS x thin films is developed. The deposited β-NiS films undergo structural and compositional changes during water splitting and high-temperature annealing.

title: Ordered mesoporous electrocatalysts for highly selective formate production from electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: Ordered mesoporous catalysts, fabricated by electrodepositing carbon electrodes with lyotropic liquid crystal templates, show outstanding electrocatalytic performance for CO 2 reduction, enabling the selective production of formate.

title: Enhancing CO 2 -facilitated transport in PVAm membranes through the synergistic effect of porous molybdenum disulfide and mobilizable sulfonic groups
abstract: Mobilizable sulfonic groups on MoS 2 expanded the hydrophilic area, which synergistically enhanced CO 2 -facilitated transport through interlayer space. Therefore, as-prepared mixed matrix membranes exhibited excellent gas separation performance.

title: Advances in gas sensors using screen printing
abstract: This review highlights that screen-printed gas sensors are cost-effective and scalable, ideal for environmental, industrial, and healthcare applications.

title: Design and prompt synthesis of a hydrazone-linked covalent organic framework with binding pockets for lanthanides: luminescent pH and ratiometric temperature sensing
abstract: A highly functional COF, namely, BTD, featuring a unique hydrazone linkage, and its lanthanide derivatives are reported for pH and temperature sensing.

title: Atomically dispersed metal catalysts for methane dry reforming
abstract: Methane dry reforming to produce synthesis gas is an effective way to solve the energy and environmental problems.

title: CO 2 utilization in energy storage and conversion
abstract: This review explores four emerging CO 2 -based energy technologies that utilize CO 2 as an active energy carrier, highlighting its roles, challenges, and future strategies in sustainable energy conversion and storage.

title: In situ study of CdS/WO 3 and CdS/SnO 2 heterostructures: comparison of photocatalytic activity behavior
abstract: This study used in situ XPS to demonstrate that the CdS/WO 3 ( n ) heterostructures exhibit enhanced visible-light active photocatalytic properties through the controlled adjustment of the CdS and WO 3 ratio.

title: Failure mechanisms and design strategies for low-temperature solid-state metal batteries
abstract: This review systematically examines the failure mechanisms of low-temperature solid-state metal batteries and summarizes corresponding design strategies to enhance their efficiency and reliability in low-temperature applications.

title: High yield ammonia production via glucose oxidation assisted electrochemical nitrate reduction
abstract: High yield ammonia production via electrochemical nitrate reduction by replacement of anodic oxygen evolution with glucose oxidation reaction.

title: Elucidating the phase transformations and grain growth behavior of O3-type sodium-ion layered oxide cathode materials during high temperature synthesis
abstract: The mechanistic pathways of the high-temperature calcination reaction in a sodium-ion layered oxide, along with its synthesis-microstructure-performance relationship are investigated.

title: Janus fabric sensor with integrated moisture-wicking, wearable monitoring and thermoelectric capabilities for fire warning
abstract: This study developed a Janus-structured fabric sensor that integrated efficient moisture-wicking, ionic thermoelectric, and motion monitoring. It is also suitable for rapid fire-warning systems.

title: Asymmetric cyano-rich crystalline carbon nitride with a powerful dipole field for efficient hydrogen peroxide photosynthesis
abstract: An asymmetric cyano-rich crystalline g-C 3 N 4 was prepared, showing high H 2 O 2 yield, attributed to the critical roles of robust spontaneous polarization, definite interlayer charge transfer channel, and long-range ordered in-plane structure.

title: Deciphering the role of hydrothermal pretreatment in the conversion of biomass waste into hard carbon with superior electrochemical performance in sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Hard carbon was prepared from biomass with a high degree of crystallinity using hydrothermal pretreatment, which improved the electrochemical performance in sodium-ion batteries.

title: Temperature- and creep-resistant Diels–Alder salogels for shape stabilization of salt hydrate phase change materials
abstract: Superior temperature resistance (120 °C) and creep-resistance (>80% up to 95 °C) of Diels–Alder (DA) salogels imparts shape stabilization and leakage prevention to salt hydrate phase change materials (PCMs) with wide-ranging melting temperatures.

title: Enhanced cycling stability and suppressed voltage decay of LiMn 0.8 Fe 0.2 PO 4 /C by Zn-gradient doping
