abstract: A Zn-gradient doping strategy was proposed to enhance the cycling stability and suppress the voltage decay of LiMn 0.8 Fe 0.2 PO 4 /C (LMFP/C).

title: Machine learning-guided discovery of thermodynamically stable single-atom catalysts on functionalized MXenes for enhanced oxygen reduction and evolution reactions
abstract: DFT and machine learning are combined to screen transition-metal-doped MXene (Ti 3 C 2 T 2 ) catalysts for oxygen electrocatalysis. Ni–Ti 3 C 2 S 2 and Cu–Ti 3 C 2 S 2 show low ORR/OER overpotentials, highlighting the power of ML-accelerated catalyst design.

title: Synergistic dual-additive engineering in composite-polymer-electrolyte empowering long-lifespan all-solid-state Li–Se batteries
abstract: This study leverages a synergistically orchestrated dual-additive design in a composite-polymer-electrolyte to refine the electrode–electrolyte interfacial dynamics in solid-state Li–Se batteries.

title: Relationship between microporous structure and light gas transport through glassy polymeric membranes revealed by molecular simulations
abstract: Microporous glassy polymers are attractive materials for gas separation membranes, due to their high permeability and tailorable selectivity, provided physical aging can be delayed.

title: The synergistic mechanism of an FeS 2 /ACFs self-standing membrane in highly efficient electro-Fenton anitibiotic degradation: carbon nanodefects and free radical analysis
abstract: Antibiotics are challenging to degrade in wastewater using conventional methods.

title: Comb-shaped proton exchange membranes with dangling polystyrene grafted onto PVDF for PEM fuel cells and water electrolysis
abstract: Herein, oxy-tethered polystyrene grafted PVDF-based comb-shaped proton exchange membranes (PEMs) are developed for proton exchange membrane fuel cells and water electrolyzers.

title: Electrochemical etching of MXenes: mechanism, challenges and future outlooks
abstract: This review focuses on the eco-friendly electrochemical etching synthesis of MXenes and their cutting-edge advancements compared to the conventional strategy, highlighting the innovations, challenges, and future outlooks.

title: Point-of-use upcycling of 3D printing waste for developing 3D-printed Zn–I 2 batteries
abstract: This study reveals a mechanical upcycling approach combined with electrode engineering to transform carbon nanofiber and polylactic acid-based 3D printing waste into functional 3D components for sustainable zinc–iodine batteries.

title: Unveiling the complexity of co-evaporation of perovskite: Why co-evaporation might not be the optimal choice
abstract: Co-evaporation enables solvent-free route for perovskite solar cell fabrication. However, it presents challenges, such as substrate dependence and non-linear FAI evaporation, which might limit systematic studies and reproducibility.

title: Decoding host–guest interactions: tuning pore functionality in MOFs for efficient capture of short- and long-chain PFAS
abstract: A family of bio-derived MOFs was synthesised for efficient PFAS removal. Hydrophobic pore tailoring boosted adsorption of short-chain PFAS, with structural and theoretical analyses confirming the preeminent role of hydrophobic interactions.

title: Reorganization of interfacial water structure boosted OH − transfer by engineering the charge redistribution and enhanced the alkaline HER
abstract: Dual doping of B and N in Ru/BCN induced charge redistribution, which not only modulated the adsorbed energy of the intermediate but also reorganized the hydrogen bond network of water, thereby boosting OH − transfer for enhanced HER.

title: Environmentally friendly regeneration of graphite from spent lithium-ion batteries for sustainable anode material reuse
abstract: Sustainable regeneration of graphite anodes through novel treatments boosts performance and supports circular economy in batteries.

title: A hollow MOF-derived capsular V x O 3 @CN cathode with high capacity and ultralong lifespan for aqueous zinc ion batteries
abstract: A capsular V-MOF-derived V x O 3 @CN cathode for aqueous zinc batteries is fabricated via in situ conversion. The unique hollow morphology and engineered V defects synergistically enhance Zn 2+ storage kinetics and stability.

title: Pulsed laser-patterned high-entropy single-atomic sites and alloy coordinated graphene oxide for pH-universal water electrolysis
abstract: We introduce high-entropy single-atom catalysts (HESACs) from FeRuPtNiCoPd HEA on GO via pulsed laser irradiation in liquids. Synergistic interactions and rapid Fe 2+ photoreduction enhance active sites, achieving superior overall water splitting.

title: Fluorinated catalysts for the oxygen evolution reaction: a comprehensive review of synthesis, structure, and performance
abstract: Fluorination has been proven to be an effective strategy to boost catalysts' OER performance by significantly reducing overpotential and the Tafel slope while enhancing stability.

title: Simultaneous cation substitution and rear interface engineering strategies for highly efficient CZTSSe-based solar cells
abstract: By implementing a MoO x interlayer and a co-cation substitution strategy in conventional CZTSSe solar cells, we successfully improved the rear-interface contact properties and effectively suppressed deep-level defects.

title: Molecular engineering of gCN-based multifunctional photocatalysts via in situ covalent modification
abstract: A multifunctional gCN-based photocatalyst was constructed through controllable surface covalent reactions. This modification contributes to electron–hole separation and improves photocatalytic degradation of organic pollutants.

title: Thermally polymerizable phthalocyanine realizes a metal–nitrogen-doped carbon material featuring a defined single-atom catalyst motif with CO 2 RR activity
abstract: Metal–nitrogen-doped carbon materials (MNCs) exhibit good electrocatalytic performance owing to the intrinsic advantages of carbon-based materials and the presence of isolated and stabilized metal atoms coordinated by nitrogen sites.

title: Pt n -Mn (II) N x and Pt n -Mn (III) N x are both winning combinations for the durability of these hybrid catalysts in PEM fuel cells: a deep insight into synergism between Pt clusters and MnN x /C sites
abstract: MnN x /C increases the adhesion strength of Pt( n ) on a carbon substrate and, in return, Pt( n ) stabilizes MnN x /C towards demetallation in strongly acidic fuel cell media.

title: Enhancing fluorescence thermo-sensitivity in Er–Tm dual-activator microrods via cooperation of partitioned doping and excitation control
abstract: Synergistic modulation of excitation mode and doping architecture enhances coupled energy levels and dual-ion ratiometric temperature-sensing performance by suppressing multiple energy exchange pathways, paving new ways for remote thermometer.

title: The effects of cationic odd–even alkyl chains on the crystal structure and energetic properties of cyclo -N 5 − salts
abstract: The “odd–even” effect changes the hydrogen bonding between the tetrazole cation and cyclo -N 5 − , which in turn affects the crystal structure of the cyclo -N 5 − salts.

title: Trace low-concentration calcium doping enhancement in ionic conductivity and electrochemical performance improvement of CeO 2 -based electrolyte for solid oxide fuel cells (SOFCs)
abstract: This work demonstrates that calcium doping in GDC electrolytes enhances both the grain boundary conductivity and the oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) activity of the corresponding composite cathode.

title: Triptycene improved crystallization characteristics and boosted energy density in PVDF-based all-organic composites
abstract: PVDF/TE composites exhibited improved crystallization characteristics and simultaneously obtained large U e and high η under high electric fields.

title: Redox-active oxide-molten salt composites as a new family of high-capacity thermal energy storage materials
abstract: Redox-active oxide-molten salt (ROMS) composites achieve thermal energy storage capacities of up to 875 kJ kg −1 , through both phase-transition and redox-based mechanisms.

title: Evaluation of Cu- and Mn-doped Co 3 O 4 /NiO composites as cathodes for intermediate temperature solid oxide fuel cells
abstract: Un-doped and doped Co 3 O 4 /NiO materials with excellent phase mixing (a) were prepared and incorporated into IT-SOFC cathodes (b). Their performance was evaluated by impedance spectroscopy and distribution of relaxation time analysis (c).

title: Heteroaromatic ring-modified naphthalimide interlayer materials for efficient polymer solar cells
abstract: We synthesize four cathode interlayer materials (NTXs) end-capped with electron-deficient heteroaromatic rings for polymer solar cells, and demonstrate NTR-modified devices exhibit the best performance and thermal stability among NTXs based devices.

title: 3D NiCo-LDH hollow nanocages for rapid and efficient removal of tetracycline hydrochloride from water
abstract: The 3D NiCo-LDH nanocage achieved TC removal based on synergistic adsorption–degradation, exhibiting a maximum removal amount of 630 mg g −1 . DFT simulations and structural characterization revealed the oxygen vacancy-driven mechanism.

title: Data-driven finding of organic anode active materials for lithium-ion batteries from natural flower scent products using capacity predictors
abstract: New organic anode active materials for lithium-ion batteries, such as dichlorobenzene and pyrrole derivatives, have been efficiently identified using performance prediction models based on natural products from flower scents.

title: Improving near-room-temperature thermoelectrics: the role of Ag 2 Se doping and Bi/Sb ratio in (Bi,Sb) 2 Te 3
abstract: Thermoelectric performance of (Bi,Sb)₂Te₃ is enhanced through Ag₂Se doping and Bi/Sb ratio adjustment, boosting the quality factor. Devices achieve superior conversion efficiency, highlighting the success of this two-step optimization process.

title: The origin of enhanced conductivity and structure change in defective Li 4 Ti 5 O 12 : a study combining theoretical and experimental perspectives
abstract: Spinel Li 4 Ti 5 O 12 (LTO): a promising “zero strain” anode for all-solid-state batteries (ASSB) offering exceptional cycling stability and prolonged battery lifespan.

title: Molecular anchoring-induced proton adsorption effect achieves stable zinc metal anodes
abstract: Dicyandiamide serves as a multifunctional electrolyte additive in aqueous zinc-ion batteries, leveraging a molecular anchoring-induced proton adsorption effect to stabilize zinc metal anodes.

title: One-pot synthesis of a magnetic polypyrrole aerogel with 1D/2D hybrid nanostructures for microwave absorption
abstract: Magnetic PPy aerogel with 1D/2D hybrid nanostructure is prepared via one pot synthesis. EM parameters led to the identification of broad bandwidth and strong microwave absorption, which may provide a foundation for design of lightweight and efficient EM absorption material.

title: Role of crystallographic orientation in atom probe analysis of Li-ion battery cathode materials
abstract: Using air-stable lithium cobalt oxide (LCO) as a prototypical system, we elucidate the role of crystallographic orientation and anisotropic transport in atom probe tomography (APT) for lithium transition metal oxide (LTMO) cathode materials.

title: Micro-sized conductive metal–organic framework nanosheets for the electrochemical hydrogen evolution reaction in acidic media
abstract: A top-down synthesis of a micro-sized conductive MOF is provided with a mass yield of 30%, following a unique arrangement–reconstruction pathway which differs from the typical exfoliation mechanism.

title: A novel strategy for discovering inorganic solar-cell absorbers: leveraging octahedral features in ABX 3 structures
abstract: High-throughput screening study presenting a novel strategy for discovering new solar cell absorbers, inspired by the octahedral framework of ABX 3 structures.

title: Challenges and advances in redox flow batteries utilizing sustainable and cost-effective non-vanadium active materials
abstract: The key parameters for grid-scale energy storage systems (ESSs) are safety, longevity, and cost-effectiveness.

title: An atomically precise Pt 17 nanocluster: its electronic structure and high activity for the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Based on the revealed electronic structure of [Pt 17 (CO) 12 (PPh 3 ) 8 ] z , highly active HER catalysts were created for water electrolysis.

title: Hydrogen peroxide driven dinitrogen reduction to ammonia
abstract: A process for mild thermal condition ammonia (NH 3 ) synthesis from dinitrogen (N 2 ) using H 2 O 2 and iron filings is reported here.

title: Efficient electrocatalytic glucose oxidation coupled water electrolysis driven by Ni-foam supported Ni–P nanowire arrays
abstract: Highly efficient electrocatalytic glucose oxidation reaction (GOR) using Ni–P@NF is realized and the mechanism is in situ explored. This catalyst also achieved superb hydrogen evolution and formic acid generation via GOR-assisted water electrolysis.

title: Dual-functional intercalated Ti 3 C 2 T x optothermic materials for water desalination and runway ice removal
abstract: A composite material combining cobalt and carbon black intercalated Ti 3 C 2 T x with a superhydrophilic PVDF disc is introduced for water desalination and runway ice removal application.

title: Highly active and stable PdRE catalysts for enhancing electrocatalytic methanol and ethanol oxidation
abstract: RE (La, Nd, and Dy) doping improves the catalytic performance of Pd-based catalysts, with the most significant improvement observed in La doping. In situ FTIR shows that PdLa/C enhanced ethanol adsorption, C–C bond cleavage and CO 2 selectivity.

title: An energy efficient and sustainable approach to structural health monitoring in carbon fiber composites: harnessing sound-induced vibration with Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene/AgNPs modified P(VDF-TrFE) sensors
abstract: 2D MXenes and silver enhance the piezo sensor sensitivity for sound-induced vibration. These flexible, self-powered sensors enable damage detection in carbon fiber composites for structural health monitoring in automotive and aerospace sectors.

title: Tailoring the room-temperature miscibility gap in ordered spinel LiNi 0.5 Mn 1.5 O 4 cathodes by multi-element doping
abstract: Multi-element doping in LiNi 0.5 Mn 1.5 O 4 (LNMO) cathodes tailors the charge–discharge mechanism from a two-phase to a solid-solution reaction. This reduces lattice strain, suppresses degradation, and enhances long-term battery performance.

title: Tailoring Fe-doped Co 3 O 4 nanoparticles via ultrasonic cell disruption: mechanistic insights and high-performance alkaline HER electrocatalysts
abstract: The alkaline HER catalytic mechanism of Fe-Co 3 O 4 UNPs.

title: Inorganic M 3 ACl 3 (M = Ca, Sr, Ba, A = N, P, As) perovskite-derivatives for next-generation solar cells and optoelectronics: in-depth analysis of stability, optoelectronic features, and temperature-dependent carrier mobilities
abstract: M 3 ACI 3 (M = Ca, Sr, Ba, A = N, P, As) compounds are promising for PVCs and optoelectronics, with a bandgap of 1.7 to 2.9 eV, carrier mobility of 38 to 192 cm V −1 s −1 , and absorption coefficient of 2.5 to 11 × 10 5 cm −1 .

title: A tin-based composite oxide confined by reduced graphene oxide as a high-rate anode for sodium-ion capacitors
abstract: Sn 2 P 2 O 7 /rGO composites were synthesized using a molecular grafting strategy, enhancing stability through strong adhesion. Sn 2 P 2 O 7 /rGO-based SIBs show high capacity and rate performance, while SICs exhibit excellent rate capability and energy density.

title: Facile synthesis of a biomass-based sustainable covalent organic network (CON) anode for high-performance LIBs
abstract: A CON material, derived from biomass derivatives, was in situ grown on CNTs, yielding BIO-4C. When applied as an anode for LIBs, it demonstrated excellent performance.

title: Enhanced CO poisoning resistance and hydrogen storage in palladium/metal–organic framework composites
abstract: The Pd/MOF-74(Ni) composite enhances the H 2 absorption kinetics and capacity of Pd under CO-contaminated H 2 via the strong interaction between CO and Ni 2+ in MOFs and H 2 spillover effect.

title: Regulation of microcrystalline and pore structures in pitch-based hard carbon via liquid-phase crosslinking-assisted grain boundary etching to enhance sodium storage performance
abstract: Preparation of pitch-based hard carbon with closed pore structure by liquid-phase crosslinking coupled with grain boundary etching effect. The microcrystalline structure of carbon materials can be modified by controlling the depth of cross-linking.

title: NCQD-induced nitrogen configuration engineering for constructing hybrid CN with exceptionally high nitrogen content and predominantly pyrrolic/pyridinic-N for catalytic hydrogenation
abstract: NCQDs induced nitrogen configuration engineering realized hybrid carbons with high nitrogen content and pyrrolic/pyridinic-N ratio, which improved electron transfer between Pt NPs and carbon support.

title: Constructing coordination compound interphases for superior zinc-ion battery performance
abstract: The IA@Zn protective layer, formed via coordination between zinc ions and IA's carboxyl groups, enhances ZIB performance by ensuring a stable interphase, reducing surface roughness, suppressing dendrites, and promoting Zn (002) plane deposition.

title: Theoretical prediction of efficient Cu-based dual-atom alloy catalysts for electrocatalytic nitrate reduction to ammonia via high-throughput first-principles calculations
abstract: The dual-atom sites in Cu-based dual-atom alloy not only exhibit high activity for electrocatalytic nitrate (NO 3 − ) reduction reaction (eNO 3 RR) to produce valuable ammonia (NH 3 ), but also can effectively inhibit the formation of by-products.

title: Universal buried interface modification with lead iodide for efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: This paper introduces a simple yet effective method for improving the buried interface of perovskite layer using a 1-step fabrication technique. The approach enhances the performance & stability of perovskite solar cells in n-i-p & p-i-n structures.

title: Tuning the oxygen-containing microenvironment of the Pt–Ni hetero-interface to accelerate alkaline hydrogen oxidation
abstract: Pt–Ni heterostructures with different oxygen-containing microenvironments were first controllably synthesized. The slightly oxidized interface can weaken the adsorption energy of the OH* intermediates and thus accelerate alkaline hydrogen oxidation.

title: A C 6 F 5 -functionalized benzimidazole acceptor enabling supramolecular fluorinated interactions for enhanced photovoltaic performance and thermal stability
abstract: The benzimidazole (BI)-centered acceptor IPF, featuring a perfluorophenyl (C 6 F 5 )-functionalized side chain, leverages fluorine–fluorine interactions to achieve enhanced OPV performance and stability.

title: Thermally enhanced substrate design for flexible thermoelectric devices via ultrasonic welding
abstract: A laminated PI film and silicone gel pad substrate was ultrasonically welded, creating a high-performance and skin-conformable f-TED with intelligent thermal management for smart wearables.

title: Nitrogen-coordinated iridium doped on blue phosphorene as a bidirectional electrocatalyst for lithium–oxygen batteries: a first-principles study
abstract: N-coordinated Ir single atoms on blue phosphorene (Ir–N3@P) serve as an efficient bifunctional electrocatalyst for Li–O 2 batteries. The near-neutral charge center enables favorable LiO 2 binding energy of 3.18 eV and outstanding redox activity.

title: Carbon dioxide adsorption properties in 3D ultramicroporous diamondoid lanthanide-oxalate frameworks
abstract: The chemistry and applications of porous materials, particularly for gas adsorption and separation technologies, have advanced dramatically with the recent development of metal–organic frameworks (MOFs).

title: Tuning hydrocarbon selectivity in electrochemical CO 2 reduction via copper-porphyrin immobilization on carbon nanotubes
abstract: Immobilized Cu-porphyrin on CNTs enhances hydrocarbon selectivity in CO 2 reduction by modulating double-layer structure.

title: Controlled oxidation of V 2 O 5 /VO 2 hollow nanospheres as photocathodes for photo-rechargeable zinc ion batteries with an ultrahigh capacity enhancement
abstract: Hollow nanospheres composed of V 2 O 5 /VO 2 were constructed to promote surface photovoltages (SPV) for photo-charging in zinc ion batteries.

title: Stable oxygen reduction catalysts for enhancing rechargeability for zinc–air batteries: FeCoCu nanoparticles embedded in N-doped carbon matrices
abstract: N-doped carbon/FeCoCu catalysts exhibit excellent ORR performance, demonstrating outstanding rechargeability in Zn–air batteries. DFT calculations reveal enhanced conductivity and a favorable surface electronic structure for oxygen reactions.

title: Regulating spin states of single transition metal atoms on N-doped graphene for efficient ammonia synthesis
abstract: We propose a strategy to regulate the spin states of single transition metal atoms on N-doped graphene by applying strain, which effectively weakens the scaling relationship and thereby boosts the activity and selectivity of ammonia synthesis.

title: Determination of Mn 2+ using a paper-based flexible electrochemical sensor modified by NiFe 2 O 4 and CeO 2 nanoparticles
abstract: Determination of Mn 2+ using a paper-based flexible electrochemical sensor modified by NiFe 2 O 4 and CeO 2 nanoparticles.

title: Electrodeposition of oxyanion films as universal chloride ion-repelling layers for efficient and stable seawater oxidation at ampere-level current density
abstract: A rapid, universal oxyanion film electrodeposition strategy to form a Cl − -repelling layer on various OER catalysts for stable seawater splitting has been developed.

title: Unveiling the reaction selectivity mechanism of molybdenum and tungsten carbides as cathode catalysts for Li–CO 2 batteries
abstract: The type of catalysts and the adsorption behavior of molecules play a decisive role in the selectivity of the lithium–CO 2 battery reaction.

title: Enhancing inverted perovskite solar cell performance via buried modification for improved interface and stability
abstract: Adding 5-1T2A between SAMs/perovskite in inverted perovskite solar cells optimizes energy alignment and enhances carrier extraction and crystallization, boosting PCE, raising V OC , and improving stability.

title: Controlled interfacial charge transfer and photon–electron conversion of Ag/Cu-supported MXene heterostructures for applications as PEC anode materials
abstract: Photogenerated carrier separation and interface engineering of Ag/Cu-supported MXene heterostructures were performed to investigate their photoelectrochemical activity and charge transfer mechanism.

title: A mixed-ligand approach to a cobalt-based electroactive framework for superior supercapacitor performance
abstract: The development of electroactive framework with tailored functionalities, which leads to improve the specific capacitance performance and outstanding performance in ASC device.

title: High-output, thermally resilient Nano-TiO 2 dielectric gel triboelectric nanogenerator for energy harvesting and reliable temperature-independent pressure sensing
abstract: By doping TiO 2 nanoparticles into PVC gel, a high-output TENG was fabricated, enabling a temperature-independent pressure sensor. This sensor achieved stable sensitivity of 2.03 V kPa −1 (10–40 kPa) and 0.97 V kPa −1 (40–100 kPa) from 25 °C to 55 °C.

title: Selective CVD growth of boron nitride nanotubes via oxidation control of supported catalysts
abstract: An oxidation-controlled supported Ni–Pd catalyst enables selective CVD growth of BNNTs and MgO–BN core/shell nanowires at 1100 °C; TEM/DFT show quasi-liquid catalyst and strong h-BN interaction governing tip growth.

title: Selective alkylation of phenol and tert -butanol using Zr-containing Beta zeolite
abstract: Zr-containing Beta nanocrystals show the desired selectivity toward 2,4-DTBP based on the transformation of 4-TBP.

title: Heat charging towards electrical energy saving and high-efficiency Zn-ion batteries
abstract: We proposed a high-efficiency Zn-ion battery by heat charging. A record absolute high temperature coefficient of 3.157 mV K −1 was achieved. When the battery is charged at 45 °C and discharged at 5 °C, a high energy efficiency of 105.16% is realized.

title: Unveiling morphology evolution and key intermediates in the self-assembly of amino acid-naphthalene diimide triads
abstract: The self-assembly processes of two l -phenylalanine methyl ester-functionalized naphthalene diimide triads were thoroughly examined. The correlation between the molecular structure, assembly morphology, and functionality was illustrated.

title: Design of bifunctional bimetallic Fischer–Tropsch synthesis (FTS) catalysts: acid functionalization of TiO 2 support for enhanced product selectivity
abstract: TiO 2 sulfation introduces Brønsted and Lewis acid sites leading to higher Fischer–Tropsch synthesis activity and promoting C 2 –C 4 hydrocarbon formation.

title: Ligand environment engineering of nickel single atomic sites for efficient electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction reaction
abstract: In situ X-ray characterization and DFT calculations reveal that Ni configuration and N-coordination modulate the binding energies of *COOH and *H intermediates, with their difference being key to predicting CO 2 RR activity on Ni single atomic sites.

title: Solid–liquid interface reassembly enhances surface piezoelectric properties: transition from the parallel interface O-MoS 2 to the spherical interface ZnS@O-MoS 2
abstract: We explore an interface reassembly technique to fabricate the spherical interface ZnS@O-MoS 2 and investigate its performance in piezoelectric catalytic CO 2 reduction.

title: Confined orientation PVDF/MXene nanofibers for wearable piezoelectric nanogenerators
abstract: The quest for high-performance wearable piezoelectric nanogenerators (PENGs) has intensified the focus on polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF).

title: In situ constructed Bi/BiOBr nanoflower for synergistic H 2 O 2 generation and pollutant degradation: coupling built-in electric field with SPR effect and environmental risk assessment
abstract: Synergistic plasmonic-electric field nanoflowers enable 100% NOR degradation with H 2 O 2 co-production.

title: Recent advances of ethylene glycol oxidation reaction: catalytic mechanism, catalyst design and applications
abstract: Ethylene glycol has broad application prospects in the upcycling of PET and direct fuel cells. This paper focuses on recent advancements in the ethylene glycol oxidation reaction, including catalytic mechanisms, catalyst design and applications.

title: A new strategy for balancing thermal stability and latent heat of solid–liquid PCMs via a solid–solid PCM as a supporting skeleton
abstract: Insufficient thermal stability of phase change materials (PCMs) remains a fundamental limitation for next-generation battery thermal management.

title: Improving the cycling stability and rate performance of an aqueous sodium-ion supercapattery via mitigating metal dissolution and boosting conductivity by anchoring FePBA on rGO
abstract: Integration of FePBA with rGO boosts conductivity and suppresses Fe ion dissolution, enabling the FePBA/rGO composite to achieve 435 C g −1 capacity, 91% retention over 10 k cycles, and an excellent energy-power balance: 62.32 Wh kg −1 at 9 kW kg −1 .

title: Co 3 Se 4 nanoparticles anchored on an inverse-opal skeleton as a sulfur host for high-performance Li–S batteries
abstract: An inverse opal structure with highly dispersed Co 3 Se 4 nanoparticles offers physical confinement and chemical adsorption for polysulfides, effectively suppressing the shuttle effect and accelerating sulfur conversion.

title: Hydrogels: a review on their versatile applications for efficient and stable oil–water separation
abstract: Oil spills and the environmental pollution accidents caused by them have gradually become a serious problem that human industrial and commercial development must face.

title: Ag 2 O-loaded Cu based metal–organic framework material as pre-electrocatalyst for efficient urea synthesis
abstract: The electrocatalytic synthesis of urea from CO 2 and NO 3 − represents a sustainable alternative to traditional energy-intensive processes.

title: Novel semiconductor materials for advanced wide temperature range supercapacitors
abstract: This article presents an overview of the utilization of semiconductor materials and electrolytes in supercapacitors, emphasizing their performance across a wide temperature range.

title: Quinones: understanding their electrochemistry, chemistry and degradation pathways to tap their full potential in aqueous redox flow batteries
abstract: Quinones are prime candidates for aqueous redox flow batteries. This review discusses the chemistry of quinones and degradation pathways in aqueous solution, illuminating their pathway to successful implementation through case studies and examples.

title: Ce-doped NiOOH generated through the electrocatalytic self-reconstruction of Ce-doped Ni-MOFs for the efficient electrooxidation of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural
abstract: Ce-doped NiOOH nanosheets were obtained via electrocatalytic self-reconstruction of Ce-doped Ni-MOFs. The optimized electrocatalyst shows excellent performance in HMFOR owing to the strong electron interactions between Ce and Ni.

title: Topochemical synthesis of an atomic Pt modified plasmonic yolk–shell nanostructure for enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: We synthesized a novel Au-yolk@Pt/CdS hetero-nanostructure via galvanic replacement and cation exchange, thereby offering insights into the synergistic effects between hot electron injection and single-atom catalysis for enhanced photocatalysis.

title: Boosting the rate performance of lithium-rich cathode materials with W-doping
abstract: A novel strategy based on W doping via a simple solid-state method was introduced to improve the rate performance and voltage stability of LRMs.

title: Multiscale-void-containing low-density polyethylene/waste plastic porous carbon composites with electromagnetic shielding interference and thermal management capabilities
abstract: Organisms suffer negative effects from EW radiation and temperature changes, except for the center regions protected by MSP-WPPC/PEG. It benefits from multi-solid waste coupling utilization and multi-scale pore structure design.

title: Employing an EMImI ionic liquid additive in Zn-salt solutions for reversible Zn stripping and plating in an advanced aqueous Zn-ion battery
abstract: Aqueous zinc ion batteries (ZIBs) have gained considerable interest owing to their low cost, intrinsic safety, and environmental friendliness.

title: Theoretical understanding of the electro-reduction of acetonitrile into ethylamine on copper: a hydride-proton stepwise mechanism
abstract: The electrochemical reduction mechanism of acetonitrile to ethylamine on copper was elucidated theoretically, revealing a hydride-proton stepwise pathway where competition with hydrogen evolution causes selectivity loss at high overpotential.

title: Revisiting the original 2003 garnet-like Li-ion conducting solid electrolytes Li 5 La 3 M 2 O 12 (M = Nb, Ta, Nb/Ta): a look into phase formation and the identification of carbonate and alumina contamination
abstract: In this study, the original garnet-type Li 5 La 3 M 2 O 12 (garnet 1.0) was synthesised and a comprehensive structural and chemical stability characterisation performed, offering renewed insights since its initial development in 2003.

title: A Ru-doping and conductive metal–organic-framework co-modification strategy for enhanced full water splitting performance of a CoCu-LDH nanosheet array
abstract: A Ru-doping and conductive metal–organic-frameworks co-modification strategy is designed for promoting the full water splitting performance of CoCu-LDH nanosheets array.

title: [CoAlO/Ni]@C heterostructures constructed based on the interface and component coupling effect toward microwave absorption and thermal conductivity
abstract: The escalating demand for miniaturization and increased power in electronics presents substantial challenges in dealing with electromagnetic wave (EMW) radiation and heat accumulation within limited spaces.

title: Operando monitoring the redox activity of sodium vanadium titanium phosphate electrodes in organic and aqueous electrolytes by magnetometry
abstract: The bulk oxidation states of the transition metals in Na 2 VTi(PO 4 ) 3 are monitored continuously based on magnetic susceptibility variations. It is shown that the same charge compensation processes occur in organic and aqueous electrolytes.

title: An efficient toluene barrier membrane for high-performance direct toluene hydrogenation via an electrochemical process
abstract: A hydrocarbon-based proton exchange membrane with tailored hydrophilic domains reduces toluene permeation compared to Nafion, improving the efficiency of direct electro-hydrogenation of toluene to methyl cyclohexane and long-term performance.

title: Discovering virtual Na-based argyrodites as solid-state electrolytes using DFT, AIMD, and machine learning techniques
abstract: In light of the current absence of experimentally synthesized Na-based argyrodites, we systematically investigated a large compositional space comprising 4375 hypothetical Na-based argyrodite structures through DFT and AIMD calculations.

title: An eco-friendly, dual-dimensional and multi-functional fibrous filter enables efficient indoor air purification
abstract: Lyocell nonwovens composed of both micro- and nano-fibres are prepared through a fibrillation strategy. When loaded with hydroxyapatite (HAP) catalyst, the degradable HAP/fibrillated filter effectively removed both PMs and formaldehyde.

title: Reasonable active site design for promoting water dissociation and carbon monoxide activation in a low-temperature water-gas shift reaction
abstract: This work considers different catalyst design ideas for enhancing the low-temperature WGSR performance based on the combination of two half-reactions, which occur on different active sites with different optimizing strategies.

title: Structural transition in crystalline SiO 2 during mechanical amorphization under frictional shear
abstract: The quantification of the fraction of milled amorphous silica product by 29 Si MAS NMR spectroscopy revealed a threshold milling speed and duration.

title: Electroless-deposited NiFeP catalyst-coated-membrane cathodes for anion exchange membrane water electrolysis
abstract: Electroless deposition enables the in situ growth of NiFeP electrocatalysts on anion exchange membrane, resulting in a binder- and ionomer-free structure that enhances the catalytic activity and durability to hydrogen evolution reaction.

title: How the balance between *CO and *H intermediates in dual atom catalysts boosts selectivity for hydrocarbons
abstract: Catalyst model of FeM–N 6 C and schematic diagram of the CO 2 RR processd.

title: Face-to-face type giant dimeric donors synergistically improve the stability and efficiency of organic solar cells
abstract: The microstructure stability and T g of giant dimeric donors were improved using a face-to-face type molecular structure, thereby achieving dramatically increased PCE and device stability of ternary OSCs.

title: Electrocatalytic performance of high-entropy alloys in alkaline hydrogen evolution reaction with high electronegativity difference
abstract: High-entropy alloys (HEAs) are emerging electrocatalysts, which can convert inert Cu into a highly active electrocatalytic site by controlling the electronic structure of Cu through electronegativity differences after alloying.

title: AFP-based anti-icing coatings: present challenges and future opportunities
abstract: AFP-based anti-icing coatings prevent surface icing through dual mechanisms: dynamic repulsion of impacting water droplets and significant suppression of ice-adhesion forces.

title: Oversaturated iron sites on mesopore-rich carbon nanocages boost adsorption and transformation of polysulfides for lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Oversaturated Fe–N 5 moieties incorporated into N-doped carbon nanocages are fabricated to facilitate polysulfide adsorption and sulfur redox reactions for Li–S batteries, which achieve excellent specific capacity and long-term cycle stability.

title: Assessing the role of morphological changes as the origin of improved cycling stability of Sn-based anodes for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Sn based anodes for sodium-ion batteries are examined in relation to the morphological changes driven by the formation of a porous “coral-like” structure. We explore conditions that promote its formation and evaluate its impact on cycling stability.

title: Significantly enhanced thermoelectric performance of a solution-processable organic polymer and carbon nanotube composite via molecular packing engineering
abstract: A high-efficiency platform with concise synthesis steps to reveal the underlying correlation between polymeric architectures and polymer/CNT based thermoelectric properties.

title: Durable Cu-doped P3-type Na 0.62 Mn 0.75 Cu 0.19 O 2 cathodes for high-capacity sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Cu doping enhances the P3-Na 0.62 Mn 0.75 Cu 0.19 O 2 cathode's performance in sodium-ion batteries. It improves electrochemical properties, structural stability, and battery performance, making it a promising material for energy storage applications.

title: A multifunctional polymer to enhance SEI stability and Li utilization for efficient lithium metal batteries
abstract: Polymer modification at the separator can promote the formation of a stable anion-derived SEI layer in an ether-based electrolyte. Moreover, the polymer as an additive in a carbonate-based electrolyte can reduce electrolyte decomposition.

title: Dual-site synergistic passivation for CsPbBr 3 perovskite solar cells with record 1.707 V V oc and 11.23% efficiency
abstract: Bidentate diphenylphosphine ligands passivate Pb 2+ defects via dual-site coordination, achieving CsPbBr 3 perovskite solar cells with record 11.23% PCE, 1.707 V V oc and >90% stability retention over 1800 h under 80 °C & 80% RH.

title: Enhanced supercapacitor performance with cerium-doped polypyrrole nanofibers
abstract: Incorporation of cerium ions into the polypyrrole (PPy:Ce) nanofiber matrix enhanced redox functionality and structural properties. Electrochemical tests in a two-electrode setup showed improved capacitance, energy density and long-term stability.

title: Anderson-typed POM-derived FeS 2 /MoS 2 heterostructure hybridized with graphene for sodium-ion batteries anodes
abstract: Benefiting from synergistic hetero engineering and graphene integration, the FeS 2 /MoS 2 /rGO-0.5 anode demonstrates superior electrochemical performance for SIBs (334 mAh g −1 at 10 A g −1 , 318 mAh g −1 after 500 cycles at 5 A g −1 ).

title: Solvent-assisted structural phase transition of Zn-MOF from 2D to 3D and its microflower-structured carbon for supercapacitors
abstract: This work presents a solvothermal method to transform 2D Zn-MOFs into 3D flower-like structures. Their carbonized form shows superior supercapacitor performance via enhanced morphology, active sites, and ion transport.

title: Separating Li-ion and electron conduction pathways to realize robust cathode interfaces
abstract: Constructing separate Li-ion and electron conduction pathways enabled high interfacial charge transfer kinetics, stability, and superior cycling stability.

title: High-performance Sn 2 S 3 as a conversion-alloying anode material for lithium-ion batteries: insights from first-principles calculations
abstract: Conversion-alloying based anode materials represent a promising frontier in the evolution of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs), offering high capacities and improved structural integrity.

title: Enhanced photoelectric properties and solar to hydrogen efficiency of S-scheme h-BP/C 3 B heterojunctions through the dual electric field effect
abstract: S-scheme heterojunctions have emerged as a key focus in the design of advanced photocatalysts due to their exceptional redox behavior and high solar-to-hydrogen (STH) efficiency.

title: Real-time evaluation of charge transfer patterns at buried ionotronic interfaces
abstract: This study introduces in situ -EIS and triboelectrification perturbation to evaluate charge dynamics at insulating–metallic buried interfaces.

title: Solvent-assisted ligand exchange induces the epitaxial growth of VSe 2 on the surface of a 3D nitrogen-doped carbon network for ultra-long-life lithium/sodium-ion batteries
abstract: VSe 2 /NC derived from V-MOFs via solvent-assisted synthesis suppresses volume distortion and polyselenide dissolution, achieving superior long-term cycling stability in lithium-ion and sodium-ion batteries.

title: Enhanced utilization of light through polystyrene microspheres for boosting photoelectrochemical hydrogen production in MoS 2 /Si heterostructures
abstract: The MF effect of PS microspheres, the defects in MoS 2 , and the p–n junction formed at the interface synergistically enhance the PEC-HER performance of PS-coupled defective MoS 2 /Si photocathode.

title: Synergistic engineering of atomic disorder and porous architectures for ultralow lattice thermal conductivity and enhanced thermoelectric performance in n-type high-entropy lead chalcogenides
abstract: Balancing reduced lattice thermal conductivity ( κ lat ) with improved charge carrier transport remains a key challenge in thermoelectric materials, further complicated by strong electron–phonon coupling.

title: A zwitterion additive promotes (100)-textured zinc anodes for deep cycling zinc ion batteries
abstract: Scheme of PPS inducing (100)-textured anode along with the construction of water-deficient EDL.

title: Moisture and air-stable cation disordered O3-type layered cathodes for sodium-ion batteries: experimental and first-principles study
abstract: A multi-dopant strategy induces cation and Na + /vacancy disorder in an O3-type cathode, suppressing phase transitions and enhancing air/moisture stability, while enabling reversible Na + cycling – supported by DFT calculations and electrochemical analysis.

title: High-performance hydrogen evolution reaction catalysts based on three-dimensional single nodal loop semimetals ScX (X = Cd/Ag/Cu/Rh)
abstract: Topological materials have garnered attention as promising catalysts in electrocatalytic processes.

title: Bimetallic organic framework nanocages enhance polysulfide trapping and redox kinetics in lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: A conductive CoZn-based MOF (CoZn-HTP) with hollow nanocage structures was prepared to suppress polysulfide shuttling and improve sulfur utilization and cycling stability.

title: Wooden electrothermal composites for fast electrothermal conversion
abstract: Wooden electrothermal composites were fabricated by hot-pressing multi-layer delignified ultra-thin wood films impregnated with MXene, demonstrating broad application potential in household heating, personal thermal management, etc.

title: Synthesis of a carbon nanotube-modified NH 2 -MIL-53(Fe) adsorbent and efficient adsorption and separation of gallium ions
abstract: NH 2 -MIL-53(Fe) was decorated with CNTs to increase active sites. X -CNT/NH 2 -MIL-53(Fe) has good regenerative and selective adsorption properties. 2-CNT/NH 2 -MIL-53(Fe) has better adsorption capacity (1483.8 mg g −1 ).

title: Regulating the electronic states of NiSe 2 by Cr-doping to promote formation of active phases for high catalytic performance of the urea oxidation reaction
abstract: Our study highlights that the Cr-doped NiSe 2 as catalyst for UOR that replaces OER for water splitting shows excellent catalytic performance, i.e. , efficient hydrogen production at a current density of 10 mA cm −2 with a cell voltage of only 1.42 V.

title: Trace NO 2 sensors based on reduced graphene@metal oxide/oxyhydroxide/hydroxychloride with ultrahigh sensitivity and good selectivity at room temperature via modulation of the nanograin boundary
abstract: Reduced graphene oxide@metal oxide/metal oxyhydroxide/metal hydroxychloride show ultra-high sensitivity, extremely low limit of detection and high selectivity to trace NO 2 at room temperature due to the modulation of the nanograin boundary barrier.

title: Investigating structural and morphological transformations of strontium chloride for thermochemical energy storage
abstract: Structural and chemo-mechanical evolution of thermochemical energy storage materials are investigated with a variety of techniques, revealing asymmetric reaction and volume change rates during charge and discharge.

title: Atomically dispersed iron sites on a multi-shelled hollow structure for highly efficient CO 2 fixation
abstract: Gradually releasing reactants within a heterogeneous catalysis system is crucial to promote catalytic performance.

title: Helix of carbon nanostructures for polarization loss enhancement under an electromagnetic field
abstract: Helical carbon structures reveal the EMW absorption mechanism, enabling high-performance absorber design.

title: Detachment of aluminium in NiCoAl-LDH modulates the active metal species to enhance oxygen evolution reaction activity
abstract: Controlled electrochemical dissolution of Al triggers the formation of high-activity Co 4+ with excellent intrinsic OER activity.

title: Microdroplet self-driven transport on the surface with a bionic coupled cone-gradient groove
abstract: Inspired by Ginkgo biloba leaf grooves and pine needle cones, this study developed a 3D-printed bioderived integrated gradient conical groove system.

title: Liquid–liquid–solid interfacial polymerization approach to rapid fabrication of large-sized, self-standing structured COF membranes
abstract: A versatile liquid–liquid–solid interfacial polymerization method for the fabrication of large, robust, flexible, and crystalline, self-standing covalent organic framework membranes in high yield in a few hours and utility in filtration is show.

title: Photothermal solid-to-liquid transition in a coordination polymer: strength enhanced by coordination bond-induced nanoconfinement
abstract: Herein, a photothermal solid-to-liquid transition polymer with mechanical strength enhanced by coordination bond-induced nanoconfinement is developed.

title: Thioether-based sulfonated polytriazoles appended with phosphine oxide moieties with enhanced oxidative stability for PEMFC applications
abstract: Herein, a set of thioether-based sulfonated polytriazoles (SPTs) appended with phosphine oxide units with enhanced oxidative stability is designed and synthesized for proton exchange membrane fuel cell applications.

title: Efficient and robust intrinsically stretchable organic solar cells via mechanically interlocked oligomer integration
abstract: Mechanically interlocked oligo[2]rotaxane enables stress dissipation while preserving the fibrillar morphology in conjugated polymer blend films, allowing intrinsically stretchable OSCs to retain 80% efficiency under 34% tensile strain.

title: Radiofrequency-triggered surface-heated laser-induced graphene membranes for enhanced membrane distillation
abstract: A hydrophobic electroconductive LIG membrane with ∼143.7° water contact angle is prepared showing average surface temperature of ∼140 °C with 91 MHz radiofrequency heating. Vacuum membrane distillation shows ∼13.5 L m −2 h −1 flux.

title: Flexible trilayer cellulosic paper separators engineered with the BaTiO 3 ferroelectric fillers for high energy density sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Development of sustainable and flexible trilayer cellulose-based composite separator membranes for full cell systems in sodium-ion batteries.

title: Enhanced electrochemical catalysis and sensing applications: fabrication process optimization and electrocatalytic characterization of polymeric matrix composite for one-step synthesis of Pd nanoparticle-decorated laser-induced graphene electrodes
abstract: A one-step method for fabricating Pd@LIG electrodes with optimized laser parameters. The resulting electrodes exhibit excellent performance for detecting NO 2 − , H 2 O 2 , and NADH, with high sensitivity and stability.

title: Development of a heat storage heater for hybrid electrothermal conversion and latent heat storage
abstract: Heat storage heater can convert electricity into heat and store the high-temperature heat above 400 °C in the form of latent heat. This novel device is expected to be applied to Carnot batteries and power to heat technologies.

title: 3D printing of MOF reinforced flame-retardant PA12 composites with customizable structures
abstract: Polyamide 12 (PA12) requires lightweight, custom moulding and excellent flame retardancy properties for a wide range of aerospace applications.

title: Orientation control (horizontal and vertical) of TiO 2 single-crystalline nanowire arrays for high-performance gas sensing: a surface supersaturation-driven approach
abstract: We report a surface supersaturation-driven hydrothermal approach that modulates the growth orientation of TiO 2 single-crystalline nanoarrays (NAs). The horizontal NAs (TiO 2 -H-NAs) exhibit one of the best ethanol sensing performances reported to date.

title: Synergistic screening of high-performance TMDs/2D-LHPs heterostructures for solar cells via deep learning and DFT
abstract: Screening TMDs/2D-LHPs heterojunctions with high photovoltaic conversion efficiency through deep learning and DFT calculations.

title: Controlled distribution of both Pt nanoparticles and ionomer in self-supported nanoporous carbon scaffolds significantly enhances oxygen reduction kinetics and stability
abstract: Keeping Pt NPs and Nafion in their own microenvironments in a PEM fuel cell cathode catalyst layer results in exceptional ORR kinetics and stability.

title: Multiphase microstructures enable high-performance BiSbSe 3 /Cu thermoelectric composites
abstract: Nano-Cu composite-driven multiphase microstructure engineering via modulated interfacial potential barriers, enhanced short-range order, lattice plainification, and defect engineering achieves a near 14-fold ZT enhancement over pristine BiSbSe 3 .

title: Selective superwettability coated surface with proactive anti-crude-oil-fouling property
abstract: Based on its finely designed structure and chemical composition, a selective superwettability coating surface with proactive anti-crude-oil-fouling properties is achieved by a simple method for various crude-oil-related applications.

title: Promoted surface reconstruction of amorphous nickel boride electrocatalysts by boron dissolution for boosting the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Boron dissolution promotes the surface reconstruction of NiB into γ-NiOOH. The dissolved B, in the form of BO 2 − , adsorbs on γ-NiOOH to reduce the energy barrier for alkaline OER to achieve a superior activity.

title: High-throughput screening of stable Ag–Pd–F catalysts for formate oxidation reaction using machine learning
abstract: The crystal graph convolutional neural networks predict stable Ag–Pd–F catalysts for the formate oxidation reaction from Materials Project data with DFT verifying their thermodynamic, dynamic stability and catalytic activity.

title: In situ construction of a crystalline/amorphous interface in SnO 2 /Bi nanobelts for efficient CO 2 electroreduction
abstract: Crystalline/amorphous SnO 2 /Bi nanobelts (C/A-SnO 2 /Bi NBs) were prepared via an in situ electrochemical dynamic reconstruction strategy for efficient CO 2 electroreduction.

title: Theoretical screening of bis(iminothiolato)metal monolayers as single-atom electrocatalysts for nitrogen reduction
abstract: TMIT monolayers are screened for the NRR via a four-step strategy, with HER selectivity evaluated at five possible H adsorption sites.

title: Upcycling waste polypropylene separators into carbon nanotubes for efficient interfacial solar-driven evaporation and hydroelectric power generation
abstract: We report the controlled carbonization of waste PP separators using Ni–Mo–Al catalysts for the preparation of CNTs and to construct CNT-based evaporators with high performance in interfacial evaporation and energy harvesting.

title: Recent progress of selectivity regulation and reaction mechanism of atomically dispersed metal catalysts for oxygen reduction electrocatalysis
abstract: This review summarizes the recent progress of atomically dispersed metal catalysts for oxygen reduction electrocatalysis, including advanced theories and descriptors, active site structure, and advanced characterization techniques.

title: Print-light-synthesis of electrocatalytically active gas diffusion electrodes for fuel cell applications
abstract: The present work reports a simple approach to manufacture electrocatalytically active gas diffusion electrodes in two steps: (i) inkjet printing and (ii) flash light irradiation from a xenon flash lamp, a process called Print-light-synthesis (PLS).

title: Carbonate electrolyte structure engineering enabling ultrahigh-rate capability and long-term cycling stability of NFPP‖Na batteries at −40 °C
abstract: Schematic diagram highlights the dipole–dipole interaction between fluorobenzene and propylene carbonate in the NPF electrolyte.

title: In situ electrochemical activation enabling high-performance cathodes for aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: In situ electrochemical activation converts bulk VC to amorphous V 2 O 5 ·3H 2 O nanosheets, boosting capacity and cycling stability.

title: Nonradiative recombination dynamics simulations of CuIn 1− x Ga x Se 2 solar cells based on RNN and transformer models
abstract: Nonradiative electron–hole recombination is a key mechanism of energy loss in optoelectronic materials, and one of its core regulation mechanisms is nonadiabatic (NA) coupling.

title: Integrated photothermal and photocatalytic degradation of micro-/nanoplastics: a mini-review with mechanistic insights and future perspectives
abstract: Sources of MNPs and various treatment approaches.

title: Intermolecular chemistry in high-entropy solid polymer electrolytes enabling room temperature solid-state lithium metal batteries
abstract: A high-entropy supramolecular zwitterion solid electrolyte (HESZ-SPE) was developed via in situ polymerization. Its unique Li + solvation and hydrogen-bond networks enable batteries with superior cycling stability and high coulombic efficiency.

title: A carbon quantum dot (CQD) modified-CuZn bimetallic catalyst for efficient electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: The development of highly selective and active catalysts for electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction (CO 2 ER) is essential for its practical application.

title: Polyanion hydrogel electrolyte with a high Zn 2+ transference number for dendrite-free aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: The synergistic effect of –SO 3 − , –CONH–, and –OH in PMZA gel electrolyte enables fast ion transport, inhibits side reactions, and achieves long cycle life.

title: Field-enhanced chemical vapor deposition: new perspectives for thin film growth
abstract: Chemical vapor deposition (CVD) is a versatile technique for producing thin films and coatings of functional materials with diverse mechanical, electrochemical, electrical, tribological, and optical properties.

title: Advancing high capacity 3D VO 2 (B) cathodes for improved zinc-ion battery performance
abstract: This study explores 3D electrodes of VO 2 (B) materials in Zn-ion batteries, employing refined synthesis approaches and diverse electrode processing methods to achieve enhanced capacities and stable capacity retention.

title: Toughening self-healable and recyclable PDMS supramolecular elastomers through an end-capping agent and a metallic crosslinker
abstract: We report a robust, self-healable, and recyclable PDMS elastomer, enhanced by end-capping with 2,4-pentanedione and aluminium coordination. By incorporating Ag nanoflakes, we develop a flexible, self-healing dry electrode patch for ECG monitoring.

title: First phosphite-bridging lanthanide–bismuth heterometallic selenotungstates and their ratio-metric-temperature luminescence sensing properties
abstract: First phosphite-bridging lanthanide–bismuth heterometallic selenotungstates {Eu 4 Bi 2 P 2 W 42 } and {Tb 4 Bi 2 P 2 W 42 } were prepared, and their ratio-metric-temperature luminescence sensing properties were studied.

title: Stability regulation of metal–organic framework materials for electrocatalysis
abstract: The stability of metal–organic framework materials (MOFs) greatly affects their catalytic performance and cycle life in practical applications.

title: High entropy alloys: a comprehensive review of synthesis, properties, and characterization for electrochemical energy conversion and storage applications
abstract: A comprehensive overview of recent developments in catalysis related to HEAs, focusing on critical areas such as the HER, OER, ORR, hydrogen storage, zinc–air batteries, and supercapacitors.

title: A near-surface sulfidation regulation strategy for inverted inorganic perovskite solar cells with 21.21% efficiency
abstract: Inverted inorganic perovskite solar cells (IPSCs) have garnered significant attention in recent years due to their optimal bandgap and exceptional thermal stability.

title: Highly transparent supercapacitor based on web-like silver nanowire film electrodes
abstract: A transparent supercapacitor was developed using a uniformly coated web-like silver nanowire thin film electrode.

title: Synthesizing Zr-based oxynitride with low defects by CaH 2 -assisted nitridation for photocatalytic Z-scheme overall water splitting
abstract: The Zr-based oxynitride, Zr 2 ON 2 , which exhibits a low defect density and was synthesized using a novel CaH 2 -assisted nitridation method, can be utilized as a hydrogen evolution photocatalyst in a Z-scheme overall water splitting system.

title: Interactions at heterointerfaces influence actuation in wet cast 1T-MoS 2 and V 2 O 5 ·0.5H 2 O thin films
abstract: Stronger MoS 2 –Au interactions result in greater transduction of strain in trilayer electrochemical actuators than analogous interactions across heterointerfaces within MoS 2 –Ni or V 2 O 5 -based systems.

title: Co nanoparticles on B, N–Co-doped sp 2 /sp 3 carbon: a synergistic catalyst design for sustainable ammonia production from nitrate
abstract: Precursor-controlled triple synergy tunes B–C/N–C bonding and sp 2 /sp 3 phases, yielding Co/BNC with strong Co–carbon coupling and 98% NH 3 selectivity.

title: Synergistic oxygen vacancy and Pt single-atom engineering in hollow SnO 2 nanospheres for ultrasensitive ppb-level DMMP detection
abstract: Pt single-atom decorated SnO 2 hollow nanospheres enable rapid and highly sensitive detection of the nerve agent simulant DMMP by virtue of the synergistic effects of atomic Pt and the hollow nanostructure.

title: Optimizing the energy storage performance of titanium carbonitride MXenes for potassium-ion batteries by modulating nitrogen content
abstract: C/N-tuned Ti 3 (C 1− y N y ) 2 MXenes achieve ultralow K + migration barriers (0.001 eV) and enhanced storage capacity for ultrafast-charging KIBs.

title: Correction: Unveiling LiF-engineered MXene: a novel Ti 3 C 2 T x /ZnO hybrid composite for highly selective and sensitive chemiresistive NH 3 detection via Schottky barrier modulation at ambient temperature
abstract: Correction for ‘Unveiling LiF-engineered MXene: a novel Ti 3 C 2 T x /ZnO hybrid composite for highly selective and sensitive chemiresistive NH 3 detection via Schottky barrier modulation at ambient temperature’ by Gowri Shonali Natarajamani et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039…

title: Correction: Unraveling the role of the Mn–[O x ]–Ce structure in MnO x /CeO 2 catalysts for the catalytic oxidation of VOCs: resistance to sintering, increasing oxygen vacancies and activation of lattice oxygen
abstract: Correction for ‘Unraveling the role of the Mn–[O x ]–Ce structure in MnO x /CeO 2 catalysts for the catalytic oxidation of VOCs: resistance to sintering, increasing oxygen vacancies and activation of lattice oxygen’ by Yuchuan Ye et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA07303K.

title: Lithiophilic Zn–Ag alloy film-modified Cu current collector prepared by magnetron sputtering for stable lithium metal batteries
abstract: Uniform and dense ZnAg140w@Cu prepared by magnetron sputtering enables dendrite-free lithium plating, stable SEI formation, and cycling stability exceeding 3500 hours.

title: Solventless, rapid-polymerizable liquid resins from solid carboxylic acids through low-viscosity acid/base complexes
abstract: In this work, we demonstrate the formation of ionic-bonded complexes between an amino methacrylate and various solid carboxylic acids, which is robust “hardener” for epoxy resins through dual-cure reactions.

title: Harnessing metal–ligand complex and photothermal conversion in hydrogels for advanced atmospheric water harvesting
abstract: Anchoring metal ions via ligand coordination enhances hydrogel water adsorption while preventing leakage or aggregation, overcoming the limits of salt-loaded AWH systems. The study also examines water desorption kinetics and thermodynamics.

title: Designing highly selective NO 2 and SO 3 sensors via doped and substituted polythiophene: a DFT, NEGF, and microkinetic study
abstract: A synergistic NEGF–DFT–MK framework reveals Li- and NH 2 -modified polythiophene as highly selective NO 2 and SO 3 gas sensors, featuring ultralow detection limits, fast recovery, and cyclic electron transfer pathways.

title: Rational design of ZrO 2 -supported d-block single-atom electrochemical catalysts for the nitrogen reduction reaction
abstract: The development of effective and robust electrochemical catalysts for the nitrogen reduction reaction (NRR) remains a substantial challenge for ammonia synthesis under mild conditions.

title: Modulation of the electronic structure of Fe/Co 2D-MOFs by amination for the promotion of 1 O 2 generation through hydrogen bonding with H 2 O 2 : the hitherto overlooked role of ligands
abstract: An amino group into the catalyst is conducive to changing the adsorption mode of H 2 O 2 , promoting the selective generation of 1 O 2 .

title: Designing an iron-doped basic magnesium sulfate photocatalyst for wide spectral photoresponse and superior catalytic activity
abstract: This study explored a self-supporting iron-doped basic magnesium sulfate (Fe-BMS) photocatalyst with a wide spectral response.

title: Unity of opposites: stabilization of cationic M 6 metal cluster and anionic Lindqvist-type polyoxotungstate in hybrid salts and synergism in photodegradation of organic pollutants
abstract: Photoinduced electron transfer from cluster to POM in hybrid salts strongly depends on cluster core composition. The obtained materials demonstrate pronounced photocatalytic activity in dye degradation under white light.

title: Sterically directed, site-preferential selenization of discrete copper sulfide nanoclusters
abstract: Site-preferential selenization of [Cu 12 S 6 ] nanoclusters arises from differentiated steric environments of apical and equatorial sites. This phenomenon is observed by X-ray crystallography and VT-NMR spectroscopy, and is supported by DFT calculations.

title: Construction of CF@Cu x O/CoFeO nanowire arrays with an optimized bandgap to facilitate photo-assisted electrocatalytic water splitting
abstract: Light-sensitive CF@Cu x O/CoFeO bifunctional catalysts in nanowire arrays were prepared to promote overall water splitting under light irradiation.

title: Extreme pH-tolerant FeOOH@NiAl-LDH nanohybrid adsorbents: ultra-stable dye adsorption driven by multiple synergistic mechanisms and potent antibacterial performance
abstract: The discharge and accumulation of organic dyes in the environment have become pressing issues that require urgent attention.

title: Architecture of imidazolium-based poly(ionic liquid)s–cobalt hexagonal thin nanosheets for high-energy density, using membrane electrolytes
abstract: Imidazolium-based poly(ionic liquid)s integrated with cobalt hydroxide nanosheets deliver high energy density in solid-state supercapacitors, supported by membrane electrolytes.

title: Position-selective modulation of carboxyl groups on thiophene rings for dendrite-free zinc metal anodes
abstract: The unique functional groups and coordination environment of 3-thiophenecarboxylic acid (3TA) enable Zn 2+ desolvation acceleration and protective layer formation, effectively suppressing dendrites, the HER, and corrosion for stabilized Zn anodes.

title: Fluorine-free sulfonated polyhedral oligomeric silsesquioxanes: replacing polymeric binder in fuel cell catalyst layers
abstract: The implementation of sPOSS as a particle-based electrode binder enables increased current densities in fluorine-free PEM fuel cells.

title: Synchronous dual additives to boost multiphase interface stability of high-voltage Li-rich Mn-based batteries
abstract: The FEC-TMSPa dual additives can regulate the solvation structure of the electrolyte and construct robust organic–inorganic hybrid CEI/SEI to ensure the long cycle stability of high-voltage lithium metal batteries.

title: Self-assembled monolayer boosts the air-stability and electrochemical reversibility of O3-type layered oxides for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: This study develops a self-assembled, hydrophobic, and compact C 32 H 67 O 4 P layer on O3-NaNi 0.33 Fe 0.33 Mn 0.33 O 2 cathode, effectively shielding the electrode from air degradation and metal dissolution, thereby enhancing sodium-ion battery performance.

title: Homogenizing a liquid-like Cu sublattice for high-performance Cu 2 Te 0.5 Se 0.5 thermoelectric materials
abstract: We homogenize liquid-like Cu atoms via phase-dependent diffusion and quenching, suppressing clustering and vacancies by freezing a uniform Cu sublattice, thereby significantly reducing carrier concentration and lattice thermal conductivity.

title: Concurrent modification of under-surface reconstruction and additional coating layers via simple phosphoric acid treatment for high-stability Li-rich cathodes in Li-ion batteries
abstract: Under-surface Li 4 Mn 5 O 12 spinel and Li 3 PO 4 coated Li 1.13 Ni 0.30 Mn 0.57 O 2 is used for a Li-ion battery cathode. Uniform reconstruction underneath and additional coating are successfully achieved via phosphoric acid treatment and thermal treatment.

title: Atomistic characterization of hydration-dependent fuel cell ionomer nanostructure: validation by vibrational spectroscopy
abstract: ReaxFF MD of Nafion membranes yield hydration-dependent local water content distributions for –SO 3 H (α) and –SO 3 − (β) sites. The α-site spectra (α-subspectrum) and β-site spectra (β-subspectrum) convolute to form observed IR fingerprint spectra.

title: Hydrate-based H 2 storage with porous materials as heterogeneous promoters: state of the art and challenges
abstract: A multiscale strategy for porous material-assisted H 2 hydrate storage, bridging materials properties and engineering operations.

title: Straight-chain thiols as chemical kinetic transporters accelerate Li 2 S 3D nucleation
abstract: 1,8-OT is proposed as a chemical power transmitter to accelerate polysulfide conversion and control Li 2 S 3D nucleation through chemical catalysis. Ultimately, electrode passivation was suppressed and excellent cycling stability was achieved in LSBs.

title: Insight into the orbital origins of anisotropic carrier mobility in TaS 2 X 2 (X = Cl, Br, I) monolayers with highly efficient photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: The low-symmetry Ta 3+ coordination increases the VBM via occupied Ta-5d orbitals, enhancing visible light absorption and charge carrier separation.

title: Low-cost polyanion cathodes for sodium ion batteries: challenges, strategies, and progress
abstract: This review focuses on the modification measures of polyanionic cathode materials, pays attention to their improvement effect on the performance of active materials, and discusses the modification mechanism and the latest development trend.

title: Nature-inspired foam for treating oil-polluted water with enhanced fire protection
abstract: The development of superhydrophobic and flame-retardant bio-derived polyurethane foam addresses the issue of oil/solvent pollution in water, contributing to long-term environmental protection and resource sustainability.

title: Subsurface hydrogen, curvature, and strain: lessons from electro-reduction of benzaldehyde on nano-structured Pd catalysts
abstract: The ability of grain boundaries in nanostructured palladium (Pd) to promote hydrogen absorption and form a bulk hydride is vital for chemical transformations that involve hydrogenation reactions.

title: Dual-functional engineering of Sn-doping and polyaniline coating for Cs 3 Bi 2 Br 9 perovskite: boosting its photocatalytic activity and photostability in selective oxidation of benzyl alcohol
abstract: Dual-functional engineering of Sn-doping and conductive PANI coating to Cs 3 Bi 2 Br 9 perovskite for the selective conversion of benzyl alcohol to benzaldehyde.

title: Substitution and isomeric effects of triarylamine polyamides for electrochromic supercapacitors and heat-shielding applications
abstract: A sterically engineered, non-conjugated TPB-polyamide achieves high optical contrast, stable energy storage, and energy-saving heat-shielding performance for multifunctional ECS applications.

title: Rapid synthesis of a highly dispersed FeCoNiRuPt high-entropy alloy bifunctional electrocatalyst and exploration of the catalytic mechanism
abstract: This paper reports a facile synthesis of high-entropy alloy nanoparticle catalysts with superior water-splitting activity, linking structural reconstruction during catalysis to electrocatalytic mechanisms via in-depth characterization.

title: Integrated NiOOH@CoMnS hierarchical nanostructured electrocatalyst for sustainable hydrogen conversion via alkaline seawater electrolysis: synergistic design and mechanistic insights
abstract: A porous nanostructured NiOOH@CoMnS catalyst with well-designed charge redistribution exhibits excellent resistance to chloride corrosion during seawater electrolysis, enabling stable OER and HER stability and performance at high current densities.

title: Exploring the heterointerface of silver nanoparticles and cobalt oxide nanorings toward the oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: A unique Ag/Co 3 O 4 interface anchored on nitrogen-doped carbon nanosheets was synthesized via a one-pot route. The heterostructure exhibits excellent ORR performance, outperforming Pt-based catalysts due to synergistic interfacial effects.

title: Electrochemical properties of superflexible CNTs/MXene/Cu-doped V 2 O 5 buckypaper electrodes and wide-voltage all-solid-state supercapacitors
abstract: Using an superflexible, strong, free-standing electrode, this FSSC achieves a wide-voltage, high energy/power density, and long-term stability.

title: Tailoring block copolymer-type compatibilizers to enhance the performance and processability of inverted all-polymer solar cells
abstract: This study presents a compatibilization strategy that enhances performance and processability of inverted all-polymer solar cells by introducing block copolymer compatibilizers into the PM6:PYIT bulk heterojunction layer.

title: A MnNi-heterometallic perovskite hydroxide (pre)catalyst for electrochemical alcohol oxidation: insight into the active phase
abstract: At deep anodic potential under alkaline conditions, (pre)catalysts undergo dynamic transformations that irreversibly produce hydrated oxides.

title: Amplifying interfacial capacitance through underpotential deposition in salt-in-ionic liquid electrolytes
abstract: Underpotential deposition in alkali salt-in-ionic liquid electrolytes can be leveraged to boost capacitance at negative metal electrodes.

title: Impact of binder content on particle fracture and microstructure of solvent-free electrodes for Li-ion batteries
abstract: The fraction of polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) binder in solvent-free electrodes for Li-ion batteries, also known as dry-processed electrodes, is shown to have a dramatic impact on their processability, microstructure and electrochemical performance.

title: Expanding the Fe–Fe distance for superior electrochemical performance of Na 2.6 Fe 1.7 (SO 4 ) 3 cathodes for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: The alluaudite-type Na 2+2 x Fe 2− x (SO 4 ) 3 (NFS) has attracted attention as a promising cathode material for sodium-ion batteries, due to its remarkable working voltage and cost-effectiveness among various cathode candidates.

title: Combined effects of the hetero-interface of NiSe 2 –Ni 3 Se 4 and Fe doping markedly boost the electrocatalytic overall water splitting performance of a self-supported nanosheet array electrode
abstract: The combined effects of the hetero-interface of NiSe 2 –Ni 3 Se 4 and Fe doping markedly boost the electrocatalytic overall water splitting performance of the Fe–NiSe 2 –Ni 3 Se 4 /NF-2 electrode.

title: An in situ grown ZnIn 2 S 4 on Zn 2 SiO 4 :Ga 3+ core–shell heterojunction for photocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: A Zn 2 SiO 4 :Ga 3+ @ZnIn 2 S 4 core–shell heterojunction achieves 15.954 mmol per g per h H 2 production (2 times higher than that of ZnIn 2 S 4 ) via synergistic charge separation and deep trap states.

title: Sb 2 WO 6 modulated Co 3 O 4 with preferential adsorption enables efficient acidic oxygen evolution at low cost
abstract: Designing low-cost, active and durable acidic oxygen evolution reaction (OER) catalysts for proton exchange membrane water electrolyzers (PEMWEs) remains challenging.

title: MXene derived-TiO 2 /β-Ag 2 MoO 4 nanocomposite: a multifunctional electrode for enhanced energy storage in supercapacitors and lithium-ion batteries
abstract: M-TiO 2 /β-Ag 2 MoO 4 nanocomposite: a multifunctional electrode with excellent cycling stability for supercapacitors and strong Li-ion battery performance, enabled by its synergistic structure and redox activity.

title: Computational discovery of a novel double transition metal nitride MXene and its applications as an anchoring and catalytic material in Li–Se batteries
abstract: Discovery of new DTM nitride MXene and its application as a catalyst in Li–Se battery systems.

title: Multi-hydrogen adsorption of amorphous ReS x cocatalysts for boosting photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: A novel multi-hydrogen adsorption mechanism is proposed, where sulfur-rich ReS x /TiO 2 effectively regulates the H* adsorption/desorption energy barrier, thereby greatly promoting photocatalytic H 2 evolution.

title: Interpenetrating double network silicone hybrid aerogels with excellent mechanical properties and thermal stability for high-temperature insulation
abstract: A facile secondary sol–gel process creates robust, double-network silicone aerogels with strong chemical bonding between the two networks. The resulting materials exhibit both exceptional mechanical strength and superior high-temperature insulation.

title: Fabrication and electrochemical evaluation of flexible spinel CdMn 2 O 4 carbon nanofibers for advanced supercapacitor applications
abstract: The synthesis and application of spinel CdMn 2 O 4 carbon nanofibers and the fabrication of a flexible supercapacitor with 88.87% retention over 12 000 cycles, demonstrated through a watch connection.

title: Lithiated polymer coating for interface stabilization in Li 6 PS 5 Cl-based solid-state batteries with high-nickel NCM
abstract: During cell cycling with NCM cathode and Li 6 PS 5 Cl catholyte, interfacial degradation leads to reduced active mass and particle cracking. We mitigate this by coating NCM with polyvinylpyrrolidone and sulfonated poly(phenylene sulfone).

title: A flexible silk film based on synergy of hydrogen bonds and a cross-linking network for magnetic sensitive skin
abstract: Flexible silk-based anisotropic magnetoresistance skin is developed and used for monitoring human joint motions.

title: Decorating unsaturated iron–nitrogen coordination sites with small-sized iron selenide nanoparticles for highly efficient oxygen reduction catalysis
abstract: Due to energy and environmental challenges, cost-effective and highly efficient catalysts towards oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) are crucial for advanced Zn–air and Mg–air batteries.

title: Deciphering the local structure of Prussian blue analogue cathodes with Raman spectroscopy for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Operando Raman spectroscopy detects subtle structural evolutions, often obscured by XRD due to low scattering by elements like C and N and XRD's averaging nature, offering deeper insight into insertion mechanisms in Prussian blue analogue cathodes.

title: In situ pyrolysis of ZIF-67 with cobalt nitrate etching for the catalytic oxidation of methane: promoting surface lattice oxygen and molecular oxygen activation
abstract: Etching strategy promoted dual activation of surface lattice oxygen and molecular oxygen. The activation of molecular oxygen was the key factor for catalyst performance enhancement.

title: Synergizing hydrazine co-crystals with hydrazine salt of TNAE: a novel strategy for developing highly insensitive explosives
abstract: The first-ever combination of di-hydrazine co-crystallization with the tetrahydrazinium salt of 1,1,2,2-tetranitroaminoethane (TNAE) has been successfully achieved, resulting in highly stable energetic materials with excellent performance.

title: Copper tungstate photoanodes with enhanced solar water splitting performance
abstract: The enhanced photoelectrochemical water oxidation ability of nanostructured copper tungstate (CuWO 4 ) thin films is presented. The roles of solution chemistry and processing conditions required to achieve high performance are elucidated.

title: How do pore sizes affect the oxygen reduction reaction kinetics of platinum electrodes?
abstract: The relationship between the pore size and the oxygen reduction reaction kinetics is investigated using platinum model electrodes. The compressive strain causes the d-band centre downshift, which results in the quasi-volcano relationship.

title: Constructing abundant 5d electron buffering interfaces for enhancing acidic water oxidation
abstract: Through metal–support interactions to modulate the electronic structure of Ru active sites, a Ru/Ta 2 O 5 ( v ) catalyst with abundant 5d electron buffering interfaces was fabricated to balance the trade-off between catalytic activity and stability.

title: Suppressing migration of Ru in a high-entropy alloy for durable acidic oxygen evolution
abstract: The high-entropy alloy (HEA) catalyst FeCoNiMnRu significantly enhances the surface migration energy barrier of Ru atoms, thereby effectively suppressing their dissolution during the oxygen evolution reaction, exhibiting enhanced durability.

title: In situ self-reconstruction and oxygen evolution reaction mechanism study of Ni–Fe–V hydroxide synthesized by electrodeposition
