abstract: The NiFeV pre-catalyst electrochemically restructures into V-doped FeOOH and NiOOH. V leaching creates oxygen vacancies, activates the lattice oxygen mechanism, and synergizes with the adsorbate evolution mechanism to generate oxygen.

title: Boosting redox activity and charge transfer kinetics in hollow CoNiS/CoNi-LDH heterostructures via interface engineering for high-performance supercapacitors
abstract: Hollow CoNiS/CoNi-LDH heterostructures are designed via partial sulfurization of ZIF-derived CoNi-LDH for high-performance supercapacitors.

title: Towards robust hydrogen evolution electrocatalysts in immiscible copper–molybdenum alloys by amorphization
abstract: A novel amorphous Cu 50 Mo 50 alloy catalyst with dual active sites is successfully synthesized for efficient alkaline hydrogen evolution reaction (HER), in which Cu serves as the OH adsorption site and Mo acts as the H adsorption site.

title: Engineering sulfur vacancies and photothermal effects in a CoAl 2 O 4 /MnCdS S-scheme heterojunction for broad-spectrum photocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: An S-scheme CoAl 2 O 4 /MnCdS-Vs photocatalyst was fabricated via a three-step method for photocatalytic H 2 evolution. Its photothermal effect synergizes with sulfur vacancies to reduce reaction activation energy and accelerate surface reaction kinetics.

title: 3D NiCoP flowers assembled by 2D carbon-coated nanosheets as an efficient and stable positive electrode for supercapacitors
abstract: The porous 3D NiCoP flowers assembled by 2D carbon-coating nanosheets can be used as efficient and stable positive electrode for advanced asymmetric supercapacitors with high specific capacitance, good rate performance and long-term stability.

title: Flexible cellulose nanofiber/graphene films with liquid metal-filled microvoids for enhanced thermal management and electromagnetic interference shielding
abstract: Flexible, ultra-thin thermal conductive materials with highly efficient electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding are urgently needed for next-generation wearable electronics.

title: Promotion mechanisms of LiBH 4 dehydrogenation dominated by charge redistribution
abstract: Li vacancies and transition metals doping cause the loss of electrons in B–H covalent bonds and the rising of electrons in the antibonding orbitals of the B–H bonds, respectively, leading to the weakening of the B–H bond strength.

title: Triazole-boosted dual-structured covalent triazine frameworks for ultra-stable high-energy and -power density aqueous supercapacitors and notable selective CO 2 capture
abstract: Triazole-functionalized covalent triazine frameworks, integrating a partially graphitic architecture and bimodal-porosity enable ultra-stable, high energy-power density aqueous supercapacitor including notable low-pressure selective CO 2 capture.

title: Ce-doped derived dual-phase 1T/2H-MoS 2 : a promising catalyst for highly efficient electrocatalytic oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: We synthesized a Ce–MoS 2 -2 electrocatalyst using a one-step hydrothermal method, achieving a low OER overpotential of 215 mV and a total water decomposition voltage of 1.562 V at 10 mA cm −2 , demonstrating remarkable activity.

title: Boosting the practical applications of zinc-ion batteries via zwitterion-mediated solvation and interfacial chemistry
abstract: Zwitterion-mediated synergy creates a self-regulating AZIB environment. It establishes a water-deficient electric double layer, regulates interfacial pH, and guides Zn 2+ flux, enhancing deposition reversibility and cycle life.

title: Operando APXPS for direct probing of Li ion battery LCO electrode/electrolyte interface chemistry during lithiation/delithiation
abstract: Operando APXPS for interface chemistry on Li ion battery materials LCO during lithiation and delithiation.

title: Enhanced electrochemical performance of aqueous Zn-ion batteries based on Na 2 V 6 O 16 ·2H 2 O cathodes: insights from DFT and synchrotron X-ray analysis
abstract: A Na 2 V 6 O 16 ·2H 2 O cathode, synthesized via Na pre-intercalation using a sonochemical method, enhances Zn-ion storage by improving structural stability. The NaVO//Zn battery delivers 126 mA h g −1 and retains 91.8% of its capacity after 10 000 cycles.

title: Ultra-flexible organic solar cells based on eco-friendly cellulose substrate with efficiency approaching 19%
abstract: An eco-friendly EC-based ultra-flexible substrate has been developed with ETPTA. Based on this substrate, an ultra-flexible and ultralight organic solar cell is fabricated, achieving an impressive efficiency approaching 19%.

title: Anion–cation synergistic interactions for low-temperature and fast-charging performance in sodium batteries
abstract: Schematic diagram of cation–anion coordinated regulation for low-temperature fast charging in the 1 M-BG2-LP electrolyte.

title: Roles of Ru on PtRu surface in electrocatalytic ammonia oxidation
abstract: Ru-based catalysts shed new light on ammonia oxidation, showing lowered onset potential on PtRu and unveiling azide formation, opening pathways for efficient fuel cells and high-value products.

title: Structural responses of metal–organic frameworks to non-thermal plasma treatment and their effects on CO 2 adsorption and conversion performances
abstract: Non-thermal plasma (NTP) broke the Cu-O 1 bond, resulting in the structure destruction of Cu-BTC; however, Zr-BDC was structurally resistant to NTP treatment, which was essential for NTP-assisted CO 2 conversion of MOF catalysts.

title: Bifunctional high-entropy alloy electrocatalysts for stable overall water splitting at industrial-level current densities
abstract: A scalable plasma-spraying approach affords the FeCoNiCrMn high-entropy alloy catalyst with robust bifunctional OER/HER activity and industrial-grade stability, bridging lab innovation and large-scale hydrogen production.

title: Balancing Ru–O bond covalency and strength via atomic Ta doping for robust acidic oxygen evolution
abstract: Atomic Ta doping via a molten-salt route forms asymmetric Ta–O–Ru active sites that optimize Ru–O covalency and reinforce the Ta–O–Ru linkage, enabling RuO x –10Ta to achieve 189 mV at 10 mA cm −2 and >700 h stability of acidic OER.

title: Thermally driven oxygen functionalization for durable Pt electrocatalysts in the oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: This study focuses on enhancing Pt/C durability by controlling oxygen groups on carbon supports and uniformly dispersing Pt nanoparticles. The high durability results from increased binding energy between oxygen groups and Pt nanoparticles.

title: Lignocellulosic biomass and its main structural polymers as sustainable materials for (bio)sensing applications
abstract: (Bio)sensors are integral to various aspects of daily life, contributing to safety, monitoring, and awareness.

title: Recent advances in dimethyl oxalate hydrogenation: integrating catalyst design with reaction engineering for sustainable production of C 2 oxygenates
abstract: Selective dimethyl oxalate hydrogenation: unlocking carbon circularity via integrated catalyst design and process innovation for sustainable C 2 oxygenate chemicals production.

title: Hematite nanobelts with ordered oxygen vacancies for bifunctional electrocatalytic water splitting
abstract: Long-range ordered oxygen vacancies optimize the adsorption/desorption kinetics of intermediates, and establish a highly efficient conduit for charge transport, which can simultaneously accelerate the kinetics of the HER and OER.

title: Molecular insights into the influence of synergistic effect on adsorption–hydration hybrid CH 4 storage in ZIF-8@AC composite materials
abstract: Synergistic effect causes enhanced surface tension and adsorption induction by coordinating the adsorption of parent materials, which promotes the mass transfer and the formation of hydrogen bonds and leads more CH 4 molecules are stored in hydrates.

title: MXene and cellulose nanocrystal co-stabilized Pickering emulsions and their applications as templates to fabricate photothermal phase change material microcapsules
abstract: An MXene and cellulose nanocrystal co-stabilized Pickering emulsion is developed and employed as a template to fabricate photothermal phase change material microcapsules for solar energy harvesting, heat storage and release, and thermal management.

title: Synergistic engineering of micron-sized porous silicon anodes via Ge doping and liquid metal alloy modification for high-energy-density lithium-ion batteries
abstract: In contrast to nanosilicon, micron-sized silicon anodes have gained widespread attention due to their high energy density, favorable processability, and reduced side reactions.

title: High performance sulfide all-solid-state batteries enabled by Li 1.26 Mg 0.12 Zr 1.86 (PO 4 ) 3 coating of iron fluoride cathodes
abstract: A scalable Li 1.26 Mg 0.12 Zr 1.86 (PO 4 ) 3 coating improves the cycle performance of nano-sized FeF 2 (n-FeF 2 ) and micro-sized FeF 3 (m-FeF 3 ) cathodes in all-solid-state lithium batteries even with lithium metal anodes under reduced stack pressure.

title: Selenium-infiltration-driven interfacial tailoring enables high-performance sodium-ion battery anodes
abstract: Selenium infiltration induces in situ Cu 1.8 Se interfaces in TiO 2 anodes, greatly enhancing sodium-ion storage capacity and cycling stability.

title: Controlling metal–organic framework crystallization via computer vision and robotic handling
abstract: A closed-loop robotic and computer vision system was developed to control and quantify MOF crystallization outcomes.

title: Strong p–d orbital hybridization on PdSn metallene for enhanced electrooxidation of plastic-derived alcohols to glycolic acid
abstract: PdSn metallene has been developed for high-selectivity electrocatalytic conversion of PET plastic-derived EG into GA due to the optimized adsorption energy and strengthen the bonding energy of the C–C bond and hydroxyl group.

title: Graph-based intermolecular interaction prediction enables rational design of co-crystals
abstract: An interaction energy-driven criterion for co-crystal screening was developed, leveraging a high-precision graph neural network-based interaction energy predictor to significantly enhance screening efficiency.

title: Impact of external short circuit on lithium-ion batteries: a post-mortem case study
abstract: X-ray computed tomography (CT) images showing cross-sectional and longitudinal views of a 21700 Li-ion cell: (A) pristine after 0 V discharge, (B) after short-circuit (ESC), highlighting internal structural changes.

title: Photocatalytic hydrazine-assisted reduction of nitroarenes promoted by Cu dopant modulated In 2 S 3 under ambient conditions
abstract: We have introduced Cu atoms into intrinsic In 2 S 3 to promote vectorial separation of reactive sites, thereby enabling efficient, mild, and CO 2 emission-free photocatalytic reduction of nitroarenes under hydrazine-assistanced.

title: Bottom-up assembly of recyclable van der Waals-integrated photocatalysts towards efficient photoelectrocatalytic degradation
abstract: Large-scale synthesis of recyclable photocatalysts for pollutant degradation remains a challenge.

title: Rational design of hierarchical hollow-core dual-shell amorphous 3D nanospheres as an effective electrode material for hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Hierarchical hollow-core dual-shell amorphous three-dimensional nanospheres were synthesized for hybrid supercapacitor (HSC). The HSC exhibited excellent electrochemical properties, demonstrating real-time applications.

title: Self-assembly of anisotropic nano-sheets to impede charge injection into polymer dielectrics
abstract: The self-assembled anisotropic coating modifies the surface defects of amorphous polymers, inhibiting charge injection and greatly improving the breakdown strength and storage efficiency.

title: A single lithium-ion conducting monomer as a SEI-forming additive for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A novel single lithium-ion conducting monomer was synthesized and introduced as an electrolyte additive in graphite/NCM cells, significantly enhancing their cycling stability and high-rate performance.

title: Graphene oxide lowers carbon monoxide polymerization pressure through chemical pre-compression
abstract: Polymeric carbon monoxide (p-CO) is one of only a few low-Z extended solids that form under high pressure and can be recovered under ambient conditions.

title: Structural regulation of manganese ferrocyanide compounds for palladium separation: improved crystallinity and enhanced selectivity
abstract: The regulated KMnFC demonstrates improved crystallinity with narrower lattice spacing and negligible defect sites, which inhibited the adsorption of Cs( i ) and enhanced the Pd( ii ) adsorption selectivity.

title: Physicochemical properties and application of concentrated KN(SO 2 F) 2 /sulfolane solution in high-voltage high-power K-ion batteries
abstract: A highly concentrated electrolyte of KN(SO 2 F) 2 (KFSA) and sulfolane (SL) was developed as a K-ion battery electrolyte. The equimolar mixture solution of KFSA/SL exhibited a wide potential window and improved the rate capability of K 2 Mn[Fe(CN) 6 ].

title: A high-pressure enabled high-entropy (CrFeCoNiMn) 4 S 5 composite anode for enhanced durability and high-rate sodium-ion batteries
abstract: A high-entropy transition metal sulfide anode composite is prepared using a high-pressure, high-temperature technique, which demonstrates enhanced cycling stability, reduced polysulfide shuttle effect, and improved sodium storage performance.

title: Excess of Zn to relieve the structural distortion of manganese hexacyanoferrate in aqueous Zn-ion battery
abstract: The effect of Zn substitution on the electrochemical performance and structure stability of MnHCF was comprehensively investigated using different synchrotron X-ray techniques.

title: Uniformly dispersed zincophilic Cu sites on copper mesh enable a dendrite-free Zn metal anode
abstract: Cu 2 O modified copper mesh is designed as a high-performance current collector for anodes in aqueous zinc metal batteries, which effectively reduces Zn nucleation barriers via an irreversible phase transition between Cu 2 O and Cu.

title: Sequence isomerization of dielectric elastomers toward high performances in electrostrain, elastic energy storage, and energy harvesting
abstract: Head-to-head and tail-to-tail (HHTT) isomerization improves DE performances in electrostrain, energy storage, and energy harvesting by enhancing the dipole alignment in network strands.

title: A biological pathway to synthesize Ag/Fe 3 O 4 heterostructures with high catalytic performance
abstract: Green biosynthesis via magnetosome microreactors yields defect-enriched Ag/Fe 3 O 4 nanocatalysts with high catalytic performance.

title: Upcycling waste PET to oxygen-rich carbon nanotubes for high-performance supercapacitor with ultra-high cycling stability
abstract: Catalytic pyrolysis converts waste PET bottles into oxygen-functionalized carbon nanotubes for supercapacitor electrodes, demonstrating 103.6% capacity retention after 10 000 cycles in a two-electrode system.

title: Shaping low-iridium IrRuO x electrocatalysts with structural and electronic modulation for proton exchange membrane electrolyzers
abstract: Reducing iridium (Ir) loading without compromising the stability of the oxygen evolution reaction (OER) activity is essential for the sustainable deployment of proton exchange membrane (PEM) electrolyzers.

title: Energy-free corrosion engineering for in situ growth of iron oxyhydroxide nanosheets on nickel foam toward efficient oxygen evolution
abstract: In situ construction of NiOOH/FeOOH heterostructure as efficient OER electrocatalyst by room-temperature corrosion engineering.

title: Sustainable synthesis of porous carbon with molecular sieve-like properties from waste biomass-derived heavy oil via dual green activation strategy for enhanced aqueous environmental remediation
abstract: Green synthesis of carbon molecular sieve adsorbents from waste heavy bio-oil residues for enhanced water remediation and resource recovery.

title: Bay-substituted perylene diimide-based donor–acceptor type copolymers: design, synthesis, optical and energy storage behaviours
abstract: Four bay-substituted perylene diimide (PDI) copolymers have been designed and synthesized successfully to demonstrate the effect of bay-substituent on the photo-physical and capacitive properties.

title: Synergistic effect of Pt single atoms, clusters and nanoparticles on carbon doping with nitrogen for highly efficient and selective hydrogenation of nitroaromatics
abstract: Pt single atoms, clusters and nanoparticles are together on nitrogen-doped carbon. There is electron interaction between pyridinic nitrogen and Pt (electrons from Pt to N), improving the catalytic performance for nitroarenes selective hydrogenation.

title: Interface engineering approach of in-air-processed Sb 2 S 3 solar cells enabling 7.5% AM 1.5G device efficiency and an 18% indoor milestone performance
abstract: Ultrathin ZnO interlayer boosts TiO 2 –based Sb 2 S 3 solar cells to record efficiencies 7.5% AM 1.5G and 18% indoor. Reduced nonradiative losses, passivated front interface, and larger Sb 2 S 3 grains enable stable power for IoT applications.

title: α and γ mixed-phase Ga 2 O 3 as a photocatalyst for CO 2 reduction with water: mechanism and the role of each phase
abstract: Gallium oxides (Ga 2 O 3 ) with mixed phases of α/β, β/γ, and α/γ are known to show high catalytic activity for the photoreduction of CO 2 with water to produce CO, H 2 and O 2 .

title: Enabling high-performance and high-rate-capability Na 4 MnV(PO 4 ) 3 sodium-ion battery cathodes through tuning the NASICON framework
abstract: Mo-doped Na 4 MnV(PO 4 ) 3 , where Mo is at the P site, demonstrating improved electronic conductivity and outstanding rate performance.

title: Aqueous ionic self-assembly of a donor–acceptor viologen and eosin Y-based porous ionic salt for enhanced photocatalytic aerobic oxidation
abstract: A photoactive donor–acceptor viologen–eosin Y porous ionic salt (VEY-PIS) was constructed by room-temperature aqueous ionic self-assembly for boosting photocatalytic oxidation.

title: Aminophenol–formaldehyde particles containing hydrophilic benzenoid-amine for a highly efficient solar-thermal water harvester
abstract: A solar-driven interfacial evaporation system utilizing aminophenol–formaldehyde resin particles as solar-absorbing materials achieves a high water evaporation rate.

title: Highly efficient and recyclable sulfonic acid-modified polypropylene fiber catalysts for epoxide ring-opening, aldol condensation and heterocyclic synthesis
abstract: Polypropylene fibers (PPFs) were functionalized with sulfonic acid via low-temperature solution grafting to afford a multifunctional catalyst (PP CSA F), which efficiently promoted reactions involving three distinct bond types.

title: Reduced graphene oxides prepared via mass loading-controlled non-explosive thermal reduction for high volumetric capacitance supercapacitors
abstract: NE-TRGO was prepared by controlling the mass loading of the GO film. The NE-TRGO exhibits a high packing density of 1.94 g cm −3 . The volumetric specific capacitance of RGO-300-1.35 is 609 F cm −3 at 0.1 A g −1 .

title: Modulation of electron transfer in electrochemiluminescence: synergistic utilization of doping effects in the material and composite effects of Au in the core–shell structure
abstract: Elucidating the electron transfer mechanism and its regulatory strategy in the electrochemiluminescence (ECL) phenomenon is undoubtedly a challenging task.

title: A hybrid pyro-phototronic nanogenerator (HPyNG) for ultra-low light detection
abstract: A pyro-phototronic nanogenerator (HPyNG) using an organic–inorganic hybrid material enables nano energy harvesting. With different pyroelectric origins, both materials synergize to enhance optoelectronic efficiency and achieve ultra-sensitive light detection.

title: Triplet–triplet annihilation upconversion sensitized with nanocrystals for a new generation of photocatalytic systems
abstract: Nanocrystal (NC) sensitizers enable triplet–triplet annihilation photon upconversion (TTA-UC), converting NIR or visible light into higher-energy photons and offering promising potential for applications, especially in solar-driven reactions.

title: High-entropy compounds for photo(electro)catalysis: diverse materials and applications
abstract: High-entropy compounds harness multi-element synergy to deliver exceptional photo(electro)catalytic activity, stability and tunable functionality, while addressing mechanistic challenges and design strategies for energy/environmental applications.

title: Core–shell porous ZnO p–n homojunction nanorod arrays for achieving superior water splitting efficiency via piezocatalytic and piezo-phototronic effects
abstract: The porous core–shell ZnO p–n homojunction exhibits a 4-fold photocurrent density increase, driven by built-in electric fields and piezocatalytic and piezo-phototronic effects.

title: Alginate–oligothiophene aerogels as photocatalysts for the degradation of emerging organic contaminants in water
abstract: Oligothiophenes bearing a benzothiophene (BT) core and S -oxide or S , S -dioxide moieties are herein proposed as photocatalysts for the degradation of three selected water emerging contaminants.

title: Mixed-phase enabled high-rate copper niobate anodes for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Mixed-phase copper niobate anodes for lithium-ion batteries consisting of various phases work synergistically to deliver high electrochemical capacities at exceptional cycling rates.

title: On the cation distribution in acceptor doped Ba(Zr,Ce)O 3 -(Zr,Ce)O 2 co-ionic composite electrolytes
abstract: The functionality of co-ionic composite electrolytes with fluorite and perovskite phases depends on choice of cations and fabrication. Acceptor dopants migrate to the fluorite phase, depleting the perovskite, but this can be mitigated by tailored strategies.

title: How common is it to get an OER overpotential that is <250 mV?
abstract: This work raises concerns about the unintentional mistakes made by researchers developing OER electrocatalysts by overlooking the fundamentals.

title: Operando elucidation of all six reversible Li–Ga phase transitions, inverted hysteresis, and interfacial dynamics in a nanoconfined CMK-3/Ga anode
abstract: Operando CV-EIS-XRD maps six reversible Li–Ga alloy phases and inverted hysteresis in ball-milled CMK-3/Ga. Nanoconfined Ga enhances Li + transport, phase reversibility, and forms a self-regulating CuGa 2 interphase that stabilizes Cu collectors for LIBs.

title: An efficient WS 2 /Ni 3 S 2 monolithic electrode for the hydrogen evolution reaction at industrial current densities
abstract: The interface of WS 2 and Ni 3 S 2 bimetallic sulfide on nickel foam can promote H adsorption at W sites for HER, enabling the catalyst to exhibit extraordinary hydrogen production from water electrolysis at industrial current densities.

title: Enhancement of electron transfer efficiency in biofuel cell anodes using biocompatible redox-active ferritin and enzyme assemblies
abstract: This study improves electron transfer in biofuel cell anodes through a spin coating-assisted layer-by-layer assembly of redox-active ferritin and enzyme, resulting in enhanced current density, stability, and biocompatibility for energy applications.

title: Molten salt-assisted synthesis of a nitrogen-doped biochar catalyst at low temperature for enhanced degradation of acetaminophen
abstract: Nitrogen (N) doping is an efficient modification route to improve the catalytic performance of biochar in peroxymonosulfate (PMS) activation, and molten salt-assisted pyrolysis is an effective and eco-friendly approach to increase the N doping level.

title: Machine vision-enabled surface temperature mapping based on thermo-responsive cholesteric liquid crystal elastomer arrays
abstract: Combining thermo-responsive cholesteric liquid crystal elastomers (CLCE) with machine vision enables precise temperature mapping. Two machine learning models detect temperature values and distributions for robust material-based monitoring.

title: TAMOF-1 for capture and separation of the main flue gas components
abstract: Atomistic and numerical simulations are combined with experiments to investigate the adsorption and separation performance of TAMOF-1. It was found that TAMOF-1 can efficiently separate CO 2 from N 2 , which is relevant for flue gas applications.

title: Intensified gas diffusion in ordered mesoporous PtCo alloys for enhanced oxygen reduction electrocatalysis
abstract: Three-dimensional ordered meso-structured PtCo alloy nanoparticles are fabricated to enhance O 2 diffusion and accelerate the kinetics of oxygen reduction in proton-exchange membrane fuel cells.

title: Recent progress in atomic-level manufacturing of two-dimensional transition metal dichalcogenides beyond exfoliation and restacking
abstract: Two-dimensional transition metal dichalcogenides (2DTMDCs) are promising in quantum computing, flexible electronics, spintronics, sustainable energy systems, and advanced healthcare.

title: A phase-optimized NiFe-LDH/NiB heterostructure as an efficient and durable oxygen evolution electrocatalyst in alkaline media
abstract: A heterostructure with NiB stabilizes the γ-Ni(Fe)OOH phase and increases its proportion, mitigating the degradation of the NiFe-LDH electrocatalyst under the OER cycling conditions.

title: Constructing highly reversible zinc batteries under high depth of discharge & current density conditions via quaternary ammonium cations modulating electric field force and competitive solvation
abstract: TMA + neutralizes negative charges on Zn deposits, optimizing Zn 2+ deposition and reducing the EDL electric field. It suppresses dendritic growth and competes with Zn 2+ for water molecules, enhancing the performance of symmetric cells under high DoD.

title: Efficient non-fused electron acceptor with C-shaped molecular geometry for photovoltaic application
abstract: A novel non-fused acceptor, AC2, with a C-shaped geometry and 3D stacking property, was designed for organic solar cells. AC2 exhibits efficient charge transport and reduced energy loss, which broadens the design prospects for non-fused acceptors.

title: Ultrahigh moisture electric output of oxidation-controlled and ion-intercalated graphene oxide films in the low humidity range
abstract: Crystallinity enhancement and ion intercalation enable ultrahigh moisture-induced electric output in graphene oxide films even at low humidity.

title: Optimization strategies for flexible aqueous rechargeable sodium-ion batteries (ARSIBs)
abstract: This review aims to discuss and summarize the optimization strategies for the performance of flexible ARSIBs, their stability under extreme conditions, and the multifunctional applications in wearable integrated electronic systems.

title: Decoding chemical structure–interface correlation in covalent organic framework for sustainable organic electrosynthesis
abstract: The role of covalent organic frameworks' physicochemical properties on the structure-dynamics of electrical double layer is rationalised for probe electrocatalysis for the first time, revealing novel insights into charge and mass transfer processes.

title: An LED-driven Hematite/Bi 4 O 5 I 2 nanocomposite as an S-scheme heterojunction photocatalyst for efficient degradation of phenolic compounds in real wastewater
abstract: Heterojunction-based photocatalysts have recently emerged as a promising solution for removing organic pollutants from wastewater, especially those synthesized using low-carbon footprint methods.

title: Tuning electrochemical performance and interfacial compatibility of oxygen electrodes in proton-conducting solid oxide electrolysis cells
abstract: Modifying perovskite into A-site deficient electrode: oxygen vacancy enhances surface kinetics and durability, enabling superior performance and stability for protonic ceramic cells.

title: Research progress of heterogeneous catalysts with different dimensional supports for catalytic hydrolysis of ammonia borane
abstract: This paper reviews the latest research progress in hydrogen production by AB hydrolysis from the perspective of supported catalysts of different dimensions and discusses their catalytic mechanism, synthesis methods and reaction processes.

title: Facilitating polysulfide conversion kinetics via multifunctional solid-state electrolytes under lean electrolyte conditions for lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: A Li 10 GeP 2 S 12 superionic conductor provides efficient lithium-ion pathways in lean-electrolyte Li–S batteries, enhancing polysulfide affinity and conversion kinetics.

title: Regulating the anionic environment of the COF@CNT composite for kinetics-boosted and wide-temperature lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Ionic COF@CNT with tailored anion centers for wide-temperature Li–S batteries.

title: Highly flexible GO–polyurethane solid–solid phase change composite materials for efficient photothermal conversion and thermal energy storage
abstract: A linearly crosslinked solid–solid phase change polyurethane network was developed. It has excellent enthalpy of melting and flexibility. It also exhibits excellent photothermal conversion efficiency after adding GO.

title: Instant self-healing adhesive hydrogel sensors: dual-network design for real-time human motion tracking
abstract: A hydrogel sensor with dual-network was designed, demonstrating excellent mechanical properties, adhesion, self-healing and sensing performance due to the dynamically reversible hydrogen bonds and electrostatic interactions.

title: Multifunctional 1-naphthoic acid additive enhancing ultraviolet stability of perovskite solar cells
abstract: Multifunctional 1-naphthoic acid additive enhances PSCs' UV stability through energy relaxation processes and passivation, achieving 25.36% efficiency and 84% retention after 240 hours under intense UV light (50 mW cm −2 ).

title: Advancing oxygen evolution reaction efficiency in iron phthalocyanines: axial coordination as a key to structural and electronic tuning
abstract: Axial coordination significantly enhances the OER activity of Fe–N 4 sites due to the electron-withdrawing effect of ligands, which destabilizes OER intermediates and improves catalysis.

title: Advanced characterization and functional evaluation of Ni-/Fe-doped Cu–Mn spinels as Co-free coatings for SOFC/SOEC interconnects
abstract: Coatings based on Cu–Mn doped with nickel show better electrical properties, corrosion resistance and lower reactivity towards Cr 2 O 3 than those doped with iron.

title: Reverse design of high-detonation-velocity organic energetic compounds based on an accurate BPNN with wide applicability
abstract: Key factors affecting detonation velocity ( D ) are identified with machine learning (2% error), and high- D energetic compounds are designed.

title: Amorphous zinc–molybdenum–sulfide chalcogel as a long-cycle, high-capacity electrode for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: This study reports an amorphous Zn x Mo 3 S 13 chalcogel, reveals its local structure, and shows outstanding Li/Zn x Mo 3 S 13 electrochemical performance enabled by its amorphous structure, Zn-mediated polysulfide anchoring, and a stable SEI layer.

title: High-performance aqueous sodium-ion storage using bundled fiber-based electrodes
abstract: Na-ion intercalated MnO 2 -based bundled fiber electrodes are designed for aqueous Na-ion energy storage with high energy density and power densities.

title: Structural phase transitions of bulk tin-based halide perovskites under pressure and excited-state properties of tin-based halide perovskites quantum dots under ultrafast laser pulses: a novel phase ppPv- Pnma (VI)
abstract: The structural phase transitions of tin-based halide perovskites CsSnX 3 (X = Cl, Br, I) under pressures of 0–120 GPa have been investigated by employing first-principles calculations combined with the CALYPSO method.

title: Regulating coordination environment of Co single-atom through Ni/Co sulphides synergistic heterointerfaces towards efficient bifunctional oxygen electrocatalysts
abstract: Achieving synergistic catalysis between different components and maintaining a high catalytic activity is an effective way to enhance the efficiencies of the oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) and oxygen evolution reaction (OER).

title: Biomass-based separators for aqueous zinc-ion batteries: advantages, strategies, and perspectives
abstract: A comprehensive review of the design, preparation, and performance of biomass-based separators in ZIBs, highlighting their roles in enhancing safety, ion transport, and dendrite suppression, along with current challenges and future prospects.

title: Metal/metal oxide–graphene nanocomposites as cathode catalysts for lithium–oxygen batteries
abstract: This review presents the development history of metal/metal oxide–graphene nanocomposites for lithium–oxygen batteries and discusses their electrocatalytic mechanisms, structure-performance relationships, technical hurdles and future opportunities.

title: Performance-enhanced catalysts derived from spent ternary lithium-ion batteries for simultaneous removal of NO x and VOCs
abstract: Herein, a performance-enhanced MnCoNiO x catalyst was regenerated from spent lithium ion batteries for simultaneous removal of NO x and benzene, and the effect of lithium ion removal on catalytic materials, especially oxygen vacancies was revealed.

title: Separation of hydrogen isotopes using a protonic ceramic fuel cell
abstract: Hydrogen isotopes can be efficiently separated using a protonic ceramic fuel cell.

title: Architecting light for catalysis: emerging frontiers in plasmonic–photonic crystal hybrids for solar energy conversion
abstract: This review explores how plasmonic–photonic crystal hybrids enable enhanced solar energy conversion by coupling photonic and plasmonic effects. Key advances in structural design, fabrication, and photocatalytic applications are critically discussed.

title: A fluorinated carbon nanodot-tube/MXene/microfiber electronic textile with high water-interference-resistance for stable amphibious human motion monitoring
abstract: A fluorinated carbon nanodot-tube/MXene/microfiber electronic textile (FCNET), inspired by snails and frogs, features a unique “point–line–plane” structure, exhibiting superhydrophobicity and stability in underwater environments.

title: In situ controllably self-assembled amorphous Co–TDPAT MOFs as superior cocatalysts of α-Fe 2 O 3 nanosheet arrays for highly efficient and ultrastable photoelectrochemical oxygen evolution
abstract: An α-Fe 2 O 3 NSA in situ self-assembled with an ultrathin, conformal amorphous Co–TDPAT MOF shows record-high activity and stability toward PEC oxygen evolution.

title: Engineering MoS 2 -based electrocatalysts for water splitting: a comprehensive review of doping, heterostructures, and support integration strategies
abstract: The article offers a thorough analysis of MoS 2 , including its crystallographic phases (1T, 2H, and 3R), basic structural, optical, electrical, mechanical, and thermal characteristics, as well as synthesis and characterisation methods.

title: Fabrication of graphene oxide/silk protein core-sheath aerogel fibers for thermal management
abstract: An encapsulated graphene oxide/silk protein aerogel fiber made by coaxial spinning is reported to exhibit ultra-high mechanical properties. The aerogel fiber showed efficient performance in thermal management applications.

title: In situ ionogel electrolyte with anion-anchoring sites and covalent bonded phosphorus enables high-performance sodium metal batteries
abstract: The designed ionogel electrolyte balances safety and electrochemical performance for sodium metal batteries by offering nonflammability via a dual-phase flame-retardant mechanism and a tripled Na + transference number due to anion-anchoring sites.

title: Correction: Accelerating the electrochemical performance of solid oxide fuel cells using a Ce(Gd, Bi, Yb)O 2− δ diffusion barrier layer acting as an oxygen reservoir at high-current loading conditions
abstract: Correction for ‘Accelerating the electrochemical performance of solid oxide fuel cells using a Ce(Gd, Bi, Yb)O 2− δ diffusion barrier layer acting as an oxygen reservoir at high-current loading conditions’ by Hye Young Kim et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/d4ta06374k.

title: Boosting the efficiency of lead-free MASnI 3 perovskite solar cells through a bilayer CIGS structure approximating a gradient bandgap distribution
abstract: A bilayer CIGS absorber approximating a gradient distribution enhances charge transport and suppresses recombination, achieving an efficiency of 33.08% in perovskite/CIGS tandem solar cells.

title: Electron-beam-evaporated NiO X for efficient and stable semi-transparent perovskite solar cells and modules
abstract: Herein, thermally stable ST-PSCs have been fabricated by using vacuum deposited CsPbBr 3 perovskite and electron-beam evaporation deposited NiO X . Furthermore, we achieved a champion efficiency of 5.5% for 5 cm × 5 cm mini-modules with an AVT of 49.1%.

title: Single-crystalline bismuth sulfide microrods for efficient visible light photodetection
abstract: Single-crystalline bismuth sulfide microrods have been successfully fabricated by a facile hydrothermal method, possessing good visible photodetection capability.

title: Comprehensive overview of machine learning applications in MOFs: from modeling processes to latest applications and design classifications
abstract: This review provides an overview of machine learning (ML) workflows in MOFs. It discusses three rational design methods, focusing on future challenges and opportunities to enhance understanding and guide ML-based MOF research.

title: Synergistic passivation and stable carrier transport enable efficient blade-coated perovskite solar cells fabricated in ambient air
abstract: Ambient-air blade-coated perovskite solar cells achieve stable efficiency via synergistic passivation and carrier transport control.

title: Quasi-2D Ruddlesden–Popper perovskites with tunable wide bandgaps and phosphonium as additives for interface defect passivation in tandem solar cell design
abstract: We design a series of quasi-two-dimensional (Q2D) Ruddlesden–Popper perovskites incorporating phosphonium-based 2D additives as a wide-bandgap absorber in perovskite tandem solar cell devices.

title: Efficient colloidal dispersion of poly(nickel-ethenetetrathiolate) in aqueous–organic solvents for solution-processed organic thermoelectrics
abstract: Herein, we demonstrate that water-assisted dispersion enables reduced poly[NiETT] films with enhanced thermoelectric performance and broadband photothermoelectric sensing capability.

title: Z-Scheme heterojunction formation between CsPbBr 3 and BiOBr: key to singlet oxygen formation for selective semidehydrogenation of tetrahydroisoquinoline
abstract: Z-scheme heterojunction between CsPbBr 3 quantum dots and BiOBr nanosheets optimized the conduction band and valence band redox potentials, regulating the 2e − oxidation of tetrahydroisoquinoline to 3,4-dihydroisoquinoline through O 2 activation.

title: Taming polysulfide shuttle and lithium dendrites in lithium–sulfur batteries via all-/quasi-solid-state electrolytes: recent progress and future prospects
abstract: Two critical challenges impede the advancement of lithium–sulfur batteries: lithium dendrite growth and polysulfide shuttle. This review systematically examines diverse solutions leveraging various all-solid-state and quasi-solid-state electrolytes.

title: Probing solid-state battery aging: evaluating calendar vs. cycle aging protocols via time-resolved electrochemical impedance spectroscopy
abstract: The degradation behavior of In/InLi|Li 6 PS 5 Cl|NCM83:Li 6 PS 5 Cl battery cell was investigated using two different accelerated aging protocols to evaluate their ability to fast screen solid-state batteries.

title: BODIPY and N -halamine-decorated covalent organic framework with synergistic antibacterial capabilities for sunlight-powered water sterilization
abstract: A BODIPY and N -halamine-decorated covalent organic framework is constructed for natural sunlight-powered, scalable and efficient disinfection of E. coli -contaminated water.

title: Nanogenerators as a fundamental of a sustainable and intelligent energy future
abstract: Prof. Zhong Lin Wang, Prof. Pooi See Lee and Prof. Ya Yang introduce the Journal of Materials Chemistry A and Nanoscale cross-journal themed collection on nanogenerators.

title: AI-designed hierarchical SAPO-34 catalyst breakthrough with experimental verification
abstract: Integrating AI towards accurate design of SAPO-34 properties enables improving insights into effectively tuning such catalyst.

title: A robust and stable phenolic resin-crosslinked graphene aerogel for efficient solar-driven steam-power co-generation
abstract: A reduced graphene oxide/phenolic resin (rGO/PR) aerogel, featuring efficient solar absorption and water transport properties, along with excellent elasticity and corrosion resistance, is demonstrated for solar-driven steam-power co-generation.

title: Highly stable PdO nanostructures self-supported on conductive polyaniline nanotubes enable extensive electrochemical hydrogen evolution
abstract: This work emphasizes the design of a distinctly stable polymer–metal nanostructure, aiming to accelerate hydrogen production by tuning the active electronic environment of the conducting polymer.

title: Theoretical calculation assisted design of dual additives enables robust electrode/electrolyte interphases for high-voltage sodium ion full cells
abstract: Theoretical and experimental results demonstrate that the dual-additive electrolyte stabilizes the electrode-electrolyte interface (EEI). FEC suppresses solvent oxidation, while TAIC preferentially decomposes, construct a inorganic-rich EEI.

title: Bimetallic Ag–M (M = Co, Rh, Ir) alloy microflowers as high-performance plasmonic catalysts for visible-light-driven imine formation: a comparative study via in situ SERS
abstract: Plasmonic catalysis of the C–N coupling reaction was performed by alloying transition metals with Ag and monitored using liquid-state in situ SERS, where the relative reaction rate followed the order: Ag–Ir > Ag–Rh > Ag–Co.

title: Realizing high energy storage performance in (Bi 0.5 Na 0.5 ) 0.7 Sr 0.3 TiO 3 ceramics via phase structure adjustment
abstract: In this paper, the 0.95BNST–0.05LMN ceramic exhibit decent ESP, with a recoverable W rec of 7.26 J cm −3 , and an energy storage efficiency ( η ) of 85.54%, as well as excellent temperature and frequency stability.

title: Advances in solvent-free syntheses of covalent organic frameworks towards catalysis and battery applications
abstract: This review provides an introduction to the recent progress of solvent-free syntheses of COFs, summarizes their preparation methods, unique advantages and applications in catalysis and batteries and discusses the opportunities and challenges.

title: Achieving stable and reliable assembly of flow battery stacks through equivalent mechanical models
abstract: The transition to a low-carbon society demands energy conversion and storage devices with high efficiency.

title: Enhancing catalytic oxygen reduction via sulfonate and phosphate crosslinkers in redox polyelectrolyte-based O 2 –glucose membraneless enzymatic biofuel cells
abstract: The development of enzymatic biofuel cells (EBFCs) that operate under mild conditions and do not require membranes as separators is crucial for advancing low-power device technologies.

title: Nanoscale quantification and structure–dynamics correlation of photoredox reactions on 2D nanoplates via single-molecule localization microscopy
abstract: Single-molecule localization microscopy was used to quantify photoredox kinetics on BiOBr nanoplates. This study reveals spatial correlations of photoredox reactions and structure–dynamics relationships at the single-molecule level.

title: Deformation mechanism and high thermoelectric performance of sodium doped Ag 2 S 1/3 Se 1/3 Te 1/3 plastic semiconductors
abstract: Inorganic plastic thermoelectric materials have attracted much attention for breaking the brittle bottleneck of traditional inorganic thermoelectric materials.

title: Rational design of Janus MXene monolayers as promising frameworks for high-performance sodium metal anodes
abstract: Through the investigation of the deposition and diffusion behavior of Na on the surfaces of six Janus MXene monolayers, HfTiCS 2 and VTaCO 2 with superior performance as sodium metal anode frameworks were screened out.

title: Interface and defect engineering strategies toward high-performance blue perovskite light-emitting diodes
abstract: DMPO passivation and PAM interlayer enable bright, efficient, and stable quasi-2D blue PeLEDs (486 nm) with a 12.94% EQE.

title: Surface pH modulation of amorphous manganese oxide for enhanced supercapacitor performance
abstract: In situ ATR-SEIRAS shows that a low surface pH enhances protonation during charge–discharge, boosting the pseudocapacitance and cycling stability of MnO 2 . This provides a new interface design strategy for supercapacitors.

title: Mechanistic study on moisture exposure of Ti-based layered oxides for sodium storage applications
abstract: The air stability and moisture sensitivity of titanium-based layered negative electrode materials for sodium storage play a critical role in their practical applications for advanced energy storage systems.

title: Single-layer group III-IV-VI semiconductors: potential photocatalysts for water splitting with high carrier mobilities
abstract: Strong optical absorption, high carrier mobility, and potential photocatalytic water splitting properties of MSiX (M = Ga, In; X = S, Se, Te) monolayers.

title: Solution ion luminescence induced by the triboelectric-discharge effect for rapid and intuitive detection of sweat ions
abstract: This work proposes a triboelectric-discharge effect-enabled visualized sweat ion detection solution with the merits of a simple design, user-friendliness, real-time monitoring, reusability, self-powering, with no need for additional materials.

title: Salt particle suspension electrolyte with trace-water for improving ionic concentrations at interfaces in zinc-based dual-ion batteries
abstract: Suspended salt particles create a high anion concentration layer at the electrode surface, reducing polarization and boosting anion utilization, thereby enhancing the electrochemical performance of dual-ion batteries.

title: Correction: Aromatic polyaroxydiazole pseudocapacitive anode materials with tunable electrochemical performance through side group engineering
abstract: Correction for ‘Aromatic polyaroxydiazole pseudocapacitive anode materials with tunable electrochemical performance through side group engineering’ by Yan Jiang et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2024, 12 , 364–374, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TA06374G.

title: Introduction of a multifunctional percolated framework into Na metal for highly stable sodium metal batteries
abstract: We developed a stable, processable Na metal anode by embedding a dual-functional NPC/Na 2 Se skeleton via simple heating and rolling process. The Na@NPC/Na 2 Se electrode ensures uniform Na deposition and suppresses dendrites during stripping/plating.

title: A solvothermal approach to nano-designing M-N-H systems: unveiling new pathways to dimensional control in the lithium nitride hydride ammonia synthesis catalyst
abstract: A new solvothermal synthesis route to Li–N–H materials enhances particle size and morphological control, with evidence for enhanced functional properties.

title: Exploiting the synergistic effect of Ag and Cu doping in eco-friendly quantum dots for boosted photoelectrochemical performance
abstract: Simultaneous Ag and Cu doping in ZIS/ZnSe QDs enables suppressed charge recombination by facilitating the doping states-related hole transfer kinetics, enhancing the charge extraction efficiency of QDs for boosted PEC H 2 evolution performance.

title: Carbon-based materials for more reliable solid-state Li batteries
abstract: The multi-functional features of carbon-based materials have shown significant potential in addressing the challenges associated with advanced solid-state lithium batteries.

title: Enhanced rate and cycle performance of all-solid-state batteries with an ionic and electronic conductive composite strategy
abstract: An ionic and electronic conductive composite strategy mitigates CNF agglomeration, reduces pore formation, and enhances ion conduction, enabling high-density cathodes with improved rate and cycle performance of all-solid-state batteries.

title: The construction of a c-SnO 2 /a-ZnSnO 3 heterostructure with abundant interfaces through topological transformation for efficient electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: Topological transformation was used to construct a c-SnO 2 /a-ZnSnO 3 heterostructure with abundant and robust interfaces, which has been validated as an efficacious catalyst for the electrocatalytic CO 2 RR.

title: Degradation analysis during fast lifetime cycling of sulfide-based all-solid-state Li-metal batteries using in situ electrochemical impedance spectroscopy
abstract: Four degradation regimes over 1000-lifetime cycling of all-solid-state Li-metal batteries were identified by performing comprehensive in situ and ex situ analyses.

title: The activity key of s-block single-atom catalysts for superior CO 2 RR: sp 2 hybridization of metal sites
abstract: Coordination regulation transforms the inert sp-hybridized states of s-block SACs to active sp 2 hybridized states, which ensures optimal intermediate adsorption and ultra-low overpotential for selective CO 2 reduction to HCOOH.

title: In situ Raman study on the impact of configurational entropy on catalytic activity for industrial water oxidation
abstract: High configurational entropy promotes the formation of high-valence metal sites, leading to the enhancement of the oxygen evolution reaction activity of catalysts.

title: DMC matters: the role of dimethyl carbonate in SEI formation on oxygen functionalized anodes
abstract: Mechanistic insights into DMC decomposition on functionalized graphite reveal surface effects, HAT-driven pathways, contributions to SEI formation and evolution, and competition with EC decomposition reactions.

title: Regenerable and stable conductive topo-polymers for selective uranium extraction
abstract: Due to the growing demand for the sustainable development of the nuclear energy industry, the capture of uranyl (UO 2 2+ ) ions from seawater has garnered unprecedented interest.

title: Enhancement of thermoelectric properties of Ag 2 Te semiconductors through In-doping induced resonant levels and multi-valley degeneracy
abstract: Indium doping increased the carrier concentration and introduced resonant levels and multi-valley degeneracy in the energy band, improving the Seebeck coefficient and significantly enhancing the power factor of Ag 2− x In x Te samples.

title: A peptide-based conductive hydrogel capable of in situ bioelectrocatalytic NADH regeneration for sustained production of 1-propanol
abstract: Herein, the authors developed a peptide-based conductive hydrogel that consistently generates NADH, which served as a bridge linking electrochemical and biocatalytic modules to foster 1-propanol production by alcohol dehydrogenase catalysis.

title: Facet-dependent adsorption and its effect on photocatalytic reactions: insights from diuron degradation on zinc oxide surfaces
abstract: This study quantifies facet-dependent interactions and pH-controlled degradation pathways of diuron on ZnO using AFM-based force spectroscopy, revealing how adsorption strength and surface chemistry dictate photocatalytic efficiency and toxicity.

title: Synergistic V–Ti–F co-doping strategy for high-performance and long-cycling LiMn 0.6 Fe 0.4 PO 4 cathodes
abstract: A V 3+ /Ti 4+ /F - co-doping strategy improves Li + diffusion and stability in LiMn 0.6 Fe 0.4 PO 4 , thus achieving high capacities of 153.5 mAh g −1 (1C) and 144.7 mAh g −1 (5C) for high-performance LMFP cathodes.

title: Advances in computational design of van der Waals heterostructures for photocatalytic water splitting
abstract: Light-driven photocatalytic water splitting is a promising approach to renewable hydrogen production, driven by the increasing global energy demand.

title: A phosphite-incorporated crystalline–amorphous cobalt-based electrocatalyst via surface-confined chemical reduction for efficient hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: An electroless chemical reduction produced a self-supported phosphite-doped cobalt boride electrocatalyst with superior hydrogen evolution activity. Its unique structure and tuned cobalt active sites significantly boost water splitting efficiency.

title: Solar driven cogeneration of freshwater and electricity based on an Al 2 O 3 nanoparticle decorated graphene aerogel
abstract: The combination of solar water evaporation and the hydrovoltaic effect (HVE) provides an effective way to solve the crisis of water shortage and fossil fuel pollution.

title: Enhanced electrochemical performance of NiSbS/NiSb/NiS nanocomposites anchored on graphite nanosheets for sodium-ion battery applications
abstract: A novel Ni-Sb-S/GN multiphase metal sulfide with long cycling life for sodium-ion battery anodes was designed and synthesized based on guidance from DFT calculations.

title: Sustainable ammonia production from nitrate reduction assisted by methanol oxidation using Co@CF bifunctional electrocatalysts
abstract: Nitrate pollution threatens water ecosystems. A Cu-based Co@CF catalyst, synthesized via in situ electrodeposition, achieves 95.16% faradaic efficiency and 7.51 mg h −1 cm −2 NH 3 yield by coupling NO 3 RR with MOR, also producing formic acid with 91.74% efficiency.

title: Tuning photoelectric conversion in hybrid perovskites by thermal cycling
abstract: The effects of thermal cycling between 25 °C and 60 °C on the photovoltaic properties of MAPbI 3 are studied. A moderate compressive lattice strain generated during thermal cycling enhances the photoelectric conversion performance.

title: Atomically precise thiolate protected hexanuclear silver nanoclusters [Ag 6 (PMBI) 6 ] as an efficient catalyst for the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Atomically precise silver nanoclusters exhibit efficient electrocatalysis for the hydrogen evolution reaction.

title: A lubricated and antibacterial hydrogel coating based on polyelectrolyte adhesion for medical catheters
abstract: A flexible hydrogel coating with good lubrication and antimicrobial properties was prepared on the surface of silicone tubes by the adhesion of polyelectrolytes.

title: Unlocking C 3–4 products in CO electroreduction via one-step square-ring coupling on Cu 4 -embedded carbon nitride
abstract: Mechanistic overview of electrocatalytic CO reduction to C 1 –C 4 products over Cu 4 –C 5 N 2 H 2 .

title: Synergistic yttrium doping accelerates surface reconstruction and optimizes d-band centers in NiFe-LDH for superior oxygen evolution catalysis
abstract: Doping modification represents an effective strategy to modulate the electronic structure and enhance the electrocatalytic OER activity of NiFe-LDH.

title: Efficient S-band electromagnetic wave absorption in hierarchically hollow CoFe 2 O 4 /C nanocomposites modified with ZIF-67 derivatives
abstract: This study presents a multi-level hollow structure electromagnetic wave-absorbing material enabling efficient S-band absorption.

title: The synergistic effect of MOF and biomass achieving dual breakthroughs in material structure and performance
abstract: In order to cope with the environmental crisis and the depletion of non-renewable resources, the combination of metal–organic framework (MOF) and biomass materials has become an important breakthrough in sustainable materials science.

title: High-voltage symmetric supercapacitors developed by engineering DyFeO 3 electrodes and aqueous electrolytes
abstract: An aqueous symmetric supercapacitor featuring porous DyFeO 3 electrodes and a Na 2 SO 4 (water-acetonitrile) electrolyte, achieving a 3.1 V potential window, 84.43 W h kg −1 energy density, and 90% capacitance retention after 10 000 cycles.

title: On-demand design of materials with enhanced dielectric properties via a machine learning-assisted materials genome approach
abstract: An on-demand design of silicon-containing arylacetylene resins with specific thermal and dielectric performance was achieved via the machine learning-assisted materials genome approach.

title: Morphology and electron regulation of B-WS 2 @MXene nanosheet array heterojunctions to upgrade the capacity and longevity of lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Polypropylene separator modifiers based on the nanosheet array structure of MXene and boron-doped WS 2 nanosheets endow lithium–sulfur batteries with high electrochemical performance.

title: Enhanced electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction on a three-dimensional Cu 3 P/SnP@CF catalyst through a multi-site synergistic effect between the heterointerface and phosphorus vacancies
abstract: An interesting multi-site tandem synergistic effect was observed, in which phosphorus vacancies increase the N 2 adsorption rate, and then, the Cu 3 P/SnP@CF heterojunction lowers the energy barrier of rate-limiting hydrogenation reactions of the NRR.

title: Enabling Cr-induced efficient hydrogen evolution on high-entropy alloys via rapid thermal shock
abstract: In high-entropy alloys prepared by rapid thermal shock process, electron transfer from Cr to Pt results in upshift of Pt d-band center, followed by optimized bond strength between hydrogen species and active sites inferring high HER activity.

title: Multi-scale enhanced energy storage performance in Sm(Mg 0.5 Sn 0.5 )O 3 -modified Bi 0.47 Na 0.47 Ba 0.06 TiO 3 ceramics with a composite structure
abstract: Multi-scale enhanced energy storage performance in Sm(Mg 0.5 Sn 0.5 )O 3 -modified Bi 0.47 Na 0.47 Ba 0.06 TiO 3 ceramics with a composite structure.

title: ZIF-derived CoS@CN with a hollow cage structure for improved electrochemical nitrate reduction to synthesize ammonia
abstract: The influence of S on the morphology and the eNO 3 RR performance of the CoS@CN catalyst has been explored through experiments and DFT calculations.

title: Recent progress on ZnIn 2 S 4 -based composite photocatalysts for photocatalytic hydrogen production coupling organic synthesis
abstract: In this review, we systematically summarize the structure, modification method and photocatalytic hydrogen production coupling organic reaction applications of ZnIn 2 S 4 -based composites.

title: Hydroxyl-oxygen vacancy synergy over In 2 O 3 –ZrO 2 catalysts: mechanistic insights into CO 2 hydrogenation to methanol
abstract: By regulating the surface structure of In 2 O 3 –ZrO 2 catalysts, the study reveals that the FLP sites constructed from hydroxyl and oxygen vacancies are favourable for CO 2 activation.

title: Advanced interfacial charge carrier transport enabling the improvement of open-circuit voltage in Sb 2 Se 3 solar cells
abstract: The incorporation of MoSe 2 forms a potential barrier at the back-contact interface, enhancing interfacial charge transport in Sb 2 Se 3 solar cells. Increased current flow and reduced carrier recombination enable the improvement of open-circuit voltage.

title: Adsorption-induced dual-layer solid electrolyte interface toward a highly reversible Zn anode
abstract: The in situ formed solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) via an adsorption-induced mechanism suppresses dendrite growth and side reactions, enabling high-performance aqueous zinc-ion batteries.

title: Bias-free solar to ammonia photoelectrochemical conversion using a perovskite-silicon tandem absorber and 1T-MoS 2 integration
abstract: An artificial photosynthesis device for N 2 to NH 3 conversion realized using a Si-perovskite tandem absorber with a 1T-MoS 2 catalyst.

title: Electrochemical H/D isotope separation
abstract: Three main systems for electrochemical H/D separation, including water electrolysis, fuel cell, and electrochemical hydrogen pump systems.

title: Synergistic dual-defect band engineering for highly efficient photocatalytic degradation of microplastics via Nb-induced oxygen vacancies in SnO 2 quantum dots
abstract: Synergistic dual-defect engineering in Nb-doped SnO 2 QDs boosts visible-light photocatalysis by creating oxygen vacancies, narrowing bandgaps, and extending carrier lifetimes, enabling efficient degradation of PE microplastics in real-world water.

title: Facile 2D In 2 Se 3 protection for enhanced BiVO 4 stability in highly alkaline photoelectrochemical water splitting
abstract: Photoelectrochemical (PEC) water splitting holds promise as a major source of sustainable energy production in the future.

title: Accelerating cathode design for zinc-ion batteries using data-driven screening and ab initio calculations
abstract: The increasing demand for sustainable energy storage has driven significant interest in zinc-ion batteries (ZIBs) as a cost-effective and environmentally friendly alternative to lithium-ion batteries (LIBs).

title: Lithophilic conductive oxide-introduced dual-substrate deposition for high current density lithium metal batteries
abstract: YBCO as a separator modifier enables uniform dual-substrate deposition of lithium on both YBCO and Cu, due to its high electrical conductivity and surface energy and low Li + adsorption energy.

title: Scalable template-free synthesis of B, N co-doped carbon bowls anchoring ultrafine Li 3 VO 4 nanocrystals for high power density and durable lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Bowl-like B, N co-doped carbon anchoring ultrafine Li 3 VO 4 nanocrystals were fabricated via in situ molecular cross-linking assisted spray drying route, improving the electrochemical lithium-ion storage kinetics and the power density of Li 3 VO 4 .

title: In situ anion exchange with redox mediators influencing the photovoltaic parameters in vacancy-ordered halide perovskite-based photoelectrochemical solar cells
abstract: The photoelectrochemical solar cells with vacancy-ordered perovskite undergo anion exchange with components in the electrolyte. This study unravels the role of iodide in the redox mediator on the phase stability of the Cs 2 Pt(Cl/Br) 6 absorber.

title: Direct recycling of degraded Ni-rich cathodes: recent advances in regeneration and upcycling
abstract: This review summarizes advances in direct recycling of spent Ni-rich layered oxides. It links the complex degradation to direct regeneration and upcycling strategies for efficient, high-value material recovery.

title: Simulation of electrical and thermal characteristics of a short circuit inside a boehmite composite separator
abstract: Lithium dendrite morphology and composite separator coating thickness influence internal electrical-thermal behaviour during short circuits in lithium-ion batteries. Studying this influence is important for understanding how to make batteries safer.

title: Energy harvesting from water's liquid–gas phase transition: mechanisms and structural designs
abstract: Water phase transition-based energy harvesting has emerged as a promising branch of hydrovoltaic technology, offering substantial potential for sustainable energy generation.

title: Mechanism insights for efficient photocatalytic seawater splitting in poly(heptazine imide): inhibited ion exudation and improved charge separation
abstract: In this work, we elucidate how seawater ions modulate photocatalytic H 2 evolution in crystalline carbon nitride. We reveal that seawater ions suppress K + leaching and incorporate into the KPHI framework, enhancing photogenerated charge separation and transfer efficiency.

title: Demonstration of Co/Ni exchange in exfoliated LiCoO 2 nanosheets: a new approach for upcycling spent Li-ion battery cathodes
abstract: A new process for upcycling LiCoO 2 via nanosheet exfoliation facilitates cobalt extraction and nickel exchange, providing an alternative pathway to Ni-doped battery materials.

title: Contact electrification of organic liquid–metal interfaces induced by interfacial energy level shift
abstract: Vacuum energy level shift and electron transfer in contact electrification at organic liquid–metal interfaces were investigated.

title: High energy storage density and efficiency of 0.5Ba(Zr 0.2 Ti 0.8 )O 3 –0.5(Ba 0.7 Ca 0.3 )TiO 3 thin films on platinized sapphire substrates
abstract: Manganese-doped 0.5Ba(Zr 0.2 Ti 0.8 )O 3 –0.5(Ba 0.7 Ca 0.3 )TiO 3 (BZT–BCT) ferroelectric thin films deposited on platinized sapphire substrates by chemical solution deposition and multistep-annealed at 850 °C, are investigated.

title: Correction: Yolk–shell structured microspheres consisting of CoO/CoP hetero-interfaced nanocomposites as highly active hydrogen evolution reaction electrocatalysts for AEM electrolyzer stacks
abstract: Correction for ‘Yolk–shell structured microspheres consisting of CoO/CoP hetero-interfaced nanocomposites as highly active hydrogen evolution reaction electrocatalysts for AEM electrolyzer stacks’ by In Tae Kim et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA07211A.

title: Efficient descriptors for the design of high-performance Ni-based catalysts modified with electronic inducers for the hydrogenation of 1,4-butynediol
abstract: EII modulated the Δ d of Ni sites, optimized the intermediates' adsorption conformation and reduced the energy barriers for hydrogenation.

title: Highly conductive LiH 2 PO 4 -based solid electrolyte at intermediate temperatures through a polymerization-hydrolysis treatment
abstract: A scalable, self-standing LiH 2 PO 4 -based electrolyte membrane was developed with high ionic conductivity at 100–200 °C. Using a novel polymerization-hydrolysis method, the membrane forms conductive interfacial phases for proton conduction.

title: Overcoming sluggish kinetics in bismuth electrodes via electronic networking enabling a 4000-cycle liquid metal battery
abstract: Cu doping in the Bi positive electrode results in an electronically networked structure that facilitates electrode kinetics. The resultant Li‖Bi 96 Cu 4 cell achieves an unprecedented cycle capability without capacity degradation over 4000 cycles.

title: Symmetry-informed graph neural networks for carbon dioxide isotherm and adsorption prediction in aluminum-substituted zeolites
abstract: SymGNN leverages crystal symmetries to improve adsorption predictions in zeolites. By encoding symmetry-aware features, the model achieves accurate isotherm and heat of adsorption predictions, even for unseen topologies.

title: Solid solution engineering of [(C 3 H 7 ) 4 N] x [(C 4 H 9 ) 4 N] 1− x [FeBrCl 3 ] plastic crystal phase-change materials
abstract: Plastic crystals are supramolecular materials with promising properties for solid state phase-change material technologies and solid solution engineering.

title: Ordered multi-modal sulfated MOF-808 and its improved catalytic performance in glycerol esterification
abstract: Surfactant-assisted porosity engineering and sulfation of MOF-808 enable efficient triacetin formation by combining hierarchical pores with tailored Brønsted acidity.

title: Simultaneous enhancement of the energy storage, transparency, and hardness properties of K 0.5 Na 0.5 NbO 3 -based ceramics via a synergistic optimization strategy
abstract: This study achieves a synergistic optimization of energy storage performance, optical transparency, and mechanical hardness under a moderate-strength electric field by designing a (1 − x )(K 0.5 Na 0.5 )NbO 3 – x (Bi(Zn 0.5 Nb 0.4 )O 3 ) (KNN–BZN) ceramic.

title: Understanding the Mn dissolution mechanism in rock salt-type Li 4 Mn 2 O 5 cathodes
abstract: For the first time, a detailed exploration of Mn dissolution in disordered rock salt (DRX) Li 4 Mn 2 O 5 is presented.

title: Tailoring active sites in trimetallic conductive metal–organic frameworks for highly efficient water splitting
abstract: The trimetallic conductive metal–organic framework grown on carbon cloth (NiFeCo–HHTP/CC) exhibits remarkable OER and HER performances. This is attributed to the introduction of Co which greatly optimizes the electronic structure of Ni and Fe sites.

title: Tunable Z-scheme photocatalytic activity in Sc 2 CBrX/MoTe 2 (X = Br, Cl) heterostructures: a combined electronic and non-adiabatic dynamics study
abstract: Z-Scheme Sc 2 CBr 2 /MoTe 2 and Sc 2 CClBr/MoTe 2 heterostructures engineered via sliding and inversion exhibit tunable strain response, efficient charge separation, and promising STH efficiencies for photocatalytic water splitting.

title: Hollow polyhedral Fe single-atom catalysts with S/N dual coordination boosting oxygen and iodide redox for high-efficiency zinc–air/iodide hybrid batteries
abstract: A hollow polyhedral Fe SAC with a dual S/N coordinated Fe–N 3 S 1 structure is designed to simultaneously catalyze oxygen and iodide redox reactions in zinc–air–iodide hybrid batteries to boost energy efficiency and cycling stability.

title: Optimization of thin-film Li 1.0 Ni 0.6 Co 0.2 Mn 0.2 O 2 cathodes enabled by rapid thermal processing in oxygen-rich environments for superior lithium-ion battery performance
abstract: Ni-rich Li 1.0 Ni 0.6 Co 0.2 Mn 0.2 O 2 (NCM622) thin-film cathodes via O 2 rich rapid thermal annealing at 600 °C, show enhanced crystallinity, suppresses Pt hillocks, and 646.8 mAh cm −3 capacity with 81% retention, ideal for miniaturized devices.

title: Interfacial configuration impact on Rashba spin splitting, excitonic radiative lifetime and band edge alignment in Janus monolayer based heterostructures
abstract: In this work, we have explored two emerging Janus monolayers BiTeBr and SbTeBr, while constructing their corresponding heterostructures by varying the interfacial terminations.

title: A fluoride-incorporated composite electrolyte enabling high-voltage all-solid-state sulfide-based lithium batteries
abstract: LAF–LPSC biphasic composite electrolyte is designed and demonstrated to be a suitable component for high-capacity and high-current-density all-solid-state lithium-ion batteries at high voltage.

title: Chemically bonded interface modulated S-scheme charge transfer in Sb 2 S 3 @ZnIn 2 S 4 core–shell heterostructures for boosted catalytic activity toward nitrogen photofixation
abstract: S-scheme Sb 2 S 3 @ZnIn 2 S 4 core–shell heterostructures exhibited boosted catalytic activity and high stability toward nitrogen photofixation.

title: Hollow spherical Cu/CuO–Fe 3 O 4 composites for high-efficiency photothermal co-catalysis of hydrogen evolution
abstract: The synthesized Cu/CuO–Fe 3 O 4 material couples LSPR and photothermal effects to achieve efficient photothermal co-catalysis of H 2 evolution from formaldehyde solution under oxygen-mediation.

title: Sodium metal anodes with multiphasic interphases for room temperature sodium–sulfur pouch cells
abstract: The multiphasic SEI design, consisting of NaF, NaI, and NaNH 2 , combines high rigidity, ductility, and low ionic diffusion barriers, enhancing stability and performance in RT-Na/S batteries.

title: Highly stable organic solar cells with robust interface using fullerenol as molecular linker
abstract: Fullerenol (C 60 (OH) x ) serves as an interfacial molecular linker, forming hydrogen bonds with ZnO (O–H⋯O) and Y6 (O–H⋯F), establishing a robust interface that substantially boosts the stability of organic solar cells.

title: Effect of nitrogen content on performance of supercapacitors composed of nitrogen–carbon materials
abstract: Nitrogen–carbon (N–C) materials are emerging as strong contenders to elemental carbon in advanced energy applications. The effect of N to C ratio on the supercapacitor electrode performance of the N–C materials is evaluated in this study.

title: Recycling spent zinc ion primary batteries for use in superior rechargeable lithium-ion energy storage
abstract: We synthesized ZMO@C from spent batteries for Li-ion storage. Graphitic carbon boosts conductivity, enabling sustainable energy and catalytic use while protecting soil fertility.

title: Vanadium-doped Li 2 TiSiO 5 anodes for boosting capacity and cycling stability of lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Li 2 Ti 1− x V x SiO 5 ( x = 0. 0.25, 0.5, 0.75) anode materials with vanadium doping achieved a lithium storage capacity of 235 mA h g −1 , higher than 62.1 mA h g −1 for Li 2 TiSiO 5 . DFT confirmed enhanced properties.

title: Exsolved medium-entropy alloy FeCoCuNi in titanate fibers enables solid oxide cells with superb electrochemical performance
abstract: The hemp rope-liked LSTFCCN nanofibers with exsolved medium-entropy-alloy (MEA) nanoparticles are fabricated, delivering remarkably enhanced electrochemical activity both in fuel cell (FC) mode and electrolysis cell (EC) mode.

title: A fluorine-free Li-ion battery features comparable cycling performance to a highly-fluorinated equivalent
abstract: Lithium-ion cells with comparable capacity retention despite differences in surface chemistry between highly fluorinated and fully fluorine-free electrolytes and binders.

title: An interpretable machine learning model for Mn-based cathode development: mapping synthesis parameters to cycling stability
abstract: An efficient and transparent machine learning model is proposed and verified for predicting manganese-based cathode materials with high cycling stability.

title: A mechanically robust, highly sensitive, resilient, and degradable dual physically cross-linked hydrogel for heart rate health detection
abstract: Hydrogel sensors with excellent stability have received wide attention in various fields.

title: Factors in the utilization of corrosive ruthenium in the oxygen evolution electrode of polymer electrolyte membrane water electrolysis
abstract: The CS operation enables Ru to function as an interlayer between the Ti PTL and IrO x for hundreds of hours.

title: Fabrication of CoSe 2 /FeSe 2 heterostructures with a stable solid-electrode interphase film and low surface activation energy for Na-ion batteries
abstract: The abundant availability and potential cost benefits of sodium-ion batteries (SIBs) have generated increasing interest as viable alternatives to lithium-ion batteries (LIBs).

title: An in situ polymerizable deep eutectic solvent electrolyte based on TFEA- co -MBA for high-safety and high-voltage lithium metal batteries
abstract: Solid-state electrolytes (SSEs) show considerable potential for improving the safety of lithium metal batteries by replacing flammable organic liquid electrolytes.

title: Single-molecule quantification of photoredox activities and dynamics at the nanoscale on multi-faceted 2D materials
abstract: In situ single-molecule fluorescence microscopy is used to quantify the photoredox activities of BiOBr nanoplates at nanometer resolution, which deepens the understanding of facet-dependent and structure-specific catalytic dynamics of 2D materials.

title: Growth of cylindrical micelles and their use to prepare porous materials with tailored dimensions and alignment
abstract: Cylindrical templates enable materials with cylindrical pores. Cylindrical persistent micelle templates enable independent control of pore and wall dimensions where increasing the material content (TiO 2 ) increases the wall thickness alone.

title: Eco-friendly non-volatile solid additives for high-efficiency sustainable organic photovoltaic cells
abstract: This study develops fully eco-friendly OPVs using eco-friendly solid additives with o -xylene. DBP shows the best PCE of 17.78% due to enhanced crystallinity and optimized morphology, representing progress in efficient, stable, and sustainable OPVs.

title: Pure hydrogen and carbon nanotube production from methane decomposition over hydrotalcite-derived NiMCrAl (M = Co, Cu, Zn) catalysts
abstract: CO x free hydrogen is produced by the decomposition of methane over hydrotalcite-derived NiMCrAl (M = Co, Cu, Zn) catalysts.

title: Elucidating conductivity and nitriding resiliency in (Al/H)-ZnO coatings for NH 3 -fueled SOFC separators via SEM-EDX and MLP-DFT protocols
abstract: Characterizing experimental Al-doped ZnO SOFC coatings via simulation protocols – phase stability, electronic structure modelling, conductivity mechanism switching, and H-defect engineering.

title: Exploring the catalytic performance of a GeTe monolayer for nonaqueous Na–O 2 batteries: an in silico study
abstract: The non-aqueous metal–oxygen battery systems have high theoretical energy densities, making them promising alternatives for use in energy storage and electronic devices.

title: A highly efficient metal-free electrocatalyst of nitrogen-doped porous carbon nanoflowers toward oxygen electroreduction
abstract: A polymer-assisted method was developed to fabricate metal-free N-doped porous carbon nanoflower as an excellent oxygen reduction electrocatalyst.

title: Electrodeposited CoP 2 on CO 2 -laser-modified graphite felt: a robust electrocatalyst for nitrite reduction to ammonia
abstract: This study presents a CoP 2 /GO–GF electrocatalyst synthesized via CO 2 laser and electrodeposition, achieving superior NO 2 RR. Enhanced reaction kinetics and high Faradaic efficiency (∼80%) enable outstanding NH 3 yield (10.6 mg h −1 cm −2 ) and durability.

title: Heterovalent doping of amorphous oxyhalide solid electrolytes for high-performance all-solid-state lithium batteries
abstract: This work exhibits that heterovalent doping (Hf 4+ /Bi 3+ ) enhances the ionic conductivity and electrochemical performance of amorphous Li 3.2 TaC l5 O 1.6 electrolytes.

title: Graphene-templated hybrid Prussian blue-MXene nanocomposites for broadband photodetection with ultra-high carrier generation
abstract: The one-pot synthesis of a novel Mxene nanocomposite with Prussian blue achieves a 14-fold increase in photocurrent compared to MXene alone, thus highlighting its transformative potential for next-generation optoelectronic devices.

title: Phosphorylated carbon cathodes achieve perfect monovalent cation selectivity in capacitive deionization
abstract: Capacitive deionization using novel phosphorylated cathodes and a small electrical potential is optimized to achieve monovalent cation adsorption with low energy consumption, removing sodium from water without reducing the calcium concentration.

title: Base-mediated exfoliation of quinoline-linked covalent organic frameworks for heavy metal ion adsorption
abstract: Quinoline-linked COFs are synthesised using a multicomponent Doebner reaction and exfoliated under mild conditions to give Qu-CONs with high selectivity and fast kinetics for Cd 2+ adsorption.

title: Ferroelectric switching driven photocatalytic overall water splitting in the As/In 2 Se 3 heterostructure
abstract: The photocatalytic overall water splitting can be achieved in the As/In 2 Se 3 vdW heterostructure by regulating the polarization direction of the In 2 Se 3 layer.

title: Layered double hydroxide-based catalysts for seawater electrolysis at industrial current densities: advances and perspectives
