abstract: Seawater electrolysis is of great significance for sustainable hydrogen production, particularly in coastal and offshore regions with abundant renewable energy but limited freshwater resources.

title: Design and synthesis of a weakly solvated electrolyte for high-performance fluoride-ion batteries
abstract: A weakly solvated electrolyte strategy for high-performance fluoride-ion batteries at room temperature.

title: V 2 CT x MXene suppresses V 5+ dissolution and boosts the stability and performance of BiVO 4 for solar water splitting
abstract: V 2 CT x MXene acts as a hole extractor and reservoir, quickly extracting holes from BiVO 4 photoanode and transferring them to the Co–Pi co-catalyst, thereby suppressing V 5+ dissolution and enhancing photostability and PEC performance.

title: Size-dependent electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution activity of arrays of edge-like defects in MoS 2 crystals patterned by focused ion beam
abstract: Introducing surface defects on molybdenum disulfide (MoS 2 ) crystals is important to enhance the electrocatalytic activity toward the hydrogen evolution reaction (HER).

title: Synergistically self-assembled in situ growth of MXene@MOF derived sodium alginate hydrogel 3D frameworks as next-generation electrocatalysts for oxygen and hydrogen evolution
abstract: The need to minimize carbon emissions and improve sustainable energy systems has stimulated significant research into multifunctional materials.

title: Designing locally ordered structures of MnO 2 for high-rate cathodes in aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: A MnO 2 cathode design is proposed. It combines locally ordered nanocrystallites with a long-range amorphous structure, improving ion transfer and electronic conductivity for enhanced high-rate performance in aqueous zinc-ion batteries.

title: Ab initio investigations of zwitterionic polymers and their interactions with water and ice
abstract: An ab initio DFT study of zwitterionic polymers and their interactions with water and ice towards understanding their anti-icing behaviors.

title: Monodisperse fluorinated 3D covalent organic frameworks for enhanced adsorption and extraction of perfluorocarboxylic acids
abstract: A monodisperse fluorinated COF with uniform morphology was pioneered for enhanced adsorption and extraction of perfluorocarboxylic acids.

title: Isomeric cyano-vinylene-linked covalent organic frameworks and their impact on photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: Rational design of vinylene-linked isomeric covalent organic frameworks with different atomic arrangements of cyano-vinylene linkages and their structure–activity relationships for photocatalytic hydrogen evolution via water splitting are presented.

title: Defect engineering of MoS 2 /borophene/WS 2 sandwich heterostructures enhanced the HER catalytic activity and improved water splitting efficiency
abstract: To enhance the stability of borophene and improve its catalytic activity for the HER, we have proposed a MoS 2 /borophene/WS 2 sandwich structure and have systematically examined the influence of defect morphology on its catalytic properties.

title: Single-ion-conducting polymer electrolytes based upon borate-chain step-growth polymers
abstract: The preparation of saturated anionic organoborate-based polymers as single-ion-conducting solid electrolytes is described. The weakly basic or absent lone pairs prevent strong binding to lithium ions, limiting affinity for the matrix.

title: A localized surface plasmon resonance effect boosts photocatalytic hydrogen evolution of ZnIn 2 S 4 /amorphous MoO 3− x nanodot Z-scheme heterojunctions
abstract: ZnIn 2 S 4 /MoO 3− x Z-scheme heterojunctions with a LSPR effect were constructed with enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen evolution. The optimized ZnIn 2 S 4 /MoO 3− x exhibits a hydrogen evolution rate of 11.08 mmol g −1 h −1 , which is 8.3 times that of ZIS.

title: Multimetallic assembly of concave-shaped rectangular Mn 4 clusters as efficient hydrogen evolution electrocatalysts
abstract: Although great efforts have been dedicated to the investigation of active, stable and cost-effective electrocatalysts, only a few noteworthy breakthroughs have been accomplished until now.

title: Engineering redox-active benzo[1,2- b :4,5- b ′]dithiophene-based conjugated polymers: tuning porosity and linker architecture for high-performance supercapacitors
abstract: DTDO-based porous polymers with tailored linkers were synthesized; the Ph-DTDO polymer showed outstanding capacitance and energy density due to the synergistic effects of high porosity and phenyl linker-enhanced ion diffusion and charge transport.

title: ZnIn 2 S 4 -based heterostructure photocatalysts for solar energy conversion: a comprehensive review
abstract: This review systematically summarizes the latest research progress in ZnIn 2 S 4 -based heterostructure photocatalysts for solar energy conversion.

title: A ZIF-L derived carbon flower with in situ grown CNTs accelerates the reaction kinetics of Li–Se batteries
abstract: A ZIF-L derived carbon flower with in situ grown carbon nanotubes (CNTs@HPC) is designed for Li–Se batteries, which facilitates the charge/ion transfer and enhances the electrochemical reaction kinetics.

title: Sequential construction of stable nitrogen–oxygen compounds using high-throughput quantum mechanical calculations and customized machine learning model
abstract: The machine learning-driven approach and sequential construction strategy enabled the design of 168 nitrogen–oxygen compounds. High-throughput calculations identified 106 stable candidates, some rivaling N 2 O 4 , with potential for space exploration.

title: Fully packed ultrasmall Au nanoclusters in covalent organic frameworks as positive electrodes for supercapacitors
abstract: Au elements are first complexed with alkynyl-modified monomer before synthesizing COFs, and subsequently in situ reduction to obtain size-focused AuNCs in each pore, which become conductive compared to parent AuNCs and COFs.

title: Accelerated degradation of Pt-coated Ti porous transport layers under dynamic potential pulses in PEMWEs
abstract: Pulsed potentials severely degrades the Pt coating on Ti porous transport layers, followed by the conductivity loss and the deterioration of the durability of PEM water electrolyzers.

title: Exploring electronic and energy descriptors to identify the dual metal center catalyst for the CO 2 ER towards C 2 products
abstract: The CO 2 electrochemical reduction reaction (CO 2 ER) has sparked immense interest due to its potential to generate valuable multi-carbon (C 2 ) products.

title: A self-assembled monolayer as a hole-transport layer forming a robust interface with the active layer for enhanced thermal stability in organic solar cells
abstract: We observed molecular degradation at the HTL/photoactive interface. While PEDOT:PSS exhibited phase separation, a SAM HTL showed a more stable interface, allowing the enhanced thermal stability.

title: Stress and airflow-sensitive 3D-printed hydrogel sensor based on Cu 2+ –alanine coordination and graphene sheet networks
abstract: A 3D-printed HN hydrogel sensor showing stress and airflow monitoring performance based on the sensitive Cu 2+ –Ala coordination cross-linked PDA network and the rough, porous graphene filler network.

title: Catalytic oxidation of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural to 2,5-diformylfuran using MOF-808(Cu) under near ambient conditions
abstract: Single site square planar Cu( ii ) species were loaded into a metal–organic framework and utilised as a heterogeneous catalyst for the liquid phase selective oxidation of a biomass platform molecule to a sustainable monomer.

title: One-pot scalable production of conjugated microporous polymers with exceptional functionality
abstract: The Fuzhou University (FZU) approach allows scalable production and synthetic control over the surface area and function of conjugated microporous polymers (CMPs).

title: Rational design of MoS 2 -based dual-atom catalysts for CO 2 -to-methane conversion: thermodynamic and electronic insights into activity and selectivity
abstract: The rational design of CO 2 electroreduction catalysts requires the establishment of robust structure–activity relationships through precise electronic descriptors.

title: Boosting oxygen migration via plasmons of Au nanoparticles in solar-driven solid oxide photoelectrolysis cells
abstract: SOPCs utilize photothermal–electric synergistic effects to enhance water splitting for hydrogen production. Au nanoparticles with the LSPR effect facilitate the hydrogen evolution reaction and enhance the bulk-phase oxygen migration rate.

title: Hydrophobic, ionically conductive, self-adhesive and fully recyclable eutectogels for stretchable wearable sensors and triboelectric nanogenerators
authors: 'Ren'ai Li', 'Hongtian Zhang', 'Lizi Li', 'Biqiang Zhang', 'Xianyong Du', 'Weiyong Shao', 'Xueren Qian', 'Yunfeng Cao', 'Zhulan Liu'
abstract: A novel hydrophobic eutectogel synthesized via thioctic acid polymerization in a hydrophobic DES matrix, demonstrating excellent flexibility, recyclability, and sensitivity for strain sensing and energy harvesting in flexible electronics.

title: N-coordinated Fe–Co catalytic sites with interatomic cooperation for proton exchange membrane fuel cells
abstract: Dual-metallic Fe–Co active sites embedded in carbon supports exhibit enhanced oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) performance compared to single-metal counterparts.

title: Potential of Na 3 AlF 6 as solid electrolyte for all-solid-state Na batteries
abstract: Na 3 AlF 6 exhibits Na + conductivity by forming solid solution with Na 2 SiF 6 . The dominant conduction ion remains Na + , even in the presence of F − vacancies. The oxidation of Na 3 AlF 6 –Na 2 SiF 6 is not observed up to 5 V vs. Na + /Na, confirming its suitability as a coating material for hig…

title: Unraveling the light-promoted synergy between highly dispersed Ni and Ni nanoparticles for efficient photothermocatalytic cellulose steam reforming to syngas
abstract: 10Ni/MgO ET catalyst with a unique light-promoted synergy enabled a sustainable strategy for efficiently converting abundant biomass into syngas with excellent durability.

title: Nucleation-density-regulated dimensional evolution of growth unit from 2D nanosheets to 1D nanoneedles in self-assembled hierarchical NiCo 2 O 4 for enhanced lithium storage
abstract: We present a supersaturation-driven nucleation-density-regulated strategy for precise dimension-tuning in self-assembled hierarchical NiCo 2 O 4 . This approach provides new insights into the growth mechanism and reveals unique electrochemical behaviors.

title: A molecularly imprinted SERS sensor with both high-accuracy quantitative analysis and high-stability reuse based on spatial separation of catalytic sites
abstract: The synergistic effect of the molecular imprinting technique and spatially separated photocatalysis can ensure the accurate quantitative analysis and high-stability reuse of SERS sensors.

title: Mechanisms of interaction between bismuth-based materials and contaminants for subsurface remediation
authors: 'Carolyn I. Pearce', 'Daria Boglaienko', 'Amanda R. Lawter', 'Elsa A. Cordova', 'Kirk J. Cantrell', 'Mark E. Bowden', 'Nabajit Lahiri', 'Odeta Qafoku', 'Sebastian T. Mergelsberg', 'Charles T. Resch', 'Ferdinan Cintron Colon', 'Vanessa A. Garayburu-Caruso', 'Nicolas D'Annunzio', 'Mahalingam Balasubramanian', 'Sarah A. Saslow', 'Nikolla P. Qafoku', 'Vicky L. Freedman', 'Tatiana G. Levitskaia'
abstract: The remarkable structural versatility of bismuth-based materials means they are cost-effective materials with the potential to be used for in situ subsurface remediation of multiple contaminants at DOE legacy sites.

title: An adsorption-catalysis bifunctional MoS 2 /Mo@rGO host material for lithium–sulfur power batteries
abstract: A MoS 2 –Mo@rGO composite host material enhances lithium polysulfide adsorption and reaction kinetics in lithium–sulfur batteries, achieving a 4 minute drone activation.

title: Cation vacancy-driven structural modulation across scales in MXene/Zn (v) Mn 2 Se 4 for enhanced supercapacitor performance
abstract: Cationic vacancy engineering boosts electrode kinetics and ion transport by fine-tuning active sites and charge conduction routes.

title: In ambient air processed Cu 2 ZnSnS 4 absorber layers from DMSO-based precursors: enhanced efficiency via device post-annealing
abstract: A straightforward approach to precursor engineering for DMSO-based CZTS solar cells, combined with post-annealing in ambient air, was developed to enhance CZTS device efficiency.

title: Alloy nanocluster artificial photosystems steering photoredox organic transformation
abstract: Atomically precise alloy nanocluster artificial photosystems are exquisitely designed for photocatalytic selective organic transformation under visible light.

title: Innovating carbon-based perovskite solar cells: the role of a CN-anchoring self-assembled molecular layer in efficiency and stability
abstract: A self-assembled molecular layer (SAML) based on hole-selective materials (HSMs) was proposed with CN-anchoring groups on a phenanthroimidazole backbone linked to Aza molecules along with carbon as the cathode electrode to obtain an efficient and stable perovskite solar cell.

title: Eutectic electrolytes enable dendrite-free and ultradurable zinc metal anodes for rechargeable zinc batteries
abstract: Optical images of symmetric Zn‖Zn cells showing plating/stripping at an ultra-high current density of 5 mA cm −2 , and characteristic GCD performance of Zn‖V 2 O 5 cells at extreme temperatures from −20 °C to 60 °C.

title: Charting the chemical space of Zintl phases with graph neural networks and bonding insights
abstract: We combine graph neural networks and an upper bound energy minimization framework to explore >90 000 candidates, discovering 1810 stable Zintl phases with high precision and revealing the key role of ionic bonding in their stability.

title: Co-irradiation induced graft polymerization of pre-swollen polyacrylonitrile nanofiber membranes for uranium extraction from seawater
abstract: A widely adopted approach in the field of adsorbent preparation for uranium extraction involves grafting acrylonitrile onto inert polymer substrates, followed by amidoximation.

title: Unraveling the standing-up orientation transition of π-conjugated molecules on graphene templates
abstract: This study investigates the influence of graphene's surface properties on the orientation of pentacene molecules in organic thin films. The roughness and strain of graphene destabilize lying-down configurations, promoting nucleation with standing-up molecular orientations.

title: Yolk–shell structured microspheres consisting of CoO/CoP hetero-interfaced nanocomposites as highly active hydrogen evolution reaction electrocatalysts for AEM electrolyzer stacks
abstract: An anion exchange membrane water electrolyzer (AEM electrolyzer) is an advanced technology for converting electrical energy into hydrogen energy with high efficiency and low cost.

title: Adverse to beneficial: upcycling residual lithium compounds on LiNi 0.8 Mn 0.1 Co 0.1 O 2 into a stabilizing Li 1+ x Mn 2− x O 4 interface
abstract: This work presents an “adverse to beneficial” approach, converting surface RLCs on LiNi 0.8 Mn 0.1 Co 0.1 O 2 into a high-voltage stable Li 1+ x Mn 2− x O 4 -based interface to improve the electrochemical performance of LiNi 0.8 Mn 0.1 Co 0.1 O 2 .

title: Optimizing LaNiO 3 surface structure for an efficient oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: We report LaNiO 3 perovskite as an ORR catalyst with synergistic effects of oxygen vacancies and functional groups. Thermal shock introduces defects, enhancing ORR performance with a limiting current density increase from 4.2 to 5.4 mA cm −2 .

title: SAPO-11 molecular sieve synthesized via a novel alcohol–water solvent vapor-induced self-assembly conversion method and the hydroisomerization performance of its NiWS-supported catalyst
abstract: SAPO-11 molecular sieve synthesized via the alcohol–water SVISC method shows better catalytic performance than SAPO-11 molecular sieve synthesized via the conventional method in the hydroisomerization of n -hexadecane.

title: Optimization strategies for an electrospinning-based wearable strain sensor: from materials to structure
abstract: This review summarizes electrospinning wearable strain sensor optimization, focusing on materials/structure to boost sensing performance. It explores health monitoring and human–machine interfaces, offering future development guidelines.

title: Discerning two-dimensional metal halide perovskite moieties using solid-state NMR fingerprints
abstract: Solid-state NMR spectroscopy allows clear differentiation of the local spatial arrangements at the supramolecular level in 〈100〉- and 〈110〉-oriented layered perovskites, which arise from the characteristic connectivity of their inorganic frameworks.

title: From alloy composition to battery performance: a machine learning strategy for the design of Mg–air battery anodes
abstract: A novel ML-driven strategy is proposed to design Mg–air battery anodes with new elements beyond the existing dataset.

title: Novel electrochemical ion-exchange of Li x Na 3− x Zr 2 Si 2 PO 12 (LNZSP): a Li-ion conducting solid electrolyte for battery applications
abstract: Room-temperature electrochemical ion-exchange produces structurally and electrochemically stable LNZSP. Optimized processing ensures framework integrity and enables sustainable lithium recovery through electrochemical extraction.

title: Construction of charge transfer channels in pyrene-based covalent organic frameworks via wall chemistry for efficient photocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: Photocatalytic hydrogen production has gained significant attention as a green, energy-efficient, and environmentally sustainable approach, positioning hydrogen as a promising clean energy source.

title: Breaking structural symmetry to facilitate fast reaction kinetics
abstract: Breaking the intrinsic symmetry in crystal structures is a promising design concept for developing high-performance battery materials, providing a pathway to fast lithium-ion transport.

title: Stress mitigation and defect passivation in CsPbI 2 Br solar cells via controlled oxidation and thermal stress control
abstract: All-inorganic perovskites have emerged as promising candidates for tandem and photovoltaic applications due to their wide bandgap and enhanced stability.

title: Electrochemical production of H 2 O 2 on palladium-based clusters driven by metal–support interaction
abstract: Low-Pd PdO–CeO 2 –carbon catalysts enable efficient H 2 O 2 electrosynthesis with high yield and activity. Synergistic interactions boost performance, though long-term stability remains a challenge due to Pd cluster agglomeration and coarsening.

title: Quantification of mobile charge carrier yield and transport lengths in ultrathin film light-trapping ZnFe 2 O 4 photoanodes
authors: 'Kumaraswamy Miriyala', 'Sa'ar Shor Peled', 'Dino Klotz', 'Daniel A. Grave'
abstract: Resonant light trapping is used to enhance the performance of Ti-doped ZnFe 2 O 4 (ZFO) photoanodes. Spatial collection efficiency analysis shows that non-unity photogeneration yield of mobile charge carriers limits the performance of ZFO.

title: 3D printed barocaloric composites for environmentally friendly thermal energy management
abstract: This work discusses two new barocaloric materials, the first 3D printed barocaloric composite in the shape of a gyroid-like heat exchanger, and new potential applications for barocaloric thermal management in hydrogen and Li-ion energy technologies.

title: Synthesis of intermetallic L 1 0 -Fe 40 Ni 5 M 5 Pt 50 (M = Mn, Co, Cu) nanoparticles for electrocatalytic methanol oxidation
abstract: Developing a highly efficient methanol oxidation electrocatalyst is crucial for enhancing the performance of direct methanol fuel cells.

title: Leveraging self-passivation of quantum dots via nitrogen-doping for multifunctional biopolymer nanocomposites
abstract: N-doped QDs offer unique blue-light/UV-blocking, crystal nucleation, lubricating and toughening effects.

title: A comparative computational and scanning electrochemical microscopy study of factors influencing electron transfer at the hydrogenated and pristine graphite – propylene carbonate electrochemical interface
abstract: Through scanning electrochemical microscopy and computational simulation, we show that hydrogen functionalization on graphitic carbon electrodes increases electron transfer kinetics to redox-active species in non-aqueous media.

title: Beyond vehicular transport: fluorination-induced structural diffusion in ether electrolytes
abstract: High fluorine substitution promotes structural diffusion via fast anion/solvent exchanges, enhancing transport properties in fluorinated DEE solvent-based sodium-ion electrolytes.

title: Thermoelectric performance boost by chemical order in epitaxial L2 1 (100) and (110) oriented undoped Fe 2 VAl thin films: an experimental and theoretical study
abstract: Boosting thermoelectric performance is achieved by growing chemically ordered Fe 2 VAl thin films by sputtering.

title: Redox-active biomineralised zeolitic imidazolate frameworks enable peroxidase bioelectrocatalysis with shielding against substrate inhibition and thermal inactivation
abstract: The intimate inclusion of redox enzyme/redox mediator in crystalline ZIF-8 via biomimetic mineralisation shields the enzyme from inhibition while enabling electrical enzyme wiring for bioelectrocatalysis.

title: Functional groups bridging the layers of nitrogen carbide nanosheets facilitate ultrafast interlayer charge transfer: ab initio nonadiabatic molecular dynamics simulations
abstract: Functional groups bridging g-C 3 N 4 nanosheets facilitate ultrafast interlayer charge transfer.

title: Structure engineering of covalent organic frameworks as metal-free sorbents for urea recovery
abstract: Covalent organic frameworks exhibit strong multidentate hydrogen bonding with urea and high adsorption capacity, offering recyclable and tunable platforms for efficient urea removal from wastewater.

title: Na 3 Zr 2 Si 2 PO 12 /cellulose acetate composite solid electrolyte unlocking high ionic conductivity and long cycle life in solid-state sodium batteries
abstract: Sustainable cellulose acetate synergizes with NASICON to yield ultraconductive, dendrite-free sodium solid electrolytes with unprecedented cyclability.

title: Enhanced tin halide perovskite solar cells via crystal growth control using a multifunctional interfacial modifier
abstract: The introduction of a multifunctional interfacial modifier controls crystal growth and reduces defects in Sn-based halide perovskites, leading to a remarkable enhancement in solar cell performance.

title: Boosting the activity of silver catalysts for soot combustion by modification of the alumina support
abstract: A low-temperature solid solution may be the best solution!

title: Demagnetization-driven enhancement of electrochemical nitrogen reduction on two-dimensional magnetic transition metal borides
abstract: The ferromagnetic–paramagnetic phase transition enhances N 2 activation by stabilizing the side-on adsorption configuration, enabling a consecutive reaction pathway with reduced limiting potential on h -M 3 B 4 surfaces.

title: Flux synthesis of single crystal bismuth vanadate (BiVO 4 ) nanowires and their visible light driven photocatalytic water oxidation properties
abstract: Single crystalline nanowires can be obtained by recrystallization of BiVO 4 microparticles from a NaVO 3 flux. The wires promote the photocatalytic oxygen evolution reaction with an apparent quantum efficiency of 0.44% at 405 nm.

title: High-performance Na 3.5 Fe 0.5 V 1.5 (PO 4 ) 3 cathodes enabled by phase control and CQD-containing carbon coating
abstract: A CQD-integrated hybrid carbon coating enables the fabrication of phase-pure Na 3.5 Fe 0.5 V 1.5 (PO 4 ) 3 cathodes with fast kinetics, excellent rate capability, and ultralong cycle life, offering a scalable route for high-performance sodium-ion batteries.

title: An optical sensor for in situ real-time detection of intermediate products in nitrate reduction reactions
abstract: We propose a nitrite concentration sensor composed of a light module, an absorption module, and an imaging module, which achieves in situ real-time sensing of the nitrite concentration distribution on the electrode surface during the NO 3 RR reaction.

title: A metal-, solvent-, halogen-, and additive-free catalysis for CO 2 fixation by carbon dots
abstract: A recyclable CDs catalyst from lignin-derived syringic acid was developed, which could activate both CO 2 and epoxides, showing excellent catalytic activity for the fixation of CO 2 with epoxides into cyclic carbonates under very mild conditions.

title: A colorimetric ammonia sensor based on interfacially assembled porous polymer membrane: coupled hydrogen-bonding and electronic structure modulation
abstract: An interfacially assembled asymmetric polymer membrane enables rapid, visible, and smartphoneassisted ammonia detection via hydrogen-bond-mediated optical shifts, advancing portable platforms for intelligent environmental sensing.

title: The effect of lithium content on the discharge and electrochemical performance of Mg–Li–Zn–Y alloys for primary Mg–air batteries
abstract: In this work, the microstructure, corrosion resistance, and discharge performance of two hot-extruded Mg alloys with different lithium contents were thoroughly investigated.

title: Structural engineering of carbon-based cathodes for advanced aqueous Zn–CO 2 batteries: from macroscopic architectures to atomic-level manipulation
abstract: This review introduces the latest progress of Zn–CO 2 batteries from the aspects of battery systems, electrolytes, electrodes, while the structural engineering of carbon cathodes at both the macroscopic level and the atomic level is summarized.

title: Reconstructing the electronic structure of nickel selenide by Cu incorporation for an enhanced alkaline hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: This work demonstrates electronic reconstruction of nickel selenide with Cu, for enhanced alkaline HER activity and the dynamic phase evolution of nickel selenide over time enables it to outperform the state-of-the-art Pt at all potentials.

title: An anti-freezing flexible polymer electrolyte for high-performance zinc-ion batteries
abstract: An optimized 1.0 M Zn–eutectic SPE offers high ionic conductivity, mechanical strength, and stability. The Zn|SPE|V 10 O 24 · n H 2 O@rGO cell delivers excellent performance and durability, ensuring stable operation over a wide temperature range.

title: Efficient charge separation and transfer in a CdS/BiOBr/Bi 2 S 3 heterojunction via a dual S-scheme mechanism and photothermal effect for enhanced Cr( vi ) photoreduction
abstract: A novel dual S-scheme CdS/BiOBr/Bi 2 S 3 heterojunction photocatalyst was successfully designed and fabricated, and achieved photothermal synergistic enhancement of full spectrum photocatalytic activity.

title: High-entropy alloy self-supporting bifunctional electrocatalysts with exceptional performance for flexible zinc–air batteries
abstract: High-entropy alloy FeCoNiCrCu nanoparticles were assembled on carbon nanofibers by electrospinning. The potential difference is 0.69 V. FeCoNiCrCu/CNFs is an efficient bifunctional oxygen catalyst for flexible zinc–air batteries.

title: Semi-interpenetrating polymer network electrolyte membrane for enhanced lithium-ion transport and interfacial stability in silicon-based lithium-ion battery
abstract: The SIPN electrolyte integrates strong Si-electrode bonding, high ionic conductivity, and robust mechanical stability. Its multifunctional design stabilizes SEI formation, prevents cracking, and enhances battery safety and long-term cycling.

title: A novel ternary Mn 2 O 3 decorated GO-MoS 2 heterostructure for enhanced tetracycline degradation and green H 2 production under visible light
abstract: A facile synthesis method of a manganese( iii ) oxide decorated on graphene oxide molybdenum disulphide composite (Mn 2 O 3 @GO-MoS 2 ) is reported as a sustainable photocatalyst for green hydrogen (H 2 ) production and tetracycline degradation.

title: Recent advancements in metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) for flexible supercapacitors aimed at wearable technology
abstract: Flexible supercapacitors have made significant progress, as they can be integral to the wearable technology field due to their unique ability to allow seamless movement for the wearer.

title: Artificial interface engineering to achieve high-performance garnet-based solid-state lithium metal batteries
abstract: LLZTO@Li–Sn/LiCl-assembled symmetric cells can operate over 6000 h at 0.2 mA cm −2 .

title: Broad-spectrum photothermal high-entropy alloy powders for efficient solar-driven antibacterial and dye degradation
abstract: The development of solar-driven photothermal materials for efficient wastewater treatment remains a significant challenge.

title: Phase behavior, crystal structure, and superprotonic conductivity of Cs[(H 2 PO 4 ) 1−2 y (HPO 4 ) y ]: phosphate deficient analogs to cubic CsH 2 PO 4 in the (1 − x )CsH 2 PO 4 – x Cs 2 HPO 4 system
abstract: Cubic CsH 2 PO 4 , which crystallizes in the CsCl structure-type, can support a large concentration of excess Cs via phosphate vacancies. Dissolution of Cs 3 PO 4 into the cubic structure lowers the temperature at which superprotonic conductivity occurs.

title: Suppressing O-type stacking and cation migration with Mg and Si doping in P2-type Fe–Mn layered oxides for sodium-ion cathodes
abstract: Layered oxide cathodes for Na-ion batteries containing Mn or Fe are of considerable interest for sustainable energy storage applications largely due to cost issues, relative abundance and high capacities.

title: Deactivation of copper electrocatalysts during CO 2 reduction occurs via dissolution and selective redeposition mechanism
abstract: During electrochemical CO 2 reduction, copper undergoes gradual morphological restructuring and deactivation due to a dissolution–redeposition mechanism.

title: In situ surface reconstruction of silver leads to competent activity for the electrocatalytic hydrogenation of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural
abstract: Preferentially oriented Ag nanoparticles (PO-Ag NPs) dominated by the Ag(110) facet were used for the hydrogenation of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural to 2,5-bis(hydroxymethyl)furan, exhibiting outstanding performance across a broad potential window.

title: A biomass derived jute carbon integrated FeCoNi alloy as a robust catalyst for alkaline water splitting
abstract: Sustainable energy solutions demand efficient electrocatalysts for hydrogen and oxygen evolution reactions.

title: A highly efficient Na-doped Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5 scintillator for γ-ray detection and flexible X-ray imaging
abstract: Na + doping in Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5 elevates the PLQY to 95%. Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5 :Na + γ-ray detector has good energy resolution and high light yield. A breakthrough spatial resolution of 25.6 LP per mm@MTF = 0.2 was achieved for X-ray imaging based on Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5 :Na + @PMMA film.

title: Morphology and doping engineering of sulfur-doped g-C 3 N 4 hollow nanovesicles for boosting photocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: Based on a supramolecular self-assembly strategy, an advanced sulfur-doped hollow g-C 3 N 4 nanovesicle (HV-SCN) is developed successfully for efficient photocatalytic H 2 evolution.

title: Research progress on the preparation of cobalt-based oxide catalysts with different morphologies and their application for catalytic purification of air pollutants
abstract: The removal and purification of air pollutants are urgently required because of the significant harm posed to humans and the environment.

title: Unveiling the significance of working electrode substrates in electrocatalytic water splitting for sustainable hydrogen energy production
abstract: This perspective highlights how electrode substrate choice critically affects electrocatalyst performance in water splitting, guiding researchers to design better catalysts by leveraging each substrate’s unique properties.

title: A medium-entropy garnet-type oxide as a solid electrolyte with enhanced air stability for Li-ion batteries
abstract: A novel medium-entropy garnet Li 6.5 La 3 Zr 0.5 Ta 0.5 Nb 0.5 Y 0.5 O 12 (LLZTNYO) was synthesized via a solid-phase method. LLZTNYO exhibited high Li-ion conductivity (1.87 × 10 −4 S cm −1 ) and maintained for 30 days in an air without decay.

title: MXene-assisted CoZnCr for efficient alkaline seawater splitting and assembly of an anion exchange membrane electrolyzer
abstract: Efficient electrocatalysts integrated into an anion exchange membrane electrolyzer enable simultaneous oxygen and hydrogen evolution in seawater, exhibiting high activity and durability while suppressing anodic corrosion.

title: Boosting perfluorooctanoic acid capture ability and water stability with a dual synergistic effect MOF-on-MOF adsorbent and insights from DFT calculations
abstract: To balance adsorption kinetics and capacity in PFOA capture/separation, we urgently need MOFs with defect-free crystals and well-defined hierarchical “micro–mesoporous” structures.

title: A dual-functional S-scheme Ti 3 C 2 /MoS 2 –ZnIn 2 S 4 heterojunction for accelerated photocatalytic H 2 evolution and efficient solar evaporators
abstract: A Ti 3 C 2 /MoS 2 –ZnIn 2 S 4 composite were designed and assembled onto 3D silk aerogel (NSA), forming a photo-thermal system that enhances H 2 production and water evaporation for dual solar desalination and energy applications.

title: Accelerating the electrochemical performance of solid oxide fuel cells using a Ce(Gd, Bi, Yb)O 2− δ diffusion barrier layer acting as an oxygen reservoir at high-current loading conditions
abstract: Ce(Gd, Bi, Yb)O 2− δ with high oxygen storage capacity (OSC) facilitates stable oxygen supply to SOFCs under high current operation, leading to enhanced performance.

title: An ion-exchange strategy to eliminate anion impurities within LDH structure for refined NCA cathode materials
abstract: Impurities of sulfate ions inside the NCA precursor adversely affect the cathode materials. This issue is mitigated by removing impurities through ion exchange.

title: Compositional and interfacial engineering for improved light stability of flexible wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells
abstract: Compositional and interfacial engineering for improved light stability of flexible wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells and a highly efficient flexible all-perovskite tandem device.

title: Excellent thermoelectric performance of Bi 2 MO 4 Cl (M = Y, La, and Bi) derived from ultra-low lattice thermal conductivity
abstract: Based on DFT calculations, the average ZT of Bi 3 O 4 Cl is predicted to reach 2.2 at 1000 K and the out-of-plane ZT of Bi 2 LaO 4 Cl to exceed 4, mainly due to their ultra-low lattice thermal conductivity.

title: Crafting Zn/nano-H-ZSM-5 catalysts for methanol-to-aromatics conversion: tailoring crystal size and modifying surface electron density
abstract: Optimizing H-ZSM-5 crystal size (60–80 nm) shortens reactant diffusion paths, enhancing mass transfer and coke resistance. In MTA, Zn species exhibit electron-deficient Lewis acidity, synergistically improving catalytic performance.

title: Atomic insight into Li + ion transport in amorphous electrolytes Li x AlO y Cl 3+ x −2 y (0.5 ≤ x ≤ 1.5, 0.25 ≤ y ≤ 0.75)
abstract: We use ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD) and machine learning interatomic potential based molecular dynamics (MLIP-based MD) simulations to explore the structure and ion transport characteristics of amorphous Li–Al–O–Cl solid-state electrolytes.

title: Nonflammable single-solvent electrolyte towards highly stable Li-rich Mn-based cathode materials
abstract: A nonflammable single-solvent electrolyte is constructed, which exhibits high intrinsic oxidation resistance and facilitates forming stable cathode–electrolyte interphase. thereby improving the electrochemical performance of Li-rich cathodes.

title: Hybrid polysaccharide–NaI derived transparent and flexible solid-state electrolyte films for sustainable design of supercapacitors
abstract: Na + -ions migrate through water channels in transparent and flexible solid-state electrolytes.

title: Dynamic pyridinethiol ligand shuttling within iron-anchored covalent organic frameworks boosts CO 2 photoreduction
abstract: A dynamic D⋯M–A structure COF can effectively suppress photogenerated charge recombination, achieving rapid electron transfer via round-trip shuttling of pyridinethiol ligands within iron-anchored COFs and thereby enhancing PCO 2 RR performance.

title: Specific Cu 2 O surfaces for electrocatalytic oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: Cu 2 O rhombic dodecahedra are more active towards electrochemical oxygen reduction reaction than octahedra and cubes on a carbon nanotube matrix. Density functional theory calculations provide mechanistic insights to understand this facet dependence.

title: Research progress in Cu–ZrO 2 catalysts: a systematic review of their preparation, doping, and applications
abstract: This review provides a systematic and comprehensive discussion of the preparation of copper–zirconium catalysts, doping methods, and catalysis in various reactions.

title: Crucial impact of degrees of freedom on the pressure-induced optical properties of water-stable 1D perovskites
abstract: We studied the effects of pressure on two water-stable 1D perovskites, using organic cations with high and low degrees of freedom. In an aqueous environment, we revealed impact on the structural and optical properties of materials under pressure.

title: Exploring hindered glymes as electrolyte solvents for sodium–oxygen batteries: impact on electrochemical performance and discharge product stability
abstract: This study shows that hindered glymes improve Na–O 2 battery performance by enhancing cycling stability, optimizing solvation, and stabilizing the electrolyte interphase, offering a safer, more efficient alternative to conventional electrolytes.

title: Colloid-like MXene/phenolic resin composite films with multi-interface architectures for enhanced light-to-heat conversion
abstract: GPTS-modified MXene nanosheets remain uniformly dispersed in phenolic resin as a colloid-like structure, enhancing interfacial light trapping with multi-interface reflection for low reflectance, low transmittance, and clear imaging.

title: Strategically designed catalysts for ammonia synthesis under mild conditions: recent advances and challenges
abstract: Ammonia is envisioned to play a pivotal role in the changing energy landscape, with immense potential to promote carbon-neutrality and circularity in the energy economy.

title: Design and mechanism investigation of aggregation-controlled double-layer SnO 2 for enhanced performance in perovskite solar cells
abstract: This study shows annealing impacts double-layer SnO 2 in perovskite solar cells: 120 °C increases roughness for electron extraction, while 150 °C improves connectivity, lowers water, boosting efficiency and durability.

title: Tuning Li/Al-LDHs nucleation and active sites toward enhanced lithium extraction
abstract: Modulation of crystal nucleation by pH at the reaction endpoint regulates the reactive oxygen species content of LDH to enhance adsorption capacity and anti-deactivation performance.

title: Mechanisms of oxygen transport resistance of mesoporous carbon-supported catalysts in fuel cells
abstract: Origin of local oxygen transport resistance for water-Pt catalyst is revealed; R ads is significantly affected by the structure of dense layer on Pt surfaces; the dense effect of interior Pt catalysts leads to the remarkable increase of .

title: Regulating metal–support interaction of Pt/CeO 2 catalysts via alkali metal modification for efficient toluene oxidation
abstract: Compared to Pt/CeO 2 with strong MSI, the doped Na in Pt/1.1Na–CeO 2 with moderate MSI promotes reactive oxygen species from Pt–O–Ce and facilitates electron delocalization, thus enhancing oxygen activation and improving toluene oxidation activity.

title: Hierarchically porous Co–N–C electrocatalysts with enhanced mass transport and cobalt utilization efficiency for oxygen reduction reaction in high-performance PEMFCs
abstract: Cobalt-coordinated nitrogen-doped carbon (Co–N–C) materials have emerged as promising alternatives to platinum-based catalysts for proton exchange membrane fuel cells (PEMFCs) due to their cost-effectiveness and durability.

title: Improved charge transfer performance of eosin Y-sensitized anatase TiO 2 by anchoring group modification: from theoretical design to experiment
abstract: Theoretical design and experimental proof of photocatalytic performance of Eosin Y (EY) on anatase TiO 2 with a pyridine linker were obtained for increasing the photobiocatalytic activity of water splitting using visible light.

title: CuMg-MOF-74 derived stable Cu/MgO@SiO 2 for the hydrogenation of bis(2-hydroxyethyl)cyclohexane-1,4-dicarboxylate
abstract: The Cu/MgO@SiO 2 catalyst was both active and stable for the hydrogenation of BHCD to CHDM.

title: Turning residual chlorides into exfoliation agents: fully gas-phase exfoliation of silicon nanosheets via amine-driven expansion for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: We turn a byproduct into a tool: instead of washing away CaCl 2 from reacted CaSi 2 , we expand it with NH 3 to exfoliate nanosheets. The outside-in process forms corrugated layers that stay interconnected, preserving structure & enhancing performance.

title: Recent advances in metal-based Janus nanomaterials: synthesis and electrocatalytic applications
abstract: This review provides the recent progress on the synthesis and electrocatalytic applications of novel Janus metal-based nanomaterials.

title: Dual-functional enhancement of argyrodite solid electrolytes with borohydride for high ionic conductivity and lithium metal compatibility via AgCl addition
abstract: Dual-functional AgCl addition enhances ionic conductivity and interfacial stability in borohydride argyrodites, improving ASSB performance and suppressing dendrites.

title: Thermal stability and electrochemical behavior of commercial polycrystalline and single-crystalline cathodes integrated with cubic Li 6.4 La 3 Zr 1.4 Ta 0.6 O 12 for all-solid-state lithium batteries
abstract: All-solid-state lithium batteries (ASSLBs) have emerged as promising next-generation energy storage systems, offering enhanced safety and higher energy density compared to conventional Li-ion batteries.

title: Analysis of tin oxide supported transition metal single-atom catalysts for oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Water electrolysis using acid stable and active oxide supported single-atom electrocatalysts offers a promising avenue for cheap green hydrogen production and efficient energy storage.

title: A frost-resistant and self-healing enhanced hydrogel for wearable sensors and electronic skin
abstract: A one-step method was employed to synthesize a freeze-resistant, self-healing, and adhesive hydrogel for strain sensing and electronic skin applications.

title: Pseudo-lithium vacancies in hydrogen rich Li 3 OCl
abstract: The antiperovskite Li 3 OCl is reported as a superionic conductor, however, due to its hygroscopic nature, it is most likely Li 3− x OH x Cl. This leads to the formation of beneficial lithium vacancy-like defects which enhance the conductivity.

title: Modulating electrolyte solvation structures with Fe-embedded carbon matrix substrates for robust lithium-metal plating
abstract: Carbonized Fe-BTC to Fe@C is utilized as a substrate to enhance lithium deposition by modulating the Li + solvation structure. This approach significantly improves cycling performance.

title: Reduction of RuO 2 nanoparticles supported on silica by organic molecules: a strategy for nanoparticle redispersion
abstract: A detailed study of reduction of supported RuO 2 nanoparticles by organic molecules. The relationship between reduction heat, reaction products, nanoparticle dispersion, and catalytic activity was investigated.

title: Polymer-assisted yttrium surface-enrichment doping of O3-type NaNi 1/3 Fe 1/3 Mn 1/3 O 2 cathodes to enhance high voltage and air stability in sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Y-surface-doped NaNi 1/3 Fe 1/3 Mn 1/3 O 2 was used as a cathode for a Na-ion battery. A uniform Y surface-enriched region was successfully formed with the assistance of PVA.

title: Effect of impurities in different activated carbon materials and their in-depth electrochemical analysis in supercapacitor coin-cell devices with organic electrolyte
abstract: Effect of impurities in different activated carbon materials and their in-depth electrochemical analysis in supercapacitor coin-cell devices with organic electrolyte.

title: Nanostructured room-temperature synthesized Cu 0.60 Ni 0.40 /PEDOT-derived sulfur-doped carbon composites for enhanced thermoelectric performance via acid-engineered interfaces
abstract: Acid-engineered Cu 0.60 Ni 0.40 /PEDOT–SC composites remove surface oxides to boost electron transport, while nanoscale defects scatter phonons, enabling selective decoupling of electrical and thermal transport for improved thermoelectrics.

title: Durable MRuO x direct seawater electrolysis enabled by the synergistic effect between p-block dopants and active site Ru
abstract: Sustainable hydrogen production via durable direct seawater electrolysis enabled by the synergistic effect between p-block M and active site Ru.

title: Undemanding synthesis of N,P co-doped carbon nanosheets for the hydrogen evolution reaction: combining experimental quantitative analysis and DFT calculation corroboration
abstract: We developed a cost-effective method to synthesize ultrathin N, P co-doped nanosheets for the hydrogen evolution reaction. The synergistic effects of N and P were investigated by combining experimental results with density functional theory.

title: Factors governing surface reactions leading to nitridation of nickel catalysts in ammonia-fueled solid oxide fuel cells
abstract: Solid oxide fuel cells that directly utilize ammonia as a hydrogen storage medium typically employ nickel-based materials, such as Ni/yttria-stabilized zirconia, as anode catalysts.

title: Thermally assisted photocatalytic industrial flue gas CO 2 conversion: 100% selective CO production via synergistic adsorption–conversion in NH 2 –MXene–MOF hierarchical interfaces
abstract: Thermally assisted photocatalytic CO 2 conversion into high value-added chemicals represents an effective strategy for carbon emission reduction and circular carbon economy.

title: Ga-Sb and Ga-AgCl liquid metal-based electrodes with self-healing for sweat pH sensors
abstract: Flexible electronic devices with self-healing ability have garnered wide attention, and the development of liquid metal-based electrodes may extend their applications.

title: An interpenetrating-polymer-network-based tough ion gel with high humidity resistance for sensitive human motion detection
abstract: A tough ion gel with high humidity tolerance and high sensing performance was developed by constructing hydrophobic interpenetrating polymer networks.

title: Surface and bulk properties of black, blue and transparent TiO x thin-film photoanodes for green hydrogen generation by water splitting
abstract: TiO x thin films were deposited by RF sputtering for green hydrogen generation. The optimized photoelectrochemical performance of photoanodes composed of anatase/rutile or anatase could be correlated with their morphology and surface defects.

title: A mechanoresponsive elastomeric binder toughened using a supramolecular zwitterionic network for silicon microparticle anodes
abstract: A multifunctional polymer binder uses a mechanoresponsive dual-crosslinking network (zwitterionic network + hydrogen-bonded network) to stabilize silicon microparticle anode interphases and improve cycling stability.

title: Thermally induced cyclic resistance transition of a transparent and flame-retardant layered oxidized MXene composite nanocoating for remote-sync fire monitoring
abstract: Controllably oxidized MXene (OM) was integrated with ANF to form a transparent, flame-retardant nanocoating. The composite-enabled fire-warning device (FWD) demonstrated rapid and multi-range alarming, offering real-time/remote fire safety solutions.

title: High-performance anion exchange membrane fuel cells and zinc–air batteries enabled by a hierarchically porous hollow Fe/N/C aerogel catalyst
abstract: This study designs a porous hollow Fe/N/C aerogel catalyst to boost electrochemical performance in AEMFCs and ZABs, emphasizing porous structure optimization for single-cell performance and the transition from half-cell to single-cell evaluations.

title: Advances in the preparation strategies and structural regulation for CeO 2 -containing electrocatalysts applied to the anodes of direct alcohol fuel cells: a comprehensive review
abstract: Direct alcohol fuel cells (DAFCs) are promising clean energy systems that directly transform the chemical energy of liquid alcohols into electricity.

title: A self-healing, deformation-resistant MXene double-network hydrogel for stable solar-driven interfacial evaporation
abstract: A self-healing, deformation-resistant MXene double-network hydrogel for stable solar-driven interfacial evaporation.

title: Synergistic and visualized toughening of elastomers through mechanophore crosslinks and multiple networks
abstract: Integrating mechanophores into multiple-network elastomers enables 20-fold toughness enhancement and 37% activation and provides insights into the toughening mechanism via bond scission visualization.

title: Construction of a multi-layer Pt-based self-supporting catalyst for high-current-density hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Multi-layer Pt-based self-supporting catalyst Pt–Cu–NiP/FP was prepared by mild electroless plating for high-current-density HER.

title: Side-chain engineering of C-shaped ortho -benzodipyrrole-based A–D–A acceptors for energy loss suppression in organic photovoltaics
abstract: Tailoring the side chains of C-shaped ortho -benzodipyrrole-based A–D–A acceptors enables modulation of crystallinity and energy levels, thereby suppressing energy loss in organic photovoltaics.

title: Template-based synthesis of porous and wrinkled Pt-supported MXenes for enhanced bifunctional electrocatalysis of the ORR and HER
abstract: This study reports the use of a melamine–formaldehyde template for synthesizing a highly porous 2D MXene doped with Pt (Pt/PMx), which serves as an efficient electrocatalyst for both ORR and HER.

title: Insight into the oxygen evolution reaction mechanism catalyzed by phosphate-substituted FeCo 2 O 4 nanosheets: proton-coupled electron transfer assisted adsorbate evolution mechanism investigated by in situ NAP-XPS
abstract: This study explores phosphate-substituted FeCo 2 O 4 as catalysts for the oxygen evolution reaction. In situ NAP-XPS analysis elucidates the reaction pathway, with proton-coupled electron transfer identified as a critical mechanistic step.

title: Interstitial cobalt nitrides (Co x N): holistic frontiers in energy storage and catalysis
abstract: This review explores the roles of Co x N in LIBs, SCs, RZABs, and electrocatalysis, focusing on fabrication strategies and structure–property–performance relationships to guide the design of efficient and durable electrochemical energy systems.

title: Efficient strategies for designing photoanodes toward selective photoelectrochemical glycerol upgrading: a review
abstract: This review systematically summarizes the strategies for designing photoanodes to improve the selectivity and activity of photoelectrochemical glycerol oxidation.

title: Alloy-assisted stabilization of thin Li metal anodes in pouch-type cells
abstract: Metal trifluoromethanesulfonate additives promote a lithiophilic alloy formation and a LiF-rich SEI, stabilizing thin Li metal anodes and achieving long-term cyclability in pouch-type full cells under industrially relevant harsh conditions.

title: Phosphorus-doped amorphous TiO 2 /C interface enables hierarchical SEI formation on micron-sized SiO anodes for ultra-stable lithium-ion batteries
abstract: This study proposes a phosphorus-doped amorphous TiO 2 /C hybrid coating for SiO anodes, which enables the formation of a hierarchical inorganic-rich SEI (Li 3 P–LiF–Li 2 CO 3 ) to enhance interfacial stability and Li + transport for LIBs.

title: The rivet effect: a new insight into improving structural stability in Mg-doped Ni-rich single-crystal layered oxide cathodes for Li-ion batteries
abstract: In this study, using the Mg-doped single crystal NMC622 as model systems, we suggest a new “rivet” effect of Mg-doping that prevents O3-to-O1 transition, accompanying tensile strain and intragranular crack initiation during electrochemical cycling.

title: Synergistic 1T/2H-MoS 2 hybrid phases enable exceptional aluminum-ion battery performance with high capacity and stability
abstract: Solvothermally synthesized 1T/2H-MoS 2 nanospheres as Al-ion battery cathodes exhibit a heterophase structure, achieving 187 mA h g −1 capacity and 99% retention after 100 cycles, enhancing electrochemical performance, stability, and ion transport.

title: A newly synthesized 2D polyaramid: structure, properties, and applications in energy storage, electrocatalysis, and sensing
abstract: This review presents a comprehensive discussion of the multifunctional applications of 2DPA.

title: Phenanthro[9,10- d ]imidazole-based hole transport materials for perovskite solar cells: influence of π-bridge units
abstract: A series of low-cost donor–π–donor structured hole-transport materials have been developed. Among them, O-FDIMD-Ph exhibits superior performance to spiro-OMeTAD, achieving higher power conversion efficiency and enhanced operational stability.

title: Fire-safe polymer composites enabled by a nature-inspired MOF-derived single atom catalysis strategy for thermal management with recyclability
abstract: Inspired by pine cones, a TPU composite material with multiple functions such as flame retardancy, thermal conductivity, and UV resistance was prepared.

title: GenAI-enhanced 4D nano-tomography for advanced battery microstructure analysis
abstract: In situ imaging is essential to understand the dynamic microstructure evolution within battery materials upon electrochemical loading, however, challenges arise in maintaining high contrast while also preserving a wide field-of-view.

title: Electrolyte design enabling practical lithium/sulfur batteries via interfacial manipulation and inhibited polysulfide dissolution
abstract: Electrolyte design enables improved electrode/electrolyte interface stability and inhibition of polysulfide dissolution in Li–S pouch cells.

title: Porous organic cages for radionuclide and heavy metal adsorption: structure–activity relationships
abstract: This work provides a closed-loop iterative strategy to develop POCs for adsorbing radionuclides and heavy metals, encompassing “synthetic strategy → structure–function analysis → performance feedback → strategy optimization.”

title: Water-attracting adsorbates for enhancing the cyclability of Zn anodes in aqueous Zn-ion batteries
abstract: Phloroglucinol adsorbs onto the Zn surface, where its hydroxyl groups attract water molecules from hydrated Zn ions, promoting uniform Zn nucleation, suppressing side reactions, and thereby enabling uniform Zn growth and dissolution during cycling.

title: Insights into the role of the covalent Ni–O bonds in LiNiO 2 positive electrodes: a correlative hard X-ray spectroscopy study
abstract: Representative NiO 2 layers composed of edge-sharing NiO 6 octahedra and a Li interlayer from the LiNiO 2 crystal structure, highlighting a local NiO 6 unit. A simplified band diagram illustrates the negative charge transfer from O 2p to Ni 3d states.

title: Self-triggered fire-extinguishing microcapsules with pronounced performance in suppressing the thermal runaway of lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A novel temperature self-triggered microcapsule was prepared by the in situ polymerization method, possessing dual effects of extinguishing and cooling. It can delay the thermal runaway of batteries and inhibit the propagation of thermal runaway.

title: Polyethyleneimine intercalated into alkyl layer for superhydrophobic interface: low-energy and O 2 & H 2 O-resistant CO 2 sorbent
abstract: A superhydrophobic alkyl-silica was developed for immobilize polyethyleneimine for CO 2 capture. PEI are intercalated into the alkyl layer, where hydrophobic chains hinder O 2 /H 2 O access while allowing selective CO 2 adsorption and desorption.

title: Efficient natural gas upgrading using a stable Cd-based porous coordination network
abstract: Efficient CH 4 purification from a ternary CH 4 /C 2 H 6 /C 3 H 8 gas mixture was achieved using a stable cadmium-based porous coordination polymer with propeller-like cages.

title: Se-doped sulfurized polyacrylonitrile as a high-performance anode for stable K-ion storage
abstract: We found out that Se doping can significantly enhance the electrochemical performance of sulfurized polyacrylonitrile (SPAN) anode materials for K-ion storage. Besides, a low-cost and high-performance K-ion full cell was constructed.

title: Progress in and outlook on polarization-sensitive perovskite photodetectors
abstract: Device structures of perovskite CPPD (left) and LPPD (right).

title: Engineering a built-in electric field in a wood-derived NiFeCo-LDH@NiFe heterojunction for enhanced bifunctional water splitting
abstract: Optimizing the electronic structure is pivotal in augmenting the intrinsic catalyst activity.

title: Sulfur-doped g-C 3 N 4 /TiO 2 anatase (101) composites for photocatalytic applications: a DFT-based computational investigation
abstract: With the aim of improving the visible-light photocatalytic activity of TiO 2 , the structural, electronic and optical properties of bare and sulfur-doped g-C 3 N 4 /TiO 2 composites have been investigated as potential candidate materials.

title: Graphite fluoride interface blocking for higher capacity fluoride ion thermal batteries
abstract: Fluoride ion thermal batteries (FITBs) have emerged as a promising next-generation energy storage system attributed to their unique ionic transport characteristics coupled with exceptional theoretical energy density.

title: Acceptor aggregation induced hole mobility degradation in polymer solar cells
abstract: The molecular conformation of electron acceptors significantly influences the evolution of hole mobility under different conditions, even when the same donor material and weight fraction are used.

title: Effects of Ce co-doping at the A site of Sm 0.5− x Sr 0.5 CoO 3± δ for a high-performance air electrode for solid oxide reversible cells
abstract: Ce co-doping at the A-site of Sr(Sm)CoO 3 improved the surface activity for the oxygen reduction and evolution reactions. (Ce)Sr(Sm)CoO 3 was proposed as a promising catalytic material for the air electrode of solid oxide cells.

title: Multifunctional dual-crosslinked Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene-based hydrogels for wearable sensors with enhanced mechanical robustness and broadband microwave absorption
abstract: A novel dual-crosslinked Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene-based hydrogel is developed with exceptional mechanical stretchability, ultra-high strain sensitivity, and broadband microwave absorption.

title: Flexibly bonded lead–halogen dual sites of coordination polymers for photocatalytic C–N coupling
abstract: This study introduces flexibly bonded Pb–X dual-site coordination polymers (XCP-3(Br/I)) engineered to harmonize electronic synergy and spatial adaptability for photocatalytic C–N coupling.

title: Reducibility, adsorption energies, surface acidity – fundamental material properties for fast oxygen exchange
abstract: Combining a comprehensive discussion of literature with new mechanistic insights, we derive two fundamental design principles for material systems with optimal oxygen exchange kinetics: a shallow O 2p band center and a low work function.

title: Ultra-micropores of hard carbons for ultrafast Na-ion storage
abstract: A hard carbon with concentrated ultra-micropore sizes of 0.4–0.8 nm is prepared by a protonation-mediated strategy, which enables high plateau capacity and rate capability for sodium-ion batteries toward high energy and power densities.

title: Accelerating lithium polysulfide conversion kinetics with a bimetallic phosphide Ni 2 P/CoP composite as a multifunctional separator in advanced lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: A bimetallic phosphide Ni 2 P/CoP composite is rationally constructed and utilized as a multifunctional separator to effectively accelerate lithium polysulfide conversion kinetics.

title: Discerning morphological evolution under thermal stress in polymerized small molecular acceptor-based all polymer solar cells
abstract: This study investigates the thermal degradation pathways of polymerized small molecular acceptor based all-PSCs, revealing that severe thermal disaggregation of polymer donors is a potentially primary cause of efficiency loss under thermal stress.

title: MXene/graphitic carbon nitride-supported metal selenide for all-solid-state flexible supercapacitor and oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: We report a new type of combination of rare earth metal selenides (Ce 2 Se 3 and Er 2 Se 3 ) with a Ti 3 C 2 T x /S-doped graphitic carbon nitride heterostructure for bifunctional application in flexible supercapacitors and oxygen evolution reactions.

title: Synergistic pyridinic-N/pyrrolic-N coordination tailors cobalt electronic states for high-efficiency oxygen reduction in Zn-air batteries
abstract: This work reveals the dual-function mechanism of pyridinic-N and pyrrolic-N in enhancing ORR activity: pyridinic-N promotes O 2 adsorption/activation at Co sites, while pyrrolic-N lowers the energy barrier for *OH → OH − conversion.

title: Hydrogen-intercalation PdZn bimetallene for urea electro-synthesis from nitrate and carbon dioxide
abstract: We have reported the incorporation of hydrogen into PdZn bimetallene, showing excellent performance for electrosynthesis of urea due to the optimized adsorption energy of intermediates.

title: Tuning nickel single-atom coordination in ternary N-, P-, and S-doped graphene for improved Li–O 2 batteries
abstract: This work pioneers the one-pot pyrolysis synthesis of Ni–N-/P-/S-coordinated single-atom catalysts (NiNPSG) for Li–O 2 batteries, achieving record bifunctional activity via asymmetric electronic modulation.

title: Unconventional-phase engineering of RuGa intermetallics for boosting alkaline hydrogen-electrode reactions
abstract: Promotion of alkaline hydrogen electrode reaction kinetics based on the phase transition via introducing gallium.

title: Bio-based adaptable dynamically cross-linked networks and their composites: multiple stimulus responses and potential electromagnetic shielding applications
abstract: Adaptable dynamically cross-linked networks were fabricated from tung oil and showed multiple stimulus responses and efficient recyclability.

title: Catalyzed eutectic LiBH 4 –KBH 4 system nanoconfined at low temperature for superior hydrogen storage reversibility
abstract: A Li/K eutectic borohydride system with NiCp 2 as a catalytic additive is proposed where the nanoconfinement of the borohydride and the introduction of NiCp 2 are achieved synchronously.

title: Surface reconstruction and defect healing of MAPbI 3 perovskite microcrystalline thick films for sensitive and self-powered X-ray detectors
abstract: Through surface reconstruction and defect healing, the self-powered perovskite microcrystalline X-ray detectors exhibit high sensitivity and low detection limit, enabling clear imaging at the ultra-low dose rate.

title: Humidity stability of halide solid-state electrolytes
abstract: This review focuses on the humidity stability of halide solid-state electrolytes, including the root causes, characterization methods and mitigation strategies for humidity instability, as well as key challenges and future research directions.

title: Construction of a photothermal self-healing epoxy coating with anti-fouling and anti-corrosion functions inspired by the hierarchical surface morphology of starfish
abstract: Marine corrosion and biofouling pose significant challenges to the durability of marine equipment.

title: ZnMn 2 O 4 applications in batteries and supercapacitors: a comprehensive review
abstract: This review critically examines the relationship between ZMO synthesis techniques, the resulting structural and morphological features, and the subsequent impact on electrochemical performance in various energy storage systems (supercapacitors and batteries).

title: Lithium ion mediated interfacial transfer and solvation structure enable stable zinc-alloying interface for aqueous zinc battery
abstract: A bi-functional strategy via lithium ion mediated interfacial transfer and solvation structure is proposed to greatly enhance the ability of the Zn-alloying interface for long-lasting Zn protection in aqueous battery.

title: Harvesting the vibration energy of Ba 0.95 Ca 0.05 Ti 0.9 Sn 0.1 O 3 /g-C 3 N 4 Z-scheme heterojunctions for nitrogen fixation
abstract: The BCST/10 wt% g-C 3 N 4 Z-type heterojunction catalyst can effectively convert nitrogen dissolved in solution into nitric acid by harvesting vibration energy.

title: Modulation of energy barriers and reaction pathways via dual-ligand engineering for enabling efficient NO removal and hydrogen production
abstract: The rational modulation of organic ligands in MOFs enables precise control over both the light absorption capability and charge carrier transfer pathways, thereby significantly optimizing the photocatalytic activity.

title: Densification-driven power factor enhancement in 2D MOF composites for waste heat recovery across broad temperature ranges
abstract: Compressed MOF-CNT composite films exhibited high power factors of up to 140 μW m −1 K −2 and enhanced ZT values, and they also showed exceptional flexibility and operational stability from cryogenic temperature (98 K) to 423 K.

title: Oxygen-driven ReS 2 –ReO 3 hybrid interfaces facilitate dual charge transfer pathways for noble-metal-free SERS
abstract: The SERS signal between the noble-metal-free surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy (SERS) substrate Oxygen-driven ReS 2 –ReO 3 and the probe molecules originates from charge transfer coupling.

title: Rehydrated laminate-assembled hydrogel: a universal platform for scalable and tailorable multifunctional sensor systems
abstract: Solving hydrogel sensors’ customization-mass production conflict, this study employs prefab dry films for extended storage and modular assembly (<2 min) of multifunctional layers, enabling toxic-free complex signal detection.

title: Hydrazine-free precursor for solution-processed all-inorganic Se and Se 1− x Te x photovoltaics
abstract: We prepare and isolate propylammonium poly-Se(-Te) precursors from a thiol-amine solvent system to produce high quality Se and Se–Te alloy films with bandgaps tunable from 1.2 eV to 1.86 eV for applications in photovoltaics.

title: The competitive influence of the intramolecular electric field and hydrophilic active sites of D–A conjugated porous polymers on photocatalytic hydrogen evolution performance
abstract: Conjugated porous polymers (CPPs) with a donor–acceptor (D–A) molecular structure usually exhibit good photocatalytic hydrogen evolution (PHE).

title: Engineering the site-accessibility for robust, continuous flow-through hydrogenation of styrene
abstract: Macro-scale, shape-tunable carbon monolith catalyst: engineered for enhanced kinetics, selectivity, and sustained performance in multiphasic reactions.

title: Enhancing long-term cycle stability of sodium-ion cathode materials via interlayer hydrogen bonding design
abstract: A novel hydrogen-bonded MOF (NaFe-MDP) enhances sodium-ion battery performance through stabilized interlayer structures and optimized Na + diffusion, improving both thermodynamic stability and kinetic performance.

title: Iodine adsorption by thiophene-based covalent organic polymer: a study of structural influence on performance
abstract: Thiophene derived COP for an enhanced iodine adsorption from vapour and liquid phase.

title: Achieving excellent charge balance and transport in low-donor bulk heterojunctions for high-performance semitransparent organic photovoltaics
abstract: Incorporating Me-4PACz into low-donor-content blends enables self-assembled hole transport pathways and reduced trap states, simultaneously achieving AVT (37.53%) and PCE (10.70%) in semi-transparent organic photovoltaics with an LUE of 4.01%.

title: Correction: Atomically dispersed cobalt on graphitic carbon nitride as a robust catalyst for selective oxidation of ethylbenzene by peroxymonosulfate
abstract: Correction for ‘Atomically dispersed cobalt on graphitic carbon nitride as a robust catalyst for selective oxidation of ethylbenzene by peroxymonosulfate’ by Jiaquan Li et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2021, 9 , 3029–3035, https://doi.org/10.1039/D0TA11503G.

title: Heterogeneous electrocatalytic oxidation of ammonia to hydrazine with a polymer-supported ruthenium-based catalyst
abstract: The heterogeneous electrochemical conversion of ammonia to hydrazine with a faradaic efficiency of 7.9% was achieved by a polymer-supported, single-site ruthenium catalyst.

title: Stabilizing copper nanoparticles for electrochemical nitrate reduction via encapsulation inside carbon nanotubes
abstract: Encapsulated copper nanoparticles (Cu-in-CNT) exhibit higher stability and electrochemical nitrate reduction performance as CNT prevents Cu leaching compared to surface-attached particles (Cu-on-CNT).

title: The impact of low-intensity illumination on the reverse bias behavior of perovskite solar cells
abstract: In a realistic partial shading event, reverse bias might coincide with low-intensity illumination. Then, two mechanisms induce opposing shifts of the breakdown voltage.

title: Efficient hydrogen evolution by using two-dimensional EMOF(Ti)/MAX-Cu novel photocatalysts synthesized via the bi-phase emulsification method
abstract: Novel and efficient EMOF(Ti)/MAX-Cu photocatalysts have been prepared by in situ loading of EMOF(Ti) on MAX-Cu via biphasic emulsification to improve its overall photoresponse as well as the photogenerated electron migration rate.

title: Polymer electrolytes for potassium batteries: incorporating ionic liquids to enhance the room temperature ionic conductivity
abstract: Potassium-based solvent-free SPEs have been developed via a simple hot-pressing method, which achieves a high ionic conductivity of 1.6 × 10 −3 S cm −1 at 20 °C, allowing the full cell to operate at room temperature.

title: Controlled aminolysis of multilayer polyurethane composites for sustainable upcycling into waterborne polyurethane emulsions
abstract: The effective chemical recycling of polyurethane (PU) waste remains a critical challenge due to its crosslinked structure and frequent integration into multilayered composites.

title: Combined evaporation-solution methodology for high-efficiency perovskite solar cells with exceptional reproducibility
abstract: The integration of rapid evaporation and solution processing enables high-efficiency perovskite photovoltaic technology, paving the way for the scalable mass production of evaporation-based processes.

title: Planarity-engineered 1,2,3-triazole-based 1D covalent organic frameworks for enhanced visible-light photocatalytic C-3 thiocyanation of indoles
abstract: Two 1D D–A 1,2,3-triazole COFs enable enhanced visible-light photocatalysis for selective C–H thiocyanation of indoles.

title: The case against hole injection through SAMs in perovskite solar cells
abstract: This study challenges HOMO level engineering approaches to SAM design by demonstrating that this leads to reduced quasi-Fermi level splitting in the perovskite and thus lower open-circuit voltage and performance.

title: Performance metrics for tensorial learning: prediction of Li 4 Ti 5 O 12 nuclear magnetic resonance observables at experimental accuracy
abstract: Predicting observable quantities from first principles calculations is the next frontier within the field of machine learning (ML) for materials modelling.

title: Elucidating Ti dopant effects in hematite photoanodes via high-throughput combinatorial screening
abstract: High-throughput gradients in composition and temperature enable both rapid optimization and mechanistic insight, revealing how titanium influences hematite photoanode performance through bulk doping and surface modification.

title: The water resistance mechanism of the (CoO) 7.5 ·(CuO) 3 ·(TiO 2 ) 6.3 catalyst in propylene combustion
abstract: The design of water-resistant oxide catalysts is of significant importance in chemical processes.

title: Tuning of the ionic conductivity of Ba 7 Nb 4 MoO 20 by pressure: a neutron diffraction and atomistic modelling study
abstract: Pressure is used to tune the crystal structure and the ionic conductivity of the dual ion conductor Ba 7 Nb 4 MoO 20 . Increasing pressure results in a reduction in the fraction of MO 4 tetrahedra within the P–L layer and a decrease in oxide-ion transport.

title: An efficient ALD- and noble metal-free charge recombination architecture for monolithic all-perovskite tandem solar cells
abstract: A simplified ALD- and noble metal-free charge recombination layer is constructed, which enables the fabrication of monolithic all-perovskite tandem solar cells with an efficiency as high as 26.56%.

title: Visible light-initiated selective aerobic oxidation of alcohols over niobium oxide nanowires: an oxygen isotope labeling study
abstract: An 18 O isotope labelling study reveals that the oxygen atom of benzaldehyde originates from benzyl alcohol rather than O 2 during visible light-initiated selective oxidation over Nb 2 O 5 nanowires.

title: Mechanistic insights into capacity discrepancies of conversion-type transition-metal compounds in wide-temperature-range lithium-ion batteries
abstract: In situ magnetometry is utilized to reveal the mechanistic insights into capacity discrepancies of conversion-type transition-metal compounds in wide-temperature-range lithium-ion batteries.

title: High-thermopower ionic thermoelectric hydrogels for low-grade heat harvesting and intelligent fire protection
abstract: An ionic thermoelectric hydrogel (HIG) for heat harvesting and fire protection was prepared. NaCl modulation enabled p–n conversion and p–n junction exhibited a much higher thermopower. HIG showed good potential for applications in supercapacitors.

title: In situ laser-induced biomimetic 3D-arched graphene with grass-like structures for efficient seawater desalination
abstract: A 3D evaporator based on laser-induced graphene (LIG) with a bilayer biomimetic structure (arched and grass-like) was in situ fabricated via laser direct writing, exhibiting an efficient salt resistance and high evaporation performance.

title: Unlocking the potential of selenium solar cells for indoor and tandem photovoltaics through theoretical and photoelectric simulations
abstract: Multiscale AIMD/DFT/SCAPS-1D simulations reveal Se film crystallization dynamics and defect suppression, achieving record PCEs of 16.2% (AM1.5) and 33.12% (1000 lux) in Se solar cells. Tandem cells retain 23.5% efficiency with 60%-thinner CIGS.

title: MOF-derived Co single atoms anchored on Fe 3 C-decorated carbon nanosheets for stable zinc–air batteries
abstract: The introduction of carbon nanosheets enhances the dispersion of MOF-derived carbon, thereby improving oxygen accessibility. The synergistic effect between Co single atoms and Fe 3 C nanoparticles boosts the reaction kinetics of both the ORR and OER.

title: Superhydrophobic and oleophobic luminescent composite materials for oxygen sensing in fuel-laden atmospheres
abstract: A PtTFPP/PDMS composite modified by PFOCTS demonstrated superhydrophobic and oleophobic properties. It exhibited high oxygen sensitivity in a fuel-laden atmosphere.

title: High-efficiency all-polymer ternary blends enable exceptional thermal stability in organic photovoltaics
abstract: We describe an all-polymeric PBQx-TF/D18/PY-IT-based organic photovoltaic device that achieves balanced charge transport and suppressed charge recombination, exhibiting excellent thermal stability with a T 80 of 1530 hours at 120 °C.

title: Heterostructure design of Mn–Ni(OH) 2 @1T-MoS 2 for enhanced aqueous asymmetric electrochemical capacitors
abstract: A Mn–Ni(OH) 2 @1T-MoS 2 heterostructure with lattice-matched interfaces was fabricated, thereby boosting conductivity and delivering 244.2 mA h g −1 capacity with a good rate capability of 51.4% at 12 A g −1 .

title: Palladium-decorated unconventional graphene oxide for unprecedented hydrogen storage
abstract: The developed Pd-rHGO nanocomposite material exhibited great potential for hydrogen storage, achieving a hydrogen storage capacity of 6.62 wt% at nearly ambient conditions and a pressure of 50 bar.

title: A multi-interaction conductive double-network polyelectrolyte hydrogel with high stretchability, self-adhesion, and tunable transparency for bioelectronic sensing and information encryption
abstract: A conductive double-network polyelectrolyte hydrogel with multiple interactions was developed. The gel is stretchable, self-adhesive, and transparency adjustable. It can be applied to flexible bioelectronic sensing and information encryption.

title: Ultra-selective and stable ethylene detection via exsolution of catalytic Ni nanoparticles in chemiresistive gas sensors
abstract: We developed a Ni–ZnO sensor for sub-ppm ethylene detection with enhanced selectivity, humidity resistance, and thermal stability. These properties were achieved by exsolving catalytic Ni nanoparticles on the surface of ZnO support sensing layers.

title: Incorporation of sulfur vacancies in the ZnIn 2 S 4 photoanode for highly efficient photoelectrochemical water splitting and urea oxidation
abstract: We report a “one stone, two birds” strategy for highly efficient photoelectrochemical (PEC) performance by introducing sulfur vacancies (Sv) in a ZnIn 2 S 4 (ZIS) photoanode, which exhibits a superior photocurrent density of 2.18 mA cm −2 .

title: Enhanced oxygen electrode kinetics at low temperatures: an infiltrated Sr(Ti 0.3 Fe 0.55 Co 0.15 )O 3- δ –La 0.8 Sr 0.2 Ga 0.8 Mg 0.2 O 3- δ nanocomposite for solid oxide cells
abstract: Sr(Ti 0.3 Fe 0.55 Co 0.15 )O 3- δ infiltrated into La 0.8 Sr 0.2 Ga 0.8 Mg 0.2 O 3- δ forms a nanocomposite oxygen electrode with exceptionally low polarization resistance, ∼0.06 Ω cm 2 at 600 °C, offering promise for next-generation solid oxide cells.

title: Reinforcing piezo-photocatalytic properties and enhancing RhB degradation efficiency of Ce-doped Bi 4 Ti 3 O 12 : mechanistic insights
abstract: Experimental and theoretical analyses indicate that Ce doping induces lattice distortion in Bi 4 Ti 3 O 12 , thereby enhancing its piezo-photocatalytic performance.

title: Synergistic bimetallic PdAg nanoalloy for the electrocatalytic reduction of nitrate to ammonia in a neutral solution
