abstract: The PdAg alloy exhibited promising NO 3 RR activity under neutral conditions.

title: Rational design of interwoven SiO 2 @Gr/N-CNT heterostructures from Fe–C–Si alloys for achieving superior electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: This study prepared SiO 2 @graphite (Gr)/N-doped carbon nanotube (N-CNT) (SiO 2 @Gr/N-CNT) composite materials with an Fe–C–Si alloy and melamine as raw materials using simple acid leaching, mechanical ball milling, and in situ pyrolysis.

title: Lactulose modulation of the inner Helmholtz plane for stable and dendrite-free zinc anodes in aqueous Zn-ion batteries
abstract: Lactulose anchors to Zn via hydroxyl groups, expels water from the Helmholtz layer to suppress corrosion, and guides uniform Zn 2+ deposition, enabling stable, dendrite-free zinc anodes.

title: Composition tuning of structural high entropy alloy (Fe-Co-Ni-Cu-Mn/Zn) nano-electrocatalysts for synergistic water splitting
abstract: Structural high-entropy alloys (HEAs), composed of five or more earth-abundant, low-cost metals, offer a versatile platform for tailoring surface composition and active sites to develop efficient electrocatalysts.

title: Self-evolution induced Cu x O/Fe 3 O 4 heterogeneous interfaces enabling rapid nitrate reduction to ammonia
abstract: A reconstructed Cu x O/Fe 3 O 4 heterointerface boosts tandem nitrate-to-ammonia conversion: Cu x O catalyzes NO 2 − formation, Fe 3 O 4 hydrogenates intermediates to NH 3 , aided by interfacial charge redistribution and reduced energy barriers.

title: Morphological insights into Se-driven charge transport and enhanced performance in Sb 2 (S,Se) 3 solar cells
abstract: Selenium incorporation in Sb 2 (S,Se) 3 thin films suppresses grain coalescence and lowers grain boundary energy. This facilitates beneficial band bending and improved charge transport, clarifying the origin of enhanced photovoltaic performance.

title: Ultrahigh mass loading, binder-free synthetic approach for mesoporous graphitic carbon in 3D current collector for high energy lithium-ion batteries
abstract: We present a synthetic approach to converting block copolymers into mesoporous graphitic carbon within a 3D current collector. This method enables high mass loading per unit area without the use of binders, facilitating the development of high-energy lithium-ion batteries.

title: Trimetallic spinel NiCo 2− x Mo x O 4 oxygen evolution catalyst enabling bias-free solar water splitting with inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: Partial Mo 6+ leaching selectively activates Ni 2+ in NiCo 2− x Mo x O 4 , forming NiOOH, to enhance OER. When integrated into a water electrolyzer powered by a p-i-n perovskite solar cell, the system enables bias-free solar H 2 production at 8.8% efficiency.

title: Direct formation of MXene domains and compositional defects in magnetron sputtered V 2 AlC-AlO x heterostructures revealed by theory and experiments
abstract: Direct synthesis of V 2 CT x and V 3 C 2 T x MXene domains in magnetron-sputtered V 2 AlC-AlO x heterostructures, alongside novel compositional defect formation, revealed by STEM and DFT calculations, enabling chemical etching-free MXene production.

title: MXene-enhanced PEGDA crosslinked quasi-solid electrolytes: a flame-retardant 3D network for high-performance sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Fire-resistant quasi-solid electrolytes for fast migration of Na + were prepared by UV-initiated in situ polymerization. MXene constructs a Na + fast migration channel and a synergistic flame retardant network of Ti–F elements.

title: In situ borate generation by amorphous Ni–Fe–B nanosheets: a highly active electrocatalyst for oxygen generation in alkaline seawater
abstract: We report in situ fabricated amorphous NiFeB/NF as a highly active and durable OER catalyst in alkaline seawater, delivering the current density of 1000 mA cm −2 at 350 mV, enabled by its structure, oxyhydroxide formation, and Cl − repulsion.

title: Dual substitution in cationic and anionic sublattices of lithium indium chloride for high-performance solid-state lithium metal batteries
abstract: Zr-doping in Li 3 InCl 6 enhances ionic conductivity by 2.4% via the creation of lithium vacancies. Zr-F co-doped Li 3 InCl 6 electrolyte improves electrochemical stability through the formation of a LiF protective layer.

title: Controlling the lattice oxygen content regulates cycling stability and rate capability of Co-free ultrahigh-Ni cathodes
abstract: Here, intrinsic Oo can improve Li + utilization, accelerate Li + diffusion and benefit rate performance of LiNi 0.95 Mn 0.05 O 2 . Intrinsic Ov can enhance electron delocalization, reduce particle strain and improve cycling stability.

title: Study of structural and composition redesign to enhance the thermostability and electrochemical performance of a Co-less Ni-rich LiNi 0.92 Co 0.04 Mn 0.04 O 2 layered cathode through transition-metal concentration gradient strategies
abstract: Among the commercialized cathodes, Co-less Ni-rich layered cathode materials (Ni > 90%) have been reported as promising materials to achieve high energy density and economically benign cathodes for Li-ion batteries (LIBs).

title: Accelerating discovery of glass materials in electronic devices through topology-guided machine learning
abstract: A multi-tasking framework with topology-informed descriptors and a confidence indicator was developed to efficiently design reliable glass compositions with tailored TEC values, enabling the cost-effective discovery of novel glasses for electronic materials.

title: Resonant defect states of the SnO 2 :Ta transparent conductive oxide revealed by excitation wavelength-dependent Raman spectroscopy and hybrid functional DFT calculations
abstract: Two delocalized V Sn - and one localized O i -type defect state(s) were identified at the upper edge of the valence band of the SnO 2 :Ta transparent conductive oxide and characterized.

title: Ytterbium-incorporated porphyrin nanostructure-modified carbon electrodes for ultra-sensitive detection of lead ions in water environments
abstract: The Yb-TCPP nanocluster-modified electrode promotes the redox process of Pb( ii ) through both efficient capture and accelerated desorption, achieving sensitive detection of Pb( ii ).

title: Machine learning-assisted computational screening of high-entropy alloy catalysts for HCOOH decomposition
abstract: Machine-learning combined with DFT calculations and microkinetic modeling accelerates high-entropy alloy catalyst discovery for HCOOH decomposition.

title: High-performance underwater weak-light p hotoelectrochemical photodetection based on ZnIn-LDH nanosheet arrays
abstract: The excellent weak ultraviolet light detection capability of PEC UV PDs based on ZnIn-LDH NSAs was demonstrated for the first time, including a high responsivity of 182.41 mA W −1 under weak 254 nm light and an ultrahigh rejection ratio of 7027.03.

title: Lead-free La 2 Ti 2 O 7 dielectric ceramics with ultra-high energy storage density and electric field durability through layered ferroelectric layers
abstract: Researchers are currently studying the energy storage properties of La 2 Ti 2 O 7 , which has a simple composition and a layered structure, and found that its energy storage strength is comparable to that of complex chemical compositions.

title: Ba(Zr, Ce)O 3 -based proton-conducting solid oxide electrolysis cells for efficient hydrogen production
abstract: This review overviews recent progress on material innovations and interfacial engineering for Ba(Zr, Ce)O 3 -based proton-conducting solid oxide electrolysis cells (H-SOECs), with a specific focus on highly stable BaZrO 3 -based systems.

title: Solar cells sensitized with doubly concerted companion dyes with optimized donors to achieve high efficiencies up to 12.5%: a record efficiency for iodine electrolyte-based DSSCs
abstract: An alkyl chain protected fluorenyl indoline donor was employed to improve the photovoltaic performance of doubly concerted companion dyes.

title: A Zr/Sm co-doped Fe 2 O 3 :NiCo-MOF heterojunction photoanode for augmented photoelectrochemical water splitting
abstract: We have designed a NiCo-MOF decorated Zr/Sm co-doped Fe 2 O 3 photoanode with a p–n heterojunction. Zr/Sm co-doping enhanced bulk conductivity, while NiCo-MOF passivated surface-trapping states and served as a cocatalyst.

title: A straightforward, efficient, and environmentally friendly lithium storage tactic: solventless fabrication of N/S co-doped SiO x /C with carbon encapsulation
abstract: A large body of research shows that reducing the particle size and applying carbon coatings can enhance the electrochemical performance of SiO x -based anodes considerably.

title: FeSb/FeS heterostructures anchored on exfoliated graphite nanosheets as an advanced anode for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: A multi-phase FeSb/FeS nanocomposite anchored on exfoliated graphite (FeSb/FeS-G) was synthesized via calcination and ball milling, showing high capacity (233.57 m Ah g −1 after 900 cycles at 6 A g −1 ) and stability, offering a promising SIB anode material.

title: Enhancement of catalytic performance by controlling the type of junctions in metal/oxide catalysts for the urea oxidation reaction
abstract: This study explores how junction type (Ohmic vs. Schottky) and oxide resistivity impact metal/oxide catalyst performance in urea oxidation, emphasizing the superior activity and stability of Ohmic junctions.

title: Incorporating high acidity cations in Co-free BiFeO 3 -based air electrodes for enhancing their electrocatalytic activity and durability in reversible solid oxide cells
abstract: The incorporation of the high-acidity cation Ti 4+ into Co-free BiFeO 3− δ -based air electrodes demonstrates superior catalytic activity and significantly enhanced durability in reversible solid oxide cells.

title: A sustainable two-step electrochemical conversion of N 2 to ammonia using rhodium nanoparticles on carbon nanosheets
abstract: One-step synthesized Rh/C catalysts drive sustainable N 2 oxidation to NO 3 − and subsequent reduction to NH 3 , achieving 16% efficiency under ambient conditions and offering a green pathway to ammonia beyond the Haber–Bosch process.

title: Functionalities of the LiV 3− x Nb x O 8 surface layer on a Li 2 NiO 2 cathode additive for enhancing the moisture stability and cycling performance of lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The highly conductive LiV 3− x Nb x O 8 surface layer effectively minimizes moisture-induced side reactions on the Li 2 NiO 2 cathode additive, reducing interfacial resistance and enhancing its durability.

title: Breaking the CO bridge trap: boron–transition-metal trimer clusters for efficient C–C coupling in CO 2 reduction
abstract: Incorporating a p-block B atom into B-2TM@NC disrupts bridging-CO adsorption, facilitating C–C coupling beyond conventional bi-metallic designs.

title: Halide vacancy passivation in cesium lead halide perovskite nanocrystals with mixed halide compositions: the impact of prolonged reaction time
abstract: Prolonged reaction in perovskite nanocrystals enables the effective passivation of halide vacancies, resulting in enhanced photoluminescence quantum yield and significantly suppressed phase segregation.

title: Carbon nanostructures embedded with bimetallic CoRu alloy nanoparticles as oxygen reduction electrode for zinc–air batteries
abstract: Defective graphitic layer coated CoRu particles exhibit high OER and ORR catalytic activity and open the door for high end applications.

title: Eco-friendly, flexible and stretchable printed electronics based on a sustainable elastic substrate and ink
abstract: Stretchable and flexible printed electronics (sensors & conductors) based on green materials.

title: Organic shell engineering of CsPbBr 3 perovskite quantum dots for efficient textile-based photodetectors
abstract: We investigate the effect of CsPbBr 3 QDs shell engineering with aromatic short-chain ligands, namely phenylethylamine (PEA) and trans -cinnamic acid (TCA) on the performance of textile-based photodetector.

title: Crystal structure modification enhances air stability and suppresses O 2 evolution in Li 5 FeO 4 : insights from experiments and DFT calculations
abstract: Cobalt-doping strategy enhances the air stability and prelithiation capacity of Li 5 FeO 4 via crystal structure modification. First-principles calculations reveal the mechanism of Co-doping effects on performance.

title: Colloidal-nanoparticle-derived nickel/ferric oxide heterointerfaces for promoting the alkaline oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Colloidal Ni/Fe 2 O 3 nanoparticles present superior alkaline OER performances based on both the intra- and inter-particle heterointerfaces, achieving low overpotential (199 mV @ 10 mA cm −2 ) and high current density of AEMWE (5.13 A cm −2 @ 2 V cell ).

title: Unlocking sunlight driven photocatalysis: synthesis, diversity, and applications of COF-based S-scheme heterojunctions
abstract: COFs with high stability and tunability face issues of self-stacking and restricted conductivity, whereas their S-scheme heterojunctions address these limitations. This review explores their synthesis, mechanism, applications, etc.

title: Breaking electrode symmetry: lithium redistribution-induced degradation polarity in high-energy Li-ion batteries
abstract: Cathode versus anode degradation reveals entirely divergent battery aging trajectories, highlighting electrode specific failure mechanisms and design priorities for future cells.

title: Defect engineering in β-ketoenamine-linked covalent organic frameworks for high-efficiency uranium extraction
abstract: Three β-ketoenamine linked COFs featuring with different anthraquinone conjugated units are synthesized for uranium extraction. Moreover, defect engineering is creatively introduced into COFs to improve the uranium adsorption performance.

title: Kinetics of CO 2 adsorption on UTSA-16(Zn) metal–organic framework: thermal, compositional, and geometrical effects
abstract: A kinetic study on the adsorption of CO 2 on MOF UTSA-16(Zn) in the presence of different binders. A mixed order model was able to describe the adsorption data, suggesting a combined controlling nature of surface adsorption and diffusion.

title: A two-step strategy improves the wide-temperature-range thermoelectric performance of Mg 3+ x Bi 1.29 Sb 0.7 Te 0.01
abstract: By adjusting the excess Mg content and refining the high-temperature sintering process, we optimized carrier concentration and mobility, in Mg 3 Bi 2 -based thermoelectric materials. Enhancing the power factor. The ZT value at 323 K increased from 0.05 to nearly 0.5, with a peak ZT of around 1 at 57…

title: Diamondoid-templated lithium single-cation conduction in solid-state organic electrolytes
abstract: Diamondoids can template lithium single-cation transport domains in solid organic electrolytes.

title: Constructing a NiCoP/FeP p–n heterojunction with built-in electric field regulation as an efficient alkaline hydrogen evolution electrode
abstract: The development of high-performance alkaline hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) catalysts with robust stability and cost-efficiency is critical for advancing sustainable hydrogen production.

title: Upcycling of degraded NCM cathode materials for prolonged high-rate stability: simultaneous dual modification from surface to bulk
abstract: The simultaneous dual modification from surface to bulk upcycles the spent NCM and prolongs the cycling stability at 4.6 V and 5 C, which is further confirmed theoretically and experimentally and extended to various systems.

title: Synergistic effects of polymer integration on the properties, stability, and applications of MXenes
abstract: This review explores MXene–polymer hybrids to enhance MXene oxidation stability and polymer functionality. It covers fabrication techniques, uses in various fields, and highlights challenges and future opportunities for MXene–polymer nanocomposites.

title: Introduction to ‘Nanomaterials for a sustainable future: from materials to devices and systems’
abstract: Guest editors Guohua Jia, Hongxia Wang, Xuyong Yang, Lina Quan and Yun Liu introduce this cross-journal themed collection on nanomaterials for a sustainable future: from materials to devices and systems.

title: A porous CeO 2 -Cu x O assembly composed of small-sized particles with an optimized electronic structure for effective catalysis
abstract: 2-Dimensional porous Cu-based assembly composed of small-sized particles were designed by taking in situ formed CeO 2 as “spacers”, which show good activity and reusability for the catalytic reduction of aromatic nitro compounds.

title: Cost-effective thermoelectric conversion from low-grade heat using a bimetallic sulfur-based thermally regenerative ammonia battery
abstract: A bimetallic sulfur-based thermally regenerative battery (B-STRB) is suggested as a solution to facilitate effective waste heat recovery with an ultrahigh power density.

title: One-step dual process strategy for holey graphite towards scalable and stable lithium-ion battery anodes
abstract: Synthesis of expanded holey graphite by a one-step process towards scalable and stable lithium-ion battery anodes.

title: VN x O y @C nanowires: a high-performance cathode material for aqueous zinc-ion batteries with dual-redox reaction mechanisms
abstract: The VN x O y @C hybrid nanowires with abundant oxygen-defects and carbon coating can effectively facilitate internal electron and ion transport, thus greatly augmenting the electrochemical reaction kinetics during Zn 2+ storage.

title: Electronic structure of CsPbBr 3 with isovalent doping and divacancies: the smallest metal Pb cluster
abstract: This study proves the existence of bromine vacancies cluster in pairs and the ease formation of Cl and I substituted dopants in CsPbBr 3 , which provides critical insight into complex defects in all-inorganic metal halide perovskites.

title: Determining the shunt resistance and photogenerated current of tandem organic solar cells by simulating their practical photovoltaic parameters
abstract: The real performance of tandem organic solar cells has been simulated based on a proposed equivalent circuit. The contradiction between high shunt resistance and large photogenerated current is limiting the efficiency of tandem devices.

title: Dual-functional engineering of Fe nanoparticles and oxygen vacancies in Sr 1.9 Fe 1.5 Mo 0.5 O 6− δ perovskites for high-efficiency CO 2 /H 2 O co-electrolysis
abstract: A-site deficiency facilitates in situ exsolution of Fe nanoparticles and oxygen vacancy generation in Sr 1.9 Fe 1.5 Mo 0.5 O 6− δ perovskites, achieving co-electrolysis current density of −1.58 A cm −2 @ 1.5 V with extended durability about 80 h.

title: Boosting redox electrochemistry of aromatic imide polymers by coupling with a sulfur-heterocyclic ring for advanced all-organic aqueous magnesium-ion batteries
abstract: A thiophene-based sulfur-heterocyclic ring coupled with aromatic polyimide (PTTDA) featuring multiple redox-active sites and high structural stability was innovatively proposed and constructed to realize ultrastable and high-capacity Mg 2+ storage.

title: Efficient photo-rechargeable aqueous zinc-ion batteries using lead-free perovskite photocathodes
abstract: The photo-rechargeable aqueous zinc-ion battery using perovskite photocathode demonstrates the capability of charging solely under sunlight without the need of an external bias achieving a good photoconversion efficiency of 2.1%.

title: Mechanically robust bifunctional hydrophobic polycarbazole-decorated multiwall carbon nanotube- Pithecellobium dulce oil polyester amide nanocomposite coatings: fabrication, characterization, anticorrosive and antimicrobial studies
abstract: This study details the formulation of nano-polycarbazole (PCz) and multiwall carbon nanotube (MWCNT) hybrid (PCz@CNT) nanofillers in Pithecellobium dulce polyester-amide coatings, showcasing their superior anticorrosive and antimicrobial properties on carbon steel.

title: Carbon dimensionality engineering in manganese oxide composite electrodes for high-efficiency electrochemical deionization
abstract: Carbon dimensionality engineering enables Na-doped MnO x @carbon composites with high salt removal capacity, stability, and efficiency in electrochemical deionization, offering design guidelines for future ion-selective desalination electrodes.

title: Interlayer pillaring influences the octahedral tilting and electrochemical capacity of tungsten oxides
abstract: Pillaring tungsten oxides with alkylammonium cations increases the interlayer spacing but decreases the band gap, leading to low electrochemical capacity.

title: Hydrogen bond-based molecular self-clustering of a hyper-dendritic molecule for boosting ultra-long reversible zinc anodes
abstract: HP-16 could form self-clusters for enhanced adsorption on the zinc anode surface to realize zinc metal batteries with increased stability.

title: Microkinetics-guided catalyst design for low-temperature CO catalytic oxidation
abstract: Crystal phase regulation was proposed to enhance the low-temperature activity of SrCoO 3 perovskite towards CO catalytic oxidation based on the microkinetics.

title: Triple hierarchical precipitation and serrated grain boundaries strengthened co-free medium-entropy alloys
abstract: The novel multi-scale spherical and lamella triple hierarchical precipitation (THP) and serrated grain boundaries (SGB) enhance the strength-ductility synergy of medium-entropy alloys.

title: Understanding charge recombination and light-induced degradation: an in-depth study of tert -butyl modified carbazole-based self-assembled monolayers for enhanced performance in organic solar cells
abstract: The design and visualization of carbazole-based SAMs for OSCs were investigated. CBZC2 demonstrated superior UV stability, reduced interface resistance, and longer carrier lifetime, emerging as a promising alternative to PEDOT:PSS.

title: Stoichiometric anion exchange by a low-dielectric-constant solvent for highly-doped conjugated polymers with enhanced environmental stability
abstract: Optimized solvent conditions during ion-exchange doping improved environmental stability in highly-doped conjugated polymer films by facilitating effective exchange between ionized redox agents and stable counter-ions.

title: In situ Janus polymerization of cyclic phosphate and ethers affording fluorine/phosphorus copolymer electrolyte for high-safety lithium metal batteries with long-cycle life
abstract: An F/P-containing gel polymer electrolyte was synthesized through in situ Janus polymerization mechanism, demonstrating ultra-long cycle stability when applied in lithium metal batteries.

title: Diffusons and strong anharmonicity in BaCaX (X = Si, Ge) thermoelectric materials
abstract: Calculations employing a two-channel model indicate that BaCaX (X = Si, Ge) exhibits ultra-low lattice thermal conductivity.

title: Hybrid ternary co-intercalation in the interlayer of a vanadium oxide cathode enables high-capacity and stable zinc ion batteries
abstract: A hybrid ternary co-intercalation vanadium oxide cathode with an expanded interlayer space has been prepared and utilized in zinc-ion batteries, demonstrating a near-theoretical capacity and high cycle stability due to its outstanding kinetics.

title: Role of the electron transport layer in dictating the nanoscale heterogeneity in all-inorganic perovskite absorbers – correlating the optoelectronic and crystallographic properties
abstract: The impact of underlying electron transport layer (SnO 2 , TiO 2 and ZnO) on the optoelectronic and structural properties of the CsPbI 2 Br perovskite overlayer is explored using Cathodoluminescence (CL) and Electron Back Scatter Diffraction (EBSD).

title: Photothermal CuS@SiO 2 nanocomposites for solar-driven anti-icing/deicing and synchronous evaporation and photocatalysis
abstract: Development of CuS@SiO 2 nanocomposites into superhydrophobic photothermal (SHP) CuS@SiO 2 coating for anti-icing/deicing, and Janus CuS@SiO 2 -MF evaporator for synchronous photocatalytic dye degradation and water evaporation.

title: Tailoring iron phosphate precursors via microcrystallization for high-performance lithium iron phosphate cathodes in lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Microcrystallizing an iron phosphate precursor under controlled pH and temperature effectively improves the crystal structure of the LiFePO 4 cathode, thereby contributing to enhanced electrochemical performance for advanced Li-ion batteries.

title: Construction of waffle-like NS-ZIF@V 2 CT x heterostructures for high-performance potassium-ion batteries
abstract: The alternately stacked waffle-like NS-ZIF@V 2 CT x heterostructures with plentiful exposed active sites, enhanced electrical conductivity and superior structural stability have been fabricated and utilized as anodes in potassium-ion batteries.

title: Strategic intercalation of AB 2 O 4 perovskite oxides for synergetic enhanced redox activity in sulphonated Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene for energy storage applications
abstract: Flexible supercapacitors have emerged as efficient and fast energy storage devices for new generation electronics.

title: An ultra-stretchable, highly resilient, and degradable dual physically crosslinked hydrogel for multifunctional sensing and self-powered human–machine interaction
abstract: An ultra-stretchable, resilient, and degradable hydrogel with a dual crosslinked network for multifunctional sensing and self-powered human–machine interaction.

title: Spatial and chemical heterogeneity in aqueous Zn/MnO 2 batteries: role of Zn and Mn containing complexes
abstract: Schematic of electrode evolution at the mesoscale: a porous structure with Zn–Mn shell-like domains forms around undissolved MnO 2 after ∼400 hours of cycling, hindering ion transport and causing capacity fade in aqueous Zn/MnO 2 batteries.

title: Combining low viscosity and high volumetric redox density of organic polymers for energy-efficient catholytes in redox flow batteries: a redox-active polyelectrolyte approach
abstract: A new redox-active polyelectrolyte achieved high-capacity and energy-efficient charge–discharge enabled by low viscosity.

title: Ultrafast formation of porosity and heterogeneous structures on 2D oxides via momentary photothermal effect
abstract: Here, for the first time, we describe an ultra-fast, reliable, scalable, and cost-effective synthetic method for achieving highly porous as well as catalyst loaded 2D heterostructures for realizing ultra-stable and selective surface reactivity.

title: Interface-driven kinetic energy barrier modulation in Fe–Mo derivative-supported surface and heterointerface engineered MXene hybrids for alkaline hydrogen evolution
abstract: The present work deals with the design of an MXene-based model electrocatalyst via surface and interface engineering for lowering the E a and accelerating the RDS of the multi-step HER in alkaline media.

title: Enhanced charge separation and prolonged carrier lifetime in mixed Sn–Ge halide perovskite enabled by spontaneous symmetry breaking and moderate disorder
abstract: The orderly alignment of Ge/Sn atoms along the (110) direction not only induces robust ferroelectric polarization but also significantly diminishes structural thermal fluctuations.

title: Band bending reversal and enhanced electron mobility at the film surface achieved through a selective polishing strategy in tin–lead perovskite solar cells
abstract: Pyrophosphoric acid (PP) in isopropanol selectively dissolve the Sn component but not the Pb component at tin–lead (Sn–Pb) perovskite surface, which enables a low-defect film and a highest efficiency of 23.85% for Sn–Pb perovskite solar cells.

title: Polyanionic surfactant modulates the substrate surface energy to achieve crystallization control for efficient perovskite solar cells
abstract: A polyanionic surfactant strategy was developed to modulate the heterogeneous crystallization kinetics. The controlled crystallization process enables the fabrication of high-quality perovskite films and optimal interface properties.

title: Activating the cathode for solid oxide fuel cells by selective removal of surface strontium
abstract: A surface engineering strategy selectively removes Sr-rich layers, enhancing the activity and stability of air electrodes in solid oxide fuel cells.

title: Simultaneously optimizing the heterojunction band alignment and passivating the absorber grain boundaries for kesterite solar cells with 14.26% efficiency
abstract: The introduction of Na 2 S simultaneously optimizes the CZTSSe/CdS heterojunction and passivates kesterite grain boundaries, reaching 14.29% efficiency with V OC improved by 42 mV.

title: Enhancing the durability of aluminium-foil anodes in rechargeable lithium batteries via uniformly distributed alloy addition in the matrix phase
abstract: Uniformly distributed Si in the Al matrix as a solid solution induces a pinning effect, prevents cracking of the Al grains, suppresses pulverization and improves the cycling stability of Al-foil anodes in rechargeable lithium batteries.

title: Radiation-cooled aramid composite films featuring tunable TiO 2 nanorod arrays anchored on the surface of 2D mica nanosheets for passive daytime radiative cooling applications
abstract: Radiation-cooled aramid/scatterer composite films featuring a well-organized 2D layered stack structure exhibit high solar reflectance, infrared emissivity and excellent environmental stability.

title: Expression of concern: Reduced graphene oxide nanosheets decorated with Au, Pd and Au–Pd bimetallic nanoparticles as highly efficient catalysts for electrochemical hydrogen generation
abstract: Expression of concern for ‘Reduced graphene oxide nanosheets decorated with Au, Pd and Au–Pd bimetallic nanoparticles as highly efficient catalysts for electrochemical hydrogen generation’ by Gitashree Darabdhara et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2015, 3 , 20254–20266, https://doi.org/10.1039/C5TA05730B.

title: Correction: Enhanced utilization of light through polystyrene microspheres for boosting photoelectrochemical hydrogen production in MoS 2 /Si heterostructures
abstract: Correction for ‘Enhanced utilization of light through polystyrene microspheres for boosting photoelectrochemical hydrogen production in MoS 2 /Si heterostructures’ by Jing Yu et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA08336A.

title: Correction: Unveiling olivine cathodes for high energy-density lithium-ion batteries: a comprehensive review from the atomic level to the electrode scale
abstract: Correction for ‘Unveiling olivine cathodes for high energy-density lithium-ion batteries: a comprehensive review from the atomic level to the electrode scale’ by Wonchan Hwang et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2024, 12 , 27800–27824, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA02338B.

title: Correction: MOF-derived vertically stacked Mn 2 O 3 @C flakes for fiber-shaped zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Correction for ‘MOF-derived vertically stacked Mn 2 O 3 @C flakes for fiber-shaped zinc-ion batteries’ by Chenglong Liu et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2020, 8 , 24031–24039, https://doi.org/10.1039/D0TA09212F.

title: Electro-epoxidation of ethylene and propylene using atomic active oxygen derived from water electrolysis on IrN 4 site in graphene at a lower applied potential and over a wide potential range
abstract: Electro-epoxidation of ethylene/propylene using *O intermediates generated through water electrolysis under low applied potentials and over a wide potential range.

title: Unveiling proton pathways between the anode and cathode in PEM electrolyzer cells via direct reaction visualization
abstract: Proton exchange membrane electrolyzer cells (PEMECs) represent a promising technology for producing green hydrogen to support global carbon neutrality targets.

title: A Rigid-flexible binder for sulfurized polyacrylonitrile cathodes for rechargeable lithium batteries
abstract: Rigid and flexible binders achieve long cycle stable structure and performance for sulfurized polyacrylonitrile cathodes.

title: A kinetic barrier modulated hollow nanobox MoSe 2 @CuS core–shell heterostructure for high-rate and durable Li–O 2 batteries
abstract: Hollow MoSe 2 @CuS core–shell heterostructures enhance Li–O 2 battery efficiency through synergistic electronic and structural engineering, with DFT insights revealing reduced kinetic barriers for reversible Li 2 O 2 reactions.

title: Dual-band modulating electrochromic smart windows with broad temperature adaptability based on triazine-ring-based molecules and hyperbranched polymers
abstract: Three redox centers enable multi-level spectral control. Hydrogen bond networks enable excellent cycling stability at extreme temperatures.

title: Hydrogel thermocells with enhanced thermopower induced by thermosensitivity
abstract: Temperature-induced phase transition in the poly(NIPAAm- co -Am) TEC regulates ion migration and Seebeck coefficient. It has potential application in powering wearable electronics and monitoring temperature.

title: N, S co-doped porous carbon for ultra-selective and cost-effective capture of palladium from highly acidic solution
abstract: This work develops N/S-doped porous carbon for ultra-selective Pd( ii ) capture in 1 M HNO 3 , achieving 103.5 mg g −1 capacity with >99% efficiency via S/O/N coordination, outperforming MOFs/POPs in cost/acid stability.

title: Unraveling the role of the Mn–[O x ]–Ce structure in MnO x /CeO 2 catalysts for the catalytic oxidation of VOCs: resistance to sintering, increasing oxygen vacancies and activation of lattice oxygen
abstract: Possible mechanism of EA and toluene oxidation on the multiple oxygen vacancies on MnO x /CeO 2 catalysts with sintering resistance.

title: Design and characterization of an adaptive polymer electrolyte for lithium metal solid-state battery applications
abstract: An adaptive polymer electrolyte is demonstrated through the incorporation of conducting polymer particles, which cause the film to expand under an electric field with potential for application as an interlayer in lithium metal solid-state batteries.

title: Self-supporting Fe 3 C@C composites derived from MOF/chitosan films for electromagnetic interference shielding and Joule heating
abstract: An Fe 3 C@C composite film was prepared by high-temperature synthesis using MOF/chitosan precursors, demonstrating an electromagnetic shielding efficiency of 67.5 dB and excellent Joule heating performance.

title: Multivalent Cu catalytic sites on TiO 2 for efficient photocatalytic hydrogen evolution and mechanistic insights from solid-state operando photochemical analysis
abstract: Multivalent Cu sites on reduced TiO 2 enable high solar H 2 evolution, visible-light activity ( λ > 380 nm), and long-term stability, with operando UV-DRS revealing dynamic Cu redox.

title: Photo-swing CO 2 capture using a branched polyethylenimine as sorbents and TiN light absorber
abstract: This study shows a photo-swing based CO 2 capture method using aminopolymer-based contactors enhanced by cost-effective plasmonic TiN and low-power LEDs.

title: Improvement of photocatalytic antibacterial action of Mn, S v -co-doped ZnIn 2 S 4 prepared by a novel O v -rich α-MnO 2 decomposition approach
abstract: This study introduces a novel solid-state decomposition method to incorporate Mn from α-MnO 2 into ZnIn 2 S 4 (ZIS), enhancing its photocatalytic antibacterial properties.

title: Achieving superior stability and cycle life in zinc anodes with aramid surface modification
abstract: Aramid interfacial layer improves the durability of aqueous zinc-ion batteries by enabling uniform Zn deposition and suppressing dendrite growth and side reactions.

title: Synergistic nitrogen-doping and carbon-coating in N-MoSe 2 /C nanoflowers enable ultra-high discharge capacity for Li–CO 2 batteries
abstract: N-MoSe 2 /C nanoflowers are developed for cathode catalyst of Li–CO 2 battery which achieves an exceptionally high initial discharge capacity of 37 720 mAh g −1 and maintains a stable discharge plateau at 2.76 V with a low overpotential of 1.56 V.

title: Asymmetric substrate supported Ni catalysts for robust photothermal catalytic dry reforming of methane
abstract: Asymmetric SiO 2 substrate supported Ni catalysts have been developed to enable high-performance and stable photothermal catalytic dry reforming of methane with CO 2 .

title: Recent advances in material regulation and structure design for high-performance aqueous anode-free zinc batteries
abstract: This review explores strategies to enhance the performance of aqueous anode-free zinc batteries through electrolyte optimization, additives, and current collector design, aiming to unlock their full potential.

title: Diameter dependent performance of silicon nanowire anodes grown on 3D current collectors for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Si nanowires (Si NWs) with diameters tuned from ∼35 to 100 nm were directly grown on large-area (30 cm 2 ) stainless-steel mesh (SSM) substrates via a facile vapour–liquid–solid approach.

title: Synergistic band modulation and phonon suppression to improve PbBi 2 S 4 thermoelectric performance
abstract: Dual optimization in weighted mobility and lattice thermal conductivity by Sb doping and Se alloying, thereby leading to a high ZT value of 0.78 in ternary PbBi 2 S 4 .

title: High-entropy oxide synthesis in concentrated alkaline solutions for plasma-catalytic formaldehyde oxidation
abstract: High-concentration NaOH co-precipitation makes high-entropy oxides with high surface area of 173 m 2 g −1 . Tests show that HEO-10%-400 boosts formaldehyde removal by 59.7%, reduces O 3 by 93.1%, and NO x by 91.8% over plasma alone.

title: A dual-stabilization strategy for tubular zinc-iodine flow batteries
abstract: This work proposes a dual-stabilization strategy for Zn plating and stripping in tubular Zn–I 2 flow batteries using a tri-helical Zn anode for geometric modulation and an NH 4 Br additive for electrolyte engineering.

title: Recent advances in Cu-based intermetallics: structures, syntheses, and electrocatalytic applications
abstract: This review focuses on the latest developments in Cu-based intermetallics, covering their structures, synthesis methods, and electrocatalytic applications.

title: Improved anionic redox reversibility of layered oxides by modulating transition metal–oxygen bonds for sodium ion batteries
abstract: Mg doping modification of Na 0.72 Li 0.24 Mn 0.76 O 2 material regulates TM–O bonds, effectively controlling the local chemical environment of oxygen, enhancing the stability of lattice oxygen and the reversibility of oxygen redox reactions.

title: Photogenerated electron–hole separation-driven CO 2 reduction coupled with tetracycline oxidation over a LaMnO 3 nanoparticles/oxygen-etched g-C 3 N 4 heterojunction: complementary effects brought by proton transfer
abstract: The complementary effects resulting from the combination of LaMnO 3 /oxygen-etched g-C 3 N 4 due to proton transfer enhance the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 coupled with the oxidation of tetracycline.

title: Composition regulation of ternary rare-earth halide solid-state electrolytes and its influence on their ionic conducting and electrochemical properties
abstract: The relationship of how the composition of the ternary rare-earth halide SSEs influences their crystal structure, ionic conducting and electrochemical properties has been fully investigated.

title: Investigation into the stability of monometallic metal oxide electrocatalysts for hydrogen peroxide synthesis through the oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: Catalyst stability crucially impacts the practical value of the catalyst in industrial applications. This comprehensive review aims to lay the foundation for the rational design of high-performance transition metal oxide (TMO) catalysts.

title: A lithiophilic bimetallic oxide interlayer enabling high-rate and dendrite-free lithium metal anodes
abstract: A lithiophilic ZnCo 2 O 4 nanowire-decorated three-dimensional conductive framework was prepared by a simple hydrothermal annealing method and employed as a multifunctional interlayer for hyperstable LMAs.

title: Synergistic influence of multivalent Ru δ + on a CeO x nanocatalyst for self-powered efficient electrochemical water splitting
abstract: A Ru δ + -fabricated CeO x based multicomponent electrocatalyst with engineered surface-electronic states acts as an efficient trifunctional ORR, OER, and HER catalyst towards zinc–air battery powered overall water splitting.

title: Defect-enriched, active, self-supporting, coralline-like, high-entropy spinel oxide (FeNiCoMnCr) 3 O 4 electrodes for high-stability oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: This work reports a self-supporting high-entropy spinel oxide (FeNiCoMnCr) 3 O 4 electrode, whose abundant oxygen vacancies promote OER intermediates adsorption and reduce energy barrier of the *O formation step, showing the excellent OER performance.

title: A multifunctional polymer surface modifier enables efficient and stable photoelectrochemical operations of organic photoanodes
abstract: The SnO 2 modified by a polymer surface modifier enables cathodically shifted onset potential and improved adhesion of bulk-heterojunction films in organic photoanodes, achieving efficient and stable photoelectrochemical operation.

title: EMI shielding performance of PPy/Fe-WS 2 nanocomposites in the Ku band
abstract: PPy/Fe-WS 2 nanocomposites have been synthesized via in situ chemical oxidative polymerization, resulting in well-integrated Fe-WS 2 within the polypyrrole matrix, and show a maximum EMI shielding effectiveness value of 36.12 dB in the Ku-band.

title: Recent advances in functional energy materials for microbial fuel cells: progress, challenges, and future perspectives
abstract: Microbial fuel cells (MFCs) are a promising sustainable technology for addressing global energy shortages and environmental pollution, attracting increasing research interest in recent years.

title: A distinctive edge-graphited hard carbon for advanced sodium-ion batteries
abstract: A novel type of edge-graphitized hard carbon material was reported, which significantly reduced the sodium ion diffusion energy barrier, decreased the irreversible capacity loss and contributed to high ion diffusion kinetics.

title: Highly efficient selective oxidation of benzyl alcohol to benzaldehyde on a PCM-engineered heat-storage catalyst via in situ thermal buffering and facilitated ROS yield
authors: 'Chang'an Wang', 'Yan Gao', 'Tao Ban', 'Chonghao Guan', 'Yuanjie Zhang', 'Zhi Huang', 'Hongyi Gao', 'Ge Wang'
abstract: Heat-storage catalysts MEPCM@CuBTC- x h with dispersed CuBTC particles on the surface and PCMs in the core were used for selective oxidation of benzyl alcohol by the synergistic mechanism of “molecular oxygen activation- in situ thermal buffering”.

title: Cathode materials for proton batteries: recent advances and perspectives
abstract: This review systematically classifies and evaluates cathode materials for proton batteries, categorizing them into three distinct groups: inorganic materials, organic materials and emerging materials.

title: Hydrazone-based covalent organic frameworks for efficient photocatalytic redox reactions
abstract: Two hydrazone-linked covalent organic frameworks have been synthesized by the strategy of molecular engineering under solvothermal conditions and exhibited prominent catalytic performance in photosynthesis of benzothiazoles.

title: Effective sensing mechanisms of O 2 and CO on SnO 2 (110) surface: a DFT study
abstract: DFT reveals oxygen vacancies on SnO 2 stabilize polarons, driving efficient O 2 activation and CO oxidation. These findings enable advanced SnO 2 -based sensor design, leveraging defect engineering to boost catalytic and sensing performance.

title: Exploring the guest–host relationship for zeolite Y: a synergistic structural and theoretical investigation
abstract: We studied dehydration of zeolite Na-Y with X-ray diffraction and a computational study. Lattice parameters and sodium occupancies were monitored and modelled at various water loadings. Computational and experimental results show contraction with dehydration then expansion due to sodium migration.

title: Value-added upcycling of spent low-nickel into a high-nickel layered oxide cathode via a eutectic salt system
abstract: A value-added upcycling method is presented for transforming spent low-nickel into high-nickel NCM material. This method not only restores the layered structure of the NCM materials but also improves their electrochemical performances.

title: Recycling and direct regeneration of valuable cathode materials from spent Li-ion batteries: a comprehensive review
abstract: This review analyzes key recycling and regeneration methods for spent cathodes, emphasizing scientific advances and their significance. It discusses challenges, future directions, and scalable solutions for industrial implementation.

title: Low-temperature access to active iron and iron/nickel nitrides as potential electrocatalysts for the oxygen evolution reaction
authors: 'Christopher R. DeLaney', 'Sergio Diaz-Abad', 'Shamus O'Leary', 'Anna Gonzàlez-Rosell', 'Ulises Martinez', 'Sandip Maurya', 'Sergei A. Ivanov', 'John Watt'
abstract: We present a low temperature synthesis for iron, and bimetallic nitride nanoparticles producing inexpensive catalysts for the oxygen evolution reaction.

title: In situ microbubble electroflotation enhanced emulsion separation and membrane antifouling ability for durable oil-in-water emulsion separation
abstract: An electroflotation coupled membrane separation (ECM) strategy is developed for cationic surfactant-stabilized emulsion treatment, demonstrating high separation efficiency and long-term antifouling capability.

title: Light-driven enhancement in the pseudocapacitance of nanosized ZnO particles and carbon nanotube-based photo-rechargeable supercapacitors
abstract: Light-originated pseudocapacitance enhances the performance of photo-rechargeable supercapacitors (PRSCs), which are useful in the technological field for powering portable and autonomous devices.

title: Coordination force-led multifunctional molecules for efficient perovskite solar cells
abstract: Three typical types of multifunctional molecules used in PSCs and their corresponding functional groups.

title: A synergistic PAN/LiF hybrid layer enabling highly stable lithium metal anodes via in situ interface engineering
abstract: Lithium metal anodes (LMAs) offer exceptional promise for cutting-edge energy storage applications owing to their unparalleled theoretical specific capacity and extremely low electrode potential.

title: A confinement-stabilized Cu 0 –Cu + redox pair on silica and its catalytic role in the water–gas shift reaction
abstract: A confined Cu in /SBA-15 catalyst achieves 5.4 μmol CO g cat −1 s −1 in the WGSR. An associative mechanism via dual sites (Cu + adsorbs CO and Cu 0 activates H 2 O) is confirmed by in situ studies and stabilization are enhanced by a domain-limiting effect.

title: Rational design of a Fe 1− x S/FeS 2 homologous heterostructure with exceptional high-rate performance for advanced sodium-ion battery anodes
abstract: The built-in electric field in the Fe 1− x S/FeS 2 heterogeneous interface endows the Fe 1− x S/FeS 2 electrode with exceptional electrochemical performances in half and full sodium-ion batteries.

title: Metal free donor–acceptor covalent organic frameworks as efficient electrocatalysts for oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: We synthesized a series of metal free donor–acceptor COFs as electrocatalysts for OER application. Porphyrin-based COF showed excellent OER activity, achieving a low η = 398 mV with a Tafel slope of 89 mV dec −1 in alkaline media with high stability.

title: A review on copper-based chalcogenide materials for supercapacitor application: exploring through experimental evidence and machine learning
abstract: Copper chalcogenides (CuS, CuSe, CuTe) are emerging as promising electrode materials for high-performance supercapacitors due to their tuneable structural and electrochemical properties.

title: Advances, challenges, and perspectives in developing CuInX 2 (X = S, Se) nanomaterials for solar energy conversion applications
abstract: CuInX 2 (where X = S, Se) are among group I–III–VI's most recognized semiconductors in the ternary chalcogenides with unique properties for solar energy converting devices.

title: Single enantiomer chiral fullerenes enable interfacial toughening of perovskite solar cells
abstract: High performance perovskite solar cell (PSC) technologies use electron transport layers (ETLs) to boost device performance. Here we show that single enantiomer chiral fullerene ETLs significantly enhanced thermal and operational stability of PSCs.

title: Viscoelasticity-controllable dynamic borate polydimethylsiloxane networks for enhancing energy dissipation of elastomers
abstract: A novel strategy has been developed to precisely enhance the energy dissipation performance of the PDMS double-network elastomers by modulating the viscoelastic properties of the dynamic borate PDMS network.

title: A PEDOT/3D-N-Ti 3 C 2 T x nanoflower hydrophilic regulation molecular imprinting electrochemical sensor for ultrasensitive detection of levofloxacin
abstract: PEDOT, 3D-N-Ti 3 C 2 T x , and CMCS to enhance charge transfer and recognition. Nitrogen doping in Ti 3 C 2 T x improves CMCS active site distribution for stable sensing. CMCS networks with imprinted cavities enable selective levofloxacin detection.

title: Deep-learning-assisted high-throughput discovery of metallophilic MA 2 Z 4 nanomaterials
abstract: An deep-learning-assisted high-throughput method to screen out the promising metallophilic MA 2 Z 4 nanomaterials.

title: A cost-effective dual-anion electrolyte stabilizing silicon anodes via a conductive LiF–Li 2 O–LiCl-rich interphase
abstract: A high-ionicity, mechanically robust ternary SEI (LiF/Li 2 O/LiCl) enables stable silicon anodes by tolerating severe volume changes.

title: Breaking performance trade-offs in polyurea by molecular design for integrated self-healing, mechanical strength and flame retardancy
abstract: A molecularly engineered polyurea breaks traditional performance trade-offs, simultaneously achieving mechanical robustness, self-healing, and intrinsic flame retardancy through rational molecular design for next-generation flexible electronics.

title: Synergistic function of Bi and B in interstitial ternary Pd–Bi–B alloy nanocrystals for highly active and durable electrochemical ethanol oxidation
abstract: Engineering interstitial alloys by integrating non-metallic atoms into bimetallic frameworks is a promising strategy for optimizing performance in various potential applications.

title: Correction: Sodium 4-styrenesulfonyl(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide-based single-ion conducting polymer electrolyte incorporating molecular transporters for quasi-solid-state sodium batteries
abstract: Correction for ‘Sodium 4-styrenesulfonyl(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide-based single-ion conducting polymer electrolyte incorporating molecular transporters for quasi-solid-state sodium batteries’ by Clemens Wunder et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2024, 12 , 20935–20946, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA0232…

title: Data-driven multi-element substitution of TiFe alloys for tunable thermodynamics and enhanced activation behaviour for hydrogen storage
abstract: TiFe alloys offer high volumetric hydrogen storage but face activation and tunability limits. Using machine learning, we propose a multi-element substitution strategy that predicts hydride stability and enables near room-temperature activation.

title: Component-specific biochar from biomass fractions regulates radical/non-radical pathway selectivity for organic degradation
abstract: The catalytic mechanism mediated by biochar derived from different components of homologous biomass by DESs fractionation was studied, highlighting the relationship of structure and properties of precursors with pathway toward organics degradation.

title: Correlating the mechanism, kinetics, and SEI formation of a boron-doped graphene anode for high-performance alkali ion batteries
abstract: The boron doped thermally exfoliated graphene anode serves as a universal anode for alkali ion batteries. The optimal heteroatom dopant, surface defects and interlayer spacing contribute in exhibiting high charge storage and cycling performance.

title: Dynamic interfacial cross-linking and chain entanglement enabling robust mechanical and conductive TPU composite fibers for knitted wearable strain sensors
abstract: This work presents a method to continuously fabricate conductive and knittable TPU fibers for electromagnetic shielding and wearable sensors.

title: Enhancing acid–gas separations using free volume manipulation for microporous poly(arylene ether)s
abstract: H 2 S/CH 4 and CO 2 /CH 4 separations show opposing trends, making simultaneous improvement challenging. This is addressed by increasing free volume to enhance competitive sorption effects and boosting diffusion selectivity through in situ crosslinking.

title: Solar hydrogen production via a Z-scheme water splitting system based solely on perovskite-type tantalum oxynitrides
abstract: Anisotropic crystal facets of SrTaO 2 N allowed the spatial isolation of the reduction and oxidation co-catalysts, enabling a Z-scheme water splitting system based solely on an oxynitride photocatalyst using visible light up to 600 nm and beyond.

title: Unlocking high-efficiency energy storage: neodymium ferrite perovskite as a cathode catalyst for vanadium redox flow batteries
abstract: The H-NdFeO 3 @GF cathode balances structure and activity for high-power VRFBs. Its 3D mesoporous network enables a high-rate capability, 78.87% initial EE at 150 mA cm −2 , and superb stability with 0.00352% decay per cycle over 1000 cycles.

title: Influence of methyl substitution on linear diboronic acids: toward spiroborate covalent organic framework formation in N , N -diethylformamide
abstract: Accelerated formation of spiroborate COF in N , N -diethylformamide by reacting (OH) 8 PcCo with methyl substituted 4,4′-biphenyldiboronic acid.

title: Large piezoelectric strain under a low driving electric field in Sm 3+ -doped BaTiO 3 lead-free piezoelectric ceramics
abstract: Sm 3+ -doping BaTiO 3 -based ceramics with high strain coefficient driven by an ultralow field of 3 kV cm −1 is prepared by constructing unique microstructure that is the atomic-scale polar nanoregions within nano-domains.

title: Alkali ion doping in ammonium vanadate nanoflowers for superior aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Alkali metal ion (Na + , K + ) doping enhances (NH 4 ) 2 V 6 O 16 nanoflowers for AZIBS via synergistic interlayer, defect, and morphological engineering. The optimized NaNVO exhibits 585 mAh g −1 at 0.2 A g −1 and 87% retention over 4000 cycles at 10 A g −1 .

title: Heterojunction engineering of NiC/NiPt promoting charge remigration on the Pt site with efficient acid hydrogen evolution
abstract: The NiC/PtNi@C catalyst with a heterojunction structure has been synthesized by non-precious metal alloying and high temperature carbonization strategies, which shows a low overpotential of 36.7 mV at −10 mA cm −2 with excellent stability.

title: Computer-aided development of bio-inspired routes to highly ordered green mesoporous silica
abstract: By combining multi-scale modelling and design of experiments, we have elucidated the mechanism of order promotion in mesoporous silica, and have proposed strategies for environmentally friendly, cheap and scalable production of those materials.

title: High toughness and low modulus MXene organogel for wearable strain sensors
abstract: High strength, high toughness, low hysteresis, and low modulus properties are crucial for conductive hydrogels to enhance their reliability in flexible sensing applications.

title: LiF doped β-PbSnF 4 with improved ionic conductivity toward high-performance all-solid-state fluoride-ion batteries
abstract: Schematic illustration of synthesis and microstructures of solid electrolyte materials, cell assembly, and predicted reaction processes for a CuF 2 |PbSnF 4 @LiF|Pb + PbF 2 full cell.

title: Highly integrated GeTe thin-film thermoelectric devices for extreme environments
abstract: By incorporating a spectrally selective absorber with self-cleaning properties into TE thin-film devices, the device demonstrates a high open-circuit voltage density of ∼10.4 mV cm −2 and an output power density of ∼2.5 mW cm −2 .

title: Surface aluminization for enhancing oxidation resistance of the Nb 0.86 Hf 0.14 FeSb thermoelectric element
abstract: Through surface aluminization, an in situ dense aluminide coating formed as an effective diffusion barrier against oxygen penetration for Nb 0.86 Hf 0.14 FeSb, which improves the feasibility and thermal stability of its practical applications.

title: Co( ii ) complex promoted PVDF β-phase crystallization: innovations in pressure sensing and energy harvesting
abstract: A Co( ii ) ferroelectric complex (Co-bpy) induces polar β-PVDF via supramolecular interaction. The Co-bpy/PVDF PENG shows superior output, fast energy storage (<2 min), and excellent pressure sensing, surpassing bulk oxide-based devices.

title: Achieving Pt-coating-free anodes using double-layered catalyst layer structure for polymer electrolyte membrane water electrolysis
abstract: A double-layered Ir-based catalyst structure enables Pt-free PEMWE anodes by positioning porous rutile IrO 2 at the PTL interface, effectively mitigating TiO x -induced electron transport issues while retaining high OER activity at low PGM loading.

title: 100 Gram-scale organic-free synthesis of Bi 2 O 2 CO 3 nanosheets for highly selective formate production in CO 2 electroreduction
abstract: A scalable and green synthesis method for bismuth oxycarbonate is developed for electrochemical CO 2 reduction with high efficiency toward HCOOH.

title: Copper single atom-modulated functionalization of iron clusters on a porous carbon nanosheet for the oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: Significant functionalization of Fe 5 clusters with CuN 2 O single atom, compared with CuN 4 and FeN 4 , in decreasing d-band center and weakening the oxygen adsorption.

title: Tracking phase transformations in LiMn 1.5+ x Ni 0.5− x O 4 by high resolution X-ray diffraction
abstract: Studying the effect of annealing LiMn 1.5+ x Ni 0.5− x O 4 ( x = 0, 0.05, 0.1) under different temperatures and atmosphere conditions, revealing new insight about the complex impurity segregation and cation ordering of these materials.

title: Specific crystallographic site occupancy induced water stability: towards facilitating ‘aqueous processing’ of ‘layered’ Na-transition metal oxide cathodes for Na-ion batteries
abstract: Precise positioning of cation at tetrahedral site of Na-layer renders ‘layered’ Na-transition metal oxide totally water-stable by directly blocking the spontaneous Na + /H + exchange pathway, facilitating ‘aqueous’ processing of such cathodes for SIBs.

title: Carboxylate-functionalized polyoxo-titanium clusters for adsorption/solar photocatalytic synergistic tetracycline degradation
abstract: Well-defined carboxylate-functionalized PTCs were used to remove tetracycline, with an efficiency of 92.41% under real sunlight.

title: Pr 6 O 11 cluster-anchored CoFe-LDH on vertical graphene nanosheets as an oxygen evolution electrocatalyst for long-term high-current-density seawater electrolysis
abstract: Our synthesized seawater OER electrocatalyst GP/VGSs-CoFe LDH-Pr 6 O 11 shows excellent catalytic activity and stability (with the aid of K 2 SO 4 ) in overall alkaline seawater electrolysis, combining a previously reported HER electrocatalyst NiMoN.

title: Mediating the carbon black–natural rubber interface with thioamide-functionalized polysulfide for energy-saving composites
abstract: Thioamide-functionalized polysulfide SCA is synthesized and utilized as an intelligent interfacial regulator for preparing rubber composites with low-hysteresis loss.

title: Tuning the nano-scale aggregate structure of conjugated block copolymers for enhanced single-walled carbon nanotube dispersion and thermoelectric applications
abstract: Conjugated block copolymers with tailored insulating segments are designed to control polymer aggregation and SWCNT dispersion, promoting SWCNT debundling through loose agglomerates and steric stabilization via solvated insulating blocks.

title: Asymmetric coordination in iron single atom catalysts enables rapid hydroxyl radical turnover and sustainable water purification
abstract: Asymmetric coordination in iron single atom catalysts enables rapid hydroxyl radical turnover and ultrafast pollutant detoxification.

title: Mixed matrix membranes based on carbon quantum dots with enhanced CO 2 capture performances
abstract: Mixed matrix membranes (MMMs) can more readily surpass the Robeson upper bound by combining the complementary advantages of organic membranes and inorganic nanoparticle fillers.

title: Functionalization of the NiCoP electrocatalyst with phytic acid enables efficient and stable alkaline seawater hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Phytic acid decorated nickel-cobalt phosphide nanoneedle array on nickel foam (IP 6 @NiCoP/NF) exhibits remarkable performance, achieving a low overpotential of 268 mV at 1000 mA cm −2 with exceptional stability of 1600 hours in alkaline seawater.

title: High-rate rare-earth-based high-entropy Co-free high-Ni cathodes for high-performance lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A rare-earth high-entropy Co-free high-Ni layered cathode LiNi 0.9 Mn 0.02 Al 0.02 Mg 0.02 Ti 0.02 Lu 0.02 O 2 achieves superior cycling stability at high rates, providing a promising strategy for advanced lithium-ion batteries.

title: Buckyball C 60 /Fe–N 4 superstructured electrodes for efficient oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: Iron-phthalocyanine intercalated C 60 electrodes with complete buckyball C 60 s exhibited superior ORR activity compared to C 60 -derived electrode with unfolded C 60 s.

title: Design of phthalocyanine metal complexes for efficient far-red to near-IR light-initiated photopolymerizations
authors: 'Connor J. O'Dea', 'Lauren M. Loftus', 'Tod A. Grusenmeyer', 'Jussi Isokuortti', 'Justin Ong', 'Sean T. Roberts', 'Zachariah A. Page'
abstract: A survey of phthalocyanine (Pc) and naphthalocyanine (Nc) photocatalysts with palladium (Pd), zinc (Zn), or silicon (Si) centres led to efficient near-infrared (NIR) Type II photopolymerizations with earth-abundant metals.

title: Aqueous alkaline pH stable halide ((PEA) 2 CoCl 4 ) perovskite for oxygen reaction electrocatalysis
abstract: Aqueous-stable NPCo catalyst was designed by coating N-doped graphene on (PEA) 2 CoCl 4 perovskite. This coating shielded perovskite from water and enhanced its conductivity for better electron transfer for excellent bifunctional catalytic activity.

title: One-step synthesis of hollow spherical Co/Ni hydroxides as multifunctional polysulfide mediators to steer sulfur redox kinetics for high performance lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: A series of hollow bimetallic hydroxides with controllable composition and modulated electronic structure have been readily constructed, and exhibit stronger affinity and a synergistic catalysis effect for LiPS intermediates.

title: Anion-guided interphase formation stabilizes reversible iron electrodeposition in aqueous electrolytes
abstract: Aqueous iron anodes are stabilized by anion-guided interphase formation. Sulfate and triflate electrolytes form oxyhydroxide-rich layers that suppress hydrogen evolution and enable reversible iron electrodeposition.

title: Linkage engineering regulated π-conjugated covalent organic framework (COF)-based anodes for high-performance LIBs
abstract: We successfully synthesized two types of COFs with varied linkages, enabling the manipulation of Li storage performance through linkage engineering. The COFs with imine linkages performed better than those with oxazole linkages.

title: Multifunctional ionogels toughened with spiropyran mechanophores for soft ionotronics
abstract: Multifunctional ionogels toughened with spiropyran mechanophores exhibit enhanced mechanical properties and responsiveness to light, force, and temperature and are suitable for human motion sensors, energy harvesters, and wearable heaters.

title: Polarization synergizes defective interface heterojunctions boosting piezocatalytic hydrogen production and simultaneous pollutant degradation
abstract: A dual functional BiOIO 3 -VO/NH 2 -MIL-125 S-scheme heterojunction engineered with oxygen vacancies, achieves exceptional piezocatalytic hydrogen evolution and simultaneous organic pollutant degradation.

title: Single-atom catalysts integrated with semiconductors for constructing a dual-potential electrochemiluminescence sensor for intracellular H 2 O 2 detection
abstract: Single-atom Co-doped g-C 3 N 4 was combined with MoS 2 to synthesize a MoS 2 /g-C 3 N 4 composite, used here for the first time to develop a dual-potential electrochemiluminescence (ECL) sensor for intracellular H 2 O 2 detection.

title: Kirkendall effect triggered by trace Mn element on a Ni–N–C catalyst for enhanced electroreduction of CO 2 to CO
abstract: Ni Mn –N–C catalyst, using two metals, features Ni–N X active sites and trace Mn inducing Kirkendall effect to optimize physicochemical structure, enhancing Ni site CO 2 RR activity.

title: Illumination-induced photoluminescence enhancement in metal halide perovskites: revisiting mechanisms and emerging perspectives
abstract: Illumination-induced photoluminescence enhancement reveals beneficial light–matter interactions in metal halide perovskites. This work critiques current models and proposes a lattice-based framework explaining reversible, fluence-dependent subgap PL.

title: In situ TEM and synchrotron SAXS/WAXS study on the impact of different iron salts on iron-catalysed graphitization of cellulose
abstract: In situ TEM and synchrotron SAXS/WAXS show that FeCl 3 as a catalyst precursor results in considerably faster graphitization of cellulose than Fe(NO 3 ) 3 .

title: Recent advances in nickel-based anodes for anion-exchange membrane water electrolyzers
abstract: This review sheds light on the developments made in the affordable Ni-based oxygen evolution catalysts and their potential application in anion-exchange membrane water electrolyzers (AEMWEs) for green hydrogen production.

title: A thiol-modified solid electrolyte interphase enhances the stability of zinc anodes under high depths of discharge
abstract: By introducing TMPEI, a stable solid electrolyte interface layer is formed on the zinc anode surface, effectively enhancing the cycling stability and long-term performance of the zinc anode.

title: An inverse ceria–copper catalyst for effective methanol steam reforming
abstract: The inverse CeO 2 /Cu catalyst exhibits a significantly higher activity than supported catalyst towards the methanol steam reforming reaction.

title: Polyoxometalate@metal–organic framework-derived Fe-Co-Mo/NC: an advanced electrocatalyst for zinc–air batteries
abstract: Defective Fe-Co-Mo/NC from Anderson-POM@ZIF-8 gives 150 mW cm −2 , 787 mAh g −1 , >300 h Zn–air battery.

title: Theoretical investigation of a C 2 N monolayer as a bifunctional electrocatalyst for rechargeable non-aqueous Li–air batteries
abstract: Our work provides a detailed mechanistic pathway for the oxygen reduction reaction during the discharging process and the potentiality of a C 2 N monolayer as the cathode catalyst in non-aqueous Li–air batteries.

title: Constructing a polycrystalline hybrid ternary CuS/Co 3 O 4 with supported graphitic nitride electrocatalyst for bifunctional water splitting reactions
abstract: The development of low-cost, durable, and highly efficient bifunctional electrocatalysts for water splitting across various media is essential for clean energy production.

title: Tuning the bifunctional properties of an α-Fe 2 O 3 /Fe 2 TiO 5 /Pt heterojunction photoelectrode for light-induced water oxidation and oxygen reduction activity
abstract: Alpha-Fe 2 O 3 nanorods modified with Fe 2 TiO 5 and Pt enable efficient light-driven water oxidation and the ORR, serving as a bifunctional cathode in solar Zn–air batteries with 50 h stability, low charging, and high discharging potentials.

title: Advances in topological quantum materials for energy conversion
abstract: Topological surface states enhances charge transport in solar cell, water splitting and photoelectrochemical cells. They can have a great impact on reducing charge recombination, dye degradation, corrosion and heat losses leading to enhanced PCE.

title: The enhancement of photocatalytic hydrogen evolution in imine-linked pyrene-based covalent organic frameworks through the regulation of active site distances
abstract: An appropriate distance between the active sites in photocatalysts would significantly enhance charge separation efficiency and photocatalytic performance.

title: Eliminating water molecules through tailored crystal orientation to enhance the lithium storage capacity of iron oxalate
abstract: Iron oxalate, a coordination polymer known for its sustainability, is a potential candidate for high-capacity and high-rate anode materials for lithium-ion batteries.

title: Electrochemical CO 2 reduction to alcohols using flexible and rigid MOF electrocatalysts
abstract: Metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) are a versatile class of materials with significant potential for electrochemical CO 2 reduction to multicarbon products.

title: Fabrication of reduced graphene oxide electromagnetic shielding films with excellent comprehensive performance using a two-step reduction strategy
abstract: Graphene materials hold significant potential for electromagnetic wave shielding applications due to their excellent electrical conductivity, mechanical properties and versatility.

title: Theoretical guidance for targeted modulation of metal–nitrogen single atom active sites on 3D porous carbon to optimize electrocatalytic performance in energy conversion applications
abstract: Functionalized carbon-based single atom catalysts M/Ni–NDPC (M = Fe, Cu, and Mo) were designed by using DFT as a guide. Fe/Ni–NDPC and Mo/Ni–NDPC exhibited optimal catalytic performance in the IRR for solar cells and the HER for water splitting.

title: Structural engineering through modulator incorporation in UiO-66-NH 2 metal–organic frameworks for piezoresponse regulation
abstract: UiO-66-NH 2 (Hf/Zr) MOFs were structurally tuned with acetic acid (AA). Increasing AA creates missing-linker defects and incorporates more modulators, breaking symmetry and boosting the piezoresponse-a simple route to engineering piezoelectric MOFs.

title: Construction of a robust cathode protection layer inspired by the wet adhesion behavior of mussels towards high-performance aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Inspired by the wet adhesion behavior of mussels, a polydopamine layer with high conductivity and robust mechanical stability was decorated onto an MnO 2 cathode to enhance the electrochemical performance of aqueous zinc-ion batteries.

title: Construction of aqueous supercapacitors with oxidation suppression of nickel phosphide via interfacial engineering and electric field modulation for enhanced secondary energy storage
abstract: Schematic diagram of oxidation inhibition and secondary energy storage mechanism of Ni 2 P@NiCo-LDHs heterojunction material.

title: An ultra-high nickel cobalt-free cathode material toward high-energy and long-cycle stable Li-ion batteries: a single-crystal and surface high-entropy design strategy
abstract: We developed a Mg/Nb/Al co-doped single-crystal LiNi 0.9 Mn 0.1 O 2 with improved cycling stability. The high-entropy surface stabilizes oxygen, enhances phase transition reversibility, and builds a HF-resistant CEI.

title: Unraveling Li-ion transport mechanisms in high-entropy anion-disordered argyrodites via machine-learned interatomic potentials
abstract: Machine-learned interatomic potentials enable analysis of Li-ion conduction, revealing that high-entropy anion-disordered argyrodites enhance ionic conductivity through inter-cage migration.

title: Impact of structural coherence and disorder on the ionic transport and lattice dynamics in Li + -conducting argyrodites
abstract: While thermal conductivity is almost unaffected by post-synthesis treatments of lithium argyrodites, reducing coherence length and crystallite size, it is changed significantly by structural disordering suppressing phonon gas-type thermal transport.

title: Effects of steady magnetic fields on NiRuO 2 nanofibers for the electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution reaction and oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: NiRuO 2 nanofibers demonstrate OER (280 mV) and HER (1 mV) overpotentials at 10 mA cm −2 under magnetic field, which drives a spin state transition from high-spin to low-spin and reduces the energy barrier for both AEM and OPM pathways.

title: Electronic structure tuning to facilitate charge transfer in Z-scheme mediated CuO/Se@WO 3 aided by synchronized Cu(OH) 2 for efficient overall water splitting
abstract: A comprehensive study on the CuO/Se@WO 3 /Cu(OH) 2 electrocatalyst, emphasizing its innovative p–n junction with electronic structure tweaking exhibits exceptional performance in the HER and OER under alkaline conditions.

title: Molecular sieve confined catalysts for the catalytic combustion of VOCs: preparation, application and future development
abstract: Review of molecular sieve-confined catalysts for VOCs abatement, merits/limitations, mechanisms, and structure-activity relationships. Future catalyst design, mechanistic studies, and in situ characterization advancements explored.

title: High-density electrostatic energy storage in a multi-layer P(VDF-TrFE-CFE)/2D mica nanocomposite heterostructure capacitor
abstract: (a) Discharge energy density and efficiency of the PTC/2D Mica/PTC heterostructure capacitor shows an energy density as high as 50 J cm −3 at E = 700 MV m −1 and an efficiency of 80%. (b) Shows the discharge time of the capacitor is 6.68 μs.

title: Interface engineering of 0D–2D CoSe 2 /ZnSe@MXene heterostructured electrodes for high-performance lithium-ion batteries
abstract: MOF-derived CoSe 2 /ZnSe bimetallic selenide nanoparticles confined in layered Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene were employed as anodes for high-performance lithium-ion batteries, exhibiting superior rate capability and cycling stability.

title: Leakage-proof nacre-like boron nitride nanosheet/phase change microcapsule composites with enhanced thermal conduction and thermal energy storage for advanced thermal management systems
abstract: Leakage-proof nacre-like boron nitride nanosheet/phase change microcapsule composites with enhanced thermal conduction and thermal energy storage were prepared via bidirectional freezing and hot-pressing for advanced thermal management systems.

title: Inorganic cation-capped SnO 2 for high-performance perovskite solar cells
abstract: Controlled surface capping of SnO 2 electron transport layers with large cations reduces lattice strain at the buried perovskite interface, thereby improving the perovskite film quality, device efficiency, and stability.

title: Dually encapsulated LiMn 0.6 Fe 0.4 PO 4 architecture with MXenes and amorphous carbon to achieve high-performance and ultra-stable lithium batteries
abstract: An LMFP@MXene@C cathode material was fabricated by electrostatic adsorption and in situ graphitization, providing optimized Li + transmission and a stable structure featuring ultrahigh average capacity and cycle stability.

title: Engineering cadmium-doped NiFe-LDH nanosheet arrays via low-temperature etching for robust high-current-density seawater electrolysis
abstract: A low-temperature etched Cd-doped NiFe-LDH anode suppresses ClER via electronic modulation, enabling ultralow overpotential (337 mV@1000 mA cm −2 ) and stable seawater splitting.

title: Identifying efficient two-dimensional transition metal oxide cathodes for non-aqueous lithium–oxygen batteries using the work function as a simple descriptor
abstract: Work function-guided screening reveals 2D TMO bifunctional ORR/OER catalysts for LOBs, identifying 1T-VO 2 , W 2 O 4 , Mo 2 O 4 , and Re 2 O 4 as effective candidates.

title: MOF-derived 3D host nanostructures combining the advantages of microporous MOFs and carbon for stable Li deposition
abstract: A MOF-derived host material with optimized calcination temperature enables bottom-up Li deposition by balancing lithiophilicity and insulation effectively suppressing dendrite growth and surface accumulation for stable lithium metal anode operation.

title: Efficient solar-driven atmospheric water harvesting in arid regions using a recyclable and biodegradable composite aerogel
abstract: The SAWH system developed utilizing a composite PHB/CNTs/MgCl 2 aerogel exhibits exceptional water vapor absorption capacity across a wide range of relative humidities and temperatures, with a desorption rate of 1.8 g g −1 h −1 under 1 sun irradiation.

title: Electron donation of violet phosphorene nanosheets to sustain the oxygen vacancies of BiOBr for excellent photocatalytic nitrogen fixation
abstract: The electron donation of violet phosphorene sustains the oxygen vacancies of BiOBr, enabling efficient photocatalytic nitrogen fixation.

title: Nanowire morphology control in Sb metal-derived antimony selenide photocathodes for solar water splitting
abstract: Sb 2 Se 3 photocathode performance improves by tuning substrate temperature during Sb metal deposition. Thinner nanorods result in reduced charge recombination, enhancing photocurrent by restricting charge transport in the hk 0 direction.

title: Small voltage hysteresis and flat plateaus in Ca-metal batteries using a Ti-based NASICON cathode
abstract: NaTi 2 (PO 4 ) 3 is proposed as a Ca-metal battery cathode, offering high Ca 2+ diffusivity and enabling reversible Ca 2+ storage with small voltage hysteresis and flat voltage plateau.

title: Delafossite-based electrode materials: design, synthesis and their application in electrocatalysis
abstract: This review outlines the design principles of delafossite and their electrocatalytic applications. Further insights and perspectives on the challenges and opportunities for developing delafossite-based advanced electrocatalysts are also provided.

title: First principles study of blue phosphorene heterostructures as Li-ion battery anode materials
abstract: Blue phosphorene (BP) and silicene heterostructure shows potential as anode material for Li-ion batteries, with significantly lowered lithium adsorption energy, low diffusion barrier of 0.2 eV, and 0.26 eV bandgap, indicating excellent performance.

title: Recent progress in alkali metal (Li/Na/K) hybrid-ion batteries: pioneering the future of energy storage
abstract: Alkali metal (Li/Na/K) hybrid-ion batteries (AHIBs) offer a sustainable and safe energy solution. This review highlights research progress in AHIB technologies, with a focus on mechanisms and innovative approaches that enhance battery performance.

title: Electrospun Fe-ZIF derived carbon nanofibers for boosting adsorption and redox kinetics of polysulfides in lithium–sulfur batteries
