abstract: Fe–N active site-exposed carbon nanofibers were synthesized via electrospinning and Fe-ZIF in situ growth. The Fe–S bonds enhanced polysulfide adsorption and redox kinetics, exhibiting excellent cycling stability in lithium–sulfur batteries.

title: A one-step dual modification strategy for enhancing sodium storage in O3-type NaNi 1/3 Fe 1/3 Mn 1/3 O 2 cathodes
abstract: A one-step dual modification strategy of bulk phase doping and multifunctional coating for cathodes of O3-type sodium ion batteries, enabling superior long-term cycling performance with 80.02% capacity retention after 300 cycles.

title: Surface modification of copper oxide nanosheets with CeO 2 for enhanced CO 2 reduction to C 2 H 4
abstract: Surface modification with CeO 2 nanoparticles to stabilize the oxidation state of OD-Cu during the reduction process promotes the synergistic effect of Cu 0 and Cu + in the CO 2 RR process and improves the selectivity for C 2 H 4 .

title: Recent advances in zeolite membranes for gas separation and pervaporation in petrochemicals
abstract: This review emphasizes novel strategies in zeolite membrane preparation and optimization for enhanced performance in petrochemical applications.

title: Repurposing PVA-based slime to address electrolyte challenges in portable electrochemical devices
abstract: PVA-based slime is an unexplored, cost-effective, and spill-proof alternative to conventional electrolytes. Its inherent ionic conductivity makes it a promising electrolyte for portable electrochemical surface-monitoring probes.

title: Journal of Materials Chemistry A Editor's choice collection: Li-metal batteries
abstract: Professor Serena A. Cussen introduces Journal of Materials Chemistry A Editor's choice collection on Li-metal batteries.

title: Photooxidation reaction kinetics of mixed-cation mixed-halide perovskite
abstract: In situ absorbance measurements reveal two photooxidation pathways in FA 0.8 Cs 0.2 Pb(I 0.83 Br 0.17 ) 3 thin films. Kinetic modeling explains the dual role of water, with a posited hydrate formation pathway retarding degradation at high temperature.

title: A synergetic oxide-chalcogenide heterostructure in metallic two-dimensional VSe 2 for the hydrogen-evolution reaction
abstract: The catalytic properties of metallic VSe 2 films are controlled by surface oxidation. Improvement of surface water interaction is validated by both ambient-pressure X-ray photoemission spectroscopy and theoretical calculations.

title: Enhancing the backbone regularity of sulfurized polyacrylonitrile for long-life Li-SPAN batteries
abstract: The intrinsic solid–solid reactions of sulfurized polyacrylonitrile (SPAN) cathode materials present a compelling solution to tackle the serious issue of “shuttle effect” in lithium–sulfur batteries.

title: Single iron site catalysts with increased metal-site loading via a high-temperature imprinting approach for proton exchange membrane fuel cells
abstract: A new active-site imprinting approach based on a vacuum-sealed reaction system enables to convert less active ZnN x into FeN 4 . The strategy yields a Fe–N–C catalyst containing exceptionally high atomic Fe site loading up to 5.65 wt%.

title: Bond-strength engineering beyond mass fluctuation: unraveling the dominant mechanism of lattice thermal conductivity suppression in neighboring-site doped β-FeSi 2 thermoelectrics
abstract: In addition to the atomic mass/size difference between Ir and Fe, the imbalance in chemical bonding caused by Co doping at the adjacent site can lead to a significant reduction in the lattice thermal conductivity of β-FeSi 2 .

title: High-entropy electrolytes towards advanced aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: This perspective consolidates mechanistic insights from non-aqueous systems and the unique properties of aqueous systems to decode the working principles and to guide the rational design of high-entropy electrolytes in aqueous zinc-ion batteries.

title: Correction: Raman spectroscopy study of K-birnessite single crystals
abstract: Correction for ‘Raman spectroscopy study of K-birnessite single crystals’ by Dong Han Ha et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, 13 , 617–626, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA06118G.

title: Direct synthesis of novel layered MCM-56 zeolite using a boron-assisted cyclohexylamine system
abstract: One-pot synthesis of MCM-56 zeolite was successfully achieved using cyclohexylamine, a low-toxicity and cost-effective organic template, along with boric acid as an additive. Comprehensive NMR analyses elucidate the crucial role of boron atoms.

title: Reducing energy loss of fused-ring electron acceptors by pyrrole extension
abstract: IP4F exhibits a narrower bandgap but stronger photoluminescence, longer exciton lifetime and smaller energy losses than ID4F. The IP4F-based organic solar cells achieve an efficiency of 19.6%, higher than that of their ID4F counterparts (17.7%).

title: Effect of Fe doping on the oxygen reduction reaction activity of a PrNi 0.5 Co 0.5 O 3− δ cathode for protonic ceramic fuel cells
abstract: An optimized Fe-doped perovskite cathode material with a nominal composition of PrNi 0.5 Co 0.3 Fe 0.2 O 3− δ has been developed for protonic ceramic fuel cells, demonstrating good electrochemical activity and favorable operational durability.

title: Photocatalytic performance of Y 2 Ti 2 O 5 S 2 prepared via carbon disulfide sulfurization
abstract: The Y 2 Ti 2 O 5 S 2 have been prepared by using carbon disulfide (CS 2 ) as a sulfurizing reagent in the presence of a flux, which represents a new approach to the synthesis of oxysulfide photocatalysts in a manner that may be suitable for mass production.

title: Defect-mediated hierarchical tubular CoSe 2 @TiO 2 heterostructure photocatalysts for boosted CO 2 photoreduction
abstract: Defect-mediated hierarchical CoSe 2 @TiO 2 heterostructure nanotubes were fabricated as highly efficient and stable photocatalysts for CO 2 photoreduction.

title: The effect of ZrO 2 - g -poly(methyl acrylate- co -maleic anhydride) on the performance of polyacrylonitrile gel polymer electrolyte in lithium ion batteries
abstract: A porous GPE with a honeycomb-like structure was made using PAN and ZMPMAM nanocomposites. It showed 590% electrolyte uptake, 2.74 mS cm −1 conductivity, and a 0.76 Li + transference number, enabling stable 5 V operation and 157 mA h g −1 at 1C.

title: Controlled growth of high-quality SnSe nanoplates assisted by machine learning
abstract: Machine learning (ML) approaches have emerged as powerful tools to accelerate materials discovery and optimization, offering a sustainable alternative to traditional trial-and-error methods in exploratory experiments.

title: Structural origin of disorder-induced ion conduction in NaFePO 4 cathode materials
abstract: Diffusion in NaFePO 4 can be enhanced through amorphization. Based on computations using DFT and machine learning potentials, we ascribe this phenomenon to the formation of less constrained Na-ion environments upon disordering.

title: Tailoring photocatalytic activity in porphyrin-MOFs: the role of amino-functionalized pillars in CO 2 adsorption and band structure modulation
abstract: Porphyrin-based pillared MOFs were synthesized via an amino-functionalization strategy, which enhanced CO 2 adsorption, light absorption and charge separation resulting in superior photocatalytic CO 2 reduction performance.

title: A biodegradable and flexible solid-state supercapacitor based on a natural polymer dual network hydrogel electrolyte
abstract: A biodegradable and flexible all-solid-state supercapacitor is fabricated with MoS 2 @rGO/Mo as the electrode and a dual-network GelMA@CMC hydrogel as the electrolyte.

title: The in situ solid electrolyte interphase formed using solid electrolyte additives for highly reversible zinc metal anodes
abstract: The in situ formed SEI by GOZIF8 altered the solvation process during Zn 2+ ion deposion and induced the epitaxial deposition of Zn 2+ along the (110) and (101) crystal planes to stabilize the reversibility of zinc anodes.

title: Oxygen-modified supra-nanometer-sized RuPt for robust alkaline HER/HOR
abstract: The supra-nanometer-sized RuPt with oxygen modification showed improved electrocatalytic performance for alkaline HER and HOR compared to the unannealed catalyst.

title: Electroless generation of nano-silver fern catalysts upon Cu foam as a highly effective bimetallic electrocatalyst design for ammonia electrosynthesis
abstract: Generation of nano silver fern centers, with copper intercalation, created a highly active electrocatalyst for ammonia electrosynthesis.

title: Synergistic linkage engineering in covalent organic frameworks for boosting photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: Enhancing π-conjugation and bond stability, along with the introduction of hydrophilic carboxyl groups into the COF channels, significantly boosts the photocatalytic performance, reusability, and durability of the COF.

title: Hierarchical carrier trapping engineering in all-organic composites for high-temperature dielectric energy storage
abstract: By incorporating wide-bandgap PVH and high-k PVTC into PEI, hierarchical traps are introduced into the sandwich-structured composite to suppress carrier migration, thus enhancing high-temperature energy storage performance of the dielectric.

title: Unveiling interfacial dynamics of zero-dimensional bismuth-based halide perovskite emitters for electrochemiluminescence applications
abstract: Low-dimensional DMA 3 BiI 6 exhibits superior ECL intensity compared to MA 3 Bi 2 I 9 , driven by stronger electronic coupling, lower activation energy, and reduced defect-mediated nonradiative recombination, highlighting DMA 3 BiI 6 as a promising ECL material.

title: In situ transformation of a liquid electrolyte into a solid polymer electrolyte: influence of TMSP in a layered LiNi 0.82 Mn 0.12 Al 0.06 O 2 cathode
abstract: Herein, we employed the concept of in situ polymerization of a liquid electrolyte to enhance the interfacial stability of a solid polymer electrolyte (SPE) along with TMSP as additive.

title: A hierarchically engineered polymer composite with a dual-scatter structure for enhanced passive radiative cooling
abstract: A nanostructured perfluoropolymer paint with high solar reflectance and selective infrared emittance has been developed, highlighting the importance of optimizing the chemical structure of polymer binder for radiative cooling.

title: Fabrication of CdSe nanosheet/CdS nanorod heterojunctions through topotactic transformation of self-template photoanode for enhanced photoelectrochemical hydrogen production
abstract: Microwave-assisted synthesis and topotactic transformation of CdSe(en) 0.5 /CdS NR yield porous CdSeNS/CdSNR photoanodes with enhanced PEC performance for efficient hydrogen generation.

title: High-energy density dielectric film capacitors enabled by grain boundary engineering
abstract: We demonstrate that the energy storage performance of 0.85BaTiO 3 -0.15Bi(Mg 0.5 Zr 0.5 )O 3 films has been significantly enhanced by designing film microstructure, providing a practical avenue for developing high-performance dielectric film capacitors.

title: Cyanobenzene-modified polymer acceptors for high-efficiency all-polymer solar cells with low energy loss
abstract: In this work, two novel polymer acceptors, P-phCN and P-BphCN, were designed and synthesized by modifying central cores with asymmetric cyanobenzene. The P-phCN-based device achieved a PCE of 17.10% with low energy loss of 0.518 eV.

title: Dual-asymmetric backbone constructed polymerized small molecule acceptors for efficient all-polymer solar cells
abstract: Dual-asymmetric polymer acceptors with finely-tailored molecular energy levels, crystallinity, and packing are developed for the first time, achieving an efficiency of 17.28% in all-polymer solar cells.

title: A fluorinated COF-encapsulated CeO 2 @Pt sandwich catalyst enables dual interfacial-electronic modulation for liquid-phase hydrogenation
abstract: Efficient liquid-phase hydrogenation of nonpolar substrates is constrained by interfacial mass-transfer limitations and catalyst deactivation.

title: A Cu 14 (dppy) 7 (dmbt) 3 cluster of chelating structure for enhanced electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction to ammonia
abstract: We synthesised a Cu 14 (dppy) 7 (dmbt) 3 nanocluster with chelating structure, showing enhanced electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction to ammonia (N 2 to NH 3 ) by supporting it on graphene.

title: Tuning atomic mismatch with trace Al for enhanced long-term hydrogen storage performance of TiCrMo alloy
abstract: Al substitution for Cr was adopted to minimize atomic mismatch in the Ti–Cr–Mo alloy, reducing lattice distortion/stress during hydrogen cycling and enhancing the cycling stability of Ti–Cr–Mo–Al BCC hydrogen storage alloys.

title: Lightweight and ultra-flexible perovskite solar cells with a “sandwich” perovskite layer based on graphene–carbon nanotube electrodes
abstract: The lightweight and ultra-flexible PSC, with a “sandwich” perovskite absorbing layer based on a graphene–carbon nanotube transparent electrode, exhibits a stable photovoltaic efficiency, remarkable power-per-weight and mechanical flexibility.

title: Nanoporous PdSnBi alloys for superior formic acid oxidation electrocatalysis
abstract: The NPG-PdSnBi catalyst exhibits high activity, superior stability and strong anti-CO poisoning ability toward the FAOR.

title: Laser-drilled functional wood materials show improved dimensional stability upon humidity changes – a neutron imaging analysis
abstract: In situ golden ratio tomography revealed that the laser drilling process is crucial for decoupling dimensional changes of wood from moisture uptake.

title: Hierarchical vanadium sulfide nanosheets with expanded interchain spacing for high-performance sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Hierarchical VS 4 nanosheets with large expanded interchain spacing are prepared and demonstrate remarkable sodium storage performance.

title: A multifunctional Mo–N/Fe–N interfaced MoS 2 /FeNC electrocatalyst for energy conversion applications
abstract: Vertically aligned MoS 2 nanosheet-anchored Fe,N-doped carbon nanospheres are synthesized and characterized. The material with engineered phase structures revealed trifunctional catalytic activities towards the ORR, the HER, and the H 2 O 2 reduction.

title: Halide perovskite stabilized in a photoelectrochemical environment by impermeable single crystal TiO 2 for semi-artificial photosynthesis
abstract: An optically transparent, conductive, and impermeable single-crystal TiO 2 protection layer stabilizes halide perovskite photoelectrodes for water splitting or trans -hydrogenation reactions, without requiring charge conduction across a resistive TCO.

title: Facile and green fabrication of segregated ABS/CNT foams with superior electrical conductivity and ultrahigh EMI shielding performance
abstract: Conductive polymer composite (CPC) foams, which possess excellent electrical properties and unique structures, offer great advantages for practical applications in electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding.

title: High-performance hydrogen evolution reaction by ReS 2 /TiO 2 hollow microcones created through microwave-hydrothermal consecutive synthesis
abstract: The ReS 2 /TiO 2 hollow microcones synthesized via the MW-HT consecutive method exhibited excellent HER catalytic performance.

title: Unlocking the catalytic potential of iMXenes: selective electrochemical CO 2 reduction for methane production
abstract: We employ first-principles calculations to systematically investigate the electrocatalytic activity and selectivity of various iMXenes for CH 4 production. Our study includes the thorough investigation of bare and O-terminated iMXenes.

title: Attempts to realize promising thermoelectric performance in n-type polycrystalline SnSe with a cubic structure
abstract: Promising thermoelectric performance at low temperatures (300–600 K) realized in n-type polycrystalline SnSe by regulating the lattice structure.

title: Enhanced photocatalytic performance for CO 2 reduction via an S-scheme heterojunction between perovskite nanocrystals and BiVO 4
abstract: Schematic demonstration of the formation of an S-scheme heterojunction between Cs 0.5 FA 0.5 PbBr 3 (CF) and BiVO 4 showing the photocatalytic mechanism for efficient CO 2 reduction reaction.

title: Construction of a cobalt-doped Ni 3 S 2 @NiFe-LDH heterojunction with enhanced local electric field for efficient oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: The effect of heterogeneous atom doping and heterojunction enhances the local electric field, thereby adjusting the adsorption behavior for intermediates, improving catalyst conductivity, and promoting reaction kinetics. Consequently, this leads to an enhanced OER activity.

title: Metal–organic frameworks: classifications, synthesis, structure–property–performance relationship, and techno-economic analysis of redox flow batteries
abstract: MOFs classification and application in redox flow batteries.

title: Electrolyte challenges and strategies toward better rechargeable magnesium-metal batteries
abstract: This review comprehensively summarizes the persistent challenges and progress in electrolyte design for rechargeable magnesium batteries (RMBs), aiming to guide the rational design of efficient electrolytes and advancing the development of RMBs.

title: Colloidal Fe nanocrystals for CH 4 decomposition: role of the support
abstract: Colloidal Fe nanoparticles supported on SiO 2 , MgO, Si 3 N 4 , and MgAl 2 O 4 were studied for CH 4 decomposition. Support nature dictated Fe stability and CNT growth. MgAl 2 O 4 enabled highest activity via base/tip growth, forming CO 2 -free H 2 and carbon nanostructures.

title: Boosting stability and rate performance in sodium-ion batteries: first-principles insights into K + /NH 4 + doped NaV 3 O 8 cathodes
abstract: This study utilizes DFT calculations to explore Na storage in NaV 3 O 8 , and K + /NH 4 + doping effects. Results show dopants improve stability and kinetics, offering key insights for high-performance sodium-ion battery cathodes.

title: Percolation-enabled long-range ion transport to achieve conductivity leap in PVDF-based electrolytes
abstract: A two-order-of-magnitude conductivity leap emerges in PVDF electrolytes at a critical solvent content of ∼7 wt%. This leap is driven by a shift from local ion hopping to long-range, continuous transport via a percolation network.

title: A polysulfide/ferricyanide redox flow battery with extended cycling
abstract: The inexpensive sulfur raw material is promising to enable cost-effective redox flow batteries for long duration energy storage.

title: Can dopants defend? Unraveling RuO 2 corrosion and reinforcement strategies for enhanced stability
abstract: Doping RuO 2 (110) with Ir, Ti, and Pt enhances stability against corrosion, while Ta, W, and Re promote degradation. Δ G tot serves as a reliable predictor for the thermodynamic stability of doped surfaces.

title: Rational design of Cu oxynitride–BiVO 4 composites as enriched bifunctional electrocatalysts for hydrogen and oxygen evolution reaction performances
abstract: This study investigates the integration of copper oxynitride (CuON) and bismuth vanadate (BiVO 4 ) to form a bifunctional electrocatalyst with enhanced performance for the hydrogen and oxygen evolution reactions (HER and OER, respectively) in 1 M KOH.

title: Innovative approaches to the molecular design, synthesis, and functionalization of conjugated organic polymer (D–A, D–π–A, A–A–D, and A–D–A) photocatalysts for sustainable hydrogen production
abstract: The exploration of conjugated polymers with various donor–acceptor types (D–A, D–π–A, D–A–A, and D–π–A–A) for developments in side-chain substitution, conjugation expansion, and the creation of photocatalysts for hydrogen generation is reviewed.

title: Oxygen vacancy engineering in MXenes for sustainable electrochemical energy conversion and storage applications
abstract: Ever-increasing global energy requirements and environmental pollution have directed major research focus on developing sustainable energy conversion and energy storage technologies.

title: Dual-network bacterial cellulose-based separators with high wet strength and a dual ion transport mechanism for uniform lithium deposition
abstract: The double network significantly optimizes Li + transport through the synergistic effect of pore transport within the double network and polymer coordination mechanism, ensuring uniform deposition on the surface of the lithium metal anode.

title: Ru nanoparticles encapsulated in defective UiO-66 def for the hydrogenation of benzene rings in polyethylene terephthalate degraded chemicals
abstract: A Ru@UiO-66 def catalyst possesses prominent activity and selectivity in the hydrogenation of the benzene ring in PET-degraded DMT to DMCD under mild conditions.

title: Dual-regulation of magnetization and multi-interfacial strategies in MXene/CoNiM-800 (M = Fe/Cu/Mn/Zn) hierarchical structures for synergistic microwave absorption
abstract: Synthesis of MXene/CoNiM-800 (M = Fe/Cu/Mn/Zn) and microwave absorption mechanism.

title: High-efficiency organic–silicon heterojunction solar cells with high work function PEDOT:F-based hole-selective contacts
abstract: Yang and co-workers report a high-performance PEDOT:F-based hole-selective contact for silicon solar cells, achieving a record efficiency of 21.6% in organic–silicon heterojunction solar cells while demonstrating good environmental stability.

title: High surface area mesoporous carbon nanodendrites – detonation synthesis, characterization and use as a novel electrocatalyst support material
abstract: SEM images reveal the dendritic structure of MCNDs with high surface area, excellent porosity, and uniform distribution leading to outstanding performance with Pt catalysts based on MCNDs and making MCNDs a highly effective supporting material.

title: Green β-cyclodextrin exfoliated molybdenum sulphide based multifunctional poly(vinylidene fluoride) nanogenerators toward piezocatalytic water remediation
abstract: Green β-CD exfoliated MoS 2 stabilized in PVDF. PMCD (1 : 6) nanocomposite showed ∼94% polar phase, d 33 ∼ 88 pm V −1 , and ∼72 V output. Efficiently degraded RhB (69.2%), Diclofenac (71.4%), Ciprofloxacin (62.5%), and Cr( vi ) (53.5%) via piezocatalysis.

title: Conversion of stored thermochemical potential into high quality heat in a continuous flow reactor/heat exchanger
abstract: Continuous TCES reactor enables sustained high-temperature heat extraction, decoupling heat generation and utilization for scalable renewable energy storage.

title: Chemical engineering of triazine and β-ketoenamine units in covalent organic frameworks with synergistic effects for boosting C 2 H 2 and CO 2 separation
abstract: Triazine- and β-ketoenamine-based COFs achieve excellent C 2 H 2 /CO 2 separation performance via synergistic π–π stacking and charge transfer interactions.

title: Engineering Ru–N 4 single atom catalysts on Ni-MOF-derived porous graphitic carbon for pH universal water electrolysis
abstract: Ru SAC on Ni-MOF-derived porous carbon achieve pH-universal water splitting with low OER and HER overpotentials and cell voltages, delivering high activity, stability for efficient green hydrogen production.

title: Mechanistic insights and predictive screening of M@C 2 N catalysts for urea electrosynthesis from N 2 and CO 2
abstract: A descriptor-guided screening of single-atom M@C 2 N catalysts enables identification of Nb as a top candidate for ambient electrocatalytic urea synthesis from CO 2 and N 2 , with high activity and stability.

title: Engineering intense Ru–TiO 2 interaction for robust hydrogen oxidation reaction
abstract: A Ru/TiO 2 heterostructure electrocatalyst with intense Ru–TiO 2 interaction demonstrates high electrocatalytic performance up to 0.6 V ( vs. RHE) toward alkaline HOR.

title: Sunlight-mediated photocatalytic uranium extraction from seawater: a bioresistant heterojunction for highly selective uranium sensing and extraction with in vivo application
abstract: A new recyclable composite material has been developed for simultaneous uranium detection and adsorption, featuring red-emissive turn-on sensing, high adsorption capacity with fast kinetics, biofouling resistance, and biosensing capabilities.

title: Desired spontaneous corrosion via a highly hydrophilic porous layer for aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: We propose an approach for a stable zinc anode based on a highly hydrophilic porous zinc layer, which guides the in situ formation of uniformly distributed zinc hydroxide sulfate (ZHS) via unavoidable corrosion in the electrolyte.

title: Isomeric bipyridine-based covalent organic frameworks for efficient electrocatalytic nitrate reduction to ammonia
abstract: Isomeric bipyridine-based covalent organic frameworks incorporating metal ions within the sidewalls of ordered mesopores were developed for efficient electrocatalytic nitrate reduction to ammonia.

title: Gram-scale production of an Fe single atom catalyst and mass transfer enhancement in PEMFCs
abstract: A Fe-SA catalyst for the ORR in PEMFC is developed using a simple adsorption and annealing process. The large specific surface area (1450.7 m 2 g −1 ) and optimized MEA structure provide abundant active sites, leading to high PEMFC performance.

title: Evaluating the phase-dependent electrocatalytic activity of manganese phosphides for the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: To accomplish the mass production of hydrogen by electrochemical water splitting, it is essential to explore cost-effective, electrochemically active, and durable electrocatalysts for the hydrogen evolution reaction (HER).

title: Progress and prospects of high-pressure CO 2 electrocatalytic reduction
abstract: This review discusses the role of pressure intervention in regulating eCO 2 RR to produce C 1 and C 2+ products, the mutual influence and synergistic effects of pressure with other reaction microenvironments, CO and non-aqueous CO 2 electroreduction.

title: Optimization of the CoFe 2 O 4 electronic structure using reticulated expanded graphite to accelerate non-radical activation of peroxymonosulfate for efficient antibiotic degradation in wastewater
abstract: To tackle the persistent challenge of antibiotic pollutants in wastewater, an expanded graphite-supported CoFe 2 O 4 catalyst (CoFe 2 O 4 -EG) was synthesized via microwave expansion and co-precipitation methods.

title: Composites of isotropic and aligned semiconducting single-walled carbon nanotubes with conjugated polymers for air-processed thermoelectrics
abstract: Carbon-based thermoelectric composites are printed and subsequently aligned by hot rubbing. Aqueous p-doping in air via the proton-coupled electron transfer method yields stable power factors in inert atmosphere for a period of at least 60 hours.

title: Monte Carlo simulations of the temperature-dependent microstructure evolution of relaxor ferroelectric polymers
abstract: Our MC modeling provides a paradigm for the method development of complex relaxor ferroelectric and ferroelectric polymers.

title: Tailoring plasmonic coupling in black gold-decorated porous polymer membranes via all-in-one synthesis for enhanced photocatalytic peroxymonosulfate activation
abstract: Integrating gold nanoparticle synthesis in polymer solution and membrane fabrication via phase separation yields black gold membranes with tailored plasmonic coupling, enabling photocatalytic peroxymonosulfate activation for micropollutant oxidation.

title: A data-driven framework to accelerate the discovery of hybrid cathode materials for metal-based batteries
abstract: A data-driven, chemistry-agnostic inverse material design framework to accelerate the discovery of hybrid cathode materials for metal-based batteries.

title: rC14: engineering a new dual-function carbon allotrope for sustainable energy technologies under conventional and micro-strain conditions
abstract: rC14, a 2D dual-function carbon allotrope, shows high Na-ion capacity, fast ion diffusion, and excellent HER activity. Its performance remains stable under microstrain, making it a strong metal-free candidate for energy storage and conversion.

title: Mechanistic study of PFAS adsorption using a QPPTA linked viologen-modified covalent organic framework
abstract: VZ-COF was synthesized from a new aromatic tetraamine linker and Zincke salt. VZ-COF features hydrogen-bond acceptor sites, positively charged functional groups and hydrophobic regions that enhance its ability to adsorb PFAS.

title: The synergistic effect in a CoNiTaRuMn high-entropy alloy regulates Ru valence state for efficient hydrogen evolution
abstract: A strategy leveraging CoNiTaRuMn HEA element synergies promotes Ru hydroxylation (Ru 0 to Ru 4+ ), highlighting HEA valence – state regulation, providing active sites, and enhancing HER performance.

title: MXene gel solar thermal evaporator: a novel pathway for seawater desalination
abstract: This review explores the properties, design, and synthesis of MXene materials and analyzes the unique advantages of MXene-based composite gels with polymers, nanomaterials, and biomass in seawater desalination.

title: First-principles statistical investigation of thermodynamic behavior with excitonic effects in Mo 1− x W x Se 2 alloys through a data-driven workflow approach
abstract: We investigate the thermodynamic stability of the Mo 1− x W x Se 2 alloy at the atomistic level and introduce an innovative cost-effective protocol for predicting and characterizing its optoelectronic properties, explicitly incorporating excitonic effects.

title: Iron-anchored black phosphorus with a phosphate proton reservoir for industrial-current-density water oxidation
abstract: In situ -generated phosphate in the Fe/BP NS OER electrocatalyst acts as a proton reservoir, decoupling the proton-coupled electron transfer (PCET) and transforming the rate-determining step (RDS) from *O to *OOH into the *OOH deprotonation step.

title: B-site high valence cation co-doping boosts fast oxygen kinetics in a cobalt-free perovskite air electrode for reversible solid oxide cells
abstract: A B-site co-doping strategy by incorporating high valence Nb 5+ and Ta 5+ into a cobalt-free Bi 0.5 Sr 0.5 FeO 3− δ (BSF) air electrode to synergistically optimize the oxygen transport capacity and surface reactivity for RSOCs.

title: High-transparency, weather-resistant nanocomposite coatings from a shear-aligned 2D unit-cell-thick perovskite for wooden artifact preservation
abstract: A “brick-and-mortar” PNS-AR nanocomposite coating enhances wooden artifact preservation. Incorporating Ca 2 Nb 3 O 10 nanosheets into acrylic resin boosts toughness, strength, and water resistance, ensuring durable protection.

title: Zinc alloy dynamic windows with excellent cyclability and thermal stability
abstract: Alloying zinc with tin and nickel enhances the thermal stability and cycling durability of reversible metal electrodeposition windows, enabling over 11 000 cycles and prolonged bistability at 85 °C.

title: Reproducible synthesis of α-MgAgSb with optimized carrier transport for low-temperature thermoelectric applications
abstract: Reproducible synthesis of α-MgAgSb enables Te-free thermoelectrics with zT = 0.84 at room temperature and 1.3 at 500 K, achieved via optimized mobility, phase purity, and annealing-driven stability.

title: Two-channel sodium-ion conducting polymer electrolytes based on perfluorinated linear hetero-oligomers prepared by enzymatic synthesis and NaTFSI
abstract: New solvent-free polymer electrolytes are proposed based on a linear perfluorinated copolymer synthesized by enzymatic methods. The system is biphasic and exhibits two distinct conductivity pathways for long-range Na + -cation migration.

title: Structural complexity in a highly reversible “anion-redox” cathode
abstract: Li-rich cathodes with an O2-type layer stacking offer high gravimetric capacities and fast charge–discharge rates, and are structurally more stable with respect to transition metal migration than O3-type Li-rich cathodes.

title: Synthesis of carbon quantum dots decorated titanium disilicide: a novel hybrid solar-driven photocatalyst for sustainable wastewater treatment
abstract: TiSi 2 /CQD, a novel hybrid solar-driven photocatalyst platform with low toxicity, narrow bandgap, high cyclic reusability and visible light activity for efficient photodegradtaion of water pollutants.

title: A local proton-transport promoter for industrial CO 2 electroreduction to multicarbon products
abstract: ex and in situ Multi-scale proton transport studied using density functional theory, molecular dynamics, ex situ four-probe and in situ SECM could not only address the pH effect on thermodynamics and kinetics, but also clarify the proton transport mechanism for the industrial-level eCO 2 RR.

title: Ball milling route for stable and high-performance FAPbI 3 perovskite single crystals
abstract: Despite their immense promise for next-generation photovoltaics and photodetectors, formamidinium lead triiodide (FAPbI 3 ) perovskite single crystals face a critical hurdle: their inherent instability.

title: Highly efficient atomic-scale design of CaF 2 for ultrafast fluoride-ion conduction
abstract: Through K-ion doping and grain boundary regulation, the fluoride ion conductivity of CaF 2 has been significantly enhanced, and its operating temperature has been reduced from 827 °C to 15 °C.

title: Extending the shelf-life of precursor solutions and inhibiting light-induced oxidation of iodides for achieving highly efficient and durable perovskite solar cells
abstract: A functional molecule sodium thiosulfate (ST) was added to extend the shelf-life of precursor solutions and improve the stability of perovskite films by inhibiting the oxidation of iodide ions, ultimately achieving high efficiency and durable PSCs.

title: Significance of oxophilic site in alkaline hydrogen evolution and oxidation reaction: Is it playing the same role?
abstract: Alkaline HER involves the oxophilic site speeding up the adsorption and dissociation of the H–OH molecule, ensuring a consistent proton supply, while *OH adsorption is crucial for HOR. This concept needs urgent attention and detailed discussion.

title: Regulating oxygen vacancy distribution in perovskites via A-site cation engineering for water oxidation
abstract: Perovskite oxides with different A-site cations regulate the distribution of oxygen vacancies and lead to distinct surface reconstructions and catalytic mechanisms during the OER.

title: Visible-light H 2 evolution using dye-sensitized TiO 2 : effects of physicochemical properties of TiO 2 on excited carrier dynamics and activity
abstract: Back electron transfer (BET) and dye aggregation govern H 2 evolution in Ru dye-sensitized TiO 2 , where crystal phase and surface area dictate interfacial dynamics. Efficient H 2 production requires both BET suppression and optimal dye arrangement.

title: Recent advances in interface engineering of iron triad metal phosphide electrocatalysts towards enhanced hydrogen evolution reaction performance
abstract: This review explores iron triad metal phosphide HER electrocatalysts with interface engineering, covering enhancement mechanisms, interface configurations, and research progress, while highlighting challenges and future directions.

title: Atomic-scale insights into air electrode degradation after 10 years of fuel cell stack operation
abstract: Atomic scale transmission electron microscopy on the degradation products at the electrode interface of a solid oxide fuel cell that operated for nearly 10 years.

title: Chain dynamics and quasi-melting transitions in mixed-halide layered perovskites by NMR spectroscopy
abstract: NMR spectroscopy quantifies how the alkyl chain dynamics change in layered perovskites across the quasi-melting phase transitions. The transitions are suppressed in mixed-halide compositions, which show intermediate dynamics between the end-members.

title: A nitrogen-rich fully conjugated covalent organic framework as a high-performance anode material for hybrid lithium-ion capacitors
abstract: A nitrogen-rich, fully conjugated TAB-COF anode is developed for hybrid lithium-ion capacitors. It achieves an exceptional discharge capacity via a unique 21-electron storage mechanism, as confirmed through experimental and theoretical approaches.

title: Regulating the interfacial stability of aqueous zinc-ion batteries via the introduction of organic functional molecules
abstract: Organic materials are promising regulators for stabilizing zinc anodes in AZIBs. This review highlights recent advances in organic interphases and electrolyte additives, explaining how organic materials regulate interfacial stability.

title: Bidentate coordination-induced trap passivation and phase stability in perovskite solar cells via ionic liquid engineering
abstract: The dicyanamide anion (DCA) stabilizes perovskite films by suppressing trap formation and δ-phase degradation, leading to enhanced phase integrity under operation. Devices exhibit improved PCE, moisture resistance, and long-term stability.

title: Three birds with one stone: rational design of organic–inorganic hybrid nanoclusters for high-performance liquid-like coatings
abstract: Achieving transparent polymer coatings that combine hardness and flexibility is fundamentally limited by the filler-induced trade-off between mechanical reinforcement and optical clarity.

title: Methods for accurate and rapid determination of purity of battery-grade silicon
abstract: Two independent methods based on TGA and XRD were developed to provide accurate determination of Si content, which is vital for the use of Si in battery anodes.

title: Multiple-crosslinking-reinforced ionogel electrolytes for safe and high-performance quasi-solid-state lithium metal batteries
abstract: The multiple-crosslinking ionogel electrolyte, combined with regulated Li + environments, enabled rapid Li + conductive pathways and high-performance quasi-solid-state lithium metal batteries.

title: Enhancing ionic conductivity and electrochemical stability via an ultrathin coating for interfacial side reaction suppression
abstract: A free radical polymerization guided interface modification strategy is leveraged to prevent side reactions between the garnet electrolyte fillers and plastic crystals, thus enabling superior performance to be attained in composite solid electrolyte.

title: Immiscible alloys as high-capacity and ultra-stable anodes for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Immiscible alloys with interlocking structures were synthesized at moderate temperatures for use as anodes in sodium-ion batteries (SIBs), showing high capacity and remarkable long-term cycling stability.

title: Flexible self-supporting CoNi alloy-doped carbon nanofibers with uniformly dispersed nanoparticles: a 3D host for stable lithium metal anodes
abstract: A flexible carbon nanofiber host with CoNi alloy nanoparticles offers abundant lithiophilic sites for uniform Li deposition and dendrite-free cycling, with experimental and DFT analyses revealing the structural origin of its superior stability.

title: Enhanced photo-Fenton catalysis via bandgap engineering of metalloporphyrin-based covalent organic framework shells on bimetallic metal–organic frameworks: accelerating Fe( iii )/Fe( ii ) loop activation
abstract: A metallized porphyrin-based COF shell was constructed on bimetal based NH 2 -Cu/Fe-MIL-101 nanoparticles, which demonstrates exceptional photo-Fenton catalytic efficiency and excellent stability.

title: Structurally stable cellulose nanofiber-based phase change aerogels for thermal management and acoustic insulation
abstract: Multifunctional cellulose nanofiber-based phase change composite aerogels (CPCAs) with enhanced conductivity, high thermal energy storage, and effective sound absorption, offering a promising solution for energy-efficient building applications.

title: Next-generation perovskite-metal–organic framework (MOF) hybrids in photoelectrochemical water splitting: a path to green hydrogen solutions
abstract: This review highlights advances in perovskite-MOF hybrids for PEC water splitting, offering design guidelines for high-performance photoelectrodes while emphasising critical factors that affect their photoelectrochemical activity and application.

title: A triple-functional Co 2 SnO 4 -enabled S-scheme heterojunction with photothermal promotion for efficient solar-driven hydrogen evolution
abstract: An engineered Co 2 SnO 4 @ZnIn 2 S 4 S-scheme heterojunction boosts photocatalytic H 2 production via enhanced charge separation, NIR photothermal conversion and catalytic enhancement.

title: Precisely controlling the nanostructure of bimetallic Mg/Zn MOFs to construct a high-performance material for supercapacitors and sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Precisely adjusting the specific surface area, mesoporous, and combining the microporous structure of carbon materials are the keys to constructing excellent performance materials for supercapacitors and sodium-ion batteries (SIBs).

title: Engineering robust oxygen vacancies towards efficient toluene purification: coupling crystal facets with Mn–Ce solid solution
abstract: Coupling the solid solution structure with high energy crystal facets exposure greatly enriches the oxygen defect structure of the 3Ce1Mn catalyst, achieving superior catalytic performance for toluene purification ( T 90 = 195 °C).

title: Rational engineering of triple-hierarchical pores in carbon nanospheres for superior organic supercapacitive storage
abstract: Triple-hierarchical pore carbon nanospheres, engineered using a dual-control strategy, achieve superior supercapacitor performance in organic electrolytes.

title: Ultrafast synthesis of porous Fe 3 C/carbon hybrid materials via a carbothermal shock reactor for advanced energy storage applications
abstract: Template-assisted pyrolysis enables the synthesis of Fe-based carbonaceous materials with a defined morphology and structure from iron–carbon precursors.

title: Fabricating stable CoNi active sites and elucidating the role of N in enhancing hydrogenation and dehydrogenation of MgH 2
abstract: N-doped CNTs anchored with stable CoNi nanoparticles significantly enhance MgH₂ hydrogen storage by accelerating kinetics via electron transfer that weakens both Mg–H and H–H bonds.

title: The role of alkali doping in enhancing bulk and interface properties of Cu 2 CdSnS 4 solar cells
abstract: Systematic alkali dopant selection tunes Cu 2 CdSnS 4 thin film properties, revealing suppressed recombination, optimal charge transport, and improved solar cell performance.

title: Preparation and application of the high-performance sodium-ion battery cathode material Na 3.6 Fe 2.6 (PO 4 ) 1.6 P 2 O 7 @CNT with dual-carbon coating
abstract: The dual-carbon structure maximizes the material's electronic conductivity, improves electron transmission at high current densities, enhances rate performance, and boosts cycle stability at elevated rates.

title: Two-dimensional conductive mesopore engineering of ultrahigh content covalent sulfur-doped carbon for superior sodium storage
abstract: 2D conductive mesoporous engineering activates C–S bonds and boosts kinetics in >20 wt% sulfur-doped carbon (USC), enabling a USC@Ti 3 C 2 heterostructure with ultrahigh capacity and exceptional rate performance.

title: Molecular anchoring induced charge transfer pathway conversion from p–n to S-scheme heterojunctions for boosting photocatalytic hydrogen evolution and N 2 fixation
abstract: An amino molecular layer acts as a heterojunction conversion switch to transform Cu 2 O/T-N from a p–n to an S-scheme heterojunction, which can induce outstanding photocatalytic hydrogen production and N 2 fixation performance.

title: Graphitic carbon cage structure encapsulating cobalt nanoparticles in nitrogen-doped biomass-derived carbon materials enables high-performance sodium–sulfur batteries
abstract: A biomass-derived porous carbon host, PN-1M-Co, fabricated from waste pine nut shells and K 3 [Co(CN) 6 ], possesses a graphitic cage structure that mitigates volume expansion, suppresses the shuttle effect, and promotes rapid one-step sulfur conversion.

title: Decoding lithium's subtle phase stability with a machine learning force field
abstract: Advanced machine learning force field enables accurate modeling of quantum and anharmonic effects in Li metal, revealing subtle phase equilibria among bcc-, fcc- and 9R-Li structures with implications for phase purity control in battery applications.

title: Bridged Ov–Ru–O–Co coordination induced by Co 2+ δ substitution in Co/RuO 2 catalysts for enhanced alkaline hydrogen and oxygen evolution reactions
abstract: A nanoporous Co 2+ δ -incorporated RuO 2 (Co/RuO 2 -1/50) catalyst, featuring unique Ov–Ru–O–Co coordination, achieved an impressive HER/OER performance (26/243 mV@10 mA cm −1 , 54/88 mV dec −1 ) with superior stability for water electrolysis.

title: Synergistically enhanced electronic modulation in trimetallic Cu–Co–Mo-based heterostructured nanomaterials for green H 2 production via efficient alkaline electrolysis
abstract: Efficient overall water electrolysis of fabricated symmetrical alkaline electrolyzers for H 2 production using Cu, Co and Mo-based bifunctional electrocatalysts as anodes and cathodes.

title: Facile engineering of CoS/rGO heterostructures on carbon cloth for efficient all-pH hydrogen evolution reaction and alkaline water electrolysis
abstract: A three-dimensional CoS/rGO@CC electrocatalyst was developed via a two-step electrodeposition, demonstrating excellent bifunctional activity and stability, with notable HER performance across various pH levels.

title: An unsaturated bond strategy in covalent organic frameworks for enhanced simultaneous photocatalytic uranium recovery and hydrogen peroxide production
abstract: Azo functionalized covalent organic frameworks for efficient photocatalytic uranium extraction and simultaneously enhanced H 2 O 2 photosynthesis.

title: Sustainable materials for organic photovoltaic devices towards transient electronics in digital agriculture
abstract: With the growing demand for sustainable energy solutions, particularly for energy supply in transient sensor systems, this study focused on environmentally friendly materials in organic photovoltaics (OPV) and their key photovoltaic parameters.

title: Polydopamine-mediated hydrogen bond network promotes hole extraction in BiVO 4 photoanodes for efficient photoelectrochemical water oxidation
abstract: Mussel-inspired polydopamine (PDA) significantly enhances photogenerated hole extraction efficiency in BiVO 4 through the synergistic effects of short-range hydrogen bond networks and optimized interfacial electric fields.

title: Versatile roles of functionalized fullerenes in perovskite solar cells
abstract: Fullerene materials have been extensively utilized in a variety of types of perovskite solar cells, primarily employed in versatile roles, performing multiple functions to increase efficiency and stability of devices significantly.

title: Simultaneously boosting electrical and thermal transport properties of CuGaTe 2 through XCl 2 (X = Cd, Zn) doping-driven band and defect engineering
abstract: The structure of CuGaTe 2 has been optimized by the introduction of XCl 2 (X = Cd, Zn), which creates acceptor defects and nanopores. This synergistically improves the thermoelectric and mechanical properties.

title: Facet-dependent photocatalytic reduction of nitroaromatics using tailored SrTiO 3 crystals: mechanism and reactivity enhancement
abstract: This work presents a selective photocatalytic reduction of nitroaromatics to amines on tailored SrTiO 3 crystals.

title: BiBERTa: a self-supervised framework for accelerating the discovery of stable organic photovoltaic materials
abstract: A novel bi-encoder architecture RoBERTa framework (BiBERTa) integrates self-supervised pretraining to accelerate the discovery of high-performance organic photovoltaic materials with limited experimental data (MAE = 1.67%).

title: Dual-phase modulation via Mo doping and Li 3 PO 4 coating for stabilized LiNi 0.9 Mn 0.07 Co 0.02 Al 0.01 O 2 cathodes in high-energy lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Li 3 PO 4 coated Mo-doped high-nickel (≥90%) cathodes material is newly suggested. The synergistic influence of doping and coating is specifically analyzed.

title: Recent advances and future directions of CdIn 2 S 4 -based photocatalysts: properties, synthesis, and modifications for energy and environmental applications
abstract: CdIn 2 S 4 -based photocatalysts are promising in many energy and environmental applications such as hydrogen production, selective oxidation of organics, and some dual-functional systems.

title: Tuning the electrochemical stability of carbon based single-atom structures via doping: trade-off between electrosorption/leaching behavior
abstract: The performance of single-atom catalysts in electrocatalytic processes can be effectively enhanced through the doping of tailored asymmetric coordination environments.

title: Band gap and photo charge carrier tailoring in zirconium doped carbon nitride using ZrCl 4 –DMF–melamine for photocatalytic degradation of rhodamine B
abstract: Improving the performance of carbon nitride (CN) photocatalysts in photocatalytic degradation applications involves optimizing their morphology, electronic properties, and optical characteristics.

title: Ni/Zn-ZIF-derived bamboo-like N-doped carbon nanotubes encapsulating Ni-based chalcogenides as efficient bifunctional electrocatalysts for hydrogen production and photovoltaics
abstract: Constructing efficient multi-active sites is vital for the next generation electrocatalysts. Here, Ni-based chalcogenides integrated with adjacent Ni–N x sites exhibit remarkable activity for hydrogen evolution and iodine reduction reactions.

title: Synergistic upcycling of Pt/Pd and graphite from city mines for highly efficient seawater hydrogen evolution catalysis
abstract: A Pt/Pd@SOG electrocatalyst prepared from city mines was synthesized for the HER in seawater.

title: An ultralight porous carbon scaffold for anode-free lithium metal batteries
abstract: A free-standing thin sheet of carbon grapes (TSCG) is used as a replacement for copper in anode-free lithium metal batteries (ALMBs). The results show that TSCG functions effectively both as a current collector and as a lithium reservoir.

title: Nitrogen vacancies rich C 3 N 4 supported PdCo catalyst for selective photocatalytic CO 2 reduction to C 2 H 4
abstract: This study shows that dual nitrogen vacancies in H 2 CN create a strong built-in electric field, enhancing charge separation. Furthermore, PdCo alloy modification further stabilizes C 1 intermediates and promotes hydrogenation reactions.

title: The synergistic effect of Ni–NiMo 4 N 5 heterointerface construction and Fe-doping enables active and durable alkaline water splitting at industrial current density
abstract: The synergistic effect of heterogeneous interfaces and doping optimizes electronic structure and accelerates charge transfer, significantly boosting alkaline water electrolysis performance.

title: Regulation of excited-state dynamics via cyano-functionalization in fused aromatic unit-based conjugated polymers for efficient photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production from air and water
abstract: The cyano-functionalization strategy is proposed to regulate the excited-state dynamics of fused aromatic unit-based conjugated polymers, thus exhibiting excellent performance in photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production from air and pure water.

title: Lattice oxygen activation through reconstruction of transition metal fluoride for efficient ampere-level current density oxygen evolution
abstract: NiF 2 /FeF x was synthesized via the co-precipitation method. The formation of multi-component fluorides promotes structural reconstruction and increases the participation of lattice oxygen, thereby improving the oxygen evolution reaction activity.

title: Breaking interdisciplinary barriers in solid-state battery research: BatteryAgent for multifaceted analysis
abstract: Batteryagent, an LLM-driven multi-agent system, unifies fragmented optimization in ASSB research through multifaceted analysis, delivering holistic insights and actionable strategies to accelerate next-generation energy-dense ASSB development.

title: An in situ polymerized electrolyte layer via frustrated Lewis pairs enables aqueous Zn metal batteries with an ultrahigh accumulated capacity of 12 A h cm −2
abstract: Zn 2+ /Zn as a Lewis acid–base pair catalyzes AMPS polymerization into a PAMPS SEI layer for high-capacity zinc deposition.

title: Enhancing Lewis acidic/basic activities of silicon anodes via amine-bridged polymeric copper phthalocyanines for high-performance lithium-ion batteries
abstract: CuPPc-NH@Si accelerates Li + transport, forms LiF-rich SEI, and strengthens electrodes, boosting full-cell performance with LFP and NCM811.

title: A bio-inspired, cross-scale, hierarchical MXene flame retardant nanocoating for reliable, ultrafast, ultralong fire warning
abstract: A cross-scale and hierarchical MXene nanocoating is developed to address multiple performance trade-offs, endowing the nanocoating with mechanical robustness, flame retardancy, ultrafast fire alarm response, and an ultralong alarm duration.

title: New insight into designing a thick-sintered cathode for Li-ion batteries: the impact of excess lithium in LiCoO 2 on its electrode performance
abstract: The electrochemical performance of a thickly and densely sintered LiCoO 2 cathode for Li-ion batteries can be drastically improved by adding excess Li. The excess Li enhances disk.

title: 3D-printed lattice structures for thermoelectric devices – a review
abstract: Lattice structures could assist in the decoupling of competing thermoelectric properties, while additive manufacturing techniques could simplify the fabrication process of thermoelectric devices and provide flexibility in design.

title: Dual-protection strategy for superior stability and performance of zinc powder-based anodes in aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: The anode features a dual-protection strategy with an rGO encapsulation layer that suppresses dendrite growth and side reactions and a PAA binder that ensures uniform zinc-ion deposition and prevents dissolution through hydrogen bonding.

title: Ultrathin high-entropy alloy nanowires as a bi-functional catalyst for the hydrogen evolution reaction and methanol oxidation reaction
abstract: Ultrathin Pt-based high entropy alloy nanowires are synthesized through a simple coreduction method, exhibiting abundant exposed active sites and outstanding catalytic activity in both alkaline MOR and HER processes.

title: Prelithiation of lithium-ion batteries towards commercialization: concepts, challenges, and application prospects
abstract: This review systematically summarizes the recent progress in prelithiation technology and discusses their commercial feasibility.

title: Semi-interpenetrating network polymer electrolytes with increased mechanical robustness and ionic conductivity for stable lithium metal batteries
abstract: A semi-IPN gel polymer electrolyte (CP-PE) with a PE skeleton is developed via crosslinked PEGDA. It exhibits excellent stability, enabling a Li|CP-PE|LiFePO 4 cell to maintain 96.2% capacity retention and 99.9% CE over 500 cycles at 0.5C.

title: Evoking piezo-catalytic activity in SrTiO 3 perovskite via lattice strain
abstract: Piezo-catalysis is evoked by introducing lattice strain through oxygen vacancies in the SrTiO 3 perovskite. This provides an unsophisticated strategy for realizing piezoelectricity and improving the piezo-catalysis of centrosymmetric materials.

title: Improved high-temperature energy storage properties of PEI-based films via constructing an electric potential well
abstract: The addition of montmorillonite–chitosan (CMC) into polyetherimide (PEI) enhances the deep electron trap effect in x %CMC/PEI films, thereby effectively improving high-temperature energy storage properties.

title: Electrocatalyst design strategies towards high performance anion-exchange membrane-based direct ammonia fuel cells
abstract: Direct ammonia fuel cells (DAFCs) with anion-exchange membranes (AEMs) are considered a valuable contributor to a carbon-neutral clean energy society, benefitting from the existence of long-established ammonia infrastructure.

title: Harnessing self-powered and photoresponsive biomechanical activity sensors by exploring the piezo-phototronic effect in lead-free layered halide perovskite/PVDF composites
abstract: Harnessing self-powered and photoresponsive biomechanical activity sensors by exploring the piezo-phototronic effect in lead-free layered halide perovskite/PVDF composites.

title: Efficient anti-icing/deicing via photothermal-wind synergistic effects on femtosecond laser-induced superhydrophobic graphene
abstract: A high-efficiency energy utilization strategy based on a photothermal-wind synergistic effect combined with superhydrophobicity has been proposed for anti-icing/deicing.

title: High entropy spinel oxide (Ni 0.2 Co 0.2 Zn 0.2 Cu 0.2 Mg 0.2 )Fe 2 O 4 nanofibers for efficient oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Developing efficient oxygen evolution reaction (OER) catalysts is urgent for the production of clean hydrogen energy.

title: Hydrogen-bonding-enhanced green wearable sensors with high generation performance and low Young's modulus
abstract: A conceptual graph of the hydrogen-bonding-enhanced green wearable sensors with high generation performance and low Young's modulus.

title: High-entropy structure design of transition metal dichalcogenides for improved electromagnetic wave absorption performance
abstract: High-entropy structure design has great potential in improving the electromagnetic wave absorption properties of TMDs.

title: Lattice-trapping synthesis enhances fixation of As( v ) in As@zeolite P
abstract: We present a novel As@zeolite P-based fixative derived from coal gasification slag using a lattice confinement strategy. The fixative demonstrated immobilization of arsenic (As( v )) and the reuse of waste. This is an environmentally friendly strategy for both pollution control and waste management.

title: Spray-drying synthesis of high-performance Na 4 MnCr(PO 4 ) 3 for sodium-ion batteries via a CNT-induced conductive network and optimized interface kinetics
abstract: Carbon nanotube (CNT)-modified Na 4 MnCr(PO 4 ) 3 /C cathode with excellent sodium storage performance was synthesized via a scalable spray-drying method. CNT constructed an efficient electron-conducting network, significantly reducing charge transfer resistance and optimizing interfacial kinetics.

title: Unlocking the mechanism of anharmonic lattice dynamics in ionic conductor β-Li 3 VO 4
abstract: Anharmonic lattice dynamics (ALD) has proven to be a promising approach for the development of advanced superionic conductors for solid-state batteries.

title: Fluorination of single-crystal copper electrodes in fluoride-ion batteries
abstract: We investigated the fluorination process of Cu in fluoride-ion batteries under well-controlled conditions using single-crystal model electrodes, analyzed by electron microscopy.

title: Confined-space synthesis of Zr-, Ti-, and Ti–Zr-oxocluster-based hybrid nanoparticles as catalysts for H 2 O 2 -mediated oxidations
abstract: Hybrid organic–inorganic nanoparticles were synthesized by incorporating Ti, Zr or Ti–Zr methacrylate-functionalised oxoclusters into a PMMA matrix through free-radical polymerisation within oil-in-water miniemulsion droplets.

title: Mesostructure control in 3D-printed monoliths using industry-grade SiO 2 powder for CO 2 capture
abstract: Direct ink writing (DIW) enables the fabrication of silica monoliths with hierarchical porosity, where the mesoporosity can be tuned by the selection of silica fillers and binders, offering potential for liquid and gas adsorption applications.

title: Synthesis challenges, thermodynamic stability, and growth kinetics of La–Si–P ternary compounds
abstract: Our MD simulations indicate that the rapid formation of a Si-substituted LaP crystalline phase is a major barrier to the synthesis of La–Si–P crystals.

title: Controlled crystal orientation and reduced lattice distortion with a cystamine dihydrochloride spacer for efficient and stable 2D/3D perovskite solar cells
abstract: A novel spacer cation, cystamine dihydrochloride, is employed to build DJ 2D/3D perovskites. It regulates crystal orientation and reduces residual strain in perovskite. The champion device achieves an efficiency of 24.54% with excellent stability.

title: Constructing dual silsesquioxane-crosslinked network channels in hybrid polymer membranes for dendrite-free and durable high-power LMBs
abstract: Here, we reported a novel membrane engineering strategy that dual silsesquioxane-crosslinked polymer networks on nanofibers for in situ controlled manipulation of SEI chemistry, and our work is an remarkable step in high-performance LMBs.

title: Thiol functionalized metal–organic framework for efficient silver adsorption and removal from aqueous solution
abstract: The modulator chemistry is used to develop novel thiol-functionalized MOF-808 that exhibits high Ag + adsorption capacity (365 mg g −1 ) with rapid kinetics (<10 min). The MOF effectively separates Ag + /Pb 2+ from Al 3+ , under acidic conditions.

title: An optimized MnO 2 photocathode by doping engineering for high capacity and stability photo-assisted zinc-ion batteries
abstract: A high-performance PAZIB of an Al-doped MnO 2 (Al-MnO 2 ) photocathode is designed with high capacity (526 mA h g −1 at 0.2 A g −1 ) and excellent stability (96% capacity retention over 1000 cycles at 1 A g −1 ) under illumination.

title: Surface-modified BaTiO 3 as a functional filler in poly(ethylene oxide)-based solid polymer electrolytes for lithium-metal batteries
abstract: A novel PEO-based solid polymer electrolyte with a surface-modified BaTiO 3 functional filler shows fast lithium transport, stable interfacial characteristics and excellent long-term stability in lithium metal batteries.

title: A MoO x -incorporated RuAu composite electrocatalyst for the hydrogen evolution reaction in proton exchange membrane water electrolysis
abstract: The electrocatalytic activity of RuAu for the hydrogen evolution reaction is improved by the formation of a composite with MoO x through electrodeposition.

title: A design strategy of high-activity swarm mobile nanocatalysts for hydrogenolysis of complex N -benzyl compounds via anchoring–confinement synergy
abstract: A design strategy aims to promote catalytic efficiency by enhancing the collision probability between active sites and complex reactants or reaction intermediates.

title: Bonding mediated coupling of electron band mass and phonon group velocity: an effective strategy to improve the thermoelectric performance of solid solutions
abstract: A judicious choice of alloying and doping elements can significantly enhance thermoelectric performance. This is due to the simultaneous effects of band flattening, phonon softening and lower scattering that occur due to the chemical substitution.

title: Cu dopants as electron buffers for stabilizing Ru-based active layers for hydrogen evolution
abstract: Cu-doped RuO 2 coatings on titanium felt substrates are prepared by heat treatment and the resulting material has a hydrophilic nanorod structure.

title: Optimized micro-silicon structure enabling ultrahigh initial coulombic efficiency and fast reaction kinetics for advanced lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Germanium-doped micro-silicon anodes for high-performance lithium-ion batteries: enhanced conductivity and optimized Li + diffusion yield a high ICE (95%) and stable cycling.

title: Potassium batteries for low temperature applications using high energy density organic cathodes
abstract: Organic polymer cathodes derived from triquinoyl and tetraaminophenazine demonstrated record-high energy densities of 800–950 W h kg −1 in potassium batteries and efficient operation with minimal capacity decay at low temperatures reaching −55 °C.

title: Design of novel bismaleimide-based single-ion conducting hybrid electrolytes for high ionic conductivity and extended cycling stability lithium-metal batteries
abstract: A strategy is proposed to design novel POSS hybrid porous crosslinked bis(maleimide)-based single-ion conductor electrolytes with high ionic conductivity and superior t Li + . A highly stable SEI enhanced by POSS is revealed on the lithium electrode.

title: Hierarchically structured Co@Fe (SA) -MoO/MoP electrocatalyst with tuned electronic states for thermodynamically optimized hydrogen adsorption in chloride-rich media
abstract: Fe (SA) -MoO/MoP heterojunctions on Co nanorods boost hydrogen production via d-band tuning, enhancing HER/OER with low overpotentials (57/290 mV) and high stability (95/97% after 40 h), showing excellent bifunctional durability for water splitting.

title: A superelastic polymeric aerogel with ultrahigh fire resistance for thermal insulation in a harsh environment
abstract: A superelastic polymeric aerogel with ultrahigh fire-resistance (up to 1300 °C) was synthesized using a novel chemical crosslinking strategy. Superior mechanical properties and low release of combustion heat and smoke were realized.

title: Plasmon-induced hot electrons trigger polymerization reactions on the surface of plasmonic nanocrystals at low temperatures
abstract: Hot electron-induced polymerization on plasmonic nanocrystal surfaces is less sensitive to changes in reaction temperature than other chemical reactions, making it feasible to form polymers on the surface of nanocrystals at low temperatures (−12 °C).

title: Advanced CIGS-mesoporous TiO 2 hybrid photocathode functionalized with cobalt quaterpyridine for solar-driven CO 2 reduction
abstract: A Cu(In,Ga)S 2 -based hybrid photocathode protected by a protective layer and functionalized by a Co molecular complexes reduces CO 2 in water with high rate and selectivity.

title: In situ formed nano-interlayer enables robust interface bonding in efficient Bi 2 Te 3 -based thermoelectric modules
abstract: Scalable magnetron-sputtered thermoelectric modules exhibit robust interfacial bonding and ultralow contact resistivity through spontaneously formed nano-interlayers.

title: Light-induced surface defects in anodic TiO 2 nanotubes as trapping centers for single Pt atoms, a co-catalyst for enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: The amorphous nature of TiO 2 nanotubes (TNT) enables the formation of air-stable light-induced surface defects, facilitating reactive Pt SAs deposition. When crystallized, these TNT with surface-anchored Pt SA reach a significant H 2 production rate.

title: Boosting the lithium transport in phase-change polymer electrolytes towards stable cycling lithium metal batteries with thermal robustness
abstract: The PCL-based electrolyte employs a semi-vehicle ion transport mechanism to achieve continuous Li + migration across phase transitions, while demonstrating excellent thermal robustness to buffer temperature changes and delay thermal runaway.

title: Oxime organic cathode materials for long-lifespan lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Oxime organic electrode materials with reduced solubility achieve excellent cycling stability for 12 000 cycles.

title: Engineering of hierarchical Z-scheme ZnSe/Fe 2 O 3 heterojunction cubic nanocages for enhanced CO 2 to CO photoconversion
abstract: Z-scheme ZnSe/Fe 2 O 3 heterojunction cubic nanocages were prepared by immobilizing ZnSe nanoparticles on the prepared Fe 2 O 3 cubic nanocages.The optimized composite exhibited an excellent CO 2 photoreduction performance compared to the pristine ZnSe.

title: An etching-coordination strategy to construct yolk–shell structured ZIF/Fe–tannic acid composites for enhancing photothermal catalytic CO 2 cycloaddition
abstract: ZIF/Fe–TA-10 with a yolk–shell structure was fabricated via an etching-coordination strategy to promote photothermal catalytic CO 2 cycloaddition.

title: Synchronous differential orientation of liquid crystal elastomers based on dual dynamic covalent bonds
abstract: A synchronous differential orientation strategy was proposed for the fabrication of programmable liquid crystal elastomers with the aid of dual dynamic covalent bonds, which are composed of dynamic boronic ester bonds and siloxane bonds.

title: Synergistic improvement of pH-universal hydrogen evolution through B, N dual-doped Mo 2 C
abstract: This paper describes a fast and efficient method for preparing molybdenum carbide (Mo 2 C) using microwave plasma chemical vapor deposition (MPCVD) with good performance at all pH.

title: Bioinspired poly(catechol sulfide) as organic cathode materials for lithium metal batteries
abstract: Novel bio-inspired poly(catechol sulfide) PCS copolymers with mono- and dual-redox properties are proposed for application as organic cathode materials in lithium metal batteries.

title: Strain and composition engineering of excited-state carrier recombination dynamics in GaAs/GaP and GaAs/AlAs superlattices: insights from time-domain nonadiabatic molecular dynamics simulations
abstract: Strain engineering in GaAs/GaP and GaAs/AlAs superlattices enables carrier lifetime tuning from picosecond to nanosecond levels, supporting applications such as photovoltaic cells, photodetectors, and light-emitting diodes.

title: Gd doping enabling a record zT value of 1.65 at 823 K in Zintl-phase BaCu 2 Te 2
abstract: A dual-strategy approach, combining process optimization and carrier concentration tuning, significantly enhances the thermoelectric properties of Zintl-phase BaCu 2 Te 2 . A record zT value of 1.65 has been achieved for Ba 0.96 Gd 0.04 Cu 2 Te 2 .

title: Correction: Atomic-scale structure and thermoelectric properties in medium-entropy PbSnTeSe alloy
abstract: Correction for ‘Atomic-scale structure and thermoelectric properties in medium-entropy PbSnTeSe alloy’ by Shuwei Zhang et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2024, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA05952B.

title: Emerging chalcogenide based van der Waals heterostructures for ultrathin excitonic solar cells with enhanced photo-conversion efficiency
abstract: The heterostructures formed by both 1T and 2H combinations of ZrSe 2 and HfSe 2 have been theoretically demonstrated using density functional theory calculations.

title: Defect engineering and opening of the ion tracks in the swift heavy ion irradiated thin films of bismuth vanadate: impact on the oxygen evolution reaction for solar water splitting
authors: 'Marko Jelić', 'Zoran Jovanović', 'Ekaterina Korneeva', 'Nina Daneu', 'Suraj Gupta', 'Jacques O'Connell', 'Tatiana Vershinina', 'Nikita Kirilkin', 'Oleg Orelovich', 'Ivana Stojković Simatović', 'Vladimir Skuratov', 'Sonja Jovanović'
abstract: Swift heavy ions were utilized to engineer the defect landscape and structural and electronic properties and to enhance the PEC performance of BiVO 4 .

title: A review of enhancement strategies for pyrocatalysis of perovskite oxides and their applications
abstract: In this review, strategies to enhance the pyrocatalytic performance of perovskite oxides are highlighted. The applications of perovskite oxide pyrocatalysis are then summarized. Finally, the outlook for perovskite oxide pyrocatalysis are presented.

title: Controlled synthesis of alkylthio-substituted poly(thienylene vinylene) and its carbon nanotube composites for enhanced thermoelectric performance
abstract: Semiconducting polymers are lightweight, low cost, and easy to manufacture, making them promising candidates for application in electronic devices.

title: A cucurbit[6]uril-based supramolecular framework material for highly effective iodine adsorption
abstract: A novel supramolecular framework material (Q[6]-(2-AP)) was constructed from cucurbit[6]uril (Q[6]) and 2-aminopyrimidine (2-AP) as foundational building blocks in formic acid solution, which was subsequently applied for iodine capture.

title: High-performance, recyclable, and multifunctional nitrile butadiene rubber based on hydrogen and urea bonds
abstract: This paper contributes a new strategy to solve the problem of difficult recycling of traditional NBR, which is of great significance and is expected to replace traditional covalently cross-linked NBR.

title: N-doped semiconductive polymer using methoxy-modified JLBI for optimized miscibility and Coulomb interactions towards high thermoelectric performance
abstract: The conductivity of MeO-JLBI doped N2200 films is ∼10 times higher than that of N-DMBI and JLBI, due to the more efficient carrier dissociation from the weaker Coulomb interactions, which is one of the highest values of doped N2200 films to date.

title: A highly flexible fabric-based hydrovoltaic generator with a core-sheath structure for wearable applications
abstract: Flexible hydrovoltaic generators, which sustainably convert abundant water resources in nature into electrical energy, have attracted significant attention in the field of wearable electronics.

title: Exploiting S 2 2− /S 2− redox chemistry in pseudo-layered chain-structured titanium trisulfide cathodes for high-energy magnesium–lithium hybrid ion batteries
abstract: TiS 3 featuring disulfide anions is explored for the first time as a cathode in Mg–Li hybrid ion batteries. Harnessing S(– i )/S(– ii ) anion redox, it delivers superior capacity compared to TiS 2 -based counterparts.

title: Bi/ZIF-8 catalysts: the important role of ZIF-8 for enhanced electrochemical N 2 -to-NH 3 conversion using a neutral electrolyte
abstract: The introduction of ZIF-8 not only contributed to the adsorption of N 2 and the exposure of active sites but also promoted the generation of electron-rich Bi. Further, the electron-rich Bi enhance the adsorption and activation of N 2 .

title: Controlling crystallographic orientation in h-WO 3 films to maximize photoelectrochemical water splitting efficiency
abstract: Facet-engineered hexagonal WO 3 (h-WO 3 ) photoanodes with a dominant (110) facet show superior photocurrent density and efficient charge separation, thereby enhancing photoelectrochemical water splitting performance.

title: A dual-channel detection mode ZnO/organic polymer/Cu 2 O heterojunction dual-band photodetector
abstract: A dual-channel detection mode photodetector is reported, and the response spectrum can be switched under different bias modulation.

title: Hierarchically structured superhydrophobic composite films for efficient radiative cooling and energy saving
abstract: A hierarchically structured composite film of porous PDMS, glass microspheres, and hydrophobic SiO 2 provides thermal insulation, radiative cooling, and self-cleaning, offering a durable, energy-efficient solution for outdoor thermal management.

title: Breaking scaling relations and boosting ammonia synthesis in nitrogen reduction with V-containing heteronuclear double metal atoms
abstract: Dual-atom catalysts composed of V and 3d TMs have enhanced NRR activity by modulating charge transfer.

title: Probing the mechanistic role of the catalyst layer microstructure in proton exchange membrane water electrolyzers
abstract: This work presents a mechanistic analysis of the microstructure-driven operational landscape of proton exchange membrane water electrolysis, thereby revealing its origins of electrode-centric limitations.

title: Recent developments in addressing the challenges associated with polymer-based solid-state lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Herein, we summarize the research on polymer-based solid-state lithium–sulfur batteries according to four areas—solid electrolytes, sulfur cathodes, lithium anodes, and interfacial interactions—as well as future development targets.

title: Synergistic optimization of minimal antisolvent processing and dopant-free HTMs for high-efficiency perovskite solar cells
abstract: A synergistic strategy combining ultra-low anti-solvent treatment under humidity with a dopant-free sulfonate-based HTL (CIM + Cs) enables enhanced stability and high efficiency in perovskite solar cells.

title: Pre-sodiation strategies for constructing high-performance sodium-ion batteries
abstract: This review comprehensively summarizes the pre-sodiation strategies for constructing high-performance sodium-ion batteries, highlighting the anode pre-sodiation and cathode pre-sodiation techniques.

title: Exfoliated two-dimensional bimetallic Ni 0.6 Co 0.4 -metal organic framework nanosheets with mixed ligands for enhanced acid electrocatalytic water oxidation
abstract: A novel two-dimensional bimetallic Ni 0.6 Co 0.4 -MOF nanosheet was obtained by improved ultrasonic exfoliation, which can be integrated with carbon black and applied to water splitting. It provides a potential material for improving water oxidation.

title: Efficiently screening organic ligands by machine learning for stabilizing 2D/3D perovskites
abstract: Currently, the commercial photovoltaic applications of non-toxic tin-halide perovskites are still fundamentally restricted by their intrinsic phase instability.

title: Mitigating trap states in halide perovskite solar cells through the synergy of coordination, hydrogen and halogen types of bonding
authors: 'Yuling Zhuo', 'Tongjun Zheng', 'Shuguang Cao', 'Shizi Luo', 'Zhuoneng Bi', 'Yupeng Zheng', 'Biniyam Zemene Taye', 'Xiaoli Chen', 'Lavrenty G. Gutsev', 'Victoria V. Ozerova', 'Nikita A. Emelianov', 'Sergey G. Vasil'ev', 'Alexander F. Shestakov', 'Hao Li', 'Chao Wang', 'Yanping Mo', 'Gennady L. Gutsev', 'Balu R. Ramachandran', 'Ning Li', 'Eric N. Maluta', 'Sergey M. Aldoshin', 'Pavel A. Troshin', 'Xueqing Xu'
abstract: This study demonstrates how an interaction between the additive BrFBN and a perovskite precursor results in a perovskite film with fewer defect states. This work provides insights on the feasibility of manufacturing stable, high-performance PSCs.

title: Personal health assistant HES-CHAT e-skins: integrated mechanosensitivity, electromagnetic shielding, and electrochemical energy storage
abstract: In this work, our HES-CHAT e-skin design, integrating mechanosensitive sensors and ChatGPT, offers a comprehensive personal health assistant with advanced functionalities.

title: Enabling high C 2 H 2 storage and efficient C 2 H 2 /CO 2 separation in a cage-like MOF with multiple supramolecular binding sites
abstract: A cage-like MOF (Cu-TPHC) with multiple supramolecular binding sites is reported to overcome the trade-off effect between adsorption capacity and separation performance for C 2 H 2 /CO 2 , affording 99.4% purity C 2 H 2 under various conditions.

title: A 7.5 V window dual-functional planar micro-device based on a biopolymer ionogel electrolyte for charge storage and neuromorphic computing
abstract: A dual-functional micro-device integrating charge storage and neuromorphic functionality, enabled by a novel biopolymer ionogel, operates at an exceptionally wide 7.5 V window, achieving 5.78 F cm −2 capacitance and tunable synaptic behaviour.

title: Nanostructured copper foam electrodes boost redox kinetics and suppress chemical side reactions of viologen anolytes in pH-neutral aqueous organic redox flow batteries
