abstract: Nanostructured copper foams (N-Cufs) hold great promise as electrocatalysts for the redox reaction of methyl viologen anolyte in pH-neutral aqueous organic redox flow batteries (AORFBs).

title: Rational design for enhanced mechanical and kinetic properties of SnSb-based yolk–shell heterostructure as long cycle-life, high-rate Na-ion battery anode
abstract: A long cycle-life, high-rate SnSb@C-SiOC nanohybrid anode for SIBs is designed. The multi-functional shell boosts electrochemical kinetics, and the heterostructure effectively suppresses huge volume change and structural degradation of SnSb.

title: Cu and Fe doping realized a high rate and low volume strain O3-type layered oxide cathode for sodium ion batteries
abstract: Cu and Fe doping improves Na + diffusion kinetics, modifies the Na + deintercalation sequence, and adjusts residual Na + distribution in layers. This inhibits phase transitions, enabling high-rate performance and low volume strain.

title: Synergistic effect of sidewall holes and encapsulated phosphorus to improve lithium storage in single-walled carbon nanotubes
abstract: Creating holes in single-walled carbon nanotubes and shortening the encapsulated red phosphorus chains improves lithium accommodation in the nanomaterial.

title: A controllable approach to nitrogen-deficient ultrathin graphitic carbon nitride: robust photo-redox properties and mechanistic insights
abstract: Polymeric graphitic carbon nitride (g-C 3 N 4 ) is an excellent visible-light-driven photocatalyst.

title: Atomic cation and anion co-vacancy defects boosted the oxide path mechanism of the oxygen evolution reaction on NiFeAl-layered double hydroxide
abstract: The uniform cation and anion defects on Ni 6 Fe 2 Al-LDH shorten the interatomic M–M distance and trigger the oxide path mechanism of the oxygen evolution reaction.

title: Electron-ion coupling enables ionic hydrogel with high thermopower for low-grade heat harvest and sensitive fire warning
abstract: We developed triple-network ionic hydrogel APSG via electron-ion synergy. High strength (1.49 MPa), 4.25 mV/K Seebeck coefficient, outputs 2.5 V (ΔT = 20 K), exceptional flame retardancy (LOI = 42.3%) & 1.7 s fire warning response.

title: Solvent polarity-dependent charge polarity recoverable polyaniline-carbon nanotube composites
abstract: Solvent-mediated polarity control strategy ( via E T parameter) suppresses oxygen doping in polyaniline/single-walled carbon nanotube (PANI/SWCNT) hybrids, enabling air-stable n-type behavior and reversible p-to-n switching without chemical dopants.

title: Nickel single-atom catalysts anchored on heteroatom (X = B, N, P, and S)-doped graphdiyne for a highly efficient hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Single-atom nickel catalysts anchored on heteroatom (X = B, N, P, and S)-doped graphdiyne were rationally designed for a highly efficient hydrogen evolution reaction, of which Ni/1N3-GDY is considered as the most efficient HER catalyst.

title: Converting waste poly(ethylene terephthalate) into hierarchical porous carbon towards a high-performance supercapacitor
abstract: Hierarchical porous carbon (HPC) was prepared via a one-step mild and efficient pyrolysis of waste polyethylene terephthalate (PET). The resultant HPC-700 showed an exceptionally high specific capacitance and a high cycling stability.

title: Polymorphs of VO 2 and their MXene-based hybrid materials for energy storage applications
abstract: The well-defined crystal structure of VO 2 polymorphs have gained significant attention as a promising electrode material. Furthermore, its composites with MXenes are summarized for efficient energy storage applications.

title: Vinylene-linked donor–acceptor covalent organic polymers with low exciton binding energy for enhanced photocatalytic oxidation of sulfides
abstract: The construction of vinylene linkage and a donor–acceptor structure extends the in-plane π-conjugation of Btt-Bdd, which reduces excitonic effects and facilitates charge separation, thus significantly improving the photocatalytic sulfide oxidation.

title: Boosting sodium-ion mobility in Na 3 V 2 (PO 4 ) 2 F 3 through anion engineering with Br − substitution
abstract: Partial Br substitution forming Na 3 V 2 (PO 4 ) 2 F 3(1− x ) Br 3 x enhances Na + ion mobility by widening 2D diffusion channel and lowering the migration barrier.

title: Thermocatalytic CO 2 dissociation over Ni-based single atom alloys: a first-principles investigation
abstract: Mitigating CO 2 emissions is critical to advancing sustainable climate strategies, with CO 2 reduction emerging as a promising approach for converting CO 2 into value-added fuels and chemicals.

title: Biological pretreatment of bamboo biomass toward hierarchical carbon architectures for high-rate sodium storage
abstract: Sodium-ion batteries (SIBs) demand sustainable anode materials to address the limitations of lithium resources.

title: Structural rule of heteroatom-modified single-atom catalysts for the CO 2 electroreduction reaction
abstract: The raised SACs have the better ability to activate CO 2 , but also lead to relatively poor activity and selectivity for CO 2 RR for the stronger CO and H + adsorption; by contrast, the planar ones exhibit relatively better CO 2 RR performance.

title: Advancing C–C coupling of the electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction reaction for C 2+ products
abstract: This review discusses recent advances in the selective production of C 2+ products via electrocatalytic CO 2 RR based on C–C coupling, offering insightful guidelines and strategies to develop efficient catalysts for real-world applications.

title: Electrochemical pretreatment of thienothiophene/single-walled carbon nanotubes: a flexible and binder-free electrode with enhanced energy performance as a positive electrode for asymmetric supercapacitors
abstract: The hybrid electrode based on thienothiophene (TT) and single-walled carbon nanotubes (SWCNTs) exhibits a superior specific capacitance of 355.3 F g −1 as a flexible hybrid asymmetric supercapacitor after electrochemical pretreatment.

title: Integrated capture and electrochemical conversion of CO 2 from flue gas mediated by carbonate/bicarbonate cycle
abstract: This study reports a sustainable and energy-efficient strategy to integrate the capture and electrochemical conversion of CO 2 from flue gas by exploiting K 2 CO 3 ⇌ KHCO 3 interconversion, opening a promising avenue for carbon capture and utilization.

title: Advancing the synthesis strategy and interface modification for efficient perovskite quantum dot solar cells
abstract: Perovskite quantum dots (PeQDs), as a promising material for photovoltaics for their unique optoelectronic properties, has seen significant improvement in cell performance by the interplay of advanced synthetic strategies and interface modifications.

title: Efficient low-temperature thermochemical CO 2 splitting enabled by Gibbs free energy engineering
abstract: Gibbs free energy engineering based on high-throughput density functional theory (DFT) calculations combined with machine learning for materials screening in low-temperature thermochemical CO 2 splitting.

title: Vat photopolymerization of tantalum-doped Li 7 La 3 Zr 2 O 12 electrolytes: a new Frontier in solid-state battery design
abstract: Vat photopolymerization based 3D printing to fabricate Ta-doped LLZO electrolytes for solid state batteries, aiming to address limitations in traditional manufacturing methods.

title: Recent advancements in carbon capture materials research: innovative optimization of materials synthesis and engineering applications
abstract: The escalating urgency of global climate change has propelled the advancement of efficient carbon capture technologies.

title: Advances in amine-functionalized metal organic frameworks for carbon capture
abstract: It summarizes synthesis methods, CO 2 adsorption mechanisms, and performance variation patterns of amine-functionalized MOFs, highlighting the key roles of structure and amine configuration in efficient CO 2 capture and reversible regeneration.

title: Sintering-resistant CuO/CeO 2 catalysts prepared via the reversed impregnation method for ethyl acetate oxidation
abstract: Possible mechanism of lattice and adsorbed oxygen over sintering-resistant CuO/CeO 2 catalysts.

title: Organic semiconductor frameworks integrating bay-substituted perylene bisimides as screw dislocation units in onion-like π–π stacked architectures
abstract: Core-twisted PBI-based COFs grown on WO 3 3D-NS photoanodes enable photocurrent densities up to 590 ± 50 μA cm −2 under green-light irradiation (>20 h, 1 sun, λ > 490 nm) using sulfonate hydroquinone as a redox mediator.

title: Lightweight Al–Mg–In alloy based seawater batteries for long endurance applications: pack mass and cost optimization
abstract: A high efficiency Al–Mg–In anode based SWB outshines LiB packs in low power long endurance underwater applications.

title: Ultrastable LiF/carbon nanocomposites as sacrificial additives for enhancing the lifespan of anode-free lithium metal batteries
abstract: A LiF nanodot/carbon composite delivers capacity up to 730 mA h g −1 and ultrastability, extending the cycling life of anode-free batteries.

title: From activity to stability: dissolution-mediated surface reconstruction and redeposition in transition metal-based electrocatalysts
abstract: A comprehensive review on the structural reconstruction and associated dissolution/redeposition equilibria of the electrocatalysts during reactions is proposed, which is pivotal for addressing the delicate balance between activity and stability.

title: Inorganic solid electrolytes for all-solid-state lithium/sodium-ion batteries: recent developments and applications
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive overview of recent advancements in preparation techniques and electrolyte engineering. It also discusses the integration of both single- and multi-phase electrolytes in ASSBs and future research potentials.

title: Expression of concern: Construction of desert rose flower-shaped NiFe LDH-Ni 3 S 2 heterostructures via seawater corrosion engineering for efficient water-urea splitting and seawater utilization
abstract: Expression of concern for ‘Construction of desert rose flower-shaped NiFe LDH-Ni 3 S 2 heterostructures via seawater corrosion engineering for efficient water-urea splitting and seawater utilization’ by Zhao-Hui Zhang et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2023, 11 , 19578–19590, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TA0…

title: Soy protein enables stable perovskite solar cells by defect passivation and ion migration suppression
abstract: Soy protein is explored for enhancing film quality and stability of perovskite solar cells under external stress.

title: ZnGa 2 Te 4 thin-film absorbers for photoelectrochemical CO 2 reduction
abstract: Tetragonal ZnGa 2 Te 4 thin films with direct bandgap ∼1.86 eV show −2 mA cm −2 photocurrent, highlighting their potential as efficient photocathodes for photoelectrochemical CO 2 reduction.

title: Synergistic solvation-surface engineering for high-performance aqueous zinc metal batteries
abstract: Cost-effective l -threonine additive in zinc metal batteries enables dendrite-free Zn deposition by modifying Zn 2+ solvation structure, breaking hydrogen-bond networks, and forming protective adsorption layers, thereby improving cycling stability.

title: NHC–Ni nanoclusters covalently ligated on carbon nanotubes: highly active electrocatalysts for the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: NHC–NiNCs covalently ligated on MWCNTs for highly efficient OER.

title: Redox potential-engineered heteropolyacid regenerative fuels for emission-free direct liquid fuel cells
abstract: Redox potential-regulated HPA fuel enables the development of efficient, noble metal-free direct liquid fuel cells with hydrogen-cycle sustainability.

title: Constructing dual interfacial gold nanodot interlayers in sandwich-structured BaTiO 3 /P(VDF-HFP) composites for high energy storage density
abstract: Dual interfacial gold nanodot interlayers are introduced into a BT/P(VDF-HFP) trilayer composite to achieve a significantly improved discharge energy density of 14.78 J cm −3 , which is approximately 176.4% that of the BT-3L composite (∼8.38 J cm −3 ).

title: Correction: Comprehensive overview of machine learning applications in MOFs: from modeling processes to latest applications and design classifications
abstract: Correction for ‘Comprehensive overview of machine learning applications in MOFs: from modeling processes to latest applications and design classifications’ by Yutong Liu et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, 13 , 2403–2440, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA06740A.

title: Correction: A gas diffusion nanocomposite layer with a hydrophilic–superhydrophobic columnar interface for enhanced water and gas transport
abstract: Correction for ‘A gas diffusion nanocomposite layer with a hydrophilic–superhydrophobic columnar interface for enhanced water and gas transport’ by Taesik Eom et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA09052G.

title: Correction: Investigating transition metal crosstalk on SEI stability as a function of anode chemistry
abstract: Correction for ‘Investigating transition metal crosstalk on SEI stability as a function of anode chemistry’ by Sunggyu Yoon et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, 13 , 13100–13113, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA08301F.

title: Correction: H 2 O assisted in improving the electrochemical performance of a deep eutectic electrolyte formed by choline chloride and magnesium chloride hexahydrate
abstract: Correction for ‘H 2 O assisted in improving the electrochemical performance of a deep eutectic electrolyte formed by choline chloride and magnesium chloride hexahydrate’ by Kaixiang Zou et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2024, 12 , 33257–33267, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA05504G.

title: Construction of a photonic radiative cooling layer based on a 3D conductive network foam for efficient electromagnetic interference shielding and environmental thermal comfort
abstract: The environment on which people depend for their survival is becoming increasingly complex and deteriorating due to increasing electromagnetic pollution and extreme hot weather.

title: Unblocking “pinned” Li + ions in novel KTiOPO 4 -structured LiVPO 4 F enables superior energy storage performance
abstract: Li + and Na + showed remarkable synergy in the VPO 4 F framework, providing a specific energy of over 530 W h kg −1 , opening up a new attractive system for creating hybrid Li/Na-ion batteries.

title: Effect of crystal defects on the selectivity of a bulk Cu–Zn alloy for electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: The elemental composition and crystal structure of catalysts are important factors affecting the product selectivity in the electrochemical CO 2 reduction reaction (CO 2 RR).

title: Architecting hydrangea-inspired nitrogen-doped hollow carbon with isolated Co atoms for superior oxygen reduction catalysis
abstract: The incorporation of atomically dispersed Co sites onto N-deficient carbon frameworks modulates the local electronic configuration, resulting in enhanced electrocatalytic performance toward oxygen reduction reaction (ORR).

title: A perovskite nanocomposite and self-assembled nanoparticle-decorated cathode for low temperature SOFCs
abstract: A nanocomposite composed of a cubic simple perovskite and an orthorhombic layered perovskite and a self-assembly nanoparticle decorated cathode for low temperature SOFCs.

title: Layered sodium vanadate (NaV 8 O 20 ) nanobelts: a new high-performing pseudocapacitive material for sodium-ion storage applications
abstract: This study presents the preparation of 1D and layered NaV 8 O 20 nanobelts using a hydrothermal method and demonstrates their applications in supercapacitors and sodium-ion batteries.

title: Plasma-derived hydrogen radical-mediated N 2 activation for mild ammonia synthesis: insights into the importance of oxygen vacancies in the reaction mechanism
abstract: The oxygen vacancy-promoted generation of H radicals and the H radical-mediated N 2 activation for ammonia synthesis.

title: In situ construction and composition engineering of PbBiO 2 Br/metal halide perovskite heterojunctions for enhanced interfacial charge transfer and photocatalytic activity
abstract: Atom-cosharing PbBiO 2 Br/MHP heterojunction photocatalysts are synthesized via a self-template acid-etching process, which enables precise manipulation of their composition and photoelectrical properties.

title: Silicon/graphite composite host for tuned lithiophilicity in Li metal anodes
abstract: Silicon/graphite composites were used to induce lithiophilicity in hosted l ithium metal anodes, with 10% Si content leading to the best performance.

title: Direct upcycling of spent layered oxide cathodes via a dual-functional eutectic salt for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Using Na 2 CO 3 –K 2 CO 3 eutectic system enables simultaneous resodiation and K + -doping, forming a low-melting phase for complete surface reconstruction. K + incorporation expands interlayer spacing, improving Na + transport kinetics.

title: Enhanced piezo-phototronic effect in carbon nitride nanosheets via oxidative exfoliation for high-efficiency piezo-photocatalysis
abstract: The obtained E500-CN serves dual functions in piezo-photocatalysis by providing effective piezoelectric responses and reduced charge carrier recombination, benefiting from the shortened diffusion pathway and the synergistic piezo-phototronic effect.

title: Achieving high-capacity sodium insertion of coal-based hard carbon anodes via closed-pore modification
abstract: The utilization of low-cost, high-carbon content coal in the production of coal-based hard carbon, a potential carbon anode for sodium-ion batteries, represents a highly promising avenue of research.

title: Identifying rate-limiting steps in photocatalysis: a temperature-and light intensity-dependent diagnostic of charge supply vs. charge transfer
abstract: At lower irradiance (bottom), temperature barely matters. Beyond the onset intensity (top), heating substantially boosts yields—uncovering if slow surface reactions or insufficient carrier supply holds back photocatalysis.

title: In situ growth of graphdiyne on ZnCo-ZIF for enhanced lithium–sulfur battery performance
abstract: ZnCo-ZIF@GDY composite material is applied as a modified membrane separator in lithium–sulfur (Li–S) battery to improve the electrochemical performance.

title: Rational regulation strategies toward perovskite precursor inks and perovskite films from the lab to commercialization
abstract: This review explores the role of perovskite inks and their impact on the quality of scalable perovskite films. Factors such as fluid dynamics, solvent selection, and additive engineering during the meniscus-assisted coating process are summarized.

title: Hydrogel electrolytes for zinc dendrite regulation: balancing conductivity and modulus
abstract: The fundamental trade-off between ionic conductivity and mechanical modulus in hydrogel electrolytes is explored, revealing that mechanical stiffness plays a dominant role in inhibiting dendrite formation and stabilizing zinc anodes.

title: Frustrated Lewis pair–cyano dual-mediated ternary crystalline carbon nitride homojunctions for highly enhanced visible-light-driven photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production
abstract: The photocatalytic mechanism of H 2 O 2 production by frustrated Lewis pair–cyano dual-mediated ternary crystalline carbon nitride homojunctions.

title: A stretchable, permeable, and biocompatible fiber-reinforced hybrid hydrogel electrode for highly stable electrophysiological signal recording
abstract: A fiber-reinforced hydrogel electrode for enhanced mechanical–electrical properties, enabling biocompatible, stable electrophysiological monitoring in wearable healthcare.

title: N-Heterocyclic carbene-promoted copper powder conditioning for thermal spray applications
abstract: Uncontrolled Cu surface oxide weakens inter-particle bonding and decreases coating performance. N-Heterocyclic carbene (NHC) chemistry reduces oxides, resulting in better particle bonding and corrosion resistance vs. untreated Cu powder coatings.

title: Enhanced optical properties of luminescent solar concentrators via metal ion doping in carbon dots
abstract: Copper-doped carbon dots enhance luminescent solar concentrators, achieving a 22% increase in optical efficiency and PCE with high stability over 90 days.

title: Electrodeposition of N-heterocyclic carbene on mild steel surfaces
abstract: The current study investigates the electrochemical deposition of NHCs on industrially relevant mild steel, highlighting the crucial role of dissolved oxygen in the deposition process and its influence on coating composition and properties.

title: Electronic structure regulation of carbon atoms from wood for enhancing Zn–air battery performances
abstract: A simple and scalable method was designed to convert balsa wood into non-metallic N, S-doped carbon catalysts with superior ORR performance and excellent application potential in ZABs.

title: Lithium inventory loss and cathode stress induced by silicon-graphite anodes in cylindrical NCA90 full cells
abstract: This work reveals how 2 wt% silicon in graphite anodes induces lithium loss, alters graphite phase transitions, and accelerates cathode degradation, impacting long-term performance of NCA90-based lithium-ion full cells.

title: Innovative conversion electrode materials and electrolyte strategies in aqueous zinc–S/Se batteries for advanced energy storage
abstract: This review summarizes recent strategies to enhance AZSBs and AZSeBs, including cathode and electrolyte optimization, advanced characterization, and reaction mechanism studies, while highlighting current challenges and future development directions.

title: Revolutionizing water splitting performance by probing the influence of electron transfer on the NiCr-LDH/VS 2 /NF heterostructure
abstract: A novel NiCr-LDH/VS 2 /NF electrocatalyst shows efficient TWS performance via a unique feature of electron transfer with charge repopulation in both NiCr-LDH and VS 2 layers, revealing it as a future-oriented material for green hydrogen technology.

title: Ionic-liquid-engineered, interfacial π–π-anchored, cobalt-dispersed, and N-, F-, B-doped carbon matrix as an oxygen electrocatalyst for advanced zinc–air batteries
abstract: An advanced catalyst drives innovation in zinc–air battery technology, delivering exceptional performance, stability, and efficiency with successfully powering a mobile phone.

title: Atomic bridging for constructing flexible boron-doped diamond supercapacitors with extended cycling longevity
abstract: A hierarchical boron-doped diamond (BDD) film was successfully fabricated by depositing a BDD film on Cu foam with TiO 2 as the interlayer using highly feasible techniques of hot filament chemical vapor deposition and atomic layer deposition.

title: Learning from combined XRD and DFT data unveils critical structural details in Na x CrO 2 electrochemical evolution
abstract: In situ XRD shows an unusual shift of superstructure peaks in the battery cycling of NaCrO 2 . A large Na vacancy nano-stripe, likely coupled with reversible Cr migration, is unveiled by XRD and DFT analyses to be embedded in Na density waves.

title: Improved stability and selectivity of CuFe 2 O 4 -based catalysts for photoelectrochemical CO 2 -to-acetate conversion
abstract: The newly developed CuFe 2 O 4 -based catalyst demonstrated stable photoelectrochemical (PEC) CO 2 reduction to produce acetate with high selectivity exceeding 90%, thereby solving the stability issue involved in the conventional CuFeO 2 –based catalyst.

title: Realizing high thermoelectric performance in MXene-incorporated Yb 0.4 Co 3.96 Ti 0.04 Sb 12 via carrier and phonon engineering
abstract: The incorporation of secondary phase inclusions is an effective strategy to develop the high-quality TE materials via extending the phonon scattering range and energy filtering effect.

title: High-efficiency solid-state quantum dot sensitized solar cells based on black TiO 2 and an activated carbon electrode
abstract: The synergistic effect of modified black TiO 2 and activated carbon, along with the introduction of gel electrolyte, helped in the preparation of solid-state QDSSCs. This ultimately resulted in a PCE of 9.48% and long-term stability for QDSSCs.

title: Enhanced solubility and stability of active species in flow battery electrolytes via suppressed anthraquinone aggregation
abstract: Mixing different anthraquinone enhances the solubility and stability of aqueous organic flow battery electrolytes by suppression of aggregation. This can enable higher energy density, longer-lasting, and cost-effective large-scale energy storage.

title: Metal deficiency tuned charge transfer in intermetallic Ni 2− x Sn ( x = 0.37–0.65) enhances selective conversion of furfural to furfuryl alcohol towards the theoretical limit
abstract: Metal deficiency tuned charge transfer in Ni–Sn based intermetallics enhances selective conversion of furfural to furfuryl alcohol.

title: Gel electrolyte interdigitation enables stable high areal capacity cycling of the 3D Zn electrode
abstract: Hydrogel electrolyte interdigitation is introduced as an effective strategy for stable 3D zinc metal anode design, allowing fast mass transport into the tortuous framework and uniform metal deposition/dissolution across the entire structure.

title: Development of non-fluorinated hybrid ester solvents for wide-temperature operation of lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Hybrid solvent shows an increased electron-withdrawing effect on the oxygen in the carbonyl group (CO), resulting in low solvation energies and superior electrochemical performance across a wide temperature range.

title: Hydride synthesis of thermoelectric skutterudites (Ba,Sr)Fe 3± x Ni 1± x Sb 12 optimized via design of experiments
abstract: Diffusion-enhanced hydride synthesis enables the modern solidstate chemist to achieve their Hephaestian aspirations through design of experiments methods and the mechanistic knowledge gleaned from in situ powder X-ray diffraction data.

title: Ultrathin oxygen deficient SnO x films as electron extraction layers for perovskite solar modules
abstract: A plasma-modified ALD (PMALD) approach has been developed for depositing SnO x thin-films with a tunable composition as electron extraction layers in perovskite solar modules using poly(triarylamine) (PTAA) as the hole transport layer.

title: Pt nanoparticles supported on porous sheath TiO 2 wrapped carbon nanotubes for selective glycerol electro-oxidation into tartronate
abstract: The introduction of porous sheath TiO 2 with controllable morphology and thickness into a Pt/CNTs catalyst effectively improves the catalytic activity toward glycerol electrooxidation and the selectivity of tartronic acid.

title: Tailoring dielectric performance via dipole density and hydrogen bonding interaction towards high-temperature capacitive energy storage polymers
abstract: The synergistic integration of co-polymer design and blending methods demonstrated herein provides a robust strategy for engineering high-temperature polymer dielectrics with optimized energy storage capability.

title: Precise design of MOF-derived single-atom catalysts with symmetric and asymmetric coordination for advanced lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Single-atom catalysts (SACs) have demonstrated great potential as ideal electrocatalytic hosts for sulfur cathodes in lithium–sulfur (Li–S) batteries.

title: A durable superhydrophobic composite sponge for efficient oil/water separation in harsh marine environments: integrating corrosion resistance and photothermal functionality
abstract: A superhydrophobic PDMS–CuS@PMS with enhanced corrosion resistance using polydopamine (PDA) and polyethyleneimine (PEI) was developed. Their synergy, along with hydrangea-like CuS, improves photothermal effects for efficient oil spill remediation.

title: Hierarchical cobalt-pentlandite (Co, Ni, Zn) 9 S 8 nanostructures: advanced electrodes for flexible solid-state supercapacitors
abstract: A hierarchical (Co, Ni, Zn) 9 S 8 nanostructure featuring nanoparticles self-assembled into nanoneedles and further into nanosheets enables high capacitance, rate capability, and stability for flexible energy storage devices.

title: Low coercive field in quasi-epitaxial Al-doped HfO 2 films for energy-efficient ferroelectric memories
abstract: Quasi-epitaxial Al-doped HfO₂ films by RF sputtering show a remarkably low coercive field (0.42 MV cm −1 at 20 nm), promising energy-efficient ferroelectric memories.

title: Dilute alloying-induced d–p orbital hybridization in Pd–Bi metallene arrays for electrocatalytic coupling of carbon dioxide and nitrate to urea
abstract: The precise dilution of d-block Pd in p-block Bi metallene arrays could indue localized electron transfer via d–p orbital hybridization and thus create highly active sites that enhance CO 2 and NO 3 − co-activation and C–N coupling for urea synthesis.

title: Two-dimensional Cu( i )-MOF with mesoporous architecture towards chemiresistive NO 2 sensing
abstract: This study presents a semiconducting copper( i )-MOF with a mesoporous structure, developed for chemiresistive NO 2 sensing. The device exhibits exceptional selectivity and rapid response/recovery with a detection limit of 3.5 ppb at room temperature.

title: Defect-engineered NiCo hydroxide nanostructures for highly efficient electrocatalytic glucose oxidation to formic acid
abstract: Engineering dual vacancies in NiCo-LDH promotes glucose adsorption and facilitates C–C bond cleavage, synergistically enhancing selective glucose oxidation to formic acid.

title: Functionalization of hierarchical porous carbon materials for adsorption of light rare earth ions
abstract: The schematic structure and selective adsorption of rare earth ions (Sm 3+ ) by functionalized hierarchical porous (macro–meso–microporous) carbon materials (HPC) are illustrated.

title: Exploring separation techniques for the direct recycling of high voltage spinel LNMO scrap electrodes
abstract: Workflow of the direct recycling of various LNMO electrode scraps through three distinct separation routes, with the resulting materials used directly for the preparation of new electrodes with no need for re-synthesis of the active material.

title: Steam activation of pitch-based carbon fibers for increasing CO 2 adsorption behaviors
abstract: Activated carbon fibers (ACFs) have attracted considerable interest as versatile adsorbents for gas separation, water treatment, and similar applications due to their high specific surface area, chemical stability, and robust mechanical properties.

title: Exploring layered nanocomposite of MoS 2 and rGO as a highly efficient supercapattery and OER electrocatalyst
abstract: Hydrothermally synthesized layered nanocomposite of MoS 2 and rGO (1 : 2.4) exhibited efficacious electrocatalytic behaviour towards supercapattery and OER due to the nanoarchitectural finesse of the material. The impact of MoS 2 : rGO ratio has also been discussed in detail.

title: Scalable conformal FeOOH cocatalyst deposition by interfacial precipitation layer deposition for photoelectrochemical water splitting
abstract: Photoelectrochemical (PEC) water splitting is a promising method for sustainable hydrogen production, but challenges remain in achieving high performance and stability, particularly for large-area photoanodes.

title: A laser-induced graphene/PDMS composite sensor with a dual structure enabling high-sensitivity under micro-strain and extended-range sensing
abstract: A flexible sensor is designed with a dual structure combining straight and serpentine LIG lines in parallel, enabling high sensitivity under micro-strain and extended-range sensing.

title: Screening of single-atom catalysts for CO 2 electroreduction to CH 4 using DFT calculations and machine learning
abstract: An enhanced high-throughput DFT screening model additionally incorporates *OH reduction to refine selectivity, identifying 9 CO 2 RR-active TM@C 5 N SACs. Machine learning reveals key descriptors, guiding catalyst design.

title: A piezo-self-Fenton system based on dual co-catalyst modified Bi 4 Ti 3 O 12 with accelerated Fe 3+ /Fe 2+ cycle and efficient in situ production of H 2 O 2
abstract: A novel piezo-self-Fenton system featuring accelerated Fe 3+ /Fe 2+ cycle and efficient in situ production of H 2 O 2 was proposed.

title: Lithium based battery systems: technological and environmental challenges and opportunities
abstract: The United Nations has prioritized decarbonization and reducing greenhouse gas emissions, leading nations to adopt policies for increased renewable energy use. Lithium-ion batteries are key to this shift, though they present challenges to overcome.

title: Piezoelectricity in excess of 30 pC N −1 with a high Curie temperature of 950 °C in strongly textured CaBi 2 Nb 2 O 9 ceramics
abstract: The Na/Sm co-doped CaBi 2 Nb 2 O 9 textured ceramics with a high texture degree of 95–98% were successfully prepared by improved grain growth method, achieving the highest piezoelectric coefficient ( d 33 > 30 pC N −1 ) in the CaBi 2 Nb 2 O 9 systems.

title: A review on photocatalytic methane conversion systems: from fundamental mechanisms to the emerging role of ferroelectric materials
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive tutorial about the promising potential of ferroelectric materials as unconventional photocatalysts for methane conversion applications.

title: Coupling the power of spinel nanoparticles: dual-function metal ferrite catalysts for advanced piezo-photocatalytic and electrocatalytic water splitting
abstract: Green piezo-photo catalysis enables sustainable hydrogen production, supporting clean energy goals through renewable sources and environmentally friendly processes.

title: Investigation of MgO additives on microstructure and properties of thin LLZO electrolytes for all-solid-state batteries
abstract: To realize high-energy density lithium lanthanum zirconate (LLZO)-based solid-state batteries (SSB), LLZO electrolytes should be fabricated with low thickness and high mechanical strength.

title: Interfacial regulation of photoanodes for photoelectrochemical water splitting: the roles of the interlayer and overlayer
abstract: Here, a systematic understanding of the role and design principles of functional interlayers is presented for improving charge separation and transport efficiency and accelerating surface catalytic reactions of photoanodes.

title: Tungsten doping reinforced structural stability of single-crystal nickel-rich LiNi 0.9 Co 0.1 O 2 cathodes
abstract: The structural stability and cyclic lifespan of single-crystal LiNi 0.9 Co 0.1 O 2 cathode materials are reinforced by the W doping, which not only reduces the surface residual alkali, but also creates W–O bonds to strengthen the lattice oxygen stability.

title: Hybrid central substitution of acceptors boosts the efficiency of near-infrared organic photovoltaics
abstract: 2D conjugated SMAs with hybrid central substitution (CH22, CH28, CH29) boost NIR OSCs. CH29-based OSC achieves a 1.33 eV bandgap, 884 mV V OC , and 19.12% PCE, showcasing low energy losses in high-performance systems.

title: Unraveling surface polarization in hydrothermally derived AgFeO 2 nanosheets for enhanced photoelectrochemical performance
abstract: The PEC performance of AgFeO 2 nanosheets is enhanced by the optimized surface polarization. Additionally, interstitial oxygen and an external magnetic field enhance multiple polarization couplings to maximize the PEC performance.

title: Surface amorphization advances activity and stability for acidic oxygen evolution
abstract: A ruthenium/iridium-oxide (RuO 2 /IrO 2 ) amorphous composite catalyst for acidic OER achieved a lower overpotential of 174 mV@10 mA cm −2 and exhibited excellent stability for 95 h in 0.5 M H 2 SO 4 electrolyte.

title: Improved electrochemical performance of nitrogen-containing copper-based 1D metal organic framework derived from highly connective pyridine-2,4,6-tricarboxylic acid for hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Extended metal-to-ligand bonding and π–π interactions in 1D MOF can boost the charge transport process and ultimately improve electrochemical applications.

title: Multi-scale nanoemulsion-directed anisotropic assembly for hierarchically porous metal–organic frameworks
abstract: A multi-scale nanoemulsion-directed anisotropic assembly strategy has been developed to fabricate mesoporous ZIF-67 nanobowls with controllable structural symmetries, adjustable pore architectures, and tunable framework compositions.

title: Come for predictions, stay for complexity: synthesis and experimental probing of ionic conductivity in Li 9 B 19 S 33
abstract: Predicted superior Li-ion ionic conductor Li 9 B 19 S 33 was experimentally synthesized and characterized.

title: Molten tin-salt liquid–liquid interfaces for graphite synthesis
abstract: Molten tin-salt interfaces enable scalable electrochemical CO 2 conversion into high-crystallinity graphitic carbon, achieving up to 96% faradaic efficiency and >450 mA cm −2 current density at a 2 V cell potential.

title: Fluorinated DOL-based copolymer electrolytes enabling wide electrochemical window and stabilized interphases for lithium batteries
abstract: Employing a fluorinated copolymer strategy enhances performance by broadening Li + pathways to 7.3 × 10 −4 S cm −1 , enhancing stability beyond 5.9 V and 300 °C, and facilitating LiF-rich interphase formation.

title: A dihydroquinoline-conjugated polymer cathode with a hierarchical structure and abundant redox-active sites for aluminum-ion batteries
abstract: A novel dihydroquinoline-based conjugated polymer (NaphPz) cathode, combined with a CNT/GO-modified separator, enabled high-performance aluminum-ion batteries with a high capacity of 245 mAh g −1 and outstanding cycling stability.

title: New opportunities for tailored nanoparticle catalysts through exsolution from inherently disordered defect fluorite-type oxides
abstract: Exsolution of Ru nanoparticles from defective fluorite-type Y 2 Zr 2− x Ru x O 7 exhibits exceptional tunability and produces a promising catalyst for the rWGS reaction, achieving near-equilibrium CO 2 conversion and 100% selectivity to CO above 650 °C.

title: Unraveling the efficacy of defect engineered mesoporous Ni–Co spinel oxide nanowires as an energy efficient electrocatalyst for the oxygen reduction reaction and fuel cell applications
abstract: Solvothermally synthesized defect-engineered, mesoporous Co-excess Ni–Co oxide nanowires exhibited outstanding catalytic activity towards ORR and fuel cell applications.

title: Multi-channel wearable fiber sensors with high sensitivity for limb motion recognition
abstract: A wearable health monitoring sensor based on flexible fibers with high sensitivity capable of detecting various limb movements and monitoring different physiological signals was developed using a coaxial co-injection capillary microfluidic device.

title: Stabilization of the nanoscale blend morphology in organic solar cells using solvent additives
abstract: Organic solar cells are lightweight, flexible, and eco-friendly, but tend to suffer from performance degradation under prolonged operation. This study finds that solvent additives help stabilize donor–acceptor morphology, boosting device lifetime.

title: Helmholtz plane engineering for stable zinc anodes: from interfacial dynamics to long-cycle battery design
abstract: By adjusting the composition of the inner Helmholtz plane and the outer Helmholtz plane, it is expected to improve the desolvation structure of zinc ions, inhibit the side reaction of zinc anode and enhance the long-cycle performance of the battery.

title: Control of crystallization dynamics via vanadium–polyethylene glycol interactions toward high-performance BiVO 4 photoanodes
abstract: Acoustic-energy-assisted aging strengthens V 5+ –PEG coordination, directing nucleation and growth to yield large-grain BiVO 4 photoanodes with substantially enhanced photocurrent.

title: Tailored CEI architectures to boost high-performance solid-state Zn-ion batteries
abstract: Cathode interface modification involves stabilizing CEI, building artificial CEI, boosting mechanics, inhibiting metal dissolution, and more.

title: Side-chain modification to boost the dielectric constant of polymers: toward high-k material synthesis and application in low-voltage operating printed electronics
abstract: Dipole-inducing side chains in amorphous polymers boost dielectric constants above 6 while preserving good insulation, enabling 3 V operating organic transistors with high mobility (∼1.90 cm² V −1 s −1 ) and operation stability.

title: Multi-scale quantitative investigation of the early structural transformation mechanism in alkali-activated materials: insights from molecular dynamics and first-principles calculations
abstract: The polycondensation process of alkali-activated materials has been evaluated in real-time at the molecular level.

title: An efficient bifunctional oxygen catalyst of CNT@FeCo–C 3 N 4 & Ni 7 Fe-LDH composite for rechargeable ammonium ion-air battery
abstract: Ammonium-ion air batteries leverage the synergy between oxygen reduction reaction and NH 4 + storage, allowing for the simultaneous enhancement of the air cathode's efficiency while using oxygen as a reservoir for NH 4 + storage.

title: Can chelatogenic molecules enhance the stability of air-processed MAPbI 3 perovskite solar cells? A case study of salophen
abstract: Salophen molecules passivate defects, improve crystallinity, and enhance hydrophobicity of MAPbI 3 films, yielding perovskite solar cells with >18% efficiency and improved stability under ambient processing conditions.

title: Ultra-economical fabrication of a Ru cluster-loaded Ni(OH) 2 self-supported electrode via sub-hour corrosion for effective bifunctional water splitting
abstract: Ultra-economical and highly active electrolytic water catalyst Ru–Ni(OH) 2 was synthesized based on sub-hour spontaneous corrosion.

title: Rational design and construction of aqueous multivalent metal ion battery systems
abstract: Energy storage chemistry of the cathode and anode of aqueous multivalent metal ion batteries and present their recent progress are reviewed and discussed. Progress in theoretical simulations of their energy storage modes is then reviewed.

title: Wet-spun Ni, N-codoped macroporous carbon fibers for efficient CO 2 electroreduction and Zn–CO 2 batteries
abstract: Ni, N-codoped macroporous carbon fibers have been developed as efficient electrocatalysts for CO 2 reduction and Zn–CO 2 batteries.

title: Synergistic sulfonyl–ether polyimide binders for stabilized high-loading NCM811 cathodes in high-energy lithium-ion batteries
abstract: LiNi 0.8 Co 0.1 Mn 0.1 O 2 (NCM811) cathodes offer high-energy-density potential for lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) due to their 275 mAh g −1 theoretical capacity and cost efficiency.

title: Highly graphitized nitrogen-doped ordered mesoporous carbon supported Ni nanocrystals for efficient hydrazine-assisted CO 2 splitting
abstract: An efficient nanoscale Ni-based catalyst enabled the energy-efficient coupling of HzOR with CO 2 RR for CO production.

title: Correction: Hollow MoC/NC sphere for electromagnetic wave attenuation: direct observation of interfacial polarization on nanoscale hetero-interfaces
abstract: Correction for ‘Hollow MoC/NC sphere for electromagnetic wave attenuation: direct observation of interfacial polarization on nanoscale hetero-interfaces’ by Wenhuan Huang et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2022, 10 , 1290–1298, https://doi.org/10.1039/D1TA09357F.

title: Correction: Enhancing the durability of aluminium-foil anodes in rechargeable lithium batteries via uniformly distributed alloy addition in the matrix phase
abstract: Correction for ‘Enhancing the durability of aluminium-foil anodes in rechargeable lithium batteries via uniformly distributed alloy addition in the matrix phase’ by Hongyi Li et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA07956F.

title: Correction: Achieving excellent charge balance and transport in low-donor bulk heterojunctions for high-performance semitransparent organic photovoltaics
abstract: Correction for ‘Achieving excellent charge balance and transport in low-donor bulk heterojunctions for high-performance semitransparent organic photovoltaics’ by Juhui Oh et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA03918E.

title: Hydrogen-bond-regulated hierarchical porous organic polymers for enhanced photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production
abstract: Hydrogen-bonded hierarchical porous POPs enable the promotion of oxygen mass transfer for photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production.

title: Quick drying process: a promising strategy for preparing an egg-shell-type Cu/γ-Al 2 O 3 catalyst for direct N 2 O decomposition
abstract: This study aims to improve the drying rate by the quick drying (QD) method to prepare a thin egg-shell-type Cu/γ-Al 2 O 3 catalyst for deN 2 O with high surface exposure of active metal.

title: Li 0.33 La 0.557 TiO 3 @BaTiO 3 core–shell fiber as a filler to promote the dissociation and migration of lithium ions in solid polymer electrolytes
abstract: An Li 0.33 La 0.557 TiO 3 @BaTiO 3 (LLTO@BTO) core–shell fiber is synthesized. The BTO shell enhances lithium salt dissociation and protects LLTO, while LLTO facilitates rapid Li + transport. PEO-LLTO@BTO exhibits 1.44 mS cm −1 ionic conductivity at 30 °C.

title: Strain uniformity and defect passivation via 2D organic halide salts to improve the air stability of perovskite solar cells
abstract: The three halide variants (PEAX, X = I, Br, and Cl) were investigated for their impact on defect traps, charge recombination, ion migration, and charge-carrier dynamics by forming the 2D/3D heterostructure.

title: Operando and ex situ Raman spectroscopies for evaluating carbon structural changes in anode-free-type sulfide-based all-solid-state Li-ion batteries
abstract: Operando Raman spectroscopy revealed reversible Li intercalation into amorphous carbon and dynamic structural changes, while ex situ analysis showed gradual changes despite clear Li deposition, highlighting distinct behaviors under each condition.

title: Bio-based vinylogous urethane vitrimers from waste-wood lignosulfonate and enzymatic lignin: explorations in stress relaxation behavior and mechanical strength
abstract: This work introduces vinylogous urethane vitrimers derived from lignosulfonate and enzymatic lignin by direct acetoacetylation and subsequent formation of vitrimers to create homogeneous and recyclable lignin-based materials.

title: Hydrogen bond-mediated polar structure engineering of PVDF toward superior energy storage
abstract: Xylitol–PVDF delivers superior energy storage, as its hydrogen bonding enhances crystallinity and reduces grain size.

title: Tailoring gas separation performance in plastic crystal membranes with polymer additives
authors: 'Muhammad Shaharyar', 'Mega Kar', 'Luke A. O'Dell', 'Durga Acharya', 'Cara M. Doherty', 'Douglas R. MacFarlane', 'Jennifer M. Pringle'
abstract: The use of polymer or inorganic additives are shown to have a synergistic effect on the gas separation performance of plastic crystal membranes.

title: Synergistic electronic effect in high-entropy PdPtSnBiAg metallene for electrochemical reforming of PET plastic into glycolic acid
abstract: The high-entropy PdPtSnBiAg metallene has been developed for the high-performance electrocatalytic conversion of PET plastic-derived ethylene glycol into glycolic acid.

title: In situ generated air-stable inorganic-rich SEI of lithium anodes for dendrite-free lithium metal batteries
abstract: A solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) was constructed through a simple hot rolling process, with LiF, LiOH, Li 2 O, and Li x SiF y phases distributed to rapidly conduct Li + and Mg metal phase as nucleation sites to guide Li + for deposition. The dense SEI also blocked air and improved the air durabi…

title: High-efficiency photoelectrocatalytic oxygen evolution reaction enabled by MXene-derived TiO 2 coupled with FeP nanoparticles
abstract: MXene-derived TiO 2 coupled with FeP nanoparticles can capture photogenerated holes, inhibit charge recombination and promote charge transfer to FeP, thereby accelerating the OER kinetics.

title: Anti-clogging and anti-tangling fog harvesting with 3D-printed mesh-harp hybrids
abstract: The practicality and efficiency of fog harvesting are dramatically improved by developing mesh-harp hybrids that bypass the clogging issue of mesh harvesters and the elastocapillary tangling issue of fog harps.

title: Regulating d-band electrons of sulfur-enriched CoS x to weaken the S–Hads bond in CoS x /ZnCdS ohmic heterojunctions for enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: A ZnCdS/CoS x ohmic heterojunction for efficient charge separation was prepared. CoS x via sulfur enrichment and amorphization expose more S active sites, weaken S–Hads bonds, and enhance hydrogen desorption, boosting photocatalytic activity.

title: Ultrafast charge transfer in two-dimensional Janus heterostructures for photoconversion
abstract: Two-dimensional Janus van der Waals (vdW) heterostructures present potential applications in photocatalysis and photovoltaics.

title: Enhanced cycling stability of LiNiO 2 cathodes through a Mg/W dual-cation modification strategy
abstract: The effect of dual cation magnesium/tungsten substitution in ultra-Ni-rich LiNiO 2 cathodes was investigated. Combined bulk and surface stabilisation afforded through this coat-doping method improved the cyclability of these cathode materials.

title: Beyond traditional TOF: unveiling the pitfalls in electrocatalytic active site determination
abstract: TOF reflects intrinsic catalytic activity by measuring per-site efficiency, unlike current density. Accurate TOF estimation requires identifying true active sites and RDS, enabling rational design of efficient electrocatalysts for sustainable water splitting.

title: “Cyano group bridge”-based enabling high zinc-ion transference number and cycling stability of aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: An acrylamide–acrylonitrile copolymer is designed as an aqueous zinc-ion battery electrolyte additive, reducing side-reactions and improving the efficiency of Zn 2+ transference through a “cyano group bridge” improving the battery performance.

title: High-throughput and data-driven search for stable optoelectronic AMSe 3 materials
abstract: The study employs computational and data-driven methods to systematically screen AMSe 3 , identifying highly promising, stable candidates free of toxic elements, paving the way for their potential use in next-generation optoelectronic applications.

title: Directional electron pumping at a tailored organic D–A interface: fueling perylene diimide dianion (P 2− ) mediated efficient photocatalytic CO 2 conversion
abstract: Facilitating the generation and stabilization of the perylene diimide dianion (P 2− ) is essential to overcome the intrinsic reduction limitations of neutral perylene diimide (P) for efficient CO 2 conversion.

title: A multifunctional strategy to improve the efficiency and stability of organic solar cells via a 2PACz/MA composite hole transport layer
abstract: A novel 2PACz/MA composite HTL strategy improves the efficiency and stability of OSCs by optimizing interface quality.

title: Topotactic phase transformation of lithiated spinel to layered LiMn 0.5 Ni 0.5 O 2 : the interaction of 3-D and 2-D Li-ion diffusion
abstract: Newly discovered cubic Li-excess spinel (LxS) structured LiMn 0.5 Ni 0.5 O 2 cathode shows unique temperature-dependent topotactic phase transformation between 400–900 °C, affecting Li ion diffusion pathways and electrochemical performance.

title: Stabilizing ultrafine intermetallics on carbon supports: from structural design to catalytic applications
abstract: Ultrafine intermetallics (<5 nm) offer unique catalytic properties but face sintering challenges. We highlight carbon-supported synthesis strategies for precise control, focusing on electrocatalytic applications and future directions.

title: Systematic design of safe, high-energy lithium-ion batteries by merging intercalation and alloying anodes
abstract: The development of advanced anode materials is critical for enhancing the energy density and safety of lithium-ion batteries.

title: Recyclable, conductive alginate-based hydrogels with high stretchability and low electrical hysteresis for wireless wearable sensors
abstract: Highly stretchable, conductive, and recyclable alginate/PEDOT:PSS hydrogels, ionically cross-linked with CaCl 2 , offer low electrical hysteresis and enable wireless, real-time soft-sensor monitoring for sustainable wearables.

title: Recent advances and future prospects of flexible and wearable applications based on liquid metal demands
abstract: Liquid metals have garnered significant attention from researchers in recent years, and possess fascinating characteristics originating from their simultaneous metallic and liquid qualities.

title: Dual alkali-carbonate activated nitrogen-doped carbons as oxygen reduction reaction electrocatalysts: a study of porosity structure effects
abstract: Structural and textural properties of N-doped carbon oxygen reduction reaction electrocatalysts have clear influence on electrocatalytic performance independent of N-content, especially on half-wave potential.

title: Disorder by design: high-entropy oxides as next generation thermoelectric materials
abstract: Entropy-engineered oxides for thermoelectric energy.

title: Optimizing d–p orbital hybridization by tuning high-entropy spinel oxides for enhanced alkaline OER efficiency
abstract: CoMn-rich HESO was synthesized using the molten salt method. The Co–Mn synergy reduces the energy barrier and boosts electron transfer, enhancing OER performance and providing a novel strategy for optimizing electrocatalyst efficiency.

title: Single-atom alloys for photocatalytic applications: material innovation and light-driven reactivity
abstract: Single-atom alloys are presented through synthesis, mechanisms, and photocatalytic applications, highlighting atomic-level and alloying effects in light-driven catalysis.

title: A solar-driven heterojunction hydrogel with reversible phase transition for efficient photocatalytic decontamination and freshwater generation
abstract: Wastewater purification using the commonly used solar-driven hydrogel with phase transition-induced mechanisms is restricted by limited adsorption capacity, a narrow working scope and finite stability.

title: Simultaneous shaping and reduction of binder-free electrochemically reduced graphene oxide electrodes through cathodic electrophoretic deposition (EPD)
abstract: Colloidal processing and cathodic electrophoretic deposition (EPD) of graphene oxide (GO) to obtain high-performance ErGO supercapacitor electrodes.

title: Free volume design promoting high-temperature capacitive energy storage of all-organic polymer films
abstract: By regulating the free volume and utilizing the electron affinity difference between FPE and PEI, inhibited charge injection and achieved excellent energy storage performance at 150 °C, opening a new path for high-performance dielectric capacitors.

title: An ultrastable porphyrin covalent organic framework with protected imine linkages for highly efficient and sustainable photocatalytic oxidation of amines
abstract: An ultrastable porphyrin COF with imine units protected by rigid bulky groups was constructed for highly recyclable, selective, efficient and gram-scale photocatalytic oxidation of amines to symmetric and asymmetric imines.

title: Interlayer-stacking mode modulation in an imine covalent organic framework for efficient photocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: An AB-stacking COF outperforms its AA-stacking isomer as a photocatalyst for hydrogen production, with the HER increasing from 46.8 to 109.6 mmol g −1 h −1 . AB-stacking outperforming AA-stacking is a rare and notable example in the COF field.

title: Molecular insights into an ether-functionalised ionic liquid electrolyte with hydrogen-modified anions at electrode interfaces
authors: 'Sreehari Batni Ravindranath', 'Jhonatan Soto Puelles', 'Agilio Padua', 'Luke A. O'Dell', 'Michel Armand', 'Patrick Howlett', 'Maria Forsyth', 'Fangfang Chen'
abstract: Computational insights reveal how ether-functionalised cations and H-modified anions tune the bulk and interfacial properties of an ionic liquid electrolyte.

title: Multicolor solid-state fluorescence and multicolor afterglow carbon dots: preparation, luminescence regulation, and applications
abstract: We review multicolor solid-state fluorescence and multicolor afterglow carbon dots (CDs) in perspectives of synthesis methods, luminescence modulation mechanisms, and applications.

title: A Li-enriched amorphous Zr-based oxychloride solid electrolyte for high-rate and long-cycling all-solid-state ultrahigh-nickel cathodes
abstract: Oxygen substitution and Li-ion-enriched strategy enhance the kinetics and solid–solid contact of amorphous Zr-based oxychloride solid electrolytes in ASSLBs, enabling high-rate and long-cycling all-solid-state ultrahigh-nickel cathodes.

title: Unraveling the role of crystallization kinetics for fibrillar morphology optimization in all-polymer solar cells
abstract: In situ GIWAXS/GISAXS reveal how crystallization and phase separation kinetics govern fibrillar morphology optimization, enhancing photovoltaic performance in all-polymer solar cells.

title: Boosting stability in Ni-rich cathodes: a synergistic approach to surface and bulk modifications for advanced lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A TiNb 2 O 7 coating on LiNi 0.83 Mn 0.06 Co 0.11 O 2 enhances structural stability, reduces side reactions, and improves lithium diffusion, boosting capacity retention from 58.6% to 81.9% after cycling – offering a robust strategy for Ni-rich cathodes.

title: Preparation of iron–manganese hexacyanoferrate from insoluble salts and mechanistic insights into enhanced sodium storage cycle stability via electrolyte modification
abstract: Synthesis of highly crystalline Fe–MnHCF from insoluble salts and its enhanced electrochemical performance by adding MnCl 2 in the electrolyte.

title: Polymers for perovskite solar cells: advances and perspectives
abstract: This review summarizes recent advancements of polymers in perovskite solar cells (PSCs), revealing their mechanisms and guiding future developments.

title: Multifactor optimization of microwave absorption properties in Co/CNT@SiC composites via temperature-driven structural and compositional modulation
abstract: Co particle-loaded carbon nanotube (CNT)–SiC nanowire composites were prepared by heat polymerization and carbonization processes.

title: Exploring modification strategies to enhance energy storage performance of BOPP dielectric films
abstract: Various modification strategies for enhancing energy storage performance of BOPP films fall into two categories of pre-stretching and post-stretching treatments.

title: Enhanced hydrogen production and fuel conversion of an iron-based oxygen carrier in the chemical looping process by a small co-addition of CuO and NiO
abstract: Nearly complete fuel conversion and high H 2 yield are obtained using Fe 2 O 3 –Al 2 O 3 with a small co-addition of CuO and NiO.

title: The effect of a CuO sintering additive on the sinterability of Ba-based perovskite electrolytes for protonic ceramic electrochemical cell applications
abstract: This work provides insight into the chemical features of a CuO sintering additive used to achieve desirable sinterability in Ba-based perovskite oxide electrolytes.

title: Microstructure-informed performance boost in solid oxide fuel cells through multiphysical modeling and machine learning
abstract: Machine learning co-optimizes SOFC macro-microstructures, boosting power density by 29% while controlling failure risk.

title: Untangling the potential of non-entangled bottlebrush block copolymers as separator coating materials for high-rate and long-life sodium metal batteries
abstract: A bottlebrush block copolymer (BBP) coating on conventional separators enables the development of high-performance sodium (Na) metal batteries by regulating stable Na electrodeposition and electrolyte–electrode interphases.

title: From lab to market: the future of zinc–air batteries powered by MOF/MXene hybrids
abstract: Zinc–air batteries (ZABs) stand at the forefront of energy storage technologies. However, challenges like slow kinetics and low rechargeability persist. MOF–MXene hybrids enhance performance, enabling sustainable ZAB technology.

title: Design of lightweight BCC multi-principal element alloys with enhanced hydrogen storage using a machine learning-driven genetic algorithm
abstract: Body-centered cubic (BCC) based multi-principal element alloy (MPEA) hydrides have demonstrated significant potential for compact and efficient hydrogen storage.

title: Exploring cascaded adsorption cooling cycles with MOF-hydrocarbon integration: an accelerated computational framework using Bayesian optimisation and Monte Carlo simulations
abstract: Three-stage screening of the CoRE MOF 2019 database identified MOF combinations with the highest COP for cascaded ARCs using MOF–propane and MOF–isobutane, achieving COP >1 for both refrigerants with Bayesian optimization.

title: Effect of molecular size on the electrocatalytic activity of M-N 4 -C catalysts for ORR, OER, and HER
abstract: This study uses density functional theory to analyze the size-dependent electrocatalytic performance of M-N 4 -C catalysts, revealing stability and activity fluctuations, particularly for n C@FeN 4 and n C@CoN 4 .

title: Monocarboxylic acid etching strategy: modulation of the chemical environment of Ni nanoparticles in defective Ce-UiO-66 to construct heterogeneous interfaces for dicyclopentadiene hydrogenation
abstract: Mesoporous Ce-UiO-66 with abundant missing sites was obtained via acetic acid etching strategy, which can anchor Ni NPs with Ce–O–Ni interfaces, showing high conversion and selectivity in the hydrogenation reaction of dicyclopentadiene.

title: Electrocatalytic microdevices based on transition metal dichalcogenides for hydrogen evolution
abstract: Chemical reactions and electronic properties are two vital aspects in the systematic study of electrocatalytic reactions.

title: Characterization of densification behavior and mechanical properties of solid electrolyte powders for all solid-state batteries
abstract: BH 4 -substituted argyrodite solid electrolytes are mechanically softer and achieve near-theoretical density at lower pressures and room temperature compared to Cl- or Br-substituted counterparts, which is consistent with nanoindentation test results.

title: Deciphering the functions of rubidium in structural stability and ionic conductivity of KAg 4 I 5
abstract: Rb + doping improves ionic conductivity of KAg 4 I 5 to 0.15 S cm −1 with an activation energy of 19 meV. MaxEnt method reveals Ag + migrates between Ag1 and Ag2 sites along (100) plane, offering an approach to map ionic transport pathways.

title: Fused benzotriazole as the A′ unit to construct A–D–A′–D–A-type non-fused ring electron acceptors for efficient organic solar cells
abstract: A–D–A′–D–A-type non-fused ring electron acceptors (NFREAs) are a kind of important photovoltaic materials, and thus developing high-performance electron-deficient central units is crucial for further enhancing their photovoltaic properties.

title: Redox-mediated bipolar hydrogen production and furoic acid electrosynthesis
abstract: A three-chamber cell utilizing redox mediators enabled on-demand energy storage and hydrogen release. Continuous hydrogen and furoic acid production was achieved without separate discharge steps, boosting the time and energy efficiency.

title: Capacity enhancement of LaAl 2 Ag ternary alloy anode for fluoride-ion batteries
abstract: We demonstrate the LaAl 2 Ag ternary alloy as a new anode for fluoride-ion batteries. Compared to pure Al metal and LaAl 3 binary alloy anodes, the LaAl 2 Ag anode shows a high reversible capacity of more than 400 mA h g −1 with good cycle stability.

title: Phase transformation of a ZIF-on-ZIF heterostructure for uniform NiCo incorporation with enhanced oxygen evolution performance
abstract: The coupling of a “ZIF-on-ZIF” core–shell assembly with in situ ligand-exchange can achieve CoNi alloy nanoparticles uniformly embedded in a hollow N-doped carbon matrix with unprecedently high performance in oxygen evolution reactions.

title: Heteroatom-enhanced dual-ion storage of a thiophene-based bipyridine polymer for high-capacity and high-rate lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A heteroatom sulfur- and nitrogen- embeded polymer, 2,2′-bithiophene-linked bipyridine, was prepared via in situ electropolymerization, achieving a compromise between low 'dead mass' and high activity with high energy density.

title: Ligand exchange-based synthesis of azide-functionalized Cu nanospheres as a platform for Mn complex immobilization and electrochemical CO 2 reduction
abstract: Functionalization of colloidal Cu nanospheres with azide-decorated aliphatic ligands by affinity-driven partial ligand exchange reactions. The azide handle is used for attaching a Mn complex to study this assembly as a tandem catalyst in the eCO 2 RR.

title: Morphology tuned viologen-based covalent organic frameworks: a fast and targeted approach to eliminate toxic organic pollutants from water
abstract: Rod- and sphere-shaped ionic viologen COFs were synthesized to investigate morphology-dependent adsorption for water decontamination. Experimental and theoretical findings demonstrate selective capture of anionic and neutral dyes by rod-shaped vCOFs.

title: The leveraging ether C–H bond shielding strategy for antioxidative electrolyte in lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The conjugation effect, induction effect, and molecular structure collectively influence the antioxidative properties.

title: A cluster-assembled B 26 monolayer and its inverse-sandwiched derivative B 24 P 4 : stable 2D boron-based materials with topological properties, superconductivity, and electrocatalytic activity
abstract: The rich bonding versatility of boron enables the design of exotic two-dimensional (2D) monolayers with multifunctional properties.

title: Solvent-free lithium-ion battery electrodes with ultrahigh loading using a reactive epoxy nanobinder
abstract: EPs provide strong bonding to LTO through covalent bonds. A series of HDEs with gradient mass loadings were prepared, achieving a maximum mass loading of 31 mg cm −2 , corresponding to an areal capacity of 3.6 mA h cm −2 .

title: “Channel” or “container”? Effect of the pore structure on ion transport in porous MXene electrodes
abstract: The pore structures on MXene electrodes act as “channels” and “containers” during electrolyte ion transport, facilitating the movement and distribution of the ions but also altering the effective charged area, affecting electrode performance.

title: Regulation of nitrogen reduction reaction catalytic performance by varying the sp/sp 2 hybrid carbon ratio in graphyne/graphene heterojunction catalysts
abstract: This work systematically investigates the influence of the sp/sp 2 hybrid carbon ratio on the NRR catalytic performance of Ti@GY/Gr heterojunctions and explores the underlying mechanisms and relevant descriptor relationships.

title: Thermal evolution of sol–gel-synthesized 8YSZ thin films: insights from coupled in situ synchrotron diffraction and electrical conductivity measurements
abstract: The thin film approach for Solid Oxide Cell (SOC) electrolytes offers a pathway to reduce the high fabrication and operating temperatures of these devices. In situ insights on the crystallization and electrical conductivity of 8YSZ thin-films.

title: Electrospun zein-based conductive composites engineered with versatile nanomaterials for high-performance supercapacitors
abstract: Corn-derived zein was electrospun into fibrous mats and modified by VPP and hybrid coatings with QD, GO, and MO. The resulting zein-based composite electrodes exhibit high performance for energy storage applications.

title: SnS 2 /covalent organic framework S-scheme heterostructures for photocatalytic water splitting: insights from ground-state properties and nonadiabatic excited-state dynamics
abstract: SnS 2 /COF3 is a promising photocatalyst for water splitting. Excited-state carrier dynamics simulations show that compressive strain can further enhance its photocatalytic performance.

title: Correction: Mixed metal–antimony oxide nanocomposites: low pH water oxidation electrocatalysts with outstanding durability at ambient and elevated temperatures
abstract: Correction for ‘Mixed metal–antimony oxide nanocomposites: low pH water oxidation electrocatalysts with outstanding durability at ambient and elevated temperatures’ by Sibimol Luke et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2021, 9 , 27468–27484, https://doi.org/10.1039/D1TA07293E.

title: Correction: Biscoumarin-containing acenes as stable organic semiconductors for photocatalytic oxygen reduction to hydrogen peroxide
abstract: Correction for ‘Biscoumarin-containing acenes as stable organic semiconductors for photocatalytic oxygen reduction to hydrogen peroxide’ by Marek K. Węcławski et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2017, 5 , 20780–20788, https://doi.org/10.1039/C7TA05882A.

title: Intrinsic proton relay in poly-phosphamides to bolster proton exchange membrane fabrication and electrocatalytic proton reduction
abstract: Facile proton conduction within poly-phosphamide network and presence of redox active {P(O)–NH} moiety makes it a potential alternative to Nafion-based proton-exchange membrane (PEM) material and metal-free hydrogen evolution reaction catalyst.

title: Creation of an immiscible OsPd solid solution as a Rh-free catalyst with NO x reduction performance exceeding that of Rh
abstract: An atomic-level homogeneously distributed OsPd immiscible solid solution was synthesized for the first time with NO x reduction performance exceeding that of Rh.

title: Nickel-mediated dynamic interfaces with dual spillover pathways in Mo 2 C/Ni/Fe 3 O 4 for water splitting
abstract: Nickel-mediated interfacial design in Mo 2 C/Ni/Fe 3 O 4 ternary heterostructures establishes decoupled hydrogen/oxygen-containing intermediate spillover pathways via interfacial Ni–C–Mo and Ni–O–Fe interactions and moderation.

title: High-performance Co 4 S 3 -MnS-MoS 2 @CC catalysts for the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: The incorporation of trace amounts of Co 4 S 3 and MnS significantly enhances both the catalytic activity and stability of MoS 2 .

title: A silver paste engineered interphase enables highly reversible and dendrite-free Zn metal anodes
abstract: Constructing a zincophilic heterometallic composite layer to regulate the electrochemical behaviors of Zn metal anodes by a facile spray-coating technique.

title: Catalysing holistic wastewater treatment, electricity generation, and emerging contaminant removal in a pre-pilot Fenton-microbial fuel cell
abstract: Novel 2.5 L Fenton-microbial fuel cell with rGO–Co 3 O 4 cathode achieved 258.33 mA m −2 , 218 mg L −1 H 2 O 2 , and high degradation of Congo red (89.41%), tetracycline (74%), and SDS (72%). Effluent had low COD, no pathogens, and LCA showed scalability.

title: Regulating high-temperature energy storage of crosslinked polyetherimides through a functional crosslinking agent
abstract: A multi-effect synergistic crosslinking strategy combining large conjugated dihedral angles and deep hole traps is proposed to improve the high-temperature energy storage properties of PEI.

title: Structure transformations and ionic conductivity in germanides of sodium and potassium
abstract: Using DFT and machine learning, this study explores Na/K germanides' structure and conductivity; Na–Ge exhibits superior ionic conductivity due to interconnected diffusion, making it a promising anode material.

title: Advancements in water electrolysis: enhancing hydrogen and oxygen production efficiency through electrocatalyst design and urea oxidation
abstract: In light of growing energy shortages and environmental challenges, hydrogen energy has emerged as a promising renewable energy alternative to fossil fuels.

title: Boosting electrocatalytic nitrate reduction to ammonia with a Cu/Ag-Ru tandem catalyst at industrial-scale current density
abstract: For a tandem electrode of (Cu 7 /Ag 3 ) 7 -Ru 3 /C, the (Cu 7 /Ag 3 ) 7 heterostructure maximized the formation of NO 2 − , and Ru nanoparticles exhibited excellent NO 2 − adsorption and water dissociation, facilitating the hydrogenation of NO 2 − and avoiding the HER.

title: The mixed-halogen layer approach of band engineering and anisotropic charge migration in X1X2 Sillén nanosheets boost cocatalyst-free photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: A mixed-halide layer strategy with nanosheet formation delivers a photostable Sillén phase catalyst featuring directional charge flow and significantly higher reduction potential with excellent photocatalytic hydrogen evolution.

title: Deciphering interfacial interactions in a dual-functional MOF@COF composite for organic pollutant removal from water
abstract: A Fe-MOF@COF composite removes organic pollutants efficiently, with synchrotron insights revealing how interfacial synergy drives performance.

title: High-performance biomass-based solar-thermal hydrogel based on silkworm excrement for water evaporation and thermoelectric integration application
abstract: A strategy is designed to prepare the efficient biomass solar-thermal material through animal metabolism, then used to construct a 2D solar evaporator silkworm excrement hydrogel, which exhibits high solar-to-vapor efficiency.

title: Ligand-assisted assembly of FeCo–phytate catalytic interfaces on WO 3 photoanodes for dual enhancement of charge separation and oxygen evolution kinetics
abstract: The PA–FeCo layer acts as a dual-function co-catalyst that synergistically accelerates OER kinetics and enhances surface charge separation.

title: High-throughput NEB for Li-ion conductor discovery via fine-tuned CHGNet potential
abstract: Schematic of the automated workflow in this study.

title: Hyperphilic/hydrophobic hybridized surfaces for efficient fog harvesting
abstract: In this paper, ZrO 2 nanoparticles, and micron CuO/nanoscale Cu 2 O particles were selected to achieve the coexistence of superhydrophilicity and superhydrophobicity on the same surface.

title: Synergistic Fe,Pd diatomic sites anchored on porous nitrogen-doped carbon for efficient oxygen reduction in the entire pH range
abstract: Highly dispersed Fe and Pd dual atomic sites on porous nitrogen doped carbon were prepared by salt-template assisted pyrolysis, constructing highly active and durable ORR catalysts in all electrolytes covering the entire pH range.

title: Pressurized well-inspired triboelectric nanogenerators for harvesting water wave energy toward marine environmental applications
abstract: An ancient pressurized well device inspired lever-based triboelectric nanogenerator shows good electrical output and potential for self-powered anticorrosion and blue energy harvesting.

title: Roll-to-roll slot-die coating of PTAA with PEDOT:PSS buffer layer for perovskite solar cells: coating analysis by XPS mapping
abstract: Boosting efficiency in roll-to-roll deposited perovskite solar cells using PEDOT:PSS as a buffer layer for the depostion of PTAA.

title: A Mo-cation/O-anion doping strategy for creating vacancy defects and cation multivalency to enhance the hydrogen evolution of ZnS under visible light
abstract: A stable and photocorrosion-resistant Mo-cation/O-anion co-doped ZnS with sulfur vacancy defects and bivalent Mo 4+ /Mo 6+ states exhibits an excellent photocatalytic hydrogen evolution activity of 41.6 mmol g −1 h −1 under visible light.

title: Gradient migration hydrogenation of adsorbed hydrogen modulated by anasymmetric configuration of S vacancies with atomic Zn dopant for photocatalytic CO 2 methanation
abstract: The migration hydrogenation of surface adsorbed hydrogen mechanism is proposed on asymmetric configuration of S vacancy induced by atomic Zn substitution confined into In 2 S 3 nanosheet for photocatalytic CO 2 methanation.

title: Facile construction of hierarchically porous carbon nanofibers modified by an FeCu/FeF 3 heterojunction for oxygen electrocatalysis in liquid and flexible Zn–air batteries
abstract: A novel strategy for the structural design and heterojunction material modification, which involves the FeCu/FeF 3 @HPCNFs as an efficient bi-functional oxygen electrocatalyst, is proposed, prepared and applied for high-performance ZABs.

title: Moisture-resistant perovskite solar cells: the role of 1,1′-methylenebispyridinium dichloride in enhancing stability and performance
abstract: Incorporating DipyCl 2 into perovskite solar cells boosts moisture resistance and operational stability, achieving a power conversion efficiency of 24.2% and retaining 80% of efficiency after 2000 hours of air exposure.

title: Triplet excited-state charge transfer induced in indolenine-based squaraine for enhanced visible-light-driven H 2 evolution
abstract: This work introduces a series of indolenine-based squaraine derivatives with diverse N-based side chains, and their monothio- and dithio-analogues coupled with Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene serve as organic–inorganic hybrid photocatalysts for hydrogen evolution.

title: Antioxidative, low-concentration MXene inks with high-viscosity for infrared encryption and thermal energy harvesting
abstract: Low-concentration MXene inks with high viscosity were reported using a gel-assisted approach. It offers a feasible strategy for realizing high-viscosity processing and excellent antioxidant capacity of MXene ink.

title: Dendrite-free, interfacially compatible Na 2 ZrCl 6 composite halide solid-state electrolyte for solid-state sodium-ion batteries
abstract: The Na 2 ZrCl 6 –PAN composite solid electrolyte achieves an excellent ionic conductivity of 1.21 × 10 −4 S cm −1 at 30 °C a wide electrochemical window of 4.37 V, and exhibits favorable interfacial stability when contact with Na metal and Na 3 V 2 (PO 4 ) 3 .

title: Machine learning-driven gait-assisted self-powered wearable sensing: a triboelectric nanogenerator-based advanced healthcare monitoring
abstract: A self-powered TENG-based machine learning-driven insole wearable sensing system for gait-assisted healthcare is designed to classify flat foot conditions, identify users, and monitor rehabilitation and athletic exercises accurately.

title: Aqueous all-iron flow batteries using surface-modified carbon electrodes improved via electrochemical exfoliation
abstract: A facile electrochemical exfoliation strategy enables the fabrication of high activity carbon electrodes with enhanced wettability and redox kinetics, significantly boosting the performance of aqueous all-iron flow batteries.

title: Interface engineering in polymer thermoelectric composites: harnessing energy-filtering effects to overcome the Seebeck-conductivity trade-off
