abstract: A systematic review of energy filtering (EF) engineering in polymer-based TE composites is presented. The effect of EF on ionic organic films, organic–inorganic composites, MXene-based hybrids and aerogel composites is analysed.

title: Synergistic enhancement of Cu 2 Se thermoelectric properties via Te and S co-doping: aqueous synthesis and cold-press sintering for power generation
abstract: Materials including Cu 2 Se, Cu 2 Se 0.96− x S x Te 0.04 ( x = 0.00–0.03) and Cu 2− y Se 0.94 S 0.02 Te 0.04 ( y = 0.00–0.04) were synthesized using aqueous solution processing at room temperature and cold-press sintered under vacuum at 650 °C for 6 hours.

title: Elucidating the effect of nanocube support morphology on the hydrogenolysis of polypropylene over Ni/CeO 2 catalysts
abstract: The nanocube support morphology improves C–C scission rates of Ni/CeO 2 catalysts to generate shorter chain liquid hydrocarbon mixtures in the catalytic hydrogenolysis process.

title: Structural evolution by heat treatment of soft and hard carbons as Li storage materials: a joint NMR/XRD/TEM/Raman study
abstract: Thermally annealed soft carbon and hard carbon samples were investigated by a variety of methods.

title: Covalent photo-crosslinking of diketopyrrolopyrrole based polymeric layers for cutting-edge near-IR absorption and DSSC performance
abstract: Two novel photo-crosslinkable polymers based on diketopyrrolopyrrole with extended NIR light absorption up to 1100 nm utilized DPP-OCT-CBZ-C barrier layers to achieve 18.2% and 7.28% PCE in DSSCs at 800 lux and 1 sun, respectively.

title: Potential dependent degradation of spinel LiMn 2 O 4 (LMO) and related structures assessed via manganese- and oxygen-sensitive scanning electrochemical microscopy
abstract: Small electrodes capable of detecting Mn dissolution and oxygen evolution are placed near operating Mn-based lithium-ion battery cathodes to track their degradation, informing on mechanism and revealing how additives might help decrease degradation.

title: Structurally disordered In 2 Te 3 semiconductor: novel insights
abstract: The crystal structures of low- (α) and high-temperature (β) modifications of In 2 Te 3 are refined for the first time.

title: Artificial intelligence assisted nanogenerator applications
abstract: This review examines the integration of artificial intelligence with nanogenerators to develop self-powered, adaptive systems for applications in robotics, wearables, and environmental monitoring.

title: Tuning intermediates' adsorption for efficient electrosynthesis of urea from carbon dioxide and nitrate via doping Au into Cu
abstract: Gold doping engineering was proposed to balance the adsorption of CO and NO on the catalyst surface, thereby promoting C–N coupling to form urea.

title: Constructing MnO 2 –CuMn 2 O 4 interfaces to enhance the activation of surface lattice oxygen for efficient toluene combustion
abstract: An interface constructed between MnO 2 and CuMn 2 O 4 regulated the microelectronic structure, ultimately achieving excellent catalytic performance for toluene purification.

title: UiO-66 (Zr) metal–organic framework for photocatalytic CO 2 reduction: functional design strategies for sustainable carbon utilization
abstract: UiO-66 (Zr), well-acknowledged as an outstanding MOF nanomaterial, has emerged as a promising candidate for photocatalytic CO 2 reduction—a green and sustainable process that utilizes renewable solar energy to convert CO 2 into valuable products.

title: Engineering stable p-type contacts towards efficient fully vacuum deposited perovskite solar cells
abstract: Self-assembled molecules enhance hole extraction, fill factors, and stability as high work function interlayers in vacuum-deposited perovskite solar cells, outperforming state-of-the art alternatives.

title: Rational design of ultrathin, functionalized UiO-66 mixed-matrix membranes for CO 2 separation: balancing CO 2 uptake and interfacial compatibility
abstract: Developing high-performance membranes for post-combustion CO 2 separation is essential in addressing the ongoing climate crisis.

title: Rh single-atom catalysts with optimized metal loading for direct CO-feed high-temperature proton exchange membrane fuel cells
abstract: The presence of carbon monoxide (CO) in crude hydrogen is a significant factor hindering the commercialization of hydrogen fuel cells.

title: Resolving the atomic structure of γ-alumina: a non-spinel phase with a distorted anion lattice and three adjacent long channels
abstract: Identify the most representative model for γ-alumina from machine learning global optimization.

title: Interface-engineered core–shell nanoparticle embedded polymer nanocomposite demonstrating low voltage and temperature dependent tunable dielectric response
abstract: Ferroelectric polymer composites with nonlinear dielectric properties are promising candidates for flexible electronics and microwave systems.

title: Dual-functional additives enabling a high-performance silicon–carbon composite anode in pouch cells at high temperatures
abstract: Optimized 8% FEC improves NCM811/Si–C cell cycling at room temperature but fails at high temperature. With 0.8% SA forming dual-additive system, high temperature performance of cells enhances via thermally stable Li 2 CO 3 -rich SEI.

title: Taming of 3,4-di(nitramino)furazan II:high-energy coordination complexes with exceptional catalytic activity for the thermal decomposition of ammonium perchlorate
authors: 'Jizhou Dong', 'Jinchao Ma', 'Shuyue Xu', 'Xingyang Cui', 'Hua Qian', 'Jean'ne M. Shreeve'
abstract: A series of high-energy solid propellant catalysts, with improved thermal stability, reduced sensitivity, and excellent AP thermal decomposition catalytic activity, were successfully synthesized from high-energy yet sensitive DNAF.

title: PtFeCoNiMoYZr/YZrO heterojunction catalysis for anion exchange membrane water electrolyzers
abstract: Heterojunction catalysts play an important role among emerging catalytic systems, as they can significantly optimize the surface adsorption energy of active sites and enhance catalytic activity.

title: Hierarchical sub-stoichiometric isomeric covalent organic frameworks with diverse topologies derived from the same monomers for efficient atmospheric water harvesting
abstract: Subchemically synthesized COF isomers from identical monomers with different topologies exhibited multi-step water adsorption in varied pores.

title: Utilizing symmetry operations for high-throughput ferroelectric materials discovery
abstract: This work computationally discovers new ferroelectric materials by applying symmetry operations within a high-throughput screening workflow.

title: Delineating the impact of Ti/Mg substitution in P2-type Na 2/3 Ni 1/3 Mn 2/3 O 2 with an advanced electrolyte for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: The systematic influence of titanium/magnesium substitution into NaNi 1/3 Mn 2/3 O 2 is discussed with a nuanced focus on surface reactivity. Results demonstrate that structural stability achieved by doping is less consequential to capacity fade.

title: Room-temperature barocaloric effect in [Fe(pap-5NO 2 ) 2 ] spin-crossover material
abstract: We report a giant barocaloric effect for a spin-crossover material. We employ a combination of high-pressure calorimetry and powder X-ray diffraction measurements.

title: 3D printing of architected sulfur cathodes with dual-site atomic catalysts for accelerated polysulfide kinetics and Li-ion transport in high areal-loading lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Current-collector-free 3D-printed sulfur cathode with dual Co/Ni single-atom sites on N-doped carbon, enabling high sulfur loading, rapid redox kinetics, and long cycle life in Li–S batteries.

title: Synergistic strain and N-doping for creating physical orientation selectivity in chemical etching of graphene nanoribbons
abstract: An innovative synthesis method combining physical stress and nitrogen doping for directional etching of graphene, enabling precise control over GNR edge and width structures with superior ORR activity.

title: Polyfurfuryl alcohol-derived hierarchical porous carbon as high-performance cathodes for lithium–sulfur batteries with high sulfur loadings
abstract: This study addresses fundamental challenges in lithium–sulfur battery technology through the development of a novel hierarchical porous carbon material.

title: Decoding aromatic diamine polymer for highly stable aqueous ammonium-ion storage with multiple redox-active sites
abstract: Poly( o -phenylenediamine) delivers a high ammonium-ion storage capacity and ultralong cycling stability with redox-active sites of CN groups and aromatic rings.

title: All-gas-phase preparation of organic/inorganic heterolayered multifunctional electrodes for hybrid-type energy storage
abstract: Lithium-ion hybrid capacitors (LHCs) are increasingly recognized as promising energy storage devices due to their ability to achieve high energy density while delivering rapid power delivery.

title: Unveiling critical role of metal oxide infiltration in controlling the surface oxygen exchange activity and polarization of SrTi 1− x Fe x O 3− δ perovskite oxide electrodes
abstract: Surface oxygen exchange coefficients, k chem , of SrTi 1− x / 100 Fe x /100 O 3− δ ( x = 35, 50, 80) before and after CaO-activation and Al 2 O 3 -deactivation at 575 °C.

title: Linear PDI-based conjugated polymers with directional charge transport driving photocatalytic water oxidation
abstract: A linear conjugated organic polymer based on PDI was designed for directional charge transfer, it exhibits efficient charge separation that move along the chain direction. The above properties enable water oxidation for O 2 production by PT-CB.

title: Maximizing oxygen permeation via catalytic functionalization under oxyfuel conditions
abstract: OORR catalysts improved surface exchange kinetics in Fe 2 NiO 4 –Ce 0.8 Tb 0.2 O 2− δ membranes, boosting oxygen flux six-fold and retaining performance in CO 2 /SO 2 -rich conditions, enabling robust, efficient high-temperature oxygen transport membranes.

title: Boosting oxygen reduction and Zn–air battery performance via the rational design of interlayer pyridinic nitrogen in Zn-N 4 -hierarchical porous carbon nanofibers
abstract: Zn-N 4 -HPCNFs exhibit superior ORR activity (half-wave potential at 0.88 V) and Zn–air battery performance (specific capacity of 855 mA h g −1 ).

title: Molecular engineering of ethereal electrolyte for ultrastable Si-based high voltage full cells
abstract: The successful application of Si-based high-energy Li-ion batteries (LIBs) depends on our ability to tailor electrolyte properties to achieve long-term stability and reliable performance.

title: Insights into the role of the phosphate group in vastly improved adsorption of thorium and uranium using a triptycene-based knitted aryl polymer network
abstract: Efficient and selective removal of Th and U from aqueous solutions using a triptycene-based knitted aryl polymer.

title: Lithium extraction from low-grade brines via strain-induced electronic structure modulation of MnO 2 nanorods through Mg incorporation
abstract: FLA-MnO 2 features flattened [MnO 6 ] octahedra, suppressing J–T distortion and enhancing Li + reversibility. It delivers 619 F g −1 capacitance and high capacity retention. In Li + capture, it shows 82%/100 cycle stability and high Li + selectivity.

title: Development of a spin selective electrocatalyst platform and its use to study spin-polarization and d-orbital occupancy effects in oxygen evolution reaction electrocatalysts
abstract: This work shows that spin transport layers enhance the efficiency of OER catalysts, regardless of location on a volcano plot, so long as the catalyst has unpaired electrons in its d-orbitals.

title: Engineering Ru nanoparticle size and metal–support interactions for enhanced catalytic hydrogen combustion
abstract: Optimised Ru nanoparticle size on γAl 2 O 3 enhances catalytic hydrogen combustion efficiency through tailored metal–support interactions.

title: Cutting-edge advances for the positive electrode in aqueous K-ion batteries: exploring electrochemical properties, structural designs, and applicable perspectives
abstract: This paper summarizes positive electrode materials for aqueous K-ion batteries, analyzing their electrochemical performance and modification strategies, while providing perspectives on positive electrode materials.

title: Anode engineering using a hybrid AlCl 3 /PTHF coating for enhanced electrochemical stability of Mg–O 2 batteries
abstract: A hybrid AlCl 3 /PTHF coating on the Mg anode forms a stable mixed ionic SEI, enabling uniform Mg 2+ transport, reducing corrosion, and lowering overpotential—key to improving electrochemical stability in Mg–O 2 batteries.

title: Graphyne-based single atom catalysts for the oxygen reduction reaction: a constant-potential first-principles study
abstract: We investigated the electrocatalytic ORR performance of M-GY and N-doped M-GY by using constant-potential first-principles calculations. Among them, N 0 -Co-GY exhibited superior ORR performance both in acidic and alkaline environments.

title: Tailoring platinum content in a PdAuMoWPt high-entropy alloy for efficient and durable hydrogen evolution across a broad pH range
abstract: The development of highly efficient hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) electrocatalysts that combine low overpotentials with broad pH adaptability remains a formidable challenge.

title: In situ grown superhydrophobic ZIF-71 layer enabling ultra-stable zinc anodes for long-cycle AZBs
abstract: ZIF-71 is a zeolite imidazolate framework, characterized by hydrophobic nanochannels that block the hydration of [Zn(H 2 O) 6 ] 2+ complexes, promote the diffusion of Zn 2+ , and help partially hinder the formation of Zn dendrites.

title: Composition engineering of a Cu 2 ZnGe x Sn 1− x S 4 nanoparticle hole transport layer for carbon electrode-based perovskite solar cells
abstract: Composition engineering via alloying Cu 2 ZnSnS 4 and Cu 2 ZnGeS 4 is studied to optimize the properties of a Cu 2 ZnSn 1− x Ge x S 4 hole-transporting layer, which contributes to carbon electrode-based perovskite solar cells with a champion efficiency of 19.8%.

title: Modulation of crystallization dynamics via multifunctional additive engineering to achieve high-performance perovskite solar cells
abstract: High-quality perovskite crystal formation and re-distribution of PbI 2 were realized by multifunctional additive engineering for perovskite solar cells.

title: Inverse vulcanised sulfur polymers as hydrophilic coatings
abstract: Hydrophilic sulfur polymers were synthesized via inverse vulcanisation using commercial comonomers. These polymers show strong potential for Hg 2+ capture as coatings.

title: Mechanical abuse and safety in sodium-ion batteries
abstract: A multiphysics study evaluates the mechanical–electrochemical–thermal response and fundamental mechanisms of SIBs under mechanical abuse, explores key safety parameters, and compares the safety of SIBs and LIBs under mechanical loading.

title: Long-lived room temperature phosphorescence in aqueous micellar systems: application in cyclobutane synthesis
abstract: The micelle-induced excimer formation of simple chalcone is investigated and applied in synthesis. The concept allowed for the visible light promoted [2+2] photocycloaddition to occur, generating cyclobutanes.

title: Boosting visible-light hydrogen evolution via d-band center engineering in an FeP/graphdiyne heterointerface
abstract: Constructing heterointerfaces with a strong built-in electric field (IEF) is a promising strategy to enhance photocatalytic activity.

title: Artificial photosynthesis of H 2 O 2 over a self-assembled two-dimensional g-C 3 N 4 film
abstract: In this paper, we report a simple interfacial self-assembly approach for the fabrication of a g-C 3 N 4 thin-film-structured photocatalyst.

title: CeO 2 facilitates electron transfer at the Fe-Ni 2 P heterointerface, enhancing the overall process of water splitting
abstract: This study shows how to create layered nanosheet structures of Fe-doped Ni 2 P@CeO 2 on nickel foam using hydrothermal synthesis and low-temperature phosphating for reliable and effective water splitting (HER and OER).

title: Inorganic–organic synergy unlocks stretchable and self-healing thermoelectric generators with high output power
abstract: An inorganic–organic synergy thermoelectric generator that offers high power, stretchability, and self-healing ability, advancing wearable energy harvesting technologies.

title: Single-atom photocatalysis based on 2D fullerene structures for water splitting and CO 2 reduction
abstract: Single-atom doping of different metals in a 2D semiconducting C 60 network extends the optical absorption and reduces energy barriers during the photocatalytic processes for multiple reactions.

title: Decoration of dual cocatalysts on ultra-thin carbon nitride for efficient H 2 O 2 photosynthesis
abstract: Ultrathin g-C 3 N 4 nanosheets co-decorated with rGO for O 2 reduction and CoO x for water oxidation, thereby enhancing charge separation, redox kinetics and overall photocatalytic performance in H 2 O 2 production from O 2 and water.

title: Solid-state vs. spray-drying synthesis for Mg-doped P2–Na 0.67 Fe 0.5 Mn 0.5 O 2 as a cathode material for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Combining spray-drying synthesis with Mg( ii ) doping offers a promising route to prepare high-performance P2-layered cathodes for sodium-ion batteries.

title: Dielectric enhancement by inorganic nano-fillers for triboelectric optimization
abstract: The relationship between TENG performance and dielectric enhancement by inorganic nano-fillers was comprehensively and systematically analysed.

title: Tailoring aggregation behavior of shamrock-shaped non-fullerene acceptors via an isomerisation strategy enables high-performance organic solar cells
abstract: The end-group halogenation strategy in banana-shaped Y-series non-fullerene acceptors (NFAs) plays a crucial role in the development of organic solar cells (OSCs).

title: Structural regulation of a P2-type cathode via multi-cation doping for high-rate and stable sodium-ion batteries
abstract: An entropy modulation strategy is used to prepare the Na 0.7 Mn 0.5 Ni 0.15 Cu 0.12 Li 0.03 Fe 0.05 O 2 cathode, providing regulated lattice parameters and configurational entropy, which can enhance its reaction kinetics and cycling stability.

title: Optimizing the pore structure in silicon–carbon anodes: the impact of micropore and mesopore ratios on electrochemical performance
abstract: We used CVD to deposit Si onto porous carbon supports with varied micro-/mesopore ratios, then characterized how these pore structures influence Si distribution and Li-ion battery performance.

title: Boiling water tolerant organic field-effect transistors enabled by a short-chain polymer blending approach
abstract: A strategy to enhance moisture resistance in bottom-gate organic field-effect transistors through strategic insulator blending, enabling water-tolerant, solution-processable organic electronics.

title: Nanoarchitectonics of carbon nitride/NiO/Zn 3 N 2 heterointerfaces for bifunctional applications in electrocatalytic water splitting and coin cell supercapacitors
abstract: The advancement of durable electrocatalysts for water splitting and high-performance supercapacitors is essential for sustainable energy conversion and storage.

title: Construction of high surface area perovskite-based catalysts by in situ interfacial reactions: Ni-doped LaAlO 3 for dry reforming of methane as a case study
abstract: Perovskite-based materials are widely studied in heterogeneous catalysis; however, their low surface area seriously limits their practical applications.

title: Revealing the role of grain boundaries in magnesium-based hydrogen storage: insights into adsorption and dissociation
abstract: A reversed-volcano relation between H 2 dissociation barriers and grain boundary angle in Mg is revealed. The χ gem descriptor explains this trend, informing microstructure design for improved hydrogen storage kinetics.

title: Pt( ii ) as an active site supported on superhydrophilic nickel foam with boosted electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution performance
abstract: A binder-free Pt( ii )/NiF(H) electrocatalyst shows high electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution reaction performance with Pt( ii ) as an active site supported on superhydrophilic Ni foam.

title: Significantly enhanced capacitive energy-storage performance of a flexible P(VDF-CTFE)-polyimide bilayer by optimizing the interface effect
abstract: We have designed and fabricated a flexible P(VDF-CTFE)-polyimide bilayer film with enhanced dielectric energy storage through interfacial space charge optimization, showing promising potential for high-performance dielectric capacitors.

title: Accelerating the discovery of disordered multi-component solid-state electrolytes using machine learning interatomic potentials
abstract: Machine learning interatomic potentials, fine-tuned for complex solid-state electrolytes, enable accurate modeling and discovery of novel compositions with enhanced ion transport for next-generation batteries.

title: Amorphous oxyhalide solid electrolytes with improved ionic conductivity and reductive stability for all-solid-state batteries
abstract: Amorphous oxyhalide solid electrolytes, Li 4 x La x Ta 1− x O 2 x Cl 5−2 x , synthesized using LiLaO 2 , demonstrate an impressive conductivity of 4.9 mS cm −1 , along with an exceptionally high critical current density of 40 mA cm −2 in symmetric cells.

title: Synergistic effect of morphology evolution with oxygen vacancies on layered cathodes derived from polyoxovanadates for aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: The synergistic effect of morphological tailoring with oxygen vacancies significantly accelerates the reaction kinetics, thus resulting in a capacity of 409.6 mAh g −1 and long-term stability for aqueous zinc-ion batteries.

title: Recent advances in tin halide perovskite solar cells: a critical review
abstract: This review summarizes the state-of-the-art development of Sn-based perovskite solar cells, and the fundamental properties of Sn-based perovskites, advanced strategies, and critical perspectives on the future research directions are discussed.

title: Pore engineering of porous framework materials for efficient SF 6 capture
abstract: Sulfur hexafluoride (SF 6 ) is an artificial inert gas widely used in the power and semiconductor industries and is known to be a significant contributor to the greenhouse effect due to its high global warming potential.

title: Research progress onconductive hydrogels and their applications in flexible sensors: a review
abstract: Conductive hydrogels are a class of novel conductive materials characterized by a biomimetic structure, excellent conductivity, adjustable mechanical properties, and diverse functionalities and structures.

title: Enhanced broad-frequency electromagnetic-wave absorption and environmental stability of carbonitrided high-entropy alloys via a green mechanochemical approach
abstract: High-entropy alloys are important candidates for efficient electromagnetic-wave absorbers , but the further design of the absorbers with harmonized impedance matching and excellent environmental stability remains a major challenge.

title: Structural regulation of sulfone-decorated conjugated polymers for boosting photocatalysis
abstract: The multifarious aromatic sulfone-decorated conjugated polymers (CPs) have recently been developed to enable them to be employed in artificial photocatalysis with encouraging achievements.

title: Benzodithiophene-based polymer donors for organic photovoltaics
abstract: This review summarizes the molecular design strategy for benzodithiophene-based donor–acceptor polymers and presents recent examples of their applications in high-efficiency solar cells.

title: Rational hierarchical micellar gel electrolytes with synergistic hydrophobic–hydrophilic integration for dendrite-free zinc-ion batteries
abstract: The LA gel electrolyte, with hydrophilic AM and hydrophobic LMA domains, forms ion channels, stabilizes hydrogen bonds, and reduces parasitic reactions. CTAB regulates Zn 2+ migration and domain interactions.

title: Recent advances in anion-exchange membranes for electrolyzers, fuel cells and redox-flow batteries
abstract: This review highlights the recent progress in the development of anion-exchange membranes for affordable electrochemical energy devices-electrolyzers, fuel cells, and vanadium redox-flow batteries.

title: Transition metal–phenanthroline intercalated montmorillonite as efficient electrocatalysts for the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: MMT-based organo-metallic catalysts M(Phen) 2 (M = Ni, Co, Fe) were synthesized via sonication-assisted intercalation. Expanded interlayers improve OER activity. MMT:Co(Phen) 2 shows the best performance with low overpotential and long-term stability.

title: Amine-functionalized CNTs@mSiO 2 with short radical mesochannels for fast and efficient CO 2 capture
abstract: Efficient amine grafting achieved through the restructuring of a short-mesochannel composite material produces a functionalized adsorbent with high CO 2 adsorption capacity and kinetics.

title: Enabling economic and safe anode pre-lithiation with high value-added material production via vacuum thermal reduction
abstract: Economic and safe anode pre-lithiation has been achieved with in situ generated lithium metal through vacuum thermal reduction.

title: Organic dual-ion batteries with low-temperature operability and structural reinforcement
abstract: This dual-ion organic battery combines carbon fiber-level mechanical strength and low temperature operability, providing high power, high capacity retention, and excellent cycling stability at low temperatures.

title: Unveiling the energy storage of supercapacitors containing water-in-salt electrolytes confined in MXenes by molecular dynamics simulations
abstract: The NaClO 4 electrolyte demonstrates greater charge accumulation. The charging mechanism with sodium WiSEs changes completely over the simulation time.

title: Synthesis of a highly durable and efficient Ir-functionalized OAPS-GMA dendrimer for an all-pH hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Ir-OAPS-GMA electrocatalyst was synthesized via hydrothermal route. The resulting Ir-OAPS-GMA demonstrated outstanding HER performance at universal pH and overall water splitting in 1 M KOH electrolyte used as cathode in a single-cell AEMWE system.

title: Breaking the poisoning paradigm: a high-throughput DFT screening of high-entropy alloys with a focus on phonon-induced uncertainty
abstract: Although surface poisoning is typically thought to deactivate electrocatalysis, this work suggests its intentional application in promoting HER via a systematic high-throughput machine learning and DFT exploration of HEAs.

title: Eco-friendly innovation: three-mode sun-actuated thermotropic devices integrating κ-carrageenan-based film doped with Arundo donax leaf-derived carbon dots and 1-butyl-3-methyl-1 H -imidazolium chloride
abstract: This study develops eco-friendly sun-activated thermotropic devices by incorporating carbon dots derived from Arundo donax leaves, and ionic liquid into kappa-carrageenan films. These CD-doped films offer great promise for zero-energy smart windows.

title: An FePb-doped RuO 2 coupled amorphous/crystalline heterophase for efficient acidic oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: A Fe and Pb dual doped RuO 2 catalyst with amorphous/crystalline heterostructures demonstrates excellent acidic OER performance and stale operation in PEMWE.

title: Highly efficient hydrogen evolution with silicon nanowire photocathodes: hierarchical triple-junction and a-MoS x catalysts achieving ABPE > 4.6%
abstract: Record-efficiency Si photocathodes (4.6% ABPE): engineered SiNWs/TiO 2 /a-MoS x with optimized light management, heterojunction charge extraction, and metastable catalyst design enables stable solar H 2 evolution.

title: Design and architecture of ZnIn 2 S 4 and ZnIn 2 S 4 -based hybrid materials for photocatalytic, electrocatalytic and photoelectrochemical hydrogen evolution
abstract: Photocatalytic innovations are employed in the production of H 2 , environmental remediation, CO 2 reduction, and additional critical disciplines because of their sustainability, ease of implementation, and dependence on solar energy.

title: Self-healing polymer binders: next-generation battery applications
abstract: This review highlights advances in self-healing polymer binders for Li-ion, Li–S, and Na batteries, emphasizing molecular design and healing mechanisms that autonomously repair of electrodes and improve mechanical integrity, cycle life and safety.

title: Decoding pH-dependent electrocatalysis through electric field models and microkinetic volcanoes
abstract: This perspective highlights advances in capturing pH-dependent surface structures, reactivity, and mechanisms via electric field-based methods.

title: In situ generation of the spinel structured FeCr 2 O 4 catalyst for CO 2 -assisted ethane oxidative dehydrogenation
abstract: In situ FeCr 2 O 4 formation reduces coke deposition, boosting ethylene yield in CO 2 -ODHE.

title: A comprehensive review of core/shell nanostructures of lead-halide perovskite quantum dots for improved optoelectronic performance and stability
abstract: This review summarizes core/shell strategies to passivate surface defects in lead-halide perovskite quantum dots (Pe-CQDs). Structures are classified by energy band alignment and physical characteristics to enhance performance and stability.

title: Confinement effects in photocatalysis: progress and challenges
abstract: The confinement effect can effectively enhance photocatalytic activity by restricting active species to the nanoscale region through a special spatial structure. This work reviews the recent advances of confinement effect in photocatalysis.

title: Recent advances in the application of magnetron sputtering for lithium metal batteries
abstract: The present review systematically outlines recent progress in utilizing magnetron sputtering for lithium metal batteries, highlighting emerging coating strategies such as target design and their corresponding characterization methodologies.

title: Selective adsorption of C 2+ alkanes over methane by using a zinc tris-pyrazolate framework
abstract: A metal pyrazolate framework was applied for adsorptive separation of C 2+ alkanes from methane. Its low-pressure uptake capacities for propane and butane facilitate its efficient capture of low-concentration C 2+ alkanes.

title: Two birds with one stone strategy: selective uranium extraction and detection by a fluorescent covalent organic framework with high-affinity binding sites
abstract: A novel COF-AO was fabricated for selective uranium detection and extraction, and it has an ultra-low detection limit and excellent uranium extraction ability in actual water bodies.

title: A novel mixed-conducting network in all-oxide composites: overcoming traditional percolation constraints
abstract: Redefined mixed-conducting composite networks by leveraging grain boundaries of the ionic conductor as “mixed-conductive bridges” that interconnect ionic and electronic phases, thereby enabling efficient and sustainable oxygen permeation.

title: Oxygen-defective ruthenium oxide as an efficient and durable electrocatalyst for acidic oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Oxygen-defective RuO x nanoparticles are synthesized through a one-step sol–gel process, which show outstanding activity and stability for acidic oxygen evolution reaction at high current densities due to the predominant adsorbate evolution mechanism.

title: Engineered s-SWCNT network/a-Ga 2 O 3 heterointerface for enhanced deep ultraviolet photodetection
abstract: Spin-coated s-SWCNT/sputtered a-Ga 2 O 3 heterojunction boosts DUV responsivity by synergizing appropriate band alignment with nanotube transport pathways, offering a scalable optoelectronic design.

title: Electrodeposition of reactive polymer networks for conformal ultrathin coatings amenable to post-deposition functionalization
abstract: An electrodeposition method is reported that forms sub-micron conformal polymer thin films on (non-)planar and porous materials alike. The non-grafted coatings are amenable to modification with click chemistry to achieve desired functionalities.

title: From waste to energy and fuel: novel Cu x Ni y /CN catalysts from waste melamine resin for efficient nitrate reduction to ammonia
abstract: The conversion of nitrate (NO 3 − ) contaminants into ammonia (NH 3 ) through electrochemical reduction presents a viable strategy for the dual purposes of wastewater purification and ammonia production.

title: Realising ultrafast perovskite photodetectors via 2D synergy for optical communication and sensitive light detection
abstract: The realization of high-speed optical communication and sensitive light detection is critical for advancing AI-driven optoelectronics, demanding exceptional detectivity and ultrafast response for practical applications.

title: Tuning the proton concentration and uptake kinetics of BaFeO 3 -based oxygen electrodes for reversible protonic ceramic fuel cells
abstract: Optimizing the Fe content in BaZr 0.88− x Fe x Y 0.12 O 3− δ enhanced proton concentration (0.36 mol%) at 600 °C. The BZFY0.7 showed minimal polarization resistance and exhibited peak performance, showing superior catalytic activity for PCFC applications.

title: Regulating the solvation structures through a high-entropy strategy for wide-temperature zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Here, a high-entropy electrolyte, Li 2 ZnBr 4 ·9H 2 O (HEE), is constructed by introducing LiBr as a supporting salt into the ZnBr 2 aqueous electrolyte.

title: Deep-learning-enabled breathable thermogalvanic hydrogel array for self-powered mental monitoring
abstract: An air-permeable self-powered thermogalvanic hydrogel array with a trade-off between mechanical and electrical performance is proposed for continuous mental monitoring based on facial expression recognition with high accuracy and stability.

title: A Z-scheme inorganic intergrowth bulk heterojunction to achieve photostimulated oxygen vacancy regeneration for photocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: An inorganic intergrowth bulk heterojunction (IIBH) NiO(Ti)/Ti 3 O 5 (Ni,Ga) has photo-induced oxygen vacancy regeneration mechanism. The photogenerated electron transfer process between the Ni 2+ /Ni 3+ and Ti 4+ /Ti 3+ across the interface of the IIBH.

title: Bionic liquid–liquid phase separation phenomenon inspired lignin molecular aggregates toward highly nitrogen-doped nanocarbon anode for sodium-ion hybrid capacitors
abstract: This work provides a high-performance and low-cost carbon anode material (TMLC) for SIHCs and also offers a reference for the application of lignin in the energy storage field.

title: Regulation of the d band center and geometric distortion via an axial nitrogen strategy of the Fe–N–C oxygen electrocatalyst for a Zn–air battery
abstract: The bifunctional catalyst Fe–N 5 C, with an axial N atom, shows improved oxygen reduction/evolution catalytic performance. The catalyst enables the aqueous rechargeable Zn–air battery to achieve a 330 h long serving time with only 0.84 V voltage gap.

title: Machine-learning-aided screening of inorganic lithium solid-state electrolytes with a wide electrochemical window
abstract: We construct an electrochemical window (ECW) dataset of over 16 000 Li-containing compounds using a thermodynamic approach for solid-state electrolytes (SSEs).

title: Influence of acceptor/donor densities and layer thicknesses on the efficiency of 2D ZnO/BFO/spiro-OMeTAD perovskite solar cells: a COMSOL simulation-based optimization
abstract: The adverse effects of global warming and the continued reliance on hazardous energy sources, such as coal and petroleum, have intensified the global pursuit of clean and sustainable energy alternatives.

title: Unveiling the role of dopant electronegativity in improving the catalytic performance of MXene catalysts in propane dehydrogenation using microkinetic simulations
abstract: The electronegativity of metal dopants for MXenes catalyst effectively regulate both reaction barrier and TOF of propene formation.

title: Machine-learning assisted screening of MXene-supported single-atom catalysts for oxygen reduction
abstract: SACs supported on Ti 2 NO 2 MXene and its derivatives with defective surfaces (O or Ti vacancies) are screened for excellent ORR electrocatalytic activity using first-principles calculations and key descriptors are identified by machine learning.

title: Compositional gradient Au–Cu bimetallic heterostructures for efficient electroreduction of CO 2 to ethanol at low potential
abstract: A compositional gradient Au–Cu catalyst with Au–Cu 0 /Cu + surfaces was synthesized. This unique structure reduces CO 2 activation barriers, enhances *CO coverage, stabilizes C 2 intermediates and achieves 84.62% faradaic efficiency for EtOH production.

title: Promotion of nitrite and nitrate conversion into ammonia by improving *H utilization via the construction of dual active centers
abstract: A specially designed NO 2 RR/NO 3 RR electrocatalyst assembled using a pulsed laser irradiation strategy is proposed, whose dual active sites perform their respective functions to promote the generation and utilization of *H in neutral electrolytes.

title: Robust, tough, ultra-low-creep and reprocessable rubber enabled by weak supramolecular-interaction-dominated yet strong covalent-bond-assisted reverse design paradigm
abstract: Weak supramolecular interactions serve as the dominant network while robust dynamic covalent bonds act as an assistant in rubber, resulting in high strength, prominent extensibility, ultra-low creep, and excellent reprocessability of the rubber.

title: Low temperature and fast response TEA sensors based on n–n WO 3 /In 2 O 3 heterojunctions
abstract: Due to the unique properties of heterojunction interfaces, heterojunction materials have broad application prospects in gas sensors.

title: Unveiling LiF-engineered MXene: a novel Ti 3 C 2 T x /ZnO hybrid composite for highly selective and sensitive chemiresistive NH 3 detection via Schottky barrier modulation at ambient temperature
abstract: A high-performance L3-Ti 3 C 2 T x /ZnO showed 50-fold higher NH 3 sensitivity than pristine ZnO, with the MDC of 0.1 ppm, fast response/recovery times, and long-term stability at 300 K for industrial and environmental safety.

title: Interface-engineered Co-Ni-S composite electrode for ultrahigh capacitance and water oxidation
abstract: Interface-engineered Co-Ni-S composite multifunctional electrode materials for next-generation energy storage and water splitting.

title: Atomic spectral imaging of lithium-ion heterogeneity for proactive battery safety alerts
abstract: Atomic spectral imaging captures lithium-ion heterogeneity and anode expansion, enabling proactive battery safety alerts.

title: Heteronuclear inorganic molecule enables the direct synthesis of PtCo intermetallic compounds for proton exchange membrane fuel cells
abstract: Small-sized and highly active Pt1Co1-IMC/C catalysts were successfully synthesized via direct pyrolysis of a Pt1Co1 heteronuclear inorganic molecule on the carbon surface.

title: Rapid in situ bulk transformation of NiFe particle for highly efficient oxygen evolution in anion exchange membrane water electrolyzers
abstract: In situ anodic oxidation for selective Mo ion leaching and bulk transformation.

title: Raman spectroscopy study of K-birnessite single crystals
abstract: The alterations in the crystal structure and Raman spectra of K-birnessite single crystals when water molecules intercalate into or deintercalate from the interlayer space were subjected to an in-depth examination.

title: Ultrathin conjugated microporous thermoset nanosheets for efficient Li-ion storage
abstract: Ultrathin conjugated microporous thermoset nanosheets (CMT-n) was successfully synthesized based on a solvent-free, catalyst-free thermosetting process and CMT-n shows excellent discharge capacity and cycling stability as anodes for lithium storage.

title: Atomically bridged palladium between nickel species and carbon microfibers and the subsequent conversion into free-standing and electrocatalytically active multifunctional electrodes
abstract: We introduce free-standing multifunctional electrodes created by combining the atomic layer deposition of palladium and nickel oxide nanostructures directly onto gas diffusion electrodes with thermal treatment, to form nanostructured electrocatalysts.

title: Phase-purity-engineered spinel-type LiM x Mn 2− x O 4 (M = Ni, Al; 0 ≤ x ≤ 1) with a single high-voltage plateau: design, synthesis and electrochemical mechanism
abstract: This study develops an ordered-phase LNMO cathode to suppress Mn dissolution and erase the 4.0 V plateau. The electrode achieves a capacity of 75.4% (200 cycles at 1C) and an energy density of 451.67 Wh kg −1 , enabling stable high-voltage batteries.

title: Comparison of life cycle assessment in the fuel cell industry chain and the lithium battery industry
abstract: Fuel cells (FCs) and lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) are currently considered the most promising energy and power sources for electric vehicles (EVs).

title: In situ studies of oxygen transport mechanisms in Ag/SrFeO 3− δ materials for chemical looping catalysis
abstract: Characterisation of Ag/SrFeO 3−δ during redox cycling using in situ XRD, TEM, NAP-XPS, and Raman reveals Ag–O x species aid low-temperature oxygen release.

title: Machine-learning-driven modelling of amorphous and polycrystalline BaZrS 3
abstract: We present a bespoke machine-learned interatomic potential (MLIP) model for the emerging solar-cell material BaZrS 3 . This potential can be used to simulate both the amorphous precursor and the polycrystalline material with complex grain boundaries.

title: Alkali metal fluoride etching for efficient and stable planar TiO 2 perovskite solar cells
abstract: We passivate TiO 2 surface with alkali metal fluorides and dilute HCl, enhancing perovskite device stability. The optimized device maintains 88% of its initial efficiency after 1000 hours of continuous light soaking.

title: Correction: Dual-phase modulation via Mo doping and Li 3 PO 4 coating for stabilized LiNi 0.9 Mn 0.07 Co 0.02 Al 0.01 O 2 cathodes in high-energy lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Correction for ‘Dual-phase modulation via Mo doping and Li 3 PO 4 coating for stabilized LiNi 0.9 Mn 0.07 Co 0.02 Al 0.01 O 2 cathodes in high-energy lithium-ion batteries’ by Srinivasan Alagar et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA01436K.

title: Correction: Atomistic characterization of hydration-dependent fuel cell ionomer nanostructure: validation by vibrational spectroscopy
abstract: Correction for ‘Atomistic characterization of hydration-dependent fuel cell ionomer nanostructure: validation by vibrational spectroscopy’ by Dan J. Donnelly, III et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, 13 , 24495–24510, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA03973H.

title: Radiation induced structural phase transition and damage in methyl ammonium lead perovskites studied by focused-beam synchrotron X-ray diffraction
abstract: Focused synchrotron XRD analysis shows that high photon flux radiation (>5 × 10 13 ph s −1 mm −2 ) induces a tetragonal to orthorhombic phase transition in MAPbI 3 at room temperature by promoting photogenerated I 0 or I − interstitials that block MA rotation.

title: Interface regulation via bidentate π-chelators for high-performance perovskite solar cells
authors: 'Mengting Han', 'Aiqing Sun', 'Yingke Ren', 'Zhiqian Yang', 'Zhaoqian Li', 'Li'e Mo', 'Hong Zhang', 'Xianxi Zhang', 'Yang Huang', 'Linhua Hu'
abstract: 7-AI forms bidentate chelation bonds and π–π stacking to passivate defects and enhance charge transfer.

title: Magnesium phosphate-based thermoelectric materials as an efficient, stable and scalable bulk power supply solution
abstract: As a cementitious material, MPTEMs can be prepared in various shapes and used in various structures, to provide continuous power supply in the future.

title: Multifunctional broadband emitters based on rare earth phosphors for all-weather and efficient radiative cooling and energy saving
abstract: Passive daytime radiative cooling (PDRC) presents an effective strategy for mitigating the global greenhouse effect.

title: Broadening the Mn 2+ /Mn 3+ redox plateau in LiMn 0.6 Fe 0.4 PO 4 cathodes for high-power and long-life Li-ion batteries
abstract: The Mn 2+ /Mn 3+ redox plateau in LiMn 0.6 Fe 0.4 PO 4 cathodes is broadened via in situ Mg 2+ and Ti 4+ dual doping.

title: Breaking the Brønsted–Evans–Polanyi relationship in N 2 adsorption driven by potential-dependent repositioning of frontier orbitals: a sweet marriage of machine learning-assisted screening and the electric double-layer effect
abstract: We conducted an upgrade prediction for NRR electrocatalysts on g-C 16 N 5 featuring the local coordinationof TM–N 4 , with Mo@g-C 16 N 5 standing out. Using the constant-potential method and an implicit solvent model, we find the electric double-layer capacitance is key in modulating the kinetic bar…

title: Electron-correlation driven structural instability and adsorption mechanism in the metal–organic framework NU2100
abstract: Electron-correlation driven structural instability reveals mechanism of remarkable adsorption and seperation profiles observed experimentally.

title: A review of photostability in organic solar cells: from mechanisms to improvement strategies
abstract: In response to the serious photodegradation problem of organic solar cells, this review explores the factors influencing photostability from materials to devices and highlights recent progress in strategies to improve the photostability of OSCs.

title: Synergistically modulating the active-site density and charge-transfer in covalent organic frameworks for boosting electrocatalytic water splitting
abstract: Two bicarbazole 2D COFs, NUST-69/70, with distinct electronics, show efficient water-splitting and good bifunctional HER/OER activity. Notably, NUST-70 with D-A benzothiadiazole moiety, modulates sites/charge transfer, boosting OER at 292 mV.

title: A novel aqueous aspartic acid modified biomass binder for high-performance Li–S batteries
abstract: An aloe–derived sustainable aqueous binder enhances Li–S battery performance by accommodating electrode expansion, suppressing polysulfide shuttle and lithium dendrites, thereby improving stability and safety.

title: Scale matters: simulation of nanoscopic dendrite initiation in lithium solid electrolyte interphases using a machine learning potential
abstract: Simulations of large models of lithium solid state electrolytes reveal possible nanoscopic mechanisms for dendrite formation.

title: Vertically aligned 3D core–shell CuO/ZnCO 2 O 4 supported on a flexible mesh for efficient and scalable electrochemical water splitting
abstract: OER activity of a sustainable, highly porous, scalable, and core–shell nano-structured 3D CuO/ZnCo 2 O 4 @FSSM electrode.

title: Data-guided design of double-atom catalysts for enhanced electrocatalytic performance
abstract: Key features of double-atom catalysts are revealed through DFT-integrated machine learning analysis, and a new ML-based screening framework is developed to enable rapid identification of efficient catalysts for CO 2 RR, HER, and OER.

title: Overcoming the probing-depth dilemma in spectroscopic analyses of batteries with muon-induced X-ray emission (MIXE)
abstract: An introductory guide to muon-induced X-ray emission (MIXE) for the battery community, highlighting both the potential and limitations of this novel characterization technique with extended probing depth.

title: Enhancing lithium–sulfur battery performance with dual-atom catalysts: a synergistic approach
abstract: This study explores advancements of Li–S batteries with dual atom catalysts (DACs), focusing on support morphology, DAC atomic coordination, battery performance, and simulations of both electronic structure and atomistic mechanisms.

title: Near-unity solar reflectance and mid-infrared transparency via microwave-engineered 2D Y 2 O 3 for passive radiative cooling
abstract: A microwave shock method has enabled the synthesis of 2D Y 2 O 3 nanosheets with exceptional high VIS-NIR reflectance and MIR transparency, advancing radiative cooling materials for efficient thermal management.

title: Molecular tuning and strategic functionalization of dopant-free hole transport materials for enhanced stability and efficiency in perovskite solar cells
abstract: Dopant-free HTMs SP-09 and SP-10 are synthesized via a simple method. SP-10 enables 22.1% efficiency and superior stability in PSCs, outperforming spiro-OMeTAD, offering a cost-effective route toward efficient and durable perovskite devices.

title: Boosting the efficiency and stability of two-step perovskite solar cells by a hydroxylamine-driven additive strategy
abstract: In this study, perovskite solar cells were prepared using a two-step spin-coating solution method. By adding Cbz-hydroxylamine to the organic salt reduces PbI 2 residue, enhances crystallization, and improves efficiency by defect suppression.

title: A constructed 3D porous hierarchical micro-flower WO 3 /CdS S-scheme heterojunction for boosting photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production and photoelectrochemical cell performance
abstract: A self-powered photoelectrochemical cell is designed by using a WO 3 /CdS S-scheme heterojunction as a photoanode combined with a mixture of iron phthalocyanine (Fe II Pc) and WO 3 on carbon paper as a cathode, for achieving energy conversion.

title: Flash graphene induced low-temperature synthesis of a single-crystalline Na 2.72 Fe 1.64 (SO 4 ) 3 cathode for boosted sodium storage
abstract: A single-crystalline Na 2.72 Fe 1.64 (SO 4 ) 3 @FG cathode material was successful fabricated for the first time, which exhibits a high-rate performance of 81 mA h g −1 at 15C and maintains an excellent capacity retention of 80.7% after 15 000 cycles.

title: Revealing the interface instability between a lithium metal anode and perovskite solid-state electrolyte
abstract: Interface instability between the perovskite Li 3 x La 2/3− x TiO 3 (LLTO) and lithium metal anode limits the application in all-solid-state lithium batteries.

title: Multilayer graphene effects on Fe–N-C catalysts: elucidating atomic aggregation and oxygen reduction reaction activity
abstract: Graphene layering regulates Fe atom aggregation in Fe–N-C catalysts, altering the ORR activity and stability. Multilayer graphene promotes interlayer aggregation with tunable effects, leading to distinct catalytic trends compared to monolayer graphene.

title: Anomalous phonon transport and thermoelectric properties in honeycomb compounds ACuTe (A = Na, K, Rb)
abstract: An anomalous mass trend in lattice thermal conductivity ( κ L ) has been observed, where substituting lighter atoms for cations results in a decrease in κ L . This effect is intrinsic to the lattice vibrations within the layered honeycomb structure.

title: A novel approach to describe the electric double layer structure of water-in-salt electrolytes in porous carbon electrodes
abstract: The ion association in an electrolyte governs the Debye length trends within the carbon pores, influencing the radii of confined ions, ion desolvation, and EDL charging dynamics.

title: Largely enhanced electromagnetic wave absorption via surface coating of carbonyl iron particles with liquid metal
abstract: CIP@LM heterointerfaces can efficiently control polarization losses, balance impedance matching in magnetic materials, and boost electromagnetic wave absorption.

title: Computational screening aided design of single atom-doped MoS 2 as electrocatalysts in nitrogen fixation
abstract: High-throughput screening efficiently identifies optimal single-atom species for NRR catalysts by evaluating adsorption energy and activated bond length via DFT-based descriptors, which enables rapid prioritization of SACs with enhanced NRR activity.

title: Highly crystalline covalent organic framework nanosheets with multiple redox-active sites and the cation–π effect for safe potassium-ion batteries
abstract: Excellent quality sp 2 carbon-conjugated COF nanosheets with multiple active sites are reported for high-capacity highly safe potassium-ion batteries.

title: Enhanced CO 2 capture performance of mesoporous silica materials with TEPA amine-based deep eutectic solvent: kinetics and mechanism
abstract: Amine-based DES-functionalized mesoporous silica materials achieve high CO 2 uptake and capacity retention over 10 cycles. DFT confirms that synergistic amine-OH interactions enhance kinetics and stability.

title: A micro Zn-modified Al current collector inducing uniform sodium nucleation for anode-free sodium batteries
abstract: High sodiophilicity micro Zn coatings are developed by a mild and rapid chemical deposition process on an Al current collector, which greatly reduces the nucleation overpotential and induces uniform sodium nucleation.

title: Beyond graphene: a review of graphene's lesser-known yne relatives and their energy applications
abstract: The experimental advances and computational predictions of the yne relatives of graphene, graphyne (GY), graphdiyne (GDY), graphtriyne (GTY) and graphtetrayne (GT4Y), in energy and environmental applications are reviewed.

title: Chromium deposition and poisoning of the proton-conducting BaZr 0.1 Ce 0.7 Y 0.1 Yb 0.1 O 3− δ electrolyte of protonic ceramic cells
abstract: Cr poisoning of the BZCYYb1711 electrolyte resulted in the deposition of BaCrO 4 surface layer (i) and a Ba depletion layer inside the electrolyte (ii).

title: Achieving excellent proton conductivity and power density by introducing stable nitrogen-rich carbonized metal–organic frameworks into high-temperature proton exchange membranes
abstract: The diagram of the proton conduction process in the CUN@OPBI membrane doped PA.

title: Rational engineering of amorphous coating/rock-salt phase dual-coupling for overcoming the capacity–stability conflict in Co-free Ni-rich cathodes
abstract: Dual-coupling engineering of an amorphous layer and rock-salt phase solves the trade-off between capacity and retention for Ni-rich cathode materials.

title: Salt-assisted activation of n → π* electronic transition in orange carbon nitride for enhanced visible-light-driven H 2 generation
abstract: The introduction of cyano group defects and Na doping activates n → π* electronic transition in an orange carbon nitride, thereby inhibiting the radiative recombination pathway and accelerating the transfer of photoinduced charge carriers.

title: Depolymerization of lignin into cycloalkanes over a hydrotalcite-derived NiFe alloy catalyst
abstract: Herein, an Ni 9 Fe 1 /NiAlO z catalyst is reported to be an efficient catalyst for conversion of enzymatic hydrolysis lignin (EHL) and 2-phenethyl phenyl ether (PPE), a lignin dimer model compound.

title: One stone, three birds: dual hydrogen production from water reduction and formaldehyde oxidation predicted on metal-free electrocatalysts
abstract: Schematics of the “one stone, three birds” strategy proposed for the p -block elemental B 2 @C 3 N 4 /Gra electrocatalyst.

title: Novel amine-functionalized Mg-MOF CO 2 adsorbents with bi-functional adsorption-screening
abstract: The adsorption process of standard MOFs and the enhanced performance achieved via crystalline variations and amine grafting.

title: CVD-induced corrosion reconstruction of defective metal–organic frameworks boosted advanced catalytic oxidation
abstract: A “waste-to-treasure” strategy is proposed to construct defective Fe-MOFs by novel CVD-induced corrosion. Simultaneous defect engineering and heteroatom introduction endows MOFs with abundant S on defective (100) crystal face for superior activity.

title: Densification of cellulose acetate-derived porous carbons for enhanced volumetric hydrogen adsorption performance
abstract: High-density porous carbon pellets were produced via high-pressure compression of cellulose acetate-derived carbons activated under optimized KOH conditions, achieving a high volumetric hydrogen storage capacity of 20 g-H 2 per L at −196 °C and 1 bar.

title: Fe-induced transformation of OMS-2 to MnO with tailored oxygen vacancies for enhanced CO-SCR performance
abstract: This study found that the addition of Fe could promote the formation of MnO, and this change significantly enhanced the redox ability of the catalyst.

title: Thick-film all-polymer organic solar cells: non-halogen solvent processing for efficient and stable photovoltaics
abstract: This study achieves high efficiency and stability in thick-film all-polymer OSCs (300 nm, 16.01% PCE, and T 80 = 465 h), showing strong potential for industrial applications.

title: Highly transparent electrostatic energy storage capacitors based on CeO 2 -doped BaTiO 3 lead-free nanocomposite films
abstract: Schematic illustration of the energy storage characteristics: the typical P – E hysteresis loop, schematic structure, and optical transmittance spectra and actual photographs of the LAO (001) substrate and 0.7BT–0.3C film heterostructure.

title: Ruddlesden–Popper Y 2 Ti 2 O 5 S 2 /SWCNT composite anode: durable cycling and ultrafast lithium-ion storage for hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Cation-deficient Y 2 Ti 2 O 5 S 2 with alternating perovskite-like TiO 5 S slabs and rock-salt [Y 2 S 2 ] 2+ layers exhibits excellent rate capability up to 230C and retains 95% capacity after 1,000 cycles, showing great promise as a durable high-power anode.

title: An exclusive CO 2 -to-CO converting single-stack electrolyzer driven by a biomass-derived N-doped carbon-based bimetallic electrocatalyst
abstract: A carbon-based bimetallic oxide catalyst derived from coconut fibers was synthesized via one pot synthesis, achieving nearly 95% selectivity for CO 2 to CO conversion, and can produce CO at industry standards in an electrolyzer.

title: High yield regeneration of 1,4-NAD(P)H via selective adsorption-/desorption-mediated visible-light photocatalysis on a noble-metal-free catalyst
abstract: The highest yield of 1,4-NAD(P)H was regenerated by noble-metal-free photocatalysts with exceptional photocarrier separation and rapid NAD(P) + but negligible NAD(P)H adsorption.

title: MXene-driven augmentation of hole-selective self-assembled monolayer interfaces for efficient and stable p-i-n perovskite solar cells
abstract: MXene nanosheets enhance electrical properties of SAM-based HTLs in perovskite solar cells to achieve 23.25% efficiency with reduced trap states and negligible hysteresis.

title: Trace cobalt-inserted platinum lattice gap to enable bifunctional oxygen electrocatalysis
abstract: Co inserted Pt lattice gap catalyst was constructed and it showed excellent ORR/OER electrocatalytic performances. The interactions between Pt and ORR/OER intermediates were stabilized and the rate-determining step energy barriers were reduced.

title: Quantifying electrokinetics of NaCa 0.6 V 6 O 16 ·3H 2 O cathode in aqueous zinc-ion batteries with ZnSO 4 electrolyte
abstract: A novel electrokinetic study combining CV, GITT-EIS and other methods for analyzing V 2 O 5 cathode redox kinetics.

title: Conductivity enhancement of argyrodite Li 6 SbS 5 I solid electrolyte via charge modulation around Li diffusion paths through Si substitution
abstract: Si-substituted Li 6 SbS 5 I (Li 6+ x Sb 1− x Si x S 5 I) achieves a high Li conductivity of 15.4 mS cm −1 when x = 0.75, because Si changes the charge state of S ions around Li diffusion paths.

title: Synergistic effect of mechanical stimuli and defects on the catalytic activity of Janus transition metal dichalcogenides for hydrogen evolution reaction: an explicit first-principles study
abstract: Our extensive first-principles calculations and ab initio molecular dynamics simulations highlight the role of mechanical stimuli in designing Janus-TMD-based catalysts and modulating their catalytic capability by combining with defect engineering.

title: Lithium and oxygen engineered SiO 0.5 materials for high performance lithium storage materials
abstract: The porous prelithiated Si-rich SiO x consisting of dual-sized crystalline Si phases prepared by lithium and oxygen engineering showed significantly improved electrochemical performance.

title: Interface modification of the electron transport layer via a dry-processed conformal MgSnO x interlayer in inorganic CsPbI 3 perovskite solar cells for enhanced performance and stability
abstract: To improve the performance of perovskite solar cells (PSCs), we fabricate low-temperature SnO 2 -based CsPbI 3 PSCs and introduce an MgSnO x interlayer via atomic layer deposition between the spin-coated SnO 2 layer and FTO substrate in n–i–p structure.

title: Dual roles of surface-supported Rh nanoparticles and isolated Pt species within high-entropy oxides in enhancing hydrogen evolution reaction driven by hydrogen spillover
abstract: Hydrogen spillover has emerged as an effective approach to improving the hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) by enabling interfacial hydrogen transfer from metals to supports.

title: Bias-free Si-based photocathode for efficient photoelectrochemical ammonia synthesis and HMF oxidation
abstract: In this work, leveraging the energy-saving characteristic of furfural with its low oxidation potential and high-performance Si–TiO 2 photocathode, we constructed a Si-based tandem bias-free ammonia production device.

title: Covalent sulfur–oxygen linkages enhanced the localized acidic environment coupled with the hydrogen spillover effect for boosting hydrogen evolution reaction kinetics
abstract: CuFe 2 O 4 @CuFeS 2 heterostructure electrodes with covalent S–O bonds are synthesized. H 3 O + intermediates accumulate at electron-rich O δ − sites, forming a localized acidic microenvironment and accelerating the hydrogen spillover process.

title: Achieving high thermoelectric performance of triple half-Heusler compositions enabled by high-throughput screening
abstract: The high-throughput experimental exploration of 90 DHH/THH compositions was conducted. MgV 2 Co 3 Sb 3 showed a zT of over 0.7 at 900 K, indicating the effectiveness of the high-throughput experiment to explore new compositions for functional materials.

title: A hydrogen-bond armored MXene hydrogel for oxidation-resistant, dual-mode wearable sensors
abstract: Hydrogen-bond armored MXene hydrogel resists oxidation while enabling dual sensing of strain and temperature with wireless transmission for real-time health monitoring.

title: A review on electrochemical CO 2 -to-CH 4 conversion for a sustainable energy future: from electrocatalysts to electrolyzers
abstract: The electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide (CO 2 ) to methane (CH 4 ) offers a promising route to renewable fuels and carbon circularity, addressing urgent climate and energy challenges.

title: Elucidating the correlation between the prelithiation concentration of a Li x Si anode and performance of all-solid-state lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: All-solid-state lithium–sulfur batteries with Li x Si anodes are evaluated. The optimal composition for practical cell design is identified as x = 1.71 and N/P ratio = 2.05, enabling the realization of high electrochemical performance and stability.

title: Optimized trimetallic selenide heterostructures as high-performance trifunctional electrodes for self-sustained hydrogen production
abstract: One pot annealing is employed to synthesize N-doped trimetallic selenides. The trisellenide that consist of Ni 3 Se 2 , Co 0.85 Se, and MoSe 2 is used as higher performance trifunctional electrode material for supercapacitor, hydrogen and oxygen evolution reactions.

title: Hydrogen evolution using alloyed AuPd/TiO 2 hollow spheres by photoreforming of polyethylene terephthalate waste
abstract: Photocatalysts with AuPdTi nanoalloys in situ formed on TiO 2 hollow spheres exhibit superior and durable activity for H 2 evolution from PET wastes, which is attributed to the strong electronic interactions between the nanoalloys and the support.

title: Rational materials design for stable interfaces in all-solid-state potassium-ion batteries
abstract: Electrochemical stability of potassium solid electrolytes was evaluated, and high-throughput screening identified twelve coatings that suppress SE–cathode decomposition.

title: Engineering core–sheath phase change fibers for thermal energy storage: fundamentals, fabrication, and smart applications
abstract: Core–sheath phase change fibers provide an innovative strategy for precise thermal regulation, scalable PCM encapsulation, and multifunctional integration, offering scalable routes toward advanced thermal management and sustainable energy systems.

title: Anchoring Pd single atoms through S vacancies of defective nickel–sulfur for efficient electrocatalytic polyethylene terephthalate oxidation coupled with hydrogen evolution
abstract: The introduction of Pd single atoms promotes structure reconstruction of d-Ni 3 S 2 , along with optimized reactant adsorption and diminished energy barriers of the rate-determining step, consequently resulting in enhanced electrocatalytic performance.

title: Coupled influence of state-of-charge and storage temperature on calendar aging and subsequent cycle degradation in LiFePO 4 /graphite pouch cells
abstract: Multi-scale analysis reveals that SOC-dependent calendar aging preconditions interfacial degradation and lithium loss in LiFePO 4 /graphite cells, governing subsequent cycle aging and highlighting the need for integrated aging models.

title: Tannic acid-assisted surface encasing of bismuth nanoparticles on carbon felt for high-performance vanadium redox flow batteries
abstract: A carbon-felt electrode with surface-encased bismuth nanoparticles is proposed via a tannic acid-assisted strategy. It presents exceptional mechanochemical stability and sustains high catalytic performance even after 2000 cycles at 300 mA cm −2 .

title: Alkaline earth metal carboxylate hydrate-mediated controllable self-assembly of three-dimensional hierarchical nanoporous graphene for high-performance supercapacitors
abstract: Three-dimensional hierarchical nanoporous graphene materials were controllably synthesized via M(RCOO) 2 · x H 2 O-mediated self-assembly, showing superior and highly tunable supercapacitor performances.

title: A passivation–reduction synergistic strategy for achieving efficient and durable inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: STAB as anion passivator with reducibility can suppress iodine vacancies on the perovskite surface via multiple coordination to uncoordinated Pb 2+ , meanwhile inhibiting the generation of I 2 through the reductive property of the B–H group.

title: Semiconducting divalent organic spacer-assisted charge transport in lead-free layered perovskites
abstract: Bulky naphthalene diimide-based cations enable layered Sn–I perovskites with type-II nanoheterojunctions, enhancing charge lifetime, out-of-plane electron mobility, and air stability, advancing prospects for stable tin-based optoelectronics.

title: From volcanic ashes to magnetic zeolites: unravelling the iron oxide phase transitions by magnetic and advanced structural characterization
abstract: Volcanic ashes from Mount Etna are hydrothermally transformed into magnetic zeolites. Combined XAS and magnetometry reveal Fe phase transitions from olivine to magnetite-like and hematite phases, tuning oxidation states and magnetic responses.

title: A multi-modal deformation sensing hydrogel with a nerve-inspired highly anisotropic structure
abstract: A hydrogel with a multi-layer sandwich structure consisting of oriented fibers–pores–fibers was developed, and exhibits rare three-dimensional signal recognition ability.

title: Near-infrared driven N 2 fixation on ZnO–MXene (Ti 3 C 2 ) heterostructures through pyroelectric catalysis
abstract: A ZnO–MXene heterostructure enables nitrogen fixation by alternating between near-infrared light and darkness, using pyroelectric and photothermal effects.

title: Correction: Enhancing the thermoelectric figure of merit of BiN via polymorphism, pressure, and nanostructuring
abstract: Correction for ‘Enhancing the thermoelectric figure of merit of BiN via polymorphism, pressure, and nanostructuring’ by Elena R. Remesal et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, 13 , 220–229, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA05891G.

title: Correction: Thermoelectric properties of the aliovalent half-Heusler alloy Zn 0.5 Ti 0.5 NiSb with intrinsic low thermal conductivity
abstract: Correction for ‘Thermoelectric properties of the aliovalent half-Heusler alloy Zn 0.5 Ti 0.5 NiSb with intrinsic low thermal conductivity’ by Blair F. Kennedy et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2023, 11 , 23566–23575, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TA04514E.

title: Photoreduction of carbon dioxide enhanced by Cu atoms doped in a Pd cluster supported on TiO 2 : mechanism, selectivity, and catalytic descriptor
abstract: Cu–Pd sites perform better than Cu–Cu sites for CO 2 photoreduction. The −ICOHP C–O is proposed as a descriptor to predict the catalytic activity. Microkinetic simulations reveal the physical origin responsible for a high CH 4 selectivity.

title: In situ reactive modification strategy to enhance the performance of n–i–p perovskite solar cells
abstract: The role played by the cation–anion interaction within phenylethylammonium salts in passivating perovskite surface defects is systematically characterized.

title: Enhanced multifunctional performance of flash graphene-polymer composites via nitrogen doping
abstract: Nitrogen doping in flash graphene modulates interfacial interactions to enable polymer composites with enhanced properties.

title: Singlet diradicaloids as n-type passivating interlayers for CsPbI 3 perovskite solar cells
abstract: We proved the use of a singlet diradicaloid molecule as CsPbI 3 interface modifier in p–i–n-solar cells. The synergy between Pb passivation, energy level alignment and the light-triggered dipole leads to a significantly enhanced open circuit voltage.

title: Vitamin C modified cathode interlayer for efficient opaque and semitransparent organic photovoltaics
abstract: Vitamin C is used to optimize cathode interlayers in organic photovoltaics, achieving a power conversion efficiency of 19.8% in opaque devices and a light utilization efficiency of 4.53% in semitransparent devices.

title: Spinel-structured photoelectrodes for photoelectrochemical energy and environmental applications: synergistic photothermal–magnetic effects
abstract: Spinel photovoltaic electrodes for PEC energy conversion and environmental treatment and their photothermal and magnetic effects synergistically promoting PEC systems.

title: Poly(styrene- co -maleamic acid)-based monoamide covalent adaptable networks
abstract: High performance poly(styrene- co -maleamic acid) (PS-MMA) covalent adaptable networks, easily derived from commercial copolymers and secondary diamines, exhibit high glass transition temperature, optical clarity, and (re)processability up to 280 °C.

title: Superior high-temperature energy-storage performance of multilayer PEI-based nanocomposites via functional filler integration
abstract: Dielectric polymer capacitors are widely used in electronic power systems, pulse power systems, and hybrid vehicles owing to their excellent charging–discharging rates and ultrahigh power density.

title: Flexoelectricity-enhanced photovoltaic effect in flexible LiNbO 3 nanorod array/PVDF nanocomposites
abstract: We investigate the flexoelectricity-enhanced photovoltaic effect on the aligned LiNbO 3 nanorod (LN-NR) arrays/PVDF nanocomposites. We demonstrated that the shape of LN nanomaterials can strongly influence the photovoltaic current of the composites, mainly due to the increase of the flexoelectricity…

title: Single-atom cobalt catalysts engineered on tunable CNNO nanosheets for hydrogen generation
abstract: Microwave-assisted synthesis of single-atom Co catalysts on CNNO nanosheets enhances photoelectrochemical water splitting by stabilizing active sites and boosting hydrogen evolution efficiency.

title: Magnetic field control in the growth process of Co 3 O 4 and its performance enhancement mechanism as an anode material for lithium ion capacitors
abstract: A schematic diagram illustrating the charge storage mechanism, emphasizing rapid faradaic reactions and their pivotal role in energy storage efficiency.

title: Achieving superior anti-corrosion performance with spherical organic additives and synergistic barrier passivation mechanisms
abstract: NTAB, a novel spherical organic material, enhances epoxy coatings with superior anti-corrosion performance through synergistic barrier passivation mechanisms, outperforming conventional fillers.

title: Recent progress in multilayer solid electrolytes for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: The construction of multilayer electrolytes can improve the electrode interface and enhance the performance of solid-state batteries.

title: Doped and functionalized non-Ti-MXenes for flexible and wearable electronic devices
abstract: The unique physicochemical properties of non-Ti-MXenes make them excellent class of materials for flexible and wearable electronics.

title: Exploring the influence of morphological variations in M/CeO 2 catalysts for enhanced CO 2 hydrogenation to C1 products
abstract: M/CeO 2 catalysts (M = metal and metal oxide) have garnered significant attention due to their exceptional catalytic performance in thermal, photocatalytic, and electrocatalytic applications.

title: Graphitic carbon nitride (g-C 3 N 4 ) as an electrolyte additive boosts fast-charging and stable cycling of graphite anodes for Li-ion batteries
abstract: This study enhances the cycle and rate performance of graphite anodes in LIBs by adding g-C 3 N 4 to the electrolyte. The g-C 3 N 4 additive optimizes the solvation structure and SEI, enhancing the structural stability and reaction kinetics of graphite.

title: Geminal-Cu catalysts drive efficient C–C coupling to boost ethylene production via electrochemical CO 2 reduction
abstract: Geminal copper electrocatalysts demonstrated promising activity towards the CO 2 RR particularly in *CO dimerization into ethylene.

title: Synthesis, mechanism and physical properties of porous geopolymer–zeolite composites by in situ hydrothermal reaction for adsorption application
abstract: Under specific conditions, three types of zeolites can be converted from steel slag-based geopolymer, including analcime (ANA), sodalite (SOD) and mordenite (MOR). The specific surface area of the material increases to 259.60 m 2 g −1 .

title: Modulating an anion-enriched Zn 2+ solvation structure via a dual weak interaction for stable zinc–metal batteries
abstract: A hydrated electrolyte with a dual weakened Zn 2+ solvation structure is reported, exhibiting low desolvation energy and suppressed water activity, while facilitating in situ formation of a hybrid SEI, enabling highly reversible zinc–metal batteries.

title: Effects of a second phase with different bandgaps on the thermoelectric performance of polycrystalline SnSe materials
abstract: The matrix retained partial tellurides as a second phase, which closely synergized the phonon-blocking/electron-transmitting approach through the embedding of endotaxially nanostructured second phases. The maximum ZT of 1.65 at 823 K.

title: The balance of structural compatibility and distortion in titanium sources for the preparation of a high performance Na 2 Ti 6 O 13 anode
abstract: The research revealed the structural regulatory effect of the dual-crystal TiO 2 strategy on Na 2 Ti 6 O 13 . It can effectively broaden the transmission channel of Na + , thereby achieving high-rate and long-cycle titanate anodes.

title: High-rate stability of Na 3 V 2 (PO 4 ) 2 F 3 sodium-ion cathode materials enabled by an entropy-increasing strategy
abstract: Na 3 V 2 (PO 4 ) 2 F 3 (NVPF) is a widely studied cathode material for sodium-ion batteries, owing to its remarkable Na + migration capability and robust structural stability.

title: Managing film crystallization via low-toxic antisolvents: stabilizing Ag 3 BiI 6 perovskite-like solar cells under ambient conditions
abstract: Xylene and xylene mixed petroleum ether have been identified as a promising antisolvent for achieving controlled nucleation and growth. Their application promotes the suppression of AgI secondary phase formation of pinhole-free and optimally loaded Ag 3 BiI 6 absorbers, contributing higher efficienc…

title: Suppressing failure mechanisms in thick Ni-rich cathodes using angstrom-level alumina coatings
abstract: A scalable ALD strategy applies ∼2 nm Al 2 O 3 coatings on Ni-rich NMC811, suppressing oxygen release and metal dissolution, enhancing thermal stability and cycling performance.

title: C–Na–O electrostatic interactions boost the kinetics of coal-derived hard carbon anodes for high-performance sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Coal-derived hard carbon with C–Na–O interactions was constructed, enabling triple sodium storage (adsorption, pore filling, intercalation), with 90.46% initial coulombic efficiency and 86.3% retention after 800 cycles.

title: High-performance of ultra-low Pt-loaded PEMFCs: carbon-encapsulated CoFe alloy supported Pt nanoparticles as high-efficiency electrocatalysts
abstract: Developing efficient ultra-low Pt-loaded proton exchange membrane fuel cell (PEMFC) cathode catalysts is crucial for practical applications.

title: Multi-functional piezoelectric energy harvesters based on porous PLLA/BaTi 2 O 5 piezocomposite films
abstract: Porous poly( l -lactic acid)/BaTi 2 O 5 piezocomposite films, fabricated through thermally induced phase separation, demonstrate multifunctional application potential in the fields of self-powered devices, wearable sensors, and medical equipment.

title: Selective captivation of DOX via topotactic surface enrichment with hydrated sodium ions on engineered MXene nanosheets
abstract: Na + –MXene with an O-rich surface shows high DOX adsorption via electrostatic and covalent interactions, higher than unmodified MXene. Selective binding to DOX suggests its potential for the removal of amine-containing pollutants from aqueous media.

title: Function-orchestrated CaO/BiVO 4 /rGO interfaces for efficient CO 2 methanation via coupled charge separation and adsorptive activation
abstract: The photocatalytic conversion of CO 2 into methane (CH 4 ) under mild conditions offers a promising route for renewable fuel production, yet it remains hindered by poor CO 2 adsorption, fast electron–hole recombination, and limited redox efficiency.

title: Semi-curing combined with low-pressure mechanical pressing strategy for realizing high adhesion and low surface roughness Ag-NW transparent electrodes
abstract: Semi-curing combined with low-pressure pressing imparts high adhesion, low surface roughness, and exceptional mechanical and optoelectronic performance to Ag-NW transparent electrodes.

title: Engineering covalent organic frameworks to enhance interfacial lithium-ion flux redistribution in dendrite-free lithium–sulfur batteries
