abstract: The fBTTP-COF material was prepared through a condensation reaction, serving as shuttling blocker and kinetics accelerator.

title: Triple-junction all-perovskite solar cells: a review
abstract: This paper reviews the working principles and key parameters of TJPSCs and presents recent research progress, focuses on how component optimization, interface engineering, and charge transport layer improvements influence efficiency.

title: Size-selective catalysis enhancement in a new nanocaged MOF through the synergistic Lewis acidic–basic sites
abstract: A novel NaCe-MOF featuring 1D nanochannels constructed from nanocages exhibits high catalytic activity in Knoevenagel condensation reactions of bulky substrates.

title: Formation and exploration of polymorphism in WO 3 nanostructures for improved catalytic and chemical sensing performance
authors: 'Hakimeh Pakdel', 'Vardan Galstyan', 'Viacheslav Golovanov', 'Annalisa D'Arco', 'Tiziana Mancini', 'Francesco Mura', 'Marco Rossi', 'Stefano Lupi', 'Alessio Mezzi', 'Saulius Kaciulis', 'Elisabetta Comini'
abstract: Monoclinic/orthorhombic polymorphic WO 3 nanomaterial synthesized by a precipitation method exhibits significantly improved catalytic and chemical sensing performance toward acetone molecules.

title: A MOF-modified NaCrO 2 cathode for high-rate and wide-temperature applications in sodium-ion batteries
abstract: The MOF-modified strategy for sodium-ion battery cathodes enhances cycling stability by suppressing interfacial side reactions, and improves conductivity via facilitated ion and charge transport.

title: Atomic-level insights into the highly conductive lithium thio-phosphate solid electrolytes with exceptional stability against lithium metal
abstract: Novel Li 7 PS 2 , Li 5 PS, Li 8 P 2 S and Li 11 P 3 S electrolytes exhibit ionic conductivities comparable to the superionic conductor Li 7 P 3 S 11 , yet, unlike Li 7 P 3 S 11 , which rapidly forms an SEI layer, they demonstrate superior stability against Li metal anodes.

title: Thermal expansion of lithiated silicon (Li 13 Si 4 and Li 7 Si 3 ) anodes: a powder neutron diffraction study
abstract: Lithium silicides exhibit much higher thermal expansion than silicon, similar to lithium, consistent with Si clusters in Li.

title: Research progress of atomically dispersed iron, nitrogen co-coordinated carbon catalysts for oxygen reduction: a mini-review
abstract: The design principles, synthesis methods and characterization techniques of Fe–N–C for the ORR are systematically summarized.

title: Mycelium–coir-based composites for sustainable building insulation
abstract: We synthesized and characterized the advanced multifunctional features of mycelium–coir-based composites as a replacement for fossil-based foams used in building insulation.

title: Recent advances and challenges in degradation issues of direct ammonia solid oxide fuel cells: comprehensive review
abstract: Schematic diagram of degradation factors in direct ammonia solid oxide fuel cells.

title: An in situ derived alloy phase stabilizes the phosphorus/carbon interface for high-performance lithium-ion battery anodes
abstract: Sn 4 P 3 as an interface modifier in Sn–P@C composite enhances P–C bonding, restrains volume expansion, and improves electron/lithium ion transport ability, enabling superior lithium storage and cycling stability.

title: Hierarchically porous polyimide/graphene aerogels with superior compressibility and electromagnetic interference shielding performance
abstract: A series of polyimide/graphene aerogels with hierarchically open pore architectures were developed by a dual-template strategy during freeze-drying and show superior compressibility and electromagnetic interference shielding performance.

title: Gradient oxygen vacancy engineering of RuO 2− x for efficient acidic water oxidation
abstract: RuO 2 nanoparticles with gradient oxygen vacancy were synthesized. The special distribution of oxygen vacancies optimizes the adsorption of oxygen intermediates and stabilizes the structure for acidic oxygen evolution reaction.

title: Engineering vacancy-defective carbon nitride nanowire clusters for dramatically enhanced visible-light-driven photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production
abstract: This work demonstrates vacancy-defective g-C 3 N 4 nanowire clusters, achieving 15-fold higher H 2 O 2 production and enhanced environmental remediation via defect-induced oxygen reduction reaction enhancement.

title: Zinc-ion storage mechanisms in poly( o -phenylenediamine): insights into structural advantages and electrochemical performance
abstract: Zinc-ion organic batteries (ZOBs) are emerging as a viable energy storage alternative, valued for their superior safety features and economic viability.

title: Dual-functional composite coating with flame-retardant and antibacterial properties for flexible polyurethane foams
abstract: An dual-functional coating, assembled with a transition-metal-substituted polyoxometalate and a catecholamine-tethered polycation via the layer-by-layer technique, enhances flame-retardant and antibacterial properties of flexible polyurethane foams.

title: Nano SiC ceramics and MOFs synergistically enhanced polylactic acid composites for efficient flame retardant performance
abstract: Metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) are new hybrid materials with customizable three-dimensional structures, excellent stability, and tunable properties, making them promising for flame retardant applications.

title: Recent progress in functional applications of polymer foams
abstract: This review summarizes the material matrix, preparation process and its latest functionalized applications of polymer foams with porous structures.

title: Interfacial engineering of Fe–O–Zn bonds in heterojunction photocatalysts: synergistic visible light PMS activation and electron transfer efficiency enhancement
abstract: A heterojunction is constructed with an FZ13 photocatalyst. The Fe–O–Zn bond accelerates electron transfer. The FZ13 catalyst exhibits superior catalytic performance. The degradation mechanism of the FZ13 + PMS + light system was revealed.

title: Wrinkles in 2D TMD heterostructures: unlocking enhanced hydrogen evolution reaction catalysis
abstract: Wrinkled configurations are crucial in influencing the mechanical, electronic properties and HER catalytic activity of two-dimensional transition metal dichalcogenides, which favors strain regulation towards improved catalytic efficiency.

title: Unlocking exceptional sodium ion storage capability of manganese dioxide via controllable incorporation of cation vacancies
abstract: This work provides an effective design strategy for the deliberate modification of MnO 2 's defect structures that play a dominant role in enhancing Na + storage capability and efficiency.

title: Hygromechanical deformation of wood cell walls regulated by the microfibril angle
abstract: Microfibril angle regulates wood cell wall deformation by influencing moisture diffusion, while hydrogen bonding competition between CNFs and hemicellulose directs water movement.

title: Microscale homogeneous refinement of CaO/Ca(OH) 2 particles for enhancing thermochemical energy storage performance
abstract: The “dynamic” doping of Ca(OH) 2 with C 3 N 4 was introduced to optimize the microstructure of particles, which enhanced thermal performance, elevated conversion rate, and improved cycling stability in related thermochemical energy storage technology.

title: Nitrogen-induced deep reconstruction and formation of a high-valent nickel species γ-NiOOH surface layer on NiFe alloy /NiFeN pre-catalysts for efficient water oxidation
abstract: The development of a γ-NiOOH surface layer on NiFe alloy /NiFeN as well as Fe doping improved the adsorption energy of the oxygen-containing species and increased the OER catalytic activity.

title: A sustainable and cost-effective industrial biomass-based coin cell supercapacitor for powering up electronic devices
abstract: Transforming industrial waste into an energy storage device addresses two key challenges: reducing industrial waste and providing a sustainable, low-cost material to meet global energy demands.

title: Alkali-enhanced scalable synthesis of trinuclear metal-cluster frameworks for electrocatalytic CO 2 conversion
abstract: A novel method for synthesizing trinuclear metal-covalent organic frameworks (M 3 -MCOFs) is proposed, using scaffold ligands that coordinate with metal ions and are stabilized by covalent bonds.

title: Mussel-inspired strong and tough hydrogel with self-adhesive properties based on dynamic interactions for flexible wearable electronics
abstract: Emerging conductive hydrogels showcase profound potential for sophisticated manipulation and various sensing applications.

title: Recyclable HF-free Ti 3 C 2 T x 3D-printed supercapacitors: their second life in sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Sodium lactate and conductive carbon are recycled to utilize them as electrolytes of supercapacitors and conductive additives of sodium-ion batteries, respectively.

title: Electric field-induced amplification of graphene oxide's visible light photocatalytic activity
abstract: An electric field accelerates a visible light photocatalysis with dye-sensitized graphene oxide in water. The effect provides the basis for new efficient photocatalytic technologies using contactless electric cells.

title: Hygroscopic salt in a mesoporous zirconium metal–organic framework for atmospheric water harvesting
abstract: Hygroscopic salt CaCl 2 in a mesoporous Zr-MOF NJTech-4 makes a promising water-harvesting candidate composite material with high working capacity and fast kinetics.

title: Synthesis, chemical bonding, and mechanical properties of Ti–Nb–Hf ternary solid solution MAXs
abstract: Novel Ti 1− x Nb 1− x Hf 2 x AlC MAX phase ceramics with excellent mechanical properties are synthesized via M-site solid solution, and their electronic structure, bonding status, and elastic properties are investigated using DFT calculations.

title: Integration of a self-attentive neural network and density functional theory for accelerated screening of graphene-based stabilized binary adsorption systems
abstract: It is crucial to study adsorbed atomic systems in graphene as exogenous atoms or molecules can precisely modulate the properties of graphene and significantly extend its wide-range applications.

title: Methane adsorption in an SDS–oil–cellulose compound system: an experimental and interface simulation study
abstract: A novel SDS–oil–cellulose composite system was developed, achieving a methane adsorption efficiency of 14.36%. Cellulose enhances adsorption by strengthening interfacial interactions and reducing the free energy barrier.

title: Negative spin-to-charge current induced by interfacial spin–orbit coupling in Pt/monolayer 1T-TiSe 2 /graphene/yttrium iron garnet quadruple heterostructures
abstract: Strong spin–orbit coupling (SOC) materials and graphene have recently emerged as promising interfacial layers for tailoring spin transport in normal metal/ferromagnet heterostructures.

title: A carboxylate-linked atomically precise one-dimensional CuI20 nanocluster-based framework for all-solid-state flexible supercapacitors
abstract: The first carboxylate-linked Cu I nanocluster-based framework (NCF), [Cu 20 ( t BuCC) 18 (tfbdc)], was made via a one-pot method. The electrochemically stable NCF enabled a flexible supercapacitor with high capacitance and robust mechanical reliability.

title: Spectroscopic analysis of Cu 2+ dissolved in Y- and Yb-doped barium zirconates and its impact on sinterability and proton conduction
abstract: The sintering behavior of BaCe 0.4 Zr 0.4 Y 0.1 Yb 0.1 O 3− δ (BCZYY441) with CuO as the sintering agent was investigated. Cu 2+ occupies interstitial sites along with Ba deficiency, which facilitates liquid-phase formation and densification.

title: Non-free water dominated electrolyte architectures for zinc-based batteries: toward sustainable long-life zinc-based energy storage solutions
abstract: Non-free water dominated electrolyte architecture is reviewed based on a new perspective of different forms of electrolytes, mainly including lean-water liquid electrolytes, gelatinized electrolytes, and solidified electrolytes.

title: Enhanced negative electrocaloric effect in Sn-doped PbHfO 3 films through phase transition induction
abstract: Sn 4+ doping in PbHfO 3 films lowers the antiferroelectric–ferroelectric (AFE–FE) energy barrier, improving polarization. By increasing the entropy change (Δ S ), it significantly enhances the negative electrocaloric effect (NECE).

title: Prospective CaSnS 3 /Si tandem solar cells: assessing material aspects and quantifying temperature-induced efficiency losses
abstract: To aid in the development of perovskite/Si tandem cells with a Sn-based absorber as the top cell, the impact of important parameters like temperature and spectral variation is studied in a CaSnS 3 /Si tandem cell.

title: Impact of LiPON incorporation on the ionic conductivity of mixed oxy-sulfide glassy solid electrolytes
abstract: Mixed oxy-sulfide-nitride GSEs show that higher di-coordinated nitrogen and binding energy enable stable cell cycling, while lower values lead to shorting.

title: Ion transport in dry and hydrated Ba 0.95 La 0.05 (Fe 1− x Y x )O 3− δ and implications for oxygen electrode kinetics of protonic ceramic cells
abstract: Proton diffusivity in doped BaFeO 3− δ determined using SIMS. Doping with Y 3+ decreases effective proton and diffusivity. Importance of and for kinetics of porous electrode.

title: Safe and stable Zn-lignin batteries with a biopolymer based hydrogel electrolyte
abstract: Safe, economical and high-performance aqueous Zn-lignin batteries with an agarose–glycerol based hydrogel electrolyte containing non-fluorinated salts.

title: Synthesis and DFT study of NH 2 -MOF235(Fe)-derived ZnFe 2 O 4 –Fe 2 O 3 –ZnO multiple heterojunction nanocomposites for triethylamine gas detection
abstract: This work designs a ZnFe 2 O 4 –Fe 2 O 3 –ZnO multiple heterojunctions from NH 2 -MOF235(Fe)@MOF5 for TEA sensing. The sensor exhibits excellent properties including high sensitivity, fast response, low detection limit, supported by DFT calculations.

title: Multifunctional amine mediated synthesis of COF nanosheets for desalination membranes
abstract: The multifunctional amine in COF nanosheet synthesis enables catalysis, defect healing, and intercalation, achieving controlled polymerization and morphology preservation. Resulting COF membranes show high performance in desalination.

title: Biomimetic CuO/ZTF-8 nanozyme-based neoteric sensor for the selective detection of superoxide anions
abstract: CuO/ZTF-8 nanocomposite with Cu–Zn superoxide dismutase-mimicking activity has been designed, and a nanozyme-modified electrode has been fabricated and employed for electrochemical detection of superoxide anion radicals at a low potential of −0.2 V.

title: Significant suppression of exothermic heat flow in silicon anodes via in situ polymerization of phosphonium ionic liquids
abstract: A flame-retardant protective layer formed via in situ polymerization of phosphonium-based ionic liquid monomers, driven by electron-induced reduction of allyl groups during lithiation on the silicon surface.

title: Suppressing nonradiative energy loss in ternary organic solar cells through elaborate disruption of guest acceptors planarity
abstract: Through controlling the terminal steric hindrance groups, we can fabricate high-performance organic solar cells with reduced nonradiative energy loss.

title: Stable ionic thermopower materials for waste heat recovery above 100 °C
abstract: The study proposes ionic thermoelectric materials (i-TEs) used above 120 °C for the first time, and the ionic conductivity reaches 67.2 mS cm −1 near 0% relative humidity, which can continuously generate thermal voltage at 120 °C.

title: Cutting-edge approaches for customizing sulfur cathode materials in sodium–sulfur batteries operating at ambient temperature
abstract: Illustration of different approaches for tailoring sulfur cathodes for RT Na–S batteries.

title: Tracking intermediates of photocatalytic hydrogen peroxide generation from a heptazine-based covalent organic polymer in water
abstract: We report a metal-free covalent organic polymer (COP) comprised of heptazine-hydrazine moieties and 2,4,6-triformylphloroglucinol (Tp) for photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production with in situ detection of transient anion radical intermediates.

title: A nitrogen-rich porous carbon electrocatalyst derived from supramolecular polymer-encapsulated iron precursors for the oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: A cucurbit[7]uril-bridged acrylic acid polymer (CB7&PAA) synthesized via one-pot Fc encapsulation integrates FeCl 2 /CsBr/ZnCl 2 . Freeze-drying/carbonization yields Fe 1,2 –N–C single-atom catalyst with high ORR activity (0.91 V half-wave, 5.50 mA cm −2 ).

title: First-principles and machine learning insights into the design of DOTT-carbon and its lithium-ion storage capacity
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) carbon-based materials are promising candidates for developing more efficient green energy conversion and storage technologies.

title: Highly dispersed copper-based nanocomposite synthesis via spray pyrolysis: towards waste-to-hydrogen production through the water-gas shift reaction
abstract: In this study, we synthesized a Cu–ZrCeO 2 catalyst using spray pyrolysis, which exhibited high activity, stability, and reusability at high temperatures.

title: A one-dimensional lead-free hybrid perovskite for stable X-ray detection with low detection limits
abstract: A new lead-free one-dimensional hybrid perovskite, (PPA) 2 BiCl 5 , was constructed, enabling green X-ray detection with low detection limits and high working stability.

title: Compositional study of Ti–Nb oxide (TiNb 2 O 7 , Ti 2 Nb 10 O 29 , Ti 2 Nb 14 O 39 , and TiNb 24 O 62 ) anodes for high power Li ion batteries
abstract: Systematic synthesis of titanium niobium oxides unveils TiNb 2 O 7 's superior cycling and rate performance, attributed to efficient Nb redox utilization, offering advancements in high-power Li-ion battery anodes.

title: Advanced 1D SWCNT-interwoven hybrid electrode architecture for enhanced electrochemical performance in Na-seawater batteries
abstract: A seawater battery using SWCNTs with a fibrous nanomaterial hard carbon (FNHC) anode and a one-dimensional bucky paper (1DBP) cathode significantly enhanced performance, achieving a specific energy density of 693 W h kg −1 at 2.0C.

title: Correction: Accelerating discovery of glass materials in electronic devices through topology-guided machine learning
abstract: Correction for ‘Accelerating discovery of glass materials in electronic devices through topology-guided machine learning’ by Huang Ming et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA08686D.

title: Correction: Enhancing the performance of indoor organic photovoltaics through precise modulation of chlorine density in wide bandgap random copolymers
abstract: Correction for ‘Enhancing the performance of indoor organic photovoltaics through precise modulation of chlorine density in wide bandgap random copolymers’ by Soyoung Kim et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2024, 12 , 2685–2696, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TA06624J.

title: Correction: Boosting biological hydrogen production by integrating functionally symbiotic bacteria/algae with engineered nitrogen-doped carbon dots
abstract: Correction for ‘Boosting biological hydrogen production by integrating functionally symbiotic bacteria/algae with engineered nitrogen-doped carbon dots’ by Tianchong Li et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA02188J.

title: Ordered nanoporous cellulose-based membranes fabricated via breath figure method for copper ion detection
abstract: Cellulose-based graft copolymers were utilized to successfully fabricate ordered nanoporous membranes via the breath figure method. These membranes exhibit ion selectivity and are capable of detecting Cu 2+ concentrations in aqueous solution.

title: Enhancing the catalytic conversion of polysulfides utilizing a covalent organic framework–carbon nanotube interlayer
abstract: Lithium–sulfur (Li–S) batteries, characterized by their exceptionally high theoretical energy density of 2600 Wh kg −1 , encounter significant challenges related to polysulfide shuttling and slow redox kinetics.

title: Study of electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction using tin-oxalate organometallic frameworks doped with cadmium
abstract: The chronoamperometric technique was combined with ATR-FTIR spectroscopy to analyse the CO 2 -derived reduction products in real time.

title: In hot pursuit: tracking ionic and phononic heat carriers in Li 3 OCl across temperatures
abstract: Using machine-learned molecular dynamics and ab initio anharmonic lattice dynamics, we reveal the temperature-dependent thermal transport mechanisms in Li 3 OCl, including a non-negligible ionic heat transport above ∼1100 K.

title: Precise active-layer morphology regulation in organic solar cells via novel volatile solid additives that drive acceptor aggregation
abstract: Two novel volatile solid additives, 2-bromo-1,3,5-trichlorobenzene (TCBB-1) and 3,4,5-trichlorobromobenzene (TCBB-2), are introduced into PM6:L8-BO non-fullerene systems to regulate film nano-morphology.

title: A three-dimensional TiO 2 @Bi 2 MoO 6 heterojunction array as photoanode for efficient photoelectrochemical water splitting
abstract: A three-dimensional (3D) TiO 2 @Bi 2 MoO 6 heterojunction photoanode achieves exceptional PEC performance (3.7 mA cm −2 , 71.4% IPCE) via enhanced light harvesting and efficient charge separation enabled by type-II band alignment.

title: Integration of p-type-doped carbon nanostructures as additives for boosting spiro-OMeTAD performance in perovskite solar cells
abstract: A comprehensive study of the implementation of carbon nanomaterials (CNMs) as additives for spiro-OMeTAD is outlined, showing the potential of SWCNT-ZnP as p-dopant for improving the performance and stability of spiro-OMeTAD.

title: A nanocrystalline La 0.6 Sr 0.4 Co 0.4 Fe 0.6 O 3− δ interlayer for an enhanced oxygen electrode–electrolyte interface in solid oxide cells
abstract: A catalytically active and intimate interface between the electrode and electrolyte is crucial for the performance of solid oxide cells (SOCs).

title: Interfacial chemistry of CuBi 2 O 4 in aqueous media: engineering strategies for energy and environmental applications
abstract: We conducted a systematic analysis of CuBi 2 O 4 's surface and interface interactions, investigated a series of novel solutions for CuBi 2 O 4 -related challenges, and summarized some of its key applications.

title: A battery–capacitor hybrid yarn device with excellent flexibility and high electrochemical performance
abstract: Battery–capacitor hybrid yarn devices assembled based on battery-based yarn electrodes and capacitor-type yarn electrodes will obtain high capacity, high energy density, wide operating voltage window, long cycle life and excellent flexibility.

title: Superior performance of an ultrathin pyridinic-layered micro-structural porous silicon anode with a silicon content exceeding 99%
abstract: Si-based materials have become the focus of current research efforts, emerging as leading candidates for next-generation lithium-ion battery anodes due to their exceptional theoretical capacities.

title: Fundamental concepts of 3,4-alkoxythiophene-based polymeric systems, from synthesis to applications
abstract: The most recent developments in the study of the 3,4-alkoxy thiophenes; including the synthetic routes, polymerization, functionalization, and applications.

title: Covalent organic framework confined nanocrystals towards interpenetrating heterojunctions for efficient photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production and selective organic catalysis
abstract: A nanoconfined interpenetrating ZnCdS–COF heterojunction enhanced interfacial coupling and built-in electric field, prolonging carrier lifetime to boost photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production and thiophenol coupling under visible light.

title: Selective electrostatic sorption of water-soluble anionic molecules over electrospun cationic polymer fibers
abstract: Electrospun fibers decorated with cationic cross-linked polymer were fabricated for selective adsorption of anionic water-soluble dyes.

title: A porphyrin co-catalyst enhancing low-temperature CO 2 hydrogenation at the water–Cu x Au y /ZnO interface
abstract: The activation of CO 2 at the liquid/solid interface is highly promising for various catalytic processes and applications, offering the significant advantage of reduced mass and heat transfer effects.

title: Nanoscale characterization of halide perovskite phase stability with scanning electron microscopy
abstract: Scanning electron microscopy yields high-resolution insight into the perovskite to non-perovskite phase transformations of materials such as cesium lead iodide and formamidinium lead iodide.

title: Oxygen vacancy-rich Pr 6 O 11 : unlocking superior catalytic support for efficient ammonia synthesis
abstract: Pr 6 O 11 with oxygen-vacancy-abundant (311) facets was developed as an efficient support to construct a highly active Ru catalyst with excellent durability for ammonia synthesis.

title: Electron-donor engineering of heptazine-based donor–acceptor conjugated microporous polymers for efficient metal-free photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: Electron-donor engineering is applied to interpret the detailed changes in the microstructure, physicochemical properties, and PHE performance of heptazine-based CMPs.

title: Improving the long-term weatherability of commercial radiative cooling materials by applying a fluorine-free superhydrophobic coating
abstract: The long-term resistance of commercial radiative cooling materials to extreme weathers is greatly improved by applying a fluorine-free superhydrophobic coating.

title: Unblocking the potential of Ni-based metallic glasses for glucose sensing through surface porous engineering
abstract: Porous MGs via controlled pitting enhanced electrochemical surface area and charge transfer, boosting sensing performance. The study also clarified the correlation between electrochemical capability and short-medium range atomic structures.

title: Interfacial bond engineering in antimony selenosulfide solar cells via methylammonium lead bromide perovskite film as a hole transport layer
abstract: Innovative MAPbBr 3 hole transport layer (HTL) in Sb 2 (S,Se) 3 solar cells yields 9.37% PCE, enabled by cliff-like band alignment (Δ E VBM = 0.12 eV) suppressing hole recombination and interfacial chemical coordination facilitating carrier transport.

title: Tuning interfacial *H coverage and aldehyde adsorption configuration for selective electrocatalytic hydrogenation of furfural
abstract: The *H coverage and furfural adsorption configuration are simultaneously regulated to probe into the interfacial structure–activity relationship and optimize the electrocatalytic hydrogenation (ECH) selectivity of furfural.

title: Gram-scale green synthesis of a highly stable cationic covalent organic framework for efficient and selective removal of ReO 4 − / 99 TcO 4 −
abstract: Green hydrothermal synthesis achieves gram-scale production of a cationic COF sorbent with high stability for the rapid and selective removal of 99 TcO 4 − contaminants.

title: In situ grown Na + -doped NH 4 V 4 O 10 nano-arrays on carbon cloth with ultra-high specific capacity for high-performance aqueous ammonium ion hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Doping sodium ions in NH 4 V 4 O 10 interlayer reduces the deammoniation. The NaNVO@CC has ultra-high specific capacity and excellent cycling stability. Systematic ex situ characterization experiments confirm reversible NH 4 + ions (de)intercalation.

title: Reconstructing a Gd 3+ -enriched inner Helmholtz plane with a dynamic electrostatic shielding effect for highly reversible Zn–bromine flow batteries
abstract: This work shows that reconstructing a Gd 3+ -enriched Helmholtz plane enhances interfacial stability, improves Zn anode reversibility, and boosts capacity and cycling stability in zinc–bromine flow batteries.

title: Advanced symmetric supercapacitor of spinel NiCo 2 O 4 -incorporated 2D g-C 3 N 4 electrodes in a PVA–KOH gel-electrolyte-based pouch cell
abstract: Synergistic nanocomposite of g-C 3 N 4 and NiCo 2 O 4 balances individual limitations, enhancing performance in a symmetric pouch cell.

title: Representation learning for long-chain hydrocarbon adsorption in zeolites
abstract: A suite of machine learning models was assessed for predicting long-chain hydrocarbon adsorption in zeolites. Graph neural networks and vision transformers struggle while ZeoNet significantly outperforms others.

title: A DFT investigation of photocatalytic water splitting properties of the InS/GaTe heterostructure: direct Z-scheme vs. traditional type-II
abstract: A new approach is presented to distinguish Z-scheme and type-II in band staggerd heterostructures: a case study on InS/GaTe.

title: Co nanoparticles encapsulated in N-doped carbon nanotube materials derived from new metal–organic frameworks for oxygen electrocatalysis
abstract: We report a novel metal–organic framework featuring Co–N 4 structures. Upon pyrolysis, Co nanoparticles encapsulated within N-doped carbon nanotubes were obtained with exceptional electrocatalytic oxygen reduction/evolution reaction activities.

title: Modulating carbon quantum dots using multi-heteroatom doping as a bifunctional electrocatalyst for efficient oxygen and hydrogen evolution reactions in alkaline electrolytes
abstract: Synthesis of multi-heteroatom doped carbon quantum dots is demonstrated. The obtained Si, N, P-CQDs showed exceptional OER in 1.0M KOH by reaching 10 mA cm −2 at 117 mV with a Tafel slope of 221.2 mV dec −1 and −10 mA cm −2 at 338 mV with 113 mV dec −1 for HER.

title: Enhancing electrocatalytic nitrate reduction to ammonia via atomically dilute iron doping in SnS
abstract: An atomically dilute amount (around 2%) of Fe doping into SnS lattice leads to nearly two-fold enhancement of electrocatalytic nitrate reduction efficieny and yield of produced NH 3 compared to that obatained for the undoped SnS.

title: Screening efficient Pt-based dual-metal atomic-level catalysts for the oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: A new class of Pt-based dual catalysts for efficient oxygen reduction was uncovered through systematic investigations involving high-throughput screening, first-principles computations, and molecular dynamics simulations.

title: Rational design of metal node-modified Ti-based MOFs for selective photoreduction of carbon dioxide to ethanol by computational screening
abstract: DFT calculations were used to screen and design highly efficient metal node-modified Ti-based MOFs for CO 2 photo-reduction to C 2 products. Ti(Nb)-ATA was identified as the most active and selective catalyst for CO 2 reduction to ethanol.

title: pH-responsive intelligent anticorrosion protection: catechol-driven organic–inorganic hybridization with TiO 2 and encapsulation by ZIF-90
abstract: A challenge in metal protection is developing anti-corrosion materials that possess both high adsorption capacity and intelligent responsiveness.

title: Computational design of phosphate fluoride cathode materials for Na-based batteries
abstract: Transition metal substitution in Na x V 2 (PO 4 ) 2 F 3 (0 ≤ x ≤ 4) is investigated using DFT (r 2 SCAN + U), revealing voltage profiles, crystal structures and Na + migration barriers. Potential synthesis routes to the novel materials are evaluated.

title: From energy to intelligence: MXenes transforming triboelectric nanogenerators
abstract: Incorporating the MXenes as electrodes, positive and negative tribolayers in triboelectric nanogenerators enhances the TENGs' performance.

title: Sn-doped Co–P-based trifunctional electrocatalysts for accelerating water splitting and hydrogen generation concurrent with ethylene glycol electrooxidation
abstract: A time-and energy-efficient one-step electrodeposition method was employed to synthesize robust Sn-doped Co–P electrocatalysts for water splitting and ethylene glycol oxidation, where metals drive HER and metal phosphides act as OER sites.

title: Covalently bonded Si–SiOC–C heterostructural nanocomposites as durable anode materials for high-energy lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Heterostructural Si–SiOC–C anode featuring strong and abundant Si–O–C covalent bonds exhibits excellent electrochemical properties in Li-ion batteries.

title: A bifunctional NiS 2 @MXene (Ti 3 C 2 T x ) composite separator for high-performance lithium–sulfur batteries with enhanced polysulfide inhibition and uniform lithium-ion transport
abstract: Capacity degradation from uncontrolled polysulfide diffusion and dendrite growth at the anode are key challenges in lithium–sulfur batteries.

title: Strain-regulated exsolution of Ru on hydrogen-storing oxide for ambient ammonia catalysis
abstract: We developed a highly dispersed Ru nanocatalyst (∼6 nm) via exsolution on BCZY, enabling high ammonia synthesis at 1 atm. TGA precisely quantified the exsolution degree (>96% at optimal conditions) and clarified that Ru nanoparticle formation proceeds through a strain-limited exsolution mechanism.

title: Advancements and challenges in g-C 3 N 4 /ZnIn 2 S 4 heterojunction photocatalysts
abstract: In this review, a comprehensive summation of the heterojunction types, synthesis methodologies, engineering approaches, and potential photocatalytic applications pertaining to g-C 3 N 4 /ZnIn 2 S 4 heterojunctions has been presented and in-depth discussed.

title: Enhancing photocatalytic activity through the manipulation of intrinsic electric fields in yolk–shell hollow AuPd@TiO 2 structures
abstract: The yolk–shell AuPd@TiO 2 photocatalyst optimized the built-in electric field by controlling Au/Pd ratios, leading to d-band center closer to the Fermi level, thus enhancing electronic interactions and stabilizing antibonding orbitals.

title: Monitoring photovoltages produced at semiconductor/liquid interfaces using in situ surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) spectroscopy
abstract: This study introduces a novel in situ SERS method to monitor photovoltages at semiconductor/liquid interfaces using vibrational Stark effects, offering real-time insights into charge dynamics across the full electrochemical range.

title: Synergistic surface charge channel and oxygen vacancy engineering in Sillén–Aurivillius Bi 7 Fe 2 Ti 2 O 17 Cl oxyhalides using in situ Ag clusters for boosting photocatalytic activity
abstract: Silver (Ag)-cluster-decorated novel Sillén–Aurivillius oxyhalides (Bi 7 Fe 2 Ti 2 O 17 Cl) with abundant oxygen vacancies (OVs) were successfully synthesized to achieve efficient light absorption and interfacial charge separation.

title: Solving ZIB challenges: the dynamic role of water in deep eutectic solvents electrolyte
abstract: The graphical abstract shows a schematic representation of Zn plating from a deep eutectic solvent with different hydration level pinpointing advantages and draw backs.

title: Coupling of high ion transport efficiency in hydrogel electrolytes and interfacial fusion for performance enhancement in all-solid-state paper-based self-powered electrochromic devices with low-temperature tolerance
abstract: Coupling the Hofmeister effect-enhanced hydrogel electrolyte with gravure-printed micro-rough paper electrode is a facile way to construct a low-temperature-tolerant all-solid-state paper-based self-powered ECD.

title: Temperature-driven phase engineering: synergistic effect between NiS 2 and MoS 2 coupled with N-doped graphene self-supported on carbon cloth for efficient electrocatalytic seawater splitting
abstract: The MoS 2 –NiS 2 /N-G/CC catalyst, synthesized at 500 °C, efficiently splits seawater due to abundant active sites and the synergy between MoS 2 and NiS 2 .

title: Flexible pressure sensors based on electrospun PAN fiber films incorporated with graphene/polypyrrole composites and engineered PDMS microstructures
abstract: Flexible pressure sensors with superior performance were fabricated based on polyacrylonitrile fiber films incorporated with graphene/polypyrrole composites and microstructured PDMS layers, realizing detection of subtle motion and associated signals.

title: Solution-processed CuI as a hole transport layer for Sn–Pb perovskite solar cells
abstract: CuI as a hole transport layer was deposited from a solution for the fabrication of methylammonium (MA)-free half Sn-half Pb perovskite solar cells with a power conversion efficiency of over 20% and enhanced stability.

title: A facile strategy to design shape memory rubber composites with tunable mechanical properties and photothermal performance
abstract: A dual cross-linking strategy has been proposed to fabricate multi-stimuli-responsive shape memory rubber composites with tunable mechanical properties and photothermal performance.

title: Cerium-optimized high-entropy spinel oxide for efficient and anti-interference removal of VOC from complex flue gas
abstract: The high-entropy spinel oxide (CeMnFeCoCr) 3 O 4 enables efficient catalytic oxidation of VOC in complex flue gas containing multi-pollutants, SO2 and H2O.

title: Synergistic engineering of buried interfaces for high-efficiency and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: This study modifies the SnO 2 /perovskite interface with p -toluenethiol, achieving a power conversion efficiency of 25.53% for perovskite solar cells and 23.27% for flexible devices, with significantly enhanced stability.

title: Multilayer composites containing anisotropic structured aerogels: a strategy for enhancing the electromagnetic shielding absorption mechanism and achieving multi-functional integration
abstract: The multilayer composite containing an anisotropic structure induces the mechanism of absorption–reflection–reabsorption, reducing the reflection coefficient and achieving an EMI SE of 67.17 dB, integrating Joule heating and pressure-sensing.

title: Mechanically and photoelectrochemically stable WO 3 |BiVO 4 |NiFeOOH photoanodes synthesised by a scalable chemical vapour deposition method
abstract: Scalable and stable WO 3 |BiVO 4 |NiFeOOH photoanodes were fabricated by a novel chemical vapour deposition method. Excellent mechanical and photoelectrochemical stability was achieved over 24 hours in a PEC flow cell.

title: Exploring the photoactive properties of promising MXenes for water splitting
abstract: Ten MXenes show robust stability, high carriers mobilities, strong visible light absorption, and promising solar-to-hydrogen efficiency for water splitting photocatalysis.

title: Establishing a long-term stable cathode–electrolyte interphase through multi-cationic competitive coordination for 4.6 V LiCoO 2
abstract: Anchoring anions and solvent through competitive coordination of Rb + and suppressing side reactions for long-term stability of the battery.

title: Dopant chemical anchoring enables ultra-low interfacial thermal resistance composite phase-change metal foil with high reliability for efficient heat dissipation
abstract: In this study, by establishing covalent bonding between Field's metal and copper particles, a non-gallium alloy-based composite phase-change thermal interface material with high reliability and ultra-low interfacial thermal resistance was developed.

title: Polyelectrolyte matrix-enabled multi-step energy transfer light-harvesting system for enhanced photocatalytic benzimidazole synthesis
abstract: A multi-step sequential energy transfer system was developed through electrostatic interactions in the polyelectrolyte matrix, which could be used as a photocatalyst for the photocatalytic synthesis of benzimidazole.

title: Synthesis of two-dimensional TaB 2 and its application in electrocatalytic water splitting
abstract: Diborides of transition metals (IV–VIB) possess a layered structure, and their exfoliation into two-dimensional (2D) materials has been attempted; however, this is challenging due to the strength of the metal–boron bonds between the layers.

title: Acidic–neutral decoupled biphasic electrolytes enhance deposition–dissolution chemistry in Zn–Mn batteries
abstract: An aqueous–organic biphasic electrolyte was designed for a high-voltage Zn–Mn battery, exhibiting Mn 2+ /MnO 2 redox chemistry in the acidic aqueous phase and stable Zn plating/stripping in the neutral organic phase.

title: Deep learning prediction of electrode voltage for metal-ion batteries
abstract: A deep learning network model for predicting the average voltage of electrode materials for metal-ion batteries has been developed, achieving a mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.04 V, representing the highest accuracy for voltage prediction.

title: Microscopic insights into the ring-opening reaction of ethylene carbonate on LiCoO 2 by on-the-fly machine learning molecular dynamics
abstract: On-the-fly machine learning molecular dynamics simulations reveal spontaneous EC ring-opening and self-healing dynamics at the LiCoO 2 /EC interface at 300 K as well as the substantial role of surface Li-deficiency in promoting EC ring-opening.

title: Honeycomb graphite network confined in biphasic TiO 2 homojunction nanotubes as the sulfur host for advanced lithium sulfur batteries
abstract: Biphasic TiO 2 homojunction nanotubes containing an N-doped honeycomb graphite network with a carbon layer are designed as the sulfur host to significantly enhance the performance of Li–S batteries.

title: Synergistic action of spatially self-reconfiguring bilayer lithiophilic alloys and inorganic passivation layers for enhancing Li metal anode performance
abstract: Synergistic interaction of spatially self-reconfiguring bilayer lithium–magnesium and lithium–zinc alloys through covered graphene oxide improves the performance of lithium metal anodes.

title: Ge nanograin-enhanced Si/C composite anodes: anchored interfaces for rapid electron and ion conduction
abstract: Ge-modulated Si/C interfaces boost ionic/electronic transport and suppress Si detachment, yielding enhanced rate capability and long-term cycling performance.

title: Nanomechanical and nanoelectrical analysis of the proton exchange membrane water electrolyzer anode – impact of reinforcement fibers and porous transport layer
abstract: An operated reinforced membrane electrode assembly anode was analyzed with high resolution. The fiber-reinforcement provided long-term stability, while nanomechanical and nanoelectrical aging was especially observed at porous transport layer marks.

title: Synergistic spin-ligand effects on the oxygen reduction activity of the FePPc electrocatalyst
abstract: As a typical single-atom catalyst (SAC), iron( ii ) polyphthalocyanine (FePPc) with an Fe–N 4 structure was experimentally found to have excellent ORR activity.

title: Atomic layer deposition of a thin TiO 2 layer on nickel-rich cathode NCM83 for improved cycling stability
abstract: Coating of a thin TiO 2 layer of about 3 nm thickness on NCM83 using the atomic layer deposition method effectively suppresses interfacial side reactions, mitigates phase transitions, and enhances cycling stability.

title: High-performance Y5–SWCNT thermoelectric alloy produced via pre-crystallization filtration
abstract: Pre-crystallization filtration (PCF) affords an air-stable Y5/SWCNT alloy with good thermoelectric properties (power factor of 257.52 μW m −1 K −2 ) together with being efficient in utilizing Y5 molecules, and stable under high humidity and upon bending.

title: A room-temperature self-healing and mechanically robust siloxane elastomer via synergistic complexation and cation–π interactions for high-performance electromagnetic interference shielding
abstract: A room-temperature self-healing and mechanically robust siloxane elastomer is combined with silver nanowires to obtain a self-healing electromagnetic interference shielding material.

title: A ductile and strong bioplastic film based on biodegradable polyurethane nanoparticle-crosslinked chitosan
abstract: This study presents a facile strategy for fabricating chitosan/polyurethane nanoparticle (PU NP) bioplastic films that simultaneously enhance both ductility and strength due to their unique elastic node-grid structure.

title: A high-performance solid-state electrolyte Na 4 ZrF 8 with exceptional ionic conductivity and electrochemical and interfacial stability
abstract: We propose a promising sodium fluoride solid-state electrolyte (SSE), Na 4 ZrF 8 (NZF), by using density functional theory combined with ab initio molecular dynamics simulations and grand potential phase diagram analysis.

title: Polycarbonate-based solid-state sodium batteries with inclusion of NaAlO 2 microparticle additives
abstract: Micron-sized NaAlO 2 fillers enhance the ionic conductivity and transference number in solid polymer electrolytes of poly(trimethylene carbonate). The electrolyte is functional in Na-based solid-state cells with Na-metal and Prussian white.

title: N/P co-doping regulates the local microcrystalline structure of hard carbon to facilitate sodium-ion storage
abstract: A N/P co-doped hard carbon was synthesized. Beneficial atomic doping positively affects closed pore formation, achieving synergistic optimization of slope and plateau capacities.

title: Engineering atomic-scale synergy of Ni and Mn dual-atom catalysts for highly efficient CO 2 electroreduction
abstract: A novel Ni 1 Mn 1 –NC dual-atom catalyst with neighboring Ni and Mn atomic interactions is constructed. The electronic interaction between Ni and Mn dual atoms achieves excellent performance for CO 2 reduction to CO at low overpotentials.

title: Voltage-dependent charge compensation mechanism and cathode electrolyte interface stability of the lithium-ion battery cathode materials LiNi 0.6 Mn 0.2 Co 0.2 O 2 , LiNi 0.8 Mn 0.1 Co 0.1 O 2 and LiNi 0.80 Co 0.15 Al 0.05 O 2 studied by photoelectron spectroscopy
abstract: Voltage-dependent charge compensation mechanisms of nickel-rich cathodes studied by photoemission spectroscopy. Insights into the cathode-electrolyte interphase revealed through in vacuo scratching followed by photoemission spectroscopy.

title: Switching between HCOOH and CO in electrochemical CO 2 reduction by doping Cu into Cd(OH) 2 catalysts
abstract: In this work, Cu-doped Cd(OH) 2 modulates CO 2 RR selectivity between HCOOH and CO, enhancing CO 2 adsorption and kinetics, and lowering the energy barrier for *COOH formation. This guides catalyst design for product control.

title: Nano-molybdenum oxide modified expanded graphite for high performance lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Nano-MoO 3 distributed the bulk of graphite enabled enhanced capacity and a stable solid electrolyte interphase for lower interfacial resistance and fast charge transfer kinetics, contributing to the high performance of lithium-ion batteries.

title: Na layer pillar ion post-doping facilitates diffusion kinetics and structural stability in NaNi 0.5 Mn 0.5 O 2
abstract: Compared to Ca pre-doping inhibiting pure-phase formation, the Ca post-doping strategy enables the synthesis of structurally stable NaNi 0.5 Mn 0.5 O 2 cathodes with fast Na + diffusion kinetics.

title: Alkane diols as a new organic phase change material encapsulated in densely packed and stacked carbonized microspheres for thermal energy storage
abstract: A novel PEG-inspired alkane diol PCM was encapsulated in carbonized, densely stacked COF microspheres and packaged with copper foil tape for thermal energy storage, exhibiting good thermal storage performance.

title: Synthesis, properties, and photocatalytic hydrogen evolution of isoindigo-based conjugated molecules
abstract: D–A–D conjugated systems containing triarylamine, isoindigo, and cyano functional groups have been constructed and coupled with Pt nanoparticles for application in photoinduced hydrogen evolution.

title: Retraction: Selective synthesis of α-Fe 2 O 3 thin films and effect of the deposition temperature and lattice oxygen on the catalytic combustion of propene
abstract: Retraction of ‘Selective synthesis of α-Fe 2 O 3 thin films and effect of the deposition temperature and lattice oxygen on the catalytic combustion of propene’ by Patrick Mountapmbeme Kouotou et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2013, 1 , 10495–10504, https://doi.org/10.1039/C3TA11354J

title: Correction: Reversible lattice oxygen participation in Ru 1− x O 2− x for superior acidic oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Correction for ‘Reversible lattice oxygen participation in Ru 1− x O 2− x for superior acidic oxygen evolution reaction’ by Jia Cao et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA01484K.

title: Correction: Unlocking the potential of selenium solar cells for indoor and tandem photovoltaics through theoretical and photoelectric simulations
abstract: Correction for ‘Unlocking the potential of selenium solar cells for indoor and tandem photovoltaics through theoretical and photoelectric simulations’ by Haoyun Dou et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, 13 , 17317–17328, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA03162A.

title: Correction: Reforming material chemistry of CIGS solar cells via a precise Ag doping strategy
abstract: Correction for “Reforming material chemistry of CIGS solar cells via a precise Ag doping strategy” by Jiseon Hwang et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2023, 11 , 19546–19555, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TA03131D.

title: Surface-enhanced and electric field-modulated reactivity of Fe( iv )O complexes: unveiling the synergy of Lewis acid additives, Au(111), and graphene surfaces in biomimetic C–H activation
abstract: This study explores how oriented external electric fields (OEEFs) and surface interactions influence the catalytic performance of heme Fe IV O complexes for C–H activation.

title: Challenges and strategies for probing the composite interface of PEM electrolyzers and fuel cells using operando AP-XPS
abstract: Left: cross-section schematic of a membrane electrode assembly, the working electrode changing state with applied potential. Center: the operando cell design that enables snapshot data acquisition during trajectory movement. Right: resulting spectra.

title: Synthesis of insensitive, high-density energetic materials through molecular self-assembly
authors: 'Jinhao Zhang', 'Jichuan Zhang', 'Richard J. Staples', 'Jiaheng Zhang', 'Jean'ne M. Shreeve'
abstract: Five high-density, insensitive energetic materials, namely, TNPDO-ADNP (1), TNPDO-DNBT (2), TNPDO-55BT (3), TNPDO-DNABO (4), and TNPDO-BTO (5), were obtained through hydrogen bonding self-assembly combining TNPDO with five acidic energetic compounds.

title: An innovative S-scheme β-Bi 2 O 3 /BaTiO 3 heterojunction nanocomposite with enhanced stability and photocatalytic performance
abstract: Herein, a novel S-scheme β-Bi 2 O 3 /BaTiO 3 heterojunction nanocomposite was prepared for the first time. The nanocomposite can efficiently degrade RhB under simulated sunlight irradiation.

title: Suppression of α-quartz in montmorillonite–SiOC ceramic nanocomposites with water vapor-assisted pyrolysis
abstract: We present unreported mechanistic insight into how water vapor-assisted pyrolysis in the presence of MMT hinders SiO 2 crystallization and carbon removal, demonstrating a reversal of water vapor-induced effects in the presence of MMT nanosheets.

title: Nanoarchitectured in situ pre-lithiated carbon anodes for high-power and long-life Li-ion capacitors
abstract: In situ prelithiation occurs due to the chemical potential difference between the lithium metal foil and the carbon. This process leads to the formation of a stable solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) layer, which improves cycle performance.

title: Constructing functionalized eco-friendly boron nitride and diatomaceous earth based phase change composite backsheets for thermal management and fire safety of solar modules
abstract: DE/BNN PCM solar backsheets enable radiative/phase-change cooling, enhancing Si solar cell efficiency by 9.3% via temperature regulation, flame retardancy, and weathering resistance.

title: A comprehensive review of form-stable phase change materials in cold chain logistics: encapsulation strategies, thermal conductivity enhancement, and applications
abstract: Integration diagram of FSPCMs, including preparation methods, thermal conductivity enhancement, and application in cold chain logistics.

title: Synergetic defect and local structure engineering to boost photocatalytic activity of ZnIn 2− x Cu x S 4 nanosheets for H 2 O 2 production
abstract: The combined aliovalent Cu substitution and exfoliation strategy provides an effective approach to enhance the photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production performance of ZnIn 2 S 4 materials.

title: An antibacterial sensitive wearable biosensor enabled by engineered metal-boride-based organic electrochemical transistors and hydrogel microneedles
abstract: We report an antibacterial, ultrasensitive wearable device for non-invasive cortisol monitoring by integrating hydrogel microneedles with silver-nanomaterial-engineered metal boride-based transistors.

title: Fluorinated imine modulating efficient sulfur redox kinetics and a stable solid electrolyte interphase in lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: The shuttle effect of lithium polysulfides (LiPSs) and the instability of the solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) lead to lithium dendrite growth and severe corrosion of lithium anodes (Li-anodes) for lithium–sulfur (Li–S) batteries.

title: Constructing TiO 6 -rich hierarchical TS-1 zeolites via biuret-assisted synthesis for improved selective oxidation catalysis
abstract: Biuret-assisted synthesis was developed for simultaneously optimizing the TiO 6 concentration and specific surface area of hierarchical TS-1 zeolites, which thus improved the catalytic performance in oxidative desulfurization and 1-hexene epoxidation.

title: Breaking barriers in nitrate electroreduction: robust Cu–Zn catalysts for selective ammonia production with ultra-high rate in neutral medium
abstract: The electrocatalytic nitrate reduction reaction (NO 3 − RR) offers a sustainable route for ammonia synthesis under ambient conditions, presenting a more environmentally favorable alternative to the energy-intensive Haber–Bosch process.

title: Performance enhancement of triboelectric nanogenerators and exploration of tactile sensing using an electrospun PAN–MWCNT layer through interface manipulation
abstract: Triboelectricity, being ubiquitous, holds promise as an energy source for achieving net zero emissions and self-powered wearables.

title: Almost-solid-state electrolyte with quadruple-hydrogen-bond-based semi-interpenetrating network for lithium metal batteries
abstract: A solid plasticizer UPU in PEO-based SPEs: facilitating Li + transport through semi-interpenetrating networks (SIPN) based on quadruple hydrogen bonds.

title: Unraveling the role of a Cu dopant in formaldehyde catalytic oxidation over a La 0.8 Sr 0.2 Mn 1− x Cu x O 3 perovskite: an experimental and theoretical study
abstract: A series of La 0.8 Sr 0.2 Mn 1− x Cu x O 3 perovskite-type catalysts were prepared through a sol–gel method and evaluated for formaldehyde catalytic oxidation.

title: A loofah-derived biological activated carbon enhanced solar interfacial evaporator for efficient and stable seawater desalination and wastewater purification
abstract: Loofah has been utilized to prepare a loofah-derived biological activated carbon evaporator through a simple combined carbonization and activation process, expecting to achieve efficient and stable seawater desalination and wastewater purification.

title: Flexocatalytically driven water splitting: unprecedented hydrogen production using symmetry invariant ZnIn 2 S 4 nanosheets
abstract: Flexocatalytically-driven H 2 production: by leveraging the flexoelectric potential, unprecedented hydrogen production (60 mmol g −1 h −1 ) was achieved using centrosymmetric 2D ZnIn 2 S 4 nanosheets.

title: Synergistic multi-physics catalysis enabled by lotus-root-inspired flow fields in proton exchange membrane fuel cells
abstract: A PEMFC flow field, inspired by the lotus root structure, has been developed and designed to enhance synergistic catalytic ability and electrochemical output performance.

title: Moderate-temperature fabrication of BaZrS 3 thin films via dithiocarbamate-based solution processing and oxygen-sink boron sulfurization
abstract: A molecular ink-based approach enables the fabrication of BaZrS 3 thin films at moderate temperatures. This method incorporates boron sulfurization, which removes oxygen from the material while ensuring the formation of phase-pure BaZrS 3 .

title: A meta -linked isomer of ITIC: influence of aggregation patterns on open-circuit voltage in organic solar cells
abstract: The aggregation behavior governed by the molecular structure of acceptor molecules significantly affects the charge-transfer energy and the open-circuit voltage in organic solar cells.

title: Proanthocyanidin-enhanced wettability and adhesion in liquid metal inks for multi-substrate patterning in soft electronics
abstract: Multi-surface-wettable and adhesive liquid metal inks are prepared and patterned on elastic fibrous membranes as super-conformal and flexible epidermal bioelectrodes.

title: The role of single-crystal substrates in synthesis of low-dimensional materials by CVD and their applications in electrocatalysis
abstract: Single-crystal substrates have significant influence on the chemical vapor deposition (CVD) method for preparing low-dimensional materials.

title: Integrated passivation strategy using multifunctional additives for tin–lead mixed perovskite solar cells
abstract: An integrated passivation strategy was employed to simultaneously suppress Sn oxidation and interfacial defect states of tin–lead mixed perovskite films effectively using p -guanidinobenzonitrile hydrochloride.

title: Unraveling the CO 2 methanation and capture ability of NiO@metal oxides
abstract: The dual role of a catalyst in CO 2 capture and utilization.

title: Interface modification and crystallization control of efficient and stable perovskite solar cells using dicyandiamide
abstract: Dicyandiamide (DCD) was used to modify SnO 2 , with –CN passivating defects and –NH 2 improving perovskite crystallization. This improved energy alignment and film quality, increasing PCE from 21.28% to 24.50% and enhancing stability.

title: Recent advances in non-noble metal catalysts toward N -ethylcarbazole hydrogen storage
abstract: This paper summarizes NEC non-noble metal hydrogenation catalysts' latest research, covering synthesis, structural features, hydrogen storage performance and development prospect. The aim is to boost non-noble metal catalyst research and application.

title: Fabrication of N-doped carbon coated CoFeS 2 anchored rGO nanosheet composites: a twin carbon design for Li-ion storage and high energy density supercapacitor applications
abstract: The twin-carbon design enhances electron/ion transport, limits volume change, and suppresses side reactions. N-doped carbon-coated CoFeS 2 /rGO improves Li-ion battery and supercapacitor performance.

title: High-throughput screening and an interpretable machine learning model of single-atom hydrogen evolution catalysts with an asymmetric coordination environment constructed from heteroatom-doped graphdiyne
abstract: Exploring high-activity and low-cost electrocatalysts for the hydrogen evolution reaction is the key to developing new energy sources, but it faces major challenges.

title: Beyond activity: from machine learning screening to stability decoding on the study of self-supported metal phosphides in alkaline hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: This review summarizes the research advancements of self-supported TMPs in alkaline HER, including machine learning-assisted screening, strategies for activity enhancement, and factors influencing stability along with improvement methodologies.

title: N-heterocyclic π-conjugated quinone cathodes with multiple chelation for robust sodium batteries
abstract: π-Conjugated N-heterocyclic quinones, particularly DNQ-PTO, demonstrated exceptional electrochemical performance due to their extended π-conjugation and dispersed chelation groups, providing key design principles for organic electrodes.

title: The growth of nanosized zeolite nuclei inside the pinholes of ultra-thin Pd composite membranes
abstract: This study introduces a “region growing method” to in situ grow nanosized zeolite nuclei inside Pd membrane pinholes under ambient conditions, providing a simpler alternative to high-pressure hydrothermal zeolite synthesis.

title: High catalytic activity and stability of visible-light-driven CO 2 reduction via CsPbBr 3 QDs/Cu-BTC core–shell photocatalysts
abstract: Unique Z-scheme CPB QDs/Cu-BTC core–shell photocatalysts were constructed via an in situ growth strategy to improve their photo/chemical stability, CO 2 capture and activation, and charge separation efficiency.

title: Engineering a high-entropy oxide with high-density grain boundaries and strong d–p orbital coupling for advanced lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: A flower-like high-entropy oxide (HEO), consisting of 3d/4d/5d and p-block metal cations with high-density grain boundaries alongside crystalline–amorphous interfaces, has been rationally designed as a high-efficiency sulfur electrocatalyst.

title: Data-driven insights into protonic-ceramic fuel cell and electrolysis performance
authors: 'Charlie Meisel', 'Jake D. Huang', 'Long Q. Le', 'You-Dong Kim', 'Sophia Stockburger', 'Zhixin Luo', 'Tianjiu Zhu', 'Zehua Wang', 'Zongping Shao', 'Ryan O'Hayre', 'Neal P. Sullivan'
abstract: Lowering the electrolyte thickness to grain size ratio, using smaller NiO particles, and removing organics before sintering boosts performance. The positrode is key for fuel cell performance, while the electrolyte impacts electrolysis.

title: Elucidating the active motifs in a reconstructed AgPdF nanoalloy for the formate oxidation reaction
abstract: The fluorinated AgPd surface promotes spontaneous OH adsorption in alkaline media, significantly boosting the formate oxidation reaction (FOR) performance.

title: From insulator to semiconductor: carbon nanotubes enhancing the electrochemical performance of Na 2.5 Fe 1.75 (SO 4 ) 3 cathode materials for sodium ion batteries
abstract: Sodium iron sulfate (Na 2+2 x Fe 2− x (SO 4 ) 3 , NFS) is a promising cathode material for sodium-ion batteries (SIBs) for large-scale energy storage systems due to its low cost and long life.

title: Designing high-performance catalysts for urea electrosynthesis: synergy between single atoms and BC 3 monolayers
abstract: By employing synergistic single-atom and support co-catalysis to activate N 2 and CO, a novel Hf/BC 3 catalyst for urea electrosynthesis was proposed with high efficiency and selectivity.

title: Exploring structural and electronic properties of transition metal carbides (T = Ti, V, Mo, & W) as efficient catalysts for overall water splitting with the DFT study
authors: K. S. S. V. Prasad Reddy
abstract: Earth abundant transition metal carbides, including molybdenum carbide (MoC) and tungsten carbide (WC), demonstrate exceptional electrocatalytic performance towards overall water splitting due to their unique electronic and structural properties.

title: Robust integration of p-MXene ink with a bacterial cellulose-reinforced polymer enables dynamic interaction of superior electromagnetic shielding and sensing
abstract: A strategy integrates p-MXene ink with robust WPU/BC composites, achieving superior electromagnetic shielding and durable sensing, enabling advanced bio-based materials for wearables and human-machine interfaces.

title: Ligand-protected and lowered-temperature hydrothermal synthesis of platinum encapsulated in TON zeolite for shape-selective hydrogenation of furfural to furfuryl alcohol
abstract: ZSM-22 encapsulating Pt (Pt@ZSM-22) is obtained by adopting a ligand-protected and lowered-temperature hydrothermal synthesis, followed by direct H 2 reduction. The Pt@ZSM-22 exhibits high furfuryl alcohol selectivity in furfural hydrogenation.

title: Multi-element co-penetration engineering in high-entropy alloys for efficient electromagnetic-wave absorption
abstract: Multi-element co-penetration engineering in high-entropy alloys for efficient electromagnetic-wave absorption.

title: Room-temperature high-efficiency electrocatalysis in two-dimensional ultrathin amorphous Ni–Mo–O nanosheets
abstract: ILE-synthesized ultrathin amorphous Ni–Mo–O nanosheets achieve exceptional mass activity for superior OER performances.

title: Versatile hydrogel towards coupling of energy harvesting and storage for self-powered round-the-clock sensing
abstract: In this work, a multifunctional, high-conductivity, flexible, anti-freezing and self-adhesive double-network hydrogel with a 3D interpenetrating framework was designed for energy harvesting and storage for self-powered round-the-clock sensing.

title: Ultra-thin anodes with controlled thickness modified using in situ Li 3 N for high-energy-density lithium metal batteries
abstract: An ultrathin 3D carbon-based anode with controllable thickness and enriched with Li 3 N artificial SEI guided the uniform deposition of Li + and inhibited the growth of Li dendrites, and it was used to realize LMBs with high energy density.

title: The critical role of potential-dependent O 2 adsorption in electrochemical oxygen reduction on goldene
abstract: This work reveals that O 2 adsorption on goldene is a potential-dependent electron transfer step. Using constant-potential DFT with hybrid and implicit solvation, we demonstrate its crucial role in governing ORR activity.

title: A band-aid-based MoSe 2 /Nb 2 C wearable supercapacitor for integrated ammonium-ion energy storage and real-time pressure monitoring
abstract: A wearable band-aid-based MoSe 2 /Nb 2 C hybrid supercapacitor with ammonium-ion storage powers glucose and pressure sensors, enabling flexible real-time health monitoring.

title: Machine learning-assisted optimization design for enhanced oxygen evolution reaction based on vanadium-doped nickel–cobalt layered double hydroxides
abstract: The increasing demand for sustainable energy has driven significant research into efficient water splitting, particularly the development of electrocatalysts for the oxygen evolution reaction (OER) which is limited by sluggish kinetics.

title: Nonfullerene acceptors based on octyl-substituted dithienonaphthobisthiadiazole as a fused π-extended electron-accepting central unit
abstract: The development of nonfullerene acceptors (NFAs) is critical for advancing organic solar cells (OSCs) and organic photo detectors (OPDs).

title: Corrosion-resistant and fluorescent thiol–epoxy ‘click’ coatings reinforced with hydrophobic N-doped carbon dots
abstract: A sustainable thiol–epoxy system reinforced with hydrophobic N-doped carbon dots, achieving superior mechanical strength, fluorescence, hydrophobicity, and anticorrosion performance through an efficient click chemistry approach.

title: High-density single-atom vanadium catalysts for efficient capture and bidirectional conversion of polysulfides in lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: The densely distributed vanadium active sites facilitate the adsorption and redox kinetics of LiPSs, resulting in good cycling stability when they are used as a barrier layer of the separator in Li–S batteries.

title: Tuning the coordination microenvironment of Co sites embedded in porous organic polymer for the hydroformylation of diisobutylene
abstract: By regulating the coordination microenvironment of Co species, Co@POPsPPh 3 &DVB promoted the formation of HCo(CO) 3 (P-frame) active species, which was the dominant active species for the hydroformylation of diisobutylene.

title: In situ formation of heterojunction of thiophene-based metal–organic frameworks with carbon dots for efficient overall water splitting and supercapacitor applications
abstract: The interface engineering-based 400N-CDs/FeNi-TDC electrocatalytic material is important for the construction of efficient self-powered OWS to produce renewable energy.

title: A Ru–MoC heterostructure electrocatalyst for efficient and stable hydrogen oxidation reaction in alkaline media
abstract: A Ru-based heterostructure catalyst with abundant Ru and MoC interfaces is elaborately designed as an efficient and stable hydrogen oxidation reaction (HOR) catalyst in alkaline media.

title: Ternary strategy for energy loss suppression toward efficient rigid and flexible organic solar cells
abstract: High-efficiency ternary organic solar cells based on PM6:L8-BO:ZY-4Cl, in both rigid and flexible configurations, were achieved by effectively suppressing voltage loss, optimizing active layer morphology, and reducing defect states.

title: A deep insight into the adsorption mechanisms of lithium-ion battery thermal runaway gases onto Cu-decorated hBN for gas sensing applications using DFT
abstract: Cu-decorated hBN as a sensing material for the lithium-ion battery thermal runaway gases.

title: An ultra-fast-charging and long-cycling titanium niobate/reduced graphene oxide anode via electrophoretic interfacial engineering
abstract: Titanium niobate (TiNb 2 O 7 , TNO) materials are emerging as high-performing anode candidates for fast charging Li-ion batteries.

title: Pinning and elongating of electric treeing induced by wrinkled nanosheets in polymer dielectrics towards significantly enhanced high-temperature energy storage performance
abstract: The polyetherimide composite with 0.2 wt% alumina nanosheets exhibits pinned, elongated breakdown paths due to wrinkles, achieving an ultrahigh energy density of 8.27 J cm –3 (>90% efficiency) at 150 °C, 420% that of pure polyetherimide film.

title: Formation of lattice vacancies and their effects on lithium-ion transport in LiBO 2 crystals: comparative ab initio studies
abstract: In this work, comparative ab initio studies showed that boron vacancies in LiBO 2 enhance its lithium-ion transport, suggesting that generation of boron vacancies ( e.g. , using neutron irradiation) could improve its ionic conductivity.

title: An ionic protector to proactively mitigate interfacial degradation induced by electrolyte decomposition in lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The presented work focuses on the mitigation strategy of high-voltage lithium-ion battery degradation based on ionic additive incorporation for the capacitive protection of positive and negative electrodes simultaneously.

title: Super-high dual-ion conductivity of BaO-doped GDC electrolytes for solid oxide fuel cells
abstract: Packed GDC grains conduct O 2− , and BaO-doping into GDC at the grain boundary forms the pathway for H + conduction.

title: Electronic asymmetry in Janus Ce/ThXY (X, Y = S and Se) promoting the photocatalytic oxygen evolution reaction in acidic environments
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) materials have emerged as promising candidates for photocatalytic water splitting due to their exceptional electronic and structural properties.

title: Molecular locking of defects via H-bonding/coordination dual-interaction enables efficient perovskite solar cells
abstract: A multifunctional additive, 4-methylsulfonylbenzoic acid (4-MeSBA), passivates Pb-site defects and stabilizes FA-based perovskites via coordination and hydrogen bonding, enabling inverted PSCs with a champion efficiency of 26.35%.

title: Enhanced charge carrier extraction and transport with interface modification for efficient tin-based perovskite solar cells
abstract: Interface modification improves charge carrier extraction in tin-based perovskite solar cells.

title: Deciphering the origin of the high capacities seen in magnesium borate polyanion cathodes
abstract: Despite promising electrochemistry we show that no Mg-ion deintercalation occurs in magnesium transition-metal borate cathodes. The observed capacity is instead attributed to reactions at the amorphous surface that are enhanced by ball milling.

title: LiF-doped sulfide solid electrolytes with a stabilized α-Li 3 PS 4 analog phase for all-solid-state Li metal batteries
abstract: All-solid-state Li metal batteries require solid electrolytes with both high reduction tolerance and high Li-ion conductivity.

title: An in-depth understanding of highly crystalline carbon nitride for near-infrared photocatalysis: from n → π* transition to midgap state engineering
abstract: This minireview systematically summarizes the research progress and mechanistic breakthroughs of highly crystalline carbon nitride in the field of near-infrared photocatalysis from n → π* electron transition to midgap state engineering.

title: Necklace-like CNT-Co 9 S 8 @C-CNT for high-performance zinc-ion batteries
abstract: A necklace-like structure consisting of cobalt sulfide encapsulated within carbon as “beads” and CNT as “strings” to form open-ended porous CNT-Co 9 S 8 @C-CNT composites is designed as high-performance AZIBs cathode.

title: Hydrazine oxidation-assisted electrocatalytic water splitting with Prussian blue analog-derived V-doped CoFe-layered double hydroxide nanosheets
abstract: Incorporation of high valent V 4+ /V 5+ in PBA-derived CoFe-LDH has been performed for improved hydrazine oxidation-assisted water splitting.

title: Ultrahigh peak power density of rechargeable Zn–air batteries using quadruple perovskite as air-cathode electrocatalysts
abstract: A quadruple perovskite synthesized at ambient pressure as an air-cathode electrocatalyst exhibits an ultrahigh peak power density in a Zn–air battery owing to the energetically favourable B–A′ bridging active sites.

title: 2M phase stability of WSe 2 –MoSe 2 alloy nanosheets via a colloidal reaction and their Se-rich model calculations
abstract: Composition-tuned WSe 2 –MoSe 2 alloy nanosheets were synthesized via a colloidal reaction. They show the decreased stability of 2M phase at higher Mo composition, which was supported by first-principles calculations using metal vacancy models.

title: Synergistic Fe and nitrogen-doped carbon nanotubes boost bifunctional water splitting of wood-derived Co electrocatalysts at high current density
abstract: Fe and nitrogen-doped carbon nanotubes boost wood-derived Co catalysts to achieve low overpotentials (OER: 411/440; HER: 305/368 mV) at 500/800 mA cm −2 in water splitting, highlighting wood's potential for high-value electrocatalytic applications.

title: Molecular engineering of polyamide-imide copolymers for high-temperature energy storage
abstract: A series of polyamide-imide (PAI) copolymers are designed through molecular engineering. The PAI-C film exhibits an outstanding discharged energy density ( U d ) of 5.9 J cm −3 with a charge–discharge efficiency of 90.1% at 150 °C.

title: Green and efficient graphitization of biomass waste empowered by molten salt electrolysis: mechanistic exploration and energy storage applications dual-driven by experiments and simulations
abstract: Molten salt electrolysis graphitization efficiently converts wood biochar (WB) into graphitic materials with petal-like nanosheets for green and high-value applications.

title: Robust superhydrophobic V 2 O 5 /CoFe 2 O 4 @SiO 2 /PDMS composite membranes for membrane distillation
abstract: A robust dual-layer (superhydrophobic–hydrophilic) composite membrane architecture with tailored surface chemistry for high vapor flux, salt rejection and fouling resistance in membrane distillation.

title: SrVO 3 -modified silver nanowire transparent conductive films with enhanced photoelectric performance and optical stability
abstract: Herein, we incorporate SrVO 3 nanofibers (0.010 wt%) into the over-coating layer of AgNW TCFs, simultaneously enhancing conductivity and UV stability (>500 h UV aging) while maintaining transmittance.

title: A biomimetic pyrimidine derivative for stabilizing a zinc anode characterized by zinc ion deposition kinetics study and in situ optical imaging
abstract: Using the ecofriendly and low-cost pyrimidine derivative pyrimidine-2-carboxylic acid (PCA) as an electrolyte additive in an ZnSO 4 electrolyte for stabilizing the zinc anode.

title: Role of acid modulators in improving electrochemical hydrogen storage in the MIL-53(Cr) framework on the Ag/f-MWCNT substrate via defect engineering
abstract: Hydrogen spillover process in the MIL-53(Cr) HNO 3 /Ag/f-MWCNTs nanocomposite, which is a supported catalyst system, generally involves the migration of active hydrogen on the surface and pores of the metal-supported catalysts.

title: Ultra-high ICE and long cycle stability sodium-ion battery anode: hybrid nanostructure of dominant pyridine N-doped sisal fiber derived carbon-MoS 2
abstract: A composite of N-doped tubular sisal fiber carbon and MoS 2 nanosheets (MoS 2 /N-TSFC) for sodium-ion battery anode material has been obtained, which has excellent electrochemical performance with an ultra-high ICE (93%) and long cycle stability.

title: Microphase-optimized sulfonated poly(biphenyl-piperidine-alkylene)- co -poly(biphenyl-trifluoroacetophenylene) proton exchange membranes with enhanced proton conductivity and oxidative stability for water electrolysis
abstract: Ether-free sulfonated poly(biphenyl-piperidine-hexylene)- co -poly(biphenyl-trifluoroacetophenone) membranes with flexible alkyl spacers show distinct phase separation, yielding high proton conductivity and oxidative stability for water electrolysis.

title: A multifunctional beryllium silicide monolayer with negative Poisson's ratio and high-capacity sodium-ion storage
abstract: Based on first-principles calculations, the α-Be 2 Si monolayer is proposed as a promising multifunctional 2D material with anisotropic negative Poisson's ratio.

title: Output power density enhancement of an intermittently contacted metal–semiconductor junction with a water interlayer
abstract: The output power density of an intermittently contacted gold–silicon (Au–Si) junction can be increased by about 3 orders of magnitude after introduction of a water (H 2 O) interlayer between the Au and Si friction layers.

title: Competitive adsorption strategy for adsorbed intermediates boosting alkaline hydrogen evolution
abstract: A competitive adsorption strategy was proposed to regulate the strong interaction between the active sites and hydroxyl intermediates by introducing highly oxyphilic species into RuSe 2 , thus enabling highly efficient alkaline HER performance.

title: Anion leaching induced amorphous Cu/CuO x on N-doped carbon for efficient electrochemical nitrate reduction to ammonia
abstract: An anion leaching-mediated surface evolution mechanism was proposed to generate a Cu/CuO x -N/CB catalyst from CuCl-N/CB for efficient N-cycling, achieving a high NH 3 yield rate of 68.11 mg h −1 and faradaic efficiency of 96.8%.

title: Regulation of Anderson localization for enhancing thermoelectric properties in Mn doped AgSbSe 2 compounds
abstract: Mn doping shifts the mobility edge ( E C ) away from the Fermi level ( E F ), weakening the electron localization effect and significantly improving electrical conductivity. Ultimately, AgSb 0.98 Mn 0.02 Se 2 achieves a zT value of 1.1 at 690 K.

title: Surface amorphized in situ RuO-NiFeOOH/Au islands for electrocatalytic oxygen evolution reaction
