abstract: A highly active OER catalyst, Ru-NiFeP/Au with optimized Ru loading, showed low overpotential ( η 10 = 223 mV). In situ SERS revealed FeOOH and NiOOH as key promoters under low and high biases, respectively.

title: Correction: Efficient ethane production via SnCl 4 Lewis acid-enhanced CO 2 electroreduction in a flow cell electrolyser
abstract: Correction for ‘Efficient ethane production via SnCl 4 Lewis acid-enhanced CO 2 electroreduction in a flow cell electrolyser’ by Sankeerthana Bellamkonda et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA00176E.

title: Graphitic nitrogen induced identical fluorescent emission of carbon dots for scalable anti-counterfeiting applications
abstract: The introduction of graphitic N renders carbon dots with identical fluorescence. This approach mitigates the pronounced reaction-condition variant problem, thereby facilitating the large-scale, precise synthesis of monochromatic carbon dots.

title: Soft template-assisted design and synthesis of anisotropic 2D–3D CuInS 2 with a controlled morphology and band gap: exploring photothermal interfacial water evaporation
abstract: Synthesizing I–III–VI 2 ternary semiconductor chalcogenide nanostructures with precise control over their shape and bandgap is a major focus of current research.

title: Polythiophene-derived nickel cobaltite nanocomposites showing excellent photo-switching and photo-assisted enhanced supercapacitor properties
abstract: A solid-state photo-assisted supercapacitor from a polythiophene derivative and spinel NiCo 2 O 4 exhibits a specific capacitance 106 F g −1 , an increased rate capability of 10.6% and cyclic stability of 5% under UV-light compared to dark conditions.

title: A gas diffusion nanocomposite layer with a hydrophilic–superhydrophobic columnar interface for enhanced water and gas transport
abstract: LPM-GDNL with a hydrophilic–superhydrophobic columnar interface enables separate water and gas transport. This structure improves current and power densities by up to 58% compared to commercial GDLs.

title: Mixed anion closo -carbaborates as Na-superionic conductors
abstract: New mixed anion closo -carbaborates of sodium salts display superionic conduction under ambient conditions. We demonstrate their potential in an all-solid-state battery with the configuration Na|Na[(CB 8 H 9 ) 0.04 (CB 9 H 10 ) 0.96 ]|NaTi 2 (PO 4 ) 3 .

title: One-step preparation of an anti-freezing and flame-retardant eutectic gel electrolyte for assembling a flexible and high-performance zinc-ion battery
abstract: Flexible zinc-ion batteries have attracted considerable attention owing to their intrinsic safety, high specific capacity, low cost, and environmental benefits.

title: Engineering CoP/FeP 4 heterostructures on epitaxially grown ultra-thin two-dimensional MOFs via competitive coordination for high-durability electrochemical supercapacitors
abstract: An ultra-thin Co-MOF based heterostructure with well-defined geometries is constructed successfully. This heterostructure effectively regulates interfacial charge states, accelerates charge transfer, and enhances the electrochemical performance.

title: Synergistic electrochemical properties of conductive additives with 1D–2D carbon networks
abstract: The demand for high energy density lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) has increased due to the miniaturization of portable electronic devices.

title: Anchoring Co–Fe alloy nano-grains on carbon fibers by an in situ alloying strategy to boost the catalytic performance for rapid oxidative degradation of emerging contaminants
abstract: Advanced oxidation is regarded as the most promising pathway for the purification of hard-to-degrade contaminants.

title: Ultra-low content-induced intercalation anomaly of graphite anode enables superior capacity at sub-zero temperatures
abstract: In this work, graphite intercalation anomaly is observed under low content in the electrode which enhances graphite's specific capacity and extends its operating range to sub-zero temperature.

title: Enhanced cycling performance of bilayered vanadium oxide cathode in Li-ion batteries via dual metal-ion preintercalation
abstract: The simultaneous chemical preintercalation of Li + and Mg 2+ ions into the interlayer region of bilayered vanadium oxide synergistically enhances its cycling performance in Li-ion cells by improving Li + diffusion and increasing structural stability.

title: An automatic segmentation and quantification method for austenite and ferrite phases in duplex stainless steel based on deep learning
abstract: Deep learning framework for austenitic ferrite segmentation using electron microscope images. Preprocessing and data enhancement enable accurate grain detection in Fe–C–Mn–Al alloys with a novel quantification method.

title: Low-temperature fuel cells using proton-conducting silicate solid electrolyte
abstract: A proton-conducting silicate solid electrolyte exhibiting excellent hydrogen barrier properties and mechanical flexibility was developed, and a fuel cell incorporating this electrolyte demonstrated a peak power density of 250 mW cm −2 at 90 °C.

title: MXene-based zinc-ion batteries: synthesis, applications, and strategies for performance optimization
abstract: Aqueous zinc-ion batteries (ZIBs) have garnered significant research attention due to their distinct advantages, such as low cost, safety, and environmental compatibility.

title: Photonic crystal-integrated thermo-responsive smart windows with multicolor and enhanced NIR shielding
abstract: Smart windows have attracted significant attention due to their potential for energy conservation.

title: Ammonium-preintercalated layered manganese oxide with single-phase intercalation chemistry and enhanced two-electron reaction in aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: NH 4 –MnO 2 not only exhibits single-phase charge storage behavior with minimal lattice changes for intercalation chemistry but also promotes the Mn 4+ /Mn 2+ redox reaction for the dissolution/redeposition mechanism.

title: Enhancing the thermoelectric performance of n-type polycrystalline SnSe with lead-free perovskite Cs 2 TiCl 6
abstract: Lead-free Cs 2 TiCl 6 is introduced as a novel dopant for n-type SnSe, which simultaneously improves electronic properties and introduces multi-scale defects, achieving a high ZT of ∼1.2 at 823 K and providing a sustainable doping strategy.

title: Microwave catalytic degradation of tetracycline using Van modified Fe-MOF: regulation of defect engineering and its performance under microwave irradiation
abstract: FBV 0.4 features Fe (CUS), OVs and multi-level pore structures. Through dual activation and enhanced microwave absorption, it achieves efficient microwave catalysis.

title: Asymmetric structure endows thermal radiation and heat conduction in graphene films for enhancing dual-mode heat dissipation
abstract: An asymmetric-structure graphene material was designed for both high thermal conductivity and enhanced radiation.

title: Ultralow voltage operation and microwatt power consumption of MXene-based pressure sensors with excellent sensing performance
abstract: A three-dimensional contact interface MXene-based pressure sensor displays an ultralow working voltage of 0.01 V and can be operated successfully by being powered by a thermoelectric generator.

title: Rapid synthesis of Fe doped Ni x P/reduced graphene oxide for enhanced oxygen evolution reaction activity in alkaline freshwater and seawater
abstract: Fe-Ni x P/RGO-M, which follows the lattice oxygen mechanism, was developed through a facile microwave-assisted strategy. This electrocatalyst requires low overpotentials of 299 and 349 mV at 1000 mA cm −2 in alkaline freshwater/seawater, respectively.

title: Room-temperature spin-coatable polyoxometalate composites for high-contrast, large-area electrochromic capacitive films
abstract: A room temperature spin-coating method for high performance polyoxometalate (POM)-based electrochromic (EC) capacitive films has been developed with the assistance of MOFs, enabling the fabrication of flexible POM-based EC films for the first time.

title: In situ synthesis of dual-functional photocatalyst x Bi 0 /BiVO 4 for the selective oxidation of cinnamaldehyde to benzaldehyde under visible light using oxygen
abstract: By in situ reduction to deposit Bi onto BiVO 4 , x Bi 0 /BiVO 4 not only retains the photocatalytic properties but also exhibits photosensitizer characteristics; furthermore, the adsorption of reactants and other photocatalytic properties are enhanced.

title: 3D-printed electromagnetic microwave absorption structures: a comprehensive review
abstract: The work focuses on the mechanisms of electromagnetic microwave (EMW) structures and the applications of various 3D printing technologies in the fabrication of EMW structures.

title: Fabrication of thin and dense GDC buffer layers: enhancing cathode performance in SOFCs
abstract: A low-temperature approach using ∼3 nm GDC nanoparticles to create ultra-thin (<400 nm) dense buffer layers that block Sr and Ba interdiffusion, enabling MS-SOFCs to achieve ∼1 W cm −2 at 700 °C with excellent 200 h stability.

title: Assembling graphene quantum dots on aminophenol-formaldehyde resin towards efficient artificial photocatalytic hydrogen peroxide synthesis
abstract: Assembling GQDs on APF resin could improve separation and transfer efficiency of photogenerated electrons for H 2 O 2 synthesis.

title: A highly connected metal–organic framework with stretched inorganic units for propylene/ethylene separation
abstract: A highly-connected metal–organic framework, composed of “stretched” [Zn 8 SiO 4 ]-based nodes and linkers with mixed binding groups, shows remarkable C 3 H 6 adsorption capacity and effectively separates C 3 H 6 /C 2 H 4 under various conditions.

title: High performing and sustainable hard carbons for Na-ion batteries through acid-catalysed hydrothermal carbonisation of vine shoots
abstract: Acid-assisted hydrothermal carbonisation of vine shoots, followed by annealing, yields tailored hard carbon anodes for Na-ion batteries. Diglyme-based electrolytes improved capacity, efficiency, and stability, showcasing sustainable energy solutions.

title: Hierarchical B and Cu co-doped Co 3 O 4 nanoarrays for nitrate reduction coupled with polyethylene terephthalate plastic upcycling
abstract: B, Cu–Co 3 O 4 /NF ultrathin nanosheet arrays demonstrate superior NO 3 RR-PETHOR activity, enabling simultaneous NH 3 and formate production in a coupled system.

title: Exploring spinel oxides from bimetallic to high-entropy with a focus on the structure and performance in the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: A schematic representation of the transformation from bimetallic to high entropy spinel oxides for the oxygen evolution reaction.

title: An Na 3 VMn 0.5 Ti 0.5 (PO 4 ) 3 NASICON cathode with multielectron reactions for sustainable energy storage
abstract: Our study develops an Na 3 VMn 0.5 Ti 0.5 (PO 4 ) 3 as a low-cost and eco-friendly alternative to V-rich materials for rechargeable hybrid aqueous batteries, demonstrating stable multielectron reaction mechanisms and promising cycling performance.

title: Oxidation behaviors of SiOCN coatings with tunable carbon content on stainless steel at 800 °C in Ar, Ar + H 2 O, and air atmospheres
abstract: The carbon content in SiOCN coatings governs the steel oxidation behavior at 800 °C. Carbon-rich coatings form Fe-rich oxides and gaps at the coating/alloy interface, carbon-free ones offer protection, and intermediate ones show partial degradation.

title: Correction: Improvement of photocatalytic antibacterial action of Mn, S v -co-doped ZnIn 2 S 4 prepared by a novel O v -rich α-MnO 2 decomposition approach
abstract: Correction for ‘Improvement of photocatalytic antibacterial action of Mn, S v -co-doped ZnIn 2 S 4 prepared by a novel O v -rich α-MnO 2 decomposition approach’ by Hui Zhang et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, 13 , 17646–17657, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA01357G.

title: Shape anisotropy in iron oxide nanocrystals: ligand field and photocatalytic efficiency under tropical sunlight
abstract: Shape anisotropy in iron oxide, dye catalyst interface, and interfacial charge transfer in sunlight driven photocatalysis.

title: A transparent, flame-retardant, and UV-resistant polycarbonate panel with high down-conversion efficiency opens up possibilities for lightweight solar cell encapsulation panels
abstract: A luminescent down-shifting, flame-retardant, and UV-resistant PC-based photovoltaic encapsulation material is developed.

title: Stabilization of zinc metal electrodes by solvation structure modulation of Zn(NTf 2 ) 2 electrolyte additives
abstract: The addition of Zn(NTf 2 ) 2 changed the solvation structure of Zn 2+ , which controlled the distribution of Zn 2+ and inhibited dendrite growth, which can extend the service life of the battery and improve its electrochemical performance.

title: Electrochemically directed synthesis of TiC nanotube arrays for aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Accurate synthesis of TiC with various morphologies and its application as MnO 2 support material.

title: A unified and consistent electrical double layer model for the treatment of core and space charge layers in solid electrolytes
abstract: By incorporating properties of defects in the core and space charge layers, we calculate the potential and defect concentration profiles near the interface, which directly affect interfacial capacitance and conductivity of solid electrolytes.

title: Electrocatalytic nitrate reduction to ammonia using a bifunctional Ni–Mo alloy catalyst
abstract: Ni–Mo alloys are efficient for nitrate reduction to NH 3 , owing to their bifunctional roles of water dissociation and N-species conversion.

title: Compromise and synergy in thermoelectric GeTe–CuSbS 2 alloys
abstract: Herein, we propose and validate an innovative viewpoint that by manipulating the compromise and synergy in thermoelectric GeTe–CuSbS 2 alloys, it is possible to reach a lattice thermal conductivity of 0.3 W m −1 K −1 lower than the amorphous limit.

title: Ti/Cr dual-doped NASICON-type Na 3 V 2 (PO 4 ) 3 cathodes for multi-electron high-energy sodium storage
abstract: A Ti/Cr dual-doped NVP cathode uses Ti/V redox for high capacity and rate stability. Doping lowers Na + migration barriers for faster kinetics. A full cell shows practical SIB viability.

title: Localized asymmetric dipole electrolyte engineering for high-performance lithium-metal batteries
abstract: MDFSA modulates local asymmetric dipoles through electron-withdrawing fluorine and sulfonyl groups, enhancing its solvation ability and promoting the entry of anions into the solvation shell.

title: Stable functional electrode–electrolyte interface formed by multivalent cation additives in lithium-metal anode batteries
abstract: This work introduces the use of multivalent cations ( e.g. Ca 2+ , Ba 2+ , La 3+ and Ce 3+ ) as electrolyte additives in lithium-metal anode batteries with a focus on solvation structure modification, SEI formation and lithium growth.

title: A comparative electrochemical study of 2H/1T phases of MoS 2 and designing 1T-MoS 2 @Cu 2 S for high-performance supercapacitors
abstract: The composite 1T MoS 2 @Cu 2 S stabilises the 1T phase of MoS 2 by increasing the interlayer spacing. Its snowflake morphology enhances the electrochemical performance surpassing the limitations of MoS 2 and shows various real world application.

title: A high power metal-supported protonic ceramic fuel cell using increased proton conductivity in the cathode functional layer of La 1− x Sr x ScO 3 (LSSc, x = 0.1–0.25)
abstract: Metal-supported protonic ceramic fuel cells (PCFCs) with LSSc layers achieve enhanced proton conductivity and improved power density.

title: Structural evolution and cycling performance enhancement of LiFe 0.5 Mn 0.5 PO 4 nanofibers as cathode materials for aqueous lithium-ion batteries
abstract: LiFe 0.5 Mn 0.5 PO 4 is a promising cathode material for aqueous Li-ion batteries, yet it suffers severe capacity fading while cycling due to Mn dissolution. By using concentrated aqueous electrolyte, the cycle performance is greatly enhanced.

title: Hydrogen adsorption energy trends in Mo/WXY (X, Y = S, Se, Te) regular and Janus TMD monolayers: a first-principles and machine learning study
abstract: Machine learning model to predict the atomic hydrogen adsorption energy in regular MX 2 and Janus MXY TMDs (M = Mo/W, X, Y = S/Se/Te).

title: Hollow ZSM-11 zeolite synthesized via slow aluminum release: enhanced acid accessibility and stability in methanol-to-olefins
abstract: A protocol for synthesis of hollow zeolite by controlling the timing of aluminium incorporation.

title: Probing redoxable organic molecules in the transient near-electrode accumulated regime unveils insidious degradation
abstract: A substrate generation/tip collection (SG/TC) SECM technique is introduced to sensitively evaluate slow chemical degradation kinetics of redox-active species in organic flow battery electrolytes with validation by RFB performance.

title: One-pot synthesis of binary quinoxaline-linked covalent organic frameworks
abstract: This work provides a facile one-pot strategy to prepare stable binary COFs linked by quinoxaline and imine or amine linkages, and the in situ conversion of imine bonds to amino linkage can be achieved by adding reducing agents.

title: Multicomponentization of a super-Na ionic conductor chloride NaTaCl 6 , enhancing ionic conductivity and electronic resistivity
abstract: Multicomponentization of chloride NaTaCl 6 increases the ionic conductivity and electronic resistivity, opening the possibility for all-solid-state Na + batteries with rapid charge–discharge capabilities and enhanced energy-storage retention.

title: Methylated naphthalene additives with various melting and boiling points enable a win–win scenario of optimizing both cost and efficiency of polymer solar cells
abstract: Readily available 2-methylnaphthalene (2-MN) was demonstrated as a volatile solid additive to achieve a good win–win situation of optimizing among efficiency, cost and environmental issues.

title: Boron site-dependent electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction at the boron-doped diamond–H 2 O interface
abstract: The dynamic regeneration mechanism of B-H 2 O active sites facilitates the electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction reaction (eCO 2 RR) on boron-doped diamond (BDD) electrodes.

title: Exciton dynamics in planar dumbbell-shaped electron-donor molecules for organic optoelectronics
abstract: We study exciton transport in bridged-columnar assemblies of dumbbell-shaped molecules by exciton–exciton annihilation and transient photoluminescence microscopy. We find that crystallization yields a twofold increase in exciton diffusion length.

title: Boosting biological hydrogen production by integrating functionally symbiotic bacteria/algae with engineered nitrogen-doped carbon dots
abstract: The production of biohydrogen (bioH 2 ) faces significant challenges, including non-spontaneous generation in heterotrophic microorganisms, inhibition of hydrogenases and inefficient photoelectron transfer in photoautotrophic microorganisms.

title: Tailored sustainable synthesis of Mo x C by electrochemical etching of a Mo 2 TiAlC 2 MAX phase toward electrochemical applications
abstract: A novel electrochemical etching strategy was developed to convert Mo 2 TiAlC 2 MAX into Mo x C nanosheets with abundant metal vacancies, exhibiting outstanding HER activity and promising supercapacitor performance.

title: S-doped Au/Pd/Pt aerogels becoming superior HER catalysts by constructing an atomic-scale Pd diffuse interface
abstract: In this work, an S 6.5 -doped Au 77.6 /Pd 0.8 /Pt 15.1 aerogel with a structure composed of an Au core, an atomic-scale Pd diffuse interface and a Pt shell was successfully prepared by adopting an unconventionally low Pd-to-Pt precursor molar ratio.

title: Nickel cobalt phosphate/phosphide as a promising electrode material for extrinsic supercapacitors: machine learning analysis
abstract: This review outlines the recent advancements in nickel cobalt phosphate- and phosphide-based supercapacitors by providing their structure–property–performance relationships and employing advanced machine learning as a predictive tool.

title: Three-dimensional Zn-MOF-derived nitrogen-doped porous carbon: unlocking high capacitance and long-term stability in supercapacitors
abstract: This work reports activated porous N-doped carbon (APNC) using an isonicotinic acid-based Zn-MOF. The APNC exhibits a high specific capacitance of 360 F g −1 at 1 A g −1 , and a symmetric device achieves 18.2 Wh kg −1 at 400 W kg −1 .

title: Single Zn atoms anchored in mesoporous N-doped carbon rods derived from metal–organic frameworks for enhanced electrocatalytic oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: Nitrogen–carbon (N–C) materials have been recently explored to activate Fenton-reaction-inactive Zn single atoms as active sites for electrocatalytic oxygen reduction reaction (ORR).

title: Unveiling the crucial morphological effect of non-conducting polymer binders on inorganic-rich hybrid electrolytes
abstract: Comparison of the non-conducting polymer binders for hybrids based on Li 3 InCl 6 . Ordered nanostructured SEBS block copolymer provides more tortuous pathways for Li ions in the inorganic electrolyte (Li 3 InCl 6 ) than disordered PIB homopolymer.

title: High-throughput computation and machine learning screening of van der Waals heterostructures for Z-scheme photocatalysis
abstract: We identify 62 Z-scheme heterostructures with excellent photocatalytic performance from 11 935 hetero-bilayer structures using high-throughput calculations and machine learning.

title: Improving nitrate-to-ammonia conversion efficiency on electrodeposited nickel phosphide via surface δ-FeOOH modification
abstract: Integrating δ-FeOOH with Ni 3 P through van der Waals forces enhances the selectivity and stability of nitrate-to-ammonia conversion. Improved stabilization of reaction intermediates leads to better electrochemical performance.

title: Elucidating the hydration state and driving force for LCST-type phase transitions in thermoresponsive ionic liquids and thermoresponsive ionic liquid-derived polyelectrolyte gels
abstract: Upon heating, the LCST-type phase transition is induced in both the monomeric and gel states of [N4][C6] by the dehydration of water molecules in the secondary hydration shell near the ammonium cation.

title: Immobilize simulated Am–Np–RE–Pu product waste from trialkyl phosphine oxide process in natural granite-based aluminum silicate glass or glass–ceramic
abstract: The effect of simulated nuclear waste content on the phase of waste forms.

title: Current collector engineering for advanced anode-free alkali metal batteries with liquid electrolyte
abstract: This review systematically summarizes current collector engineering strategies for anode-free alkali metal batteries, including materials optimization, crystal orientation regulation, porous structure design, and surface modification strategies.

title: Lithium alloy anodes for all-solid-state lithium batteries: from failure mechanism to performance-oriented design
abstract: This review addresses the challenges faced by all-solid-state lithium metal batteries (ASSLMBs), analyzes the properties of lithium alloys, summarizes the recent applications of lithium alloy anodes in ASSLMBs, and presents outlooks.

title: Atomically dispersed Fe/Zn synergy in sulfur-modified nitrogen-doped carbon for boosting oxygen reduction activity
abstract: The introduction of S enables the optimization of Fe single-atom sites to promote the ORR process. The developed electrocatalyst demonstrates exceptional performance in ZABs, highlighting its great potential for energy conversion applications.

title: UV-modified polydopamine zincophilic protective layer for durable and alkaline-stable zinc anodes in zinc–air batteries
abstract: This work demonstrates the design of a UV-treated polydopamine protective layer modified Zn anode, along with an anion-conducting polymer electrolyte membrane, for attaining an alkaline stable and long cycle life rechargeable Zn–air battery.

title: Bionic Boston ivy adhesive foot – photothermal flexible phase change hydrogel
abstract: Inspired by the adhesion and peeling mechanism of Boston ivy, a novel dual-layer photothermal cPCM is proposed, incorporating LIG as the photothermal conversion layer and a hydrated salt-based phase change hydrogel as the energy storage matrix.

title: Recent advances in surface modification for enhanced electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: We reviewed the recent advances in surface modification strategies of catalysts for improving CO 2 RR performance.

title: Generalizable classification of crystal structure error types using graph attention networks
abstract: Graph neural network models classifying errors ( e.g. , omitted hydrogens, charge imbalance, disorder) in crystal structure databases are developed which attain high accuracy and generalizable performance across various domains of chemistry.

title: Emerging nanomaterials for the detection of per- and poly-fluorinated substances
abstract: This review explores the advancements in PFAS nano-sensor technology, detailing their molecular interactions, cutting-edge materials, and challenges, with an aim to improve the detection of PFAS for human and environmental safety.

title: Synergistic electrolyte design for dual-interface stability in sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Study boosts Na-ion battery sustainability & fast-charging with 2-FP/FEC, solving EEI stability.

title: Mechanistic understanding of the antimony–bismuth alloy promoted electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction to formate
abstract: Introducing bismuth (Bi) into antimony (Sb) to form Sb–Bi alloys offers a promising way to enhance the electrocatalytic activity of Sb for CO 2 reduction to formate.

title: Promoting electrochemical CO 2 reduction by pyridinium in the secondary coordination sphere of copper-sites anchored on a covalent organic framework
abstract: A bipyridine-containing covalent organic framework was prepared by the Zincke reaction, and modified with copper ions for highly efficient electrochemical CO 2 reduction.

title: Synergistically coupled nickel oxide nanoparticles with single-atom nickel catalysts for high-performance wide-temperature adaptable quasi-solid-state zinc–air batteries
abstract: NiO/Ni-SACs/mNC1000 was synthesized for electrochemical water oxidation and demonstrated for quasi-solid-state rechargeable Zn–air batteries via modifying a PAM hydrogel.

title: Engineering a surface functionalized Pt@SnS 2 /Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene sensor with humidity tolerance and high sensitivity at room temperature for NH 3 detection
abstract: The design of hierarchical heterostructures that can detect volatile organic compounds (VOCs) at room temperature with good selectivity, sensitivity, and humidity tolerance is an intriguing and practically useful area of research.

title: Tri-coordinated PdNP architecture for simultaneous capture, activation, and catalytic conversion of dilute CO 2 via multisite synergy
abstract: Ultrafine PdNPs with AcGlu/NHO/π-bond coordination enable simultaneous capture, activation, and conversion of dilute CO 2 , propargylic amine, and aryl iodide with 600 h −1 TOF over 7 cycles. Advances CO 2 utilization and nanomaterial design.

title: Photon-induced isomerization enables high-performance polymer solar cells
abstract: The introduction of isomeric components into the active layers demonstrates effective mitigation of morphological defects arising from thermodynamic immiscibility in all-polymer solar cells (all-PSCs).

title: Ultrasound-assisted directional freezing enables high-performance hydrogel evaporators with tunable microchannels for solar desalination
abstract: Improving interfacial evaporation rates and optimizing energy utilization efficiency remain critical challenges in solar-driven interfacial evaporation (SDIE) systems.

title: Advancing Li 3 VO 4 with high coulombic efficiency and super lithium storage by interface in situ regulation
abstract: The highly dispersed Ni nanoparticles can catalyze the decomposition of Li 2 O, leading to a thinner and denser SEI layer. This results in a higher initial Coulombic efficiency and improved cycling stability for LVO/Ni/C.

title: Thermostable nanofiltration membranes via a co-monomer strategy for high-temperature separation
abstract: Thermostable thin-film composite nanofiltration membranes are fabricated via interfacial polymerization by integrating PIP with MPD as the aqueous monomers, which show high MgSO 4 rejection at 85 °C.

title: Impact of heterogeneous construction on the low- and high-index facets of Cu 2 O on the catalytic performance and reconstruction in the electrochemical CO 2 reduction reaction
abstract: In this work, a series of Cu 2 O@Cu 2 S catalysts with different exposed crystal facets is successfully synthesized to afford CO productivity at low potential via the electrocatalytic CO 2 RR.

title: Data-driven design and green preparation of bio-based flame retardant polyamide composites
abstract: This work presents a bio-based flame retardant for biocomposites, using data-driven experimentation and optimization to boost tensile strength by 22.3% and reduce heat release rate by 73.7% vs. the neat polymer.

title: Network capture effect-driven enhanced activation of peroxymonosulfate by iron-doped carbon quantum dots derived from ferrous gluconate for efficient ciprofloxacin degradation: DFT calculations and mechanism analysis
abstract: A peroxymonosulfate (PMS) system activated by network capture effect-enhanced iron-doped carbon quantum dots (Fe-CQDs) was constructed. The Fe-CQDs/PMS system showed high degradation efficiency for ciprofloxacin and multiple antibiotics.

title: Better together: integrating adhesion and ion conductivity in composite binders for high-performance silicon anodes
abstract: The composite binder, combining poly(acrylic acid) and a tailored terpolymer (PSUOH), enhances Si anode performance by improving adhesion, stability and ionic conductivity, achieving multifunctionality without trade-offs seen in conventional binders.

title: Engineering metal–organic framework towards suppressed leakage current in polymer nanocomposites
abstract: This work introduces ZIF-11 as a filler for PEI, showcasing its dual functionality in suppressing both ionic and electronic conduction.

title: Zn 0.5 Cd 0.5 S photocatalysts with loaded Cu 2+ and Ni 2+ dual active sites for promoted syngas production
abstract: Cu 2+ and Ni 2+ are co-introduced into Zn 0.5 Cd 0.5 S nanoparticles to selectively promote the evolution of CO and H 2 , respectively, thereby synergistically enhancing the photocatalytic activity for syngas production.

title: Combining LiBF 4 with a donor solvent to optimize Li + -solvation and reinforce interphases for lithium ion capacitors
abstract: A thermally stable electrolyte comprising LiBF 4 , high-donor tetrahydropyran (THP), and carbonates modulates Li + -solvation and LiF-growth for speedy ion-transport and robust interphases.

title: Surface defect engineering and MOF derivatives regulate the electron transport pathway of polymeric carbon nitride for an efficient photocatalytic 2e − oxygen reduction reaction to form H 2 O 2
abstract: K-CZ-2 achieved an H 2 O 2 yield of 7.8 mmol g −1 h −1 , showing 1.5- and 19.6-fold improvements compared to those of CN and g-C 3 N 4 , respectively, along with an AQY of 3.08% at 420 nm and an SCC efficiency of 0.63%.

title: Unified anodes with different nickel–iron-based phases for durable AEM electrolyzers achieving high performance of 8000 mA cm −2 at 2.05 V
abstract: Identifying the optimal nickel–iron (NiFe) phase for the oxygen evolution reaction (OER) and demonstrating its application in high-performance anion-exchange membrane water electrolyzer (AEMWE) single-cell and stack testing.

title: Patterned electrodes for advanced energy conversion and storage: from precision engineering to practical applications
abstract: This review showcases patterned electrodes as an emerging solution that replaces random composites with precisely designed micro-/nanostructures in batteries and fuel cells, thereby accelerating the development of high-performance energy devices.

title: Rigid-flexible dynamic polymers based on borate bonds
abstract: A rigid-flexible dynamic polymer that exhibits distinct glass-like rigid behavior under normal conditions and flexible-solid characteristics at very low strain rates is reported.

title: Bimetallic Mg/Zn-based zeolitic imidazolate frameworks for zinc–air batteries: disclosing the role of defective imidazole-Mg sites in the electrocatalytic performance
abstract: Magnesium doping in ZIF-8 introduces dicoordinated imidazole-Mg species, which create surface defects that enhance O 2 adsorption and improve ORR electrocatalytic performance.

title: Heterogeneous diffusion and remnant hardening with excellent electromechanical compatibility in alkaline niobate composites
abstract: A novel approach involving heterogeneous diffusion and remnant hardening techniques is presented for KNN-based composites, which overcomes the limitations of traditional precipitation hardening, achieving a remarkable d 33 of 370 pC/N and Q m of 205.

title: Multi-dentate organic additives for simultaneous Zn 2+ capturing and interfacial engineering toward stable aqueous zinc metal batteries
abstract: Aqueous zinc-ion batteries (AZIBs) have recently attracted widespread interest due to their intrinsically high specific capacity and inherent safety.

title: Optimizing gate-opening pressure to boost propylene/propane separation by metal doping in isostructural MOFs
abstract: By means of an easily operated metal doping strategy, an optimized trimetallic-doped Co0.377Mg0.597Mn0.026-odp-a showed sieving separation for the C 3 H 6 /C 3 H 8 mixture, achieving an exceptional selectivity of 84.4 and polymer-grade C 3 H 6 at 298 K.

title: Alkali triel chalcogenide nanocrystals: a molecular reactivity approach to ternary phase selectivity
abstract: A molecular reactivity approach using dichalcogenide precursors enables the phase-selective synthesis of ternary, lithium- and sodium-based triel chalcogenide nanocrystals (LiTrCh 2 , NaTrCh 2 , NaTr 3 Ch 5 ; Tr = Ga, In; Ch = S, Se, Te).

title: A simple surface engineering approach to enhance the Schottky barrier of polymer dielectrics for superior energy storage performance
abstract: A scalable dip-coating method enables the deposition of a broadband SiO 2 /P (VDF-HFP) composite layer on PET to improve the charge barrier and dielectric strength of polymer capacitors at high temperatures.

title: Simultaneous morphology control and excited-state engineering in organic photovoltaics using volatile solid additives
abstract: The influence of 2-BCB and 4-BCB on the device performance and excited states dynamics caused by different multiple intermolecular interactions.

title: Synergistically enhanced energy storage performance of Bi 0.47 Na 0.47 Ba 0.06 TiO 3 -based relaxor ferroelectrics via dual engineering of dynamic nanodomains and defect regulation
abstract: A design strategy to achieve high energy storage performance via dual engineering of dynamic nanodomains and defect regulation.

title: Double transition metal MXenes as anode materials for high-capacity multivalent metal-ion batteries: a computational study
abstract: We explore the potential of novel double-transition-metal MXenes as electrode materials for multivalent metal-ion batteries using density functional theory. Our results reveal that these MXenes exhibit excellent specific storage capacities and low diffusion barriers.

title: Self-assembled SnO 2 /COF catalysts for improved electro-synthesis of hydrogen peroxide
abstract: The electrochemical oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) via a two-electron pathway offers a sustainable route for on-site hydrogen peroxide (H 2 O 2 ) production.

title: Tailoring self-assembled monolayers with post-assembly nicotinic acids for efficient and ultraviolet stable inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: Post-assembly nicotinic acid treatment mitigates buried interface defects and relieves interfacial compressive lattice strain, achieving a PCE of 25.98% and enhanced UV stability in inverted perovskite solar cells.

title: High-activity carrier-based catalysts from iron tailings: activating peroxymonosulfate for tetracycline hydrochloride degradation—the link between active sites and pollutant degradation
abstract: This study focuses on overcoming the obstacles encountered during peroxymonosulfate (PMS) activation utilizing iron ore residues.

title: n-Type doped SWCNT films prepared using organophosphorus compounds as cathodes in inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: Organophosphorus compounds induce n-type doping in single-walled carbon nanotubes, enabling their use as cathodes in inverted perovskite solar cells. PCBM overcoating improves interfacial contact, charge extraction, and device efficiency up to 8.03%.

title: Cohesive Co and Mo 2 C heterostructure catalysts strongly confined to hollow carbon support for enhanced kinetics and durability in the alkaline hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: A hollow-confined Co–Mo 2 C heterostructure enhances interfacial charge transfer, lowers water dissociation energy, suppresses Mo leaching, and boosts HER kinetics and stability.

title: Microdroplet-confined oxygen reduction forms highly active transition metal alloy hydroxides at low overpotentials for oxygen evolution
abstract: Electrodepositing transition metal hydroxide catalysts via heterogeneous oxygen reduction within adsorbed aqueous microdroplets from water-in-oil emulsions.

title: Highly sensitive and stable identification of graphene layers via the topological edge states and graphene regulation to enhance the photonic spin Hall effect
abstract: Schematic diagram of the LWS, showing the overall structure composed of different dielectrics arranged in a quasi-periodic pattern along the + z -axis, denoted as G(AB) N (CD) N , where (AB) N is denoted as LS 1 and (CD) N is symbolized by LS 2 .

title: Synergistic catalysis at in situ -formed Pt–NiOOH nanodot interfaces for highly efficient ammonia borane hydrolysis
abstract: Local NiOOH nanodots, rather than Ni(OH) 2 , which are indispensable for achieving superior catalytic performance, are in situ formed to create a Pt–NiOOH heterointerface on RGO.

title: Recent progress in Ru electrocatalyst design for acidic oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: This review surveys recent advances in Ru-based electrocatalysts for the acidic OER, focusing on strategies to enhance activity and stability, mitigate Ru dissolution and performance degradation, and inform the design of robust PEMWE.

title: Theoretical study of Cu-based alloy catalysts for oxidative coupling of methane
abstract: The Cu 3 Ir and Cu 3 Ir@Cu intermetallic catalysts promote methane activation and C 2 hydrocarbon formation through low dissociation barrier of C–H bond and the Langmuir–Hinshelwood surface mechanism, advancing the design of efficient industrial methane conversion catalysts.

title: Electronic and structural programming via electrochemical dealloying to generate Bi–Pb electrocatalysts for CO 2 reduction to formate
abstract: Direct one-step electrochemical dealloying to fabricate bicontinuous Bi–Pb electrocatalysts, with optimized electronic and structural properties, demonstrating excellent performance for CO 2 reduction to formate with enhanced selectivity and stability.

title: Alleviation of concentration polarization using density-controlled duplex electrode architecture to improve quick-charging performance of lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The presented work focuses on alleviating the concentration polarization of the negative electrode through density-controlled duplex electrode architecture introduction, which improves the fast-charging performance of lithium-ion batteries.

title: High-performance antimony selenide solar cells enabled by in situ potassium doping
abstract: In situ K doping during CBD of Sb 2 Se 3 films resulted in a more compact morphology, larger grains, better-oriented crystal growth, and reduced defects, yielding a device efficiency of 8.56%.

title: Sustainable nickel recovery via chlorination roasting from primary and secondary resources
abstract: Chlorination roasting enables nickel recovery rates up to nearly 100% from primary and secondary sources. This technology has great potential in promoting nickel recycling and resource conservation in modern industry.

title: Competition between dissolution and ion exchange during low temperature synthesis of LiCoO 2 on porous carbon scaffolds
abstract: The intentional design of ionic and electronic pathways in battery electrode architectures is one strategy to optimize battery performance and maximize the utilization of expensive and/or scarce electrode active materials.

title: High-pressure synthesis of PbN 2 , the missing group 14 AN 2 -type compound
abstract: The direct reaction of Pb with molecular nitrogen was investigated using a laser-heated diamond anvil cell combined with high-pressure in situ synchrotron X-ray powder diffraction measurements and Raman spectroscopy.

title: Efficient caustic and hydrogen production using a pressurized flow-through cathode
abstract: Flow-through electrodes for efficient caustic production.

title: GQD-modified CoFe-LDH@nickel foam as a high-performance anode catalyst for anion exchange membrane water electrolysis
abstract: Graphene quantum dots (GQDs)-modified CoFe-LDH promote interfacial electron transfer, stabilize M-OH intermediates, and accelerate OER kinetics on the surface.

title: Improved storage stability of conjugated polymer solutions with a versatile Non-halogenated solvent for organic solar cells
abstract: The utilization of TMB, featuring a large solvent polarity and molar volume, can generally enhance the solution stability of conjugated polymers for OSCs, which will benefit the preparation, storage, and processing of active layer solutions.

title: Biopolymer-based buried interface modification for efficient semitransparent perovskite solar cells with improved carrier dynamics
abstract: The natural biopolymer sodium hyaluronate is applied as a buried interface layer in ST-PSCs for achieving high PCE (16.02%), AVT (33%) and improved stability.

title: Corrosion resistance of hydrangea-like nano-Ni/amino N-doped rGO epoxy composite coatings constructed via magnetic field-oriented alignment
abstract: Hydrangea-like Ni nanoparticles on amino-nitrogen-doped rGO: a chemical coupling and magnetic alignment strategy for enhanced corrosion protection.

title: Electron redistribution assisted enhanced hydrogen evolution activity of NbSe 2 –NiSe nanoheterostructures
abstract: We report electron redistribution assisted enhancement in the electrochemical Hydrogen Evolution Reaction (HER) in NbSe 2 –NiSe nanoheterostructures (NHSs) exhibiting metal–metal interfacial contacts, synthesized via a hot injection route.

title: Origin of electrochemical cycling stability induced by calcination temperature for cobalt-free nickel-rich cathodes
abstract: LiNi 0.9 Mn 0.1 O 2 synthesized at low temperature showed superior cycling stability due to a slightly higher cation mixing degree and smaller primary particle size, while high-temperature synthesized samples degraded from microcracks and side reactions.

title: High-temperature composite phase change material with “concrete-like” strength even beyond the eutectic temperature
abstract: A latent heat thermal energy storage composite with an Al–Cu–Si alloy-based microencapsulated phase change material has been developed. This composite exhibits a high compressive strength of 32 MPa, even when the internal alloy is in a liquid state.

title: A polymeric mixed conductor-based solid state charge storage device
abstract: This work presents a charge storage device based on polymeric mixed conductors and a solid-state ionic liquid electrolyte, featuring minimized self-discharge and enhanced stability thanks to an integrated barrier against water and oxygen.

title: In situ engineered matched pockets for efficient substrate-selective catalysis in complex mixtures: synergistic binding-catalysis in magnetic imprinted nanoreactors
abstract: An enzyme-mimic matched pockets for efficient substrate-selective catalysis in complex mixtures.

title: Fully recyclable, catalyst-free, highly adhesive, and resilient poly(β-amino esters) covalent adaptable network-based solid polymer electrolytes for lithium metal batteries
abstract: PBAE covalent adaptable network-based electrolytes enable the development of safe, recyclable solid-state batteries with durable cycling and sustainable lithium salt recovery.

title: Enhancing photocatalytic water splitting via GeC/SGaSnP Z-scheme heterojunctions with built-in electric fields
abstract: This study designs a high-performance GeC/SGaSnP Z-scheme photocatalyst with excellent charge separation, strong visible light absorption, and superior HER/OER activity, making it a promising candidate for efficient water splitting.

title: Bi 2 WO 6 /COF S-scheme heterostructure photocatalyst for H 2 O 2 production
abstract: An innovative inorganic/organic (Bi 2 WO 6 /TpPa-Cl-COF) S-scheme heterojunction is fabricated by an electrostatic self-assembling method, which demonstrates superior photocatalytic performance in H 2 O 2 production.

title: Removal of thorium from high-acidity solutions achieved on a flexible polymeric network bearing phosphonic acid ligands: understanding adsorption and binding mechanisms
abstract: A phosphonic acid functionalized flexible polymeric network P(MBA-VPA-AA) prepared by solvothermal polymerization not only demonstrated high adsorption efficiency for thorium in highly acidic solution but also showed good acid-resistance.

title: Siloxane-decorated polymer acceptors enable humidity-tolerant air-processing and mechanical durability of all-polymer solar cells
abstract: Siloxane-decorated polymer acceptors afford all-polymer solar cells with advantages in air-processing, mechanical durability, and device stability.

title: Strategies for back contact engineering in high-performance flexible kesterite solar cells
abstract: Back contact engineering of flexible (Ag,Cu) 2 ZnSn(S,Se) 4 solar cells on Mo foil was performed. Despite the formation of a thick MoSe 2 layer, MoO 3 insertion at the back reduced kesterite decomposition and promoted grain growth, enabling η of 11.2%.

title: Enhancing the stability of air-processed perovskite solar cells through a self-healing polymer with dynamic molecular locks for grain boundary engineering
abstract: Self-healing polymers are widely used in perovskite solar cells (PSCs) to reduce mechanical damage to perovskite films during operation.

title: High-density small Pt 3 Fe@Pt nanoparticles in carbon materials becoming superior bi-functional catalysts through oxygen bridges with Fe single atoms
abstract: High-density small Pt 3 Fe@Pt nanoparticles with atomic Pt shells O-bridged to Fe single atoms exhibit superior performance.

title: Gradient architecture design of porous aramid nanofiber separators for robust and safe lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A robust aramid nanofiber separator with gradient nanopore architecture (GANFM) serves as a thermally stable and electrochemically superior separator for lithium-ion batteries.

title: Bio-inspired polypyrrole nanowire arrays on melamine foam with high-performance photo/electro-thermal conversion for all-weather cleanup of crude oil
abstract: Moth-eye structured polypyrrole nanowire arrays (PPy NWAs) enable all-weather crude oil adsorption via photo/electrothermal conversion.

title: Tuning ferrite nanoparticles for optimal inductive heating in thermal swing adsorption processes
abstract: Maximizing ferrite susceptor performance critically enables inductive heating swing adsorption as electrified, CO 2 -lean alternative for fossil energy heated processes.

title: Understanding and tuning organocatalysts for versatile condensation polymer deconstruction
abstract: This article details how Hammett analysis is used to design organocatalysts for condensation polymer glycolysis, and demonstrates subsequent selective and sequential mixed plastic waste deconstruction.

title: Elucidating the effects of bromine substitution in asymmetric quinoxaline central core-based non-fullerene acceptors on molecular stacking and photovoltaic performances
abstract: Asymmetric central cores and substitution modification were implemented to adjust the interactions in non-fullerene acceptors. The bromine atom optimized molecular stacking and film morphology, leading to 19.65% efficiency binary organic solar cell.

title: Hierarchical multi-dimensional nanostructures enabled by Cu/Cu 2+ -doped MoS 2 with synergistic polarization–conduction loss for broadband electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: A multidimensional CNFs@Cu/Cu 2+ -doped MoS 2 (CMS) composite was fabricated, where Cu/Cu 2+ doping enhances electrical conductivity and introduces carbon defects and Cu interstitials, enabling synergistic polarization and conduction losses.

title: Hydrophobic silsesquioxane-modified lignin-incorporated polyurethane film
abstract: Polyurethane (PU) is widely used in adhesive, foam, and coating applications.

title: Photochemical reduction of low concentration CO 2 to solar fuel in a metal free 2D-covalent organic framework
abstract: We report a crystalline, porous BDT-TTA COF. It shows high surface area, strong visible-light absorption, and enables photocatalytic low-concentration CO 2 -to-CO reduction, supported by in situ DRIFT and theoretical studies.

title: Modulating hydroxyl adsorption on Pd–Rh heterostructures through interfacial electron redistribution: a pathway to high-efficiency alkaline HOR catalysis
abstract: The Pd–Rh heterostructure catalyst optimizes adsorption energies for reaction intermediates through interfacial electronic modulation. The PdRh 0.05 /C exhibits exceptional alkaline HOR performance with high CO tolerance.

title: Restrained reshaping for ultra-compact, aligned, highly thermally conductive boron nitride nanosheet all-ceramic fibers
abstract: A restrained reshaping method to produce BNNS all ceramic fibers with a round-edge belt structure is developed. The method transforms uniaxial pressure into circumferential compression, providing effective densification and alignment.

title: Correction: Cartilage-inspired rapid in situ fabrication of seamless interlocked electrolyte–electrode interface for high-performance flexible supercapacitors
abstract: Correction for ‘Cartilage-inspired rapid in situ fabrication of seamless interlocked electrolyte–electrode interface for high-performance flexible supercapacitors’ by Yu Guo et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2023, 11 , 24146–24157, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TA04985J.

title: Correction: Multimetallic assembly of concave-shaped rectangular Mn 4 clusters as efficient hydrogen evolution electrocatalysts
abstract: Correction for ‘Multimetallic assembly of concave-shaped rectangular Mn 4 clusters as efficient hydrogen evolution electrocatalysts’ by Chandan Sarkar et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA01854D.

title: Correction: Growth of p/n-type BiFeO 3 thin films for construction of a bilayer p–n junction for photodegradation of organic pollutants
abstract: Correction for ‘Growth of p/n-type BiFeO 3 thin films for construction of a bilayer p–n junction for photodegradation of organic pollutants' by Hao-Yun Tu et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2024, 12 , 12752–12761, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA01615G.

title: Rate-determining step backshift effectively boosts ORR performance by excess electron transfer to the O–O antibonding orbital
abstract: The Pt 23 Pd 77 NSs/C catalyst has a mass activity of 6.78 A mg PGM −1 and the specific activity was 7.56 A cm −2 , respectively.

title: Integration of material sintering and p–n connection for high-performance PbTe thermoelectric modules
abstract: A co-sintering strategy is developed in this work for U-shaped p–n PbTe thermoelectric legs with low interfacial resistance and thermal stress. The module achieves an η max of 9.1% while maintaining stability over 50 cycles.

title: Synergistic effect of Mo and Sn in a quaternary metal sulphide to activate N 2 adsorption for selective solar-driven ammonia production
abstract: Incorporation of Mo and Sn into CdS (Cd 0.60 Mo 0.20 Sn 0.20 S) boost 8-fold photocatalytic NH 3 production with 100% selectivity from N 2 compared to CdS via optimizing band structure, enhancing charge carrier separation, and facilitating N 2 activation.

title: Visible lithiation gradients of bulk MoS 2 in lithium-ion coin cells
abstract: Lithiation of MoS 2 electrodes in coin cells (100–200 mA g −1 ) results in incomplete lithiation and MoS 2 phase inhomogeneities. Upon deep discharge (0.01 V) the outer ring has better contact with the counter electrode and forms a gel-like layer.

title: Influence of highly flexible di(biphenyl)ethane units on the properties of poly(arylene piperidinium) anion exchange membranes
abstract: Synthesis and polymerization of a highly reactive monomer with angled biphenyl units bridged by an ethane segment enable the preparation of flexible high-performance anion-exchange membranes.

title: Unraveling the performance enhancement mechanism of Ti doping for Li 4 SiO 4 -based solar energy storage: a combined experimental, kinetic, and DFT study
abstract: The reason for the performance enhancement of the heat carrier is that the lattice defects caused by Ti doping increase the ion migration rate.

title: Synthesis and reversible Li-ion intercalation of a novel chromium-doped iron phosphate with an α-CrPO 4 structure
abstract: Li-ion batteries based on environmentally friendly, cheap and available elements are in high demand for sustainable economic, societal and technological development.

title: Conformal electrochemical deposition of intermetallic AuCu thin films for convergent C–N coupling
abstract: A compositionally tunable series of AuCu films is active for electrocatalytic urea generation from carbon dioxide and nitrate.

title: A biomolecular self-assembly assisted synthesis strategy for ultra-small bismuth nanoparticles toward ultra-high-rate sodium-ion storage
abstract: A biomolecular self-assembly strategy was used to synthesize ultra-small bismuth nanoparticles toward ultra-high-rate sodium-ion storage.

title: Facile fabrication of MOF and natural polymer-derived carbon-aerogels with multiscale porosity for persulfate activation in water treatment
abstract: Heteroatom-doped carbon materials have attracted significant attention as efficient catalysts for sulfate radical-based advanced oxidation processes (SR-AOPs).

title: Advances in CO catalytic oxidation on typical metal oxide catalysts: performance, mechanism, and optimization
abstract: Review of CO oxidation over CuO x , MnO x , CeO x , CoO x : surface, lattice, vacancies, valence; L–H, MvK, E–R mechanisms; low-T, poisoning, stability issues; defect, facet, synergy, interface tuning; flue gas & air purify.

title: Probing photochemically-induced dynamic transitions by magic-angle-spinning NMR combined with in situ irradiation
authors: Patrick C. A. van der Wel
abstract: We demonstrate the potential of magic angle spinning NMR to probe chemical and dynamic processes during light-driven (dis)assembly and polymerisation in semi-solid samples, enabled by efficient in situ illumination by an LED light source.

title: An oxazole-linked donor–acceptor covalent organic framework as an efficient electrocatalyst for lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: An oxazole-linked donor–acceptor COF (BTT-DABD) is developed and demonstrated as an excellent electrocatalyst for lithium–sulfur batteries. The oxazole linkage endows BTT-DABD with enhanced chemical stability and catalytic activity.

title: Theoretical screening of transition metal atoms supported on two-dimensional Janus VSSe for HER/OER/ORR single-atom catalysts
abstract: A single-atom catalyst was designed by doping transition metal atoms on the surface of Janus material VSSe, which exhibited great catalytic performance in HER/OER/ORR reactions.

title: Multi-heterostructure with multi-interface for efficient hydrogen evolution reaction in industrial alkaline seawater
abstract: The multi-heterostructure H 2 –NiCo 2 O 4 @NiMoO 4 electrocatalyst with multi-interface demonstrates outstanding HER performance in both 1 M KOH and alkaline seawater electrolyte, particularly at an ultra-high current density of 4000 mA cm −2 .

title: Enhancing CO 2 photocatalytic reduction with a novel polymer catalyst: inducing reactive C–N bond formation through altered thermodynamic trends and exploring reduction kinetics
abstract: This work presents an eco-friendly Na 2 CO 3 -modified photocatalyst fabricated via the optimization of thermodynamic bonding during polymerization with improved mass and energy transfer.

title: Solvometallurgy: design of ternary deep eutectic solvents for the electrochemical recovery of nickel from lithium-ion cathode materials
abstract: A sequential solvometallurgical leaching and electrodeposition method utilizing a green ternary deep eutectic solvent demonstrates nearly 100% recovery of pure nickel from lithium-ion battery cathodes at benign temperatures (≤70 °C).

title: Assignment of elementary reactions to impedance components for solid-oxide fuel cells and proton-conducting ceramic fuel cells with Yb and Co-doped barium zirconate as the cathode
abstract: Using isotope effects, the anode and cathode impedance components were separated, and the component of the steam formation reaction was determined. Components at the cathode were identified by comparing PCFCs and SOFCs.

title: A facile synthesis of bulk LiPON in solution for solid-state electrolytes
abstract: We present a facile, solution-based approach to prepare bulk lithium phosphorus oxynitride (LiPON) utilizing a stepwise reduction between lithium tert -butoxide (LiO t Bu) and a phosphazene base, diethyl phosphoramidate (DEPA).

title: Failure of protonic ceramic fuel cells (PCFCs) under gaseous Cr and CO 2 exposure and the introduction of a protective barrier layer for mitigation
abstract: Cr species and CO 2 rapidly degrade the PCFC electrolyte BaCe 0.4 Zr 0.4 Y 0.1 Yb 0.1 O 3− δ , while the air electrode PrBa 0.5 Sr 0.5 Co 1.5 Fe 0.5 O 5+ δ remains stable. A thin PBSCF layer enhances air electrode/electrolyte interface stability.

title: Leveraging the ingenuity of carbon nanomaterials towards water quality amelioration
abstract: The fascinating nanoforms of carbon when uniquely tailored, capture diverse impurities in water contributing to an impressive nanotechnology solution for water quality amelioration.

title: In situ construction of solid-electrolyte interfaces for metallic Zn anodes in aqueous zinc batteries
abstract: An in situ SEI plays a critical role in ZMBs, and hence, a concise summary is presented comprising its formation mechanisms, materials and functions.

title: Cellulose hierarchical sponge-aerogel fibers via ionic liquid-assisted coaxial wet spinning: lightweight architectures for gas detection and adaptive thermal management
abstract: The development of cellulose-based aerogel fibers integrating exceptional mechanical robustness with multi-functionality remains crucial yet challenging for advancing next-generation smart textiles in wearable applications.

title: Construction of a synergistic Pt dual-active site catalyst for promoting low temperature CO oxidation
abstract: Designing and fabrication of high performance Pt-based catalysts with high atom-utilization efficiency are essential but challenging for CO oxidation.

title: Tuning ZnCdS heterostructures for enhanced photocatalysis: hybrid architectures for sustainable energy and environmental applications
abstract: ZnCdS-based photocatalysts offer tunable band gaps and excellent activity, enabling solar-driven hydrogen evolution and pollutant removal. This review highlights their evolution, challenges, and strategies for enhanced photocatalytic performance.

title: Densification and performance optimization of NaSICON solid electrolytes via a low-temperature cold sintering process with sodium ionic salt doping
abstract: Sodium-ion batteries (SIBs) have emerged as a sustainable alternative to lithium-ion systems, offering cost-effective and environmentally friendly energy storage solutions.

title: Superior magnetocapacitance in ferro/ferrimagnetic Fe 3 O 4 /Fe/Fe 3 C integrated N-doped carbon hybrid nanostructures under mild magnetic fields
abstract: Encapsulating magnetic materials in a carbon network increased the interfacial area, enhancing storage performance under a magnetic field. This led to a specific capacitance of 2057.3 F g −1 at 6 mT and 1.5 A g −1 .

title: AB 5 -type intermetallic compounds as catalysts for ammonia synthesis
abstract: Mechanical milling of LaNi 5 alloy significantly enhances ammonia synthesis, boosting the synthesis rate from 77.38 to 2080 μmol NH 3 g −1 h −1 . which is attributed to the cooperative interactions between H*, H − and surface nitrides.

title: Unveiling the chemical reconstruction of Fe(OH) 3 -embedded Ni-MOF nanorods for enhanced oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: The Fe(OH) 3 -embedded Ni-MOF nanorods undergo complete reconstruction into CO 3 2− -adsorbed Fe/NiOOH, effectively suppressing FeOOH dissolution and reducing the energy barrier of the rate-determining step on Ni sites during the OER.

title: Rapid synthesis of an aluminum-doped ultrathin Ru x –RuO 2 heterostructure optimized through combined wet–dry microwave radiation for efficient acidic and alkaline overall water splitting
abstract: By employing an efficient “wet-dry” microwave strategy, we have developed Al-doped Ru x /RuO 2 (A-RRO@G) in just 110 s. Its unique structure optimizes electronic configurations, enhancing water-splitting activity in both acidic and alkaline conditions.

title: Improvement of the performance of Cu 2 ZnSn(S,Se) 4 solar cells by annealing Li-doped Cu 2 ZnSnS 4 precursor films in air
abstract: The PCE of the CZTSSe solar cell was enhanced from 9.06% to 10.21% by optimizing the Li doping content, and then further increased from 10.21% to 11.13% by adjusting the annealing temperature and time of CZTS doped with the optimal Li content in air.

title: Advancements towards optimization of metal–organic framework-based polymer electrolyte membranes for aqueous redox flow batteries
abstract: Functionalizing the membrane framework with the introduction of MOFs, robust hybrid membranes separator devised in ARFBs that outperformed the pristine membrane separators in terms of electro-chemical efficiencies and capacity retention rates.

title: Advancements in perovskite solar cell concentrators and future prospects
abstract: This review summarizes recent progress in perovskite materials for concentrated photovoltaics (CPVs), highlighting their properties, addressing thermal challenges, and discussing strategies to enhance CPV performance and feasibility.

title: Polar atom-pair catalysts for electrochemical C–N coupling
abstract: The polar B–V atom pair catalyst was constructed to realize the polarization and activation of the dinitrogen molecule, thus accelerating the electrochemical coupling of C–N.

title: Unraveling e g -band modulation as an alternative strategy to enhance lattice oxygen participation and oxygen electrocatalytic bifunctionality via switching the active site
abstract: A novel strategy of uplifting the active-metal e g -band in a high-entropy perovskite oxide enhances lattice oxygen participation, leading to significant improvements in oxygen electrocatalytic bifunctionality.

title: A multicomponent donor–π–acceptor covalent organic framework as a nanoporous cation-selective separator for durable aqueous zinc ion batteries
abstract: A multicomponent covalent organic framework with donor–π–acceptor structure is designed and composited with glass fiber as the separator for zinc-ion batteries. The ordered zinc-ion channel regulates zinc-ion transport, ensuring battery durability.

title: Synergistic effect of an oxygen-defective TiNb 2 O 7 anode and lithiated polyacrylic acid for high-power lithium-ion storage
abstract: Highly conductive TiNb 2 O 6.93 , synthesized in under 60 seconds, features oxygen deficiencies at edge-shared octahedra and enlarged d -spacing. With a lithiated polyacrylic acid binder, it accommodates >3 Li + ions without electrolyte decomposition.

title: High-performance, ambient-processable organic solar cells achieved by single terpene-based entirely eco-friendly process
abstract: We developed entirely eco-friendly organic solar cells using eucalyptol (Eu) as a single terpene solvent, achieving a record-high PCE (15.1%). The Eu processing enables fabrication in air and enhances stability, promising for sustainable production.

title: Hierarchical porous Co-rich PtCo thin films for alkaline seawater hydrogen evolution with chlorine corrosion resistance
abstract: The creation of cost-effective and efficient catalysts for hydrogen evolution in seawater is highly desirable, yet it still poses considerable challenges.

title: Water-stable MOFs and composites: a greener and sustainable approach for enhanced reactivity towards the electrochemical nitrate reduction reaction
abstract: The electrochemical nitrate reduction reaction (eNO 3 − RR) is a promising strategy to mitigate nitrate pollution, which has become a critical environmental concern due to its harmful effects on water resources and ecosystems.

title: Critical role of interfacial polarization suppression in enhancing breakdown strength and energy storage performance of NBT-based dielectric ceramics
abstract: In this study on (1 − x )Na 0.5 Bi 0.5 TiO 3 - x Ca 0.7 Nd 0.2 TiO 3 ceramics, we find that the interfacial polarization suppression strategy can effectively enhance breakdown strength and energy storage performance despite notably reduced insulation.

title: Insights into the hybrid evaporation-spin coating method: process optimization and consequences for wide band gap perovskite solar cells
abstract: This study focuses on experiment design and perovskite formation kinetics for 1.68 eV wide-bandgap perovskite solar cells with a hybrid evaporation-spin coating method, offering insights for textured Si-based tandem solar cell development.

title: Multifunctional and highly stretchable gold-leaf/WPU/TPU fibers by a rolling adhesive method
abstract: GLWT fibers, created by a rolling adhesive method, enable strain sensing, smart fabrics, and stretchable electrodes. Arrayed for pressure sensing, they recognize object shapes, ideal for wearables and sensors.

title: Hidden symmetry lowering, nanoscale order–disorder transition and ionic conductivity in Na 1/2− x La 1/2− x Ba 2 x ZrO 3
abstract: Local structure model and corresponding Na + conduction network of Na 1/2− x La 1/2− x Ba 2 x ZrO 3 , x = 6/32, obtained from analysis of X-ray spectroscopy and neutron total-scattering data.

title: Pb defect passivation and FA immobilization: towards efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: The –CF 3 group in EMTC forms H-bonds with FA + , anchoring it and suppressing ion migration. Meanwhile, CO, S, and –CF 3 coordinate with undercoordinated Pb 2+ , passivating defects, promoting larger grains, stabilizing [PbI 6 ] 4− , and reducing organic cation volatilization.

title: A quasi-solid-state Li-ion hybrid capacitor based on a colloidal metal–organic framework interwoven by in situ polymerized PANI
abstract: An in situ gel-confined polymerization method was developed to create MOF gel/polyaniline (MOG/PANI) composites for a quasi-solid-state Li-ion hybrid capacitor, overcoming the conductivity limitations of MOFs.

title: Proton co-intercalation enabled high-performance aqueous multivalent metal-ion batteries
abstract: Aqueous rechargeable batteries have emerged as promising candidates for large-scale energy storage due to their inherent safety, low cost, and environmental friendliness.

title: Enhanced colossal permittivity in mono-doped BaTiO 3 via particle hydroxylation-induced defect dipoles
abstract: Defect-engineered colossal permittivity with controlled dielectric loss in BaTiO 3 ceramics via particle hydroxylation through wet chemical synthesis.

title: Breaking scaling relations via Fe/Ni diatomic catalysts towards highly efficient electrocatalysts for rechargeable Na-air batteries
abstract: Single-atom catalysts with two metal sites can circumvent the inherent scaling relations, achieving high-performance sodium-air batteries.

title: A rational approach for the synthesis of near-infrared absorbing porous hypercrosslinked polymers for solar steam generation
abstract: NIR-absorbing porous polymer, DBP-HCP is synthesised using electron-rich DBP by Friedel–Crafts alkylation. The resultant polymer shows good Vis-NIR absorption (300–1600 nm) and solar steam generation with a conversion efficiency of 74.3%.

title: Simultaneous enhancement of thermoelectric performance and mechanical properties in lead-free cubic GeTe-based composite materials
abstract: This study demonstrates that alloying with AgSbTe 2 enhances the symmetry of the crystal structure of Ge 0.81 Mn 0.15 Bi 0.04 Te, while simultaneously improving the thermoelectric performance and mechanical properties.

title: Morpholine-linked metal-phthalocyanine covalent organic frameworks for enhanced photocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: Innovative morpholine-linked metal-phthalocyanine covalent organic frameworks were constructed. The electron delocalization properties of Co-DB-COF accelerate the charge transfer rate, improving the photocatalytic CO 2 reduction reaction performance.

title: Demystifying charge-compensation mechanisms and oxygen dimerization in Li-rich Li 2 NiO 3 cathodes
abstract: Li-rich cathodes are gaining popularity for Li-ion batteries due to their higher capacity compared to standard layered cathodes. Here we elucidate the complex redox and O dimerization mechanisms using advanced materials theory.

title: Enhanced stretchability and conductivity in self-healing ionogels: a hybrid PEDOT:PSS/IL/PAA composite
abstract: Composite ionogels based on poly(acrylic acid) (PAA) and an ionic liquid (IL), further incorporated with poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene):poly(styrene sulfonate) (PEDOT:PSS) to enhance properties, are presented.

title: Defect structures in supercapacitor electrodes: non-oxide 2D materials and metal oxides
abstract: This review highlights how crystallographic and interfacial defects control charge storage in supercapacitors by modulating electronic structure, ion transport, and redox kinetics, ultimately shaping capacitive behavior and stability.

title: Truxenone-based donor–acceptor covalent organic frameworks incorporated with metal sites for enhanced photocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: Under the synergistic effect of the D–A structure and metal active sites, TRO-based COFs facilitated charge separation, exhibiting efficient photocatalytic performance towards photocatalytic CO 2 reduction reaction.

title: 3D wood-derived vertical multichannel carbon framework with functional fillers for high-performance Zn-ion hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: The 3D wood-derived vertical multichannel carbon framework regulated by carbon spheres realizes uniform Zn deposition due to its well-designed hierarchical porous structure and oxygen-rich functional groups.

title: Avoiding surface defect-catalyzed oxidation for extraordinary thermoelectric performance in n-type iodine-doped PbTe compounds
abstract: Argon protection suppresses oxygen-induced defects, enabling ultrahigh mobility (1800 cm 2 V −1 s −1 ) and a record power factor in n-type PbTe.

title: Exquisite restructuring of the electronic structure of cobalt phosphide via rationally controlling iron induction for water splitting under industrial conditions
abstract: Ultrathin Fe-doped CoP nanosheets with exquisite restructuring of the electronic structure presented preferable dual-function water splitting properties in laboratorial and industrial conditions.

title: Beryllium dinitride monolayer: a multifunctional direct bandgap anisotropic semiconductor containing polymeric nitrogen with oxygen reduction catalysis and potassium-ion storage capability
abstract: The novel α-2D-BeN 2 monolayer shows exceptional potential as a multifunctional semiconductor for ORR/OER catalysis as well as for potassium-ion batteries.

title: Synergistic cation/anion modulation of metal phosphorus trichalcogenides for enhanced potassium-ion storage performance
abstract: Through comprehensive theoretical computations and experimental analyses, ME-SSe nanoparticles in the dual carbon framework are obtained via a medium-entropy cation and alloy anion co-tuning strategy, exhibiting enhanced potassium-ion storage.

title: Boosting the catalytic activity of water splitting and oxygen reduction reactions through axial coordination to MN 4 –C model catalysts
abstract: By exploring 161 distinct MN 4 X–C SACs, optimal HER, OER, and ORR catalysts were identified. The axial ligand-induced d–p orbital hybridization ( Δ d–p ) could govern the activity via orbital passivation and electron redistribution.

title: Leaf-inspired nanofibrous mat-based composites for liquid–gas phase change-driven dynamically adaptive electromagnetic interference shielding
abstract: Tunable electromagnetic interference shielding performance through liquid–vapor phase transition-driven internal electron transfer in nanofiber mat-based composites.

title: Electrolyte additive strategy for uniform nucleation of Cu–Bi toward low-voltage self-powered dynamic windows
abstract: The choline chloride additive is proposed for uniform nucleation of Cu–Bi, and l -PAA interface modification and Pb(Ac) 2 doping of CsPbI 2 Br are reported toward low-voltage self-powered dynamic windows, in which Cu–Bi RMED was driven by solar cells.

title: Supramolecular framework crystallinity engineering via surface-confined polymerization for enhanced C 3 H 6 /C 2 H 4 separation
abstract: A new highly porous SMF, NUT-161, was reinforced through surface-confined polymerization, thereby enhancing its performance for MTO product separation.

title: Robust P–N heterojunction polymer nanocomposites: advanced pH-universal electrocatalysts for highly efficient and stable water-splitting hydrogen production
abstract: A novel organic–inorganic heterojunction electrocatalyst shows efficient, pH-universal HER activity and long-term stability in acidic, alkaline, and seawater electrolytes, offering potential to replace commercial Pt/C catalysts.

title: Unveiling the performance of ultrathin bimetallic Co x Ni 1− x (OH) 2 nanosheets for pseudocapacitors and oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Bimetallic Co–Ni hydroxide nanosheets for pseudocapacitors and oxygen evolution reaction.

title: Enhanced tribological performance of a supramolecular gel with F-doped carbon dots via in situ directional ultrasonication
abstract: The Gel@F-CDs gel composite material is prepared using directional ultrasonication, effectively addressing the dispersion stability issue of F-CDs. This material can maintain a low COF (<0.115) under high contact pressure (150 N) for over 10 h.

title: Ferroelectric materials as photoelectrocatalysts: photoelectrode design rationale and strategies
abstract: The utilization of ferroelectrics offers an additional lever to surpass the performance limits of traditional photoelectrodes. In this review, design strategies for ferroelectric photoelectrodes from materials to PEC system design are assessed.

title: Electrochemical performance of MoS 2 , Sb 2 S 3 , and SnS anodes in sodium-ion batteries using a conductive polypyrrole-carbon black composite and an LBG sustainable binder
abstract: A sustainable electrode architecture combining MoS 2 , SnS, or Sb 2 S 3 with a polypyrrole–carbon black (PPyCB) network and locust bean gum (LBG) binder improves sodium-ion battery performance and structural stability.

title: The strain-mediated orbital synergy in Co 3 O 4 for enhanced oxygen evolution reaction activity
abstract: Strain-regulated on Co 3 O 4 (100) enhances OER performance by inducing Jahn–Teller distortion of Co 3d orbitals and improving oxygen intermediate adsorption, with optimal strain at −2%.

title: Correction: Pure hydrogen and sulfur production from H 2 S by an electrochemical approach using a NiCu–MoS 2 catalyst
abstract: Correction for ‘Pure hydrogen and sulfur production from H 2 S by an electrochemical approach using a NiCu–MoS 2 catalyst’ by Mukesh Kumar et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2022, 10 , 13031–13041, https://doi.org/10.1039/D2TA02751H.

title: Correction: Screening of single-atom catalysts for CO 2 electroreduction to CH 4 using DFT calculations and machine learning
abstract: Correction for ‘Screening of single-atom catalysts for CO 2 electroreduction to CH 4 using DFT calculations and machine learning’ by Jingzhuo Zhou et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, 13 , 24073–24083, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA03431K.

title: Correction: Phase diagram of ZIF-4 from computer simulations
abstract: Correction for ‘Phase diagram of ZIF-4 from computer simulations’ by Emilio Méndez et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2024, 12 , 31108–31115, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA05026F.

title: A sustainable l -serine-induced hydrogel with ultrafast gelation, mechanical resilience, and environmental robustness for efficient sand stabilization
abstract: An l -serine-mediated hydrogel enables ultrafast gelation, strong sand adhesion, and long-term durability under harsh conditions, offering a scalable and biocompatible strategy for sand stabilization and ecological restoration in arid environments.

title: Improved tetracycline degradation via integrated peroxymonosulfate activation and photocatalytic self-Fenton using hybrid Type-II/Z-scheme Se/g-C 3 N 4 /Bi 2 WO 6 carboxymethyl cellulose hydrogels
abstract: Developing efficient integrated advanced oxidation processes (AOPs) is vital for sustainable treatment of antibiotic-contaminated water.

title: All-organic superhydrophobic cellular coatings with durability, robustness, flexibility, and liquid impalement resistance
abstract: Fabrication of porous PDMS superhydrophobic capsules loaded with PTFE nanoparticles. Coatings containing these capsules exhibit chemical stability, mechanical wear resistance, and low ice adhesion.

title: Dual-functional defect passivation in Dion–Jacobson perovskite for low-dose X-ray detection and imaging
abstract: A DJ perovskite X-ray detector with dual-functional passivation achieves a sensitivity of 21 000 μC Gy air −1 cm −2 and a limit of detection of 6.75 nGy air s −1 . The TFT-integrated flat-panel imager gives high-contrast plant and electronics imaging.

title: Selective hydrocracking of polyolefin waste into branched liquid fuels over a non-noble metal bifunctional Ni–W/ZrO 2 catalyst
abstract: Non-noble Ni–W/ZrO 2 enables efficient LDPE hydrocracking to highly branched alkanes via synergistic metal–acid balance.

title: Replacing electrochemical measurement by colorimetric measurement to evaluate supercapacitor electrodes
abstract: Colorimetric analysis reveals electrode performance trends, degradation states, and temperature-dependent behavior in supercapacitor characterization.
