title: A chromium and nitrogen co-doped NiMo-based catalyst achieving high-efficiency and durable intermittent water electrolysis
abstract: A chromium and nitrogen co-doped NiMo-based catalyst achieving high-efficiency and durable intermittent electrochemical hydrogen evolution reaction.

title: Enhanced photocatalytic H 2 O 2 yield by single-atom Pt decorated carbon nitride sheets via boosting *OOH intermediate generation
abstract: Pt-SA/g-C 3 N 4 achieves an H 2 O 2 yield of 836 μmol L −1 h −1 under visible light. Single-atom Pt promotes charge separation. In situ SERS directly observed OOH formation. DFT calculations indicate Pt inhibits OOH cleavage, enhancing H 2 O 2 production.

title: Multimetallic layered double hydroxides as efficient and durable oxygen evolution catalysts for anion exchange membrane water electrolysis at high current densities
abstract: Multimetallic NiCrFeMo layered double hydroxides have been synthesized through a facile microwave-assisted hydrothermal approach and exhibit outstanding electrocatalytic performance for oxygen evolution in anion exchange membrane water electrolysis.

title: Enhancing inverted perovskite solar cells via hydrophilic surface modification of NiO x using aluminate coupling agents
abstract: Hydrophilic monolayer modification of NiO x with aluminum di(isopropoxide)acetoacetic ester chelate enhances perovskite solar cell efficiency to 23.82% and stability (97.7% retention after 600 hours of illumination).

title: Graphene accelerates electron transfer over copper-cobalt nanoparticles for efficient electroreduction of nitrate to ammonia
abstract: A novel CuCo–Gr0.1 electrode with enhanced electron transfer properties through graphene decoration was developed. CuCo–Gr0.1 exhibits outstanding NO 3 − RR performance, enabling dual-function nitrate-to-ammonia conversion and uranyl ion removal.

title: Advancing HT-PEM fuel cell technology: durability and performance under start–stop conditions
abstract: This review highlights degradation mechanisms and emerging mitigation strategies for HT-PEM fuel cells under start–stop conditions, guiding the design of durable, high-performance energy systems.

title: Recent advances in p-type polymeric electrode materials towards high-voltage 4.0 V-class organic lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The p-type polymer electrodes have received an exponential growth of interest for organic lithium-ion batteries. This review summarizes their recent developments focusing on structure, performance, advantages, and challenges.

title: Hydrogen-bond-guided micellar self-assembly-directed carbon superstructures for high-energy and ultralong-life zinc-ion hybrid capacitors
abstract: A hydrogen-bond-guided micellar self-assembly strategy is leveraged to construct flower-like carbon superstructures, which employs aggregated micelles to serve as a structural guide to enable attaining superior performance in ZHCs.

title: A single-ion-conducting polymer and high-entropy Li-garnet composite electrolyte with simultaneous enhancement in ion transport and mechanical properties
abstract: A schematic comparison between a single-ion-conducting polymer and its composite incorporating high-entropy Li-garnet, illustrating simultaneous enhancement in mechanical modulus and ion transport.

title: Inverse analysis-guided development of acid-tolerant nanoporous high-entropy alloy catalysts for enhanced water-splitting performance
abstract: Through material-driven inverse analysis, the acid-tolerant high-entropy catalyst, composed of Al, Au, Ir, Nb, Pt, Rh, Ru, and Ta, exhibited superior catalytic activity and stability in acidic environments, outperforming Pt and IrO 2 catalysts.

title: Synergetic effect of surface frustrated Lewis pair and localized surface plasmon resonance on tuning the catalyst from inert to highly reactive for photocatalytic CO 2 hydrogenation
abstract: Cu/AlO(OH) x nanocrystals with the synergetic effect of SFLP and LSPR can serve as effective photocatalysts for the reverse water gas shift (RWGS) reaction with a remarkable near-unity (∼99%) selectivity.

title: Photocatalytic oxidation of glycerol with red light employing quinacridone sensitized TiO 2 nanoparticles
abstract: TiO 2 nanoparticles sensitized with the quinacridone industrial pigment enable the photocatalytic oxidation of glycerol to glyceraldehyde exploiting red light.

title: Facile encapsulation strategy for uniformly-dispersed catalytic nanoparticles/carbon nanofibers toward advanced Zn–air battery
abstract: A facile synthesis strategy for carbon nanofibers with uniformly dispersed metal nanoparticles encapsulated in a thin carbon layer significantly enhances ORR/OER dual-functionality and cycle stability in zinc–air batteries.

title: Heteroatom substitution and molecular configuration engineering in self-assembled materials for high-efficiency and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: A novel SAM (β-Th-2PACz) is developed as a hole-selective layer for perovskite solar cells, which outperformed its α-thiophene counterpart.

title: Mn-modified nickel oxide for selective methanol oxidation: a route toward integrated formate electrosynthesis and hydrogen generation
abstract: Mn-doped NiO enables efficient methanol electroreforming into formate, achieving high current density, above 97% faradaic efficiency, and long-term stability, while lowering energy input for hydrogen production and value-added chemical generation than the traditional water splitting.

title: Dual passivation and UV shielding by bifunctional UV0 Molecule for high-performance and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: An ultraviolet (UV)-resistant molecule, 2,4-dihydroxybenzophenone (UV0), is introduced into perovskite films. To inhibit the formation of iodide ions through hydrogen bonds and enhance the ultraviolet stability of perovskite solar cells.

title: Perovskite oxides as a new family of tunable CO 2 sorbents
abstract: Schematic representation of the synthesis and CO 2 adsorption/desorption behavior of perovskite oxide Sr x La 1− x FeO 3 as a novel CO 2 sorbent.

title: Defect mitigation via fullerene-based functional additives for enhanced efficiency and stability in tin perovskite solar cells
abstract: This work demonstrates the critical role of fullerene derivatives with functional groups (COOH, OH, and OSO 3 H) in reducing Sn 4+ formation and defect passivation. The device with F-COOH improves efficiency from 8.20 to 11.22% and stability.

title: W-promoted OER kinetics of bimetallic hydroxide: an experimental analysis via operando EIS and temperature-dependent study
abstract: W 4+ ion doped NiCo-LDH, an effective electrocatalyst towards the OER has been developed. W-NiCo-LDH exhibits a TOF value 31 times greater and an activation energy ( E a ) value 3 times lower than that of bare NiCo-LDH.

title: Correction: Ionic-liquid-engineered, interfacial π–π-anchored, cobalt-dispersed, and N-, F-, B-doped carbon matrix as an oxygen electrocatalyst for advanced zinc–air batteries
abstract: Correction for ‘Ionic-liquid-engineered, interfacial π–π-anchored, cobalt-dispersed, and N-, F-, B-doped carbon matrix as an oxygen electrocatalyst for advanced zinc–air batteries’ by Nadar Allwyn et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2025, 13 , 13935–13950, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TA00770D.

title: Se-vacancy induced structural reconstruction of iron nickel selenium nanosheets for efficient and durable electrocatalytic alkaline water and seawater oxidation
abstract: Se-vacancy-rich Fe-doped NiSe nanosheets were synthesized for the oxygen evolution reaction in both alkaline water and seawater. In situ Raman revealed Se vacancies promotie NiFeOOH and SeO 4 2− formation enhancing catalytic performance.

title: Boosting hydroxyl radical production via floatable S-scheme heterojunction-integrated polyacrylic acid hydrogels as a photo-self-Fenton catalyst for pollutant removal
abstract: Floatable S-scheme heterojunction-integrated polyacrylic acid hydrogels as photo-self-Fenton catalysts showed enhanced photocatalytic activity for chloroquine phosphate degradation by boosting hydroxyl radical production.

title: Photocatalytic upcycling of marble waste into acetic acid by copper sulfide nanoparticles
abstract: Disk-like CuS nanoparticles facilitate the photocatalytic conversion of carbonate-rich waste marble-dust to acetic acid under monochromatic green light, offering a green route for carbon upcycling and waste utilization.

title: Effects of electrolyte, state of charge, and strain rate on the mechanical properties of lithium-ion battery electrodes and separators
abstract: The state of charge, electrolyte environment, and strain rate synergistically govern the mechanical integrity of lithium-ion battery electrodes, revealing complex solid–liquid interactions vital for battery safety and durability.

title: Porphyrin-based heterojunction photocatalysts: design principles and applications in solar-to-chemical energy conversion
abstract: As global climate change and energy crises continue to escalate, the development of efficient solar energy conversion technologies has become imperative for addressing pressing environmental and energy-related challenges.

title: Delineating the kinetic limitations of Mn 2+/3+ redox in LiMn x Fe 1− x PO 4 cathodes for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Novel operando impedance spectroscopy methods conducted on various LiMn x Fe 1− x PO 4 (LMFP) cathodes reveal the asymmetric behavior of the Mn 2+/3+ redox couple. Significant impedance growth during Mn 2+ oxidation in high-Mn LMFP limits its performance.

title: Regeneration of spent NCM622: reconstructing the rich lattice oxygen surface for enhanced stability
abstract: Assisted by CaO powder, the regenerated cathodes are successfully achieved through the removal of CaF 2 and deliver a considerable capacity and excellent rate performance.

title: Single-atom N–Co–O charge-transfer bridges in SnNb 2 O 6 :Co/g-C 3 N 4 :Co heterojunctions for improved photocatalytic CO 2 reduction performance
abstract: Co single atoms not only accelerate photogenerated charge transfer through N–Co–O charge transfer bridge but also have multiple synergistic effects with type II heterostructures.

title: A mechanically robust polyurethane elastomer with excellent crack tolerance, recyclability and weathering resistance via microphase dynamic ordering
abstract: This study explores polyurethane synthesis to promote mechanical robustness and weathering resistance through ordered hard domains, mechano-responsive crystallization and a high degree of H-bond crosslinking.

title: Highly sensitive and stretchable strain sensor based on wrinkles of a twisted multilayer graphene
abstract: CVD-grown twisted multilayer graphene (tMLG) hosts network of wrinkles capable of reversible structural changes with external strain, causing enormous changes in device resistance, thereby serving as a highly sensitive, stretchable strain sensor.

title: Measuring the buried interphase between solid electrolytes and lithium metal using neutrons
abstract: Neutrons allow one to probe interfaces in next generation high-energy batteries including solid-state Li metal batteries. Understanding these interfaces is key to enabling these batteries to reach their potential.

title: Breaking the electronic distribution symmetry at Fe–N 4 sites in iron phthalocyanines enhances CO 2 electrochemical reduction
abstract: The local electronic state symmetry of the Fe atom can be precisely tuned by axial N coordination to enhance CO 2 adsorption.

title: Tailoring the electronic structure of O3-type layered oxide cathodes to achieve long-cycle life and high-rate performance sodium-ion batteries
abstract: By optimizing the electronic structure, the LCNFM electrode exhibits long-cycle life and high-rate performance. This approach effectively inhibits the irreversible O3–P3 phase transition during cycling by suppressing the Jahn–Teller effect of Mn 3+ .

title: Progress on polymer-based materials and composites for humidity sensor applications: from materials aspects to sensor performances
abstract: Polymers and their composites are efficient sensing materials for humidity sensor applications because of their favorable properties such as water ad/absorption, flexibility, and hygroscopicity.

title: Strategies to mitigate Sr segregation of the LSCF oxygen electrode for solid oxide cells
abstract: Addressing the Sr segregation in LSCF oxygen electrode to enhance the long-term stability is essential for solid oxide cells (SOCs) operating in both fuel cell and electrolysis cell modes to realize large-scale energy storage.

title: Advances in metal–organic framework-based materials for sustainable energy solutions
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances in using MOFs and their derivatives for energy storage, focusing on MOF-derived carbon heterostructures and metal compound modifications to enhance performance through material and structural optimization.

title: A multifunctional ionic liquid additive providing a solvation structure and electrostatic shielding layer for highly stable aqueous zinc ion batteries
abstract: Multifunctional ionic liquid additive 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate enhances AZIB stability by modifying Zn 2+ solvation and improving deposition via electrostatic shielding, achieving 92.7% retention at 10 A g −1 after 3000 cycles.

title: Recent advances in hybrid supercapacitors: a review of high performance materials and scalable fabrication techniques
abstract: A comprehensive review highlighting recent advancements in high-performance materials and scalable fabrication techniques for next-generation hybrid supercapacitors.

title: Recent advances in electrocatalytic reduction of nitrate to ammonia: current challenges, resolving strategies, and future perspectives
abstract: This review clearly elucidates the lineage of the development of NO 3 − RR from the theoretical mechanism to practical reactions and deepens the understanding of NO 3 − RR, pointing out the direction for the advanced design of NO 3 − RR electrocatalysts.

title: Progress in crystalline silicon heterojunction solar cells
abstract: Key materials and device structures of crystalline silicon heterojunction solar cells.

title: Sulfur element achieves rapid and stable migration of Li + in oxide cathode materials
abstract: Higher energy density can be obtained by increasing the charging cut-off voltage of Ni-rich materials to meet the range requirements of electric vehicles.

title: Mechanistic insights into superionic thioarsenate argyrodite solid electrolytes via machine learning interatomic potentials
abstract: Machine learning interatomic potentials reveal that optimal anion disorder boosts Li-ion conductivity beyond 20 mS cm −1 in thioarsenate argyrodite solid electrolytes.

title: Linker polarization engineering in covalent organic frameworks enabling metal-free electrocatalysts for high-efficiency oxygen reduction
abstract: The high selectivity of hydrogen peroxide was achieved by adjusting the polarity of the connecting segment, and in situ characterized by SG/TC-SECM.

title: Unveiling the crystallographic effect of Li(Ni 0.90 Co 0.09 Al 0.01 )O 2 with a high Ni content on high capacity generation in Li-ion batteries
abstract: Li(Ni 0.90 Co 0.09 Al 0.01 )O 2 is a cathode material with high capacity. However, the high capacity generation leads large anisotropic strains and cracks. Therefore, the chemo-mechanical properties is crucial for enhancing the battery performance.

title: Physics-guided machine learning of excited-state properties for the design of high-performance TADF emitters
abstract: Physics-informed ML accurately predicts singlet–triplet gaps (Δ E ST ) and oscillator strengths ( f ), outperforming QM/ML baselines. Interpretable models reveal key factors and accelerate high-throughput discovery of TADF emitters for OLEDs.

title: Data-driven design and experimental validation of high-precision Ni–Co bimetallic compound-based pseudocapacitor models
abstract: A bidirectional database for Ni–Co supercapacitors was built using literature-derived descriptors. ML models (MLP) were developed and experimentally validated, confirming predictive accuracy and offering a scalable data-driven design framework.

title: Mechanical and electrical changes in electrochemically active polyimide binders for Li-ion batteries
abstract: A study on polyimide binder for Li ion batteries, showing active lithiation/delithiation with first cycle capacity loss, formation of dendrite-like morphology, elastic modulus increase, and overall electronic resistivity increase during lithiation.

title: MoO x -based high-density nanoarrays on a substrate via smart anodizing as novel 3D electrodes for nano-energy applications
abstract: Arrays of multi-dimensional MoO x -based semiconducting nanostructures, spatially separated and vertically aligned on a substrate, were synthesized via self-organized electrochemical anodizing for application in emerging on-chip pseudocapacitors.

title: Effective inhibition of Zn dendrites in Zn–air batteries through electrolyte engineering
abstract: A novel electrolyte engineering approach using zinc citrate as an electrolyte additive is introduced, which can significantly enhance the performance and lifespan of ZABs.

title: Biomass carbon enabled charge transport network optimization in layered double hydroxides for high-rate energy storage
abstract: This work demonstrates a charge transfer optimization strategy of LDHs by a bifunctional biomass carbon for high-rate hybrid supercapacitors and batteries.

title: An all-fluorinated electrolyte based on a fluorinated cosolvent toward a high-voltage LRMO cathode
abstract: The designed all-fluorinated electrolyte greatly enhanced the stability of the cathode electrolyte interphase and solid electrolyte interphase.

title: d-Band center modulation and surface Pt–O bonding promoted Pt nanoparticles with high performance for stable pH-universal hydrogen evolution at ampere-level current densities
abstract: A strategy of d-band center modulation and surface Pt–O bonding through CNT support and ultrathin carbon coating is used to develop Pt nanoparticles for the pH-universal HER. The obtained catalyst shows high performance for overall water splitting.

title: High-resolution in situ photo-irradiation MAS NMR: application to the UV-polymerization of n -butyl acrylate
abstract: An in situ irradiation MAS NMR setup is demonstrated via high-resolution 1 H and 13 C NMR of the UV-polymerization of n -butyl acrylate.

title: Exploring metal halide perovskites as active architectures in energy storage systems
abstract: Metal halide perovskites are expanding beyond photovoltaics, showing promise in lithium-ion batteries, supercapacitors, and photo-induced energy storage systems through their unique properties.

title: Research on the enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen production performance of anatase synergistically co-doped with aluminum, zinc and cerium
abstract: This innovation lowers transfer impedance and reduces recombination losses. The optimized catalyst shows high activity under light, enabling enhanced directional transport in doped photocatalytic systems.

title: Spin and ferroelectric-driven photocatalysts for efficient CO 2 reduction: material design and future perspectives
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances in spin- and ferroelectric-driven photocatalysts, highlighting material design strategies that control electron polarization to promote charge separation and boost CO 2 photoreduction efficiency.

title: Local charge polarization by introducing a cyanamide group and sulfur dopant with accelerated exciton dissociation and promotion of charge separation for improving CO 2 photoreduction performance
abstract: The –C≡N group and a S dopant were introduced into crystalline GCN to reduce the exciton binding energy ( E b ) for enhancing exciton dissociation efficiency and promoting charge transfer dynamics, realizing efficient photocatalytic CO 2 reduction.

title: Guest-induced self-adaptive pore dynamic behaviour of 2D covalent organic frameworks
abstract: Due to framework dynamics, a mesoporous two-dimensional (2D) covalent organic framework (COF) exhibits solvent-induced pore breathing behaviour, arising from changes in the vibrations of sp–sp 2 carbons.

title: Electrochemically chlorinated graphene for ultrafast NO 2 detection at room temperature
abstract: Chlorinated graphene (Cl-Gr) was synthesized by a non-toxic and facile electrochemical process. The Cl-Gr gas sensor demonstrates ultrafast NO 2 detection and rapid recovery at room temperature, which is consistent with DFT analysis.

title: Tuning the local chemistry of SPAN to realize the development of room-temperature sodium–sulfur pouch cells
abstract: The viability of RT-Na/S batteries is limited due to the sluggish kinetics of the cathode. Cathode additives, e.g. , ZnS, can accelerate the reactions by inherently increasing the electronic and ionic conduction of Na + within the SPAN matrix.

title: Role of magnetic exchange in catalytic spin effects: insights from nitrite reduction on Co 3 O 4
abstract: We found spin effects especially the magnetic exchange of the substrate have a huge impact on NO 3 RR performance by DFT calculation, and revealed the mechanism of enhanced NO 3 RR activity of low-spin doped Co 3 O 4 from the perspective of spin catalysis.

title: High-energy gamma photon engineering of g-C 3 N 4 : low dose rate radiation as a metal-free modification enhancing solar-driven photoactivity
abstract: Gamma-induced modification of graphitic carbon nitride (g-C 3 N 4 ) has emerged as a novel strategy for enhancing its photocatalytic performance.

title: A liquid-like quasi-solid polymer electrolyte for high-performance sodium metal batteries
abstract: A blend QSPE exhibits a liquid-like ionic conductivity of 1.01 mS cm −1 at RT, with solid-like robustness for dendrite suppression. This dual functionality enables reversible cycling up to 5C, demonstrating its potential for advanced SMB applications.

title: Impact of nitrogen configuration on the anchoring mechanism of platinum nanoparticles on carbon substrates: synergistic modification for hydrogen evolution in acidic and alkaline media
abstract: Pyridinic-N, pyrrolic-N, and graphitic-N fully exploit their distinct roles, amplify their collective influence and maximize the synergistic interaction between Pt and NC, ultimately leading to exceptional HER performance over a broad pH range.

title: In situ generated bilayer functional coatings on manganese-rich LiMn 0.84 Fe 0.15 Mg 0.01 PO 4 for high-rate lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The dual coatings with different functional roles synergistically improve electronic conductivity and lithium-ion diffusion coefficient, while effectively inhibiting manganese dissolution.

title: Unveiling the roles of phase and nanostructure in the electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution activity of cobalt phosphide nanoparticles
abstract: Phase- and nanostructure-controlled synthesis of cobalt phosphides provides insight into their formation mechanism and the roles of phase, composition, and nanostructure in electrocatalytic HER performance.

title: Data-driven approach to the performance of SnSe-based thermoelectric materials
abstract: An interpretable machine learning approach is used to optimize SnSe-based thermoelectric materials, identifying key doping strategies and offering data-driven insights for future material design.

title: Interface M–O–Fe (M = Ru, Rh, Pd, Ag) bonding excitation stabilized bifunctional electrocatalysts for water splitting
abstract: Bifunctional M/Fe 2 O 3 (M = Ru, Rh, Pd, Ag) catalysts with interfacial M–O–Fe bridge bonds are designed for water splitting.

title: Thermally-induced agglomeration tailors the stability of Pt SAs on TiO 2 and use in photocatalytic H 2 generation
abstract: Thermally-induced agglomeration of Pt single atoms (SAs) on anatase TiO 2 thin films is exploited for tuning their stability and activity in photocatalytic H 2 generation.

title: Plasma-assisted nitrogen-doped NiHf nanoalloy for efficient seawater electrolysis
abstract: The N-doped NiHf alloy was successfully synthesized through plasma, demonstrating excellent electrocatalytic performance toward the hydrogen evolution reaction in alkaline seawater.

title: A Ni single-atom catalyst for advanced environmental disinfection based on electrochemical production of hydrogen peroxide
abstract: This work designed a Ni single-atom catalyst with a Ni–N 4 –O structure for 2e − ORR to produce H 2 O 2 for advanced environmental disinfection.

title: Enhancing lithium metal battery safety and performance: a thermally stable poly(aryl ether benzimidazole) separator with 2D-functionalized boron nitride for 3000 hours lithium plating/stripping
abstract: The Li/OPBI@f-BN/Li cell maintained long-term stability for 3000 hours, and the cells assembled with the OPBI@f-BN separator achieved a high specific capacity of 153.98 mA h g −1 .

title: Controllable ion-release separators: in situ engineering of zincophilic/hydrophobic interfaces and solvation regulation for durable Zn metal anodes
abstract: The separator controls ion release, thus inhibiting Zn anode dendrite formation and hydrogen evolution via in situ interface and electrolyte regulation.

title: Efficient photocatalytic nitrogen reduction by MoS 2 doped with transition metal and containing sulfur vacancies: enhanced nitrogen activation and inhibition of water decomposition
abstract: Effectively enhancing nitrogen activation and inhibiting water splitting reactions are key factors in photocatalytic nitrogen reduction.

title: Recent advances in liquid metals as electrodes, electrolytes and interface stabilizers for lithium batteries and beyond
abstract: This review presents the recent advances in liquid metals (LMs) as key electrodes, electrolyte materials, and interface stabilizers for lithium and other batteries. The remaining challenges and future opportunities are also illustrated.

title: Upcycling of electric arc furnace dust into ZnO–Fe 3 O 4 nanocomposites for high-performance supercapacitor applications
abstract: ZnO–Fe 3 O 4 obtained from electric arc furnace dust revealed the following properties as an asymmetric supercapacitor; C s = 35.2 mF cm −2 , E = 25.03 μWh cm −2 , P = 430.81 μW cm −2 , and 81% capacitance retention after 7000 cycles.

title: Multifunctional polyurethane elastomers with high mechanical robustness and exceptional crack tolerance performance based on bi-incompatible soft segments and dynamic coordination bonds
abstract: Elastomers with high strength and super toughness are widely used in aerospace, flexible electronics, biomedical, and other fields.

title: ZnSe/V 2 C MXene Schottky junction: mechanism of interface barrier promoting carrier directional separation and enhancing photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: ZnSe nanoparticles are grown in situ on the surface of V 2 C MXene to construct Schottky junctions. The interface barrier reduces the recombination of the carrier and improves the hydrogen production performance of the composite catalyst.

title: Optimizing discharge product morphology with hetero-nanostructured NiCoP/NiCo 2 O 4 for enhanced sustainability in Li–O 2 battery performance
abstract: Dual-phase electrocatalysts help to enhance Li–O 2 battery performances with a sheet-like Li 2 O 2 morphology.

title: Enhanced electrochemical performance of CTAB-assisted Zn-doped perovskite NiMnO 3 nanocrystals on reduced graphene oxide for supercapacitors and oxygen evolution reactions
abstract: This paper introduces a novel chemical reduction synthesis method for preparing bimetallic Zn-doped perovskite NiMnO 3 ultrafine nanocrystals, which are further supported on reduced graphene oxide (rGO).

title: Sustainable preparation of waste heavy bio-oil based 3D porous carbon materials via in situ binary template activation and its application in zinc-ion hybrid capacitors
abstract: Eco-friendly and efficient fabrication of hierarchically 3D porous carbon from waste biomass-derived heavy oil residues for enhanced electrochemical performance and resource recycling.

title: Supramolecular nanobelts assembled from tri-urea-armed thiophene derivatives as efficient metal-free photocatalysts for hydrogen evolution
abstract: Nanobelt photocatalysts assembled from organic small-molecule monomers facilitate photo-generated charge separation and transfer, enhancing photocatalytic hydrogen evolution performance.

title: Metal–organic frameworks for photocatalytic CO 2 conversion: bridging fundamental insights to practical solutions
abstract: Photocatalytic conversion of CO 2 into value-added fuels and chemicals via light-driven redox reactions, highlighting the sustainable pathways for greenhouse gas mitigation and renewable energy production.

title: Shape memory alloy-reinforced self-healing aluminum composites with in situ low melting point healing phase
abstract: A novel synergistic self-healing design for SMA-reinforced aluminum composites incorporating an in situ low-melting-point tin (Sn) phase offers a scalable, capsule-free approach for semi-autonomous crack repair.

title: Prediction of discharge performance of hard carbon materials for high-performance sodium-ion batteries based on machine learning
abstract: The development of high-performance hard carbon anodes is crucial for enhancing the performance of sodium-ion batteries (SIBs).

title: Advanced n-type ionic hydrogel with simultaneously enhanced thermoelectric and mechanical performances via the synergy of hydrophilic and hydrophobic polymers
abstract: Through the synergy of hydrophilic and hydrophobic polymers, the TE performance is increased by 113 800%, accompanied by better mechanical properties.

title: Enhancing oxygen activation toward promoted photocatalytic oxidation of methane to liquid oxygenates with ReO 2 @TiO 2 : the regulation of oxygen affinity
abstract: The ReO 2 @TiO 2 catalyst has been designed and applied in photocatalytic methane oxidation to liquid oxygenates.

title: From structure to electrochemistry: the influence of transition metal ordering on Na + /vacancy orderings in P2-type Na x MO 2 cathode materials for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: In this work, the origin of Na + /vacancy orderings in P2-type Na x Ni y Mn 1− y O 2 is investigated. A general guideline to suppress Na + /vacancy orderings in P2-type Na x MO 2 cathode active materials is postulated.

title: Modulating charge transfer pathway via halide tuning of layered Bi-oxyhalides on an MOF-derived CuO nanorod photocathode
abstract: Modulation of charge transfer pathways by varying halides in bismuth oxyhalides over 1D CuO nanorods derived from 3D metal–organic framework.

title: Size-confined Co nanoparticles embedded in ultrathin carbon nanosheets for enhanced oxygen electrocatalysis in Zn–air batteries
abstract: This work demonstrates a composite strategy to modify the MOF structure for the purpose of regulating the size and accessibility of active sites in derived Co@N-CNSs catalyst, enabling enhanced oxygen electrocatalysis in Zn–air batteries.

title: Solid-state organic electrochemical transistors (OECTs) based on gel electrolytes for biosensors and bioelectronics
abstract: A minireview focusing on OECTs for biosensors and bioelectronics applications, highlighting gel electrolytes as effective strategies for enhancing flexibility, adhesiveness, self-healing ability, biocompatibility, and long-term stability of OECTs.

title: Ultra-stable solid-state lithium metal batteries with ferroelectric oxide-enhanced PVDF-based hybrid solid electrolytes
abstract: PVDF-based electrolyte modified with Li-garnet and ferroelectric BaTiO 3 effectively suppresses dendrite growth and enables superior cycling stability of solid-state lithium batteries.

title: Simultaneously enhanced benzene adsorption and benzene/cyclohexane separation via decorating the pore environment of metal–organic frameworks
abstract: Functionalization of a stable MOF with fluorine and azo functional groups can simultaneously enhance the capabilities for benzene adsorption and benzene/cyclohexane separation.

title: Conductive metal–organic-framework enhanced highly efficient electrocatalytic performance of hollow CoNiFe-LDH nanocages for the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Conductive metal–organic framework modified hollow CoNiFe-LDH nanocages (labeled as CoNiFe–HHTP) were prepared via a mild wet-chemical route. The CoNiFe–HHTP exhibited excellent electrocatalytic OER performance.

title: A heteroepitaxial interface of Pt//CeO 2 nanoparticles for enhanced catalysis of the oxygen reduction reaction (ORR)
abstract: Heteroepitaxially aligned metal-oxide nanocomposites, Pt//CeO 2 , are successfully formed on graphene nanoplatelets by promoting nanophase separation of Pt 5 Ce nanocrystals.

title: Covalent organic framework spherical nanofibers bearing carbon quantum dots for boosting photocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: Carbon quantum dot-functionalized covalent organic framework spherical nanofibers prepared through the modulation approach exhibit an excellent photocatalytic performance in hydrogen production.

title: Optimization of the MoO X buffer layer for single-junction and four-terminal perovskite–silicon tandem solar cells
abstract: An optimization of the vapor deposition technique for MoO X enabled efficiencies of 20.5% for semitransparent perovskite solar cells and 28.9% for perovskite–silicon tandem cells.

title: Low-cost, large-area carbon electrode perovskite solar cells
abstract: The application of carbon electrodes, as an interface and a terminal electrode in carbon-based perovskite solar cells (C-PSCs).

title: Hydrophobic deep eutectic solvent-based hydrophobic polymer adhesive with on-demand detachability and strong broad-spectrum adhesion in air/aquatic environments
abstract: Novel hydrophobic polymer adhesive via one-step copolymerization in hydrophobic deep eutectic solvents offers strong broad-spectrum adhesion, environmental tolerance, and reversible temperature-dependent bonding for durable air and underwater use.

title: In situ growth of sulfur-incorporating NiCo-LDH for a high-performance hybrid all-solid-state supercapacitor
abstract: S-incorporating NiCo-LDHs with different S% are directly grown on Ni-foam as efficient electrodes for supercapacitors. The formation of metal sulfite in the resulting electrode material is found to increase the energy storage performance.

title: Sulfur doping activated metal–support interaction drives Pt nanoparticles to achieve acid–base hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: The sulfur doping strategy activates the interfacial effect, thereby promoting Pt NPs to attain efficient acid–base hydrogen evolution.

title: Geometrical engineering of nearly fully cation-selective 2D angstrom-scale ionic diode membranes for highly efficient osmotic energy conversion
abstract: Remarkable promotion of osmotic energy conversion performance has been demonstrated by geometrical engineering of a 2D MXene/aramid nanofiber (MXA) ionic diode membrane with in-plane angstrom-scale channels.

title: Calcination-driven enhancement of LAGP for high-performance solid-state lithium metal batteries
abstract: Tailored calcination and sintering processes influence the microstructure and ionic conductivity of LAGP electrolytes. Optimized LAGP_700 shows improved conductivity and cycling performance.

title: Enabling ionic transport in Li 3 AlP 2 : the roles of defects and disorder
abstract: Lithium metal phosphides are an emerging class of solid electrolytes. By introducing defects and disorder into the Li 3(1− x ) AlP 2 system, enhanced ionic conductivity was observed.

title: Epitaxial domain of Pd oxide with a superperiodic structure formed on Sr 3 Ti 2 O 7 : a promising environmental catalyst for exhaust gas purification
abstract: Pd oxide, which is epitaxially bonded to the ab surface of Sr 3 Ti 2 O 7 , exhibits cyclic structural fluctuations and excellent catalytic activity for automotive exhaust gas purification.

title: Surface structure evolution of bimetallic nickel tungsten nitride (Ni 2 W 3 N) for high performance hydrogen evolution
abstract: Ni 2 W 3 N/NF is prepared via a one-step annealing, showing excellent HER performance in alkaline solution. Surface transformation to Ni(OH) 2 enhances water dissociation, lowering overpotential to 46.5 mV at 50 mA cm −2 , revealed by in situ Raman and DFT.

title: Advances and industrialization of LiFePO 4 cathodes in electric vehicles: challenges, innovations, and future directions
abstract: Evolution, research focus, industrialization and recovery techniques of LiFePO 4 cathodes are reviewed, highlighting their critical role in meeting energy demands, especially in EVs.

title: Collaborative reconstruction of FeOOH/FeNiCo-LDH heterogeneous nanosheets for enhancing anion exchange membrane seawater electrolysis
abstract: FeOOH/FeNiCo-LDH/HCNC, by virtue of the reconstruction-generated highly active and stable FeNiCoOOH together with the conductive honeycomb-channel carbon, exhibits superior OER performance for highly-stable industrial-level seawater electrolysis.

title: Recent advances in Ni-based catalysts for hybrid CO 2 electrolysis
abstract: The utilization of nickel-based catalysts in hybrid CO 2 electrolysis systems enhances the efficiency of CO 2 reduction by coupling low-energy alternative oxidation reactions, thereby offering an innovative route towards a sustainable carbon economy.

title: Adaptive liquid-like surface coatings: balancing mechanical strength, optical clarity, and dynamic liquid repellency
abstract: This study introduces a scalable dynamic phase separation strategy to fabricate silicone based liquid-like coatings with ultralow PDMS content.

title: Single-ion conductor gel polymer electrolytes enabling an anionic polymer-induced solid electrolyte interphase for dendrite-free lithium-metal batteries
abstract: A novel 3D crosslinked single-ion conductor gel polymer electrolyte to construct an anionic polymer-induced SEI can achieve low interfacial impedance, a stable electrolyte/electrode interface, and effective suppression of lithium dendrite growth.

title: Nanostructured Fe 2 O 3 /Cu x O heterojunction for enhanced solar redox flow battery performance
abstract: An α-Fe₂O₃/CuxO p–n junction SRFB achieves 0.46 mA cm⁻² photocurrent density, >0.35% STC efficiency, and 0.18% SOEE, leveraging earth-abundant, scalable materials for enhanced charge separation, electrochemical activity, and practical deployment.

title: Enhanced capacity of aluminum-ion batteries by adjusting the average pore size of the porous carbon cathode
abstract: Porous carbon materials exhibit unique performance as cathodes for aluminum-ion batteries (AIBs), with capacity primarily influenced by average pore size, while the effect of specific surface area is relatively limited.

title: Autonomously adhesive and anti-freezing MXene-organohydrogel bionic sensor for wearable interfaces and silent speech recognition
abstract: An autonomous adhesion MXene-organohydrogel sensor enables real-time monitoring and silent speech recognition for wearable electronics in diverse environments.

title: Iron-based polyanionic cathodes for sustainable sodium-ion batteries
abstract: Sodium-ion batteries (SIBs) have emerged as a compelling alternative to lithium-ion batteries, driven by the abundance of raw materials and lower costs.

title: Advancing paper-based sensors with MXenes and MOFs: exploring cutting-edge innovations
abstract: Recent advancements pertaining to the applications of MXenes and MOFs in paper-based sensors are discussed, focusing on challenges and future perspectives.

title: Engineering oxygen vacancies in TiO 2 for isotope-selective catalysis: a strategy for hydrogen isotope separation
abstract: For the key D/T separation challenge in the nuclear fuel cycle, the oxygen vacancy-tailored TiO 2− x photocatalyst achieved a high separation factor of 5.31, offering strong support to existing methods.

title: Electrosynthesis for biomass refining and green H 2 by a Ni(OH) 2 –V 2 O 3 heterostructure: tailoring oxygen vacancies and electronic interactions
abstract: The Ni(OH) 2 –V 2 O 3 heterostructured catalyst introduces oxygen vacancies that improve charge transfer and boost catalytic performance for HMFOR and HER, which achieves a high FDCA yield of 97.5%, generating H 2 with nearly 100% faradaic efficiency.

title: Construction of a Z-scheme heterojunction with camellia-like MnCo 2 O 4 /TiO 2 for photogenerated cathodic protection
abstract: A photogenerated cathodic protection system powered by solar energy has been proposed to reduce metal corrosion in the ocean.

title: Engineered 3D copper ferrite/kaolinite/polypyrrole carbon black hydrogels for efficient persulfate activation in tetracycline degradation: kinetics, mechanistic insights, toxicity, and green metrics
abstract: This study develops reusable CKP hydrogels (CuFe 2 O 4 /kaolinite/polypyrrole–carbon black/CMC), achieving 98% tetracycline degradation in 15 min via LED-persulfate activation.

title: Engineering the CoSe 2 phase transition in a Br-induced confined space for high-performance electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: The controlled transition from o-CoSe 2 to the c-CoSe 2 crystalline phase was achieved by using different levels of Br doping strategies to modulate the microwave absorption properties.

title: Co 2 (OH) 3 Cl/WC for improved high-energy aqueous asymmetric supercapacitors
abstract: Modified cobalt hydroxide structures are promising new energy storage materials that offer many advantages.

title: Prediction of structural stability and explosive performance of N-rich Mo–N compounds under high pressures
abstract: High-energy-density materials (HEDMs) can release enormous amounts of energy through redox reactions, exhibiting high stability, low sensitivity, and environmental friendliness, which are crucial for national defense and energy storage applications.

title: High toluene uptake at a trace concentration in a novel gallium-based metal–organic framework
abstract: A novel gallium-based MOF, termed Ga-BPT SC (single crystal) and Ga-BPT P (powder), was synthesized.

title: Morphology engineering of 2D Zn-catalysts to wrinkled particles using NaCl microcrystals: enhanced recyclability for the synthesis of poly(caprolactone)s
abstract: Wrinkled Zn-gallate nanoparticles were engineered using micron-sized NaCl crystals, showing enhanced recyclability in the ring-opening polymerization of ε-caprolactone to poly(caprolactone).

title: A sulfonate ligand hybrid ZIF-8 modified separator achieved high-performance Li–S batteries
abstract: A novel sulfonated metal–organic framework material (ZIF-8-SO 3 H) enhances Li–S battery performance through electrostatic interactions and optimized Li + transport.

title: Structure–property relationship in phenothiazine-based hypercrosslinked organic electrode materials through porosity adjustment
abstract: Phenothiazine-based hypercrosslinked p-type porous polymers were synthesized via knitting polymerization, with increasing benzene as co-monomer to systematically study the porosity–electrochemical performance relationship.

title: Reactive hot-pressed Na 3.4 Zr 2 Si 2.4 P 0.6 O 12 : nanoscale grains, glass-free microstructure, high total conductivity, enhanced chemical stability
abstract: Hot-pressing of amorphous Na 3.4 Zr 2 Si 2.4 P 0.6 O 12 powders at 1225 °C followed by annealing for 30 min resulted in a total conductivity of 7.3 mS cm −1 compared to 3 to 5 mS cm −1 for sintered Na 3.4 Zr 2 Si 2.4 P 0.6 O 12 .

title: Accelerating the exploration of novel perovskite-structured metal borohydrides with enhanced dehydrogenation performance through machine learning
abstract: Perovskite-type metal borohydrides are studied using machine learning (ML) with metal halides as analogs to address data scarcity. ML predicts that KMn(BH 4 ) 3 has a dehydrogenation temperature of 207.5 °C, highlighting its hydrogen storage potential.

title: Coordination environment design for enhanced catalytic performance of diamond-based nonmetallic and single-atom catalysts
abstract: By adjusting the ratio of B/N atoms and anchoring Sc atoms, the coordination environment is altered, effectively modulating the electronic structure of Sc@N 4− x B x and thus enhancing the electrocatalytic activity.

title: Alkali-activated materials from steel industry slags: optimization of prewashing and its effect on zinc adsorption and regeneration
abstract: Interest in alkali-activated materials (AAMs) has increased because of their effectiveness as adsorbents and their low cost.

title: Practice and tactics in the construction of novel degradable plastics at polymer and molecular levels
abstract: The technology for achieving controlled degradation of plastics at the polymer and molecular levels has been comprehensively reviewed.

title: Synergistic strategy of composition design and multiscale structure regulation for high energy storage performance of PLZT under low electric fields
abstract: This work achieves both high W rec (>4 J cm −3 ) and high η (>85%) under low electric fields (190 kV cm −1 ), outperforming other similar works.

title: Linear fused-ring donor polymer with suppressed aggregation and a deep HOMO level for efficient organic solar cells
abstract: A fused-ring BTST acceptor enables a linear backbone with a deep HOMO and wide bandgap, suppressing aggregation and enhancing charge transport, yielding high-efficiency organic solar cells.

title: Interfacial synergy between Cu nanoclusters and oxygen vacancies on CeO 2 for enhanced selective photoreduction of CO 2 to C 2 H 4
abstract: The oxygen vacancies in Cu@CeO 2 -OVs significantly increase the interfacial electron density, promote electron transfer and intermediate stability, thereby reducing the C–C coupling energy barrier and achieving highly selective C 2 H 4 generation.

title: Sustainable high-pressure homogenization of hexagonal boron nitride for triboelectric nanogenerators: advancing self-powered environmental monitoring in portable electronics
abstract: This work presents high-pressure homogenization method to develop 2D hBN flakes for triboelectric nanogenerators (TENG), achieving ∼135 V V oc , ∼17.0 μA I sc and a power density of 18 W cm −2 , enabling humidity monitoring and portable device operation.

title: Cobalt vanadium bimetallic oxide nanoplates modified with RuO 2 for efficient electrocatalytic overall water splitting
abstract: The work reports porous RuO 2 -modified cobalt-vanadium bimetallic oxide for water splitting. The catalyst exhibits efficient bifunctional activity in 1 M KOH, achieving 10 mA cm −2 at overpotentials of 49 and 220 mV for the HER and OER, respectively.

title: Manganese-based catalysts for the catalytic oxidation of chlorinated volatile organic compounds (CVOCs): critical properties, deactivation mechanisms, and design strategies
abstract: This review summarizes the characteristics, deactivation pathways, and design strategies of Mn-based catalysts for the catalytic oxidation of CVOCs. The objective is to provide insights for the development of highly efficient Mn-based catalysts.

title: MXene/nanocellulose/carbon sphere composite films with a multistage “egg-box” structure for electromagnetic-interference shielding and pressure sensors
abstract: With advances in smart wearable electronics, there is a substantial demand for flexible electronic skins with electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding.

title: 2D/2D W-MoSe 2 @Ti 3 C 2 MXene heterostructure harnesses high-rate lithium–oxygen batteries: momentous roles of high-valence metal sites and interfacial bridge-oxygen bonding
abstract: Synergistic high-valence W sites and interfacial bridge-oxygen bonding in W-MoSe 2 @MXene harness high-rate lithium–oxygen batteries.

title: Simultaneously improved reversibility and hydrogen production of solid oxide cells through infiltrating air electrode
abstract: Infiltration of SrFe 2 O 4− d into LSM/YSZ air electrode significantly mitigates the catastrophic delamination and improves reversibility upon the electrochemical operation due to the increased ion conductivity of the Fe-doped LSM mixed conductor.

title: Highly oxidative GaN:ZnO@α-Ga 2 O 3 heterostructure as a visible-light-driven, round-the-clock photocatalyst for dye degradation and disinfection
abstract: In a visible-light-driven GaN:ZnO@α-Ga 2 O 3 heterostructure, the GaN:ZnO core absorbed O 2 and stored photo-electrons at V O sites to form ·O 2 − , while the α-Ga 2 O 3 shell absorbed H 2 O/O 2 at V O and trapped photo-holes at V Ga to generate ·OH/ 1 O 2 .

title: A facile method to construct proton exchange membranes based on metal organic frameworks decorating binary polymer nanofibers
abstract: Nanofibers are believed to effectively conduct protons by regulating proton conduction pathways in proton exchange membranes.

title: A recyclable, reshapable and UV-curable polybenzoxazine vitrimer enabling closed-loop 3D printing applications
abstract: The additive manufacturing of a bio-based, UV active, recyclable and reshapable polybenzoxazine is demonstrated.

title: Dynamic response crosslinking units endow polyurethane elastomers with synergistic mechanical robustness, multiphase pH sensing capability, and recyclability for sustainable environmental monitoring
abstract: By incorporating tetra( p -hydroxyphenyl)ethylene into the polyurethane matrix via a dynamic crosslinking strategy, the TTPU-3 material achieves high toughness, on-demand activation, and pH responsiveness driven by protonation and deprotonation.

title: Deciphering the role of van der Waals heterostructures in enhancing layered perovskite anodes for high-performance lithium-ion batteries
abstract: A layered perovskite with a van der Waals heterostructure enables superior lithium storage performance.

title: Facile modification of nylon filter via vacuum coating with chitosan@MCM-41/GO for efficient oily wastewater treatment
abstract: We present a superhydrophilic and underwater superoleophobic membrane by functionalizing a nylon filter with a homogeneous emulsion of chitosan-functionalized MCM-41 and graphene oxide via vacuum coating. The membrane exhibited exceptional water permeation flux and oil–water separation efficiency.

title: A MXene-derived tetrathiafulvalene metal–organic framework for ultra-long supercapattery
abstract: A tetrathiafulvalene-MXene-based 2D MOF was synthesized using MXenes as metal precursor, which exhibited a 2D nanosheet morphology, and revealed excellent electrochemical performance, and high specific capacity as a supercapattery.

title: Study on mechanisms of two-step hydrogen sorption in a MgH 2 –TiCrMnFeZr high-entropy alloy composite
abstract: Benefiting from the effective catalytic effect of the TiCrMnFeZr HEA and the protective effect of MgH 2 , the MgH 2 -40 wt% TiCrMnFeZr composite exhibits superior hydrogen sorption in two steps.

title: CoNCNTs anchored with Ru/RuO 2 heterojunction nanostructures as an electrocatalyst for highly effective water splitting
abstract: CoNCNTs anchoring Ru/RuO 2 heterojunction nanostructure can induce Ru site charge density redistribution, balance the hydrolysis dissociation energy and free energy of adsorption of intermediates, thus reducing the reaction energy barrier of HER/OER.

title: First-principles design of a superior electrocatalyst to Pt for hydrogen production in alkaline media
abstract: Elucidation of the key mechanism of the inactive Pt catalyst for hydrogen production in alkaline media and description of two control factors for the catalytic activity of a pseudo-binary Pt–M catalyst.

title: A novel green approach for Au/Pt recovery from lean solutions through photodeposition and alkaline leaching on WO 3 nanoparticles
abstract: Photocatalysis can achieve efficient, environmentally-friendly and cost-effective extraction of trace amounts of noble metals.

title: N/O co-doped hierarchical porous carbon nanosheets with enhanced Zn-ion storage capability and ultrastability
abstract: N/O co-doped hierarchical porous carbon nanosheets were produced from magnolia tree. A capacity of 234.3 mA h g −1 at 0.1 A g −1 with 105.2 mA h g −1 at 50 A g −1 , 181.6 Wh kg −1 energy density and 96.9% ultrastability after 100 000 cycles were gained.

title: Designing covalent organic frameworks for organic catalysis: bridging reticular and molecular chemistry
abstract: This work highlights how covalent organic frameworks can be structurally designed for efficient organic catalysis by combining molecular and reticular chemistry, through backbone tuning, ligands design, metal incorporation, and nanostructure hosting.

title: Defect engineering of Na-induced oxygen vacancies in nickel ferrite for dual-site electrocatalytic water splitting
abstract: A defect-engineered electrode featuring metal–oxygen vacancy active sites and metal–phosphorus active sites, which is capable of directing OER via a dual functional mechanism involving OVSM and AEM.

title: Nanostructured sodium vanadate hydrate as a versatile sodium ion cathode material for use in organic media and for aqueous desalination
abstract: Hydrated sodium vanadate outperforms the dehydrated form in sodium-ion batteries and shows promise for efficient water desalination, offering greener energy storage and clean water solutions.

title: Biodegradable starch-based hydrogel as a multifunctional SEI for ultra-stable and flexible zinc-ion batteries
abstract: The STA/PAAm hydrogel forms a biodegradable SEI that promotes Zn 2+ desolvation and uniform deposition, effectively suppressing dendrites and HER compared to bare Zn.

title: Synergistic construction of defect-rich nanozymes via montmorillonite support loading and iron doping for enhanced peroxidase-like activity
abstract: A defect-rich MoS 2 nanozyme was prepared by MMT loading and Fe doping (FMM). Fe facilitates the formation of S vacancies and MMT promoted exposure. These S vacancies improve substrate interaction and offer both kinetic and thermodynamic advantages.

title: An electronic structure tailored all non-precious Zn-promoted FeCo alloy anchored on a porous N-doped carbon aerogel by thermal reduction for boosting the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: The development of cost-effective and highly active electrocatalysts for the oxygen evolution reaction (OER) is essential for advancing clean energy and energy storage systems.

title: A convenient method of ternary alloys design for CO 2 -to-C 2 H 4 electroreduction
abstract: A universal method integrating catalytic activity, selectivity and stability was proposed to design and screen Cu-based ternary alloy catalysts with better CO 2 RR performance.

title: Unravelling the charge transfer and kinetics of BiVO 4 by Co dopant and NiFeO x co-catalyst for efficient photoelectrochemical water splitting
abstract: The enhanced built-in electric field in CoO x /BiVO 4 and OER active sites in NiFeO x of Co–BiVO 4 /NiFeO x were favourable for charge transfer and elemental OER reactions during PEC water splitting, with a remarkable photocurrent density of 6.42 mA cm −2 .

title: Anchoring intermediate phases via few-layer MoSSe nanosheets in flexible porous carbon fiber for stable lithium ion storage
abstract: Few-layer ultrathin MoSSe nanosheets undergo a phase transition to optimized Se-doped MoS 3 after the initial discharge/charge cycles, leading to stable lithium-ion storage.

title: Inversion symmetry-broken CuBO 2 delafossite through anionic site doping for improved piezoelectric composites with PVDF and its application in nanogenerators and optoelectronic neuromorphic computing
abstract: Self-charging photodetectors drive the development of energy-autonomous electronics for efficient use in memory, portable devices, neuromorphic computing, and optoelectronic systems.

title: Post-synthetic modification of covalent organic frameworks with active manganese centers for electrocatalytic CO 2 reduction in water
abstract: A new COF incorporating terpyridine manganese complexes has been synthesized for evaluation as a CO 2 RR catalyst. This material has been found to be active for the heterogeneous electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide in water.

title: Direct hydrogen production from pure water through contact-electro-catalysis
abstract: PTFE-mediated contact-electro-catalysis enables efficient pure-water splitting into H 2 (1286.6 μmol g −1 h −1 ) and H 2 O 2 , revealing that electrons drive reduction and ·OH is key oxidative species. This work advances clean energy technology via CEC.

title: A new quaternary sphalerite-derivative compound for thermoelectric applications: Cu 7 VSnS 8
abstract: A new sphalerite derivative compound, Cu 7 VSnS 8 , for thermoelectric applications was discovered using the pseudo-binary approach within the “Cu 3 SnS 4 ”–“Cu 4 VS 4 ” system.

title: Cation- and CO 2 -assisted electrochemical synthesis of clean, shape-controlled Cu nanocrystals for selective CO 2 reduction to C 2 + products
abstract: Electrochemical synthesis of ultra-clean copper nanocubes applicable for C 2 -selective CO 2 RR was achieved using Cs + and dissolved CO 2 as shape directing agents with its mechanism probed in situ by ATR-IR spectroscopy.

title: Stabilizing lithium metal anodes with bismuth oxide-coated 3D copper foams via an in situ bifunctional mediation layer
abstract: The BO@CF‖Li half-cells can be stably cycled for 600 cycles with an average coulombic efficiency of 98.8%, enabling dendrite-free lithium deposition and stripping.

title: Dual internal electric field synergistic interface and surface modification enhance photoelectrochemical performance of hematite photoanodes
abstract: An multilayer Fe 2 O 3 photoanode with a dual internal electric field synergistic interface and surface modification was developed to enhance charge transport and separation efficiency.

title: From atoms to algorithms: a review of machine learning approaches to cathode material innovation in zinc-ion batteries
abstract: This review explores how ML accelerates data-driven design and synthesis of Zn-ion batteries, addressing data gaps, interpretability, and validation while emphasizing standardized datasets, explainable AI, and experiment–theory integration.

title: Carbon felt coated with tungsten–bismuth-based oxides as highly active and selective negative electrodes for high power density all-vanadium redox flow batteries
abstract: To develop the VRFB negative electrode, W–Bi-based oxides were solvothermally synthesized on carbon felt (W–Bi@CF). The W–Bi 2 @CF electrode showed superior catalytic activity, selectivity, and stability, with great potential for employment in VRFBs.

title: Tailoring lattice chlorine in perovskite through dual-additive engineering for enhanced photovoltaic performance
abstract: This research investigates chloride residues originating from MACl in perovskite solar cells. A dual-additive strategy employing MACl and DMACl effectively mitigates it in printable mesoscopic perovskite solar cells, leading to enhanced efficiency.

title: Effect of potassium on the structure, characteristics and Cs sorption ability of porous materials in the system Na 2 O–B 2 O 3 –SiO 2 –GeO 2
abstract: Functional porous materials actively used as sorbents and membranes have complex microstructural morphologies that satisfy different requirements for industrial or medical purposes.

title: Enhanced high-temperature capacitive energy storage in PMIA-based dielectric films by tailoring a short-range ordered conformation
abstract: The PMIA chain conformation shifts from long-range disorder to short-range order, enhancing charging and discharging processes for high-temperature energy storage.

title: Achieving active and durable oxygen reduction/evolution reactions on protonic ceramic electrochemical cells with spinel-based air electrodes
abstract: We report a composite electrode of spinel oxide MnCo 1.9 Cu 0.1 O 4 and BaZr 0.8 Y 0.2 O 3 (MCCO : BZY = 9 : 1). The singles with this air electrode delivered a maximum power density of 1.81 W cm −2 and a current density of −3.57 A cm −2 at 1.3 V at 700 °C.

title: Atomic layered NiO/phosphorus-doped MnO 2 p–n junctions: a pathway to high-performance supercapattery devices
abstract: An approach to enhancing energy storage by leveraging the inherent electric field of a p–n junction formed between a p-type NiO atomic layer and n-type phosphorus-doped MnO 2 nanosheets.

title: Electrochemical reactivity of graphene under mechanical strain
abstract: Mechanical strain affects graphene's electrochemical reactivity towards 4-NBD, first decreasing then increasing at higher strains. This nonlinear behavior is explained by Marcus–Gerischer theory and strain-induced shifts in Dirac-point energy.

title: Atomic-scale insights into the oxygen evolution reaction on hematite: a detailed mechanistic investigation of different surface coverages
abstract: The oxygen evolution reaction (OER)—2H 2 O → O 2 + 4H + + 4e − —is the limiting half-cell reaction in the electrochemical water splitting process for the production of green hydrogen.

title: Effect of MXene on Co 3 O 4 –LaVO 4 nanocomposites for synergistic charge transport enhancement and high-performance VARTM assisted solid-state supercapacitor devices using woven carbon fiber
abstract: Optimized Co 3 O 4 –LaVO 4 /MXene nanocomposites deliver high capacitance and energy density. Integrated via VARTM on woven carbon fiber, the solid-state device shows excellent stability, offering a scalable path for structural energy storage.

title: Structural and chemical mapping of Au–Co(OH) 2 electrocatalytic films shows the formation of ordered Au crystallites and the suppression of Co oxidation
authors: 'Rusha Kamar', 'Cameron M. Kewish', 'David J. Paterson', 'Tony Wang', 'Anthony P. O'Mullane', 'Michael M. W. Jones'
abstract: Simultaneous micro X-ray diffraction (XRD) and X-ray absorption near edge spectroscopy (XANES) mapping highlights transition metal oxide catalysts reconstructing both chemically and structurally during the oxygen evolution reaction (OER).

title: Enhancing redox stability of lithium–oxygen batteries via introducing an oxygen pre-coordinated vanadyl phthalocyanine catalyst
abstract: Oxygen-pre-coordinated vanadyl phthalocyanine is introduced as a catalyst for Li–O 2 batteries, stabilizing superoxide radicals and enhancing Li–O 2 reaction. Redox mediation mechanisms and reaction pathways confirm its catalytic efficacy.

title: Intrinsic interlayer electric field induced switched regulatory mechanisms of photovoltaics and photocatalysis in Z-scheme In 2 Se 3 /Sb heterobilayers
abstract: The critical role of the interlayer electric field in the Z-scheme carrier dynamics in heterobilayers is explored, and it induced superior characteristics and switched regulatory mechanisms in photovoltaics and photocatalysis.

title: Binary organic solar cells with efficiency over 20% enabled by solid additives with side-chain halogenation
abstract: An effective side-chain halogenation strategy realizes high-performance OSCs with efficiency over 20%.

title: In situ Mo doping in NiS 2 : enhancing electron density and stimulating electronic conductivity of Cu 3 P–GDY for efficient photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: The electronic conductivity of a catalyst can be enhanced by strategically doping with specific elements.

title: Efficient exciton dissociation in isomeric BINOL-based porous polymers for sacrificial agent-free H 2 O 2 photosynthesis and biomass valorization
abstract: Triphenylamine and isomeric BINOL-based porous polymers, synthesized through in situ BINOL formation, demonstrated sacrificial agent-free H 2 O 2 production rates of up to 6.47 mmol g −1 ·h −1 , along with concurrent photocatalytic biomass valorization.

title: Effect of post-annealing temperature distribution on the orientation growth of Sb 2 Se 3 films for efficient solar cells
abstract: The orientation growth of grains in Sb 2 Se 3 films prepared using different annealing methods. Sb 2 Se 3 films develop simultaneous [301]/[311] orientation under close-space annealing, but only [311] under open-space annealing.

title: Nature-inspired dynamic nanoconfinement enables life-like mechanical adaptability and robust environmental resilience in polyurethane-urea elastomers
abstract: This work proposes a core strategy of “dynamic nano confinement” to endow polyurethane urea elastomers with ultra-high toughness, room temperature self-healing, excellent environmental stability, and anti-fouling performance.

title: Structural chemistry of the n = 3 Dion–Jacobson phases: controlling polarity and band gap
abstract: Our experimental and theory study gives reliable structures for Dion–Jacobson phases, known for their polar, and photoactive properties. This understanding gives a rationale for their polar or non-polar structures, and the variation in bandgap.

title: Advances in defect modulation strategies of indium antimonide semiconductor colloidal quantum dots for infrared photodetection
abstract: Outlines of the properties, synthesis, surface modification, and photodetection applications of InSb CQDs.

title: Recent advances and prospects of flexible zinc ion batteries from material principles to device structure designs
abstract: Mechanisms of flexible zinc-ion battery components and device design principles were explored, guiding innovative structural designs based on mechanistic analysis.

title: Advances in the design strategy of bimetallic catalysts for the electrocatalytic reduction of CO 2
abstract: The bimetallic catalysts are promising for CO 2 ER. In the current work, bimetallic catalyst preparation methods have been collected and analyzed, challenges and perspectives are also declared.

title: Electrolyte additive for improving anodic stability in aqueous Zn-ion batteries
abstract: The development of durable aqueous Zn-ion batteries (ZIBs) faces significant challenges due to severe parasitic reactions as well as the uncontrolled generation of dendrites on the anode.

title: Oxygen vacancy-enriched NiFe-MOF/Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene composite as a binder-free cathode for high-performance hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Oxygen-vacancy-rich NiFe-MOF/MXene cathode achieves high energy and power in hybrid supercapacitors.

title: Over 10% efficiency Sb 2 (S,Se) 3 solar cells by enhancing the depletion width and interface passivation via methanol-assisted surface treatment
abstract: Methanol-assisted surface etching treatment (SET) improves Sb 2 (S,Se) 3 solar cell performance by expanding the depletion width and passivating interface defects, achieving >10% efficiency and 90% ambient stability after 225 days.

title: An anion-sorted Li-ion electrolyte and flexible MnVO@SWCNT hybrid electrode for an efficient supercapacitor system
abstract: Portable and wearable electronics create a wide application opportunity for flexible energy storage materials.

title: Unlocking the potential of strontium iridate for the oxygen evolution reaction: from fundamental insights to advanced electrocatalyst design
abstract: This review explores the properties of strontium iridate (SrIrO 3 ) and its potential as a high-performance catalyst for water splitting in both acidic and alkaline environments, providing key insights for the advancement of electrolysis technologies.

title: Integrating HER and OER active sites in a two-dimensional covalent terephthalaldehyde-based framework for metal-free photocatalytic overall water splitting
abstract: Two-dimensional covalent terephthalaldehyde-based framework (2D CTF) featuring spatially separated HER and OER active sites serve as promising metal-free photocatalysts for visible-light-driven overall water splitting.

title: In situ engineered triple phase boundary enhancement in 3D structured carbon supported catalyst for high-temperature PEMFC
abstract: This work presents a novel in situ ionomer with 3D carbon-supported Pt for high-temperature PEMFCs, enhancing triple-phase boundaries and preventing pore blockage. The design improves Pt utilization and mass transport, beating Nafion-based systems.

title: Highly efficient organic solar cells enabled by ultraviolet-ozone treated molybdenum oxide hole transport layers
abstract: Ultraviolet ozone treatment on MoO x films improves the wettability, charge extraction, efficiency and the stability of organic solar cells.

title: 2D covalent organic frameworks: organic electrode materials for aqueous batteries
abstract: A review about 2D covalent organic frameworks as organic electrode materials for aqueous batteries.

title: Fast sodium storage kinetics of titanate-based anode materials via Bi 3+ /S co-doping engineering for advanced sodium-ion capacitors
abstract: The sodium storage kinetics of a layered titanate nanoarray anode are improved by co-doping engineering of Bi 3+ intercalation and S doping.

title: Emerging innovations in polymeric hollow fiber membranes for sustainable natural gas valorization
abstract: This review highlights advanced hollow fiber membranes for natural gas processing, focusing on innovative materials and fabrication techniques that overcome permeance-selectivity trade-offs while addressing plasticization and aging challenges.

title: Boosting hydrogen production from alkaline water splitting via electrochemical active surface reconstruction of transition metal sulfide electrocatalysts
abstract: The degree of surface reconstruction reaction of sulfide catalyst is controlled, 20 s-NMSO could reach a current of 1 A cm −2 at an ultra-low overpotential of 231 mV, and the voltage only increased by 0.06% after 6 days of constant current testing.

title: Highly dispersed Ni–Fe active sites on a fullerene based electron buffer to boost the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: A fullerene-based carbon material supported NiFe species (NiFe-OC 60 ) is explored as a novel electrocatalyst for the oxygen evolution reaction with impressive activity and stability performance in alkaline electrolyte.

title: Can the high throughput yield of solar thermal interfacial evaporation systems be beyond theoretical efficiency?
abstract: Strategies and influencing factors for enhanced efficiency of solar steam generation systems with an analysis of the gap between theoretical and experimental efficiency.

title: Azo coupling enables isomeric anthraquinone porous organic polymers with enhanced lithium-ion battery performance
abstract: Anthraquinone-based porous organic polymers (AQ-POPs) are synthesized via a facile azo coupling strategy using isomeric DAAQ linkers. Structural isomerism enables control over porosity, redox activity, and lithium-ion transport.

title: Activating glycerol deep oxidation via Au–TiO x interfacial synergy in bimetallic nanocatalysts
abstract: A Au–Ti/C catalyst enriched with Au–TiO x sites for GOR optimizes glycerol–OH adsorption, promotes hydroxyl formation via water dissociation, and enhances CO-assisted pathways, thereby boosting deep glycerol oxidation.

title: Weaving strength and conductivity: spider silk-modulated polymer electrolytes for high-performance all-solid-state lithium-metal batteries
abstract: This study leverages the distinct and tailorable properties of bioengineered spider silk proteins to create a true solid PEO electrolyte with markedly enhanced performance for all-solid-state lithium-metal batteries.

title: Dehydration-resistant amino acid additives for stable and efficient perovskite precursor solutions and solar cells
abstract: β-Glutamic acid suppresses water-forming dehydration reactions, sustaining defect passivation and stabilizing perovskite precursors and films, enabling solar cells with 25.6% efficiency and outstanding stability.

title: A dual-constrained assembly strategy of highly aligned two-dimensional montmorillonite membranes for efficient proton transport
abstract: A dual-constrained assembly strategy that enhances the electrostatic confinement effect between nanosheets and offers a capillary force compensation effect is proposed to construct highly aligned nanofluidic channels for efficient proton transport.

title: An ultra-sensitive covalent organic framework based smoldering sensor for early warning of a granary fire
abstract: To prevent granary fires, this paper analyzes the pyrolysis products of rice and proposes a novel granary smoldering early warning method based on the color reaction between furfural (main product during rice smoldering) and aniline hydrochloride.

title: Forecasting the viability of complete stage transitions in aluminium-based batteries and predicting capacity fluctuations in response to high-rate discharges
abstract: Unlike lithium-ion batteries, fully charged aluminum-ion batteries have been widely reported to struggle in producing stage 1 configuration which hinders energy density.

title: Desert beetle-inspired fog-harvesting surfaces integrating buckled microchannels and alternating wettability
abstract: We developed a dual-biomimetic fog-harvesting surface inspired by desert beetles and bird's nest ferns, where buckled hydrophilic–hydrophobic microstructures enhance water collection efficiency by ∼30%.

title: Regulating the crystallinity of dimeric acceptors via central core engineering for efficient ternary organic solar cells
abstract: Strategic incorporation of sulfur and selenium into the molecular backbone enables effective regulation of crystallinity. Ternary devices exhibit significantly enhanced V oc and FF, resulting in high PCE.

title: TE-three/TM-four channels nonreciprocal thermal radiation and mechanism by Ge/InAs metamaterials
abstract: Unusual nonreciprocal thermal radiation is presented by Ge/InAs metamaterials, especially for TE polarization in three channels and TM polarization in four channels at an incidence angle of 4.7° and an external magnetic field of 0.8 T.

title: A hierarchical 3D-nanoflower LDH heterojunction: a bifunctional electrocatalyst for total water splitting
abstract: The NiFe-LDH/NiCo-LDH heterostructure reveals an excellent OER, HER and total water splitting with TOF 24 and 13 times greater than NiCo-LDH and NiFe LDH, respectively. The electron transfer in the interface was verified by Bader charge analysis.

title: Multifunctional hydroxyurea additive enhances high stability and reversibility of zinc anodes
abstract: The performance of aqueous zinc-ion batteries (AZIBs) is greatly influenced by both the electric double layer (EDL) at the Zn electrode/electrolyte interface and the solvation structure of Zn 2 + .

title: Features of exchange kinetics of BaFeO 3− δ -based triple-conducting materials: current state of research
abstract: In the preceding decade, significant efforts have been devoted to the development of efficient triple conductivity cathode materials.

title: Meniscus-guided coating for organic photovoltaic cells
abstract: This review specifically focuses on the fluid flow dynamics and drying kinetics involved in the fabrication of organic photovoltaic cells via the meniscus-guided coating process.

title: Tuning the interfacial chemistry in metal organic framework/graphene electrodes for boosting energy storage
abstract: Covalent interactions between two-dimensional conductive metal–organic frameworks and selectively functionalized graphenes improve the performance in electrochemical energy storage.

title: Exceptional piezoelectricity and high electromechanical coupling coefficients in Eu 3+ -doped PMN-PZ-PT textured ceramics via synergistic heterogeneity engineering
abstract: By employing “synergistic design of local structural heterogeneity and microstructure texture”, Eu doped textured PMN-PZ-PT ceramics were synthesized, achieving excellent electromechanical properties ( d 33 = 1310 pC N −1 , k 33 = 0.87 and T C = 170 °C).

title: A novel 1D van der Waals V 2 PS x Se 10− x ( x = 7–10) anode for enhanced electrochemical kinetics and high-rate performance via Se alloying in lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Selenium-alloyed V 2 PS 9 Se in 1D van der Waals materials boosts lithium-ion battery anode performance via atomic-level charge modulation and accelerated electron highways along V–S chains, achieving 83% capacity retention after 400 cycles at 1 A g −1 .

title: Fire hazard mitigation in bi-continuous phase polymer composites: surface vs. bulk and experimental vs. computational
abstract: This study elucidates the distinct roles of the functional surface and bulk chlorine content in enhancing fire safety of polymer composites through an integrated approach combining structural design, performance evaluation, and safety simulation.

title: Ionic liquid modified BiOCl/CNT catalyst with high hydrophobicity and aerophilicity for efficient electrosynthesis of hydrogen peroxide
abstract: A high-performance electrocatalyst for H 2 O 2 production is created by ionic liquid modification, which enhances aerophilicity/hydrophobicity and 2e − ORR selectivity.

title: In situ decorated SrCO 3 nanorods on a porous electrode: detrimental or beneficial toward oxygen reduction for solid oxide fuel cells?
abstract: While alkaline-earth metal segregations ( e.g. , SrCO 3 formation) are widely considered detrimental to ORR activity, we demonstrate that strategically engineered SrCO 3 nanorods, formed via in situ CO 2 induction, significantly enhance ORR kinetics.

title: Photoelectrochemical glyoxylates and hydrogen production from polyethylene terephthalate wastes using BiVO 4 anode with dominant (040) facets
abstract: BiVO 4 photoanodes with dominate (040) facet facilitate ethylene glycol oxidation and H 2 production, achieving an onset potential of 0.215 V and 3.55 mA cm −2 photocurrent, which is ascribed to the high surface activity and charge transfer kinetics.

title: Polytype selection in the antisolvent-free crystallization of formamidinium lead iodide using alkylammonium chlorides
abstract: This work presents a systematic study of how the size of the C 0 –C 4 alkylammonium chloride additive directs the crystallization pathway in antisolvent-free FAPbI 3 films. We find that larger cations promote the intermediate formation of hexagonal polytypes, correlating with enhanced film crystalli…

title: Comprehensive insights into hydrolysis-mediated hydrogen production using high-entropy quintuple alloy-grafted carbon black
abstract: FeCrMnCoNi-based quintuple high-entropy alloys for NaBH 4 hydrolysis with enhanced activity via favorable reaction intermediate adsorption and optimal electrode–electrolyte interface formation.

title: High entropy MXenes in energy storage: structural design, characterization, and applications
abstract: As a new generation of two-dimensional materials, high-entropy MXenes are becoming a frontier research direction in materials science. This review aims to guide the design and optimization of high-entropy MXenes, facilitating their energy storage development.

title: Optimizing the electronic structure of carbon-based NiFe nanoparticles via a vanadium mediated strategy for efficient oxygen reduction catalysts in Zn–air batteries
abstract: The V-NiFe@NC catalyst shows good ORR activity and Zn–air battery performances. Its V-doped NiFe substrate effectively activates the outer N-carbon layer as the primary active site, lowering the energy barrier and enhancing catalytic activity.

title: Synergistic enhancement of thermoelectric and mechanical properties in Bi–Sb–Te alloys enabled by Zn based metal–organic frameworks (ZIF-8)
