abstract: The ZT of the Bi 0.42 Sb 1.58 Te 3 /0.3 wt% ZIF-8 sample reaches 1.42 at 348 K using solid-state sintering and spark plasma sintering, and the thermoelectric conversion efficiency reached 5.03% at Δ T = 250 K.

title: Correction: A NiCu–MoS 2 electrocatalyst for pH-universal hydrogen evolution reaction and Zn–air batteries driven self-power water splitting
abstract: Correction for ‘A NiCu–MoS 2 electrocatalyst for pH-universal hydrogen evolution reaction and Zn–air batteries driven self-power water splitting’ by Mukesh Kumar et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2023, 11 , 18336–18348, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TA02668J.

title: On the use of bioprecursors for sustainable silicon-based anodes for Li-ion batteries
abstract: Plants and protists: promising sources for Si-based Li-ion battery anodes.

title: Manipulating defects simultaneously boosts the crystal stability and the electrochemical reversibility toward long-life aqueous zinc ion batteries
abstract: Combining sulfur-doping and heterojunction in vanadium oxide cathodes is first proposed herein toward long-life aqueous zinc ion batteries. The obtained S-VO 2 /V 6 O 13 -2 manifests a high capacity retention rate of 85.8% after 500 cycles at 0.5 A g −1 .

title: Highly efficient thermoelectric converters based on metalloporphyrin nanotubes
abstract: Novel devices based on porphyrin nanotubes may lead to a wide range of uses in electronic functionality and thermoelectric conversion.

title: Synergistic sodium storage in bismuth-loaded polycellular carbon spheres: high diffusion kinetics and stability
abstract: Polycellular carbon spheres (PCSs) were prepared by using ZnO as a templating agent. Successful loading of Bi was achieved by improving the wettability of PCSs. PCSs/Bi achieved high diffusion kinetics and long-term cycling stability.

title: Unveiling the effect of structural asymmetry on photocatalytic performance: Bi-substituted Y 2 Sn 2 O 7 system with mechanistic insight
abstract: This study investigated the impact of structural distortion on the photocatalytic activity of Bi-substituted Y 2 Sn 2 O 7 .

title: Enhanced photovoltaic performance in MA-free triple-cation-based perovskite solar cells with guanidinium modification
abstract: Incorporation of 3% guanidinium into an FA/Cs perovskite yields a PCE of 20.7%, with improved photovoltaic performance and stability over the pristine device.

title: Enhanced NO 2 sensing performance in organic field-effect transistors via regioregularity-controlled P3HT blend films
abstract: RRa-P3HT enhances self-assembly of RR-P3HT by steric hindrance, optimizing both charge mobility & gas diffusion in OFET-based NO 2 sensors. A 10 wt% blend achieves 3.5× higher mobility and 480% responsivity, exhibiting a synergistic sensing mechanism.

title: Density functional theory studies on tuning Ta X Ti (1− X ) S 2 for insoluble Li 2 S 2 –Li 2 S conversion in lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Due to the complexity of insoluble Li 2 S 2 –Li 2 S conversion, few descriptors exist to correlate the catalytic performance and the underlying electronic structures of a given catalyst, which inhibits the development of lithium–sulfur catalysts.

title: Ion diffusion driven by dynamic lattice deformations in perovskite solid electrolytes
abstract: The lattice deformations contribute to ion diffusions in perovskite solid electrolyte.

title: Recent advances in the orbital modulation of multi-element nanoparticles for boosted catalytic performance
abstract: Multi-element nanoparticles (ME NPs) show unique and crucial roles in numerous electrochemical reactions involving conversion of energy, particularly in the hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) and oxygen evolution reaction (OER).

title: [2 + 2] light-driven cycloaddition synthesis of an organic polymer and photocatalytic activity enhancement via monomer truncation
abstract: [2 + 2] photocycloaddition is exploited as a tool for the synthesis of organic extended materials. As a result, polymeric semiconductors achieved are able to carry out the oxidative photocoupling of benzyl amines with versatile performance.

title: Epitaxial growth of phase-pure and stable β-Fe 2 O 3 films by the pulsed laser deposition method
abstract: Epitaxially grown β-Fe 2 O 3 films exhibit a phase transformation temperature of 650 °C and different PEC activities for [100]- and [111]-orientations.

title: High-performance silicon-based n–i–p heterojunction photoanode for efficient photoelectrochemical water splitting: fabrication, optimization, and large-scale application
abstract: Silicon-based n–i–p heterojunction photoanode with a standard FTO/TiO 2 /Si/NiO architecture has been fabricated using earth-abundant materials, leveraging the advantage of the heterostructure for efficient photoelectrochemical water splitting.

title: Fluorine-doping-induced phosphorus vacancy engineering in NiCoP@NC for enhanced sodium storage performance
abstract: The F-NiCoP@NC electrode exhibited enhanced capacity and stability. Combined DFT and experimental analysis reveal the superiority of defect vacancy engineering. The F-NiCoP@NC//NVP full cell demonstrated its practical viability.

title: Optimized amounts of excess Li in cation-disordered rocksalt cathodes assisted by carbon nanotubes
abstract: This work investigated the electrochemical performances of Mn-rich disordered rocksalt cathodes depending on the amounts of lithium content.

title: How thermally stable are vanadium cathode Li-ion cells?
abstract: Vanadium-based cathodes (ζ-V 2 O 5 , ε-VOPO 4 ) showed high capacity for enabling multi-electron transfer, with thermal stability comparable to that of LFP.

title: High performance La 2 NiO 4+ δ oxygen and Ni–Ce 0.9 Gd 0.1 O 2− δ fuel electrodes for thin film reversible solid oxide cells
abstract: Optimized dendritic La 2 NiO 4+ δ and nano-porous Ni–Ce 0.9 Gd 0.1 O 2− δ thin films exhibit excellent electrochemical performance as oxygen and fuel electrodes, respectively, enabling efficient operation of reversible solid oxide cells at low temperatures.

title: Orderly hybrid aerogel-based hydrate salt for wide-temperature range thermal regulation and flame retardancy in Li-ion batteries
abstract: Orderly hybrid aerogel-based hydrate salt possesses wide-temperature-range thermal regulation from −30 °C to 60 °C and efficient flame-retardant capability during battery thermal runaway.

title: The mechanism of Li + transport in an ether-based electrolyte
abstract: Molecular dynamics simulations in an ether electrolyte reveal that at low temperatures, the coordination is dominated by a slow-moving Li + –DME 3 , while other coordinating configurations decrease, hindering Li + migration.

title: Repurposing e-waste cathodes as catalysts for CO 2 reduction via the reverse water-gas shift reaction
abstract: This study repurposes e-waste retired ternary lithium-ion battery cathodes as catalysts for CO 2 reduction to CO via the reverse water–gas shift reaction.

title: Metastable-state structures promote the surface reconstruction of spinel NiFe 2 O 4 for efficient oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Changes were made to this description. The modulation of the catalyst surface structure during the OER process is an important means to improve the catalytic activity.

title: Advances in perovskite-based functional fibers and fabrics: toward smart, sustainable, and wearable technologies
abstract: Perovskite materials have emerged as a class of multifunctional materials with exceptional optoelectronic, physical and chemical properties, which have aroused great interest in their applications in functional fibers and fabrics.

title: Synthesis, assembly, and electrochemical performance of ultrasmall Sb 2 S 3 nanoparticles
abstract: The synthesis and ligand-mediated assembly of ultrasmall antimony( iii ) sulfide nanoparticles is reported. These Sb 2 S 3 nanoparticles exhibit fast electrochemical cycling and long lifetimes for lithium and sodium ion systems.

title: Bridging the gap between manganese oxide precursor synthesis and lithium manganese oxide cathodes for high-voltage lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The link between MnO 2 precursors and LiMn 2 O 4 cathodes is shown, with precursor properties influencing spinel formation, stability, and performance. Operando XRD and ARC highlight the structural and cycling stability for eco-friendly energy storage.

title: Sintering-resistant and active-species-adjustable Ni catalysts supported on P-doped biochar via nanopore-confined activation for efficient levulinic acid hydrogenation
abstract: An anti-sintering high activity catalyst was prepared for biomass hydrorefining by regulating the activation strategy of biomass-based carbon materials.

title: Modulating the electronic interactions via heterostructure engineering for energy-saving hydrogen production at high current densities
abstract: The Ni 0.2 Mo 0.8 N/F,N–C catalyst facilitates water dissociation and accelerates the kinetics process, achieving high activity in hydrogen production when assisted by the MOR.

title: Enhanced proton conductivity in zirconium-rich perovskites via barium diffusion-assisted low-temperature sintering
abstract: BaCO 3 addition to NiO-BCZYYb4411 anode enables Ba diffusion-assisted sintering of BCZYYb4411 electrolytes at reduced temperatures (1200–1400 °C). Enhanced grain growth (∼10 μm) and many-fold conductivity improvement achieved.

title: Recent progress on metal–organic framework-based separators for lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: We review the progress on MOF-based separators for LSBs, with a particular focus on the relationship between the MOF structures and their functional roles in polysulfide capture, catalytic conversion, and uniform Li + ion flux regulation.

title: Sustainable thermoplastic elastomer-based nanocomposites and their 3D printing for flexible and stretchable sensors
abstract: A series of sustainable polymer nanocomposites that are compatible with pellet-extrusion based 3D printability were developed. These soft and stretchable nanocomposites show unique and sensitive electrical responses to mechanical deformations.

title: New tetrabutylphosphonium organic ionic plastic crystals incorporating borate anions
authors: 'Haris Amir', 'Mega Kar', 'Luke A. O'Dell', 'Maria Forsyth', 'Małgorzata Swadźba-Kwaśny', 'John D. Holbrey'
abstract: Organic plastic crystalline tetrabutylphosphonium borate are of interest as potential new fluorine-free solid-state electrolyte materials conductive under ambient conditions.

title: Optimization of LiCoO 2 thick electrodes with low tortuosity for lithium-ion batteries by 3D printing
abstract: This work designs ordered array-structured electrodes, enhancing rapid ion transport in thick electrodes by optimizing ion diffusion paths, overcoming the limitations of plate-structured electrodes that restrict electrochemical performance.

title: Covalent grafting of redox-active sites onto MXenes with spinel ACo 2 O 4 (A = Zn, Cu) integration for tailored interfacial charge storage in high-performance supercapacitors
abstract: Sulphonate-functionalised MXene anchored with spinel ACo 2 O 4 nanostructures enables synergistic charge transfer, enhanced capacitance, and long-term stability, advancing MXene-based supercapacitors for efficient next-generation energy storage.

title: High-resolution electron microscopy imaging of MOFs at optimized electron dose
abstract: Electron dose thresholds were analyzed for pristine and metalated PCN-222 MOFs. RiCOM images from low-dose 4D-STEM experiment data sets, reveal structural details, allowing investigation of local features while minimizing beam-induced damage.

title: Construction of a Z-scheme PCN-222/CoFe 2 O 4 heterojunction for efficient photo-Fenton tetracycline hydrochloride degradation: mechanism and pathways
abstract: A P/CF-20 magnetically recyclable Z-scheme heterojunction for >99% tetracycline degradation in 30 min via enhanced charge separation to drive h + /O 2 − /·OH pathways.

title: Bio-based and recyclable adhesives based on β-hydroxyester bonds and hydrogen bonds via molecular dynamics simulation
abstract: An epoxy resin adhesive based on β-hydroxyester bonds and multiple hydrogen bonds was prepared with tung oil and lignin. Molecular dynamics simulation was used to regulate the structure and properties of adhesives.

title: High-throughput screening of Ruddlesden–Popper perovskites with layer-dependent mechanical and thermal properties using DFT calculations
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) Ruddlesden–Popper perovskites have recently shown great promise in photovoltaics, thermoelectrics, catalysis and other energy applications owing to their diverse structures and intriguing properties.

title: Two-dimensional self-assembled TiSe 2 micro–nanoparticles toward high-performance sodium ion storage
abstract: TiSe 2 micro–nanoparticles with high rate and long life have been prepared by a simple one-step selenization method. The sodium storage mechanism was investigated by in situ and ex situ technologies.

title: A flexible high-temperature insulating high entropy ceramic fiber membrane for thermal runaway protection in lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The high-entropy membrane demonstrates a stress–strain value of 2.56 MPa and outstanding insulation performance at 1200 °C, offering valuable insight into thermal runaway protection for lithium-ion batteries.

title: Exploring dehydration mechanisms and conductivity optimization in Li 3 InCl 6 · x H 2 O via in situ synchrotron techniques
abstract: In this study, in situ techniques were utilized to elucidate the hydration and heating reaction mechanisms of the Li 3 InCl 6 solid-state electrolyte, offering valuable insights into an effective reaction pathway.

title: Enhanced stability of Ge-doped CsPb(I 1− x Br x ) 3 perovskite solar cells
abstract: Ge element doping is used to inhibit all-inorganic CsPb(I 1− x Br x ) 3 perovskite phase separation and improve the photovoltaic performance and stability of solar cells.

title: The rise of two-dimensional covalent C 3 N 5 : a molecular perspective
abstract: This work reviews the cutting-edge research progress of C 3 N 5 from a molecular perspective, including six precursors and five chemical structures of C 3 N 5 , as well as its heteroatom doping, compositing, and applications.

title: Engineering of interfacial active sites in composites of troilite phase nano-leaves interacting with nickel oxide adorned carbon nanotubes for robust overall water splitting
abstract: The NiO/FeS@CNT hybrid achieves low overpotentials and favorable Tafel slopes for the HER and OER in KOH media. Notably, the electrolytic cell operates at an exceptionally low voltage of 1.465 V, which is significantly lower than various literature.

title: Bifunctional spinel ZnMn 2 O 4 nanostructures for efficient supercapacitors and water splitting electrocatalysts: a synergistic experimental and modeling study
abstract: This study demonstrates ZnMn 2 O 4 nanostructures as high-performance supercapacitors and efficient HER electrocatalysts, synthesized via a modified co-precipitation method, enabling scalable, cost-effective materials for clean energy applications.

title: Bidirectional S-bridge coordination in the magnetic Au/FeO x S y catalyst for the catalytic oxidation of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural to 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid
abstract: A magnetic Au/FeO x S y catalyst with bidirectional S-bridge coordination was prepared for the selective catalytic oxidation of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural under mild conditions.

title: A zinc-doped nickel–boron reconstructed catalytic electrode for efficient hydrogen evolution in a multi-pH system
abstract: We designed a Zn-doped NiB electrode on flexible filter paper via a mild electroless plating method (NiB–Zn@HP), the electrode has structural stability and high HER efficiency in a multi-pH system.

title: A facile surface engineering approach for fabricating the superwetting Ni–Fe–Co LDH@Ni–S heterojunction as a bi-functional electrode for green hydrogen production: experiment and theory
abstract: In comparison to the oxygen evolution reaction (OER), the urea electrooxidation reaction (UOR) is a more energy-efficient technique for producing hydrogen.

title: Kinetic analysis of cathode-solid electrolyte interface in all-solid-state batteries
abstract: High interfacial impedance hinders the development of all-solid-state batteries.

title: A graphene/C-PDA/carbon fiber ternary heterostructure network enables lightweight polyimide composites with enhanced electromagnetic shielding and thermal management capabilities
abstract: A ternary carbon heterostructure polyimide composite with outstanding EMI shielding (∼95 dB) and high thermal conductivity (3.38 W m −1 K −1 ) was synthesized via interfacial engineering, defect modulation, and a vacuum-assisted impregnation method.

title: A theoretical study of 2D polymeric C 24 networks as high-performance anchoring materials for lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Recently reported 2D polymeric C 24 networks were investigated as anchoring materials for Li–S batteries, and the bilayer C 24 one, in particular, demonstrates outstanding anchoring and catalytic performance.

title: Direct Z-scheme Hf 2 CO 2 /MoSSe van der Waals heterostructure for photocatalytic water splitting: high solar-to-hydrogen efficiency and excellent carrier mobility
abstract: The direct Z-scheme Hf 2 CO 2 /MoSSe heterostructure exhibits high solar-to-hydrogen efficiency and high carrier mobility, demonstrating excellent photocatalytic performance.

title: Strong relaxor enabling excellent capacitive energy storage performance in Bi 0.5 Na 0.5 TiO 3 -based binary system
abstract: The introduction of SmFeO 3 induces fluctuations in the local structure, disrupts the original domain configuration, and enhances the energy storage performance of BNT dielectric ceramics through relaxor regulation.

title: Strong electronic interaction in a P and Fe dual-doped Co 3 O 4 nano-network structure supported on 3D Ni foam for highly efficient overall water splitting
abstract: A cation and anion dual-doped P–Fe–Co 3 O 4 nano-network structure, in situ grown on Ni foam, stands out as an efficient and stable bifunctional electrocatalyst for overall water splitting.

title: Effective degradation of antibiotics using near-infrared excited nonlinear optical heterojunctions through atomic-level regulation
abstract: A series of g-C 3 N 4 :Tm@Ba 4 Yb 3 F 17 :Tm composite catalysts with infrared light response based on Tm single atoms were constructed for the degradation of antibiotics. The possible mechanisms are discussed by combining multiple characterization techniques.

title: Hybrid ZnO–porous graphene nanostructured electrode for the electrochemical detection of cadmium, lead and paraquat
abstract: Hybrid ZnO–porous graphene nanostructured electrode fabricated by laser writing technique, enabled sensitive, selective, electrochemical detection of toxic cadmium, lead and paraquat in real samples.

title: Coaxial electrospinning-driven elemental gradient engineering for voltage decay suppression in Li-rich cathodes
abstract: This study synergistically integrates Mn/Ni/Co concentration gradients with spinel-layered heterostructures via coaxial electrospinning, significantly suppressing both the capacity fade and voltage decay in lithium-rich manganese-based cathodes.

title: Dispersive precipitation of PbI 2 for the formation of α-FAPbI 3 solar cells with optimized optoelectronic properties
abstract: The phase evolution and precipitation processes of δ-FAPbI 3 and PbI 2 from α-FAPbI 3 demonstrate four distinct degradation pathways. Throughout these degradation pathways, the photoelectric properties of the films exhibit a unique unimodal variation.

title: Insights into the catalytic valorization of industrial high-concentration nitrous oxide for propylene synthesis
abstract: Industrial nitrous oxide (N 2 O) emissions at high concentrations pose a significant challenge to climate change, while the redox center separation bridged by hydrogen spillover presents a promising strategy for sustainable N 2 O valorization.

title: Lewis acidic W/WO 3− x heterostructure-coupled Ir catalysts with low Ir loading for efficient pH-universal water splitting
abstract: Different reduction degree tungsten compound supported Ir catalysts, Ir–WO 3− x , Ir–W/WO 3− x , and Ir–W catalysts were designed for full pH water splitting, the Ir–W‖Ir–W electrolyzer shows a low cell voltage of 1.593 V at 10 mA cm −2 in 1.0 M PBS.

title: Ingenious strategy of a strong intermolecular force-linked donor–acceptor–donor system in COFs for efficient and improved photocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: The preparation of the covalent organic framework (COF) photocatalyst TATT-COF has been designed by the in situ growth heterojunction strategy.

title: External electric field control of charge transport in Y-shaped and linear non-fullerene acceptors
abstract: Density functional theory coupled with machine learning reveals the EEF-dependent charge transport behavior in non-fullerene acceptors, highlighting how molecular architecture influences charge mobility and stability in organic photovoltaics.

title: CO 2 reduction by dielectric barrier discharge plasma in collaboration with Cu 2− x Se/AlO(OH) catalyst
abstract: Hydrothermal temperature modulation of AlO(OH) morphology and Cu 2− x Se dispersion. CO 2 decomposition rate of 28.07% at SIE of 58.07 kJ L −1 . Cu 2− x Se-Al-180 promotes CO 2 excitation in DBD plasma fields.

title: Study on the regulation mechanism of a coral-like Li 7 La 3 Zr 2 O 12 inorganic filler on the properties of poly(ethylene oxide)-based composite solid electrolytes
abstract: Multi-physical field simulation and Raman results show that Li + ions mainly migrate in the interior of LLZO. A Li + -migration channel is also formed at the PEO/LLZO interface due to the large interfacial contact area.

title: Metal–organic nanocages enhance fluoride adsorption through the synergy of ternary amino cryptands and metal sites
abstract: PTiO 2 @UiO-66-NH 2 combines ternary amino cryptands (H-bonding/electrostatics) and Ti/Zr sites for synergistic, efficient fluoride adsorption through adaptive electron dispersion.

title: Coupled ion desolvation and nucleation control for stable zinc anodes enabled by a polyoxometalate-crosslinked nanocellulose separator
abstract: Polyoxometalate-crosslinked nanocellulose separators induce Zn 2+ partial desolvation and ion flux uniformity, enabling dendrite-free plating and enhanced kinetics, thus stabilizing zinc anodes for practical aqueous zinc batteries.

title: Rapid synthesis of phase-junction CoSe 2 -based nanocomposites (O/C-CoSe 2 @NC) for enhanced sodium-ion storage
abstract: An ultrafast, solid-phase microwave selenization strategy was employed to synthesize cubic/orthorhombic-CoSe 2 homojunction nanoparticles (O/C-CoSe 2 ) in situ within a nitrogen-doped carbon matrix (O/C-CoSe 2 @NC).

title: A defective 2D Fe–N–C nanofilm embedded with porous carbon derived from dicyandiamide as an effective oxygen reduction catalyst for PEMFCs
abstract: This work enhances the morphology and pore structure of a 2D Fe–N–C catalyst through dicyandiamide (DCDA) doping, which improves active site density (SD) and mass transport within the catalyst layer, thereby boosting PEMFC performance.

title: Demonstration of a 650 °C operating high-performance metal-supported solid oxide fuel cell using a Gd-doped CeO 2 electrolyte, Ni anode and Sm(Ba 0.5 Sr 0.5 )Co 2− x Fe x O 5+ δ –Ce 0.9 Gd 0.1 O 2− δ cathode
abstract: Metal-supported solid oxide fuel cells (MS-SOFCs) exhibit numerous advantages, including thermal cycle stability, rapid start-up and lower costs.

title: Biocompatible passive radiative cooling rapid-curing fiberglass casts
abstract: Passive daytime radiative cooling coating on fiberglass casts effectively lowers the surface temperature compared to the uncoated control cast.

title: Synergistic doping and microenvironment engineering enable efficient electrocatalytic oxidation of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural
abstract: We synthesised a composite catalyst, Co-NiO/Cr 2 O 3 , employing a synergistic approach of doping and microenvironment optimisation. This catalyst demonstrates exceptional HMFOR activity, with a faradaic efficiency of 99.2% and selectivity of 99.17%.

title: Supramolecular organic framework nanosheets for efficient singlet oxygen generation: a multilevel energy transfer approach for photocatalytic Minisci reactions
abstract: We have developed a supramolecular organic framework (SOF) for the construction of a light-harvesting sequential energy transfer system, which exhibited enhanced generation ability of singlet oxygen ( 1 O 2 ) for photocatalytic Minisci reaction.

title: Synergistic bulk–interface stabilization of single-crystal cobalt-free high-nickel cathodes via a fast-ionic-conductor coating
abstract: The LWTP-modified SNM9 cathode exhibits significantly enhanced electrochemical performance. The coating reduces side reactions and microcracks, ensuring structural and interfacial stability at high voltage.

title: Au nanostars/Bi 2 S 3 /TiO 2 Schottky/S-scheme dual heterojunctions for efficient photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: We designed Au nanostar-modified Bi 2 S 3 /TiO 2 Schottky/S-scheme dual heterojunction photocatalysts that extend the light absorption range to NIR and enhance carrier separation/transfer efficiency, achieving efficient photocatalytic hydrogen production.

title: A bio-inspired thermo-responsive hydrogel purifier for effective water harvesting in seawater
abstract: Solar desalination technology has garnered considerable attention due to its eco-friendliness, sustainability, and cost-efficiency; however, its practical application is impeded by the drawback of low water yield.

title: Shear-aligned nanocellulose enabling the development of stable all-solid-state lithium batteries
abstract: Shear-induced alignment of CNCs-Li constructs CPEs with ordered ion channels for enhanced all-solid-state Li-battery performance.

title: Freestanding high-entropy phosphide electrodes for industrial-scale hydrogen evolution via far-from-equilibrium electrosynthesis
abstract: A far-from-equilibrium electrosynthesis enables one-step fabrication of freestanding high-entropy material electrodes through alloying, phosphidation, dendritic structuring and integration, while avoiding binders and multi-step assembly.

title: Decoupling charge and ion transport in oxygen evolution reaction through surface hydration engineering of molecular graphene catalysts
abstract: Surface hydration engineering enables decoupled charge and ion transport, enhancing OER kinetics via inner-sphere transfer and intermediate stabilization.

title: Machine learning-accelerated exploration on element doping-triggering material performance improvement for energy conversion and storage applications
abstract: The prediction performances of machine learning in the field of element-doped materials for energy conversion and storage applications are summarized.

title: Stabilizing Cu + species by Al-doping with enhanced *CO coverage for highly efficient electrochemical CO 2 reduction to C 2+ products
abstract: Stabilization of Cu + sites by Al-doped Cu-LDHs to enhance the coverage of *CO intermediates, and thereby encourage the electroproduction of C 2+ compounds from CO 2 .

title: A hierarchical Cu(OH) 2 nanograss-decorated bimetallic nanoporous glucose sensor with an ultra-low detection limit
abstract: Electrochemical glucose oxidation catalyst NP-CuAg@TSO achieves the construction of hierarchical Cu(OH) 2 nanograsses through a two-step oxidation strategy and demonstrates outstanding electrocatalytic performance in glucose detection.

title: Mass transport and grain growth enable high thermoelectric performance in polycrystalline SnS
abstract: Grain boundary engineering enables high thermoelectric performance in polycrystalline SnS by synergistically reducing lattice thermal conductivity through mass transport and enhancing carrier mobility via grain growth promotion.

title: Thermodynamic insights into the Ba–S system for the formation of BaZrS 3 perovskites and other Ba sulfides
abstract: Pressure–temperature phase diagram for the Ba–S system.

title: Engineering surface-exposed LaCoO 3 perovskite nanotubular catalysts for catalytic combustion of toluene through acid etching
abstract: In this study, we prepared a series of nanotubular perovskite-based catalysts using electrostatic spinning technique. The Ce-doped LaCoO 3 catalyst treated with acid exhibited excellent water and SO 2 resistance for the catalytic oxidation VOCs.

title: “Aggregation and rebalance” mechanism-guided design and discovery of efficient bimetallic catalysts for the nitrogen reduction reaction
abstract: A new mechanism, “aggregation and rebalance”, is proposed to explain the effect of the synergistic interaction between the intrinsic features of the active center atoms and coordinating atoms on catalytic activity.

title: Tobacco-waste homologous red-/blue-emitting carbon dots for enhancing photosynthesis
abstract: The proper combination of blue- and red-emitting carbon dots, which are made from tobacco waste, considerably boosted the photosynthesis rate, biomass, and nutritional quality of lettuce.

title: Central π-conjugated extension in quinoxaline-based small-molecule acceptors as guest components enabling high-performance ternary organic solar cells
abstract: Ternary strategies have critical roles in pursuing high efficiencies for organic solar cells (OSCs).

title: Unbiased photoelectrochemical tandem reaction for concurrent oxidations with ultra-high overall faradaic efficiency
abstract: This diagram shows a dual-core PEC system converting HMF to value-added chemicals. The NiOOH/BiVO 4 photoanode generates ˙OH radicals, while Ru/CuBi 2 O 4 photocathode produces ˙O 2 − radicals, oxidizing HMF with high selectivity and faradaic efficiency.

title: Dual-template-driven in situ synthesis of Cu-SSZ-39 catalysts: synergistic structure regulation for excellent NH 3 -SCR activity and hydrothermal stability
abstract: Dual-template-driven in situ synthesis of Cu-SSZ-39 enhances intrinsic catalytic activity towards NH 3 -SCR.

title: Amorphous-like thermal conductivity and high thermoelectric figure of merit in “π” SnS and SnSe
abstract: Using state-of-the-art ab initio modelling, we predict the structurally-complex “π” phases of SnS and SnSe to show glass-like thermal conductivity and a low-temperature figure of merit ZT > 1 if they can be stabilised with heavy n-type doping.

title: Heteroatom engineering for enhancing the thermoelectric power factor of molecular junctions
abstract: Chemical engineering of organic molecules by heteroatom substitutions leads to a dramatically boosted power factor (PF) over 10 5 for the molecular junctions of alkylthiol-based derivatives.

title: Correction: Colloidal synthesis of Au nanomaterials with a controlled morphology and crystal phase via the [Au( i )-oleylamine] complex
abstract: Correction for ‘Colloidal synthesis of Au nanomaterials with a controlled morphology and crystal phase via the [Au( i )-oleylamine] complex’ by Gang Wang et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2021, 9 , 19534–19553, https://doi.org/10.1039/D1TA03666A.

title: Typical abuse tolerance behavior in LiFePO 4 batteries under lithium plating versus normal aging
abstract: The tolerance of differentially aged batteries under typical abuse conditions plays a critical role in battery safety protection design.

title: Atomically engineered cobalt-doped boron nitride nanosheets for the water oxidation reaction
abstract: Cobalt-doped hexagonal boron nitride has been synthesized using the molten salt technique, where cobalt acts as an active centre which enhances the OER performance of h-BN while preserving nanosheet-like morphology.

title: Synergistic band gap expansion and grain refinement toward high thermoelectric efficiency and robust mechanics in p-type (Bi,Sb) 2 Te 3
abstract: The Ag 9 GaSe 6 alloying into p-type Bi 2 Te 3 expands the band gap and refines grains, yielding a high zT ∼1.4 at 375 K (average zT ∼1.25 over 300–500 K), superior Vickers hardness (∼0.92 GPa), and ∼6.4% conversion efficiency at Δ T = 200 K.

title: Proton transport enhancement by octahedral distortion and built-in electric field at the PMN–TiO 2 heterointerface
abstract: Mixed ion–electron conductor-based electrolytes have shown great promise in solid oxide fuel cells (SOFCs), demonstrating attractive performance at low temperatures (<600 °C) due to their multi-interface conduction and interfacial effects.

title: Bioinspired multi-structured hybrid surfaces for directional droplet transport and ultra-efficient atmospheric water collection
abstract: By integrating enhanced superhydrophilic pores, sharp corners, and guide channels into a structurally optimized hybrid surface on a superhydrophobic background, atmospheric water harvesting efficiency was significantly improved.

title: In situ X-ray diffraction guided synthesis of Ni 2 P nanoparticles for the oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Hydrothermal synthesis is used to obtain phase-pure highly crystalline Ni 2 P nanoparticle catalysts for the oxygen evolution reactions.

title: Humidity-independent acetone gas sensors with ppbv-level sensitivity and high selectivity, using an In 2 O 3 microporous nanoneedle array
abstract: A humidity-independent acetone gas sensor using In 2 O 3 microporous nanoneedle arrays fabricated via chemical bath deposition.

title: Modulating photocatalytic decarbonylative Minisci alkylation through conjugation engineering in pyrene-based hydrogen-bonded organic frameworks
abstract: We have designed and synthesized three hydrogen-bonded organic frameworks, which can be used as photocatalysts for decarbonylative Minisci alkylation, and revealed the influence of the π-conjugation effect on their photocatalytic activity.

title: Phase-engineered zirconium MOF-based titanium single-atom catalysts: phase-dependent properties and applications in biodiesel synthesis
abstract: The paper presents a study on the development and application of zirconium-based metal–organic framework (Zr-MOF) supported titanium (Ti) single-atom catalysts (SACs).

title: Mitigation of gas-induced damage in bipolar membranes for CO 2 electrolysis
abstract: Bipolar membranes operated in forward bias are a promising platform for CO 2 electrolysis, enabling alkaline cathode environments and pure-water feed while preventing CO 2 crossover and salt precipitation. A porous AEL prevents gas induced damages.

title: Tuning flexibility in metal–organic frameworks via linker per-fluorination: revisiting the adsorption-induced breathing of MIL-53(Al)
abstract: In situ characterisation of the multi-step adsorption-induced breathing behaviour of F 4 -MIL-53(Al) unveiled the impact of fluorination on framework flexibility and adsorption properties, offering new insights into dynamic porosity in MOFs.

title: Precisely engineered conjugated polyimide cathodes with dense redox-active carbonyl sites for superior lithium-ion battery performance
abstract: Carbonyl-rich polyimide cathodes prove to be more beneficial for achieving superior cycling stability in lithium-ion batteries.

title: An efficient H 2 O 2 -based propylene to propylene oxide (HPPO) reaction catalyzed by ZnO/ZnO 2 materials
abstract: ZnO nanorods and their composites with MCM-41 were prepared to achieve efficient catalytic conversions in the HPPO process.

title: Anion-modulated bifunctional electrocatalytic activity of nickel telluride/cobalt telluride mesoporous nanosheets for high-efficiency and stable overall water splitting
abstract: P-doped NiTe 2 /CoTe 2 mesoporous nanosheets on carbon cloth show enhanced HER/OER activity and stability for overall water splitting, driven by P-induced electronic modulation, Ni–Co synergy, and mesoporous architecture.

title: Efficient photocatalytic H 2 O 2 generation utilizing surface functional duality in carbon dots-polydopamine heterojunctions
abstract: Herein, we report a carbon dots–polydopamine (CD/PDA) heterojunction system that exhibits outstanding photocatalytic performance by leveraging the surface functional duality of the organic components.

title: Defect engineering-driven enhancement of piezocatalysis in (K, Na)NbO 3 lead-free piezocatalysts
abstract: Defect engineering induced by annealing in N 2 increases the carrier concentration and significantly improves the piezocatalytic degradation of MO and RhB organic dyes by (K, Na)NbO 3 piezocatalysts.

title: Thermally conductive phase change electrodes for in situ thermal management of lithium-ion batteries
abstract: Different from the outside thermal management, the implementation of thermally conductive PCMs imparts the functions of heat adsorption and conduction to the electrode of LIBs, achieving in situ thermal management and avoiding temperature gradient.

title: Accelerating the drug-releasing performance by a flexible zinc–air fuel cell based on a polyacrylamide/cellulose nanofibril (PAM/CNF) hydrogel
abstract: With the merits of being non-toxic, biocompatible and non-sensitizing, hydrogel wound dressings are considered as one of the promising choices to prevent wound infection and accommodate the healing drugs.

title: Doping and thermoelectric properties of the zero-dimensional inorganic halide perovskite derivative, Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5
abstract: Doping of Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5 with Ba reveals ultralow thermal conductivity and a high Seebeck coefficient.

title: High-entropy materials for electrocatalytic oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: This review highlights the structural diversity and entropy-driven design of high-entropy materials for oxygen reduction, revealing key structure–activity relationships and guiding future electrocatalyst development.

title: Polymerization of succinonitrile on a garnet surface for preparing a single-ion conducting composite solid-state electrolyte
abstract: SCN transformed into a conjugated CN structured coating layer at the CSE interface, enhancing Li-ion enrichment, promoting faster ion conduction, and enabling single-ion conduction.

title: Ion-selective transport in surface-modified cellulose membranes for aqueous ionic thermoelectrics
abstract: Efficient recovery of low-grade heat (≤100 °C) remains a significant challenge in sustainable energy conversion.

title: Challenges and mitigation strategies for general failure and degradation in polymer electrolyte membrane-based fuel cells and electrolysers
abstract: This review investigates the challenges and recommends various mitigation strategies for general cell failure and degradation mechanisms in polymer electrolyte membrane-based fuel cells and electrolysers.

title: Reversible lattice oxygen participation in Ru 1− x O 2− x for superior acidic oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: RuO 2 catalysts with dominant (101) facets and O/Ru vacancies were synthesized to promote the lattice oxygen-mediated pathway, where upshifted O 2p orbitals enhance H 2 O-derived oxygen capture and sustain cyclic lattice oxygen exchange.

title: Mycelium network inspired PVDF-HFP/g-C 3 N 4 /Mo 2 AlB 2 piezoelectric fibrous mat-based hybrid nanogenerator for underwater wireless electro-mechanical energy harvesting and battery-free aquatic rescue systems
abstract: A highly sensitive hybrid nanogenerator with a mycelium network-inspired piezoelectric mat, combined with Maxwell's displacement current, enables underwater wireless signalling for a Morse code-supported battery-free aquatic rescue system.

title: Interface engineering based lignin-derived hollow carbon/nickel–cobalt–manganese layered double hydroxide composite structures for high-performance supercapacitors
abstract: A lignin-derived hollow carbon/NiCoMn-LDH composite is synthesized to prevent LDH nanosheet restacking and expose more electroactive sites, delivering high charge storage capacity, excellent rate performance, and outstanding cycling stability.

title: Regulating blend miscibility by adding multifluorinated polymer acceptors enables the development of 18% efficiency all-polymer solar cells
abstract: Three multifluorinated polymer acceptors were designed as the third component for ternary PM6:PY-IT blends. The PM6:PY-IT:PY4F-C20 device exhibited favorable phase separation (∼20 nm) and crystallinity, achieving a device efficiency of 18.0%.

title: Band flattening and localized lattice engineering realized high thermoelectric performance in GeTe
abstract: As a lead-free material, GeTe, along with its derivatives, has garnered significant attention as a promising medium-temperature range thermoelectric material, offering a balance between high performance and mechanical stability.

title: Understanding the structure and mechanism of Na + diffusion in NASICON solid-state electrolytes and the effect of Sc- and Al/Y-substitution
abstract: Multimethod study reveals distinct Na + diffusion mechanisms and structural transitions in NASICON solid-state electrolytes for next-generation solid-state batteries.

title: A Zr–O co-doping strategy for halide solid electrolytes toward high-performance all-solid-state batteries
abstract: A synergistic Zr–O co-doping strategy enhances halide solid electrolytes, where O improves chemical stability and Zr induces lattice distortion, yielding 1.12 mS cm −1 conductivity and stable cycling with 4 V-class cathodes.

title: Regulation of ligand-induced solvation structure for stable aqueous Zn-ion batteries
abstract: We have screened out an efficient ligand additive PDM to regulate Zn 2+ solvation structure. By loading a trace amount of PDM, the electrochemical performance of both anode and cathode were significantly improved.

title: Odd-even effects in lead-iodide-based Ruddlesden–Popper 2D perovskites
abstract: An odd-even parity effect in Ruddlesden–Popper 2D perovskites is demonstrated, where spacer cation chain length governs structural distortions that systematically influence band gap, ionization energy and in-plane carrier mobility.

title: A transparent, tough, highly stretchable and self-adhesive zwitterionic dual-network eutectogel for wearable flexible sensors
abstract: Eutectogels possess significant potential for use in wearable flexible sensors due to their low volatility, chemical stability, and high ionic conductivity.

title: Corrosion stability of NASICON-based membranes against aqueous solutions: case study for sodium iodine batteries
abstract: NaSICON materials are promising membranes for aqueous medium-temperature sodium-iodine batteries after optimization of their corrosion stability.

title: Advances in the influencing factors and reaction mechanisms of Pd-based supported catalysts for catalytic methane combustion
abstract: This article reviews various factors that influence the catalytic performance of palladium-based supported catalysts and their reaction mechanisms in the methane combustion reaction.

title: In situ fabrication of TiO 2 nanoparticles/2D porphyrin metal–organic frameworks for enhancing the photoreduction of CO 2 to CO
abstract: In situ synthesis of TiO 2 loaded onto the two-dimensional Al-MOF by the in situ synthesis method to prepare the catalyst Al-MOF@TiO 2 , which can achieve the highly selective reduction of CO 2 to CO.

title: Carbon-to-nitrogen atom transmutations in a covalent organic framework transform the visible light-triggered selective oxidation of sulfides
abstract: TpBmd-COF outperforms TpBD-COF significantly for blue light-triggered selective oxidation of thioanisole with dioxygen due to four carbon-to-nitrogen atom transmutations.

title: Unraveling structure–performance relationships: tailored d-band centers in monolayer MSi 2 N 4 and MoSi 2 Z 4 by atomic substitution
abstract: This work investigates the influence of substituent atomic size on electrochemical performance, providing guidelines for designing high-performance anodes while emphasizing the critical role of the steric hindrance effect.

title: Stoichiometric gradient rebalancing achieves surface reconstruction and bulk homogenization in high-performance vapor-deposited perovskite solar cells
abstract: Vapor-deposited perovskites with stoichiometric rebalancing exhibit enhanced crystal quality and high photovoltaic performance with excellent stability.

title: Trends in competing oxygen and chlorine evolution reactions over electrochemically formed single-atom centers of MXenes
abstract: Single-atom catalysts (SACs) have garnered widespread attention in the catalysis community due to their ability to catalyze transformations relevant to energy conversion and storage with high activity and selectivity and maximum atomic efficiency.

title: Single-atom catalyst with a hollow rod/plate-like structure for enhanced oxygen reduction reaction performance in zinc–air batteries
abstract: Herein, we used a self-templating strategy and an in situ polymerization approach that enables the precise encapsulation of transition metal single-atoms within a nitrogen-doped carbon shell, resulting in a robust hollow architecture.

title: Surface modification and heterointerface engineering into cobalt-hydroxide nanowires by ion exchange for high-energy asymmetric supercapacitors
abstract: The development of novel transition-metal heterostructures with rich redox activities, high capacity values, and stable cycling performance is essential for fabricating high-energy-density supercapacitors.

title: Anthraquinone-modified triazine rich g-C 3 N 4 for high efficiency photocatalytic H 2 O 2 synthesis via promoting singlet oxygen conversion
abstract: We developed a dual-modified carbon nitride photocatalyst achieving enhanced H 2 O 2 production via improved charge separation and AQ-driven 1 O 2 conversion.

title: Advances in reversible protonic ceramic electrochemical cells operated below 723 K: theoretical insights and experimental developments
abstract: Lowering the operating temperature of reversible protonic ceramic electrochemical cells below 723 K can not only prevent component degradation and lower system costs but also enhance the overall efficiency.

title: A review on 2D materials for surface acoustic wave gas sensors at room temperature
abstract: In surface acoustic wave (SAW)-based gas sensing applications, the sensitive material coated on the SAW sensor is critical for selectivity, limit of detection, and sensitivity, as it directly affects changes in sensing signals.

title: High-strength millable polydimethylsiloxane-based polyurethane elastomers with a broader application temperature range
abstract: Millable polydimethylsiloxane-based polyurethane elastomers are prepared with an ultra-wide temperature range application, excellent strength, non-cytotoxicity, fluorescence, and other beneficial properties.

title: Fully non-fused acceptors with alkylsilyl side-chains for efficient indoor organic photovoltaics
abstract: Two low-cost, fully non-fused ring acceptors were designed for indoor photovoltaics, achieving a power conversion efficiency of 21.41% under indoor illumination.

title: Advancements in biomass-derived cellulose composite electrodes for supercapacitors: a review
abstract: This review explores cellulose chemistry, trends, and future prospects in supercapacitor electrodes, focusing on LCA, computational tools, and strategies to connect lab research with practical energy storage applications.

title: A review on the development of covalently connected donor–acceptor molecular materials for single-component organic solar cells
abstract: This review showcases the development of covalently connected donor–acceptor molecules for single-component organic solar cells.

title: Capacity-weighted figures-of-merit for battery transport metrics
abstract: Fast-charging materials are necessary for a battery-centric future. Ionic and electronic transport crucially determine performance where their capacity-weighted figures-of-merit account for performance across all states-of-charge.

title: Adsorption enhanced catalytic degradation of airborne formaldehyde in bifunctional composite materials
abstract: This work focuses on the multistage synergistic degradation of HCHO in bifunctional composite materials from the perspective of adsorption enhanced catalytic degradation of HCHO.

title: Cation-driven hydrogen bond dynamics in energy storage hydrogel electrolytes: unraveling ion-water-carbon interactions
abstract: Hydrogel electrolytes, recognized for their pliability and superior ionic conductivity, present a viable substitute for liquid electrolytes in energy storage systems.

title: π-Extended dihydrophenazine based redox responsive polymers of intrinsic microporosity
abstract: In this work, π-extended dihydrophenazine derivatives are exploited to generate redox responsive PIMs. The resulting Phen-PIMs present a high surface area >600 m 2 g −1 and a CO 2 /N 2 selectivity up to 49, which upon reversible oxidation exceeds 60.

title: High-loading single-atom chromium catalysts on graphullerene for the oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: Chromium atoms supported on graphullerene exhibit excellent catalytic activity for the oxygen reduction reaction, which is attributed to their half-filled d orbitals.

title: Application of MOF-808 and its derivatives in carbon capture and CO 2 catalytic conversion
abstract: The present document delineates the optimization strategy of MOF-808 and its intrinsic mechanism of CO 2 capture, separation and catalytic process.

title: MXene-derived nanostructures: pioneering anodes for lithium-ion and sodium-ion batteries
abstract: To keep up with the demands of energy storage applications, lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) and sodium-ion batteries (SIBs) must advance with the development of high-performance anode materials.

title: Cu–Ag interactions in bimetallic Cu–Ag catalysts enhance C 2+ product formation during electrochemical CO reduction
abstract: The interactions between Cu and Ag, such as in Cu–Ag nanoalloys, are crucial to enhance the selectivity to C 2+ products in the electroreduction of CO (CORR).

title: Long-term stability of GaN-based photocatalyst nanostructures through dynamic oxide protection
abstract: Substantially improved photocatalytic long-term stability is demonstrated using a dynamically synthesized Al 2 O 3 protection architecture on a cocatalyst-decorated p-InGaN nanowire sample.

title: From lab to fab: solution-processed top electrodes for commercializing perovskite solar cells
abstract: Solution-processed top electrodes are crucial for PSC commercialization. This TOC image illustrates solution-processed top electrodes for commercial-scale perovskite photovoltaics.

title: Layer-by-layer techniques incorporating upcycled TPEE: from waste to conductive, multi-responsive, self-healable, and highly-stretchable electronics
abstract: Upcycled thermoplastic polyester elastomers, poly(ionic liquid- co -acrylic acid), and MXene nanosheets are utilized to fabricate conductive, stretchable, self-healable, and alkaline-responsive multilayered films.

title: Energy storage behavior of side chain-engineered Si-bridged redox-active donor–acceptor conjugated polymers operated in organic electrolytes
abstract: Asymmetric hybrid supercapacitors using Si-bridged redox-active conjugated polymeric anodes.

title: Construction of bimetallic CoCu@BC for sustainable ammonia synthesis with minimal nitrite accumulation
abstract: The CoCu@BC catalyst exhibits stability of ammonia formation for 120 hours while maintaining a NO 2 − faradaic efficiency of 0.26%.

title: Synergy of metal halide doping and a polymeric interface enables improved electrochemical performance of all solid-state Li batteries
abstract: Sulfide solid-state electrolytes are promising candidates for all-solid-state lithium metal batteries (ASSLBs) having higher energy density and practical safety due to their high ionic conductivity and favorable mechanical properties.

title: Compositional flexibility in irreducible antifluorite electrolytes for next-generation battery anodes
abstract: Antifluorite-like solid solutions in the LiCl–LiBr–Li 2 S–Li 3 N phase space combine high ionic conductivity with stability against reduction versus lithium metal, opening a promising avenue towards solid-state batteries with next-generation anodes.

title: Ti–Cu alloy-based converted suboxides: robust electrode scaffolds for enhanced electrocatalytic glycerol oxidation
abstract: Suboxide Cu–TiO 2 nanotube arrays fabricated using Ti–Cu alloy foils exhibit excellent scaffolding properties and enhanced glycerol electrooxidation performance.

title: Manganese cobalt sulfide-doped fibrous sulfurized polyacrylonitrile for high-rate and long-life lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Manganese cobalt sulfide-doped fibrous sulfurized polyacrylonitrile with an increased graphitization degree and stable cathode electrolyte interphase shows fast electronic and ionic conductivity and improved kinetics.

title: Hydroxyl-functionalized ultrathin NiO x interlayer for minimized energy loss and enhanced interface stability in perovskite photovoltaics
abstract: Ultrathin, hydroxyl-functionalized NiO x interlayers prepared via low-temperature, watermediated atomic layer deposition significantly enhance self-assemble monolayer adsorption, and enable efficient perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells.

title: Revealing the hydrogen bond network effect at the electrode–electrolyte interface during the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: The table of contents (TOC) graphic illustrates the effect of solvation on the hydrogen bond network (HBN) and water migration on the A100-6 surface at varying ionic concentrations, specifically in pure water, and 1 M KOH, and 6 M KOH solutions.

title: Mode of electroactive species evolution from Fe 3 X 4 (X = Se, S, O): guidelines from the redox chemistry of chalcogen anions
abstract: The redox and aqueous speciation chemistry of chalcogenides present in the Fe 3 X 4 (X = Se, S, O) structure also controls the electrochemical evolution of the reactive phase for alkaline oxygen evolution reaction.

title: Regioisomeric control of planarity enhances exciton dissociation in conjugated polymers for high-efficiency photocatalytic H 2 evolution
abstract: Organic conjugated polymers (CPs) are promising photocatalysts for solar-to-hydrogen conversion, yet their efficiency is often limited by large exciton binding energies and poor charge separation.

title: Ni/Al co-doping induces FeO 6 octahedral distortion to activate lattice oxygen in Ca 2 Fe 2 O 5 for enhanced chemical looping hydrogen generation
abstract: Ni/Al doping significantly enhances the catalytic performance and oxygen transport capability of Ca 2 Fe 2 O 5 in the CLSMR process, thereby achieving higher syngas and hydrogen yields.

title: A magnetorheological fluid based infinitely-regulatable triboelectric tactile sensor
abstract: An adaptive triboelectric pressure sensor offers highly adjustable sensing performance by introducing a magnetorheological fluid. Through the reversible phase transition, it can quickly switch between liquid–solid and solid–solid modes.

title: Pivotal role of Sb vacancies in quaternary half-Heusler thermoelectrics
abstract: The pivotal role of intrinsic defects in nominally stoichiometric X(Fe 0.5 Ni 0.5 )Sb quaternary half-Heuslers (X = Ti, Zr, or Hf) is revealed. The Sb vacancies naturally occur during synthesis and can alter the conduction behavior from n-to p-type.

title: Flexible, optically transparent and self-healing ionogels for microwave/infrared dual-band stealth
abstract: A transparent stretchable ionogel with autonomous self-healing ability enables multi-band stealth metasurfaces by synergizing ionic conduction and dynamic bonds.

title: Boosting nitrate-to-ammonia electrosynthesis via hierarchically branched TiO 2 nanorods
abstract: Hierarchical TiO 2 branched nanorods synthesized via TiCl 3 treatment exhibit abundant oxygen vacancies and enhanced electrochemical surface area, enabling efficient nitrate-to-ammonia conversion with high faradaic efficiency and long-term stability.

title: Ion flux regulation in aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Establishing uniform ion flux profiles collectively mitigates cathode dissolution, suppresses zinc dendrite proliferation and stabilizes interfacial charge transfer, which are critical milestones toward practical development of zinc ion batteries.

title: Enhanced photocatalytic performance of g-C 3 N 4 by introducing gradient energy band structure and activated n–π* electronic transition for visible light hydrogen generation
abstract: g-C 3 N 4 -based materials (CNs) are a promising hydrogen generation photocatalyst for solar energy conversion.

title: Reversible two-electron redox conversion enabled by an activated electrode and stabilized inter-halogen electrolyte for high performance zinc–iodine flow batteries
abstract: A zinc–iodine flow battery with a two-electron transfer behavior is proposed by utilizing a tailored chloride cathode and modified electrode, which exhibits a high capacity of 110.56 Ah L −1 at 100 mA cm −2 and a high energy density of 132.25 W h L −1 .

title: High thermoelectric performance of Pb and Er co-doped polycrystalline SnSe via endogenous hetero-/homo-nanostructures and band alignment
abstract: SnSe demonstrates unparalleled potential in thermoelectric applications, combining non-toxicity, low cost, and abundant sources.

title: Effect of π-bridge lengths in oligomerized fused-ring electron acceptors on the photovoltaic performance of organic solar cells
abstract: Two oligomer acceptors with a specific A–DA′D–A structure and different π-bridge lengths were constructed for organic solar cells, and the PM6:CH-TRZ binary solar cell achieves an impressive efficiency of 17.07% along with excellent device stability.

title: Research on NiMoTi alloy thin film hydrogen evolution catalysts via high-throughput screening using total internal reflection imaging
abstract: High-throughput TIRi screening reveals optimal NiMoTi alloy composition for efficient hydrogen evolution, enabling rapid catalyst optimization through composition–performance mapping.

title: Enhancing photoactivity of defective g-C 3 N 4 via self-polarization effect of tourmaline for CO 2 reduction
abstract: The self-polarizing electric field of tourmaline promotes the photocatalytic CO 2 reduction performance of nitrogen-deficient carbon nitride.

title: Synergistic integration of polysulfobetaine brush-grafted porphyrinic covalent organic frameworks with native enzymes for electrolyte-sensitive aerobic photobiocatalysis
abstract: COFs with polysulfobetaine brushes (TpDh-P) could efficiently regenerate NAD + from NADH under red light. The polysulfobetaines brushes capture and neutralize ROS, protecting native enzymes, and modulate NADH oxidation with electrolyte-sensitivity.

title: A revolutionizing polymeric framework with integrated aluminium fragment for superior water decontamination empowered by a statistical modeling approach
abstract: A new porous three-dimensional polymeric framework integrated with an aluminium fragment, poly(aluminium trimethacrylate), is designed, for remediation of arsenic and fluoride from water. The performance was validated via statistical modeling.

title: Dual engineering of pores and surfaces in metal–organic frameworks via precision etching for enhanced photocatalytic performance
abstract: The pore and surface properties of metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) were concurrently regulated by a facile etching method. This structural modification obviously impacts the photocatalytic performance of MOFs in activating peroxymonosulfate.

title: Sulfur quantum dots-assisted functionalization of aryl radicals towards metal-free cross-coupling
abstract: Sulfur quantum dots rapidly separate electron hole pairs through ethanolamine ligands, promoting aryl cross-coupling reaction under ambient air conditions without external reducing agents.

title: A multilayered Co–Fc MOF/GO 3D evaporator for efficient solar-driven water generation and wastewater purification
abstract: A multilayered Co–Fc MOF microspheres/graphene oxide 3D inverted cone evaporator with high photo-Fenton catalytic activity and photothermal conversion ability for achieving efficient solar-driven evaporation and organic degradation concurrently.

title: Improved capacitive energy storage in K 0.5 Na 0.5 NbO 3 -based high-entropy ceramics with order–disorder polarization configurations
abstract: A high-entropy design guided by the phase field simulation is adopted to construct local order–disorder polarization configurations, which results in the formation of polar nanoregions embedded in a disorder polar matrix.

title: An interface-tailored NaCrO 2 @Na 2 FePO 4 F/C heterostructure cathode synchronizing high-rate capability and stability for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: The heterostructure design plays a crucial role in enhancing the kinetic performance and interfacial stability of NaCrO 2 @Na 2 FePO 4 F/C.

title: Elucidating the mechanism of perovskite surface passivation with organic molecules: the impact of π-conjugation length
abstract: Organic molecules with extended π-conjugation improve solar cell efficiency and durability by enhancing defect passivation, hole extraction, and radical-cation stability, increasing initial efficiency to 24.6% and retention to 85% after 1000 hours.

title: Recyclable layered chromite-based porous film for water cleaning
abstract: We designed an efficient and recyclable composite porous film containing 2D chromite nanosheets and a polymer material. The primary goal is to remove harmful dyes and separate oils from the surface of large water bodies using the film.

title: Improving the ecological index in the solution processing of hybrid perovskites
abstract: Replacing toxic solvents with bio-based green alternatives, combined with nucleation/growth control via MACl and thiourea addition, yields high-quality perovskite films. Solar cells reach 95% of the performance of DMF-based counterparts.

title: 3D printable, super compressible, antibacterial and environmentally stable dual networked ionogels as wearable pressure sensors for wireless early warning of precise health
abstract: The ionogel CNF-P@PHEAA 12 exhibits 3D printing properties, compressibility and antibacterial properties, and is used as a pressure sensor to monitor physiological activities. The pressure sensor array shows spatial pressure distribution abilities.

title: Thermally-induced phase transformations of CALF-20: insights into its phase stability and carbon capture properties
abstract: This study reveals that thermally-stable β-CALF-20 can be synthesized directly from α-CALF-20 through thermal treatment above 100 °C.

title: Suppression of extensive ice formation in hydrogel electrolytes enabling low-temperature aqueous Zn batteries
abstract: We designed a PVA-0.5SL hydrogel electrolyte with SL–Zn(ClO 4 ) 2 clusters that disrupt the H-bonding network, enabling low-temperature performance in aqueous Zn batteries.

title: Assembly of an actinide-uranium single atom catalyst on defective MXenes for efficient NO electroreduction
abstract: Ab initio screening identifies UO 2 @Nb 2 CO 2 as an efficient NO-to-NH 3 electrocatalysts with a low −0.14 V limiting potential.

title: Structure–property relationship of atomically-precise silver acetylide clusters in the electroreduction of CO 2
abstract: Synthesis and properties of a library of all-alkynyl silver cluster catalysts for the electroreduction of CO 2 .

title: Unlocking the synergistic effects of gradient engineering, Mg doping, and in situ Li conductive coating for high-performance Ni-rich LiNi 0.92 Co 0.04 Mn 0.04 O 2 cathode materials
abstract: A Ni-rich, Co-less NCM cathode with a concentration gradient structure, featuring Ni enrichment at the core and Mn at the surface, offers a promising balance of high energy density and cost-effectiveness.

title: Freestanding cathodes with vertically arranged microchannels via phase inversion for quasi-solid-state lithium metal batteries
abstract: We design thick freestanding cathodes with reduced tortuosity and increased porosity via phase inversion, and prepare quasi-solid-state lithium batteries with excellent electrochemical performance using the cathodes by in situ polymerization.

title: Ag single-atom modification of MgAl-LDH to enhance the CH 4 product selectivity in CO 2 reduction: a DFT study
abstract: Herein, a Ag single-atom loaded MgAl-LDH catalyst is constructed. The introduction of Ag atoms triggers the electronic structure reorganization of hydroxyl oxygen atoms in the layered double hydroxide matrix, leading to enhanced CH 4 selectivity.

title: How does an antisolvent additive affect all interfaces in aqueous Zn–MnO 2 batteries?
abstract: Low levels of acetonitrile (0.5–2 vol%) have the lowest nucleation, growth, and stripping overpotentials at the Zn anode and highest δ-MnO 2 phase reversibility at cathode suggesting a balance between suppressed conversion and retained Zn 2+ mobility.

title: Effective reversible calcium/aluminum ion intercalation into VOPO 4 enabled by organic molecular assistance
abstract: In this work, large interlayer spacing was obtained by intercalating VOPO 4 nanosheets with phenylamine to achieve fast kinetics of Ca 2+ and Al 3+ , as well as excellent specific capacity levels and long cycle life.

title: Crosslinked sulfonated polyphenylenes: overcoming the performance–gas permeation–stability trilemma in water electrolyzers
abstract: Thermal crosslinking of Pemion®, a PFAS-free membrane, enables stable and efficient operation with low gas crossover in a PEM water electrolyzer.

title: Erosion behaviors of silicon carbide at micro–nano scales under atomic oxygen exposure
abstract: Reactive molecular dynamics (RMD) methods are employed to investigate the interface evolution and oxidative erosion behavior of silicon carbide (SiC) under extreme atomic oxygen (AO) impact (high energy and flux).

title: Ga doping enhances the oxygen evolution reaction performance and stability of NiFe layered double hydroxides
abstract: We have successfully synthesized ultrathin NiFeGa-LDH@NF nanosheets. 258 mV overpotential provides high current density up to 100 mA cm −2 . Stability tests at high current density for 72 h showed a voltage variation of only 1.3%.

title: Multifunctional NiO/Ti 3+ –TiO 2 for concurrent water reduction and glycerol oxidation to value added products by sunlight driven photocatalysis
abstract: Self-doped Ti 3+ containing NiO/Ti 3+ –TiO 2 catalysts and their applications towards co-production of H 2 and VAPs from a glycerol water mixture by concurrent utilization of electrons and holes.

title: Synthesis of high-temperature hydrophobic nanoparticles and their applications in superlyophobic coatings
abstract: The oxygen scavenging effect enhanced the thermal stability of high-temperature hydrophobic nanoparticles.

title: AuPt nanostructures with a high hydrogen evolution reaction activity through a halide-mediated microwave assisted route
abstract: In light of the escalating scarcity and rising costs of platinum, it is imperative to take a strategic approach to its rational utilization as an electrocatalyst for the hydrogen evolution reaction (HER).

title: Machine learning computes the elastic response of cross-linked polymers
abstract: This article presents a generalized machine learning pathway to compute the elastic response of cross-linked, rubber like polymers.

title: Microwave shock-driven thermal engineering of unconventional cubic 2D LaMnO 3 for efficient oxygen evolution
abstract: A novel microwave shock-driven method has enabled the successful synthesis of the unconventional cubic-phase 2D LaMnO 3 , marking a significant advancement in catalytic design for efficient oxygen evolution.

title: Solid polymer electrolytes incorporating Zn-based metal–organic frameworks for advanced all-solid-state lithium metal batteries
abstract: A novel PZM-10 solid polymer electrolyte incorporating Zn-MOF reduces polymer crystallinity and suppresses TFSI − , enhancing Li + transport. The resulting LiFePO 4 //Li cell exhibits improved cycling stability and dendrite suppression ability.

title: A CuFe-PCN single-atom catalyst with highly dense diatomic sites for alkaline Fenton-like decontamination
abstract: Synergy between CuN 3 and FeN 3 sites in a highly-dense diatomic CuFe-PCN catalyst boosts the intrinsic activity of CuN 3 sites for H 2 O 2 activation to generate 1 O 2 in alkaline solutions for wastewater decontamination.

title: Predicting neutron experiments from first principles: a workflow powered by machine learning
abstract: From electronic structure calculations via molecular dynamics to neutron spectra.

title: Atomic engineering in covalent organic frameworks for high-performance proton conduction
abstract: Pyrene-based 2D COFs with phenolic hydroxyl groups exhibit proton conductivity of 1.09 × 10 −1 S cm −1 under high humidity, surpassing most COF-based conductors.

title: Strategies for performance and stability advancement in multicomponent perovskite photovoltaics
abstract: State-of-the-art strategies adopted for the improvement of photovoltaic performance and stability in multi-component perovskite materials and devices.

title: Shaping nanoparticle-based aerogels for efficient light-driven catalysis
abstract: In this article we present a synthesis concept to shape aerogels by self-assembly of nanoparticles from colloidal dispersions to optimize mass transport and illumination allowing for efficient light-driven degradation of methanol.

title: Molten salt synthesis of low-dimensional nanostructured perovskite oxide electrocatalysts for the oxygen evolution reaction: a review
abstract: This work summarizes research progress in molten salt synthesis of low dimensional nanostructured perovskite oxide electrocatalysts for the oxygen evolution reaction, which covers their synthesis, structural characterization and applications.

title: Oxidized covalent organic frameworks with enhanced local polarization for superior photocatalytic production of hydrogen peroxide
abstract: A post-oxidation strategy was employed to significantly modulate the polarity of a thiophene based, non-substituted quinoline linked COF, enhancing its photocatalytic hydrogen peroxide production in an aqueous solution of furfuryl alcohol.

title: In silico design and experimental validation of a high-entropy perovskite oxide for SOFC cathodes
abstract: High-entropy perovskite oxides offer promising electrode performance for SOFCs and batteries. This study identifies a single-phase composition, validated theoretically and experimentally, with high surface stability and electrocatalytic properties.

title: High-entropy CoNiFeCuP nanoparticles as efficient and durable catalysts for alkaline oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: High-entropy CoNiFeCuP nanoparticles show outstanding alkaline OER performance (244 mV@10 mA cm −2 , 100 h), driven by multimetal synergy and strong metal–phosphorus interactions, which modulate electronic structures and enhance stability.

title: Advances in the application of ionic liquids in PEO-based lithium-ion solid-state electrolytes: from the perspective of fillers
abstract: The latest developments in PEO/ILs for lithium-ion solid-state polymer electrolytes are reviewed, covering the roles and interaction mechanisms of ILs and PEO and representative applications from the perspective of different fillers.

title: Accelerated screening for high-efficiency inorganic photovoltaic materials: from ensemble voting regression to device performance
abstract: A Voting Regressor model is used to predict the SLME of 51 472 inorganic materials. DFT calculations are performed to identify key factors, leading to five candidates exceeding 30% SLME with strong optical absorption and efficient carrier transport.

title: Mechanical-dielectric optimized graphene aerogels with strain-tunable microwave attenuation and shielding functions
abstract: We developed graphene aerogels with bridge-lamellar microstructures that provide strain-tunable microwave attenuation and shielding, achieving stable efficiency after cyclic compression and harsh conditions, which is ideal for practical usages.

title: Perfluoroalkyl-functionalization of zirconium-based metal–organic framework nanosheets for photosynthesis of hydrogen peroxide from dioxygen and water
abstract: Hydrophobization of Zr-based MOF nanosheets using perfluoroalkyl carboxylates dramatically promotes photocatalytic hydrogen peroxide production from dioxygen and water.

title: Charge-state-regulated transition metal doped g-C 3 N 3 for the electrochemical nitrogen reduction reaction: defect physics and constant potential study
abstract: The NN bond length strongly correlates with NRR activity and can be effectively tuned by the magnetic moment ( μ total ) and the d-band center ( ε d ) of TM@g-C 3 N 3 . The charge state can induce μ total and ε d relocation, thus improving the NRR performance.

title: Engineering crystalline property of polymer solid electrolytes for boosted electrochemical performances: a critical review
abstract: Summary of crystalline property engineering of SPEs.

title: A CNT- and ZnO nanorod-incorporated fish gelatin nanocomposite towards developing flexible pressure/strain sensors for human motion monitoring
abstract: This work provides an experimental and computational analysis of a novel nanocomposite composed of fish gelatin integrated with CNTs and ZnO nanorods and evaluates its performance as a pressure/strain sensor.

title: Synchronously enhanced flame retardancy and mechanical properties of epoxy/carbon fiber composites achieved via an interfacial structure design
abstract: Carbon fiber (CF)–reinforced epoxy resin (EP) composites (E/C composites) have been widely used in numerous fields, but they are always plagued by the low flame retardancy because of the ‘candlewick’ effect of the CF.

title: Sustainable wood fiber composites with antistatic and high-thermal-conductivity performance for energy-efficient home environments
abstract: Eco-friendly biocomposites of 3D wood fibres with antistatic properties and high thermal conductivity: a sustainable solution for smart home applications.

title: Single-metal-atom-incorporated quantum dots and covalent organic frameworks: a comparison of quantum effects and electronics
abstract: Article describes the properties of isolated atoms on QDs and COFs for the importance of combining state-of-the-art design and attributes. Described methods for fabricating M-SA-QDs and M-SA-COFs and their structural and electronic properties.

title: Self-healing epoxy resin film enabled by the synergetic effect of coumarin and MXene
abstract: (a) Mechanical properties of DDCm-EP and MXene/DDCm-EP; the self-healing efficiency of DDCm-EP (b) and MXene/DDCm-EP (c) under xenon lamp after different repair times; (d) the self-healing diagram and mechanism diagram of MXene/DDCm-EP.

title: Preparation of platinum nanocatalysts from mixed-valence precursors: investigation on the size effects of complete oxidation of propene
abstract: Changes in Pt particle size result in varied surface valence states, altering the reaction pathways for C 3 H 6 oxidation.

title: Construction of the sp-C–O–V interface for selective conversion of methane to methyl hydroperoxide under mild conditions
abstract: Two-dimensional V 2 O 5 /graphdiyne nanosheets with electron-rich sp-C–O–V interfaces significantly enhance the activity and selectivity for methane oxidation to produce CH 3 OOH.

title: Accelerating chlorine desorption from metal active sites in thermal catalytic oxidation of dichloromethane via UV-vis assistance for persistent and complete mineralization
abstract: UV-vis irradiation is integrated into the thermal catalytic oxidation process to promote chlorine desorption from Pt active sites, thereby enabling efficient and stable degradation of dichloromethane over the Pt nanoparticle-loaded TiO 2 catalyst.

title: N , N -Dimethylacrylamide-mediated composite artificial solid electrolyte interphase for long-cycling lithium metal batteries
abstract: We report a LiNO 3 -rich artificial SEI via N , N -dimethylacrylamide (PDMAA) polymerization that enables high LiNO 3 solubility, HF suppression, and dual CEI/SEI stabilization for high-performance lithium metal batteries.

title: Engineering asymmetric MOF composites toward high-efficiency catalytic reactions
abstract: Asymmetric metal–organic framework composites (AMOFs) have garnered increasing attention due to their distinctive functional properties, demonstrating considerable promise in catalysis, particularly as micro- and nano-structured catalysts.

title: Recent progress in high-entropy intermetallics for advanced catalysis
abstract: Recent progress in high-entropy intermetallic nanocatalysts: synthesis and advanced catalytic applications.

title: Oxygen defects boost the lithium-ion storage kinetics of γ-phase Li 3.1 V 0.9 Ge 0.1 O 4− x for high-rate lithium-ion capacitors
abstract: Oxygen-deficient γ-Li 3.1 Ge 0.1 V 0.9 O 4− x with a tailored electronic structure and fast Li + transport enables lithium-ion capacitors with high capacity, broad voltage window, and excellent energy-power density for advanced energy storage.

title: ∼100% enhancement of cryogenic thermoelectric performance of Bi 80 Sb 20 alloys by incorporation of Fe 3 O 4 nanoparticles
abstract: Bi 80 Sb 20 /Fe 3 O 4 nanocomposites, prepared via stearic acid-assisted ball milling and SPS, showed a peak zT of 0.31 at 242 K (∼100% enhancement) and improved hardness, enabling simultaneous TE–mechanical optimization.

title: Strain regulation of microscopic processes in lithium-rich cathodes: thermodynamics and kinetics
abstract: Stress and strain drive structural degradation in Li-rich cathodes. Both thermodynamic and kinetic properties of microscopic processes are strain-sensitive, offering principles to improve cycling stability.

title: Tuning valence-variable single atomic metal for efficient antibiotic degradation and in situ chlorinated byproduct elimination under current pulsation
abstract: This work provides a new perspective for tuning the valence-variable metal in a single-atom electrode to achieve efficient antibiotic degradation and in situ elimination of chlorinated byproducts.

title: An effective green molecular reactor for photocatalytic aerobic oxidation of sulfide under natural sunlight
abstract: This study reports a novel Zr-based metal–organic framework with scu topology, exhibiting extended light absorption in the region of 600–800 nm and efficient photocatalytic sulfide oxidation under natural sunlight.

title: Flower-like NiZnCu composite microstructures assembled by nanosheets as a highly active bifunctional electrocatalyst for urea-assisted energy-saving hydrogen production
abstract: Flower-like NiZnCu microstructures assembled by nanosheets are electrochemically deposited on NF at room temperature. The deposited NiZnCu microstructures serve as a bifunctional electrocatalyst for HER and UOR.

title: Effect of crystal growth rate on crystal direction, defect formation, and photovoltaic performance of Sb 2 Se 3 thin-film solar cells
abstract: The study examines how controlling the grain growth rate via temperature ramping during selenization affects the crystal direction, defect formation, and photovoltaic performance of Sb 2 Se 3 thin-film solar cells.
