title: Correction: Hybrid d 0 and d 10 electronic configurations promote photocatalytic activity of high-entropy oxides for CO 2 conversion and water splitting
abstract: Correction for ‘Hybrid d 0 and d 10 electronic configurations promote photocatalytic activity of high-entropy oxides for CO 2 conversion and water splitting’ by Jacqueline Hidalgo-Jiménez et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2024, 12 , 31589–31602, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TA04689G.

title: Thiophene vs. benzene: how π-spacer engineering transforms photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: Conjugated porous polymers (CPPs) are promising materials for photocatalysis, yet their efficiency is highly dependent on the rational design of molecular frameworks.

title: Nitrogen-doped rock-salt Li 3 V 2 O 5 nanosheet arrays with improved rate capability as an anode for thin film lithium-ion microbatteries
abstract: Nitrogen doping significantly boosts the rate capability of the rock-salt Li 3 V 2 O 5 anode in thin film lithium-ion microbatteries.

title: Enhanced Na storage performance through in situ depolymerization–repolymerization in bamboo-derived hard carbon anodes
abstract: High-performance bamboo-based hard carbon is synthesized by controlling the in situ depolymerization and repolymerization of bamboo components.

title: Scalable reflux synthesis of a bimicroporous hydrogen-bonded framework with specific molecule recognition ability for one-step ethylene purification
abstract: A scalable reflux synthesis of penetrated cage-channel bimicroporous hydrogen-bonded organic framework with extra-high stability and specific molecule recognition ability for the one-step purification of C 2 H 4 .

title: Probing charge trapping sites in M–N–C electrocatalysts via time-resolved transient absorption spectroscopy
abstract: Investigation of charge trapping in Fe- and Co-decorated carbon electrocatalysts for oxygen reduction reaction using transient absorption spectroscopy.

title: Synergizing photocatalysis with Aurivillius-phase Bi 4 Ti 3 O 12 : current insights and emerging trends
abstract: This contribution distills the advances in Aurivillius-phase Bi 4 Ti 3 O 12 based photocatalysts by highlighting structure-properties correlations, principles and key strategies used to enhance photocatalytic functionality.

title: Development of single crystalline Al-doped ZnS nanodiscs as efficient photocatalysts for H 2 evolution reaction
abstract: Al-doping is implemented in single crystalline ZnS without affecting its morphology and crystallinity. The intermediate trap levels generated by Al-doping helped significantly improve its photocatalytic performance for H 2 evolution.

title: An n-type ionic thermoelectric hydrogel with confined cation diffusion for boosted low-grade heat harvesting
abstract: An n-type ionic thermoelectric hydrogel with high thermopower and superior output power density was demonstrated through coordination and electrostatic interactions between cations and functional groups.

title: Fatigue-resistant hydrogels with programmable crystalline domain crosslinking enabled by coordinated thermal-solvent strategy
abstract: We developed a WE-PVA hydrogel via wet annealing and solvent exchange, achieving ultrahigh strength, exceptional fatigue resistance, and stable sensing, making it ideal for smart artificial ligaments with mechanical adaptability and self-diagnostics.

title: Organic ligands and CeO 2 -induced generic valence modulation strategies to design Fe active sites for promoted oxygen involved reactions in rechargeable zinc–air batteries
abstract: CeO 2 -induced multivalent state modulation strategies aim to modulate Fe active sites to enhance oxygen reduction and oxygen evolution reactions.

title: Doping hafnium with a high ligand field to modulate the electronic structure of Fe/NC for achieving remarkable ORR activity in Zn–air and Mg–air batteries
abstract: Doping high-ligand-field Hf atoms can optimize the binding energy of oxygen-containing intermediates at Fe–N x sites. The Hf–Fe/NC electrocatalyst exhibited potential for practical applications in Zn–air and Mg–air batteries.

title: A fluorinated bifunctional additive achieving stable electrode/electrolyte interfaces for high-voltage lithium-metal batteries
abstract: A fluorinated additive, M-3FEn-IO, preferentially undergoes redox reactions at the electrode/electrolyte interfaces (EEIs), forming LiF-rich EEIs to enhance cycling stability and lifespan in high-voltage Li-metal batteries.

title: Mechanism of water vapor and SO 2 poisoning resistance in iron-fortified micron spherical Ce 1 Mn 7 O x for ultra-low temperature NH 3 -SCR of NO x
abstract: Achieving ultra-low temperature resistance to water vapor and SO 2 poisoning in deNO x catalysts remains a critical challenge for enabling ultra-low NO x emissions from high-humidity, SO 2 -containing flue gases in non-electric industries.

title: Phenothiazine-based self-assembled monolayers for efficient tin perovskite solar cells with Co-cations
abstract: Schematic demonstration of the mixed cationic tin perovskite solar cells fabricated using a two-step approach with the hole-transport layer replaced by the bromide substituted phenothiazine self-assembled monolayer (SAM) molecules.

title: Enhanced 3D sponge with asymmetric wettability: an efficient solution for ultra-low resistance oil aerosol filtration over extended durations
abstract: The pressure drop of the 3D filter was two orders of magnitude lower than that of previously reported 2D fibrous filters and its quality factor was 30 times superior.

title: Direct hot deformation of as-grown n-type Bi 2 Te 3 ingots: a simple thermoelectric optimization strategy
abstract: A simple and effective strategy for direct hot deformation is proposed to tune the carrier concentration and induce dense dislocations in zone-melted n-type Bi 2 Te 3 ingots, leading to improved output performance in thermoelectric power generators.

title: Born liquid to live solid: in situ polymerized electrolyte enables stable operation of organic-Li metal batteries
abstract: Simultaneous application of LiPF 6 /carbonate and LiTFSI/dioxolane-based electrolytes leads to the formation of gel electrolyte enabling stable cycling of organic cathodes due to suppression of material dissolution and formation of optimal interfaces.

title: Highly sensitive ion regulation enabled by photothermal wood nanochannels
abstract: MoS 2 -decorated wood nanochannels achieve photothermally enhanced ion regulation in complex electrolyte environments.

title: A carbon-based hybrid catalytic-protective overlayer for kesterite photocathodes for solar water splitting
abstract: The kesterite photocathode was developed using a carbon-based protective-catalytic layer to improve charge transfer and resistance to photoelectrolysis degradation. Such materials may support continued investigation into solar-fuel technologies.

title: Realizing ultrahigh energy storage performance in sodium bismuth titanate-based ceramics via local polarization engineering
abstract: Herein, we designed a Bi 0.5 Na 0.5 TiO 3 -based ceramic and achieved a high W rec of 14.83 J cm −3 and a high η of 90.15% by regulating local polarization.

title: Spider silk-mimetic cross-linked elastomer: phenyl phosphonate-driven hierarchical hard domain for unprecedented integrated flame retardancy, mechanical robustness and recyclability
abstract: The spontaneous self-assembly of phenyl phosphonate units into hierarchical hard domains within a dynamically cross-linked elastomer enables an unprecedented integration of flame retardancy, mechanical robustness and recyclability.

title: Synergistic engineering of anthracene- and thiazolo[5,4- d ] thiazole-based donor–acceptor conjugated microporous polymers with heteroatom adoption for enhanced energy storage capacity
abstract: The ANPh-TzTz CMP demonstrates excellent electrochemical properties, offering a high specific capacitance of 541 F g −1 with 94% retention after prolonged cycling. Its symmetric coin-cell device delivers 220 F g −1 capacitance.

title: Non-noble-metal catalysts for electrocatalytic ammonia oxidation
abstract: Ammonia emerges as a high-energy density, carbon-free medium for the storage, transport, and utilization of green electricity.

title: Experimental and theoretical insights into the support-dependent N 2 selectivity of CuO-based NH 3 -SCO catalysts
abstract: The inclusion of a support could determine the selectivity of CuO-based catalysts by tuning the local coordination environment of active sites and interactions with intermediates.

title: CdS nanofeathers enable efficient electron-mediator-free photocatalytic regeneration of coenzyme NAD(P)H via direct electron–proton coupling
abstract: CdS nanofeathers enable efficient electron-mediator-free photocatalytic regeneration of coenzyme NAD(P)H via direct electron–proton coupling, providing a green, economical, and sustainable strategy for coenzyme regeneration.

title: Bimetallic Ag–Au nanoparticles from nanoconfinement: adjusting properties by electrochemical synthesis
abstract: Synthesis of Ag–Au nanoparticles of different sizes, compositions, element configurations (alloy- and core–shell-like) and electrocatalytic activities by using precursor-loaded PS–P2VP reverse micelles as nanoreactors.

title: Towards longevity in solid oxide electrolysis cells: multi-scale modeling and machine learning for degradation diagnosis and mitigation
abstract: The degradation of SOECs is influenced by coupling across multiple scales. It is necessary to consider establishing a full-chain research framework that integrates multiscale numerical simulations with artificial intelligence methods.

title: Impact of catalyst engineering on the durability performance of self-supported catalysts in anion exchange membrane water electrolyzers: recent advances and perspectives
abstract: Optimizing these catalyst engineering strategies leads to substantial enhancements in durability performance, resulting in widespread implementation.

title: Novel CuCo 2 O 4 photonic crystals for optical hydrogen sensing: catalyst-free detection and mechanistic insights via in situ Raman spectroscopy
abstract: We report the proof-of-concept optical sensor based on a photonic crystal of CuCo 2 O 4 and its mechanism using in situ raman spectroscopy. The detection of hydrogen gas, an emerging promising fuel, is achieved with novel and stable oxide without any noble catalyst or thermal activation.

title: Navigating ionic conductivity in MOF electrolytes: addressing measurement pitfalls and performance limits
abstract: Schematic of a solid-state battery with a MOF-based electrolyte, where Li + ions migrate through the porous framework, facilitating ionic conduction between the cathode and anode.

title: Zirconium-based MOFs for light-driven reactions: a critical assessment of recent progress
abstract: This review summarizes progress on Zr-MOFs in light-driven CO 2 reduction, hydrogen evolution, and pollutant degradation. It highlights their stability, tunable porosity, and strategies to boost photocatalytic performance for sustainable and environmental applications.

title: Superhydrophobic granular filter media modified with mesoporous nanoparticles for magnetically guided oil cleanup
abstract: A superhydrophobic ceramic filter embedded with Fe 3 O 4 /mesoporous silica, designed for oil–water separation, demonstrates stable performance under harsh conditions and enables rapid magnetic recovery.

title: In situ growth of redox-active porous organic polymers on Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene for high-performance pseudocapacitors
abstract: An MXene/POP hybrid material with abundant redox-active sites was prepared through an in situ growth strategy and exhibited high specific capacitance, excellent rate performance, and outstanding long-term cycling stability for pseudocapacitors.

title: Tuning of mechanical properties of doped PbTe-based thermoelectric materials driven by intrinsic defects
abstract: Several types of intrinsic and external defects are commonly observed in PbTe, which influences mechanical brittleness and thermoelectric performance either positively or negatively.

title: Source dependence of polyacrylonitrile electrospun nanofibers on piezoelectric response
abstract: The piezoelectric properties of electrospun polyacrylonitrile nanofiber membranes depend heavily on the polyacrylonitrile material's source.

title: Enhancing the electrochemical catalytic performance of novel bifunctional oxygen vacancy-enriched silver niobate (AgNbO 3 ) through electrochemical activation
abstract: Oxygen vacancies enhanced overall water-splitting efficiency in the AgNbO 3 electrocatalyst.

title: A general method for precise modification of –O termination on MXenes by low-pressure flash annealing
abstract: MXenes terminated with the –O group are of paramount importance for fundamental research and applications; herein, we report a general method for the precise modification of –O termination on MXenes by employing low-pressure flash annealing.

title: Tunable ferroelectric diatomic catalysis on In 2 Se 3 monolayers for NO reduction
abstract: A ferroelectric In 2 Se 3 -supported Ru diatomic catalyst enables dynamically tunable NO-to-NH 3 conversion, achieving a remarkably low barrier of 0.54 eV via polarization switching.

title: 4D printing of programmable liquid–vapor phase change composites for multi-responsive flexible actuators
abstract: The 3D printing process, expansion mechanism and deformation process of a magneto-thermal responsive liquid–vapor phase change actuator.

title: Improved charge delivery within a covalently ligated cobalt phthalocyanine electrocatalyst for CO 2 reduction
abstract: Cobalt( ii ) phthalocyanine (CoPc) complexes are some of the most active catalysts for the CO 2 electroreduction reaction (CO 2 ERR).

title: Structural insights into mononuclear Cu 1 motifs for efficient CO electroreduction
abstract: σ–π synergy and spatial confinement in Cu 1 –C 2 N motifs optimize the reactivity–stability balance for CO electrocatalysis over conventional Cu–N x sites.

title: Impact of chitosan's degree of deacetylation, molecular weight, and crystallinity on the photoresponsive properties of azobenzene-modified films and membranes
abstract: Reversible photomodulation of chitosan thin-films with an azobenzene: impact of degrees of deacetylation, polymerisation and azobenzene concentration on mechanical properties and water vapor permeability under UV irradiation.

title: Rational design of nitrogen-doped porous carbon support on single atom catalysts for efficient CO 2 electroreduction
abstract: The relationship between support properties and CO 2 electroreduction performance was elucidated. A series of N-doped porous carbons with varying porous structures and degrees of graphitization were prepared to support single atomic Ni–N 4 sites.

title: Boosting charge migration kinetics using an Fe–S bridge for efficacious photocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: Fe–S bond acts as charge bridge and built-in electric field within the Fe 2 O 3 /defective Bi 19 Br 3 S 27 S-scheme heterojunction for effective charge separation, exhibiting robust photocatalytic CO 2 reduction with exceptional CO selectivity.

title: Revealing complex magnetic interactions in Fe 2 P-based compounds: a study using Mössbauer spectroscopy and neutron diffraction
abstract: In Fe 2−2 x Mn 2 x P 1− x Si x , the magnetic properties have 4 different regions out of which 3 have ferromagnetic character. In region (ii), a glassy magnetic behaviour is found with an incommensurate ordering, exemplified with Fe 1.8 Mn 0.2 P 0.9 Si 0.1 .

title: A localized surface plasmon resonance-interface induces ultrafast hot-electron spatiotemporal transfer for boosting photocatalytic H 2 evolution integrated with benzylamine C–N coupling
abstract: LSPR-interface structures in plasmonic MoO 3− x @ZnIn 2 S 4 heterojunctions provide an energy-loss-free charge transport channel to enable ultrafast hot-electron transfer for boosting photocatalytic H 2 evolution integrated with benzylamine C–N coupling.

title: Thermally-driven conformational twist in organic azobenzene linker activates molecular doping effect in thin films of lanthanide MOFs
abstract: Azobenzene-based lanthanide MOF thin films fabricated via electrodeposition exhibited a unique thermally-driven conformational twist, distinct from trans – cis isomerization, significantly enhancing electrical conductivity upon molecular doping.

title: A vanadium-doped Cu x O nanorod array with modulated electronic structure for enhanced aqueous energy storage
abstract: Vanadium isomorphic substitution at copper sites can modulate the electronic and lattice structure of the pristine materials, thereby driving electron transfer and enhancing electrical conductivity and reaction kinetics for energy storage.

title: Scalable and environmentally friendly MXene-tetrahedrites for next-generation flexible thermoelectrics
abstract: Traditional thermoelectric generators (TEGs) face scalability challenges due to high-temperature, long-duration curing processes and rare-earth/toxic chalcogenides such as bismuth telluride.

title: Microenvironment regulation via nitrogen atoms in covalent organic frameworks for enhanced selective photocatalysis
abstract: Through the microenvironment regulation via nitrogen atoms, TpBTPm-COF delivers significantly enhanced photocatalytic activity for the selective oxidation of sulfides, compared to TpTAPT-COF and TpBTPy-COF.

title: Development of a smart plasma-treated nano-filter using carbon nitride nanostructures for oil–water emulsion separation: experimental and theoretical studies
abstract: In response to global concerns over oil and water separation, the research and development of super-wetting filters with high permeation flux has increased.

title: Chemical modification of a small-molecule acceptor with an adamantyl side chain for efficient and thermally stable organic solar cells
abstract: A simple strategy of side-chain engineering on SMAs for thermally stable OSCs is reported. The mono-substituted BOAD with an adamantyl group enables an outstanding efficiency of 17.13% and improves thermal stability for the corresponding OSCs.

title: Scalable assembly of flexible ultrathin all-in-one MXene-based supercapacitors
abstract: Flexible ultrathin all-in-one supercapacitors with fast electrochemical reaction kinetics and stable electrochemical performance were fabricated using a scalable blade coating strategy.

title: Characterization and reuse of SiC flakes generated during electrochemical etching of 4H-SiC wafers
abstract: Silicon carbide porous flakes are produced during the electrochemical etching (ECE) of n-doped 4H-SiC wafers. The differences in porosity between the Si-face and C-face allow for the adsorption of various dyes.

title: Self-healing and stretchable tetrameric PVA-CNF-PVP-IL complex ionogel with high-performance ionic thermoelectric properties
abstract: We prepared self-healable PVA-CNF-PVP-IL thermoelectric ionogels with enhanced mechanical strength. Hydrogen bonds between PVA and CNF/PVP form a dynamic network, enabling excellent self-healing and thermoelectric properties through multiple cycles.

title: Balancing atomic hydrogen supply and nitrate electroreduction for enhanced ammonia synthesis on Pt-Cu 2 O/Cu electrocatalysts
abstract: Pt-Cu 2 O/Cu electrocatalysts achieve efficient nitrate-to-ammonia conversion by balancing atomic hydrogen supply from Pt sites with nitrate hydrogenation at stabilized Cu + sites.

title: Band energy engineering: precise regulation of P-band centers to reasonably construct S-scheme heterojunctions for boosting photocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: Here, Co 2 VO 4 /CdS S-scheme heterojunction has been successfully prepared. Co 2 VO 4 keeps the center of S-p band away from Fermi level, promotes H* desorption, reduces Gibbs free energy, and greatly improves photocatalytic hydrogen evolution capacity.

title: Microwave and NaCl driven synthesis of P-doped graphitic carbon at atmospheric pressure for long-life vanadium redox flow batteries
abstract: Phosphorus-doped graphitic carbon with enhanced performance for VRFBs synthesized via sustainable, scalable microwave and NaCl-driven process.

title: Design of a multifunctional gradient double coating layer for a stable thin zinc anode with high depth of discharge
abstract: A gradient double-layer (gelatinized starch top layer and bottom carbon layer) design suppresses dendrite growth and corrosion via optimizing the desolvation kinetics and guiding Zn deposition, thus enabling cycling stability for ultrathin Zn anode.

title: Stable and efficient organic solar cells featuring an ultra-thin and transparent solution-deposited MoO 3 hole extraction layer
abstract: Our study reveals that prolonged thermal and light illumination leads to chemical interactions between the transparent electrode and the active organic semiconductor, compromising the interface quality and long-term stability.

title: Synthesis of MnM–NC (M = Ga, In, Sn) dual-single-atom catalysts for efficient electrocatalytic oxygen reduction
abstract: A series of dual-single-atom catalysts that consist of Mn and p-block metal single atoms dispersed on N-doped carbon are developed, and enhanced electrocatalytic oxygen reduction performance is achieved due to p–d orbital hybridization.

title: What impact does ammonia have on the microstructure of the precursor and the electrochemical performance of Ni-rich layered oxides?
abstract: This study explores how ammonia concentration affects particle morphology, nucleation rates, and the electrochemical performance of Ni-rich cathode materials, emphasizing the critical role of microstructural control in improved battery performance.

title: Microtextured coatings from superhydrophobic bark-derived bioparticles for fog harvesting
abstract: Microtextured hybrid coatings made from superhydrophobic bioparticles derived from birch bark greatly enhance the water collection efficiency of fog harvesters.

title: Modulation of the photo-response of SrTiO 3 : influence of surface defects on solid oxide photoelectrochemical cells
abstract: A novel light-assisted fuel cell, termed a solid oxide photoelectrochemical cell (SOPEC), is proposed to enhance energy conversion efficiency at low temperatures.

title: In situ polymerization of an electrochemically stable dual-salt gel polymer electrolyte for lithium ion batteries
abstract: The LiPF 6 /LiTFSI dual-salt gel polymer electrolyte possesses a well-distributed framework which benefits lithium ion transport pathway and further enhances its compatibility with lithium metal and electrochemical performance of lithium ion batteries.

title: A bifunctional electrocatalyst engineered via polyanionic synergy and heterointerface modulation for robust seawater electrolysis
abstract: FeNi-LDH/Co-Ni 3 S 2 heterostructure excels in seawater electrolysis. Its multilayer blocks Cl − via electrostatic repulsion. Modified FeNiOOH enhances OH − adsorption, and the nanoflower–nanosheet structure maximizes active sites.

title: MOF-derived nickel cobaltite: a pathway to enhanced supercapacitor performance
abstract: A streamlined design of MOF-derived electrode nanoarchitecture for hybrid supercapacitors featuring hierarchically layered nickel cobaltite nanosheets with extensive porous networks.

title: Multi-orbital engineering of single-atom catalysts: unlocking high-efficiency nitrate reduction
abstract: The crucial role of heteroatom doping-induced coordination symmetry breaking in SACs is reported and provides vital insights into the impact of coordination microenvironments on electrocatalytic mechanisms and the rational design of NO 3 RR catalysts.

title: Boosting the capacity of Mg-stabilized Na 0.67 Ni 0.27 Mg 0.06 Mn 0.66 O 2 cathodes via particle size control in an emulsion-based synthesis route
abstract: We employed an emulsion-based synthetic route to optimize the particle size of sodium-ion battery cathodes and achieve excellent electrochemical properties.

title: Probing sodium structures and dynamics in hard carbon for Na-ion batteries using 23 Na operando solid-state NMR spectroscopy
abstract: In this study, we aim to (i) provide an in-depth analysis of the formation and composition of the SEI, (ii) understand and differentiate the sodium storage processes, and (iii) distinguish between Na metal plating and dendrite growth.

title: Regulating the electronic structure of CoMoO 4 via La doping for efficient and durable electrochemical water splitting reactions
abstract: Metal molybdates (M′MoO 4 , M = Fe, Co, and Ni) are recognized as active catalysts for water-splitting reactions.

title: Mo-doped CdS: optimized electronic structure boosts carrier separation
abstract: The photocatalytic mechanism of Mo-CdS/8 in the degradation of pollutants under visible light.

title: Improving zinc anodes with a vapor-deposited uniform ultrathin copper layer for enhanced ion distribution and dendrite suppression
abstract: Aqueous zinc ion batteries are a promising technology for rechargeable batteries due to their low cost and high safety.

title: Switching the formation of intermediates through inherent Cu + –OH structures over Cu-based catalysts for enhanced electrochemical CO 2 reduction to ethylene
abstract: Inherent surface Cu + –OH structures over tentacle-like Cu 2 (OH) 2 CO 3 precursor-derived Cu-based catalysts could switch the formation of key intermediates to boost the production of ethylene from electrochemical CO 2 reduction.

title: Vanadium-based oxyhalide photocatalysts for visible-light-driven Z-scheme water splitting: advancing conduction band engineering
abstract: This study demonstrates the potential of V-based oxyhalides as oxygen-evolving photocatalysts in Z-scheme water-splitting systems.

title: Correction: Cellulose derived magnetic mesoporous carbon nanocomposites with enhanced hexavalent chromium removal
abstract: Correction for ‘Cellulose derived magnetic mesoporous carbon nanocomposites with enhanced hexavalent chromium removal’ by Bin Qiu et al. , J. Mater. Chem. A , 2014, 2 , 17454–17462, https://doi.org/10.1039/C4TA04040F.

title: Molecular photoswitches for energy storage
abstract: Rachel Evans, Grace Han, Tao Li and Hermann A. Wegner introduce the Journal of Materials Chemistry A and C themed collection on molecular photoswitches for energy storage.

title: Self-protecting interlocked electrodes for highly efficient and stable alkaline seawater electrolyzers
abstract: A self-protecting strategy to design a dual-layer graphene oxide anode and interconnected nanorod-array cathode is developed to simultaneously mitigate electrode corrosion and precipitation.

title: Machine-learning-assisted prediction of highly active and stable PrBaCo 2 O 5+ δ -based oxygen electrodes for solid oxide cells
abstract: A new highly active and stable PBC-based material was screened by a machine learning technique. The ABE and η descriptors are identified as important factors for the activity and stability of the PBC-based electrode, respectively.

title: Porous polymer in polymer structure created using carbon dots for high-performance gel polymer electrolytes
abstract: Porous PEO@PVDF–HFP, created using carbon dots for liquid electrolyte absorption, forms highly conductive gel polymer electrolytes, which are assembled into lithium metal batteries, demonstrating excellent cycling stability at room temperature.

title: All-in-one solar-activated MXene@CuS membrane for advanced water purification
abstract: A multifunctional MPCT membrane, developed by integrating MXene@CuS and PVA-CMC onto melamine foam, exhibited a water evaporation rate of 3.4 kg m −2 h −1 along with excellent oil/water separation and photocatalytic degradation.

title: Evaluating the terminal group switching of responsive monolayer films: examining structural rearrangements via molecular simulations and experimental wetting measurements
abstract: Functionalized alkylsilane films exhibit switching interfacial behavior in response to different solvents. Image shows exposed oxygen (red) and carbon atoms (green). Hydroxyl films switch more favorably than carboxyl counterparts.

title: Magnesium borohydride pyridine derivatives as electrolytes for all-solid-state batteries
abstract: Pyridine magnesium borohydrides, Mg(BH 4 ) 2 · x N(CH) 5 ( x = 2, 3), are new types of fast Mg-ion conductors with crystal structures built from tetrahedral ( x = 2) or trigonal bypyramidal ( x = 3) molecular units.

title: Recent advances in characterization of rechargeable battery materials via scanning probe microscopy
abstract: Scanning probe microscopy can be used to obtain topographical, mechanical, electrical, and electrochemical information on a wide range of materials in a variety of environments, including in situ and operando studies for rechargeable battery systems.

title: Multifunctional ion-conductive polymer coatings for high-performance sulfide solid-state batteries with Ni-rich cathodes
abstract: Li + -conducting polymerized ionic liquid coatings chemomechanically stabilize and improve Li + transport at the cathode/electrolyte interface in solid-state batteries.

title: Effect of Ni–W–S electrocatalysts on the stability and electrochemical properties of photocathodes
abstract: Schematic of NiWS/TiO 2 /Si photocathode, where W-doped Ni sulfide is photoelectrodeposited on TiO 2 -passivated Si to boost charge transfer, achieving −30.8 mA cm −2 at 0 V RHE and stable operation over 1000 h for efficient solar H 2 generation.

title: Spatial engineering of electrode architectures with conducting polymer for high-performance lithium hybrid capacitors: interior 3D networks versus outer 2D layers
abstract: In this study, we investigate the strategic spatial integration of a conducting polymer into lithium electrodes for energy storage applications.

title: Precision engineering of copper vacancies in CuInTe 2 for synergistically enhanced thermoelectric performance
abstract: Copper vacancy engineering in CuInTe 2 tailors band structure and defect chemistry for improved thermoelectric performances.

title: Synthesis of photoresponsive liquid crystal elastomers: a general chemical approach
abstract: This work presents a general strategy for integrating photoresponsive molecules into liquid crystal elastomers (LCEs) using Diels–Alder chemistry. The method introduces various photochromes, offering a scalable route for multifunctional LCEs.

title: Engineering nitrogen-doped porous carbon positive electrodes for high-performance sodium-ion capacitors: the critical role of porosity, structure and surface functionalities
abstract: Sodium-ion capacitors currently suffer from a lack of understanding of porous carbon cathodes. Linear correlations revealed that the specific capacity is driven by the specific surface area, structural defects, and functional groups.

title: Relieving strain on silver nanowires embedded in polyimide films for efficient flexible organic photovoltaic modules
abstract: Strain caused by polyimide drying degrades electrical conductance of embedded AgNWs. It is relieved by reducing the thickness of polyimide to fabricate flexible organic photovoltaic modules.

title: Free COOH-tethered layered Co( ii ) framework and flexible composite as a size-reliant, tandem and robust catalyst for mild and scalable synthesis of bioactive molecules
abstract: Synopsis: the free COOH-tethered layered Co( ii ) framework and its light-weight flexible composite act as a size-reliant and superior catalyst for scalable synthesis of bioactive 4H-pyrans via a multicomponent tandem reaction under benign conditions.

title: Computational investigation of high stability and solar-to-hydrogen efficiency in two-dimensional SiP, GeP, and SnP for enhanced photocatalytic water splitting
abstract: SiP, GeP, and SnP monolayers demonstrate exceptional stability, high electron mobility (1558.15–6243.65 cm 2 V −1 s −1 ), strong solar absorption (∼10 5 cm −1 ), and impressive solar-to-hydrogen efficiencies (8–13%).

title: Low-cost and transparent cooling films with solar-selective nanoparticles for reducing the energy consumption of buildings
abstract: The solar selective films proposed in this work can enable excellent cooling performance, resulting in a decrease in the indoor temperature by 11 °C in summer and annual air conditioning energy saving of ∼25% in Hong Kong.

title: Investigation of electrode passivation during oxidation of a nitroxide radical relevant for flow battery applications
abstract: Electrochemical behavior of 4-hydroxy-TEMPO (HT) is studied at realistic flow battery conditions such as high concentration and low rate. HT passivates the electrode surface, but a self-cleaning mechanism is a solution for HT use in flow batteries.

title: Flexible core–shell difunctional nanoreactor CsPbBr 3 @Bi 2 MoO 6 –CuS/PAN for real-time monitoring of photocatalysis
abstract: CBC/PAN flexible core–shell nanoreactor with synergistic effects of efficient photocatalysis and real-time temperature monitoring provides a new strategy for the application of photocatalytic materials in environmental pollution.

title: Modulating the discharge capacity and cycling performance of the LiMn 0.6 Fe 0.4 PO 4 cathode for lithium-ion batteries via titanium introduction
abstract: Ti doping significantly enhances electrochemical performance through improved lithium-ion diffusion kinetics, especially for the Mn 2+ /Mn 3+ redox couple.

title: One-step synthesized iron foam-based NiFe 2 O 4 applied for self-powered water splitting hydrogen production
abstract: The achieved one-step iron foam-based NiFe 2 O 4 delivers a large current density in electrocatalytic hydrolysis and exhibits ultra-long-life charging–discharging cycling stability when assembled as a zinc–air battery.

title: Cross-linked electrodeposited conjugated polymers based on bis-thiophene-carbazole bis-adducts with an aromatic core for high performance supercapacitor electrodes
abstract: Electrodeposited conjugated microporous polymers (CMPs) are attractive materials for energy storage as they can meet the key requirements of high capacitance, mixed electronic/ionic conductivities, and easy integration in (micro)storage systems.

title: Crystallization kinetic engineering for growth of thin metal halide perovskite platelets
abstract: Thin Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 platelets possessing optimized optical and photo-response properties are obtained via a crystallization kinetic regulation strategy.

title: Precision engineering for optimizing multi-metallic IrRuO x catalysts via electronic structure quantification for sustainable hydrogen production
abstract: ESMI bridges atomic electronics to macroscopic OER kinetics in metal oxides. Precise electronic tuning in Ir 0.6 Ru 0.4 O x yields high 7152.60 mA mg −1 activity. Strategic hydroxyl-lattice oxygen ratio (1.224) maximises electron transfer efficiency.

title: Enhanced P-type conductivity in Sb 2 Se 3 through alkali and alkaline earth metal doping
abstract: Doping of alkaline earth atoms significantly increases p-type conductivity of antimony selenide (Sb 2 Se 3 ) compared to the undoped system due to the presence of shallow, low-energy, and hole-generating acceptor defects.

title: Investigation of charge transfer models on the evolution of phases in lithium iron phosphate batteries using phase-field simulations
abstract: Phase evolution in batteries are more reliably modelled by the Marcus–Hush–Chidsey expression, as it modulates the current density.

title: Synergistic optimization of composition–structure–conductive network for high-performance integrated transition metal oxide anodes for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: The composition, structure, and conductive network achieve exceptional lithium storage performance of the hpCM-CMO-Cu electrode.

title: Investigating transition metal crosstalk on SEI stability as a function of anode chemistry
abstract: This study explores the effects of transition metal (TM) ions—Ni, Co, Mn, and Fe—on the solid-electrolyte interphase (SEI) formation and electrochemical behavior of graphite and silicon (Si) anodes in lithium-ion batteries (LIBs).

title: Efficient electrochemical nitrate reduction reaction and a zinc–nitrate battery using atomically thin Cu-modified V 2 C MXenes
abstract: The optimized Cu–V 2 C catalyst achieves 11.25 mol g cat. −1 h −1 ammonia yield rate and ∼100% FE, doubling pristine V 2 C performance.

title: Au atom tailoring of palladium nanocatalysts to boost cathodic coupling of carbon dioxide and methanol into dimethyl carbonate
abstract: Au single atom tailoring of palladium nanocatalysts boosts the cathodic coupling of carbon dioxide and methanol into dimethyl carbonate through effective electronic modulation.

title: High-performance plasma doping as an advantageous vacancy engineering approach for the catalytic activation of materials: the case example of hydroxyapatite
abstract: Hydroxyapatite is doped with oxygen atoms using non-thermal plasma. The resulting material presents a high catalytic activity for the conversion of CO 2 under mild conditions.

title: Insight into the effect of d-orbital electron rearrangements induced by Zr–H interactions: first-principles calculations
abstract: The study elucidates the differences in hydrogen adsorption mechanisms between zirconium and zirconium hydride from the perspective of orbital bonding, offering a theoretical basis for understanding hydrogen embrittlement.

title: Robust Cu(0) active sites stabilized by high surface pH gradient for ultra-low-potential HMF oxidation
abstract: The HMFOR active potential window can be regulated by controlling the formation of the OH − depletion region on high-roughness catalysts.

title: Graphene oxide dough composites: direct mixing and structural design strategies for high-performance electronics and energy applications
abstract: A deformable, binder-free graphene oxide dough enables direct nanomaterial integration into dense composites, offering a scalable platform for multifunctional energy and electronic applications.

title: SiOC ceramic aerogels with high optical transparency and ultralow thermal conductivity for solar power application
abstract: Transparent SiOC aerogels with ultralow thermal conductivity and high NIR transmittance are demonstrated as promising candidates for solar power applications, combining thermal insulation with excellent stability up to 1000 °C.

title: High-conductivity, low-temperature sintering-compatible NASICON solid electrolyte for enhanced compositing with hard carbon electrode in all-solid-state batteries
abstract: Enabling hard carbon anode material to perform effectively in a safe and robust all-inorganic solid-state system.

title: Emerging paired diatomic sites toward synergistic NH 3 synthesis
abstract: We summarize the state-of-the-art dual atom catalysts (DACs) for the synergistic catalytic conversion of N2/NO x to NH3. Strategies for modifying DACs to enhance catalytic activity are also discussed.

title: Interfacial adsorption-enabled trace additives for stable zinc-based flow batteries
abstract: The DIPSO as a bifunctional additive restrains the side reaction and promotes uniform deposition of zinc for zinc–iron flow battery.

title: Understanding ionic transport in perovskite lithium-ion conductor Li 3/8 Sr 7/16 Ta 3/4 Hf 1/4 O 3 : a neutron diffraction and molecular dynamics simulation study
abstract: Solid-state Li-ion electrolytes (SSEs) are essential for the development of next-generation solid-state Li-metal batteries and new Li-extraction electrochemical cells.

title: Understanding the roles of ionic liquids in photocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: This review summarizes the multifaceted roles of ionic liquids in photocatalytic CO 2 reduction, highlighting the promotional effect of ILs on photocatalytic activity of the CO 2 reduction reaction.

title: Integrating bimetallic MOF-derived sulfides with MnO 2 : synergistic Cu–Co–S@MnO 2 heterojunctions for flexible hybrid supercapacitors
abstract: Cu–Co–S@MnO 2 supercapacitor demonstrate high capacity, stability and energy density.

title: One nanometer matters: quantum-induced discontinuity in the oxygen reduction reaction catalyzed by platinum nanoparticles
abstract: Pt nanoparticles show a quantum-induced ORR catalysis transition near 1.5 nm, where classical size–activity trends fail. Below this size, quantum confinement enhances activity, making a quantum boundary for size- and symmetry-driven catalyst design.

title: Origin of surface reconstruction via oxygen termination and improved hydrogen evolution reactions in MXenes
abstract: Surface reconstruction via oxygen termination facilitated by fundamental adsorbate–adsorbate interactions exposing heterogeneous catalytic sites with improved HER activities.

title: Tracing proton conduction pathways in polycrystalline MOF-based core–shell systems
abstract: A new approach based on hierarchical structures is demonstrated to study proton transport in polycrystalline systems. Interchangeable core–shell metal–organic frameworks offer precisely engineered pathways with spatially separated acid sites.

title: Efficient roll-to-roll intense pulsed light treatment of ZnO layer for scalable flexible organic photovoltaics
abstract: A roll-to-roll-based intense pulsed light treatment is proposed as an annealing method for ZnO layers in flexible organic solar cells, which can induce oxidizing and close packing of ZnO nanoparticles by completely removing the residual solvent.

title: Hydrogen evolution on halogenated MXenes via surface termination engineering: a data-informed computational and experimental strategy
abstract: Surface termination engineering in two-dimensional (2D) MXenes offers a transformative approach to tune electrocatalytic performance, particularly for the hydrogen evolution reaction (HER).

title: Engineering heteroatomic structures in Pt single-atom anchored covalent organic frameworks for enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Heteroatomic structural engineering on covalent organic frameworks (COFs) achieved optimal photoelectrochemical properties and single-atom Pt anchoring on TAPAT-TFP-Pt 1 for enhanced photocatalytic hydrogen evolution reaction (HER).

title: Lattice nitrogen-mediated optimization of intermediate evolution pathways enhances ammonia decomposition reaction kinetics
abstract: The lattice nitrogen-mediated reaction pathway promotes efficient hydrogen production from ammonia decomposition.

title: 3d transition metal oxide-loaded Cu nanowires for enhanced electrochemical nitrate-to-ammonia conversion
abstract: The interaction of Cu and CoO x on Cu nanowires supported CoO x promotes the nitrate adsorption and *H retention capability because of the synergistic effect, generating excellent electrocatalytic nitrate reduction reaction efficiency.

title: Progress in porous metal chalcogenides for electrocatalytic water splitting
abstract: This work reviews the progress in porous metal chalcogenides for electrocatalytic water splitting, highlighting their innovative design, the importance of the surface area, electrochemical performance, challenges, and future opportunities.

title: Mesoporous gold decorated MXene (Ti 3 C 2 T x ) flexible composite films for photo-enhanced solid-state micro-supercapacitors
abstract: Unique mesoporous Au NPs improved the capacitance, electrical conductivity, cycling stability and photocurrent response of MXene-based film electrodes.

title: Engineering Co–O–Zn bonds in CoWO 4 /ZnO heterojunctions toward boosted charge separation and photothermal antibacterial activity
abstract: Engineering electron-bridge bonds at heterojunction interfaces offers an effective strategy to enhance photocatalytic performance.

title: Polynorbornene copolymers combining flexible ether side chains and rigid hydrophobic segments for AEMWE
abstract: A polynorbornene-based membrane material is characterized, demonstrating excellent performance in PGM-free, dry cathode mode anion exchange membrane water electrolysis (AEMWE).

title: In situ assembly of the FeOOH@MOF-74 heterostructure on IF as efficient electrocatalysts for water splitting
abstract: In this study, FeOOH@MOF-74/IF nanostructures were designed. Through the strong electron interaction and efficient electron transfer between FeOOH and MOF-74, it shows good electrocatalytic activity.

title: Structure fine-tuning of benzoxazole-linked covalent organic frameworks (COFs) for photocatalytic hydroxylation of phenyl boronic acids
abstract: Novel structure fine-tuning benzoxazole-linked COFs H–BO-COF and MeO–BO-COF with different substitutes were successfully prepared. As photocatalysts for the hydroxylation of benzyl boronic acid, the performance of MeO–BO-COF is obviously better than that of H–BO-COF.

title: Engineering tunable dual dynamic networks toward strong yet reversible adhesion, healable and recyclable polyolefin elastomer-based adhesives
abstract: The design of dual dynamic networks from commodity polyolefin elastomers simultaneously achieves strong yet reversible adhesion, autonomous damage healing, and end-of-life recycling.

title: Two-dimensional covalent triazine frameworks for advanced electrochemical energy storage applications
abstract: The utilization of 2D CTFs in advanced electrochemical energy storage systems not only demonstrates the enhancement of the energy and power densities of these devices, but also promotes their cycling stability and rate performance.

title: Structural engineering of bimetallic NiMoO 4 for high-performance supercapacitors and efficient oxygen evolution reaction catalysts
abstract: Advancing energy storage and conversion research on 2D nanostructures hinges on the critical development of bifunctional electrodes capable of effectively catalyzing oxygen evolution reactions and facilitating charge storage applications.

title: Electronic structure engineering of heterogeneous ceria-incorporated Pt/C as an efficient catalyst for boosting DMFC performance
abstract: Combining CeO 2 and ultra-low-electronegativity Ce tuned electronic structure of Pt to form heterogeneous PtCe–CeO 2 /C. The higher reaction kinetics and faster turnover of intermediates led to superb MOR and ORR activity, and reliable DMFC operation.

title: Solvate ionic liquids: past, present and future
abstract: Solvate ionic liquids (SILs) are modular and tuneable, enabling their utilisation in diverse applications spanning energy storage, electrodeposition, and advanced synthesis.

title: Thermally tuned oxygenated covalent triazine framework via self-templating for a durable 3.8 V aqueous supercapacitor with record energy and power density
abstract: Cost-effective oxygenated covalent triazine frameworks, structurally tuned via in situ partial O -demethylation and graphitization, showing durable benchmark aqueous supercapacitor performance.

title: Parallel exploration of the optoelectronic properties of (Sb,Bi)(S,Se)(Br,I) chalcohalides
abstract: Chalcohalides are an emerging family of semiconductors with tunable material properties, shaped by the intricate interplay between their unique structural chemistry and vibrational dynamics.

title: Covalent organic framework-derived functional interphase for improving Zn chemistry in aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: This review systematically summarizes the cutting-edge development of covalent organic framework-derived functional interphases for improving Zn chemistry in aqueous zinc-ion batteries.

title: Establishing design principles for functional additives in antimony chalcogenide solar cells
abstract: By controlling the pH, the formation of Sb 2 O 3 is effectively suppressed through an in situ conversion mechanism, facilitating the direct transformation to Sb 2 S 3 and enhancing the material quality for high power conversion efficiency in solar cells.

title: Comparative study of molecular beam epitaxy-deposited ZnS:O and CdS:O as electron-transporting materials in Sb 2 (S,Se) 3 solar cells
abstract: ZnS:O is a promising CdS alternative for electron transport. Molecular beam epitaxy-deposited ZnS:O films enable5.15% efficiency in Sb 2 (S,Se) 3 solar cells. This study reveals key factors limiting the ZnS:O/Sb 2 (S,Se) 3 device performance.

title: Photocatalytic synergies: mechanisms, enhancement strategies, and applications
abstract: Synergistic multi-field photocatalysis—integrating photo-piezoelectric, photothermal, photomagnetic, and other effects—uses in situ characterization and ab initio methods to boost charge separation and reaction kinetics for environmental/energy use.

title: Functional nickel iron sulphide/hydroxide catalysts for hydrazine oxidation and energy saving hydrogen production
abstract: The synergistic interaction between Ni and Fe sites in NiFe-hydroxide/sulfide enhances catalytic activity, enabling high-performance water and hydrazine electrolysis at industrial-scale current densities.

title: Hierarchical hybrid crosslinking multifunctional gelatin-based hydrogel: ideal platforms for flexible wearable devices, brain–computer interfaces and biomedical applications
abstract: An ultrastretchable, self-healing multilevel hybrid crosslinking gelatin-based hydrogel for flexible wearable devices, brain–computer interfaces and biomedical applications.

title: Enabling rational electrolyte design for lithium batteries through precise descriptors: progress and future perspectives
abstract: The physicochemical properties accurately captured by the precise descriptors enable researchers to efficiently screen and identify optimal compounds for designing high-performance electrolytes for Li batteries.

title: Multifunctional hydroquinone additive for enhanced zinc–iodine hybrid battery capacitors: suppressing polyiodide shuttle effects and dendrite growth
abstract: Multifunctional HQ additive for high energy zinc–iodine hybrid battery capacitors.
